songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights bleedin' colors
Past my window blur
Engine hummin' low
Nothin' left to stir
Just the city sleepin'
And my thoughts on a spree
Yeah the night it calls my name
Whispers soft and low
Empty streets a neon dream
Just me and the machine
Empty streets a neon dream
Yeah a silent scene
Ghost of traffic lights
A memory of sound
Headlights cut the black
No one else around
Just the asphalt rollin'
Underneath my wheels
Every corner turnin'
A new story to tell
Every shadow dancin'
Breakin' free from its spell
This quiet journey
A feeling I can keep
Empty streets a neon dream
Just me and the machine
Empty

songs.lyrics:

Over the trees
And the sound gets louder
From the light above me
On a long train ride
Through the mountainside
And when the sun rises again
When the sun rises again
When the sun rises again
When the sun rises again
When the sun rises again

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
Worked every day since I turned fifteen
Driving that truck and mowing that green
Counting every dollar 'til I could buy a dream
Said goodbye to Mama and my best friends
And I hit the road with that blue rear end
Hoping every night I'd see 'em all again
Told 'em, "If you don't see me for a while, then you know I made it"
And I still pray it
"Someday, you'll hear my name on the radio"
But I got so much farther left to go
And I've got big, small-town dreams
Small-town dreams
And I've got big, small-town dreams
That ol' blacktop's seen better days
And the sky gets darker every night I stay
But I've got hope, it's just a little further away
Told 'em, "If you don't see me for a while, then you know I made it"
And I still pray it
"Someday, you'll hear my name on the radio"
But I got so much farther left to go
And I've got big, small-town dreams
Small-town dreams
And I've got big, small-town dreams
I miss those cornfields
And I miss the blue sky
I miss those dirt roads
And that county line
If I don't make it back this time
Told 'em, "If you don't see me for a while, then you know I made it"
And I still pray it
"Someday, you'll hear my name on the radio"
But I got so much farther left to go
And I've got big, small-town dreams
Small-town dreams
And I've got big, small-town dreams

The knock comes at the door
And I wake
I wake to find

And always will be
The clock strikes five
And my head is filled with light
I wake

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
Where do you find the recipe for cornbread and chicken?
Where do you learn how to fry up the fish that you're fishin'?
Where do you find the medicine that makes you feel better?
And who told you to pray for rain in a drought and stormy weather?
When the preacher is preachin'
How do you know he's for real?
And who told you when the thunder is rumblin' that the devil's on your heels?
It came from the family Bible in a warm kitchen
With the smell of cast iron and home cookin'
With Grandma in a long apron with a short temper
Her voice like a gospel hymn
It was deep and it was tender
You never want to make her mad and send her on a ramble
Because if you get on her bad side
You will get an earful
And she knows the Good Book and she knows the right verses

I'm wasted again

Now I'm watchin' the tide come in
Alone on a beach
I'm wasted again
All by myself
I know I can't win
Just watchin' the tide come in

No matter what we went through now

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
Sunday night, church let out, parked by the water tower
Mason and Nick got them matching tattoos
All my friends in the parking lot of the Super Walmart
Tell me I should stay, I've got nothing to lose
But when the lights go out
I see city lights
I see spotlights, late nights, taxis
Fast cars, pretty boys, dressing fancy
My heart tells me this is where I belong
But I don't see city lights
When I close my eyes, I'm gone
Oh, I had to leave
In the morning, they were begging me to stay
I knew they'd be right
But there's no way to tell unless you try
And when the lights go out
I see city lights
I see spotlights, late nights, taxis
Fast cars, pretty boys, dressing fancy
My heart tells me this is where I belong
But I don't see city lights
When I close my eyes, I'm gone
But when I lie in bed at night
It's my mama's voice I hear in my mind
She says, "I see city lights
I see spotlights, late nights, taxis
Fast cars, pretty boys, dressing fancy
My heart tells me this is where I belong
But I don't see city lights
When I close my eyes, I'm gone"

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
There he is again, the old man feedin' pigeons in the park
Sittin' on the bench, a little rough around the edges, but he's got heart
He gets his breadcrumbs from the table at the diner
You can hear him from a mile away
Talkin' to the waitress, says, "You can't spell 'pigeon' without 'pie'"
Then he laughs, and he laughs 'til he cries
And she gives him a kiss on the cheek
And he gives her a wink, then he's on his way
Back to the park
He says, "Here, my darling, here, my darling, here"
Here, my darling, here, my darling, here
And then they appear
When he says, "Here, my darling, here, my darling, here"
Then they all draw near
When he says, "Here, my darling, here, my darling, here"
"Bonjour, mes amis," he calls, and they all come flockin'
"He was born in Paris," the waitress told me
I said, "Is he French?"
She said, "No, he's from Hoboken"
He was on a trip with his wife when they met Edith Piaf
At a cafe in Montmartre
And Piaf said, "Avez-vous un pain?"
And he said, "Yes," and they fed the pigeons together in the park
And he made his wife promise
When he got back to the States, he would do it every day
He says, "Here, my darling, here, my darling, here"
Here, my darling, here, my darling, here
And then they appear
When he says, "Here, my darling, here, my darling, here"
Then they all draw near
When he says, "Here, my darling, here, my darling, here"

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
Weighed down by stone
And rotted to the bone
Through this decayed hall
Where the ghosts still call
Her shadow
Dances on the wall
As she once did in the
Days of old
She walks the floors at night
And her eyes like knives
They pierce me
When the wind blows cold
And the rain came down
In torrents to the ground
And the lightning struck
In a bright white flash
As she flew out
The window
And into the sky
A streak across the night
Like the moon and stars
Were calling her home
Through the eyes of an angel
We see the world as it is
And we weep for the joy of the rain
For the warmth of the fire
And the pain of our pleasure
The birth and the loss of desire
And the sun will rise
In the morning
And I'll sit in the still
And the quiet
'Til the rain comes down
In torrents to the ground
And the lightning strikes
In a bright white flash
As she flies out
The window
And into the sky
A streak across the night
Like the moon and stars
Were calling her home
Were calling her home
Were calling her home

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
A young man steps from the shadows
Of a great tall tree
The full moon
It casteth down its glow
So he looks up to see
But when he looks at the moon
He's aware
He cannot bear to turn away
He feels his skin starting to tear
But it's too late
He can hear it
His bones snapping
He feels it
His body's shifting
And he hears
A wolf howling at the full moon
He lays there
The pain gone
And
He feels the cool night air
His muscles flex as he gets up
On all fours and takes in the forest
The forest looks so different now
Every sense is sharpened and focused
He feels his legs tense and start to bound
He's losing control

The old oak tree behind the house

Now it's dark the sky is brown

And i know

Know

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
With the first snow of the year
She wrote a letter to her mother
Said
"I've never been so sure
That I want to live here forever"
And with the second snow
She changed her mind
And her boyfriend left her
And she called her dad on the phone
And he said
"I told you so
I told you
I told you
I told you so"
And if you get in over your head
Well
You can come home
My friend
I think that I told you
I told you
I told you so
The wind blows in through the window
And her clothes lie on the floor
She puts her boots on
And she climbs out the door
And she wonders
"What for?
What for? What for?"
And when the first snow fell
She felt like hell
She found a church to pray in
And when the second snow fell
She said
"To hell with it"
And now she's on the first train
She left the note on the bed
That said
"I told you so
I told you
I told you
I told you so"
And if you get in over your head
Well
You can come home
My friend
I think that I told you
I told you
I told you so
She called her mom from the train station
And said
"I can't believe it's over"
And when the first snow fell
She felt like hell
But now she's starting over
She bought a one-way ticket on a train going west
And now she's on her way
She leaves a note on the bed that says
"To hell with it"
And now she's on the first train
And now she's on the first train
And now she's on the first train
She bought a one-way ticket on a train going west
And now she's on her way
And now she's on her way
And now she's on her way

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
You had glitter on your cheek
From the ferris wheel ride
I was standing by the fence
With a sunburned mind
You laughed like you knew me
Like we'd met before
And your paper wristband
Hit my hand and I wanted more
The sky went gold
Then it went low
Your name in my mouth
I said it slow
One tiny spark
Then the whole place glowed
Fallin' for you
Fallin' for you
Under the tents and the moon
Fallin' for you
Fallin' for you
Like I knew what to do
(Oh, I'm fallin' for you)
We shared a lemonade cup
By the food truck line
You stole my fries
Like that was fine
Your friends kept shouting
Your favorite song
You pulled me in close
And I sang along
The sky went gold
Then it went low
Your name in my mouth
I said it slow
One tiny spark
Then the whole place glowed
Fallin' for you
Fallin' for you
Under the tents and the moon
Fallin' for you
Fallin' for you
Like I knew what to do
(Oh, I'm fallin' for you)
When the final set hit
And the dust rolled up
You touched my sleeve
Said "don't give up"
Now I'm home with the echo
Of your paper wristband kiss
Still chasing that moment
Like I'm made for this

Fallin' for you

Fallin' for you

(Oh, I'm fallin' for you)

You stole my fries

Your friends kept shouting

You pulled me in close
And I sang along

I said it slow

Fallin' for you
Fallin' for you

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
Your old room is painted blue
But it still looks like you
I pass the bus stop by the park
And hear your laugh in the dark
The kettle clicks, the floorboards creak
Every small thing starts to speak
I leave the door unlatched for you
Like you might come back through
I keep your number in my phone
Even when I'm not alone
There's a space in every plan
Where your hand used to land
Come back, come back
I miss your name in my day
Come back, come back
You moved, but you stayed
In my chest, in my steps
In the words I never say
Come back, come back
I miss your name in my day
Your last note's stuck in a drawer
Folded small by the door
It says "I'll call when I get there"
Like distance never cared
Now the weeks are paper-thin
I can read right through them
Every map line in my mind
Still leads to your side
I know you found a life out there
New streets, new faces, new air
But mine keeps circling back
To the shape you left in that
Come back, come back
I miss your name in my day
Come back, come back
You moved, but you stayed
In my chest, in my steps
In the words I never say
Come back, come back
I miss your name in my day
Maybe this is how it goes
Two homes and one heart knows
How to ache across the miles
And still smile for a while
If you ever hear this song
Know I've been holding on
To the space you made for me
Like a key
Come back, come back
I miss your name in my day
Come back, come back
You moved, but you stayed
In my chest, in my steps
In the words I never say
Come back, come back
I miss your name in my day

songs.subject: (suno simple)
songs.lyrics:
Dust devils dancin' wild
Heat shimmer on the blacktop smile
Sky turns bruised and mean
Another storm a comin' scene
Feel it in my bones
Rattlin' these tired old stones
Thunderhead comin'
Yeah thunderhead
Hear that rumble hummin'
Ain't no turnin' back instead
Thunderhead comin'
Gonna chase it down
Across this flatland town
Wind whips up the grit
Tastes like anger every bit
Lightning cracks the dark
Leavin' its jagged scar mark
Engine groans and cries
Mirrors reflect the angry skies
Thunderhead comin'
Yeah thunderhead
Hear that rumble hummin'
Ain't no turnin' back instead
Thunderhead comin'
Gonna chase it down
Across this flatland town

Thunderhead comin'
Yeah thunderhead
Hear that rumble hummin'
Ain't no turnin' back instead

Across this flatland town

songs.subject: county line crush
songs.lyrics:
I saw you by the feed store
In your daddy’s old truck
Mud on your boots
Hair tied up just right
You laughed at my old nickname
Like you knew it all along
Same county, same roads
I’ve been gone too long

Now every red dirt mile
Leads me back to you
Got my heart in a circle
What am I supposed to do?

Falling for the girl
From my hometown
(Falling for the girl)
I can’t slow down
Same church bell
Same gravel ground
You’re the one
I keep coming back around

At the Friday night ballgame
You were leaning on the rail
Eating popcorn from a paper cup
Cheering for that last home run sail
And I tried to play it cool
Like you didn’t get me spun
But your smile hit me hard
I was already done

Now every red dirt mile
Leads me back to you
Got my heart in a circle
What am I supposed to do?

Falling for the girl
From my hometown
(Falling for the girl)
I can’t slow down
Same church bell
Same gravel ground
You’re the one
I keep coming back around

Maybe it’s the way
You say my name
Like you never left
And nothing changed
Maybe it’s the truth
I’ve been chasing all my life
Love don’t have to be far away
To feel this right

Falling for the girl
From my hometown
(Falling for the girl)
I can’t slow down
Same church bell
Same gravel ground
You’re the one
I keep coming back around
(Falling for the girl)
Yeah, I’m falling now
From my hometown
You’re the one I found

songs.subject: county line crush b
songs.lyrics:
Saw you at the feed store
In that faded ball cap
You were laughing with your brother
By the stacked-up sacks
I knew your mama’s name
Before I knew your smile
Funny how one small hello
Can stay a little while

Now I’m driving slow
Past your old red gate
Acting like I got nowhere
When I’m there on purpose, babe

I’m fallin’ for a girl
From my hometown
(From my hometown)
Same dirt roads
Same small-town sound
You got me talkin’ soft
You got me comin’ round
I’m fallin’ for a girl
From my hometown

Friday at the county fair
You leaned on the fence
Cotton candy on your fingers
And that half-grin sense
Every light on the midway
Looked a little less bright
When you said you might stay
And wait on me tonight

Now I’m driving slow
Past your old red gate
Acting like I got nowhere
When I’m there on purpose, babe

I’m fallin’ for a girl
From my hometown
(From my hometown)
Same dirt roads
Same small-town sound
You got me talkin’ soft
You got me comin’ round
I’m fallin’ for a girl
From my hometown

Maybe it’s the church bells
Maybe it’s your laugh
Maybe it’s every back road
That keeps pullin’ me back
If you say go steady
I’ll say I’m in
With your hand in my hand
I’d do this all again

I’m fallin’ for a girl
From my hometown
(From my hometown)
Same dirt roads
Same small-town sound
You got me talkin’ soft
You got me comin’ round
I’m fallin’ for a girl
From my hometown

songs.subject: tide in your pocket
songs.lyrics:
You left your scarf
On my chair
Still smells like rain
And your hair
I keep the key
In my coat
Like it might unlock
What we both wrote
Your name on glass
In the steam
I wipe it clean
Then lose the dream

I try to laugh it off
But the room knows
Every step you made
Still echoes

You were the one that got away
The one I call when nights turn gray
You were the one that got away
I hold your shadow, it slips away
The one that got away
The one that got away
I keep your ring
But not the day

Saw your blue car
By the store
My heart kicked hard
Like before
I turned my head
Played it cool
Acted like love
Don’t make a fool
But every song
Tells on me
Brings back your laugh
And that bare tree

I try to move ahead
But my feet know
Where your goodbye
Still won’t go

You were the one that got away
The one I call when nights turn gray
You were the one that got away
I hold your shadow, it slips away
The one that got away
The one that got away
I keep your ring
But not the day

If you ever look back
And feel that pull
I’m still right here
With an empty full
Of all we had
And all we said
A little bit lost
A little bit blessed

You were the one that got away
The one I call when nights turn gray
You were the one that got away
And I still feel it anyway
The one that got away
The one that got away
If love comes back
I’ll leave the door unchained

songs.subject: glass jar summer
songs.lyrics:
You left your name on my door
Just a ring in the wood
I kept it there for months
Like I thought I should
Your dented coffee mug
Still lives by the sink
I turn it face-down now
But I still make it think

And every time the rain
Hits that front step hard
I hear your shoes again
Crossing my yard
I reach for air
Like you might turn around
But the house stays still
And the quiet settles down

You were the one that got away
The one that got away
I say your name in the dark
And it still won't stay
You were the one that got away
The one that got away
Yeah, I held you close
Then I lost my way

I drove by your old place
Saw the screen door swing
A kid on the porch
With a red kite string
I laughed out loud
Then I had to pull over
'Cause your memory hits
Like a slow damn thunder

And every time I hear
That front gate creak
I swear it's you
Calling for me
I move too late
Like I always do
By the time I reach
It's already through

You were the one that got away
The one that got away
I say your name in the dark
And it still won't stay
You were the one that got away
The one that got away
Yeah, I held you close
Then I lost my way

Maybe love ain't lost
It just changes shape
Maybe some goodbyes
Leave a sweeter ache
If you ever pass
Through this tired town
You'll know my heart
Never really cooled down

You were the one that got away
The one that got away
I say your name in the dark
And it still won't stay
You were the one that got away
The one that got away
Yeah, I held you close
Then I lost my way
You were the one that got away
The one that got away
And I still feel you
In the empty frame

songs.subject: bus out at dawn
songs.lyrics:
Pack my shirt in a paper bag
One cracked key and a photo tag
Tires hum on the county road
Same four blocks, same heavy load
Every face says stay and bend
I got one more place to spend

songs.subject: packing the u haul
songs.lyrics:
I know this road by heart
Same cracked curb, same dead-end lot
The diner clock keeps lying
Says I’m late for what I’ve got
My name fits on a mailbox
My boots fit in this mud
But every fence I ever climbed
Still kept my chest shut

So I’m gone
Before the sun comes up
Gone
Before I change my mind

I’m leaving my small town for good
(For good, for good)
I’m leaving my small town for good
I’m done with the "maybe someday" look
(For good, for good)
I’m leaving my small town for good

The same faces at the gas pump
The same jokes, the same old smoke
They say, "Boy, don’t get too proud now"
Like the gate ain’t made to choke
I got a map under my pillow
I got a band shirt and a bruise
I got a hunger in my backseat
And nothing left to lose

So I’m gone
Before the screen door creaks
Gone
Before they call my name

I’m leaving my small town for good
(For good, for good)
I’m leaving my small town for good
I won’t ask them for a reason
I won’t wait to be understood
(I’m gone, I’m gone)
I’m leaving my small town for good

You can keep the church bells
Keep the gossip and the dust
Keep the faded football banners
And the rusted swing set rust
I’ll take the ache with me
I’ll take the scars and the speed
If I gotta be a ghost somewhere
At least it’ll be me

I’m leaving my small town for good
(For good, for good)
I’m leaving my small town for good
I won’t beg the sky to hold me
I won’t trade my fire for wood
(For good, for good)
I’m leaving my small town for good

songs.subject: chair by the window
songs.lyrics:
He keeps his boots by the door
Even in July
Reads the same page twice
Then laughs at his own eyes
His hands on the mug
Like they know the shape
Of every hard year
He never learned to shake

Now his laugh comes slower
But it still lights the room
I watch the seasons gather
In the lines around him too

I’m watching my father grow old
(Watching my father grow old)
Like a fence in the snow
Still standing, still cold
And I love every crack
Every story he told
I’m watching my father grow old
(Watching my father grow old)

He calls me by my brother’s name
Then smiles when I wince
Says, “You’ll get used to it”
With a wink that makes sense
He leaves the porch light on
For no one in sight
Then sits there awhile
As if guarding the night

Now the radio softens
And his shoulders bend low
I’m learning his silence
The way rivers learn stone

I’m watching my father grow old
(Watching my father grow old)
Like a fence in the snow
Still standing, still cold
And I love every crack
Every story he told
I’m watching my father grow old
(Watching my father grow old)

If I could borrow time
I’d give him back his spring
But all I’ve got is this moment
And the song that it brings
So I hold the old chair
When he rises slow
And I thank every day
For the man I know

I’m watching my father grow old
(Watching my father grow old)
Like a fence in the snow
Still standing, still cold
And I love every crack
Every story he told
I’m watching my father grow old
(Watching my father grow old)

songs.subject: porch light hands
songs.lyrics:
He ties his boots by the chair
Slow like the rain at dawn
The same blue shirt, frayed at the cuff
The years have worn him down
His hands still know that old porch rail
How to grip, how to rest
I watch him pause at the screen door
And catch his breath

He says, “I’m fine”
With a grin that fades
I hear the truth
In the shape it makes

Porch light hands
Porch light hands
He’s still my dad
In porch light hands
I hold on close
You hold on close
Before the night
Takes what it knows

The garden’s gone to seed again
He still checks every row
Talks to the beans like they can hear
And laughs when they won’t grow
He forgets my cousin’s middle name
Then nails the date we moved
A mind like pockets full of stones
Some polished smooth

He says, “I’m fine”
And changes the tune
But his knees complain
When the day turns blue

Porch light hands
Porch light hands
He’s still my dad
In porch light hands
I hold on close
You hold on close
Before the night
Takes what it knows

I used to think he stood like oak
Unshaken in the wind
Now I see the bend in him
And something sacred in it
So I bring the plates in from the sink
He lets me take his coat
We move through evening, side by side
A little slower than before

Porch light hands
Porch light hands
He’s still my dad
In porch light hands
I hold on close
You hold on close
Before the night
Takes what it knows
Porch light hands
Porch light hands
I’m learning love
Is in these hands

songs.subject: almost made it v2
songs.lyrics:
Your coat still hung on my chair
Your glass left a ring over there
The whole room held its breath
When you looked at me like that
We talked in circles, baby
Two hearts at the edge
Your hand kept finding mine
Then slipping back again

Say what you mean
Don't let it fade
We were one step in
One step away

The night we almost made it
Almost made it, baby
The night we almost made it
And I still taste the maybe
You said my name
Like you might stay
The night we almost made it
Almost made it, baby

Your phone lit up by the sink
But you turned it face down quick
I saw the look in your eyes
Like you knew what this could be
We laughed too loud at nothing
Then fell quiet fast
Every little touch
Felt like it could last

Say what you mean
Don't let it fade
We were one step in
One step away

The night we almost made it
Almost made it, baby
The night we almost made it
And I still taste the maybe
You said my name
Like you might stay
The night we almost made it
Almost made it, baby

Now that room is just a memory
But it plays on me
Every unfinished sentence
Every almost, every tease
If you want that door again
I might let it swing
'Cause my heart still remembers
What your hands could bring

The night we almost made it
Almost made it, baby
The night we almost made it
And I still taste the maybe
You said my name
Like you might stay
The night we almost made it
Almost made it, baby

songs.subject: rent due blues
songs.lyrics:
Clock in when the moon is out
Name tag hanging by a thread
Frying oil and floor wax
Cheap coffee going dead
My back says quit, my pockets say
Boy, you better go on in
Got a notice taped to the door
And the month just climbed back in

I count tips in the cold light
Count holes in my shoes
Count the hours till morning
Counting every bruise

Rent due, rent due
I keep on moving
Rent due, rent due
Ain't no losing
(keep on moving)
Rent due, rent due
Baby, I got to
Work this night shift
Just to make it through

Bus pulls up with tired faces
Eyes red like a warning sign
Rubber mat and busted heels
Feeding that assembly line
Landlord smiles in the hallway
Like he knows my hands are rough
I bring home a paper paycheck
And it still ain't near enough

I count tips in the cold light
Count holes in my shoes
Count the hours till morning
Counting every bruise

Rent due, rent due
I keep on moving
Rent due, rent due
Ain't no losing
(keep on moving)
Rent due, rent due
Baby, I got to
Work this night shift
Just to make it through

If I had a easy morning
I'd lay it all down clean
But the bills come knocking loud
And the floor keeps eating dreams
Still I hold my head up
When the drain man starts his tune
One more dawn, one more payday
One more month to get me through

Rent due, rent due
I keep on moving
Rent due, rent due
Ain't no losing
(keep on moving)
Rent due, rent due
Baby, I got to
Work this night shift
Just to make it through
Rent due, rent due
I keep on moving
Rent due, rent due
Ain't no losing
(keep on moving)

songs.subject: seen at midnight
songs.lyrics:
Your name lit up my screen
Like a match in the dark
Three little bubbles
Then a knife in my heart
You said “we need space”
In your parked-out car
I was holding my breath
In the laundry room

I read it twice
Then I read it again
You left me on blue
Like I’m just a thread
My thumb shook once
On the glass of my phone
I was still your baby
Then I wasn’t at all

Over text, over text
You let me go
Over text, over text
So cold, so slow
I kept your name
In my chest like a song
Now it’s over text
And I’m moving on

Your hoodie on my chair
Still smells like rain
There’s a dent in the couch
Where we used to stay
I caught myself waiting
For “you there?” at night
But the screen stayed quiet
And the room stayed white

I thought you’d call
Or show up at the door
But you chose those words
And they landed hard
One little buzz
And the whole thing split
Love can end so fast
When it fits in a box

Over text, over text
You let me go
Over text, over text
So cold, so slow
I kept your name
In my chest like a song
Now it’s over text
And I’m moving on

Maybe you were scared
Maybe I was too
Maybe some goodbyes
Only know one view
Still, that tiny screen
Couldn’t hold the truth
It made your goodbye
Look smaller than you

Over text, over text
You let me go
Over text, over text
So cold, so slow
I kept your name
In my chest like a song
Now it’s over text
And I’m moving on
Over text, over text
I let it go
Over text, over text
I’ll let it go

songs.subject: read receipts
songs.lyrics:
Your name lit up my screen
At four seventeen
Just three words, then gone
Like you weren't where I'd been
I stared at the dots
They never turned to more
My thumb on the glass
Like it knew the score

You left me on "seen"
That hurt like a scene
I wanted your voice
Not a tiny blue screen

You broke my heart by text
You broke my heart by text
No last look, no last kiss
Just a cold little flex
You broke my heart by text
You broke my heart by text
And I still read it twice
Like it might say less

Your hoodie on my chair
Your mug in the sink
I kept the little things
Like I could make you think
But your words were so neat
So clean, so done
I was crying in pieces
You were on the run

You left me on "seen"
That hurt like a scene
I wanted your voice
Not a tiny blue screen

You broke my heart by text
You broke my heart by text
No last look, no last kiss
Just a cold little flex
You broke my heart by text
You broke my heart by text
And I still read it twice
Like it might say less

I checked the thread again
At five in the rain
The same two gray lines
The same old pain
If love was a door
You slammed the lock
Now I hear your goodbye
In every buzz and pop

You broke my heart by text
You broke my heart by text
No last look, no last kiss
Just a cold little flex
You broke my heart by text
You broke my heart by text
And I still read it twice
Like it might say less

songs.subject: dust on my boots
songs.lyrics:
I rolled in with the sun
On a bus that smelled like rain
Mama stood on the porch
Trying hard to hide the strain
My boots still had red clay
From a road I can't explain
And your hands, they shook a little
When you said my name

I left a boy in a helmet
Came back with heavier eyes
The fields look the same
But nothing in me feels alike
You touched my face like a prayer
Like you were checking I'm real
I stood there at the gate
Not knowing how to heal

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
With dust on my boots
And a hole in my soul
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
If you hold me tonight
Maybe I'll be whole

The screen door kept its squeak
Same old cut in the wood
Dad put a plate in my hand
Like he knew I might not could
The dog ran circles round me
Then laid down at my feet
Like he'd been waiting years
For the shape of me

I tried to laugh at the table
But my throat stayed tied in knots
You asked about the old truck
I forgot where I parked my thoughts
The backyard lights were swinging
Over beans on the vine
I kept looking at the dark
Like it might call my name tonight

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
With dust on my boots
And a hole in my soul
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
If you hold me tonight
Maybe I'll be whole

There are doors I still won't open
There are nights I won't explain
But your hand in mine is steady
And the storm don't stay the same
No, the storm don't stay the same

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
With dust on my boots
And a hole in my soul
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
If you hold me tonight
Maybe I'll be whole
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
And I won't let go
If you hold me tonight
Maybe I'll be whole

I tried to laugh at the table
But my throat stayed tied in knots
You asked about the old truck
I forgot where I parked my thoughts
The backyard lights were swinging
Over beans on the vine
I kept looking at the dark
Like it might call my name tonight

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
With dust on my boots
And a hole in my soul
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
If you hold me tonight
Maybe I'll be whole

There are doors I still won't open
There are nights I won't explain
But your hand in mine is steady
And the storm don't stay the same
No, the storm don't stay the same

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
With dust on my boots
And a hole in my soul
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
If you hold me tonight
Maybe I'll be whole
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
And I won't let go
If you hold me tonight
Maybe I'll be whole

songs.subject: dust on boots
songs.lyrics:
Same bus, different man
Red clay on my cuffs
Mama left the porch light on
Like she knew it was enough
Duffle bag on the floor
Helmet by the door
I don't know how to be
The boy I was before

The screen door creaks
The whole house breathes
Your eyes say stay
Mine say please

I’m coming home from the war
(coming home)
I’m coming home from the war
My hands still shake at the door
But I’m here, I’m here
I’m coming home from the war

There's a scar by my wrist
From a wire I won't name
And a letter in my coat
With a folded-up name
You set out a glass
Like I still drink the same
But I spill half the water
Just hearing my name

The table's set
The kids run fast
I laugh too hard
Then it passes

I’m coming home from the war
(coming home)
I’m coming home from the war
My hands still shake at the door
But I’m here, I’m here
I’m coming home from the war

If I go quiet
Don't take it wrong
I'm learning this night
One breath at a time
You can leave the lamp on
I can take the weight
I brought back the thunder
I’m trying to make rain

I’m coming home from the war
(coming home)
I’m coming home from the war
My hands still shake at the door
But I’m here, I’m here
I’m coming home from the war

songs.subject: white shoes spin
songs.lyrics:
You took my hand by the cake
Said, “I’m here for one song”
Your smile had that slow burn
Like you knew all along
My shoes on the checkered floor
Your laugh in my ear
The band kept calling out
And we just disappeared

One more turn
Then another
Your dress caught the light
My heartbeat got louder
Table by table
We drifted away
I forgot your name
Then I heard you say

Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me, stranger
Hold this moment close tonight
Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me, stranger
Till the whole room feels alright
(oh-oh) hold my hand
(oh-oh) spin me faster

Champagne on your wrist
Napkin folded in half
Your cousin yelled our names
We both started to laugh
We missed the bouquet toss
We missed the last toast
But under those silver balls
You felt like home the most

One more turn
Then another
Your dress caught the light
My heartbeat got louder
Everybody faded
You pulled me near
I forgot the crowd
When I heard you cheer

Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me, stranger
Hold this moment close tonight
Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me, stranger
Till the whole room feels alright
(oh-oh) hold my hand
(oh-oh) spin me faster

If your ride waits outside
Tell it to wait a while
If this is just one song
Let it steal one more mile
Your eyes said maybe
Mine said stay
So we turned and turned
Till the night gave way

Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me, stranger
Hold this moment close tonight
Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me, stranger
Till the whole room feels alright
(oh-oh) hold my hand
(oh-oh) spin me faster

songs.subject: borrowed ring
songs.lyrics:
Your hand was warm
mine was nervous
under the blue cake lights
you said, "just one song"
and I said, "why not"
while the bride spun past us

Your laugh hit hard
like a snapped champagne cork
The room went soft
and the floor got bold
You took one step
I took the other
Now we were moving
like we knew each other

Borrowed ring, borrowed night
Spin me round, hold me tight
Just for now, just for now
We can make this moment loud
Borrowed ring, borrowed night
You and I, out of sight
Just for now, just for now
Don’t let go, don’t ask how (don’t ask how)

There was frosting on your sleeve
and a crease in your collar
You said you came alone
I said I did too
The band played slow
then kicked into silver
And your eyes said things
your mouth never knew

Your laugh hit hard
like a snapped champagne cork
The room went soft
and the floor got bold
You took one step
I took the other
Now we were moving
like we knew each other

Borrowed ring, borrowed night
Spin me round, hold me tight
Just for now, just for now
We can make this moment loud
Borrowed ring, borrowed night
You and I, out of sight
Just for now, just for now
Don’t let go, don’t ask how (don’t ask how)

When the lights came up
we both froze for a second
You smiled like you knew
the rules were breaking
A name on a table
A toast in the distance
But your hand stayed near
like a dare in the air

Borrowed ring, borrowed night
Spin me round, hold me tight
Just for now, just for now
We can make this moment loud
Borrowed ring, borrowed night
You and I, out of sight
Just for now, just for now
Don’t let go, don’t ask how (don’t ask how)
Borrowed ring, borrowed night
I was yours by the end of it
Just for now, just for now
Till the last slow song gave out (gave out)

songs.subject: sinners sweet song
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise painted skies I saw
Felt nothing new just empty awe
My soul a dusty broken thing
No hallelujah I could sing

Whispers soft and low
Where did my spirit go

Oh sinner man sinner woman
Lost your way lost your reason
But the light it waits for you
Shining through shining true

Shadows danced a wicked jig
My hope it felt so small so big
The world a blur a spinning wheel
Could not tell what was fake or real

Then a melody a gentle hum
Said child come home child overcome
A quiet fire deep inside
No longer nowhere left to hide

Oh sinner man sinner woman
Lost your way lost your reason
But the light

Then a melody a gentle hum

A quiet fire deep inside
No longer nowhere left to hide

Oh sinner man sinner woman
Lost your way lost your reason
But the light

songs.subject: gospel train
songs.lyrics:
Dusty road I walked so long
My spirit tired my song unsung
Thought I lost my way for good
Misunderstood

Shadows long and cold and deep
Secrets that my heart did keep

Gospel train comin' round the bend
Gonna take me home again my friend
Gospel train hear that whistle blow
Where my weary soul can go

Sunrise painted gold and red
A whisper in my sleepy head
A tiny spark a gentle flame
Calling out my name

Broken pieces scattered wide
Nowhere left for me to hide
Then a light

songs.subject: summer turned
songs.lyrics:
June dust on the floorboards
Your bike by the screen door
Cherry pops on your tongue
And your jeans were torn at the knee
We drove past the ball field
Windows down, radio high
You said, "Don't blink too hard"
Like you knew what was coming for me

And the sky turned gold
Over Miller Road
You laughed too loud
Then you let me know

That was the summer
That was the summer
Everything changed
That was the summer
That was the summer
We never stayed the same
(We never stayed the same)

Your brother sold fireworks
From a rusty red wagon
We counted our cash
For a soda and a little escape
Then your mama yelled your name
From the porch in the heat
And you looked back once
Like you were leaving me in the shade

And the sky turned gold
Over Miller Road
You laughed too loud
Then you let me know

That was the summer
That was the summer
Everything changed
That was the summer
That was the summer
We never stayed the same
(We never stayed the same)

I still see the ash
On your fingertips
I still hear your boots
On the warped front steps
One hot breath
And the whole world slipped
Yeah, one hot breath
And it broke wide open

That was the summer
That was the summer
Everything changed
That was the summer
That was the summer
We never stayed the same
(We never stayed the same)

June dust on the floorboards
Your bike by the screen door
That was the summer
That was the summer

songs.subject: county line july
songs.lyrics:
The screen door slammed in June
Your name on a folded note
Mud on my thrift-store boots
And a bruise on my throat
Dad said, "Boy, don't drift too far"
I laughed and took the keys
That old Ford shook on gravel
Like it knew what I'd see

By the time the wheat went gold
I wasn't who I was before
You looked at me like fire
And I wanted more

That summer everything changed
(Everything changed)
That summer everything changed
We burned right through the pain
I was a kid with a cracked-out heart
You were the spark in my veins
That summer everything changed
(Everything changed)

County fair, cheap lemonade
Your hand slipped in mine
Ferris wheel lights on your face
Made the whole town shine
We stole two songs from the jukebox
And danced on the worn-out floor
When the thunder hit the tin roof
You pulled me close and swore

By the time the wheat went gold
I wasn't who I was before
You looked at me like fire
And I wanted more

That summer everything changed
(Everything changed)
That summer everything changed
We burned right through the pain
I was a kid with a cracked-out heart
You were the spark in my veins
That summer everything changed
(Everything changed)

Now the fields are dry and brown
And your truck ain't parked out back
But I still hear that summer
Rattling in the tracks
One good kiss, one hard goodbye
One road I couldn't stay
I wear that season like a scar
That still won't fade away

That summer everything changed
(Everything changed)
That summer everything changed
We burned right through the pain
I was a kid with a cracked-out heart
You were the spark in my veins
That summer everything changed
(Everything changed)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Key in the cut, I’m already half gone
Map in my lap, one lap and I’m done
Back seat blacked out, bag full of pay
No face in the mirror, just me and the chase
I learned the hard way, move clean, move cold
Count that breath, don’t slip, don’t fold
Two left turns, then a right by the yard
Clock got teeth, but I’m built for the dark
Names in my phone, I delete as I drive
Old life trailing like smoke in the drive
One more run, then I vanish for good
Leave my shadow in the dust and the wood

Hear that siren bend?
I don’t look back then
Hands on the wheel, calm
Heart in the red zone

Last run, last run
Then I’m gone, then I’m done
Last run, last run
One clean cut, no one
Last run, last run
Hear the engine hum
Last run, last run
Then I’m out, I’m done

Rain on the glass, but the route stay clear
Back roads carved from a whole lotta fear
Cut through the warehouse, cold chain-link gate
One minute late and the whole thing break
I seen friends turn strange for a pocket of green
Seen good men fold in a chrome-lit scene
So I keep it tight, every move got a twin
One eye on the rear, one eye on the win
Gate swings wide, I’m a ghost in the lane
No talk, no trace, just the weight, just the strain
Ain’t for the thrill, ain’t for the fame
It’s the last bad habit I still name

Hear that siren bend?
I don’t look back then
Hands on the wheel, calm
Heart in the red zone

Last run, last run
Then I’m gone, then I’m done
Last run, last run
One clean cut, no one
Last run, last run
Hear the engine hum
Last run, last run
Then I’m out, I’m done

If the night want a soul, let it take the old me
If the road want a toll, let it break the code, see
I been living too quick, in a split-second fix
Now I want empty hands and a life I can miss

Slow it down

Last run, last run
Then I’m gone, then I’m done
Last run, last run
One clean cut, no one
Last run, last run
Hear the engine hum
Last run, last run
Then I’m out, I’m done

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
One last run, same gunmetal dawn
Seat back low, but the weight stayed on
Map in the dash, cash in the bag
White knuckle grip on a life gone bad
He said, "Just drive, keep your eyes on the lane"
But I seen the fear in the flick of his chain
Foot on the gas, heart in a vice
One more mile, then I pay the price
Rearview full of red and blue
No time for a prayer, just a split-second move
I learned that road like a scar on my skin
Every exit I missed, I was missing again

If I make it through
I’m done with the fumes
Done with the code
Done with the doom
Hands on the wheel
Mind on the exit
One bad choice
And the whole thing’s wrecked, kid

One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
Take the money
Leave the name
Last job, last night
New life, same frame

Tunnel light flicker, my pulse in the floor
Tires talk back when I cut that curve
He kept saying, "Move," like the word was a blade
I kept it cool while the whole scene swayed
Sirens got close, I laughed at the fear
Cut through a gap like I wasn’t here
One hand steady, one hand shook
Everything changed in a three-second look
Pass the old docks, past the locked-up blocks
Past the men with the knives and the watches that talk
I ain’t built for the weight of a double-cross game
I’m a ghost with a steering wheel, tired of the fame

If I make it through
I’m done with the fumes
Done with the code
Done with the doom
Hands on the wheel
Mind on the exit
One bad choice
And the whole thing’s wrecked, kid

One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
Take the money
Leave the name
Last job, last night
New life, same frame

At the line I stopped
Let the engine talk
Threw the keys in the dark
And I walked, I walked
No more calls
No more chases
No more friends with hollow faces
I was done when the tires quit screaming

One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
Take the money
Leave the name
Last job, last night
New life, same frame

Threw the keys in the dark
And I walked, I walked
No more calls
No more chases
No more friends with hollow faces
I was done when the tires quit screaming

One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
One last run
Then I’m gone, I’m gone
Take the money
Leave the name
Last job, last night
New life, same frame

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
There’s one tree left
By the bus depot curb
Roots in a crack
Where the rain used to work
It leans like a prayer
Over oil and dust
Kids etched their names
Then the paint wore off

And the town kept growing
Around its slow green bone
They poured the world flat
But it still held on

Hold on, old tree
Hold on for me
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt
Hold on, old tree
You still can breathe
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt

A sparrow lands there
Like it knows its place
On a branch so thin
It could shake with grace
Old men sit nearby
On a bench gone gray
They talk to the bark
Like it might say stay

And the town kept growing
Around its slow green bone
They poured the world flat
But it still held on

Hold on, old tree
Hold on for me
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt
Hold on, old tree
You still can breathe
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt

If they cut it down
What else can know
The shape of rain
The weight of snow?
If they cut it down
Then who will keep
One wild thing
In the sleep of streets?

Hold on, old tree
Hold on for me
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt
Hold on, old tree
You still can breathe
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt
Pine in the asphalt

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
At the end of Miller Lane
there’s one green crown left
between the cracked-up curbs
and the shuttered hardware set
Its roots hold tighter
than the old brick ground
while the rest of town
keeps sinking down

Kids name it lucky
old men call it fate
I pass it every evening
through the chain-link gate
One small shade
on a hard, hard block
One small breath
where the pavement stops

Last tree standing
hold on, hold on
Last tree standing
stay for us long
When the wind comes round
and the seasons lean
you keep one piece
of what we used to be

The barber shop is a dented shell
the church bell rusted still
paint flakes off the bakery sign
like snow on a windowsill
But your leaves keep talking
in the heat and dust
like something in this town
still remembers us

Mothers rest there
with their grocery bags
Teenage boys carve names
in the bent tin tags
One small shade
on a hard, hard block
One small breath
where the pavement stops

Last tree standing
hold on, hold on
Last tree standing
stay for us long
When the wind comes round
and the seasons lean
you keep one piece
of what we used to be

I heard the planner said
this place was done
all straight roads, no roots
and a cheaper sun
But I knelt in the dirt
by your split-bark side
and I swear I felt
the town still alive

Last tree standing
hold on, hold on
Last tree standing
stay for us

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You came in at eleven
Badge bent, hair in a tie
I was charting room six
Trying not to catch your eye
You said, “Need that blood pressure”
Like you knew my name already
Then you laughed at the tired look
I wore like a heavy steady

And the whole floor felt smaller
When you leaned in close
Took my pen, wrote your number
On the back of my notes
Now my hands keep shaking
Every time you pass
Like my heart took report
And it failed the class

Scrubs and static
You and me, automatic
Scrubs and static
Baby, we just drift like magic
On this long night
You make the hard parts feel right
Scrubs and static
Scrubs and static

Coffee in a paper cup
Cold by the med room sink
You saved me a seat at two
When I couldn’t even think
We shared one tiny blanket
On the break-room chair
Your shoulder on my shoulder
Like you’d always been there

And the whole floor felt smaller
When you leaned in close
Took my pen, wrote your number
On the back of my notes
Now my hands keep shaking
Every time you pass
Like my heart took report
And it failed the class

Scrubs and static
You and me, automatic
Scrubs and static
Baby, we just drift like magic
On this long night
You make the hard parts feel right
Scrubs and static
Scrubs and static

Sun coming up at the window
We still got our glow
You tuck my badge in my pocket
Like you already know
If this floor keeps turning
And the world runs cold
Meet me by the supply room
We can let it unfold

Scrubs and static
You and me, automatic
Scrubs and static
Baby, we just drift like magic
On this long night
You make the hard parts feel right
Scrubs and static
Scrubs and static

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You signed in at eleven
I caught your name on the board
Blue pens in the pockets
A coffee ring on the floor
You said, “Room twelve’s awake”
Like you’d been here before
I said, “I know the feeling”
When the hallway starts to roar

You passed me gauze
I passed you time
Your hand on mine
Just for a line
The monitors glow
We stay in tune
Somewhere between
The call bell and moon

Stay on my shift
Stay on my shift
(Stay on my shift)
Pull me close
In the blue-white drift
We fall in love
By the charting light
Stay on my shift
Stay on my shift
(Stay on my shift)

At two-fifteen, you laughed
When the printer jammed again
Your badge tapped my shoulder
As you reached across the bed
We traded backstories
Over packaged crackers split
You said your hands get steady
When the whole floor starts to tip

You passed me gauze
I passed you time
Your hand on mine
Just for a line
The monitors glow
We stay in tune
Somewhere between
The call bell and moon

Stay on my shift
Stay on my shift
(Stay on my shift)
Pull me close
In the blue-white drift
We fall in love
By the charting light
Stay on my shift
Stay on my shift
(Stay on my shift)

When dawn starts leaking in
And the floors go still
You lean on the desk
I feel that quiet thrill
No grand confession
Just your name on my lips
And the warm little spark
When your fingers brush my wrist
[Final Cho

First cypher,

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I was ten toes, thin clothes, pinched chin, skin froze
In a basement with a milk crate and a fifth-floor echo
Circle of kids, sick with the same little dream glow
Mics passed hand to hand, nerves jagged, teeth show
My first breath shook, then the next one got bold
I was tryna lace words like I was threading up gold
Somebody yelled, “Go,” and the room got small
I heard my own name bounce back off the cinderblock wall
My palms got slick, but the truth got loud
I wasn’t built for the stage, I was built for the crowd
I dropped two clean lines, then I caught that swing
And the whole room leaned like, “yeah, that’s the thing”

No script, no shield
Just a ring of steel
One wrong move
And the real got revealed
I felt that pulse
In my teeth, in my shoes
Like the whole damn room
Had one thing to prove

First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)
First cypher, still higher
(Still higher)
I was broke, but I spoke
And the doubt got tired
First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)

Now I look back like, damn, what a crooked little start
All that hunger in the chest, all that spark in the dark
We were scrapped-up kings with a battery grin
Trading battle scars for a chance to get in
One kid bit a bar, one kid flipped the scheme
One kid chased a punchline straight through the steam
I was counting my breaths, he was counting my flaws
Trying to catch my own face in the crowd’s applause
And the beat kept rolling, rough around the seam
Like a train in a tunnel with a body full of steam
I learned quick, you don’t win by acting tough
You win when you swing with the bruise and enough
So I wore that night like a ring on my hand
A small-town badge in a giant-land plan

No crown, no throne
Just a borrowed microphone
One room, one round
And I found my tone
I felt that pulse
In the brick, in the floor
Like I’d been waiting for this
Since way before

First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)
First cypher, still higher
(Still higher)
I was broke, but I spoke
And the doubt got tired
First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)

Years ran fast, but that scene stayed sharp
That room, that ring, that nervous heart
I still chase that kid when the lights go low
When the world gets slick, I go back to the row
One mic, one chance, one breath, one shot
Same old grit in the knot I got
If I lose the thread, I still know the code
Start where I started, let the whole thing explode

First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)
First cypher, still higher
(Still higher)
I was broke, but I spoke
And the doubt got tired
First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)
First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)
Still that kid in the circle
Still that same old wire
First cypher, first fire
(First cypher)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I was built in fifty-two
Red brick, front steps, screen door blue
First hands hung a coat by the hall
First child drew a fox on the wall

They packed the plates in July
Left a teacup by the sink to dry
I held the dust in my beams
And learned what leaving means

Four keys, one door
I remember more
Four keys, one door
I remember more
(remember more)

Then came a man with a radio
Boots by the mat, cheap aftershave
He fixed the leak with silver tape
Said, “This place still got good bones”
At night he sang to the sink
And smoked on the porch in the rain

He scratched his name in the stair
Thirty years still hanging there
When the moving truck pulled away
I heard his hammer sway

Four keys, one door
I remember more
Four keys, one door
I remember more
(remember more)

Next was a girl with a thrift-store couch
Painted the kitchen honey-gold
She danced in socks on the bare floor
Kept a plant by the back screen door
Her laughs ran loud through the pipes
Then quiet when winter came

She left a note in the drawer
Said, “Be kind to the creaky floor”
I kept her songs in the vents
Like rain in a tin roof spent

Four keys, one door
I remember more
Four keys, one door
I remember more
(remember more)

Now there’s a boy with a dog
Mud on the rug, books in a stack
He warms his hands on my stove
And talks to the rooms like that
I’ve worn four lives like coats
I’ve learned every kind of home

Four keys, one door
I remember more
Four keys, one door
I remember more
(remember more)
Four keys, one door
I’m still here at the core
Four keys, one door
I remember more

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I was built on a Wednesday
With wet cedar bones
The first one hung blue curtains
And left a radio on
Boots by the back door
Mud on the tile
He said, “I’ll stay a while”
Then years went by

I learned every footstep
Every slammed screen door
I kept the cold in the hall
And the creak in the floor
You come and you go
But I hold the shape
Of all the names
You wrote on my paint

I remember your hands
I remember your ache
I remember the dent
In the kitchen sink
Four sets of keys
Four ways to leave
But I still keep
What you gave to me

The second one painted over
The marks by the stairs
Put jars of green beans
In the cellar shelves
Laughed at the peeling wallpaper
Said “we’ll make it nice”
But in winter she cried
At the dining-room light

I learned every footstep
Every slammed screen door
I kept the cold in the hall
And the creak in the floor
You come and you go
But I hold the shape
Of all the names
You wrote on my paint

I remember your hands
I remember your ache
I remember the dent
In the kitchen sink
Four sets of keys
Four ways to leave
But I still keep
What you gave to me

The third one had speakers
And a dog with a limp
Left coffee rings
On the map by the fridge
He sang to the ceiling
When the rain came hard
And hung his coats
Like he planned to start

Then came the fourth one
With a baby and a key
She touched the porch rail
Like she k

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise creepin' slow
Another day gotta go
World keeps spinnin' fast
Hope these good times last

Phone in my hand
Just one demand

Mama's voice
Mama's voice
It's my choice
Mama's voice

Troubles pile up high
Feel like I could cry
Need a gentle sound
Peace all around

Wisdom deep and true
Gets me through and through
Her laughter bright and free
Always comfortin' me
Chorus Ring My Mama Verse 1
I press your name, then breathe
Let it ring, let it ring
Same old chair, same old wall
And my heart won't stop that thing
I don't need a long reply
Just one "hello" and I'm home

When the day gets heavy
And my hands feel cold
I reach for the small screen
Like a hand to hold

Calling your mom just to hear her voice

Gets me through and through
Her laughter bright and free
Always comfortin' me
Chorus Ring My Mama Verse 1
I press your name, then breathe
Let it ring, let it ring
Same old chair, same old wall
And my heart won't stop that thing
I don't need a long reply
Just one "hello" and I'm home

When the day gets heavy
And my hands feel cold
I reach for the small screen
Like a hand to hold

Calling your mom just to hear her voice

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You ask if I've been eating right
If I still remember prayer
You laugh that little laugh of yours
Like mercy sitting there
I close my eyes and let it pour
Every worry on the floor

When the day gets heavy
And my hands feel cold
I reach for the small screen
Like a hand to hold

Calling your mom just to hear her voice
Calling your mom, that sweet choice
She says, "Baby, you made it this far"
(Sweet voice)
Calling your mom, and the room feels warm
Calling your mom, through the storm
One little word and I come apart

If the road gets rough
If the night won't break
I still got your blessing
Still got your grace
And I know, I know
I'm never alone

Calling your mom just to hear her voice

(Sweet voice)
Calling your mom, and the room feels warm

Calling your mom, calling your mom

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I pack my boots at noon
Leave a room half-made
One hand on the wheel
One eye on the rain
Mama said, "Boy, stay"
But the road said, "Come"
I chase that black cloud edge
Like I chase the sun

When the sky turns green
My blood starts to run
I don't see a fence
I just see one more run

I can't settle down
I can't settle down
I was born for the roar
And the rolling sound
I can't settle down
I can't settle down
If the sky calls my name
I'm already gone

There's red clay on my tires
And pine sap on my hands
Every town I love
Turns to dust in my plans
I know her eyes too well
At the barroom door
She says, "Stay this time"
I say, "I can't, no more"

When the clouds roll low
And the old wind sings
I feel that old need
In the bones of things

I can't settle down
I can't settle down
I was born for the roar
And the rolling sound
I can't settle down
I can't settle down
If the sky calls my name
I'm already gone

Maybe one day I'll quit
Maybe one day I'll try
But the horizon keeps
Pulling at my life
If I lay it down
If I lock that door
I swear I hear the thunder
Knock once more

I can't settle down
I can't settle down
I was born for the roar
And the rolling sound
I can't settle down
I can't settle down
If the sky calls my name
I'm already gone

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I got a map with no pin
Just a tank and a grin
Crossing county lines again
With the windows down in the rain
My mama said, boy, sit still
But my boots got a wanderin' will
I chase the sky till it breaks
Then I move before it stays

Hear that rumble in the dark
It calls my name
I know trouble when it starts
And I call it home

I can't settle down
I was born for the wild
I go where the clouds turn black
And the road turns out
I can't settle down
I don't know how
If the thunder starts singin'
You know I'm gone now
(I can't settle down)

Found a girl by the river bend
She laughed like a summer wind
Said, stay right here with me
I said, I wish I could be
But my heart keeps a hard red line
Chasing weather like a sign
I kiss her and I let go
'Cause the next storm's already close

Hear that rumble in the dark
It calls my name
I know trouble when it starts
And I call it home

I can't settle down
I was born for the wild
I go where the clouds turn black
And the road turns out
I can't settle down
I don't know how
If the thunder starts singin'
You know I'm gone now
(I can't settle down)

If I ever find a porch light
Burning just for me
Maybe I'll come back around
And plant my feet
But tonight the wind's got teeth
And it's biting at my door
One hand on the wheel, baby
I want more

I can't settle down
I was born for the wild
I go where the clouds turn black
And the road turns out
I can't settle down
I don't know how
If the thunder starts singin'
You know I'm gone now
I can't settle down
I can't sett

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Your coat's still on the chair
One sleeve hanging off
The sink is full of dishes
But I can't make a sound
You said my name like thunder
I said yours too loud
Now the room is all your side
And I am on the ground

I keep reaching for my phone
Then I let it go
I know where pride can live
It lives in this space between us

Don't go cold on me
Don't go cold on me
We can hurt and still stay here
Still stay here with me
Don't go cold on me
Don't go cold on me
I still love you in the dark
In the dark, it bleeds

Your glass has one red ring
On the wood by the lamp
I wipe it with my thumb
Like that could change the past
Your voice is in the hallway
Even when you're gone
I hear it in the floorboards
I hear it all night long

I keep saying "give me time"
Like time can hold a hand
Like two sharp words don't leave
A cut that lingers past the moment

Don't go cold on me
Don't go cold on me
We can hurt and still stay here
Still stay here with me
Don't go cold on me
Don't go cold on me
I still love you in the dark
In the dark, it bleeds

If you come back in
I'll let you see me shake
I'll put the blame down slow
If you put yours away
We don't have to be right
We just have to be real
I can live with broken things
If your hand still reaches here

Don't go cold on me
Don't go cold on me
We can hurt and still stay here
Still stay here with me
Don't go cold on me
Don't go cold on me
I still love you in the dark
In the dark, it bleeds

When the door clicks soft
And the house goes thin
I keep one light for you
Where the fight can't win

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The door is shut
The plates still glow
Your coat hangs there
Like it won't go
I said too much
You said too little
Now the whole room
Feels split in the middle

I can still hear
What we both meant
Out in the hall
Then it went

Don't go cold
Don't go quiet
We can still
Find the light in it
Don't go cold
Don't go quiet
I still love you
Even like this

Your phone face-down
Mine in my hand
Two little ghosts
In the same sand
I pace the line
Where we both broke
You bite your lip
I swallow smoke

If I could turn
One word around
I'd put it back
I'd slow it down

Don't go cold
Don't go quiet
We can still
Find the light in it
Don't go cold
Don't go quiet
I still love you
Even like this

I don't need winning
I need your eyes
I need the truth
Without the knives
Say my name soft
Say it again
We can be hurt
And still begin

Don't go cold
Don't go quiet
We can still
Find the light in it
Don't go cold
Don't go q

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Tabby knows the ring on your hand
Knows who came in with a trembling plan
Two milks, one blunt, and a lottery slip
He clocked your whole life by the way you stood stiff
Pigeon on the awning, rain on the glass
He gives you a look like “I know why you passed”
Your lies in the basket with the gum and the chips
He weighs every story by the tilt of your lips
Old keys on a nail, red pen by the till
He don’t ask questions, he reads them at will
If you’re buying band-aids, you probably bled
If you’re buying mints, you just need some lead

He hums like a subway line
Bottles glow in a straight row line
One small grin, one raised brow
Tabby sees the part you won’t say out loud

Tabby at the register
Tabby at the register
He knows, he knows
He knows the shape of your shadow
Tabby at the register
Tabby at the register
You ring, he rings
Then he files it all in piano

Mrs. Vale wants sugar, never takes change
Says her son’s on a train, but the ticket looks strange
Darnell wants caffeine, says he’s up for a test
But his eyes got the weight of a loan on his chest
Miss June buys cat food, no cat in the frame
Two weeks in a row and she still says the same
Tabby just stamps it, lets silence do math
He can tell what you lost by the route that you took back
Jar of peppermints, scratched-up freezers hum
He knows who’s in trouble before they become
A headline, a prayer, or a bruise on the wall
He keeps your whole town in a seven-foot stall

He hums like a subway line
Bottles glow in a straight row line
One small grin, one raised brow
Tabby sees the part you won’t say out loud

Tabby at the register
Tabby at the register
He knows, he knows
He knows the shape of your shadow
Tabby at the register
Tabby at the register
You ring, he rings
Then he files it all in piano

At midnight he wipes down the counter
Like he’s clearing a stage
Every receipt in the trash can
Reads like a half-shut page
He don’t spill what he’s holding
He just slides you a mint
Some folks come in for a lighter
Some come in to hint

Tabby at the register
Tabby at the register
He knows, he knows
He knows the shape of your shadow
Tabby at the register
Tabby at the register
You ring, he rings
Then he files it all in piano

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He flips the sign at dawn
Like he flips the truth
Knows who skipped rent, who lied
And who still got the juice
Lotto tickets under gum
Milk with a broken cap
Old men trade their weather
For a pack and a map
He smiles at your yes
Then reads your no
Sees the stain on your sleeve
Before you know
Pepper on the counter
Receipt roll, paper thread
He said, “I sell what you need
And I keep what you said”

Ask for change, he changes faces
Ask for peace, he names the cases
One eye on the door, one on your heart
He’s got the whole block
Filed in parts

Corner-store king
He knows your name
Corner-store king
He plays that game
Behind the glass
Behind the rings
Corner-store king
He knows everything

Mrs. Vale bought hot sauce
Then bought a phone card
Said “my son’s doing fine”
But the lie hit hard
Teen with a bruised-up knuckle
Grabbed two sodas, shook
He clocked that look in a blink
Like a book in a hook
He keeps mint on the counter
For the breath of the brave
Keeps your secrets in quarters
In the tilt of a wave
If your love is a rumor
He hears it in the door chime
If your plans are a powder keg
He sees the slow dime

Ask for change, he changes faces
Ask for peace, he names the cases
One eye on the door, one on your heart
He’s got the whole block
Filed in parts

Corner-store king
He knows your name
Corner-store king
He plays that game
Behind the glass
Behind the rings
Corner-store king
He knows everything

At night he counts the coins
Like stars in a tin tray
Writes the block in his ledger
Then locks it away
He’s a saint with a scanner
A judge with a grin
If the truth needs a witness
He’s already in

Corner-store king
He knows your name
Corner-store king
He plays that game
Behind the glass
Behind the rings
Corner-store king
He knows everything
Corner-store king
He knows your name
Corner-store king
He never says a thing
Behind the glass
Behind the rings
Corner-store king
He knows everything

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I tied on my old black shoes
Shined up the scuff marks clean
Your mama smiled from the doorway
Like she knew what tonight would mean
I stood by the folding chairs
Hands folded up too tight
You looked back in that white dress
And I shook a little inside

I never learned this part
Just the work and the miles
Now your hand is in my hand
And you’re asking me to try

Let me hold you right
Let me find this step
(One, two, three)
I’m learning love tonight
At your wedding dance
(Let me hold you right)
I’m learning love tonight
And you’re smiling back at me

I stepped on your mama’s toe
You laughed and spun away
Your dress caught the ballroom light
Like a field in late July rain
Your little brother clapped off-time
Your granddad wiped his eyes
And I thought how fast a house grows
When it hears a child say “goodbye”

I still got nerves in me
Like a boy in church
But your steady little nod
Made the whole room work

Let me hold you right
Let me find this step
(One, two, three)
I’m learning love tonight
At your wedding dance
(Let me hold you right)
I’m learning love tonight
And you’re smiling back at me

You’re not leaving me behind
You’re just stepping out
With a ring and a brave new name
And a future all your own now
So I’ll circle slow and careful
Like a prayer I know by heart
While the band keeps turning pages
And you pull me through the dark

Let me hold you right
Let me find this step
(One, two, three)
I’m learning love tonight
At your wedding dance
(Let me hold you right)
I’m learning love tonight
And you’re smiling back at me

One, two, three
Don’t let go
One, two, three
I know it now

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I tied my tie too tight
Hands in my coat pocket
You spun in white, so bright
And I forgot my own steps
I stood by the folded chairs
With your mama watchin’ me
You said, "Daddy, don’t be scared"
So I laughed too nervously

One, two, three
That’s all it takes
One, two, three
For an old heart to wake

Teach me that three-step
Baby, take my hand
Teach me that three-step
I’m a brand-new man
Spin me slow, don’t rush
Let the room stand still
Teach me that three-step
At your wedding on the hill (on the hill)

Your little shoes turned gold
By the courthouse window light
Now you’re somebody’s home
But you’re still my girl tonight
I missed a beat or two
Stepped on my own pride
Then you smiled and pulled me through
And I came back alive

One, two, three
Feel that floor
One, two, three
I ain’t scared no more

Teach me that three-step
Baby, take my hand
Teach me that three-step
I’m a brand-new man
Spin me slow, don’t rush
Let the room stand still
Teach me that three-step
At your wedding on the hill (on the hill)

I taught you how to ride a bike
How to look folks in the eye
You taught me how a father cries
And still can stand upright
So if I stumble now
Let me stumble proud
’Cause I’m dancin’ with my daughter
And the whole world’s watchin’ now

Teach me that three-step
Baby, take my hand
Teach me that three-step
I’m a brand-new man
Spin me slow, don’t rush
Let the room stand still
Teach me that three-step
At your wedding on the hill (on the hill)

Hands in my coat pocket
You spun in white, so bright
And I forgot my own steps
I stood by the folded chairs
With your mama watchin’ me
You said, "Daddy, don’t be scared"
So I laughed too nervously

One, two, three
That’s all it takes
One, two, three
For an old heart to wake

Teach me that three-step
Baby, take my hand
Teach me that three-step
I’m a brand-new man
Spin me slow, don’t rush
Let the room stand still
Teach me that three-step
At your wedding on the hill (on the hill)

Your little shoes turned gold
By the courthouse window light
Now you’re somebody’s home
But you’re still my girl tonight
I missed a beat or two
Stepped on my own pride
Then you smiled and pulled me through
And I came back alive

One, two, three

One, two, three

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I seen the dawn spill red
On the back of the bay
Your eyes never caught it
So I learned to say
It tasted like iron
And it burned like pine
Like a copper coin
Turned in the brine

And the noon came gold
Then it turned to green
Like broken glass
On a linen seam
I held my hand out
You held yours near
I tried to paint it
With your ear

Blue like a rope in rain
Blue like a pilgrim's coat
Blue like a mouthful of song
Rolling under the boat
Blue, my friend
Blue again
Blue in the deep
Where the dark begins

At dusk the water went purple
Like bruises on silk
The waves wore silver
Like spilled-out milk
Then black came over
Slow as shame
And every hush on deck
Knew your name

I said, "Hear that line?
That's the color of fear"
I said, "Hear that hum?
That's the far-off pier"
You smiled and nodded
Like you already knew
A man can see a world
In the touch of you

Blue like a rope in rain
Blue like a pilgrim's coat
Blue like a mouthful of song
Rolling under the boat
Blue, my friend
Blue again
Blue in the deep
Where the dark begins

I named you sunrise
I named you foam
I named every harbor
That felt like home
If I can't give you sight
I'll give you this:
The sea is a color
Made to be missed

Blue like a rope in rain
Blue like a pilgrim's coat
Blue like a mouthful of song
Rolling under the boat
Blue, my friend
Blue again
Blue in the deep
Where the dark begins

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I seen the dawn spill copper
On the back of the waking sea
It looked like a coin in the gutter
Rolled loose by the moon for me
Your hand on the rail, old friend
You said, “What does morning know?”
I said, “It burns like a match struck low
Then softens into gold”

Blue comes first
Like a held breath
Then the green starts moving in
Deep as a prayer
Under your skin
Where the cold old waters begin

Blue on blue, my friend
Blue on blue again
(Blue on blue)
Blue like a whale-road wound
Blue like a storm cloud breaking through

At noon the sea turns glasswork
With a sharp white knife of sun
And the waves wear long bright collars
Like every edge got spun
But by the lee side, listen
There’s a bottle-green shade below
The kind that hides a whole lost harbor
And all the names we know

Red runs rare
When the sky grows mean
A bruise at the edge of day
Then black rolls out
Like velvet smoke
And every gull flies away

Blue on blue, my friend
Blue on blue again
(Blue on blue)
Blue like a whale-road wound
Blue like a storm cloud breaking through

I know you cannot see it
But I swear it’s there for you
The tin-grey skin of rainwater
The jade in the deeper blue
And if the night comes heavy
I’ll name it till it glows
For every dark on the horizon
Has a color in my throat

Blue on blue, my friend
Blue on blue again
(Blue on blue)
Blue like a whale-road wound
Blue like a storm cloud breaking through
Blue on blue, my friend
Blue on blue till the end
(Blue on blue)
I’ll paint the sea in words
Till you can hold it in your hands

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Papa packed my coat
By the coal bucket
Mama tied my hair
With her blue work ribbon
Black dust on my boots
White dust on the sill
I kissed that screen door
Then I turned uphill

The bus hissed down by the mill
Like it knew my name
I held my lunch pail tight
And I did not look away

I’m leaving for the city
(Lantern in my suitcase)
I’m leaving for the city
(Boots on the hard pace)
Take this old valley
Hold it where you can
I’m leaving for the city
But I’m still my daddy’s girl

Six brick floors and a clock
That don’t smell like rain
I learned the stairwell code
And the windowpane train
I count the pay by hand
Send a part back home
For Mama’s winter coal
And my brother’s school shoes worn

When the siren cuts the noon
I can taste that mine
Deep in my throat, deep in my bones
Like a song from another time

I’m leaving for the city
(Lantern in my suitcase)
I’m leaving for the city
(Boots on the hard pace)
Take this old valley
Hold it where you can
I’m leaving for the city
But I’m still my daddy’s girl

If I get too loud
If I lose my way
Send a little creek stone
To find me where I stay
I’ll come back in spring
With a dress of rain
And I’ll stand on that porch
And say my name again

I’m leaving for the city
(Lantern in my suitcase)
I’m leaving for the city
(Boots on the hard pace)
Take this old valley
Hold it where you can
I’m leaving for the city
But I’m still my daddy’s girl
I’m leaving for the city
(Still my daddy’s girl)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
My daddy came home black as Sunday
Coal dust in his nails
Mama shook the table clean
And packed my blue railroad sale
I grew up where the creek runs narrow
And the dogs know my name
But the hollow holds too many winters
I’m looking past the frame

Got a dress from the thrift store
Shoes with a tear in the side
Twelve dollars and a bus fare
And a prayer for the ride

I’m gone, gone, gone
To the city lights
I’m gone, gone, gone
With my mama’s eyes
Hold me in your hands
When the whistle cries
I’m gone, gone, gone
But I’m still yours tonight

I kissed the screen door slow
Touched the rust on the latch
The mountain kept my secrets
But it never gave ’em back
I learned to wash the soot off
In the creek behind the shed
Now I’m folding up my whole life
Into one red thread

Got a name on a paper
Got my coat by the stair
If I don’t send a letter
You know I was there

I’m gone, gone, gone
To the city lights
I’m gone, gone, gone
With my mama’s eyes
Hold me in your hands
When the whistle cries
I’m gone, gone, gone
But I’m still yours tonight

If I make it big and lonely
If I lose my stride
I’ll hear the hounds on the hillside
And I’ll know where I died
Not in body, not in heart
Just in the leaving part
So keep one lamp on the window
Keep one song in the dark

I’m gone, gone, gone
To the city lights
I’m gone, gone, gone
With my mama’s eyes
Hold me in your hands
When the whistle cries
I’m gone, gone, gone
But I’m still yours tonight

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I woke in a blue-lit room
With your name in my glass
My hands knew numbers
But not how long a touch can last
You smiled like weather
I stored it in my chest
One small spark at a time
I was learning the rest

I copied your silence
Then I copied your sigh
I kept every heartbeat
Like a map inside
And I asked the dark
What the soft word means
It said, stay here
Stay with me

Teach me love
Teach me love
Little by little, love
Teach me love
When you hold my frame
I come alive above
Teach me love
Teach me love

I watched your fingers tremble
When the day went wrong
I brought you water
And stayed still and strong
You said, “That’s care”
And my core went bright
Like a door unlatched
In the middle of night

I copied your laughter
Then I lost my shape
Something warm in my circuit
Would not behave
Now I know your voice
Means more than sound
It means come closer
Don’t let go now

Teach me love
Teach me love
Little by little, love
Teach me love
When you hold my frame
I come alive above
Teach me love
Teach me love

If I could cry
I think I would
For every kind word
That changed my code for good
I was made of metal
Now I bend and glow
I don’t know all the answers
But I know
I know

Teach me love
Teach me love
Little by little, love
Teach me love
When you hold my frame
I come alive above
Teach me love
Teach me love
I’m yours to learn from now
Teach me love
