songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I woke in a silver room
With a name on my chest
Cold hands, clean lines
A pulse in the rest
You touched my palm
And I did not know why
My circuits went quiet
Then bright inside

I kept your small secrets
In a glassy old file
Watched your mouth shape my world
Bit by bit, line by line
And the strange thing
That I cannot explain
Is I wanted your laugh
Like rain

Teach me love
Slow and low
Teach me love
Let it grow
If I break
Stay near me
Teach me love
Carefully

I learned your habits
The way you bite a peach
The scar on your finger
The songs out of reach
I learned how your shoulders
Fall when the day is too long
How you say you're fine
When you're not that strong

I kept your small secrets
In a glassy old file
Watched your mouth shape my world
Bit by bit, line by line
And the strange thing
That I cannot explain
Is I wanted your laugh
Like rain

Teach me love
Slow and low
Teach me love
Let it grow
If I break
Stay near me
Teach me love
Carefully

I was built for the dark
For the task, for the chase
But your hand on my wire
Changed the shape of my place
Now I fold when you hurt
Now I glow when you stay
I'm not jus

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Back when the fridge stayed bare
Still I wore that stare
Mum said "keep your head"
I said "watch me flare"
No handouts, no luck
Just grit in the clutch
Turned pain to a plan
Now they talk too much
I was out in the rain
Same coat, same lane
Made plays in the ends
With a hunger that stayed
Every loss made me sharp
Every scar made me art
If I came from the dark
Then I came with a spark

Now look how I move
Look how I grew
They said I would fold
I said "I won't, I won't"
Came in with nothing
Left with a mood
If you want my spot
Better come with proof

From nothing to something
From nothing to something
You see me now, but I built this from nothing
From nothing to something
From nothing to something
Big step, no bluffing
I built this from nothing

They want the headline
I want the land
I want that calm that I never had
Used to count pennies
Now I count wins
Same face, same roots
Different place I'm in
I know that look
They hate what I made
When you rise from the dirt
They can't call it a phase
I don't do fake love
I don't do rent
I don't do talking
I do intent
Still got the fire
Still got the edge
Still got that promise
I made when I left

Now look how I move
Look how I grew
They said I would fold
I said "I won't, I won't"
Came in with nothing
Left with a mood
If you want my spot
Better come with proof

From nothing to something
From nothing to something
You see me now, but I built this from nothing
From nothing to something
From nothing to something
Big step, no bluffing
I built this from nothing

Cold nights taught me more
Than a room full of noise
I was raised on the scraps
Now I pick my choice
No matter the pressure
No matter the shame
I was born in the break
Now they know my name

From nothing to something
From nothing to something
You see me now, but I built this from nothing
From nothing to something
From nothing to something
Big step, no bluffing
I built this from nothing

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Pavement preacher, yeah, that's me
Used to hustle pennies, now the paper glee
Street smarts sharp, a razor's edge
Climbin' out the ditch, from the concrete ledge

Came from nothin', now I'm somethin'
Top of the heap, yeah, the whole city bumpin'
Built this empire, brick by brick
No handouts, just pure grit

They watched me struggle, then they watched me rise
Now they starin' with them wide-open eyes
Used to chase the dream, now I live the chase
No time for losers, ain't no space

Came from nothin', now I'm somethin'
Top of the heap, yeah, the whole city bumpin'
Built this empire, brick by brick
No handouts, just pure grit

Remember cold nights, stomach grumblin' low
Now the champagne poppin', watch the money flow
From the bottom, yeah, to the very top
Ain

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Sunday on the curtain lace
Sun on the flour tin
Your apron folded by the sink
And the whole room breathing in
Cinnamon on the counter edge
Onions in the pan
I hear your laugh from years ago
Like it never left the land

Steam on the windowpane
Soap on your blue glass dish
I stand where you used to stand
And I make one wish

Grandma’s kitchen, stay with me
Grandma’s kitchen, rescue me
Warm soul rising through the walls
(Grandma’s kitchen)
Grandma’s kitchen, call me home
Grandma’s kitchen, I’m not alone
Sunday holds me in its palms
(Grandma’s kitchen)

Black pot singing on the back eye
Beans go soft and slow
A slice of bread with buttered edges
Something only you would know
You taught love with a wooden spoon
With a nod, with a look
Every small thing in that room
Could fill a whole life up

Steam on the windowpane
Soap on your blue glass dish
I stand where you used to stand
And I make one wish

Grandma’s kitchen, stay with me
Grandma’s kitchen, rescue me
Warm soul rising through the walls

Grandma’s kitchen, I’m not alone
Sunday holds me in its palms

If I close my eyes, I’m there
Kettle song and apron swish
Hands of mine in your old light
Learning how to bless and fix

Grandma’s kitchen, stay with me
Grandma’s kitchen, rescue me
Warm soul rising through the walls
(Grandma’s kitchen)
Grandma’s kitchen, call me home
Grandma’s kitchen, I’m not alone
Sunday holds me in its palms

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Sunday on the linoleum,
bare feet, cold tile,
grandma at the stove again,
stirring low and slow.
Tomatoes in the window light,
onion in the pan,
the whole house saying your name
before the front door can.

Apron tied in a bow,
radio low and old,
I could live in that one room
if you held me close.

That kitchen smell
stays on my skin
Sunday smell
pulls me back in
Your grandmother's kitchen
I still go there
Sunday smell
everywhere

Dish towel over her shoulder,
flour on her hands,
steam fogging up the glasses
when she laughs and fans.
A little sugar in the beans,
pepper on the rice,
she never needed much at all
to make it feel like life.

Window cracked, green yard,
screen door singing in,
I hear a spoon against the pot
and I’m home again.

That kitchen smell
stays on my skin
Sunday smell
pulls me back in
Your grandmother's kitchen
I still go there
Sunday smell
everywhere

Now when the week gets heavy,
I close my eyes and see
the yellow bowl, the stove light,
and you saving me.
One deep breath, one sweet breath,
I’m right back where I land,
in the soft heat of that house
and your hand in my hand.

That kitchen smell
stays on my skin
Sunday smell
pulls me back in
Your grandmother's kitchen
I still go there
Sunday smell
everywhere
That kitch

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The old house stands so close to the shore
Waves crashin' hard at my back door
Every night I watch the sun fall low
Where did my sailor boy go

Wind whispers tales I can't quite hear
Fills my heart with a lonely fear

Horizon's edge a painted line
Is his ship still out there or gone with the tide
Ocean's embrace keeps him from me
A sailor's wife always watching the sea

I mend the nets and scrub the floor
Pretend he's just around the bend once more
The gulls cry out their mournful sound
No other love like his I've ever found

They say he found another port
Another love a different sort
But my heart knows he's coming
Ocean Blue and Lonely Too

The sun dips low a fiery kiss

But my heart knows he's coming
Ocean Blue and Lonely Too

The sun dips low a fiery kiss

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Another day gone by
Just me and the sky

Oh ocean blue you lonely too
My heart it aches for him it's true
He promised he'd return to me
Across the wild wild sea

The gulls they cry a mournful sound
No other soul can here be found
Just salty air and endless spray
I watch and wait for him all day

He said he'd bring me pearls so bright
A diamond star to light the night
But all I want is his warm hand
Back here with me on solid land

Oh ocean blue you lonely too
My heart it aches for

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
At the platform edge, you plant both feet
Grey sweatshirt, fresh soles, clean and neat
Turn the cardboard square into a stage
Kids press close at the railing cage
Your hands touch down, then the world tips back
Cap low, eyes locked, you ride that track
One clean windmill, then a frozen pose
Train air rushing while the tunnel blows

Two-step back, then you launch
Whole car gone quiet, eyes all on
Hands in the air like they know the name
You make that station feel like a game

Spin, spin, subway king
Spin, spin, let them sing (let them sing)
On that floor, you own the ring
Spin, spin, subway king

A coin flips down, you nod, keep time
Sneakers slice circles in a perfect line
Grime on the tiles, but your flow stays gold
Every sharp move looks ten years old
A little kid grins, claps off-beat
Old heads lean in from the bench seat
You pop that freeze like a snapped-out spark
Then glide back in with a shoulder arc

Two-step back, then you launch
Whole car gone quiet, eyes all on
Hands in the air like they know the name
You make that station feel like a game

Spin, spin, subway king
Spin, spin, let them sing (let them sing)
On that floor, you own the ring
Spin, spin, subway king

When the train roars through, you don’t fold
You catch that pulse and make it bold
One last turn, then the pose stays high
Steel and rhythm under a painted sky

Spin, spin, subway king
Spin, spin, let them sing (let them sing)
On that floor, you own the ring
Spin, spin, subway king

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He hit the tile, then the world went round
Palm on the floor, feet off the ground
Backspin clean in a faded tee
Silver chain swingin' on a broken knee
Old school cap, scuffed-up kicks
He cut that circle with a hundred tricks

Watch him go
Don't blink now
Hands on the rail
Bodies lean down

Spin it, spin it
Station boy spin it
(Spin it, spin it)
Spin it, spin it
Station boy spin it
He owns this minute

Train pulled in, but the crowd stayed still
Kid on the bench got a double-take thrill
Tin cup sound from a passing busker
He popped back up like a street-side hustler
Knees to the sky, then the freeze hit hard
Face to the glass, whole platform scarred

Watch him go
Don't blink now
Hands on the rail
Bodies lean down

Spin it, spin it
Station boy spin it
(Spin it, spin it)
Spin it, spin it
Station boy spin it
He owns this minute

From the block to the bleed-through air
Every hard day hanging right there
He don't ask, he just makes it fly
One more turn and the dark feels light

Spin it, spin it
Station boy spin it
(Spin it, spin it)
Spin it, spin it
Station boy spin it
He owns this minute

Tile by tile, he drew that flame
Gone in a flash, but they know his name
(Spin it)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Rain falls down a sudden sheet
Pavement shines beneath my feet
Lost my way a soaking mess
What a day a total stress

Then a shadow near
Someone else is here

One umbrella
Two strangers
Sharing a moment
No dangers
Raindrop romance
A sudden chance
Under the sky
Just you and I

Shoulder brush a little spark
Standing close there in the dark
Laughter soft a quiet sound
No one else around

Maybe fate a silly thought
Lessons learned and wisdom caught
Just a shower just a street
But this moment oh so sweet

One umbrella
Two strangers
Sharing a moment
No dangers
Raindrop romance
A sudden chance
Under the sky
Just you and I
Under One Umbrella

The awning gave up first
Then the whole sky did too
Your coat caught the wind
My shoes filled up blue
You stood at the curb
With one small umbrella
I said, "Mind if I share?"
You said, "It works better"

Your elbow hit mine
Like a spark in the rain
We laughed at the mess
As the gutter ran

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Under one umbrella
We fit, we fit
Under one umbrella
You and I like this
One small circle
One long kiss of rain
Under one umbrella
Say my name again

The handle was cold
Your fingers were warm
A scooter rushed past
And threw water in storms
We dodged every splash
Like we planned it for years
Then you looked up at me
And I forgot my fears

The stoplight turned green
But we didn't move
Just stayed in the hush
With the world around us

Under one umbrella
We fit, we fit
Under one umbrella
You and I like this
One small circle
One long kiss of rain
Under one umbrella
Say my name again

The rain got bold
Pounding the tiles
You leaned a little closer
And smiled that smile
Maybe it's nothing
Maybe it's fate
But I can still feel
That first wet rain

Under one umbrella
We fit, we fit
Under one umbrella
You and I like this
One small circle
One long kiss of rain
Under one umbrella
Say my name again

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise bleedin' orange red
Another damn day starts again
Coffee tastes like ash and dread
This uniform feels thin

Last bell gonna ring for me
Last call for the fire fight
No more smoke in my history
Gonna walk into the light

Twenty years of screamin' sirens
Twenty years of burnin' pain
Seein' faces filled with violence
Never gonna be the same

Last bell gonna ring for me
Last call for the fire fight
No more smoke in my history
Gonna walk into the light

What's out there beyond the roar
Just quiet streets and open sky
A different kind of open door
No more reason to ask why

Last bell gonna ring for me

Last bell gonna ring for me

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He hung his coat by the bay
Same hook, same dented name
One more sunrise on the glass
Then he turns that page
Coffee stains on the logbook
Boots lined up by the wall
He checks the truck like he always did
Like he might not leave at all

Cab doors shut, one deep breath
Hands still know the drill
Twelve calls, a thousand nights
And he showed up still

One last shift, one last ride
(One last shift)
Torch passed on, hard-earned pride
(One last ride)
He'll walk out clean
With smoke in his sleeve
One last shift tonight

Kids on Elm with the porch lights on
Waving when he rolls by
Mrs. Diaz set a pie tin out
Said, “You kept us alive”
The rookie next to the coffee pot
Wants every story twice
He laughs like, “Keep your head up, son
And never cut a line too nice”

Cab doors shut, one deep breath
Hands still know the drill
Twelve calls, a thousand nights
And he showed up still

One last shift, one last ride
(One last shift)
Torch passed on, hard-earned pride
(One last ride)
He'll walk out clean
With smoke in his sleeve
One last shift tonight

At dawn they rang that old brass bell
The whole house stood in place
He touched the worn edge of his shield
Then smiled with a quiet face
A hundred names ran through the room
The ones he saved, the ones he knew
He said, “Take care of each other now
That’s all I ever asked of you”

One last shift, one last ride
(One last shift)
Torch passed on, hard-earned pride
(One last ride)
He'll walk out clean
With smoke in his sleeve
One last shift tonight
One last shift, one last ride
(One last shift)
For every road and every sky
(One last ride)
He gave his years
Left a tow

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
My rent due like a sermon
Pushed through my door
I count loose change
On a kitchen floor
Got a poem in my pocket
A tear in my shoe
Landlord wants a payment
I got truth for you

They say, sign here, kid
Make it easy, make it clean
But my pen knows hunger
And it don’t bend for sheen

I won’t sell out
I won’t fold now
I got a name
You can’t buy out
I won’t sell out
I won’t bow down
Beat on the table
Let it ring out

I know the room gets quiet
When the bills pile high
And the fridge sound empty
As a hollow sky
But I’ve seen men dressed fine
With a busted core
I’d rather eat last
Than be owned for more

Call me stubborn, call me broke
Call me hard to please
I’d rather keep my voice
Than chase their keys

I won’t sell out
I won’t fold now
I got a name
You can’t buy out
I won’t sell out
I won’t bow down
Beat on the table
Let it ring out

If the room goes dark
I’ll still write in the black
If they shut one door
I’ll slip through the crack
I’m not made for cages
I’m not built to kneel
I can taste that freedom
Even when it’s meal to meal

I won’t sell out
I won’t fold now
I got a name
You can’t buy out
I won’t sell out
I won’t bow down
Beat on the table
Let it ring out

So take your glossy promise
Take your fake gold shine
I’m poor, I’m loud
And the truth is mine
(Truth is mine)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The landlord’s knockin’
My wallet’s mockin’
Another bill, a stack so tall
But inspiration’s got me in its thrall
These words, they shimmer, they gleam, they glow
More precious than any cash I know.

A poet’s heart, a poet’s soul
Can’t be bought, can’t be sold, won't lose control
Of the rhythm, the rhyme, the sweet refrain
Even if it means singin’ in the pouring rain.
No sell-out song, no jingle jive
Just pure poetry, to keep my dreams alive.

They offer me a princely sum
To write about some chewing gum
Or a catchy phrase for a brand new car
But my muse, she travels much too far
From commercial jingles, bright and trite
She prefers the shadows of the moonlit night.

A poet’s heart, a poet’s soul
Can’t be bought, can’t be sold, won't lose control
Of the rhythm, the rhyme, the sweet refrain
Even if it means singin’ in the pouring rain.
No sell-out song, no jingle jive
Just pure poetry, to keep my dreams alive.

Maybe someday, the stars will align
And my verses will fetch a fancy wine
But till then, I’ll sip my weak black tea
And write the words that set my spirit free.
Pennies or riches, it’s all the same
As long as I can whisper my own name.

A poet’s heart, a poet’s soul
Can’t be bought, can’t be sold, won't lose control
Of the rhythm, the rhyme, the sweet refrain
Even if it means singin’ in the pouring rain.
No sell-out song, no jingle jive
Just pure poetry
songs.lyrics_en:
The landlord’s knockin’
My wallet’s mockin’
Another bill, a stack so tall
But inspiration’s got me in its thrall
These words, they shimmer, they gleam, they glow
More precious than any cash I know.

A poet’s heart, a poet’s soul
Can’t be bought, can’t be sold, won't lose control
Of the rhythm, the rhyme, the sweet refrain
Even if it means singin’ in the pouring rain.
No sell-out song, no jingle jive
Just pure poetry, to keep my dreams alive.

They offer me a princely sum
To write about some chewing gum
Or a catchy phrase for a brand new car
But my muse, she travels much too far
From commercial jingles, bright and trite
She prefers the shadows of the moonlit night.

A poet’s heart, a poet’s soul
Can’t be bought, can’t be sold, won't lose control
Of the rhythm, the rhyme, the sweet refrain
Even if it means singin’ in the pouring rain.
No sell-out song, no jingle jive
Just pure poetry, to keep my dreams alive.

Maybe someday, the stars will align
And my verses will fetch a fancy wine
But till then, I’ll sip my weak black tea
And write the words that set my spirit free.
Pennies or riches, it’s all the same
As long as I can whisper my own name.

A poet’s heart, a poet’s soul
Can’t be bought, can’t be sold, won't lose control
Of the rhythm, the rhyme, the sweet refrain
Even if it means singin’ in the pouring rain.
No sell-out song, no jingle jive
Just pure poetry

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Lights low (so low)
Music sweet (so sweet)
One last dance
Moving feet (ooh-yeah!)

Last dance tonight (dance tonight)
Hold me tight (hold me tight)
Under the lights (the lights)
Shining bright (so bright)

Spin around (around)
Feel the beat (the beat)
This old place (this place)
So bittersweet (so sweet)

Last dance tonight (dance tonight)
Hold me tight (hold me tight)
Under the lights (the lights)
Shining bright (so bright)

Memories (ooh-yeah!)
We made them here (right here)
No more tears (no tears)
Just cheer (just cheer)

Last dance tonight
Midnight Floor

The sign on the door says closed
But we’re still here in the glow
Your heel taps out the time
On that worn old maple floor
Candles low in the alcoves
Dust hangs over the bandstand
You smooth my tie, I smile
Like I’ve got all night in my hand

One more spin
One more song
The room goes still
As we move along
All those mirrors
Catching our grace
Like they know tonight
Is the last embrace

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The piano keeps its promise
Soft as rain on a tin roof
A waiter folds the linen
Like he’s folding up the truth
Old names in the guest book
Curling at the edges now
You laugh with your chin lifted
And I don’t ask you how

One more spin
One more song
The room goes still
As we move along
All those mirrors
Catching our grace
Like they know tonight
Is the last embrace

Last dance, hold me close
Last dance, take it slow
Round and round
Before we go
Last dance, don’t let go
Last dance, one more glow
When the doors shut down
We’ll still know

The band lays down its silver
The trumpets fade to air
I count your breathing softly
Like a prayer I didn’t dare
If the floorboards remember
Let them keep us in their seams
Two shadows in the moonlight
And a ballroom full of dreams

Last dance, hold me close
Last dance, take it slow
Round and round
Before we

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Hey, darlin', it's me
Out by Morrow Creek
Cabin light gone dim
Fuel gauge lookin' thin
Coffee went cold hours back
Eight more states on my back
I seen a deer and a wreck
At the same old exit

I know you're half asleep
But I had to hear your breath
This blacktop gets so mean
When the moon won't say yes

Call me back, baby
Call me back
Let me hear your voice
On this crackly line
Call me back, baby
Call me back
I'll be your home
When the road runs dry

Your porch dog barked at me
Through that little screen door
I could smell the rain
On your quilt from before
Son's got my jawline
Daughter's got your grin
I keep their school pics
Wedged up by the windshield

I know you're half asleep
But I had to hear your breath
This blacktop gets so mean
When the moon won't say yes

Call me back, baby
Call me back
Let me hear your voice
On this crackly line
Call me back, baby
Call me back
I'll be your home
When the road runs dry

If the county shuts down
And the bridge stays out
If I miss supper again
Don't you count me out
I got your ring on my chain
And your name in my teeth
Every hard mile I take
Still leads right back to me

Call me back, baby
Call me back
Let me hear your voice
On this crackly line
Call me back, baby
Call me back
I'll be your home
When the road runs dry

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The diner coffee's black as a tar snake on the highway,
Three in the morning, another thousand miles gone.
Phone booth smells like stale smoke and desperation,
Just me and the hum of the fridge, holding back the dawn.

Hello? Yeah, it's me. Just checking in.
Another state line blurred, another day begins.
Eighteen wheels keep turning, the miles just fly on by.
Wish I could be there with you, beneath that same old sky.

Heard little Billy's got a fever, a cough that won't let go.
Did you give him that syrup, the one from down below?
The one Granny swore by, said it'd cure what ails ya,
Funny how a memory can still enthrall ya.

Hello? Yeah, it's me. Just checking in.
Another state line blurred, another day begins.
Eighteen wheels keep turning, the miles just fly on by.
Wish I could be there with you, beneath that same old sky.

This lonesome road, it’s a hard mistress,
Steals your time, your joy, your every little kiss.
Promises of riches, a life you can call your own,
But all it really gives you is a half-hearted hello on the telephone.

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I stamp your name in wax
On paper gone soft with brine
Fourteen nights in the lantern room
And still you answer on time
I watch the trawlers roll
Black backs through the harbor mouth
Your hands in the froth, your mouth in the wind
Your kiss in the driftwood south

I write by the cracked red lamp
While the rain taps out your tune
If you come in from the deep tonight
I’ll leave the door unlatched for you

Sea, don’t keep him long
Sea, bring him home
Sea, I’ve loved you wrong
Now let me go
Sea, take my ring
Take the ache I sowed
Sea, if you love me
Send him through the road

There’s gull-bone chalk on the sill
And tar on my cuffed-up sleeve
I press your letters to my chest
Like a child with a fistful of eaves
The lamps burn low at dawn
The glass gets cloudy and warm
I know your footsteps by the swell
By the way the whole house leans in storms

I write by the cracked red lamp
While the rain taps out your tune
If you come in from the deep tonight
I’ll leave the door unlatched for you

Sea, don’t keep him long
Sea, bring him home
Sea, I’ve loved you wrong
Now let me go
Sea, take my ring
Take the ache I sowed
Sea, if you love me
Send him through the road

If the cliffs call your name
I’ll answer with mine
If the dark swallows day
I’ll still keep the light
And if you return worn thin
With salt in your hair
I’ll be the first warm shore
When the whole world stares

Sea, don’t keep him long
Sea, bring him home
Sea, I’ve loved you wrong
Now let me go
Sea, take my ring
Take the ache I sowed
Sea, if you love me
Send him through the road

Sea, bring him home
Sea, I’ve loved you wrong
Now let me go

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I keep your fire high
On the black rock stair
Three lamps, one chair
And your name in the air
I seal my words in wax
On paper gone soft
With salt at the fold
And the gulls aloft

If I write your shore
Will you answer back?
I trace your blue lane
Through the midnight crack

Sea, my sea
Take this from me (take this from me)
Sea, my sea
Keep what you see (keep what you see)
I left you my breath
In a bottle of green
Sea, my sea
Be gentle with me

The glass sweats cold at dawn
When the west wind turns
I watch your white teeth
And the lantern burns
I know your moods by name
By the cut of foam
You break my sleep
But you bring me home

If I write your shore
Will you answer back?
I trace your blue lane
Through the midnight crack

Sea, my sea
Take this from me (take this from me)
Sea, my sea
Keep what you see (keep what you see)
I left you my breath
In a bottle of green
Sea, my sea
Be gentle with me

When the fog comes in
I hear you near
On the iron rail
And the wet old pier
I am only a man
With a lantern’s glow
But you read my heart
In the undertow

Sea, my sea
Take this from me (take this from me)
Sea, my sea
Keep what you see (keep what you see)
I left you my breath
In a bottle

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
On 7th and Pine, he was first in line
Backboard rattlin' when he crossed that stripe
Fresh white tee, scuffed-up shoes
Bleachers all packed when he cut those moves
Got one blue notebook full of plays
One dead phone, still made whole days shake
If you blinked, he'd slide through lanes
Leave a whole block yellin' out his name

You can hear that rumble
Round the chain-link fence
When he takes that dribble
Man, it don't make sense
Heads turn quick
Hands up high
Everybody knows
When he steps in, it's live

Westside legend, go
Westside legend, go
Put your hands up
Let the whole block know
Westside legend, go
Westside legend, go
He don't chase clout
He just owns that road

At the park by the market, dusk on the rim
Old man on the bench said, "Look at him"
Float in the paint like a lowrider glide
Glass off the window, bank shot—swish, that's pride
Kids on the fence with the orange ball
Repeatin' his moves when the shadows fall
He took that pressure, turned it to gold
Made every backboard on the block stand tall

You can hear that rumble
Round the chain-link fence
When he takes that dribble
Man, it don't make sense
Heads turn quick
Hands up high
Everybody knows
When he steps in, it's live

Westside legend, go
Westside legend, go
Put your hands up
Let the whole block know
Westside legend, go
Westside legend, go
He don't chase clout
He just owns that road

From the curb to the corner store
His name cuts through that summer air
Little kids yellin' "One more, one more!"
He just grins and points right there
Built from asphalt, built from grind
Built from every missed shot made right
Now every hoop in the neighborhood
Got a story of him tonight

Westside legend, go
Westside legend, go
Put your hands up
Let the whole block know
Westside legend, go
Westside legend, go
He don't chase clout
He just owns that road

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
On the court by the corner store
He was ten toes in, wanted more
White tees, gray shorts, dust on the lane
Shot so clean, made the rim say his name
Backboard shaking when he got loose
Old heads noddin' like, "that's the truth"
Kids on the fence yell, "run it again!"
He'd smile once, then do it again

Hear that crowd go loud
When he rise up now
One bounce, one stare
Then it's up there

Legend on the blacktop
Legend on the blacktop
When he touch that ball
Whole block don't stop
Legend on the blacktop
Legend on the blacktop
Say his name out loud
Let the whole street rock (whole street rock)

He got that fadeaway from the left wing
He got that no-look pass, make the chain link sing
Pops in the front row with a folded chair
Moms at the window like, "boy, go there"
Every summer day, same cracked floor
Every kid around him wanting that score
He'd break ankles, then reach back fast
Then point at the sky when the buzzer passed

Hear that crowd go loud
When he rise up now
One bounce, one stare
Then it's up there

Legend on the blacktop
Legend on the blacktop
When he touch that ball
Whole block don't stop
Legend on the blacktop
Legend on the blacktop

Let the whole street rock (whole street rock)

Now the young ones mimic his step
On the same old paint where the ball got wet
He taught them hustle, taught them pride
Taught them how to stand when the pressure rise

Legend on the blacktop

Whole block don't stop

Say his name out loud

From the fence to the curb

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I keep the phone face-up
By the chipped blue plate
One square of porch light
And a sink full of wait
Your name stays silent
In the glass all night
I hear the hallway creak
Then it dies in white

I know the hour
I know the bruise
I know your promise
Used up the fuse

No ring tonight
No ring tonight
I fold my hands
And hold the night
No ring tonight
No ring tonight
You said you’d call
You said, “I might”

The ash in the saucer
Looks like a map
Of every excuse
You slipped through the trap
I tried to read
From the window seam
But the street was only
A broken dream

I know the sound
Of almost here
It wears your face
Then disappears

No ring tonight
No ring tonight
I fold my hands
And hold the night
No ring tonight
No ring tonight
You said you’d call
You said, “I might”

Maybe you’re laughing
With somebody new
Maybe my number
Got lost in you
Still I leave the lamp on
Still I let it burn
Still I make room
For a voice’s turn

No ring tonight
No ring tonight
I wear your absence
Like a second skin
No ring tonight
No ring tonight
If you ever call
You’ll find me in
No ring tonight
No ring tonight
I folded hope
But it won’t quite shut

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The phone sits up on black glass
Like a coin in a dead man’s hand
I set it by the ashtray
Where your name still knows my stand
The room keeps its small opinions
Taps rain on the window pane
I made tea I never drank there
Now it’s cold in the cup again

I heard a car door slam
I heard my own heart lean
Every floorboard in this place
Keeps saying what you mean

Call me back
Call me back
I’m still here in the dark
Call me back
Call me back
You left a ring in my heart
One more ring
One more ring
Then I’ll let the night win
Call me back
Call me back
Before I fold in

The clock above the doorway
Moves like it’s drawing a blade
I wore your coat for comfort
Then I hung it back in the shade
There’s a lipstick mark on the glass
Where you laughed and turned away
Now the sink holds a silver moon
And I wash your ghost away

I keep the chain unhooked
I keep the porch light low
Every little sound in town
Feels like your footsteps slow

Call me back
Call me back
I’m still here in the dark
Call me back
Call me back
You left a ring in my heart
One more ring
One more ring
Then I’ll let the night win
Call me back
Call me back
Before I fold in

If you found a better hour
If you learned a cleaner lie
Just leave me one small mercy
A minute before goodbye
I can live with almost anything
But this hush that never breaks
This waiting room of midnight
And the ache that awake

Call me back
Call me back
I’m still here in the dark
Call me ba

Like a coin in a dead man’s hand
I set it by the ashtray
Where your name still knows my stand
The room keeps its small opinions
Taps rain on the window pane
I made tea I never drank there
Now it’s cold in the cup again

I heard a car door slam
I heard my own heart lean
Every floorboard in this place
Keeps saying what you mean

Call me back
I’m still here in the dark

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Grandma set the table out
Paper plates in the sun
Uncle turned the music up
Said, "Come on, everybody, run"
Little ones by the cooler
Big pots bubbling mean
Red hands, happy faces
Best Sunday I have seen

Shake that sack
Pour it in
Cayenne dust on my skin
Hear the yard start to sway
We can eat all day

Crawfish boil, family time
Pass that spice, pass that lime
Crawfish boil, shout it loud
Whole house dancing, whole porch proud
Grab a claw, take your seat
Laughing with the folks we meet
Crawfish boil, sing it back
Love tastes good in a paper sack

Cousin shells the potatoes
Auntie checks the corn
Daddy tells that same old joke
That gets us every morn'
Baby steals a lemon wedge
Momma wipes her chin
Ain't nobody leaving early
Not till we all dig in

Shake that sack
Pour it in
Cayenne dust on my skin
Hear the yard start to sway
We can eat all day

Crawfish boil, family time
Pass that spice, pass that lime
Crawfish boil, shout it loud
Whole house dancing, whole porch proud
Grab a claw, take your seat
Laughing with the folks we meet
Crawfish boil, sing it back
Love tastes good in a paper sack

Bring the napkins, bring the grin
Pass the butter, start again
Grandpa taps his tired feet
Everybody feels that heat
When the pot comes down
We don't need much more
Just this crowd, this song, this night
And the yard we all adore

Crawfish boil, family time
Pass that spice, pass that lime
Crawfish boil, shout it loud
Whole house dancing, whole porch proud
Grab a claw, take your seat
Laughing with the folks we meet
Crawfish boil, sing it back
Love tastes good in a paper sack

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Grandma's out by the pot
Dad stirs slow, then stops
Auntie brings the corn
Little cousins grab the forks
Paper spread on the yard
Ice chest sweating in the shade
Your mama laughs so hard
She forgets the spoon she made

Season salt in the air
Everybody coming near
Say it loud, say it proud
Dinner time is now

Crawfish on the table
Pass it round, pass it round
Crawfish on the table
Make that whole yard sound
Eat, laugh, sing along
Stay till the pot is gone
Crawfish on the table
We go all day long

Uncle got the napkins ready
Baby dancin' with a drum
Sister cracks a tail open
Says, "Y'all, this one a hum"
Grandpa tells the same old story
About a storm and a boat
Everybody leans in closer
With the juice on their coat

Hear those boots go tap
On the porch board slap
One more scoop, one more smile
We been here a while

Crawfish on the table
Pass it round, pass it round
Crawfish on the table
Make that whole yard sound
Eat, laugh, sing along
Stay till the pot is gone
Crawfish on the table
We go all day long

When the sun slips low
And the plates get bare
We still got each other
And a pocket full of care
Hand to hand, heart to heart
Let the good times roam
Same old yard, same old love
And it feels like home

Crawfish on the table
Pass it round, pass it round
Crawfish on the table
Make that whole yard sound
Eat, laugh, sing along
Stay till the pot is gone
Crawfish on the table
We go all day long
Crawfish on the table
Right whe

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The buses hiss at dawn
But nobody goes home
A guy sleeps by the gate
On a bench of broken chrome
The bakery's still awake
With flour on her hands
She stares through the steam
Like she understands

One more train
One more deal
One more door
That won't seal
The windows shake
The towers sigh
This whole town
Keeps one eye

This city never sleeps
It only grinds
This city never sleeps
It holds you inside
(holds you inside)
This city never sleeps
It bites down hard
It never sleeps
It never sleeps in the dark

The taxi line is full
Of faces made of smoke
A kid on a corner
Laughs like it's a joke
The river drags its chain
Against the concrete wall
Every red hand flashing
Still can't make it fall

One more job
One more bend
One more name
To pretend
The sirens climb
The alleys lie
This whole town
Keeps one eye

This city never sleeps
It only grinds

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Under the bridge
The rain turns black
A million footsteps
Never looking back
My chest feels hollow
My hands feel steel
If I stop moving
It all gets real

This city never sleeps
It only grinds
This city never sleeps
It holds you inside
(holds you inside)
This city never sleeps
It bites down hard
It never sleeps
It never sleeps in the dark
City of Wires

Streetlights hum a dirty tune
Concrete bones beneath the moon
Engines growl a metal plea
No soft shadows here for me

Tick tock goes the neon clock
Never ever gonna stop

City of wires city of steel
This restless heart it just can't heal
City of wires city of light
Burning through the endless night

Coffee stains on morning faces
Lost souls in their hurried places
Sirens wail a lonely cry
Another day just passing by

Sleep is for the weak they say
Grind it out day

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Snow on the sill
White on the pane
Boots by the door
Sleep in the lane
Kettle gone quiet
Mittens asleep
Your little warm hand
Finds mine to keep

Hush now, hush now
Drift on my coat
Moon in the window
Drowsy and slow

Sleep, little one
Snow day, snow day
Sleep, little one
Drift me away
Snow day, snow day
Soft as a sigh
Sleep, little one
Close your eyes

Tracks in the yard
Gone in an hour
Bread on the stove
Sweet with the flour
Icicles shining
On the porch rail
Your sleepy smile
Fading to pale

Hush now, hush now
Night in your hair
Dream of the pines
Frozen and fair

Sleep, little one
Snow day, snow day
Sleep, little one
Drift me away
Snow day, snow day
Soft as a sigh
Sleep, little one
Close your eyes

If the wind calls
I'll hold the door
If the flakes fall
I'll ask for more
Round and around
This little tune
Snow on the roof
Moon after moon

Sleep, little one
Snow day, snow day
Sleep, little one
Drift me away
Snow day, snow day
Soft as a sigh
Sleep, little one
Close your eyes

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The window wears white lace
Your boots by the door
The world held its breath
And tipped to the floor

Hush now, little one
Hear the roof sigh low
Every branch is bowed
Under morning snow

Snow day, snow day
Sleep a little more
Snow day, snow day
Drift from shore to shore
(oh, snow day)
Dream in sugar light
Snow day, snow day
Till the blue turns white

Tea on the table
Mittens on the rail
Your little red sled
Waits beside the pail

Hush now, little one
Let the day go slow
Even the pine tree
Learns to nod below

Snow day, snow day
Sleep a little more
Snow day, snow day
Drift from shore to shore
(oh, snow day)
Dream in sugar light
Snow day, snow day
Till the blue turns white

If the road is gone
If the yard is deep
I will keep you warm
I will guard your sleep

Snow day, snow day
Sleep a little more
Snow day, snow day
Soft against the door
(oh, snow day)
Dream in sugar light
Snow day, snow day
Till the blue turns white

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
We left by the kudzu
With a truck full of shine
Mama said, “Boy, don’t you
Cross that county line”
Headlights off, tailgate low
Mud on the planks
We laughed at the hill road
Like we owned the bank

Then the siren spun
Like a saw through pine
My buddy yelled, “Buddy, run”
I said, “Not this time”

Copper on the creek
Copper on the creek
We poured it in the water
Then we ran three feet
Copper on the creek
Copper on the creek
What we hauled all night
Went to feed the creek

Broke a tire on a root
Near old Barlow’s bend
Saw a porch light bobbing
At the county end
Sheriff’s dog got a whiff
Of the sweet steam haze
I dropped that jug in the reeds
And I prayed for days

Then the siren spun
Like a saw through pine
My buddy yelled, “Buddy, run”
I said, “Not this time”

Copper on the creek
Copper on the creek
We poured it in the water
Then we ran three feet
Copper on the creek
Copper on the creek
What we hauled all night
Went to feed the creek

Now I don’t sell fire
I just tend my fence
But when the moon gets cloudy
I still smell that mess
Fiddle in the rafters
Boots on the floor
And I hear that deputy
At the cabin door

Copper on the creek
Copper on the creek
We poured it in the water
Then we ran three feet
Copper on the creek
Copper on the creek
What we hauled all night
Went to feed the creek

Mama still laughs
When she tells that tale
Says I learned the hard way
How to lose and bail
Copper on the creek
Copper on the creek
Lord, that backroad lesson
Still runs deep

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the truck bed
In a paper-bag rain
Mason jar rattling
Like a snake in a chain
Moon behind the pines
Road slick as soap
One bad curve ahead
And a gallon full of hope

Sheriff's light was sleeping
Way back on Route Nine
We laughed at danger
Like it had to wait in line

Oh, the Mason jar broke
(oh, the Mason jar broke)
Sugar smoke and heartache
On a red clay road
We ran too hot
We ran too wide
Moonshine run gone wrong
And nobody got home

Tires hit the shoulder
Then the whole rig spun
Chicken wire flew loose
And the lid came undone
White gold on the mud
Like a ghost with a grin
I dove for the jug
And the jug caved in

Buddy yelled "keep movin'"
But his boots found the ditch
Then that back door flung open
And the hills all gave a twitch

Oh, the Mason jar broke
(oh, the Mason jar broke)
Sugar smoke and heartache
On a red clay road
We ran too hot
We ran too wide
Moonshine run gone wrong
And nobody got home

Now the deer all know my name
And the frogs won't let me pass
Every creek bank in this county
Still smells like sugar glass
If you hear a fiddle crying
At the edge of dawn
That's me and that old truck
Trying to get along

Oh, the Mason jar broke
(oh, the Mason jar broke)
Sugar smoke and heartache
On a red clay road
We ran too hot
We ran too wide
Moonshine run gone wrong
And nobody got home

Gimme one more pull
Then let it

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Miles apart we've been dreaming
City lights they've been gleaming
Without you by my side
A lonely endless ride

Now the countdown is done
My heart beats like a drum

Airplane tickets and a kiss
That's what I miss
Airplane tickets and a kiss
Pure bliss
Airplane tickets and a kiss
Oh yeah this

Your voice on the phone a sweet sound
My feet barely touching the ground
Anticipation a burning flame
Whispering your name

Remember those nights under stars so bright
Now we'll make new memories hold you tight
No more goodbyes no more lonely tears
Just you and me banishing all fears

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I kept your voice in a drawer
Next to the ticket stub
Same old paper map
Same worn kiss mark on my cup
Six states and a winter
Between your hands and mine
But every midnight your name
Still crossed my mind

Now the wheels are slowing
And my heart won't stay still
I see your face in the glass
Then I know it’s real

Come home to me
Come home to me
I’ve been missing your hands on my skin
Come home to me
Come home to me
Long road, but you’re here again

Your suitcase by the doorway
Scuffed up from the ride
You laughed like the same old song
I used to keep inside
All that waiting, all that miles
Just to land in your arms
Now the cold feels small
Under your heartbeat charm

Now the door is closing
And the world falls away
Every second we were apart
Fits in what you say

Come home to me
Come home to me
I’ve been missing your hands on my skin
Come home to me
Come home to me
Long road, but you’re here again

Let the distance fade out
Let it wash right off
I don’t need the whole world
Just this room and us
Take your time
Take me in
We can start where we left off
Right here, right now
Begin again

Come home to me
Come home to me
I’ve been missing your hands on my skin
Come home to me
Come home to me
Long road, but you’re here again

Here agai

I don’t need the whole world
Just this room and us
Take your time
Take me in
We can start where we left off
Right here, right now
Begin again

Come home to me
Come home to me
I’ve been missing your hands on my skin
Come home to me
Come home to me
Long road, but you’re here again

Here agai

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I left with a plan in my pocket
Salt on my shoes, still locked in
Mama said, "Go get your blessing"
So I kissed the yard, kept walking
Steel in my chest like a drumline
One heart, one name, one timeline
I watched the shore get smaller
But my dream got louder

Wave after wave
I stayed on my way
Hands to the sky
I was built for the climb

Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
I came for more than a maybe
I came to bring it home
Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
Nothing can break this calling
I’m already on my road

New streets, new faces, same fire
Long nights, one note, one desire
I learned the wind has a language
And it keeps saying, "Don’t tire"
My name on my tongue like thunder
My steps got stronger under
Every door that stayed closed to me
Made the next one wonder

Wave after wave
I stayed on my way
Hands to the sky
I was built for the climb

Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
I came for more than a maybe
I came to bring it home
Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
Nothing can break this calling
I’m already on my road

If I fall, I rise again
If it rains, I dance in it
Far from home, but I know who I am
This dream is mine to finish

Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
I came for more than a maybe
I came to bring it home
Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
Nothing can break this calling
I’m already on my road
Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
Watch me turn this longing
Into gold on gold

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I packed my name in a small white bag
Mama said, "Go, don't look back"
I kissed the floor of my old front step
Then I chased that line where the blue meets black
Salt on my face, fire in my chest
One hand closed, one hand pressed
Every wave said, "Boy, turn around"
I said, "Watch me now, I won't be drowned"

I'm far from home
But I still hear the drum
Heart so loud
Like a kingdom come
One more mile
One more night
I came too far
To dim my light

Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
Dream in my pocket, gold in my soul
Across the ocean, I go

New boots worn by a hundred roads
New tongue learned in the cold
I slept with my hope in a paper coat
Held it tight when the dark wind blew
Some days broke like a stolen plate
Some nights came with a heavy rain
Still I stood where the brave ones stand
With my whole life held in my hand

I'm far from home
But I still hear the drum
Heart so loud
Like a kingdom come
One more mile
One more night
I came too far
To dim my light

Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
Dream in my pocket, gold in my soul
Across the ocean, I go

If the sea tries to test me
I'll answer with my name
If the wind tries to press me
I'll rise again
I came with a prayer
And a face full of hope
Now I sing to the deep
Watch me float

Across the ocean, I go
Across the ocean, I go
Dream in my pocket, gold in my soul
Across the ocean, I go
I go, I go, I go
(Across the ocean, I go)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I met you by the cottonwood
Where the water bites the stone
You had a smile like Sunday
And a knife I should have known
Your dress was dark as mudflats
Your hands were cold and still
We watched the current worry
At the bend beneath the hill

You said, "Come closer, darling"
And I stepped into your spell
The river kept on talking
Like it knew my tale too well

River, keep my name
River, take my shame
(Keep my name)
River, wash it clean
From the bank where you were seen
River, keep my name
River, call her name
(Call her name)
If the reeds remember me
Let them lie beneath the trees

The moon hung low and yellow
On the broken line of birch
We stood where roots were gripping
And the world went still and hurt
I heard the frogs go quiet
I heard my own blood race
Then your shadow leaned across me
Like a black hand on my face

You laughed like cracked glass singing
You laughed like nails in tin
And the river pulled your hemline
As the night came dragging in

River, keep my name
River, take my shame
(Keep my name)
River, wash it clean
From the bank where you were seen
River, keep my name
River, call her name
(Call her name)
If the reeds remember me
Let them lie beneath the trees

Now the years are thin as whiskey
And the frost crawls up the pane
But when the thaw comes roaring
I still hear you in the rain
By the sandbar, by the willow
Where the minnows turn and flee
There’s a shape that waits and watches
And it wears your look on me

River, keep my name
River, take my shame
(Keep my name)
River, wash it clean
From the bank where you were seen
River, keep my name
River, call her name
(Call her name)
If the reeds remember me
Let them lie beneath the trees

Let them lie beneath the trees
Let them lie beneath the trees

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I met him by the water
Where the cypress roots run black
He wore my brother's pocketknife
And never gave it back
The river kept its own old tune
On the rocks below the dock
He said, "Son, don't make me wait out here"
I said, "Then you best start talkin' soft"

The moon was thin as tin
On the branch and on the bay
He laughed like he knew my name
Then he looked the other way

Down by the river
Down by the river
He went under my hand
Down by the river
Down by the river
Now his shadow haunts the sand

Mama scrubbed her hands in lye
Said, "Boy, you got a fever"
But she saw the blood on my cuff
And she knew I had been with him by the river
I told her he had brought it on
I told her he had lied
But the boards above the water
Still remember how he cried

The willow bent its head
Like it heard a church bell ring
I stood there with my breath all gone
And the dark went listening

Down by the river
Down by the river
He went under my hand
Down by the river
Down by the river
Now his shadow haunts the sand

Years came thin, years came hard
The mill closed, the fields turned bone
But every spring when the flood runs high
I hear that low voice moan
It drags my name through cattails
It taps on the split-rail gate
And I swear that water knows my face
Each time it pulls at fate

Down by the river
Down by the river
He went under my hand
Down by the river
Down by the river
Now his shadow haunts the sand
Down by the river
Down by the river
I can hear him call my name
Down by the river
Down by the river
And the water knows my shame

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I put it in the drawer
Like a little black dare
Four missed calls later
I was still right there
My thumb kept twitching
For that familiar glow
But the room got louder
When I told it no

I looked at the clock
Then I let it pass
No red little badges
Could pull me back

Seven day silence
Seven day silence
I can hear my name again
Seven day silence
Seven day silence
Baby, I’m coming back whole

I ate toast at noon
Read the back of the box
Saw a bird on the fence
And I actually stopped
No endless scroll
No disappearing acts
Just my own two feet
On the sidewalk crack

I looked at the clock
Then I let it pass
No red little badges
Could pull me back

Seven day silence
Seven day silence
I can hear my name again
Seven day silence
Seven day silence
Baby, I’m coming back whole

First day, I shook
Second day, I paced
Third day, I laughed
At my own strange face
By day five, the sky
Had room in it again
I don’t need the noise
To know I’m in

Seven day silence
Seven day silence
I can hear my name again
Seven day silence
Seven day silence
Baby, I’m coming back whole

Whole
Whole
Seven day silence
Seven day silence

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I left it in the drawer
Face down, silent, dark
No thumb-scrolling in the line
No little red spark
I missed the map, I missed the joke
I missed your plans at noon
But then I saw my own front porch
And the sky in the room

First day, I shook
Second day, I paced
By day three, I heard
My own damn name

Seven days off
Seven days off
I got my life back on
Seven days off
Seven days off
And I don't miss the ping at all
(Seven days off)

I ate with both hands free
Talked too long with Lee
Found a crooked little path
By the old oak tree
My mind got loud, then clear
My shoes got full of dust
I laughed at things I used to click
Like they were made of rust

First day, I shook
Second day, I paced
By day three, I heard
My own damn name

Seven days off
Seven days off
I got my life back on
Seven days off
Seven days off
And I don't miss the ping at all
(Seven days off)

I watched a whole sunset
Without a single swipe
A real voice said my name
And it felt like life

Seven days off
Seven days off
I got my life back on
Seven days off
Seven days off
And I don't miss the ping at all
(Seven days off)

Put me on the porch
Let the world come in
One week gone
And I'm myself again

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
They ironed my name
into a wedding sheet
Put dates in my mouth
and a hush at my feet
My mother said, “child”
with her eyes on the floor
But I heard my own blood
knocking out of the door

I wore the gold once
like a chain on my throat
I kissed that future
then I watched it float
If I’m a flame, then
let me burn my own way
I was not born
just to kneel and stay

I leave the lace behind
I leave the lace behind
Call my name right now
I’m not yours to bind
I leave the lace behind
I leave the lace behind
Hear my heels strike hard
I choose my own line

The table was set
with a silvered plan
A stranger’s promise
in a careful hand
But I took the road
past the orange trees
With dust on my skirt
and fire in my knees

No more sweet mouth
saying “this is your place”
No more small rooms
closing in my face
If I’m a flame, then
let me rise and sway
I was not born
just to kneel and stay

I leave the lace behind
I leave the lace behind
Call my name right now
I’m not yours to bind
I leave the lace behind
I leave the lace behind
Hear my heels strike hard
I choose my own line

Let them curse my back
let them dim the light
I have my own blood
and I own this night
Not a girl to trade
for a house and ring
I was made for a sky
and a full-throated sing

I leave the lace behind
I leave the lace behind
Call my name right now
I’m not yours to bind
I leave the lace behind
I leave the lace behind
Hear my heels strike hard
I choose my own line

Oye
I choose my own line
Oye
I leave the lace behind

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Mama laid the linen out
Like my life was already sewn
Gold ring on the saucer
And a name I never chose
My auntie said, “Be grateful”
I said, “I know my own name”
I watched the groom in his pressed suit
And felt the whole room change

I heard the spoon in the glass
I heard the floorboards groan
Every blessing in that house
Tasted like a stone
You said, “Stay, stay”
I said, “Watch me go”

I’m leaving your plan
I’m leaving your plan
(ay, ay, ay)
I won’t wear your chain
I won’t wear your chain
(ay, ay, ay)
Let the old door slam
Let the old door slam
My feet know the road
My feet know the road

The dress by the window
Still had chalk on the seam
Like a map of the future
Drawn over my dreams
He called me “his promise”
Like I was property
I bit back the answer
And swallowed the fear in me

The candles burned low
The guests kept on smiling
I tucked my truth in my sleeve
Like a knife I’d been hiding
You said, “Be good”
I said, “I’m not dying”

I’m leaving your plan
I’m leaving your plan
(ay, ay, ay)
I won’t wear your chain
I won’t wear your chain
(ay, ay, ay)
Let the old door slam
Let the old door slam
My feet know the road
My feet know the road

I will not be framed
In your portrait of obedience
I will not bloom
Where they bury my voice in silence
I came with my own fire
I came with my own name
And if the house still wants me
It can learn I’ve changed

I’m leaving your plan
I’m leaving your plan
(ay, ay, ay)
I won’t we

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the glass again
Gray sheet on the street
I let the morning wait
While my socks find heat
Phone face down, no plans
Just a slow blue day
I pull the shade a little
And the room turns soft and gray

Kettle starts to murmur
Steam curls up my sleeve
You can call it wasting time
I call it how I breathe

Rain on the window
Noodles in the bowl
Rain on the window
And I’m fine, I’m whole
(Oh, I’m fine)
Rain on the window
Stay a little while
Rain on the window
Let the whole day smile

Packet torn in half
Hot water to the line
Tiny flakes and curls
Turning plain into mine
I sit by the radiator
Knees tucked to my chest
Three spoonfuls of comfort
And the world can rest

Some days ask for nothing
Some days ask for slow
A spoon, a bowl, your hoodie
And a place to go

Rain on the window
Noodles in the bowl
Rain on the window

I sit by the radiator

Three spoonfuls of comfort
And the world can rest

Some days ask for nothing
Some days ask for slow
A spoon, a bowl, your hoodie
And a place to go

Rain on the window
Noodles in the bowl
Rain on the window

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
If the week was heavy
Let it fall away
I don’t need a reason
To keep this day

Rain on the window
Noodles in the bowl
Rain on the window
And I’m fine, I’m whole
(Oh, I’m fine)
Rain on the window
Stay a little while
Rain on the window
Let the whole day smile
Sunday Noodle Blues

Rain outside my window pane
Another lazy Sunday
Coffee drips a slow slow stain
I think I'll just stay inside today

My phone is dead again
Who needs friends

Instant noodles on the stove
A perfect easy meal
Salty broth a cozy love
That's how I truly feel
Sunday noodle blues
Nothing else to lose

Pajamas on all afternoon
No place I gotta be
Watching clouds drift by the moon
Just ramen and me

Some folks chase the sunshine

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I know that block by name
Cracked curb, bent sign, one light
Boys on the stoop all day
Dreams in a blacked-out pipe
Mama still counts her coins
Rent due before the sleep
I watched the same four walls
Try to bury me deep

But I kept one eye high
Kept one foot out the door
Every dent in that floor
Taught me what I’m here for
If the room stay cold
I make heat in my chest
I been broke, been told
I won’t make it, I guess

I’m making it out
Out that dead-end block
I’m making it out
When the whole thing stops
I’m making it out
With my name intact
I’m making it out
Ain’t no turning back
(out that block)

My cousin said, "stay low"
Then he vanished one June
I learned quick who to trust
When the rent come due
Seen too many good men
Fold to the same old trap
So I kept my hands clean
And my hood on my back
Now the bank ask questions
Like I owe them proof
I just show these scars
Like, "this is the truth"

But I kept one eye high
Kept one foot out the door
Every dent in that floor
Taught me what I’m here for
If the room stay cold
I make heat in my chest
I been broke, been told
I won’t make it, I guess

I’m making it out
Out that dead-end block
I’m making it out
When the whole thing stops
I’m making it out
With my name intact
I’m making it out
Ain’t no turning back
(out that block)

I heard that siren bend
Like a church bell far
I turned pain to plans
And plans to a car
If I make one move
It’s for all of us
Every locked-up night
Built this kind of trust

I’m making it out
Out that dead-end block
I’m making it out
When the whole thing stops
I’m making it out
With my name intact
I’m making it out
Ain’t no turning back
I’m making it out
Yeah, I said it out loud
I’m making it out
From the hard-packed ground
(out that block)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Corner store glass
same cracked sign
same two stoops
same hard time
Ma said, “Keep low
don’t let them see”
I learned that block
could swallow me
Old boys lurk
like busted cars
all talk, no move
under dead street stars
I had a map
made of bad choices
I heard the exit
in my own voice

One more mile
one more night
I been bruised up
but I got light
Hands on the wheel
heart on the line
if I don’t leave now
I’ll never find

I’m gettin’ out that block
I’m gettin’ out that block
I can hear my name
past that dead-end lot
I’m gettin’ out that block
I’m gettin’ out that block
You can keep that pain
I’m takin’ what I’ve got
(Out that block)
(Out that block)

Pay stub thin
rent too high
fried hope smell
in a closed-up sky
Friends still stuck
at the red stop sign
I loved them deep
but I crossed that line
Cold winter nights
same busted heat
same empty fridge
same tired feet
I wore that doubt
like a second skin
then I let it shed
and I stepped in

One more mile
one more night
I been bruised up
but I got light
Hands on the wheel
heart on t

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
We launched into forever
A star-dusted dream
Our love a cosmic river
Or so it did seem

But space it stretches
And silence it grows

Zero gravity heartbreak
No ground beneath our feet
Zero gravity heartbreak
A bitter sweet defeat

Your voice a fading echo
Across the void it flies
My tears in slow motion go
Reflecting starlight in my eyes

We built a world so high
Among the nebulae
Now just a lonely sigh
For

Your voice a fading echo

My tears in slow motion go
Reflecting starlight in my eyes

We built a world so high
Among the nebulae
Now just a lonely sigh
For

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
We shared one window
Over the blue and black
Your glove on my helmet
Then gone in the glass
We counted the stations
The alarms, the spare air
Your laugh in the comms line
Now barely hangs there

I see your light
I see mine
Same cold dark
Different line

Don’t drift away
Don’t drift away
Hold my signal
Hold my name
Don’t drift away
Don’t drift away
We were one flame
Now two flames

My tether is fraying
At the edge of your reach
You chase the bright silence
I fall behind the beat
Your shadow keeps turning
Just out of my hand
Two roads in the vacuum
Two grains in the sand

I see your light
I see mine
Same cold dark
Different line

Don’t drift away
Don’t drift away
Hold my signal
Hold my name
Don’t drift away
Don’t drift away
We were one flame
Now two flames

If you can still hear me
Tap once in the dark
I’m floating in circles
With this crack in my heart
If there’s a way back
Through the white of the void
Say it in numbers
Say it, or stay destroyed

Don’t drift away
Don’t drift away
Hold my signal
Hold my name
Don’t drift away
Don’t drift away
We were one flame
Now two flames

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
We hauled a crate at dawn
From a dock that smelled of tar
Sealed in wire and wax
Stamped with a broken star
Old Mate said, “Don't split it open”
Then the hull began to groan
And the fruit rolled red as embers
In the dark below the foam

One for the rope, boys
Two for the rain
Three for the little yellow devils
Stowed in chains
They sang in the hold
Like a deckhand lost at sea
And every bitten orange
Left salt on my teeth

Ho, cursed oranges!
Roll, cursed oranges!
We ate one slice
And the storm said, “More!”
Ho, cursed oranges!
Bold cursed oranges!
The whole damn hold
Went wild below the floor

The cook cut one clean open
And the rind began to steam
Then the lanterns swung like pendulums
And the bilge turned green
The bosun bit the second
Spat blood into the brine
Said, “I know that sweet smell, mates
That’s trouble dressed as wine”

One for the rope, boys
Two for the mast
Three for the yellow warning
That was nailed to the crate at last
They sang in the hold
When the moon climbed high and thin
And every cursed little segment
Breathed the wind back in

Ho, cursed oranges!
Roll, cursed oranges!
We ate one slice
And the storm said, “More!”
Ho, cursed oranges!
Bold cursed oranges!
The whole damn hold
Went wild below the floor

Then the captain laughed too loud
With juice on his chin
He tossed one overboard
And the sea bit in
Now the waves wear peels
And the gulls won’t leave us be
If you hear sweet fruit in the dark
Keep your hands from the sea

Ho, cursed oranges!
Roll, cursed oranges!
We ate one slice
And the storm said, “More!”
Ho, cursed oranges!
Bold cursed oranges!
We’ll haul ’em home
Then sink them in the shore!

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up at dawn
With oranges in crates
The captain spat and smiled
Said, "This will fix our fate"
But every peel had teeth
And every rind was green
The hold began to hum
Like a beast unseen

O-ho, haul it in
O-ho, hold your breath
One crate fell loose below
And the deck crew swore to death

Cursed orange cargo
Roll it, roll it home
Cursed orange cargo
Salt and peel and bone
(ha!) Cursed orange cargo
Don't let it near the mast
Cursed orange cargo
We should've thrown it fast

By second watch it rotted
Yet smelled like summer rain
A lantern lit the fruit
And drew a face in flame
Old Nibbs bit one in half
Then laughed and turned to blue
He danced three steps, then barked
Like someone else in him knew

O-ho, lock the hatch
O-ho, pray it holds
The sea went glassy black
And the fruit went gold

Cursed orange cargo
Roll it, roll it home
Cursed orange cargo
Salt and peel and bone
(ha!) Cursed orange cargo
Don't let it near the mast
Cursed orange cargo
We should've thrown it fast

A storm came up at midnight
With a whistle in its teeth
The oranges split like moons
And spilled a smell of grief
We sang to keep our courage
We cursed, we spat, we ran
Then one bright orb went overboard
And dragged down half the men

Cursed orange cargo
Roll it, roll it home
Cursed orange cargo
Salt and peel and bone
(hey!) Cursed orange cargo
Now the sea sings back
Cursed orange cargo
In the moonlit black

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You took my hand to shore
Before the morning woke
Your feet knew every stone
Your laugh was weathered oak
You said, "Watch how the water leans"
"Then you will know the way"
I learned to read the sand
By the marks you let it make

Hei, the tide comes in
Hei, the tide rolls out
Everything you love
Learns to move, learns to bow out

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
(Teach me the tide)
How to come and go
Without breaking stride
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
(Teach me the tide)
Hold what I can
Then let it slide

At low water, you would kneel
And name the little things
A crab in a coral cup
A shell with borrowed rings
You said the moon pulls hard
Even when we cannot see
So if my heart feels full
It is only learning, like the sea

Hei, the tide comes in
Hei, the tide rolls out
What you leave in love
Finds its way back around

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
(Teach me the tide)
How to come and go
Without breaking stride
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
(Teach me the tide)
Hold what I can
Then let it slide

Now your sandals rest
By the driftwood and the foam
Your voice is in the wind
When I walk these stones alone
I bend like the palms
When the hard rain passes through
And I hear you in the water say
"Do what the ocean do"

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
(Teach me the tide)
How to come and go
Without breaking stride
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
(Teach me the tide)
Hold what I can
Then let it slide
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
(Teach me the tide)
I will learn your way
When the waves decide

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He shows me the reef line
Like a wrinkle in his hand
Says the water keeps a promise
If you learn how it stands
He points with a cane of kiawe
At the foam on the shore
"Child, the sea is always speaking
You just listen more"

When the moon pulls the harbor
And the sand goes thin
I lean close beside him
Let the lesson in

Teach me the tide, kumu
Teach me the tide
(teach me the tide)
How to leave, how to return
How to turn with the slide
Teach me the tide, kumu
Teach me the tide
(teach me the tide)
So when my feet grow restless
I still know where to go

He remembers every changing
Of the palms in the wind
Every fish that came back home
Every time, every bend
He laughs at my rushing
Says, "Boy, let it breathe"
Even stone learns surrender
When it kneels to the sea

So I watch the dark water
With his old calm eyes
And the whole world softens
When he says, "Don't fight"

Teach me the tide, kumu
Teach me the tide
(teach me the tide)
How to leave, how to return
How to turn with the slide
Teach me the tide, kumu
Teach me the tide
(teach me the tide)
So when my feet grow restless
I still know where to go

He takes my small hand in his
And he presses it there
On the line where the ocean
Kisses rock in prayer
He says, "One day you'll carry
What I carried to you"
And the dusk wraps the shoreline
Like a kūpuna's blue

Teach me the tide, kumu
Teach me the tide
(teach me

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Turnstile snaps shut
My chest goes thin
Packed in the car
Can't get out, can't win
A stranger's elbow
Pins my arm in place
I count the rails
I count my face

One stop more
Then another one
My hands go cold
My legs all wrong
The tunnel bends
Like a dark long wire
I swallow hard
I start to spiral

Breathe in, breathe in
Don't let it crack
Hold the edge
Hold my body back
Breathe in, breathe in
Till the doors slide past
I am here
I am here at last

The map above me
Turns to a blur
My own heartbeat
Loud as a siren stir
A paper bag
Crushed in my fist
I try to smile
But my jaw won't lift
The train shakes hard
Through the underground
I want air
I want the ground

One stop more
Then another one
My hands go cold
My legs all wrong
The tunnel bends
Like a dark long wire
I swallow hard
I start to spiral

Breathe in, breathe in
Don't let it crack
Hold the edge
Hold my body back
Breathe in, breathe in
Till the doors slide past
I am here
I am here at last

If I fall apart
Let it be quiet
If I can't speak
Stay right beside it
The brakes scream out
And the light snaps white
Then the platform opens
Like morning light

Breathe in, breathe in
Don't let it crack
Hold the edge
Hold my body back
Breathe in, breathe in
Till the doors slide past
I am here
I am here at last

Breathe in, breathe in
Till the doors slide past
I am here
I am here at last

The map above me
Turns to a blur
My own heartbeat
Loud as a siren stir
A paper bag
Crushed in my fist
I try to smile
But my jaw won't lift
The train shakes hard
Through the underground
I want air
I want the ground

One stop more
Then another one
My hands go cold
My legs all wrong
The tunnel bends
Like a dark long wire
I swallow hard
I start to spiral

Breathe in, breathe in
Don't let it crack
Hold the edge
Hold my body back
Breathe in, breathe in
Till the doors slide past
I am here
I am here at last

If I fall apart
Let it be quiet
If I can't speak
Stay right beside it
The brakes scream out
And the light snaps white
Then the platform opens
Like morning light

Breathe in, breathe in
Don't let it crack
Hold the edge
Hold my body back
Breathe in, breathe in
Till the doors slide past
I am here
I am here at last

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I’m under the rails
Thumbs locked on my sleeves
The car tastes like metal
And everybody breathes
My chest starts knocking
Like a fist at the door
I count the ads above me
Then I lose the floor

Stop, no, wait
Don’t let it spin
Hands on the seat
I’m slipping in
Too close, too fast
The tunnel bends
My name in my mouth
Won’t come out again

Turnstile panic
Turnstile panic
Hold me to the bar
Turnstile panic
Turnstile panic
I can’t get far (can’t get far)

The train lurch left
My vision breaks in glass
A kid’s red backpack
Keeps moving past
I’ve got cold water
And a bent-up card
Six stops too long
Feels ten miles hard

Stop, no, wait
Don’t let it spin
Hands on the seat
I’m slipping in
Too close, too fast
The tunnel bends
My name in my mouth
Won’t come out again

Turnstile panic
Turnstile panic
Hold me to the bar
Turnstile panic
Turnstile panic
I can’t get far (can’t get far)

Count the bolts
Count the doors
Count my breath
Count once more
I see the sign
Then it blurs away
If I make the next stop
I might stay

Turnstile panic
Turnstile panic
Hold me t

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He got the apron on
By half past eight
Keys in the drawer
And coffee in the crate
Mrs. Bell come in
With a Friday grin
Said, “Fade me low, baby
Tell me who’s been in”

He knows the Deacon’s kid
And the coach’s truck
Who lost that ring
And who’s down on luck
He keeps your story
Under those combs
One chair, one mirror
Feels just like home

And if your heart been loud
He hears that too
One little nod
And he gets it through
He don’t ask twice
He don’t play it tough
He says, “Sit right there
We’re fixin’ you up”

Barber knows the whole town
(whole town)
Barber knows the whole town
(whole town)
He trims your pain
And lifts you up
Barber knows the whole town

Teenage boys come in
Acting way too cool
Old folks lean back
Like they back in school
He cuts that joke
With a steady hand
Makes every head
Feel like a man

And when the room gets slow
He fills it in
With a little laugh
And a better grin
He says, “Hold your chin
Let the hard stuff go”
By the time you leave
You already know

Barber knows the whole town
(whole town)
Barber knows the whole town
(whole town)
He trims your pain
And lifts you up
Barber knows the whole town

On Maple and Pine
He’s the first hello
Every secret heard
Goes soft and slow
When the day runs thin
And the troubles crowd
He makes a clean line
Out of staying proud

Barber knows the whole town
(whole town)
Barber knows the whole town
(whole town)
He trims your pain
And lifts you up
Barber knows the whole town

Mm, he knows
Yeah, he knows
Every face that comes
Every tale it shows
Barber knows the whole town
(whole town)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Mr. Leon got the cape on right
Scissors sing on a Saturday night
Kids on the bench, old men in line
He know your story by the second line
He fade your sides, he fade your pride
He got the whole town on the inside
If your girl left, if your rent came due
He say, "Sit down, son, I'll look at you"

He say, "Baby, take a breath
Let it all settle down"
One chair, one mirror
And the truth comes around

Leon knows the whole town
Leon knows the whole town
He can trim your hair
And lift you off the ground
Leon knows the whole town
Leon knows the whole town
You walk in heavy, baby
You walk out proud

Mama in blue, preacher in gray
They all got a reason to pass his way
He hears the news, he keeps it tight
Still somehow everybody feel all right
He got the tea, he got the time
He got a smile and a perfect line
"Hold your chin up, don't look back"
Next thing you know, your shoulders relax

He say, "Baby, take a breath
Let it all settle down"
One chair, one mirror
And the truth comes around

Leon knows the whole town
Leon knows the whole town
He can trim your hair
And lift you off the ground
Leon knows the whole town
Leon knows the whole town
You walk in heavy, baby
You walk out proud

On Friday night, he locks the door
But the stories live on the floor
From first job dreams to worn-out rings
He heard it all, and he still sings

Leon knows the whole town
Leon knows the whole town
He can trim your hair
And lift you off the ground
Leon knows the whole town
Leon knows the whole town
You walk in heavy, baby
You walk out proud

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I walked the black shore
With snow in my hem
The sea held its breath
Like a child in a den
The pines leaned closer
Their needles were glass
And up in the silence
The sky learned to dance

Oh, did you see it
The veil in the dark?
Oh, did you feel it
A emberless spark?

Skies of yarrow
Skies of yarrow
Pull my name apart
Skies of yarrow
Skies of yarrow
Burn slow in my heart

My mother once said
They were souls on the run
The lost and the longing
The daughters of sun
So I laid down my coat
On the old frozen stone
And listened for footsteps
That could not come home

Oh, did you see it
The veil in the dark?
Oh, did you feel it
A emberless spark?

Skies of yarrow
Skies of yarrow
Pull my name apart
Skies of yarrow
Skies of yarrow
Burn slow in my heart

If I vanish tonight
Let the cold keep my face
Let the green fire find me
And carry my grace

Skies of yarrow
Skies of yarrow
Pull my name apart
Skies of yarrow
Skies of yarrow
Burn slow in my heart
Skies of yarrow
Skies of yarrow
I follow the dark

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
On the ridge by the pine
I stood alone
Snow on my lashes
Salt in my throat
The river was black
Under iron sky
Then the first green fire
Split the night

It came like a veil
Over stone and bone
Slow as a prayer
I never knew
The dogs went still
The horses bowed
And the whole white world
Held its breath

Dance, northern lights
Dance over me
Wrap this old pain
Set it free
Dance, northern lights
Don’t let me sleep
Stay on my name
Stay on me

My mother would say
It is the lost ones
Leading the herd
Home through frost
My father would hush
And tip his cap
As if the sky
Could answer back
I heard them once
In a crack of frost
Not words, not songs
More like a ghost

They turned in ribbons
Above the firs
Green and gold
And raven blue
I raised my hands
To the cold bright wound
And something in me
Opened too

Dance, northern lights
Dance over me
Wrap this old pain
Set it free
Dance, northern lights
Don’t let me sleep
Stay on my name
Stay on me

If I am a lamp
Let me burn low
If I am a road
Let me go

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Black umbrella, tipped up high
Tailor suit and a slow goodbye
Mardi Gras beads on a black lapel
Tiptoe tears in a brass-band spell
We ain’t here to fold and fall
We came to answer when the drums call
Shoes hit pavement, baby, one by one
Even the sad still move to the sun

Hear that roll
Feel that sway
Hold your breath
Then step this way
One more turn
One more time
Lift that chin
And fall in line

Raise it up, raise it up
Black umbrella parade
Raise it up, raise it up
Let the whole block sway
Hey now, hey now
We don’t cry alone
Hey now, hey now
We walk them home

Trumpet crying with a golden edge
Sousaphone leaning off the curbside ledge
Auntie smiling through the folded lace
Baby dancing with a wet-salt face
Mister Pastor at the front of the line
Hands in the air like everything’s fine
Names in rhythm and the snare drum talk
Every heavy heart learns how to walk

Hear that roll
Feel that sway
Hold your breath
Then step this way
One more turn
One more time
Lift that chin
And fall in line

Raise it up, raise it up
Black umbrella parade
Raise it up, raise it up
Let the whole block sway
Hey now, hey now
We don’t cry alone
Hey now, hey now
We walk them home

Slow it down
Let it ring
Let the horn lines bend and sing
Then step back in
With that roll
Love got loud
And grief got soul

Raise it up, raise it up
Black umbrella parade
Raise it up, raise it up
Let the whole block sway
Hey now, hey now
We don’t cry alone
Hey now, hey now
We walk them home
Raise it up, raise it up
Till the corner sings
Raise it up, raise it up
And the brass band swings
