Say my name
But it stays still
So I breathe in
And I let go

Last box taped up tight
Last box by the door
Last box in the truck
And the room is yours no more
Last box taped up tight
I can’t stay here tonight
Last box taped up tight
I’m finally outside
(Last box taped up tight)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He got chalk on his sleeve
And a tape on his chest
Doorbell ring at noon
He know the block need best
Blue jeans hemmed clean
Jacket cut mean
Took Mr. Lee from broke
Made him look like a king

Seven suits in a row
On a clothesline swing
He don't just stitch fabric
He fix everything

Step in, baby, step in
Let him take your size
Pin that waist, slick cuffs
Watch the whole street rise

Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
(He dress the whole block)
Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
(He make it all pop)
Tailor on the block

Mrs. Diaz came by
With a coat too long
He took two inches off
Now she walking strong
Kids need patchwork knees
He do it overnight
Even old man Joe
Got his collar sitting right
He got the good thread saved
In a coffee tin
If your week came frayed
He can stitch you back in

Step in, baby, step in
Let him work that cloth
One quick fit, one sharp grin
Got the whole block tossed

Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
(He dress the whole block)
Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
(He make it all pop)
Tailor on the block

From the laundromat line
To the corner stoop
Everybody want a cut
And he got the scoop
He takes the rough days
Turns them crisp and neat
When he snaps that waistline
The whole block move to the beat

Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
(He dress the whole block)
Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
(He make it all pop)
Tailor on the block

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Yo
Check the stitch
The cut
The gleam (oo-yeah!)
My threads so fresh
They a vibrant dream
Walkin' down the block
Heads start to turn
For the tailor man
Lessons they gon' learn

I got the fabric of the funk
Baby
The style so deep
Dressin' up the whole darn block while the rhythm just creeps (uh-huh!)
From the fedora to the shoe
Every seam is tight
I got the fabric of the funk
Shinin' oh so bright!

Miss Rosie needs a gown
Shimmerin' in the light (shine on!)
Old Man Jenkins wants his Sunday suit
Lookin' just right
Little Timmy got his first dance
Lookin' sharp and new
Yeah
I got the needle and thread
Makin' dreams come true

I got the fabric of the funk
Baby
The style so deep
Dressin' up the whole darn block while the rhythm just creeps (can't stop!)
From the fedora to the shoe
Every seam is tight
I got the fabric of the funk
Shinin' oh so bright!

Who's the man with the plan
The master of the thread?
(You know it!)
Who's makin' sure you look good from your toes to your head?
(That's me!)
Sewin' up the soul
Stitchin' up the groove
Watch the whole block move! (Get down!)

I got the fabric of the funk
Baby
The style so deep
Dressin' up the whole darn block while the rhythm just creeps (so funky!)
From the fed

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
At seven, I found you
Face turned to the wall
Your hands kept on moving
But you never lost the fall
Dust on your numbers
Cold on the glass
Every last second
Went back to the past

Tell me where it goes
When the minute unwinds
Tell me why your heart
Keeps beating behind

Backward clock, take me home
Backward clock, take me home
I can hear my old name
From the room below
Backward clock, spin again
Backward clock, spin again
Every second I lost
Comes back in the rain

You showed me December
Inside a June moon
A broken white teacup
And a tune out of tune
Moths in the hallway
Footsteps in ash
I held your face still
You slipped through the past

Tell me what you know
Under all that wood
Tell me if the dead
Remember the good

Backward clock, take me home
Backward clock, take me home
I can hear my old name
From the room below
Backward clock, spin again
Backward clock, spin again
Every second I lost
Comes back in the rain

If I turn the key
Will the hours kneel?
Will your quiet hands
Tell me what to feel?
I was never brave
I was just there
Watching you unmake
The air

Backward clock, take me home
Backward clock, take me home
I can hear my old name
From the room below
Backward clock, spin again
Backward clock, spin again
Every second I lost
Comes back in the rain
Backward clock, keep me here
Backward clock, keep me here
Till the last small light
Learns how to disappear

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
At six, the room went still
Your coat hung in the hall
The clock above the sink
Started climbing the wall
Its brass hands lost their nerve
They turned from right to left
And every chime it made
Sounded like a breath

I watched the minute marks
Crawl back into their graves
Your name on the wood frame
Kept fading with the waves
And I said, stay
Stay where I left you
But the numbers fell
Like rain through a broken roof

Backward hands, backward hands
Take me where you ran
Backward hands, backward hands
Undo me if you can
(Backward hands)
(Backward hands)

The kettle cooled to black
The candle burned in blue
The needles on the dial
Were stitching through the moon
I found your empty chair
Facing the open door
Each second backed away
Till it was here no more

I heard the wood creak low
Like someone in the walls
The hour that I lost
Kept answering my calls
And I said, stay
Stay where I left you
But the numbers fell
Like rain through a broken roof

Backward hands, backward hands
Take me where you ran
Backward hands, backward hands
Undo me if you can
(Backward hands)
(Backward hands)

Now midnight walks in reverse
Through every crack and seam
The clock is almost kind
It gives me back the dream
One by one, the days
Fold into your face
If time can learn to kneel
Let it kneel in place

Backward hands, backward hands
Take me where you ran
Backward

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Got a broomstick bass
And a washtub grin
Old banjo crooked
But it jumps right in
Milk-pail drum
With a paper-thin crack
One box fiddle
And a shoe box stack

Come on, lean in close
Hear the whole porch sway
Every busted thing
Found a brighter way

Porch full of odds
Porch full of cheer
Play what you got
Bring what you hear
Porch full of odds
Make a whole band sing
Mismatched hands
Make the rafters ring

Soda bottle blue
With a mouth-blown moan
Washboard zipper
Keeps that rhythm known
Tin cup chimes
On a split-rail beat
Barefoot stamp
On the summer heat

Come on, lean in close
Hear the whole porch sway
Every busted thing
Found a brighter way

Porch full of odds
Porch full of cheer
Play what you got
Bring what you hear
Porch full of odds
Make a whole band sing
Mismatched hands
Make the rafters ring

Grandma claps once
Dog howls along
Uncle on the jug
Blows a laughing song
If it squeaks
If it bends
That’s the part
That makes good friends

Porch full of odds
Porch full of cheer
Play what you got
Bring what you hear
Porch full of odds
Make a whole band sing
Mismatched hands
Make the rafters ring

Whoa-oh-oh
On that old front step
Whoa-oh-oh
Best mess we ever kept

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
There’s a dented tub bass hummin’
By the screen door in the shade
A banjo leans on a milk crate
Like it came to hear us play
One old jug’s got the wobble
One tin can’s got the shine
And that washboard keeps the rhythm
Like it’s been there all its life

Come on, pull up a chair
Get your hands in the air
If it’s crooked, if it’s homemade
We can make it ring right here

Porch full of mismatched, hey
Porch full of mismatched (hey!)
Tap your toe, let it sway
Porch full of mismatched, hey
Porch full of mismatched
Every odd sound feels okay
(Feels okay)

A fiddle with a busted corner
Still can sing a silver line
Spoons are clackin’ in my pocket
Like a packet of sunshine
Mabel drums on a biscuit tin
Jesse blows that homemade horn
Even Grandpa’s chair is shakin’
Like it’s never been this born

Come on, pull up a chair
Get your hands in the air
If it’s crooked, if it’s homemade
We can make it ring right here

Porch full of mismatched, hey
Porch full of mismatched (hey!)
Tap your toe, let it sway
Porch full of mismatched, hey
Porch full of mismatched
Every odd sound feels okay
(Feels okay)

Let the moon hang low
Let the dogs bark once
We got bottles, boards, and rattle
And a dozen ways to dance
If you got a beat in your pocket
Bring it up and set it free
This whole porch was built for joyful
Little accidents like me

Porch full of mismatched, hey
Porch full of mismatched (hey!)
Tap your toe, let it sway
Porch full of mismatched, hey
Porch full of mismatched
Every odd sound feels okay
(Feels okay)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Four AM
Cold steel moans
Wind bites deep
Empty lanes

Concrete veins
Across the black
This bridge it screams
No turning back

Headlights blur
Flicker out
Another ghost
Another doubt

Concrete veins
Across the black
This bridge it

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
At four, the bridge breathes slow
Steel ribs, rain on the rail
My hands leave white on the bars
Cold coin in my coat pocket
The river keeps its own name
Under the black plank sky

I came to stand
Not to go
I came to hear
What won't let go

Hold me, iron bridge
Hold me, iron bridge
Under my feet
Nothing but the deep
(hold me) (hold me)

One yellow truck passes once
Then the dark gets bigger
A shoe print fills with water
Then disappears at the edge
Your last words keep pace with me
Three steps behind, three steps back

I came to stand
Not to go
I came to break
And not fall through

Hold me, iron bridge
Hold me, iron bridge
Under my feet
Nothing but the deep
(hold me) (hold me)

The bolts keep their old prayers
The wind learns my name
I lean over, I listen
For a room down below
For a door in the water
For the day to arrive

Hold me, iron bridge
Hold me, iron bridge
Under my feet
Nothing but the deep
(hold me) (hold me)

Four a.m.
Still here
Four a.m.
Still here

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You handed me a page
Still wet with winter ink
I learned your crooked lines
Before I learned to think
Your commas felt like rain
On the bridge I walked alone
I turned your quiet mouth
Into something I could hold

Line by line
I found your pulse
Under mine

I keep your words
I keep your words
Like a light in my hands
I keep your words
I keep your words
And I understand
(Oh, I understand)
(Oh, I understand)

At the café by the station
You traced a jagged star
I said it three ways over
You laughed and said I’m far
Then you wrote my name
On a napkin, soft and slow
I felt my throat go still
Like a door I couldn’t close

Line by line
You crossed my life
And stayed inside

I keep your words
I keep your words
Like a light in my hands
I keep your words
I keep your words
And I understand
(Oh, I understand)
(Oh, I understand)

You spoke of broken cities
I heard a private sun
Every phrase you gave me
Made my careful heart undone
If love is just a window
Then let me be the glass
I’ll catch the way you tremble
And let your meaning last

I keep your words
I keep your words
Like a light in my hands
I keep your words
I keep your words
And I understand
I keep your words
I keep your words
Now you know where I stand
(Oh, I understand)
(Oh, I understand)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I sat with your page
Like a room with one lamp
Turned your hard little silence
Into something I can hold
Your commas felt like footsteps
On a wooden floor at dawn
I kept finding your meaning
Where your ink ran thin and strong

And I knew every line
Was a door to your chest
So I leaned in closer
To learn what you meant

I fall between your lines
Between your lines, I fall
You say it plain, I hear gold
You say goodnight, I want more
Between your lines
Between your lines, I fall

You wrote of rain on aprons
Of a train that shook the west
Of a mother at the window
And a name you couldn’t keep
I turned your ache to language
For the people in the hall
But the truest thing I carried
Was the way you said it all

Every word had a pulse
Every pause had a face
I was mapping your heartbeat
In the margins of the page

I fall between your lines
Between your lines, I fall
You say it plain, I hear gold
You say goodnight, I want more
Between your lines
Between your lines, I fall

If I mistranslate forever
Let it be this tender way
I could lose the perfect meaning
Just to keep your breath in play
Say it once, and I’ll follow
Say it soft, and I stay
I was never just the reader
I was learning you that way

I fall between your lines
Between your lines, I fall
You say it plain, I hear gold
You say goodnight, I want more
Between your lines
Between your lines, I fall

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Dicen que cruza la sierra
con sombrero y cicatriz
no presume su apellido
pero paga hasta el frijol
Si le aprietan a un humilde
se le enciende el corazón
trae la ley en la mirada
y la noche en el cinturón
Va con polvo en las botas
y una manta en el calor
nunca toca lo que no es suyo
ni le tiembla el valor
Si promete, cumple entero
si da mano, da de más
pero al que venda a un amigo
lo borra sin mirar atrás

No se vende
No se arrodilla
Si hace trato
lo firma con sangre fría

Código de fuego
código de honor
(código de fuego)
No traigo miedo
traigo dirección
Si caigo, caigo
de frente y con razón
(código de fuego)
código de honor

En la troca va callado
con la frente al vendaval
no se cuelga de grandezas
ni se cree de mármol ya
En la mesa deja doble
si el compadre dio el pan
y si ve llorar a una madre
se le quiebra el metal
Una vez le ofrecieron oro
por entregar a un rival
él soltó una sola frase:
“yo no juego a traicionar”
Y en la boca de la sierra
se oyó fuerte al rematar
que el hombre vale por su sello
y no por lo que alzar

él miró la cruz del pecho
y no se quiso quebrar
“Yo no cargo con basura
ni le debo al caporal
si me buscan por mis pasos
me conocen al pasar”

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Nació donde el cerro arde
con un rezo y un puñal
su madre le dijo: “m’hijo,
la palabra es tu señal”
anda fino en la trocha seca
con la cara en el vendaval
si promete, cumple pronto
si falla, no vuelve a hablar
Lleva botas con lodo viejo
y un sombrero de costal
en la hebilla va su nombre
y en la mente, el paternal
no presume de su sombra
ni se vende por metal
pero al que cruza su línea
le enseña respeto total

No roba al pobre
no toca al fiel
si da su mano
la firma es él
su ley es dura
su voz, un relámpago
si dice “vamos”
se va con honor

Código del fuego
código del fuego
manda su pecho, no el miedo
código del fuego
código del fuego
paga la deuda primero

Dicen que entra por la sierra
cuando ruge el temporal
deja huellas en la arena
y ni el polvo lo hace hablar
si una madre pide auxilio
él la deja pasar
si un niño cruza llorando
le abre un rato el portal
En la mesa no hay mentira
ni tequila de más
si juró sobre su padre
lo sostiene hasta el final
y al traidor que vende hermanos
lo borra del camino ya
porque el hombre sin palabra
es más frío que un alacrán

No roba al pobre
no toca al fiel
si da su mano
la firma es él
su ley es dura
su voz, un relámpago
si dice “vamos”
se va con honor

Código del fuego

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hummin low
Taxi cabs glow
Another day done
Another race won
Or lost who cares
The city it stares

My stomach it rumbles
My mind it fumbles

Neon Noodle Night
Everything feels right
Bright lights callin
Never fallin
Neon Noodle Night
Shining so bright

Pink glow a-flashin
Blue lines a-dashin
Sign says eat here
No room for fear
Just hungry thoughts
Untying all knots

Steam rises high
Reaching for the sky
Chop
Midnight Ramen Run

Glass doors slide open slow
Steam crawls out to the rain
Your laugh in the window glow
I count your coins again
A red sign hums above us
Your chopsticks tap in time

We missed the last train
So let the night stay
Keep the bowl warm
Keep your hand in mine

We missed the last train

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Late-night ramen, stay a little longer
Late-night ramen, make it taste stronger
Save me that seat
Stay close to me
Late-night ramen, stay a little longer

Paper cups on the counter
Salt on your bottom lip
A silver spoon in my hand
And your sleeve at my wrist
The chef calls out one order
You smile like you know me

We missed the last train
So let the night stay
Keep the bowl warm
Keep your hand in mine

Late-night ramen, stay a little longer
Late-night ramen, make it taste stronger
Save me that seat
Stay close to me
Late-night ramen, stay a little longer

Outside, the puddles mirror
That red and gold above
You lean in, softly grinning
Like this was always us
One more sip
One more glance
One more reason not to leave

Late-night ramen, stay a little longer
Late-night ramen, make it taste stronger
Save me that seat
Stay close to me
Late-night ramen, stay a little longer
Late-night ramen (stay a little longer)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
They pulled the posters down at noon
From the feed store window glass
Dust on the ticket booth roof
Like the fair had gone to grass
The ring toss sat in a crate
Milk bottles in a box
And the old barker's chair
Leaning back by the locks

Mama said, "Don't look long"
But I could not move on
With the sun on that empty lot
And the canopy all gone

Last Ferris wheel
Last cotton candy sky
Last time we heard that midway call
Fade out and say goodbye
Last Ferris wheel
Turn slow in my mind
(last Ferris wheel)
The county fair is closing
For good this time

The painted horses lost their shine
Gum stuck under every seat
A prize ribbon in the mud
A pink stuffed bear with one glass eye
The corndog stand is rolled up
Grease gone cold in the pan
Even the bingo tent poles
Are stacked in a farmer's van

Kids still ask, "Where'd it go?"
Like it skipped town overnight
But the sheriff's sign on the gate
Says the lease won't make it right

Last Ferris wheel
Last cotton candy sky
Last time we heard that midway call
Fade out and say goodbye
Last Ferris wheel
Turn slow in my mind
(last Ferris wheel)
The county fair is closing
For good this time

I can still hear the speaker hum
Over gravel, over rain
See your hand in mine at dusk
As the blue bulbs flicked like trains
Now the lot just holds the wind
And a bent-up rusted rail
If memories had tickets left
I'd buy one for this trail

Last Ferris wheel
Last cotton candy sky
Last time we heard that midway call
Fade out and say goodbye
Last Ferris wheel
Turn slow in my mind
(last Ferris wheel)
The county fair is closing
For good this time
For good this time

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
They took down the tents today
Folded up the cotton candy stand
Sawdust gone from the midway lane
Just tire tracks in the gravel sand
Old Hank locked the prize booth door
Tilted his cap and stared at ground
Said, "Ain’t much left to pack no more"
When the last few lights went out

You could hear the metal groan
You could hear the empty spaces
Where the laughing used to run
Past the painted faces

Last Ferris wheel
Turning in my mind
Last Ferris wheel
Leave the county behind
(Leave it behind)
Last Ferris wheel
Slow, then gone for good
We were young that August
Now it’s splinters in the wood

The pie-eating ribbon faded red
Against a post with peeling paint
Little shoes in the mud outside
Where the church ladies bought their cane
Mabel swept the ticket slits
Like she could sweep the years away
And the ferris cage swung once
In the wind that never stayed

You could hear the metal groan
You could hear the empty spaces
Where the laughing used to run
Past the painted faces

Last Ferris wheel
Turning in my mind
Last Ferris wheel
Leave the county behind
(Leave it behind)
Last Ferris wheel
Slow, then gone for good
We were young that August
Now it’s splinters in the wood

I walked the edge of the parking lot
Past the prize fish in the rain
Past the faded map of games and darts
Past a hand-lettered "closed" sign frame
And I swear I heard one distant ring
One bell in the darkened air
Like the fair was trying to call us back
But nobody was there

Last Ferris wheel
Turning in my mind
Last Ferris wheel
Leave the county behind
(Leave it behind)
Last Ferris wheel
Slow, then

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Badge on the bench
Key in the tray
Same gray floor
Same dead wage
Palm full of grease
Back full of ache
One more punch
For old time's sake
Clock face staring
Like it knows my name
I kept showing up
For a rigged-up game

Count me out
Count me down
Hear that click?
I'm done now
Hands shake once
Then they go still
I won't crawl
I won't kneel

Clock out, clock out
For the last time
Clock out, clock out
Leave it all behind
No more line
No more lie
Clock out, clock out
I'm done tonight (done tonight)

Coffee ring
On a stained-up sheet
Boss kept talking
I heard my teeth
Burnt-out boots
By the metal door
I've been half-dead
Since the winter storm
They want my hours
They want my spine
Want my whole life
To punch in on time
But I got one breath
And I got one choice
I found my voice
In a broken voice

Count me out
Count me down
Hear that click?
I'm done now
Hands shake once
Then they go still
I won't crawl
I won't kneel

Clock out, clock out
For the last time
Clock out, clock out
Leave it all behind

Let the siren scream
Let the doors slam shut
I was made of fire
You made me rust
I don't need your hand
I don't need your chain
I don't need your name
To say I changed

One last punch
One last scan
One last breath
Then I am gone
Take that card
Take that key
Take your whole damn
Machine from me

Last punch

Last punch

Last punch

Boss man's sneer a dull echo
His words they sting no more
My spirit starts to grow

They said I was a cog a wheel a drone
But I got

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Midnight shadows stretch and creep
Across the marble floor so deep
He walks his rounds a lonely beat
Past painted faces bittersweet
He sees her there in gilded frame
She whispers secrets speaks his name

A silent gaze
A timeless maze

Oh Mona Lisa she just smiles
Through endless days and endless miles
He dreams of colors bold and bright
Under the museum's pale moonlight
His canvas heart
Torn apart

The velvet ropes they keep him out
He paces with a silent doubt
Her eyes they follow every step
A secret vow he's always kept
He knows her brushstrokes every line
This painted love so truly divine

They say she's just a work of art
But she's stolen pieces of his heart
A masterpiece of human grace
Trapped in time in that lonely place
He yearns to touch to feel her near

This painted love so truly divine

They say she's just a work of art

A masterpiece of human grace
Trapped in time in that lonely place
He yearns to touch to feel her near

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Oh Mona Lisa she just smiles
Through endless days and endless miles
He dreams of colors bold and bright
Under the museum's pale moonlight
The Guard and the Girl

Fluorescent lights hum a low lonely tune
Another Tuesday night under the pale plastic moon
She stares from the canvas a secret in her eyes
I walk these polished floors beneath the museum skies

Always watching
Never touching

Oh Mona Lisa smile so sweet so sly
I'm just a guard until the day I die
You're framed in gold I'm stuck in time
A masterpiece forever mine

The cleaning crew ghosts through the halls with their mops
My coffee gets cold my weary heart stops
I imagine your world beyond this glass case
A whisper of color a forgotten embrace

They say art speaks to souls but what does it say to mine
Just a silent echo a forgotten sign
Maybe tomorrow maybe never again
I'll see your

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
When the last train sighs
And the big clock blinks
I push through the screen door
For a plate and a wink
The pie sits shining
By the coffee urn
You know I come here
When my hard days burn

And the cook just nods
Like he knows my name
The whole room glows
In a sugar flame

Blue Plate Midnight
Stay open, stay bright
Blue Plate Midnight
Till morning light
Blue Plate Midnight
You hold me right
(Blue Plate Midnight)

Old red booths hold
Every busted plan
A tired trucker laughs
At the counter fan
There’s a girl in the corner
With a nickel ring
She taps her spoon
And starts to sing

And the jukebox hums
Like a sleepy train
I take one more sip
And forget my rain

Blue Plate Midnight
Stay open, stay bright
Blue Plate Midnight
Till morning light
Blue Plate Midnight
You hold me right

When the last train sighs
And the big clock blinks
I push through the screen door
For a plate and a wink
The pie sits shining
By the coffee urn
You know I come here
When my hard days burn

And the cook just nods
Like he knows my name
The whole room glows
In a sugar flame

Blue Plate Midnight
Stay open, stay bright
Blue Plate Midnight
Till morning light
Blue Plate Midnight
You hold me right
(Blue Plate Midnight)

Old red booths hold
Every busted plan
A tired trucker laughs
At the counter fan
There’s a girl in the corner
With a nickel ring
She taps her spoon
And starts to sing

And the jukebox hums
Like a sleepy train
I take one more sip
And forget my rain

Blue Plate Midnight
Stay open, stay bright
Blue Plate Midnight
Till morning light

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The screen door sighs at two
Coffee in a chipped white cup
Grease on the counter shines
And the pie is all dressed up
The man in the apron grins
Says, “Sit, you look beat down”
I park my coat on the chair
This place never closes down

Oh, the clock hands spin
But they don’t win
Not here
No, not here

This diner stays open all night
This diner stays open all night
When your heart needs a booth
And your eyes need light
This diner stays open all night
(stays open all night)

A trucker hums by the glass
A kid with a borrowed dime
The jukebox coughs out a slow one
And the sugar clumps just fine
You pour me one more refill
Like you know what I came for
A seat, a grin, a warm plate
And a room that asks for more

Oh, the clock hands spin
But they don’t win
Not here
No, not here

This diner stays open all night
This diner stays open all night

This diner stays open all night
(stays open all night)

This diner stays open all night
This diner stays open all night
When the world feels too wide
And the road runs dry
This diner stays open all night
This diner stays open all night
(stays open all night)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I left on a moonless tide
With salt on my hands and a knot in my coat
He said, “Boy, keep your head up high
The sea learns your name by the lines you hold”
I found my chart in the dark
Not on paper, but in the sky’s cold breath
Every spark had a path
Every path had a price I could never guess

And when the waves got tall
I’d lift my eyes
Read the silver marks
In the blackened sky
I could hear the north wind call
Through the rigging lines
Saying, “Hold that light
You’ll be fine”

I read the stars
I read the stars
They spell my home
Across the dark
I read the stars
I read the stars
And when I drift
They lead me back

There was ice on the rail at dawn
And gulls like knives in the open blue
I had your letter folded close
With a little map of the life we knew
The lantern swayed in the hold
While the crew sang low through the biting spray
But I kept one eye on the crown
Of the night, where the old roads stayed

And when the storm came down
Like a breaking door
I held the wheel
And asked for more
Then a pale bright thread
Cut through the gray
I saw my name
In the Milky Way

I read the stars
I read the stars
They spell my home
Across the dark
I read the stars
I read the stars
And when I drift
They lead me back

Some men trust the shore
Some trust the maps
I trust the fire
That never collapses
If I’m lost tonight
I’m not alone
The sky is a compass
And the compass knows

I read the stars
I read the stars
They spell my home
Across the dark
I read the stars
I read the stars
And when I drift
They lead me back
I read the stars
I read the stars
Till morning comes
And pulls me back

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Ocean wide a dark canvas
Diamonds scattered bright
He steers by ancient whispers
Guiding through the night

Wind howls a lonely tune
He feels it in his bones

Star sailor star sailor
Eyes to the sky
Star sailor star sailor
Never asks why

Charts are just old paper
The real map above
Every flicker every shimmer
A story of love

He dreams of distant shores
Beyond the ocean's roar
A silent promise whispered
Forevermore

Star sailor star sailor
Eyes to the sky
Star sailor star

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You took the left side.
I took the right.
Same floor, same glare,
same cut of the light.
Our hands meet once,
like a match on steel.
Smile for the room,
but we both know the deal.

One step close.
One step back.
Your eyes say war.
Mine say track.
The band counts down,
the floor turns thin.
If I slip now,
you still win.

Dance with me, rival
dance with me
Heel to heel
and let it bleed
Dance with me, rival
dance with me
We can’t leave
so make it clean

Your jaw is set
like a locked front gate.
My ring on your wrist
keeps perfect weight.
We turn in circles,
sharp as knives.
Every little bow
keeps score of lives.
You pull, I yield,
then pull you near.
That’s not forgiveness—
that’s survival here.

One step close.
One step back.
Your breath hits first.
I bite it back.
The room goes still,
the floor gets tight.
If we must burn,
we burn in time.

Dance with me, rival
dance with me
Heel to heel
and let it bleed

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Now the music thins.
Now the room can see
all the old blood
between you and me.
You were my match,
I was your scar.
Look at us now—
still circling hard.

Dance with me, rival
dance with me
Heel to heel
and let it bleed
Dance with me, rival
dance with me
If we fall down
we fall with teeth
Dance of Rivals

Eyes meet across the room (ooh-yeah!)
A silent challenge
Doom
We stand apart
So far
Underneath the star

My heart beats fast
It will not last

Dance with me
Rival
Dance
One last chance
Rival
Chance
Close to me
Rival
Close
Nobody knows
Rival
Knows

Your hand in mine
So cold
A story to unfold
We turn
We sway
So tight
Lost in the night
The night

Two shadows on the floor
Want

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
She stamps your name at noon
With a smile like a porch light
Knows the book you hid
When your hands shook in the spring rain
She keeps your secrets safe
Between the atlas and the hardbacks
And when you won't speak up
She already knows your pain

She heard it in your silence
She saw it in your face
Every folded corner
Every line you couldn't place

Ask the librarian
She knows your story
(oh, she knows)
Ask the librarian
She keeps it for you
In the dust and glory
In the little things you do
Ask the librarian
She knows your story

There’s the boy with the train set
And the girl with the red scarf
The mason after dark
Who only reads by the back wall
The old man with the weathered hands
Borrowing love poems again
She finds the right page
Like she’s been there all along

She hears what you’re not saying
Between the pages turned
A kind word, a quiet seat
And a lesson gently learned

Ask the librarian
She knows your story
(oh, she knows)
Ask the librarian
She keeps it for you
In the dust and glory
In the little things you do
Ask the librarian
She knows your story

When the town feels heavy
And the road runs thin
She lights up the front desk
And lets the lost ones in
No grand speech, no thunder
Just a name and a place
And she puts your heart
Back on the shelf in grace

Ask the librarian
She knows your story
(oh, she knows)
Ask the librarian
She keeps it for you
In the dust and glory
In the little things you do
Ask the librarian
She knows your story

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
She knows your name by spine and dust
Your secrets tucked in maple shelves
The shy ones come in after school
The loners leave a little more themselves

She says, "Sit down, stay awhile"
And hands you back your brave
One story leads to another
Like keys in a wooden tray

She holds your story
She keeps it safe
(holds your story)
She knows the ache
She holds your story
In quiet hands
(holds your story)
And understands

Old Mr. Bell lost his wife in June
So she finds him poems by the sea
The girl with paint on both her wrists
Gets books on wolves and running free

She marks the page with a yellow note
And smiles like she already knows
The broken, funny, hard-earned parts
The things we never say in rows

She holds your story
She keeps it safe
(holds your story)
She knows the ache
She holds your story
In quiet hands
(holds your story)
And understands

When rain taps hard on the front glass
And the town feels small and thin
She lights the desk lamp by the returns
And lets the lost come in

She holds your story
She keeps it safe
(holds your story)
She knows the ache
She holds your story
In quiet hands
(holds your story)
And understands

So if you need a place to rest
Or someone who can name the bend
Go down where the catalog glows
She's there, till closing, every end

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Shoes in my hand, stained with rain
Your laugh still ringing through my brain
The room was wild, the floor was glass
Now every heartbeat starts to pass
Cold air bites my face outside
Last drink gone, but I feel alive
My friends look gold in the parking lot
We lost the night, but we lost it hot

And the sky
Starts to turn
Soft and slow
Let it burn
Hands up now
Don’t look down
We made it through
Feel that sound

Here comes the sun
Here comes the sun
We made it, we made it
Into the morning
Here comes the sun
Here comes the sun
Hold me in this moment
Hold me in the morning

Taxi windows, fog and steam
Your head on my shoulder, half a dream
Street signs fade in a silver glow
We were lost, but we both know
Every bruise and every scar
Led us here to who we are
Night fell hard, but it couldn’t stay
Now the whole world’s turning day

And the sky
Starts to break
Golden lines
On your face
One more breath
One more mile
We come back
Alive, alive

Here comes the sun
Here comes the sun
We made it, we made it
Into the morning
Here comes the sun
Here comes the sun
Hold me in this moment
Hold me in the morning

All the silence after the noise
All our tired, broken joys
Watch that color flood the cold
Watch the dark let go, let go
If we fall, we fall in light
If we burn, we burn tonight
Then we rise with shaky hands
New day pulling on the land

Here comes the sun
Here comes the sun
We made it, we made it
Into the morning
Here comes the sun
Here comes the sun
Hold me in this moment
Hold me in the morning
Here comes the sun
Here comes the sun
We made it, we made it
Into the morning

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Shoelaces tied on the floor
Wristband bent in my hand
Cold air on my jacket
Dawn is pulling up the land
Mascara on the collar
Taxi salt on my skin
I was lost in the noise
Now the sky is letting me in

And the windows start to burn
Gold on the empty curb
My heart won't stay down
It remembers how to turn

Sunrise, take me higher
Sunrise, take me home
Sunrise, you're the fire
That pulls me back to my bones
Sunrise, take me higher
Sunrise, don't let go
Sunrise, I'm alive now
(I'm alive now)

Bottle caps in the gutter
Heel marks by the door
A paper cup, half empty
But I don't need that anymore
Your laugh still in my pocket
Like a coin I didn't spend
I was gone for the whole night
Now I come back again

And the windows start to burn
Gold on the empty curb
My heart won't stay down
It remembers how to turn

Sunrise, take me higher
Sunrise, take me home
Sunrise, you're the fire
That pulls me back to my bones
Sunrise, take me higher
Sunrise, don't let go
Sunrise, I'm alive now
(I'm alive now)

Let the dark fall off my face
Let the last hour fade away
New heat on my shoulders
New name for the day

Sunrise, take me higher
Sunrise, take me home
Sunrise, you're the fire
That pulls me back to my bones
Sunrise, take me higher
Sunrise, don't let go
Sunrise, I'm alive now
(I'm alive now)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I laid you down in a rain-stained shop
With a cracked red sign and a deadbolt lock
Cash in my hand, shame in my jaw
You were all I had, and I needed more
The man behind the counter grinned
Ran his thumb where the dings ran thin
Said, "Come back soon if luck turns round"
I walked out light and way too proud

But every night
That empty case
Sat on my floor
Like a ghost in place

Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
That old six-string came home to me
Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
Still had a little fight in the keys
(come back)
(come back)
I lost my nerve, I lost my cash
Pawned and bought back

I took up work on a loading dock
Hands split open, back like rock
Saved every bill in a coffee tin
Counted my sins and I counted them in
Friday came with a yellow glare
I pushed that door like a prayer
He slid me the case, cold and scarred
Like a dog still loyal with a broken heart

And when I flipped
The latches slow
There was my blood
In the worn-in gold

Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
That old six-string came home to me
Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
Still had a little fight in the keys
(come back)
(come back)
I lost my nerve, I lost my cash
Pawned and bought back

I knew that wood
I knew that burn
I knew every scar
And every wrong turn
The shop door sighed
The world stood still
I set it on my shoulder
And I meant to heal

Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
That old six-string came home to me
Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
Still had a little fight in the keys
(come back)
(come back)
I lost my nerve, I lost my cash
Pawned and bought back

Now I play it mean
Now I play it loud
What I threw away
Won't leave me now

That old six-string came home to me
Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
Still had a little fight in the keys
(come back)
(come back)
I lost my nerve, I lost my cash
Pawned and bought back

I took up work on a loading dock
Hands split open, back like rock
Saved every bill in a coffee tin
Counted my sins and I counted them in
Friday came with a yellow glare
I pushed that door like a prayer
He slid me the case, cold and scarred
Like a dog still loyal with a broken heart

And when I flipped
The latches slow
There was my blood
In the worn-in gold

Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
That old six-string came home to me
Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
Still had a little fight in the keys
(come back)
(come back)
I lost my nerve, I lost my cash
Pawned and bought back

I knew that wood
I knew that burn
I knew every scar
And every wrong turn
The shop door sighed
The world stood still
I set it on my shoulder
And I meant to heal

Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
That old six-string came home to me
Pawned and bought back
Pawned and bought back
Still had a little fight in the keys
(come back)
(come back)
I lost my nerve, I lost my cash
Pawned and bought back

Now I play it mean
Now I play it loud
What I threw away
Won't leave me now

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I laid it down on the glass
Twenty bucks and a cracked black strap
My old six-string with the chipped red neck
One bad week, one hard check
The man at the counter smiled
Like he knew my kind by name
He said, "Come back when your luck turns round"
And I walked out in the rain

But every night
That case in my mind
Kept calling my hands home

Bought it back
Bought it back
That old guitar came back
Bought it back
Bought it back
From the pawn shop rack
Same scar
Same scratch
Still sings when I hit that chord
Bought it back
Bought it back
Like it knew me before

I did the work, I kept my head
Skipped a meal, ran the miles
Took every shift they threw my way
Just to see that maple smile
Friday came with my paycheck fat
Folded tight in my jeans
I crossed that street like a man possessed
To steal back my own dreams

He set it out
I knew that sound
Before I even plugged it in

Bought it back
Bought it back
That old guitar came back
Bought it back
Bought it back
From the pawn shop rack
Same scar
Same scratch
Still sings when I hit that chord
Bought it back
Bought it back
Like it knew me before

Now the bars are full of smoke and sweat
And the floorboards shake all night
I wear that loss like a dog-eared bruise
Then I turn it into light
Yeah, I lost my name for a little while
Lost my pride and my nerve
But one good song and one hard month
Put my hands back where they were

Bought it

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I watched your hands
move like rain
over a morning glass
I knew your smile
but not your name
in the shape it passed
So I bought a book
with bent-up corners
and a little blue page
I traced each sign
like I was learning
how to stay

You laughed at my first try
when I said it all wrong
Then you slowed it down
and let me come along

Say it with hands
say it with hands
I want your world
in my palms
Say it with hands
say it with hands
I’m learning love
the way you talk

At the kitchen sink
I spelled my fears
one clumsy letter at a time
You kissed my wrist
and fixed my shape
when I crossed the line
Now the mirror knows
my slow mistakes
and my stubborn little pride
But your fingers land
like a safe small promise
on the other side

You laughed at my first try
when I said it all wrong
Then you slowed it down
and let me come along

Say it with hands
say it with hands
I want your world
in my palms
Say it with hands
say it with hands
I’m learning love
the way you talk

I used to wait
for the perfect words
to save me from the dark
Now your name arrives
in a circle of light
right where you start
Teach me again
slow enough
I don’t want to miss
the way your love
can live in this

Say it with hands
say it with hands
I want your world
in my palms
Say it with hands
say it with hands
I’m learning love
the way you talk
Say it with hands
say it with hands
I know at last
where I belong

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I watched your fingers move
Like rain on a window pane
I knew I wanted closeness
So I started with your name
Thumb to chin, then open palm
Clumsy in my kitchen light
You laughed so soft, then kissed my hand
And taught me “good night”

I was slow
You were patient
I was lost
You made a space for me
One small sign
Then another
Now your silence
Feels like a part of me

I learned your hands
I learned your hands
So I could reach you here
I learned your hands
I learned your hands
And now your heart is clear
(Your heart is clear)
When words fall down
When words fall down
My fingers know the way
I learned your hands
I learned your hands
To say what I can’t say

At the bus stop, in the cold
You traced “home” against my skin
I signed it back too late, too proud
Then you grinned and tried again
Every little finger curve
Feels like drawing in the air
Now even in a crowded room
I find you there

I was rough
You were gentle
I was all
Broken pieces in my grip
You made love
Something touchable
A little light
At the tip of every fingertip

I learned your hands
I learned your hands
So I could reach you here
I learned your hands
I learned your hands
And now your heart is clear
(Your heart is clear)
When words fall down
When words fall down
My fingers know the way
I learned your hands
I learned your hands
To say what I can’t say

Teach me again
Slow and honest
Let my hands
Keep the

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The docklamp's coughing gold
On the peel of a blackened tide
We pushed in through the weather door
With the whole damn town inside
Captain's got one foot on the plank
And a grin like he knows the score
He says, "If you miss this boat tonight
You'll be sleeping on the shore"

So pass that mug down the line
And tighten up your coat
The wind's got teeth tonight
And the water wants the vote

Last ferry of the season
(last ferry of the season)
Last ferry of the season
Take me where the lights won't freeze in
One more song, one more cheer
Then the long dark water clears
Last ferry of the season
(last ferry of the season)

Old Nora thumps the table
Says the ice won't wait on us
Finn's got salt in his beard
And a story full of rust
Mick's got a basket of pears
And two queens up his sleeve
The fiddler's drunk on purpose
Says the fog's his guarantee

So pass that mug down the line
And tighten up your coat
The wind's got teeth tonight
And the water wants the vote

Last ferry of the season
(last ferry of the season)
Last ferry of the season
Take me where the lights won't freeze in
One more song, one more cheer
Then the long dark water clears
Last ferry of the season
(last ferry of the season)

If I miss my bed tonight
I'll borrow yours, I'll mend my pride
But the moon's a silver shovel
And it's digging up the tide
So raise it up, you stubborn fools
To the chain, to the wake, to the drum
When the harbor shuts its heavy mouth
We sing till the last one's gone

Last ferry of the season
(last ferry of the season)
Last ferry of the season
Take me where the lights won't freeze in
One more song, one more cheer
Then the long dark water clears
Last ferry of the season
(last ferry of the season)

Hey!
Last ferry home
(last ferry home)
Hey!
Last ferry home
(last ferry home)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The dock lights blink on the black water
Cold wind chewing through my coat
Old mates leaning on the rail
With a flask and a one-way joke
The captain says, "Last call, lads"
And the whole pier lets out a groan
We’ve got one more run to the island
Then the season’s over, gone

So pour it high, my friends
If the night is gonna fold
We’ll sing the shore goodbye
And let the ferry take its hold

Last ferry, last ferry
Carry me home tonight
Last ferry, last ferry
Till the harbor drops from sight
One more song for the season
One more round for the road
Last ferry, last ferry
Take us where the cold winds blow

Mrs. Doyle with the wool cap
Got her herring in a tin
Young Tom’s got a red nose
And he’s laughing at the spin
Boot heels thumping on the deck boards
As the bottle makes its pass
We all know this one’s the last one
So we drain the season fast

Raise your voice, my friends
Let it ring across the foam
If the island goes quiet now
We’ll be loud enough for home

Last ferry, last ferry
Carry me home tonight
Last ferry, last ferry
Till the harbor drops from sight
One more song for the season
One more round for the road
Last ferry, last ferry
Take us where the cold winds blow

And the gulls cut circles overhead
Like they know we’re out of time
The engine coughs, the ropes go slack
And the dark begins to climb
So hold my arm, you rascals
If the water starts to rock
We’ll be the last ones singing
When the island locks the dock

Last ferry, last ferry
Carry me home tonight
Last ferry, last ferry
Till the harbor drops from sight
One more song for the season
One more round for the road
Last ferry, last ferry
Take us where the cold winds blow
Last ferry, last ferry
Hear the whole damn pier unload

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Dust motes dance in the sunbeams, thick and slow
Across the worn-out floorboards, where the secrets grow
Every scratch in the pine, every stain on the wall
This old house remembers, remembers it all

Oh, the house remembers, the house don't forget
Every harsh word spoken, every tear ever wet
The laughter that echoed, the whispers so low
The house remembers, and it just won't let go

Grandma cooked biscuits on that cast iron stove
Saw her reflection in the window, where the shadows wove
Watched her children grow tall, then watched them all leave
This house remembers, and it still seems to grieve

Pa built this place with his own calloused hands
Dreamed of rich farmland, across these dry lands
He died in that armchair, by the cold fireplace
This house remembers, every line on his face

Oh, the house remembers, the house don't forget
Every harsh word spoken, every tear ever wet
The laughter that echoed, the whispers so low
The house remembers, and it just won't let go

The wind through the eaves, a long, mournful sigh
Like a ghost in the attic, that just can't say goodbye
It holds all the memories, like dust in its lungs
The stories unspoken

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I unlocked that front door slow
Same brass key, same broken glow
The hallway held my name like sin
And let the cold air wander in
The wallpaper peeled like skin
Where the candle smoke had been
Three nail marks on the pantry side
Like somebody counted days inside

And the floorboard gave me back
Every step, every crack
The mantel kept a blackened scar
Like it knew just where we are

This house remembers
This house remembers
Every slammed door, every tender lie
Every tear that dried
This house remembers
This house remembers
It keeps your shadow by the stairs
It keeps us there

Your hat still hangs on that back nail
Dust in the brim like pale old hail
The sink still hums with a rusted ache
From all the mornings we couldn’t break
There’s a handprint in the ash
Half a prayer and half a crash
I swear the mirror blinked at me
When I passed that nursery

And the floorboard gave me back
Every step, every crack
The mantel kept a blackened scar
Like it knew just where we are

This house remembers
This house remembers
Every slammed door, every tender lie
Every tear that dried
This house remembers
This house remembers
It keeps your shadow by the stairs
It keeps us there

I tried to leave that place behind
But the boards had learned my mind
They creaked my secrets one by one
Till the back door wouldn’t let me run
And in the dark behind the frame
I heard you say my name
Soft as a chain
Soft as rain

This house remembers
This house remembers
Every kiss that turned to stone
Every night alone
This house remembers
This house remembers
It keeps your shadow by the stairs
It keeps us there
It keeps us there

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He says good morning, I lose my seat
Coffee in hand, and a tap-tap beat
Big yellow bus with the shaky floor
And I’m acting normal, then I’m not for sure
His name on the tag, but I don’t need that
I know the way he says, “next stop back”
Gum in my pocket, nerves in my chest
I’m trying to be cool, but I’m doing my best

When he checks the mirror
My heart goes loud
My stop comes faster
I’m staring down
He says, “move along”
And I melt right there
I swear that smile
Is a moving prayer

Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me swerving
Bus driver, bus driver
I’m never learning
One little grin
And I’m gone again
Bus driver, bus driver
Drive me insane
Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me smiling
Bus driver, bus driver
My day starts shining
I wave too hard
At the corner bend
Bus driver, bus driver
Do it again

Rain on the glass, and the seats all wet
He hums to the radio, I never forget
Old blue jacket and the clean white cap
I pretend I’m sleepy, but I keep looking back
He says “take care” like it means so much
And my whole world tilts with that tiny touch
Not on my phone, not in my head
Just his voice rolling through the morning bus

When he checks the mirror
My face turns red
I miss my stop
Then I blame my head
He says, “you all right?”
And I say, “I’m fine”
But my whole front row
Is a Valentine

Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me swerving
Bus driver, bus driver
I’m never learning
One little grin
And I’m gone again
Bus driver, bus driver
Drive me insane
Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me smiling
Bus driver, bus driver
My day starts shining
I wave too hard
At the corner bend
Bus driver, bus driver
Do it again

Maybe I’ll bring him a cherry mint
Maybe I’ll say more than, “here’s my ticket”
If he laughs, I’m done, that’s it
I’ll be a mess and I’ll still admit it
One more stop, and I’m feeling brave
One more look and I forget my name
If love’s a route, then I’m on this ride
With a little crush tucked way inside

Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me swerving
Bus driver, bus driver
I’m never learning
One little grin
And I’m gone again
Bus driver, bus driver
Drive me insane
Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me smiling
Bus driver, bus driver
My day starts shining
I wave too hard
At the corner bend
Bus driver, bus driver
Do it again

If love’s a route, then I’m on this ride
With a little crush tucked way inside

Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me swerving
Bus driver, bus driver
I’m never learning
One little grin
And I’m gone again
Bus driver, bus driver
Drive me insane
Oh, bus driver, bus driver
You got me smiling
Bus driver, bus driver
My day starts shining
I wave too hard
At the corner bend
Bus driver, bus driver
Do it again

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You tap the wheel like a metronome
In that blue shirt, way too neat
I hop on at the corner stop
And suddenly I miss my seat
You say, "Morning" at the front door
Like you know my little heart
I act cool with my headphones on
But I’m already falling hard

Badge shines, eyes down
Then you look my way
My stop comes, but I stay
One more block today

Route twelve smile, route twelve smile
You got me grinning all the while
Route twelve smile, route twelve smile
I take the long way for a while
(ride on) Route twelve smile
(so fine) Route twelve smile
You say, "next stop," and I feel
My whole day go wild

You count change with your steady hands
While the bus rocks side to side
I pretend I’m checking messages
But I’m watching in my mind
You know every kid by nickname
Every dog and every turn
And when you laugh at some backseat joke
I swear my cheeks start to burn

Badge shines, eyes down
Then you look my way
My stop comes, but I stay
One more block today

Route twelve smile, route twelve smile
You got me grinning all the while
Route twelve smile, route twelve smile
I take the long way for a while
(ride on) Route twelve smile
(so fine) Route twelve smile
You say, "next stop," and I feel
My whole day go wild

Maybe it’s silly, maybe it’s small
But you make my mornings feel tall
Just one wave when I get off
And I’m gone, but not at all

Route twelve smile, route twelve smile
You got me grinning all the while
Route twelve smile, route twelve smile
I take the long way for a while
(ride on) Route twelve smile
(so fine) Route twelve smile
You say, "next stop," and I feel
My whole day go wild

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Her hands were a map
Of folding bread and prayer
Knuckles like old roads
Still leading me somewhere
Thumb on my forehead
When fever took my name
She held the whole room
In a palm full of flame

She said, baby, slow down
Let the hurt run through
What these hands have carried
They can carry you

Grandma's hands
Held me up
Grandma's hands
Never gave up
Rough and kind
Still my shelter
Grandma's hands
Made me better
(Grandma's hands)

I watched her in the garden
Dust under her nails
Pulling up tomatoes
And brushing off the hail
She could mend a sleeve
She could calm the door
She could make a broken day
Feel like before

She said, baby, slow down
Let the hurt run through
What these hands have carried
They can carry you

Grandma's hands
Held me up
Grandma's hands
Never gave up
Rough and kind
Still my shelter
Grandma's hands
Made me better
(Grandma's hands)

Now the chair sits quiet
By the window light
But I feel her reaching
Every time I fight
In my own two hands
I can still feel her
Guiding me home
One grace at a time

Grandma's hands
Held me up
Grandma's hands
Never gave up
Rough and kind
Still my shelter
Grandma's hands
Made me better
(Grandma's hands)
Grandma's hands
Hold me still
Grandma's hands
Teach me will

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Wrinkles like rivers
Running down her skin
Stories they whisper
Where do I begin
Every crease a memory
A love so deep and true
Grandma's hands holding me
Always pulling through

Oh grandma's hands
They worked so hard
Grandma's hands
Left their loving mark
Grandma's hands
Always soft and kind
Grandma's hands
One of a kind

Knuckles a little swollen
From years of baking pies
Secrets they were holding
Reflected in her eyes
Patting down my worries
Brushing back my hair
Through all the hurries
Her gentle touch was there

They held me when I stumbled
They cheered me when I won
Never once they grumbled
From dusk till rising sun
These hands they raised a family
With patience and with grace
A living breathing symphony
In this sacred space

Oh grandma's hands
They worked so hard
Grandma's hands
Left their loving mark
Grandma's hands
Always soft and kind
Grandma'

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Forty tabs deep
I swear I had two
One for the lease
One for the thing you said
Now there's a recipe
A map I never used
A blue cat video
And five blank dead ends
My mouse hand shakes
Like it's caught in rain
I click one close
Two more get born again

Don't ask me why
I can't let them die
Every little page
Keeps calling my name

Too many tabs
Too many tabs
My head won't load
Too many tabs
Too many tabs
Too many tabs
I can't go back
Too many tabs

The top bar bends
Like a loaded shelf
I got a school form
Next to bad health
A thrift store receipt
A train I missed
A half-read post
And a shopping list
My fan goes hard
Like it's mad at me
The cursor hangs
Like a tiny referee
I close one tab
Then I hear that sound
That smug little pop
And the swarm comes down

Don't ask me why
I can't let them die
Every little page
Keeps calling my name

Too many tabs
Too many tabs
My head won't load

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
One by one
They blur and bite
Green checks, red dots
A flood of white
I need one line
I need one sign
But the whole damn screen
Won't fall in line

(tab-tab-tab)
(click-close-click)
(buffer scream)
(tab avalanche)

Too many tabs
Too many tabs
My head won't load
Too many tabs
Too many tabs
Too many tabs
I can't go back
Too many tabs
Too many tabs
Too many tabs
I close one
And ten more tabs
Tabzilla

Ninety-nine tabs open a digital mess
My RAM is screaming under duress
Each one a promise a thought a quest
My brain is buzzing put it to the test

Too many clicks
Too many tricks

Tabzilla's here
Swallowing all my cheer
Tabzilla's roar
I can't take anymore

A Wikipedia wormhole a shopping spree
A news article I'll never truly see
A YouTube rabbit hole staring back at me
This digital chaos is setting me free maybe

Close one tab
Open three

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I kept your coat by the door
Like you might come back for it
Dust on the shoulders, dark in the seams
Still holding your shape in it
Your name on my tongue was a stone
I carried it room to room
But the house got quiet enough
For me to hear the truth

You never asked me to wait
You never said goodbye
I was the one with the chain in my hands
I was the one still tied

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
For leaving me here
With the salt and the sound
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You’re gone from my reach
But you’re lighter now

I saw you in every hard rain
In every cracked blue plate
In the chair by the window glow
In the floorboards creaking late
I blamed the sky, blamed the years
Blamed the way the blood runs thin
But the hurt was a locked-up room
And I was the one within

So I turned the key at last
Let the whole thing fall
If love is a wound that stays open
Then mercy can change it all

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
For leaving me here
With the salt and the sound
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You’re gone from my reach
But you’re lighter now

If there’s a road where you are
I hope it’s kinder than this
I hope the weight fell off your heart
The day you vanished in mist
And me, I’m learning the shape
Of a life that still goes on
I can miss you and let you go
Both in the same song

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
For leaving me here
With the salt and the sound
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You’re gone from my reach
But you’re lighter now
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
I lay down the hurt
And I let you out

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I kept your coat too long
Hung by the door
Like you might walk in
And ask for more
The cup by the sink
Still knows your shape
I flinch at the sound
Of your old name

But time keeps moving
Through the frame
And all my anger
Calls your name

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Even in the dark
I lay it down
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You’re out of reach
But I’m here now

I wrote every wound
Like it would stay
Sharp as a knife
Day after day
Then your silence grew
Larger than blame
And I saw my pain
Was saying your name

So I let the story
Break and bend
Not for the ending
But for me, my friend

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Even in the dark
I lay it down
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You’re out of reach
But I’m here now

If there’s a field
Beyond this sky
I won’t follow
Where hurt leads on
I’ll keep the love
And lose the storm

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Even in the dark
I lay it down
I forgive you now
I forgive y

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I was born where the stone walls break
On a path cut thin by the sea
Black boots on the wet green grass
And the tide kept calling me
Gulls spun white over the drop
Like notes on a restless tune
I knew your face in the foam below
And the smoke from the town at noon

Oh, the wind said, "Run"
Oh, the wind said, "Stay"
My heart was a rope pulled tight
From the hill to the bay

Home, home
Salt on the wind, take me home
Home, home
Cliffs in my bones, take me home
(Bring me home)
I hear it in the gulls
I hear it in my name
Home, home
Pull me back again

There’s a door with paint worn thin
And a lamp by the kitchen pane
A kettle song, a wool coat hook
And your hands in the pouring rain
I’ve chased the road past breaking waves
Past all I thought I knew
But every mile just turned me round
To the shore that made me true

Oh, the wind said, "Run"
Oh, the wind said, "Stay"
My heart was a rope pulled tight
From the hill to the bay

Home, home
Salt on the wind, take me home
Home, home
Cliffs in my bones, take me home
(Bring me home)
I hear it in the gulls
I hear it in my name
Home, home
Pull me back again

If I fall where the heather burns
If the dark rolls in too fast
Throw my song from the highest rock
Let it echo, let it last
I was never made for leaving
I was made for the shore
For the long way back to your open arms
And the life behind the door

Home, home
Salt on the wind, take me home
Home, home
Cliffs in my bones, take me home
(Bring me home)
I hear it in the gulls
I hear it in my name
Home, home
Pull me back again
(Back again)
Home, home
I’m yours again

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I came up the lane at dawn
Boots on wet stone, heart worn thin
Salt on the air like a prayer
And the gulls were calling in
Gray waves beat the black cliff face
White foam tearing at the base
I knew that edge, I knew that sound
It knew my name, it knew this place

And the wind said
"Keep going, keep going"
Over the roar
I felt it growing

Cliffs call me home
Cliffs call me home
Where the gulls cut through
And the wild heart roams
Cliffs call me home
Cliffs call me home
I was lost at sea
Now I know this road

Mum's old chair by the fire
Wood smoke tangled in my coat
Your door was painted the same
Blue as the boats down in the cove
Kids on the strand threw stones
Laughing as the tide came in
Every crack in that old wall
Was a place my life began

And the wind said
"Keep going, keep going"
Over the roar
I felt it growing

Cliffs call me home
Cliffs call me home
Where the gulls cut through
And the wild heart roams
Cliffs call me home
Cliffs call me home
I was lost at sea
Now I know this road

Take my name in the spray
Take my ache in the rain
I have wandered far
But I come back again

Cliffs call me home
Cliffs call me home
Where the gulls cut through
And the wild heart roams
Cliffs call me home
Cliffs call me home
I was lost at sea
Now I know this road

Home, home
On the edge of the world
Home, home
Where the

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The ashtray’s full of rain
The bartender counts the till
Your coat hangs on the chair
Like it’s waiting there still
One last tune on the keys
Then the room goes thin

Sweeter when it’s quiet
Closer when you stay
I can hear your laugh
Across the empty trays

Last call, velvet
Stay a little longer
Last call, velvet
Make the hush get stronger
Hold me in the dim
Where the night turns slow
Last call, velvet
Don’t let me go

A red scarf on the stool
A lipstick mark in glass
The saxophone clocked out
But your eyes still ask
For another minute
For another slow goodbye

Sweeter when it’s quiet
Closer when you stay
I can hear your laugh
Across the empty trays

Last call, velvet
Stay a little longer
Last call, velvet
Make the hush get stronger
Hold me in the dim
Where the night turns slow
Last call, velvet
Don’t let me go

Let the sign go dark
Let the front door sigh
We can keep one candle
Burning by and by
If the world is done
We can borrow time

Last call, velvet
Stay a little longer
Last call, velvet
Make the hush get stronger
Hold me in the dim
Where the night turns slow
Last call, velvet
Don’t let me go

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The ashtray's full
The tab's run long
Your red coat's draped
On the upright song
The barman yawns
He stacks the glass
I play your name
In a minor pass

One more round
Then the room turns slow
Your ring on the bar
Catches amber glow

Last call keys

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Punch in at six
With gum on my shoes
The fryer was screaming
I was already used
Mop water on my hands
Burn mark on my wrist
The boss said, "Smile, kid"
Like that ever fixed this

Break room gone stale
Fan blade moans
I checked my name tag
Like it might say home

That summer broke me
(That summer broke me)
Back pain, cold pay
(Back pain, cold pay)
I laughed till I shook
Then I split at the seams
That summer broke me
(That summer broke me)

Got sun in my eyes
Got grease in my hair
Clock said "ten more minutes"
Like it even cared
The dining room swam
With a dead blue sheen
I kept my mouth shut
Till my teeth felt mean

Hands in the sink
Knees in the dirt
Every cheap little joke
Hit like it hurt

That summer broke me
(That summer broke me)
Back pain, cold pay
(Back pain, cold pay)
I laughed till I shook
Then I split at the seams
That summer broke me
(That summer broke me)

I know that hallway
I know that stain
I know the sound of my own
Name said plain
If you ask me now
What I learned that year
I learned how a body
Can disappear

That summer broke me
(That summer broke me)
Back pain, cold pay
(Back pain, cold pay)
I laughed till I shook
Then I split at the seams
That summer broke me
(That summer broke me)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
July came mean
In a plastic name tag
Mop bucket stinking
By the freezer bags
Eight hours bent
On a floor that stuck
Grease in my hair
And a mouth full of dust

Boss kept barking
Move faster, kid
My hands went numb
By the second shift
Lunch in a break room
Warm soda, bad chair
I stared at the wall
Like I lived there

That job broke me
Broke me clean
Summer on repeat
Like a fever dream
That job broke me
Broke me down
(kept me there)
Till I couldn’t stand my own sound

Drive-thru headset
All static and spit
Smile for the line
While your back just splits
Ice machine sweating
In a metal box
Clock kept dragging
And my bones got locked

Teenage skin
Under cheap blue light
Counting pennies
Till the end of night
I laughed too hard
When the fryer screamed
Something in me
Just split at the seams

That job broke me
Broke me clean
Summer on repeat
Like a fever dream
That job broke me
Broke me down
(kept me there)
Till I couldn’t stand my own sound

Now the scar still lives
In my wrists and knees
In the smell of oil
On my memory
I learned real quick
What a body costs
How a whole season
Can leave you lost

That job broke me
Broke me clean
Summer on repeat
Like a fev

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
He taps the hood
like it knows his name
Two hands on the wheel
one foot on the brake
You smell old vinyl
and cut grass rain
He says, "Easy now
let the car be tame"

Left foot down
right hand slow
Find the bite
then let it go
He smiles at you
like he knows

Easy now, girl
easy now
(hold it there)
Easy now, girl
we'll figure it out
Half clutch, half faith
roll forward, don't doubt
Easy now, girl
easy now

Your sneaker shakes
at the stop sign line
He points to the hill
says "Not this time"
You stall, you laugh
he laughs too
Then he reaches over
and lets you through

Left foot down
right hand slow
Find the bite
then let it go
He says your name
soft and low

Easy now, girl
easy now
(hold it there)
Easy now, girl
we'll figure it out
Half clutch, half faith
roll forward, don't doubt
Easy now, girl
easy now

In the cracked rearview
his eyes go bright
Like he can see
your whole road light
He used to carry
you on one hip
Now you carry
this old shift

Easy now, girl
easy now
(hold it there)
Easy now, girl
we'll figure it out
Half clutch, half faith
roll forward, don't doubt
Easy now, girl
easy now
(you've got this now)
Easy now, girl
easy now

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning sun
Engine cold and still
Keys in my hand a tiny thrill
You just looked at me with a grin
Said "time to learn to spin"

Clutch in gas out slow
That's the way we go

First gear love
A little lurch a little shove
First gear love
Pushing the clutch above
First gear love
We're gonna get there my dove

Stalled it twice we laughed so hard
My foot slipped off the pedal card
You said "no worries kiddo it's a start"
A patient teacher with a kind heart
I felt like a rockstar a true work of art

Clutch in gas out slow
That's the way we go

First gear love
A little lurch a little shove
First gear love
Pushing the clutch above
First gear love
We're gonna get there my dove

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Mama said, “Pass the plate”
Uncle said, “You too late”
Auntie shook her head real slow
“Who burned the bottom, though?”
Steam rose up in the bowl
Pepper hit, and tempers rolled
Cousin reached, I pulled it back
“First come, first served” — we all snapped

But the spoon keeps moving
Round and round the dish
Even when we’re arguing
We all still want a bite of this

Jollof on the table, hey
Jollof on the table, hey
We fight, then we laugh again
Pass that red gold, amen (jollof on the table)
Jollof on the table, hey
Jollof on the table, hey
Everybody wants their share
Family loud, love right there (jollof on the table)

Grandma cut the silence quick
“Children, stop that foolishness”
She tapped the pot with one small grin
“Truth is, this is how we win”
Brother said it needs more spice
Sister said, “You said that twice”
Even with the noise and smoke
Somehow that’s the way we cope

And the spoon keeps moving
Round and round the dish
If the words get too heated
We still come back to this

Jollof on the table, hey
Jollof on the table, hey
We fight, then we laugh again
Pass that red gold, amen (jollof on the table)
Jollof on the table, hey
Jollof on the table, hey
Everybody wants their share
Family loud, love right there (jollof on the table)

So let the voices rise
Let the old scores fly
When the pot comes out
We all gather by
One big bowl, one house
One stubborn crowd
We may argue all day
But we eat it proud

Jollof on the table, hey
Jollof on the table, hey
We fight, then we laugh again
Pass that red gold, amen (jollof on the table)
Jollof on the table, hey
Jollof on the table, hey
Everybody wants their share
Family loud, love right there (jollof on the table)

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Mama say my future bright
Papa say I waste my time
Sister steal my favorite shirt
Brother eat my last sweet lime

Sun go down
Heat is on

Jollof dreams
Red red rice
Spicy heat
Everything nice
Jollof dreams
Family fight
But still we love
With all our might

Auntie say I need a man
Uncle ask about my grades
Cousin dance with all his might
Neighbors gossip through the shades

Kitchen full of laughter loud
Kitchen full of angry words
But the aroma pulls us close
Like a flock of happy birds

Jollof dreams
Red red rice
Spicy heat
Everything nice
Jollof dreams
Family fight
But still we love
With

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The field lies low and still
A fence post leans in sleep
I watch the far rows blur
Where the wet earth takes its leave
White from the river bend
It comes in on a hush
Thick as a held breath
Soft as a closing brush

It folds the stalks in half
It hides the hooved paths first
I know that shape by heart
The way it moves through hurt

Fog on rye, fog on rye
Cover my eyes, cover my eyes
Fog on rye, fog on rye
Take the edge, leave the line

The barn roof loses shape
The gate turns into bone
A crow calls from the gray
Then drops it and is gone
My boots make little scars
Then every scar is gone
Nothing here stays named
Once the mist rolls on

It folds the stalks in half
It hides the hooved paths first
I know that shape by heart
The way it moves through hurt

Fog on rye, fog on rye
Cover my eyes, cover my eyes
Fog on rye, fog on rye
Take the edge, leave the line

If you were here, you'd see
How the world forgets its frame
How the distant trees go thin
How even fear gets tame
I let the silence grow
I let it call me home

Fog on rye, fog on rye
Cover my eyes, cover my eyes
Fog on rye, fog on rye
Take the edge, leave the line

Mm
Fog on rye
Mm
Fog on rye

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The fog came low this morning
It laid its hand on rye
It crossed the fence in silence
And blurred the crows from sight
The ditch held one white ribbon
The grass bent under sleep
I heard the gate chain answer
Like a bone in the trees

Oh, it pulled and pulled
At the edge of the day
Oh, it took the field
And carried it away

Fog over rye
Fog over rye
Hold my name
Then let it die
Fog over rye
Fog over rye
White on the furrow
Black in the sky

My boots left prints of water
By the broken post and wire
A lamb was calling softly
From somewhere through the mire
The scarecrow lost its body
Just a coat on a pole
And every shape I knew here
Turned into a hole

Oh, it pulled and pulled
At the edge of the day
Oh, it took the field
And carried it away

Fog over rye
Fog over rye
Hold my name
Then let it die
Fog over rye
Fog over rye
White on the furrow
Black in the sky

When the last crow vanished
I stood and held my breath
The ground felt old beneath me
Like it knew about death
And the mist kept moving
Through the thistle and stone
Till the whole wide meadow
Became a bone

Fog over rye
Fog over rye
Hold my name
Then let it die
Fog over rye
Fog over r

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
Step in the shop, fresh line on my neck
Old heads look up, give me a nod of respect
Soap on the blade, got a point to make clear
They start talkin' wild, I been waitin' right here
Check the mirror, I don't flinch, I don't fold
Got the facts in my mouth and the truth in my soul
You talk big game, but you bend when it count
I let that calm voice hit, then I call the amount
One chair squeaks, whole room leans in
I don't raise my voice, I just grin and begin
Every claim you threw, I picked it apart
Like a clean edge-up, I line up the dark

You thought you'd run the room
You thought you'd set the tone
But I got that steady proof
And I came for the throne

I win this barbershop debate
I win this barbershop debate
Talk slick, I don't break
I win this barbershop debate
I win this barbershop debate
(hey, hey) I win this barbershop debate
Sharp wit, clean slate
I win this barbershop debate

By the time you reached point three, you was stuck in a loop
I laid out the trail like a fresh lace-up coupe
You reached for the crowd, but the crowd stayed low
'Cause they know real talk when the real talk show
Auntie from the back said, "That boy got sense"
Barber shook his head, said, "That logic intense"
You tried to pivot fast, but the floor stayed firm
I kept it straight like a part with a perfect return
Now the jokes get light, but the lesson lands
I don't need a crown, I just need clean hands
When the dust settles down and the mirror stay still
They remember my name for the way that I feel

Yeah, I played it smooth
Didn't need no stunt
You came in loud
I came in first

I win this barbershop debate
I win this barbershop debate
Talk slick, I don't break
I win this barbershop debate
I win this barbershop debate
(hey, hey) I win this barbershop debate
Sharp wit, clean slate
I win this barbershop debate

Chair spin slow, but my point still sticks
Clean cut words and the timing hits
Yeah, I win that room, let 'em all relate
Next time you speak, bring a better debate

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
I walked in calm, cape on straight
New cut fresh, same old debate
Three chairs full, all talk, no proof
Then I pulled that chair like "move"
Old head said "best? that's me, no cap"
I said "bro, that line don't hit like that"
Line by line, I peeled it back
Edge so sharp, it split the facts
You got good clippers, I give you that
But your fade ain't clean, it fades to black
Mine got blend, mine got grace
Mine make silence leave the place

Hands up, now watch me work
One more line, whole room jerk
He said "nah" with a shaky grin
I said "say that again, and sit back in"

Fade me in, fade me in
I win, I win again
Fade me in, fade me in
My side got the sharper end
(oh, that’s cold)
Fade me in, fade me in
I win, I win again
Fade me in, fade me in
Your talk don't hold the chair, man

Young dude jumped in with the loudest voice
Said "nah, that edge is my first choice"
I laughed once, then I laid it out
That weak fade got a crooked route
I said "watch the curve, watch the line
A real cut don't need to shout all night"
The whole shop nodded, one by one
Even the radio said "yeah, son"
He tried to flex with a namesake trim
I said "that shape don't fit your brim"

New cut fresh, same old debate

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
The clock on the hall says nine
Your blue dress knows the way
I smooth the crease on my cuff
Like I did on our first day
Your ring taps the glass of wine
A small sound in the room
We laugh at the shaky tune
Then step into the gloom

One last dance
One last dance
Hold me slow
Hold me slow
We were here
We are here
One last dance
Before we go

The rug still smells like rain
From shoes by the back door
The polaroid on the piano
Holds us at twenty-four
Your hand finds mine by habit
No need for words tonight
Even the floorboards seem to know
How to lean with our goodbye

One last dance
One last dance
Hold me slow
Hold me slow
We were here
We are here
One last dance
Before we go

You rest your cheek on my shoulder
Like summer on the pane
I hear our youngest laughing
In the dishes down the lane
If time can bend like willow
Let it bend one more turn
I’ll meet you where the music ends
And let the candle burn

One last dance
One last dance
Hold me slow
Hold me slow
We were here
We are here
One last dance
Before we go

The final note hangs soft
We stand where we began
Then you whisper, “dance with me”
And I do, while I can

songs.subject: varied
songs.lyrics:
You fixed my collar slow
Like you used to do
Your hands found the seam
On my borrowed suit
The ballroom chairs were stacked
By the coatroom wall
We left one table lit
So we could see it all

And the band kept time
Like it knew our names
Three old steps between us
And nothing felt strange

One last dance
One last spin
Hold me where we began
One last dance
Let it sink in
We were young as we can
One last dance

(One last dance)

I still choose you

Your ring caught the light
When you raised your hand

In a trembling band
I laughed at your joke
You said it twice before
Same crooked little smile
I loved at twenty-four

Now the floorboards creak
Like they know the score
Every step we saved
Is the step we use once more

One last dance
One last spin
Hold me where we began
One last dance
Let it sink in
We were young as we can
One last dance
Blue hem, old shoes

I still choose you

The record turns
And the night leans near
Your breathing in my ear
Is the map I keep here
No grand goodbye
Just this measured grace
Your forehead to mine
And the whole long place

One last d

Let the hours lean
Let them drift on by
Your hand on the mug
My hand close by

Good coffee, good coffee
Stay here a while
Good coffee, good coffee
Soft as your smile

Feels soft enough

Let the hours lean
Let them drift on by
Your hand on the mug
My hand close by

Good coffee, good coffee
Stay here a while
Good coffee, good coffee
Soft as your smile
Good coffee, good coffee

I won't let it get to me
I'm gonna win eventually

Overworked and underpaid
