How much longer will I keep payin' my dues
When the things that I work so hard for never come true?
How many hours will I be up on my feet?
I've been workin' two jobs
Still can't make ends meet

Is it the color of my skin
Or just the world we live in?

How much longer?
How much longer?
How much longer?
How much longer?

How much longer will I go to sleep hungry
When I've been workin' two jobs just to make some money?
How many nights will I be all alone?
I barely see my family 'cause I'm never home

Is it the color of my skin
Or just the world we live in?

How much longer?
How much longer?
How much longer?
How much longer?

songs.subject: trap song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Cardboard dust on my jeans
Room look hollow, bare
One lamp left by the wall
And your old chair sitting there

Marker says "kitchen"
Coffee stains on the lid
I been boxing up my life
One plate, one book, one crib

Bubble wrap on the glass
Tape gun stuck in my hand
I swear this place got smaller
When the lease hit the stand

Keys on the counter
One more fast
One more strip
Pull it tight
Make it stick

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Pull it tight, lock it down
I'm gone when the truck rocks

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One last run, one last lock
Then I'm off that block

Found a sock by the vent
And a bill in the sink
Old takeout on the counter
Gone back to the blink

Moving van's in the hall
Neighbor heard me curse
Got a dent in my palm
From the edge of the box first

I can hear the new spot
Even with the door shut
Fresh paint, empty closets
Same stress, new sun

One more fast
One more strip
Pull it tight
Make it stick

Last room clear
No more stack
See the chapter
Stack by stack

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Pull it tight, lock it down
I'm gone when the truck rocks

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Concrete king I sit alone
My chessboard kingdom my only home
Sunlight dapples through the leaves
Another sucker what he believes

Queens gambit declined
My mind entwined

Pawn shop heartbreak every single game
Just a few bucks for my hungry fame
Checkmate baby you're out of luck
Another soul another easy buck

He thinks he's clever a sly old fox
Moves his knight in little box
I see his plan before he starts
I break his spirit I break his heart

The pigeons watch the world goes by
Another tear in another eye
The city hums a lonely tune
Underneath the afternoon moon

Pawn shop heartbreak every single

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
He sets out three white queens
On the cracked green table
A paper cup of dimes
By the elm with the scar
He smiles at your move
Like he knows your name
And the pigeons lean in close
For the next cheap game

Move slow, baby
He taps the board
That look in his eye
Says you’ve lost before
Sun on the clock hand
Dust in the air
He reaches for your rook
Like he’s already there

King under sycamore
King under sycamore
You pay, you play, you fall
King under sycamore
(under sycamore)
He takes your heart
And your last small dollar

There’s mint on his collar
From the gum he chews
Old ring on his finger
With a stone gone loose
Kids on the swing set
Laugh when he grins
He leaves you one pawn
Then he pulls you in

Move slow, baby
He taps the board
That look in his eye
Says you’ve lost before
Sun on the clock hand
Dust in the air
He reaches for your rook
Like he’s already there

King under sycamore
King under sycamore
You pay, you play, you fall
King under sycamore
(under sycamore)
He takes your heart
And your last small dollar

At dusk he folds the cloth
And counts the day
One more boy with pride
Gave it all away
But when you stare too long
At his easy flame
You can’t tell if he’s running
Or winning the same

King under sycamore
King under sycamore
You pay, you play, you fall
King under sycamore
(under sycamore)
He takes your heart

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up at dawn
With a crate from Cobh
Stamped in black wax
And tied with rope
Old Captain Wren
Spit and made the sign
Said, “Keep that lid shut
Till you see the line”
The deck was clean
The sea was kind
Then that crate began
To knock inside

Tap-tap, low and mean
Like a knuckle on the board
Every mile we took
It wanted more

Cursed cargo, stay low
Cursed cargo, don’t roll
Keep your bones in line
Keep your hands off the gold
Cursed cargo, stay low
We’re not home yet, no
If the lid comes loose
Lord, we lose this boat

By the second night
The moon went thin
A gull fell dead
On the main beam
Billy heard it laugh
From the hold below
Said it knew his name
And wanted smoke
We salted the plank
We prayed all day
But the crate kept time
With the waves away

Tap-tap, hard and clear
Like a heel on chapel wood
The youngest swore
It understood

Cursed cargo, stay low
Cursed cargo, don’t roll
Keep your bones in line
Keep your hands off the gold
Cursed cargo, stay low
We’re not home yet, no
If the lid comes loose
Lord, we lose this boat

Third storm came fast
All black and foam
Mast split clean
Like a snapped old bone
Captain Wren went pale
When the lash gave way
And the crate stood up
In the ship’s dim sway
It had no face
Just a hole of night
And every man aboard
Saw his own inside

Cursed cargo, stay low
Cursed cargo, don’t roll
Keep your bones in line
Keep your hands off the gold
Cursed cargo, stay low
We’re not home yet, no
If the lid comes loose
Lord, we lose this boat

So if you sail
When the fog runs cold
Hear that knock below
Leave it alone
Cursed cargo, stay low
Cursed cargo, don’t roll

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up in June
By the end of the pier
Three crates of black glass
And a pewter chandelier
The captain read the bill
Then he crossed his chest
Said, “Count them one by one
And never count the rest”

By day four out to sea
The gulls quit the rails
The ropes grew slick as soap
And the lanterns burned pale
I heard the floorboards tap
Like someone walked below
But the hatch was chained tight
And the moon was moving slow

That cargo had a name
Though nobody said it
We tied it with good rope
But the knots all bled out of it
The men kept their distance
From the tar-black seam
And every time we prayed
It answered in our dreams

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
That cargo wants my soul
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
We should’ve let it go

On the seventh gray dawn
Old Ezra went down
Said he’d fetch the tally book
From the locked-up room below
He came up white as ash
With a brand new scar
Said the crates had opened
And they were breathing hard

We threw lines overboard
We threw beads to the waves
We threw the captain’s keys
And the names of every grave
But the sea gave back a groan
Like a throat full of stones
Now the ship leans left at night
And nobody walks alone

That cargo had a name
Though nobody said it
We tied it with good rope
But the knots all bled out of it
The men kept their distance
From the tar-black seam
And every time we prayed
It answered in our dreams

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
That cargo wants my soul
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
We should’ve let it go

When the storm took the mast
We saw inside the crate
Not gold, not silk, not bones
Just a mirror stained with hate
It showed our faces old
With the same dead stare
And every man who looked too long
Kept seeing one less there

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
That cargo wants my soul
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
We should’ve let it go
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old man Mendel
Sits on his stool
Fabric piles high
He ain't no fool
Snip snip goes the shears
A master at work
Making trousers making dreams
Never a jerk

Threads of gold
Threads of blue
Making old things shiny new

He's the tailor man
The neighborhood king
Dressing everyone
Making their hearts sing
From the baker to the mayor
A stitch in time
Mendel's magic touch
Truly sublime

Mrs. Goldstein's dress
Too long too wide
Mendel just smiles
With nothing to hide
A tuck and a fold
A button a bead
Everyone knows
He fulfills every need

Some say he whispers
To the cloth he cuts
Secret patterns
And stylish struts
He sees the person
Not just the frame
Giving confidence
Calling their name

He's the tailor man
The neighborhood king
Dressing everyone
Making their hearts sing
From the baker to the mayor
A stitch in time
Mendel's magic touch
Truly sublime
songs.lyrics_en:
Old man Mendel
Sits on his stool
Fabric piles high
He ain't no fool
Snip snip goes the shears
A master at work
Making trousers making dreams
Never a jerk

Threads of gold
Threads of blue
Making old things shiny new

He's the tailor man
The neighborhood king
Dressing everyone
Making their hearts sing
From the baker to the mayor
A stitch in time
Mendel's magic touch
Truly sublime

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old Mendel he sews all day
With his needle and thread he makes hay
For the whole block he cuts and he drapes
No two suits ever quite the same shapes

Mendel Mendel tailor man
Stitches stories across the land
From the rabbi to the baker too
Mendel makes clothes for me and for you

Mrs Goldstein needs a new dress
For her granddaughter's big happiness
Young Moishe wants pants for his dance
Mendel gives everyone a second chance

Mendel Mendel tailor man
Stitches stories across the land
From the rabbi to the baker too
Mendel makes clothes for me and for you

His fingers fly fast like a bird
Every snip every stitch every word
He hums a tune soft and low

songs.subject: a house song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone was cold in my hand
Like a coin from another life
Your name on the screen sat still
By the sink and the silver knife
I stood there in the doorway
With rain on my shoes and cuffs
Years folded up in my throat
I had swallowed too much

Then I pressed, and I pressed
Till the line came alive
My heart hit hard
When I heard you breathe
I said, “Ma, it’s me”
And the room went small
Like the whole world leaned
To listen through the wall

Mama, pick up, pick up
Mama, pick up, pick up
I’m still your son
I’m still your son
Mama, pick up, pick up
Mama, pick up, pick up
I found my way back
I found my way back

You said my name like a prayer
Like you’d kept it warm for me
I laughed through the tears
At the edge of the bed
You asked if I’d eaten
If I still forget my keys
If I still slam every door
When I can’t find peace
I said I got hard to hold
I said I learned to run
But every road I took
Led me back to your love

Then you went quiet
For a breath or two
And I heard the years
In the space between us
You said, “Baby, I was here”
And I could finally feel
The whole old hurt
Start to peel

Mama, pick up, pick up
Mama, pick up, pick up
I’m still your son
I’m still your son
Mama, pick up, pick up
Mama, pick up, pick up
I found my way back
I found my way back

I’m not asking for perfect
I’m not asking for gold
Just leave the porch light on
In the story we told
If my voice shakes, hold it
If I break, stay near
I was gone too long
But I’m here, I’m here

Mama, pick up, pick up
Mama, pick up, pick up
I’m still your son
I’m still your son
Mama, pick up, pick up
Mama, pick up, pick up
I found my way back
I found my way back
Mama, pick up, pick up
Mama, pick up, pick up
Don’t let this line go
Don’t let this line go

songs.subject: a house song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I let your name sit
in my phone too long
dust on the screen
same old green song
You’d pick up fast
like you never left
I kept the silence
like a second death

Now my thumb is shaking
over your line
I don’t know what I lost
but I know it was mine
One breath in
one breath out
If I call tonight
I might break out

Mother, can you hear me?
I’m here now, I’m here now
Mother, can you hear me?
I’m here now, I’m here now
One ring later
I’m coming back
(coming back)
One ring later
I’m coming back

I drove past your block
with the window down
Saw your porch light on
and the whole town drown
In everything I left
on a hard white night
Your last voicemail
still sat in my life
You said, “Baby, come home”
like it wasn’t late
I held that wound
like a dinner plate

Now my thumb is shaking
over your line
I don’t know what I lost
but I know it was mine
One breath in
one breath out
If I call tonight
I might break out

Mother, can you hear me?
I’m here now, I’m here now
Mother, can you hear me?
I’m here now, I’m here now
One ring later
I’m coming back
(coming back)
One ring later
I’m coming back

If you say my name
I might cry through it
If you say “son”
I might see it all clear
All those years
in a locked-up room
I’m at your door
and I’m calling you

Mother, can you hear me?
I’m here now, I’m here now
Mother, can you hear me?
I’m here now, I’m here now
One ring later
I’m coming back
(coming back)
One ring later

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the corner
You took a step back
I said, “go on, come in”
You laughed and said, “what’s that?”
One umbrella
Two strangers close
Your sleeve on my wrist
That’s how it goes

Drip on your hair
Mine on my shoes
We keep moving slow
Like we got time to lose
You look at me
I look away
Then you get a little closer
And the rain can stay

One umbrella, one umbrella
You and me under
One umbrella, one umbrella
Stuck in the thunder
We were strangers a minute ago
Now you’re standing too close
One umbrella, one umbrella
Don’t let go

Bus pulls up loud
We don’t move yet
You shake out your bangs
Like you won a bet
I say your name
You say mine too
That little smile hit me
Harder than the rain could do

Drip on your hair
Mine on my shoes
We keep moving slow
Like we got time to lose
You look at me
I look away
Then you get a little closer
And the rain can stay

One umbrella, one umbrella
You and me under
One umbrella, one umbrella
Stuck in the thunder
We were strangers a minute ago
Now you’re standing too close
One umbrella, one umbrella
Don’t let go

Your hand finds the edge
My heart skips low
The storm keeps talking
But we don’t go
If this is just chance
Let it be mean
One small umbrella
And a whole new scene

One umbrella, one umbrella
You and me under
One umbrella, one umbrella
Stuck in the thunder
We were strangers a minute ago
Now you’re standing too close
One umbrella, one umbrella
Don’t let go

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
I backed the Mercury out
Past the paint shop fence
You were all black coat
And a paper bag full of sense
Gas hand on E
Radio dead again
You said one clean mile
Then we don’t come back again

Map folded wrong
Hands shook once
Your gold ring flashed
When you shut the trunk

This is my last job
My last job, baby
This is my last job
Then you’ll forget my face
We burn one road
And leave no trace
This is my last job
My last job, baby

Blue tarp on the seat
Case notes on your lap
You stared at the clock
Like it owed you a trap
Cops at the gas stop
Coffee gone cold
You laughed like a dare
And I laughed like I was sold

One more light
One more bend
You said “drive”
Like it was the end

This is my last job
My last job, baby
This is my last job
Then you’ll forget my face
We burn one road
And leave no trace
This is my last job
My last job, baby

At the bridge toll booth
You touched my sleeve
Said “if it goes bad
Don’t wait for me”
Then your door swung wide
And the night split clean
I heard boots hit gravel
Like a broken dream

This is my last job
My last job, baby
This is my last job
Then you’ll forget my face
We burn one road
And leave no trace
This is my last job
My last job, baby

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine coughs and sputters
Night air thick with lies
One last job they whispered
See the fear in their eyes
Headlights cut the darkness
Just a flicker then gone

My hands grip the wheel
Sweat on my brow

Midnight Run
Yeah Midnight Run
This ain't no game no
Midnight Run
Gonna outrun the sun

Radio static crackles
Whispers of the law
Backseat breathing heavy
Feel the tension raw
Got a ghost in the rearview
Shadows dance and sway

My heart pounds a drum
Gotta get away

Midnight Run
Yeah Midnight Run
This ain't no game no
Midnight Run

Ooh, I could hear my heart go

My first open mic
My first open mic

At my first open mic
My first open mic
My first open mic

At my first open mic

I took one breath

Ooh, the glass on the counter

My first open mic

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
I wore my lucky shirt
One cuff still came undone
My name on a crumpled note
Shook more than the room
The mic stood like a dare
Under that buzzing fan
I laughed a little too loud
To hide my shaking hands

Then you called my name
And my heart went "wait"
I stepped into the light
Like I was late

First open mic
Oh, first open mic
I still taste that night
(oh, first open mic)
First open mic
Oh, first open mic
I was nervous, I was wide
(so alive)

The stool had one short leg
The floor kept moving slow
A girl at the back row
Nodded, told me go
I missed the second line
Then found it in the laugh
When strangers clapped on time
It split the dark in half

Then you called my name
And my knees turned weak
But I found my voice
On the third damn beat

First open mic
Oh, first open mic
I still taste that night
(oh, first open mic)
First open mic
Oh, first open mic

(so alive)

And if I lose my nerve
I go back there again
One breath, one shaky step
And I begin

First open mic
Oh, first open mic
I still taste that night
(oh, first open mic)
First open mic
Oh, first open mic

Take me back

Take me back

First open mic
Oh, first open mic

Sunrise, already tired, clock in, punch in, another day, another dollar
Minimum wage, maximum rage, brain dead, still gotta holler
Boss on my back, bills in a stack, landlord knockin', can't relax
Second job, graveyard shift, sleep? What's that? A myth.

Two jobs, no joy, just grind, all my time, what a ploy
Workin' hard, still fallin' short, this ain't livin', it's a sport
Runnin' laps, no finish line, trapped in this hustle, ain't no sign
Of a break, a little peace, just more debt, no release.

Stomach rumblin', ramen noodles, that's the feast
Brain cells scramblin', deadlines loom, gotta appease the beast

songs.subject: a hip hop song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in with the dawn
Clock out to the dark
One name on the badge
One name on the card
Paper in my shoe
Gas light in the tank
Milk in the fridge
But the bank says blank
Boss says “good work”
Like that pays the rent
I count my tips twice
Still it all gets spent
Pants got a tear
Lunch stays in the bag
Smile for the customer
While my body drags

I run, I run
Still the floor gets me
I give and I give
Still it won’t let me breathe

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I’m on my feet all night
Still I can’t get aligned
Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I keep my head up high
But I’m running out of time

After-shift hands smell like fryer oil
Wristwatch dead, but the bills recoil
Landlord text got that cold reply
Due date close and the check ran dry
Phone on low, gotta save that charge
Take the long way home through the parking lot
Kids asleep by the time I get in
Leave my shoes by the door, let the day sink
My girl says “baby, you look worn thin”
I say “I know, I’m trying to win”
But the week keeps chewing on every win
And the moon comes up, and I clock in again

I run, I run
Still the floor gets me
I give and I give
Still it won’t let me breathe

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I’m on my feet all night
Still I can’t get aligned
Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I keep my head up high
But I’m running out of time

One more shift, one more ride
One more promise I’m keepin’
If I break, if I slide
I still wake up believin’

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I’m on my feet all night
Still I can’t get aligned
Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I keep my head up high

songs.subject: a punk song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Cab smells like diesel and rain
Ten wheels hum through the dark
Your kids are taped to the dash
I keep your photo by the card

At the truck stop on Route Nine
I fed the meter, got a dime
Your voice comes soft through the crackle
Like a porch light through the pines

I’m tired, but I’m rolling
Hands locked on this wheel
Just wanted to hear you
So I know you’re real

Pick up, pick up
I’m on the line
Pick up, pick up
I called to find
Your voice, your voice
Cuts through the night
Pick up, pick up
I’m coming home tonight

The bunk’s got a blanket of crumbs
My boots still wear the road
I got ten more states to swallow
And a heart that’s running low

I’m tired, but I’m rolling
Hands locked on this wheel
Just wanted to hear you
So I know you’re real

Pick up, pick up
I’m on the line
Pick up, pick up
I called to find
Your voice, your voice
Cuts through the night
Pick up, pick up
I’m coming home tonight

If I sound rough, blame the miles
If I sound small, blame the load
I got your name in the ashtray
And your laugh on the road

Pick up, pick up
I’m on the line
Pick up, pick up
I called to find
Your voice, your voice
Cuts through the night
Pick up, pick up
I’m coming home tonight

songs.lyrics:
Pennies in my palm
Grease on my sleeve
Diesel on my boots
I call you to believe
The rig’s still coughing

I’m counting broken lines

Hands on the wheel
Mind on the hall

Pick up, pick up

Pick up, pick up
Hear me now
I’m still your man
I’m still around

Under frozen rain
Coffee gone cold
And I say your name

Like it pulls me through

Pick up, pick up
Hear me now
I’m still your man
I’m still around

And a busted sky
I’ll kiss that screen
If you say hi
No hero speech
No shiny lie
Just hold the line
Till I pull in drive

Pick up, pick up
Don’t let it ring
I’m coming home
With a worn-out thing
Pick up, pick up
Hear me now
I’m still your man
I’m still around

Blacktop stretched forever
Concrete dreams
They paved the last meadow
Crushed all the green
Where’d the birds go man
Where’d the wild things run

Last tree standing
Just one last tree
Against the grey tide
What’s left of me
Roots down deep down
Fight the stone ground

Wind whispers through it
A lonely sound
Tells tales of old dirt
Before this whole town
Just steel and glass towers
No place to breathe

Last tree standing
Just one last tree
Against the grey tide
What’s left of me
Roots down deep down
Fight the stone ground

They call it progress
A brave new world
But what about solace
A flag unfurled
Of bark and of branch
A silent

songs.subject: a indie rock song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They tore the diner down
Made it into a lot
Painted lines on every block
Like that could make a heart stop
But at the dead-end fence
Where the bus stop used to be
You still lift your branches up
Like you're keeping watch for me

And the kids walk by
With their shoes full of dust
They touch your bark like a promise
Like they still believe in us

Last tree standing
Last tree standing
Hold your ground for me
Last tree standing
Last tree standing
Don't let them bury the leaves

The barber shop is glass now
The river got a curb
They named the old main road
After some man no one heard
But you grew through the cracks
In that busted sidewalk seam
Roots under broken concrete
Like you were guarding a dream

And the sun still lands
In your green, small crown
Like a stubborn little blessing
On a town that gave out

Last tree standing
Last tree standing
Hold your ground for me
Last tree standing
Last tree standing
Don't let them bury the leaves

I know they want a cleaner map
A flat and final line
But your rings still keep the stories
Of the things they left behind
So bend, but don't break
When the hot wind comes
Stay here in the open
For the ones who need some

Last tree standing
Last tree standing
Hold your ground for me
Last tree standing
Last tree standing
Don't let them bury the leaves
Last tree standing
Last tree standing

songs.subject: a gospel song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning sun it shines so bright
But my heart it feels like darkest night
Preacher man he preaches grace and love
But my soul just aches for what's above

Oh Lord have mercy on this fool
Lost my blessing broke every rule
The one that got away my sweet refrain
Now all I feel is pouring rain

I saw her face in every hymn they sang
Heard her laughter in every bell's clang
Thought I knew it all thought I was so wise
Now I see the truth in tear-filled eyes

Oh Lord have mercy on this fool
Lost my blessing broke every rule
The one that got away my sweet refrain
Now all I feel is pouring rain

Maybe one day maybe I will mend
This broken spirit

songs.subject: a gospel song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I saw your shadow in the pew
Right by the window, wearing blue
You held my hand and then you ran
Left me with dust in my plans

I kept your number in my phone
Kept that old coat you used to own
But every time I reached for you
Grace was already breaking through

Lord, You know my stubborn heart
You knew this pain before it старт
If I can't keep what I can hold
Keep my soul when nights turn cold

You were the one that got away
But You were never out of sight
You took my tears and taught me praise
You turned my midnight into light
The one that got away
Still covered by Your hand today

I tried to chase what wouldn't stay
Tried to make a grave out of the way
But mercy met me in the loss
And love was shining through the cross

Lord, You know my stubborn heart
You knew this pain before it started
If I can't keep what I can hold
Keep my soul when nights turn cold

You were the one that got away
But You were never out of sight
You took my tears and taught me praise
You turned my midnight into light
The one that got away
Still covered by Your hand today

Maybe some doors close for good
So You can lead me where I should
What I lost was not my crown
What You gave me holds me down

Hold me now
Hold me now

You were the one that got away
But You were never out of sight
You took my tears and taught me praise
You turned my midnight into light
The one that got away
Still covered by Your hand today
The one that got away

songs.subject: a afrobeats song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
He clocks in at noon
Keys on a worn blue cord
Walks past the marble lions
Checks every corner, every door
There’s one frame by the stairs
Gold crack in the side
She wears a red cap tilted
Like she knows he’s by
He says, “Morning, miss”
Though she never talks back
But her eyes hold the hallway
And that’s where his mind stays at

He wipes the glass, slow
She stays in place
Every visitor walks through
He can’t walk away

Guarded frame, guarded frame
You got me standing here again
Guarded frame, guarded frame
I swear you look right back at me
Guarded frame, guarded frame
One step and I’m gone, then you pull me in
Guarded frame, guarded frame
That painted face got hold of me

Kids run by with sticky hands
Tour guide voice in the air
He leans on the rope line
Like he just needs a second there
She’s painted with a city fruit
And a silver chain
One cheek lit by window heat
One side holding rain
At closing time she glows
When the floor turns still
He lowers all the blinds down
Then she owns the room for real

He locks the last gate
She keeps her stare
Funny how a quiet room
Can feel like a dare

Guarded frame, guarded frame
You got me standing here again
Guarded frame, guarded frame
I swear you look right back at me
Guarded frame, guarded frame
One step and I’m gone, then you pull me in
Guarded frame, guarded frame
That painted face got hold of me

Tonight the power hums low
Just him and the walls
He swears she smiled wider
When the moon hit the glass
He laughs at himself
Then the room feels warm
Like a brushstroke moving
Through the middle of the storm

Guarded frame, guarded frame
You got me standing here again
Guarded frame, guarded frame
I swear you look right back at me
Guarded frame, guarded frame
One step and I’m gone, then you pull me in
Guarded frame, guarded frame
That painted face got hold of me

Tomorrow he’ll be back
Same keys, same pace
But every long shift feels shorter
When she knows his face
Guarded frame
Guarded frame

songs.lyrics:

Like a chain of small moons
Shoes kiss the marble floor
By the red rope rooms
At six, he checks the frame
At seven, she steals his breath
Gold light on the brushstrokes
And he forgets the rest

She got his eyes in a corner
He got her name on the chest
He says, "Don't move too fast now"
She says, "Watch me instead"
He knows every silent hallway

Like a chain of small moons
Shoes kiss the marble floor
By the red rope rooms
At six, he checks the frame
At seven, she steals his breath

And he forgets the rest

She got his eyes in a corner
He got her name on the chest
He says, "Don't move too fast now"
She says, "Watch me instead"
He knows every silent hallway

But that painting on the wall
Make the whole room feel home

She got his keys in a circle
He got her glow on his skin

songs.subject: a gospel song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
I was halfway underground
When my chest turned hard as stone
The car was full of strangers
But I felt so far from home
My hands went cold on the rail
My breath forgot my name
I stared at the door and whispered
Lord, don’t leave me in this frame

Then I counted one, two, three
Like a child at Sunday time
I said, Jesus, if You’re near
Reach in and hold my mind
When the tunnel shook and roared
I felt my fear lean in
But something in me started
Saying, “He is here again”

Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
Jesus, stay with me
Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
Till my lungs can sing

The man beside me looked away
The kid across my seat just slept
The station signs kept sliding by
While my eyes stayed wet
I pressed my palms against my jeans
Like I could keep me still
Then mercy hit me in the dark
Soft as an anchored will

Then I counted one, two, three
Like a child at Sunday time
I said, Jesus, if You’re near
Reach in and hold my mind
When the tunnel shook and roared
I felt my fear lean in
But something in me started
Saying, “He is here again”

Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
Jesus, stay with me
Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
Till my lungs can sing

If I can’t find the words
You know my shaking frame
If I can’t lift my head
You still know my name
You meet me in the crush
You meet me in the crowd
You meet me in the dark
And You bring me out

Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
Jesus, stay with me
Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
Till my lungs can sing
Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
I am not alone
Hold me on the subway
Hold me on the subway
Take me safely home

songs.subject: a gospel song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Steel doors close my name in
Everybody looks right through me
Hands won’t stay still on my knees
Breath gets small in the back row seat
The tunnel leans and my chest turns tight
I say help me, Lord, tonight
I count the stops to keep me here
But the fear gets loud and near

So I lift my eyes
Past the shaking frame
I call Your name
I call Your name
When the air runs thin
And my heart won’t stay
You hold me in
You hold me in

Jesus, hold me now
Hold me now
In the fifth car, hold me now
Jesus, hold me now
Hold me now
You are here, You hold me now

A child in a red coat smiles
Like the world is still worth saving
My palm leaves the cold pole
And finds a promise I was bracing
The floor keeps rolling underneath
But it can’t pull me away
I taste tears and borrowed peace
As the crowd turns into praise

So I lift my eyes
Past the shaking frame
I call Your name
I call Your name
When the air runs thin
And my heart won’t stay
You hold me in
You hold me in

Jesus, hold me now
Hold me now
In the fifth car, hold me now
Jesus, hold me now
Hold me now
You are here, You hold me now

If I fall apart, You stay
If I lose my words, You pray
If my mind won’t let me rest
You are nearer than my breath
I was trembling, now I’m found
I was sinking, now I’m grounded
Every station, every mile
Your mercy meets me on this ride

Jesus, hold me now
Hold me now
In the fifth car, hold me now
Jesus, hold me now
Hold me now
You are here, You hold me now
Jesus, hold me now
Hold me now

songs.subject: a R&B song about a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates in the rack
Now one gets dried by hand
I still set out two forks
Then laugh when I understand
Her apron hangs by the stove
Like she just stepped out for air
I learned the rice by the box
And your name in every prayer

I burned the onions once
Tears ran for both of us
Now I know the timing
Even when the house feels rough

One plate left
One plate left
Still I make it look like home
One plate left
One plate left
But I’m not eating all alone
(One plate left)
(I’m learning slow)

I buy the small tomatoes
The ones you liked to split
I cut the crusts off my toast
Guess I kept your habit
The measuring cup gets low
So I trust my own two hands
I season by memory
And that’s where your kindness stands

I burned the onions once
Tears ran for both of us
Now I know the timing
Even when the house feels rough

One plate left
One plate left
Still I make it look like home
One plate left
One plate left
But I’m not eating all alone
(One plate left)
(I’m learning slow)

Tonight I made your soup
Then saved a bowl by the sink
Old love in a new dish
Harder than I thought to think
I talk to the steam
When the kitchen goes quiet
Say, “I’m getting there, babe”
And the ache don’t like it

One plate left
One plate left
Still I make it look like home
One plate left
One plate left
But I’m not eating all alone
(One plate left)
(I’m learning slow)
(One plate left)
(I’m still learning to come back home)

songs.lyrics:
Two plates in the sink
One mug on the side
Your ring still glints
When I reach for the rice
I stand by the stove
With your old blue pan
Burnt onions and salt
Learning my hand

I used to make it for two
Now the quiet fills the room
I stir and I breathe
I’m trying to

Cooking for one
Cooking for one
(For one)
Still making it warm
Still setting the sun
Cooking for one
Cooking for one
(For one)
If I feed this heart
Maybe I won’t come undone

Half a lime left
In the door shelf tray
A lone tomato
Going soft today
I chop too slow
Then I laugh out loud
You’d say, “Baby, easy”
From the other side of the cloud

I used to make it for two
Now the radio knows my shoes
I plate up hope
Like I’m learning to

Cooking for one
Cooking for one
(For one)
Still making it warm
Still setting the sun
Cooking for one
Cooking for one

Too much thyme, too much heat
But I sat at the table
And I ate it with me
I miss your laugh
At the corner of the pan
Still, I’m here
With a spoon in my hand

Cooking for one
Cooking for one
(For one)
Still making it warm
Still setting the sun
Cooking for one
Cooking for one
(For one)

Maybe I won’t come undone

songs.subject: a reggae song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise glowin'
Airwaves flowin'
Through the static through the haze
We send out our sonic craze
No government no big machine
Just good vibrations on the scene

Pirate signal risin' high
Underneath the open sky
Pirate signal tellin' truth
For the old folk for the youth
Pirate signal can't be stopped
Till every last beat has dropped

Speakers thumpin'
Riddim jumpin'
From a rusty metal shack
No one ever lookin' back
Vinyl spinnin' sweet and low
Plantin' seeds where good vibes grow

Pirate signal risin' high
Underneath the open sky
Pirate signal tellin' truth
For the old folk for the youth
Pirate signal can't be stopped
Till every last beat has dropped

They try to trace us
Try to chase us
But our frequencies they roam
Always findin' a new home
From the rooftops to the sea
Radio sets us all free

Pirate signal risin'

songs.subject: a reggae song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Old van parked by the pier
A wire runs up to the mast
I flip that switch at midnight
And the whole wide harbor wakes up fast
Blue ink logbook in my lap
Names, requests, and battered cans
Mummy radio, hidden treasure
Turn the dial, touch the land

Hear that crackle?
That’s my name
Spin the wheel now
Call my lane
One little signal
Two hundred miles
Cross the water
Light the smiles

Seafoam frequency
Come through me
Seafoam frequency
Set them free
(Seafoam frequency)
From the shack to the sea
Seafoam frequency
Come through me

Captain says I’m trouble
Cause I send out love by night
I’ve got salsa, ska, and old souls
And a preacher’s voice in fight
A milk crate throne in salt air
A bent mic and a careful hand
I patch the wires with guitar string
Till the static learns to dance

Hear that crackle?
That’s my name
Spin the wheel now
Call my lane
One little signal
Two hundred miles
Cross the water
Light the smiles

Seafoam frequency
Come through me
Seafoam frequency
Set them free
(Seafoam frequency)
From the shack to the sea
Seafoam frequency
Come through me

If they come with boots and paper
I’ll be gone before the dawn
Hide the deck in oilcloth
Keep the rhythm rolling on
Every kid with a stolen earbud
Knows the code I leave behind
When the harbor looks too quiet
Find me in the line

Seafoam frequency
Come through me
Seafoam frequency
Set them free
(Seafoam frequency)
From the shack to the sea
Seafoam frequency

songs.subject: a post-rock song about quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Empty pews just wood and dust
Sunday mornings lost their trust
Whispers faded hymns too weak
Truth I searched but could not speak

Unanswered bells ring hollow now
Unanswered bells I wonder how
Unanswered bells the silence grows
Unanswered bells nobody knows

Stained glass colors dim and grey
Childhood faith just slipped away
No big fight no angry word
Just a quiet un-heard bird

Unanswered bells ring hollow now
Unanswered bells I wonder how
Unanswered bells the silence grows
Unanswered bells nobody knows

Footsteps echo down the aisle
Left behind a forced smile
Searching for some other light
Lost in the long cold night

Unanswered bells ring hollow now
Unanswered bells I wonder

songs.subject: a post-rock song about quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I folded my coat at the back
By the hymn book rack
Didn’t say a word to the pews
Just tied my shoes
The stained glass watched me
Like it knew my name
I kept my hands in my sleeves
And left the same

I heard the voices climb
Through the wall of pine and dust
I held the door a second
Long enough to trust

I’m leaving by the side door
I’m leaving by the side door
My hands are still shaking
But I know what they’re for
I’m leaving by the side door
I’m leaving by the side door
Soft as a prayer
Then gone to the floor

Mama’s Bible on the shelf
Still bent from use
Dad’s old tie on the chair
Like a borrowed truth
I learned the names of mercy
The shape of fear
I loved that old room
I just can’t stay here

The choir rose up behind me
Like rain on tin
I took one last breath
And let the quiet in

I’m leaving by the side door
I’m leaving by the side door
My hands are still shaking
But I know what they’re for
I’m leaving by the side door
I’m leaving by the side door
Soft as a prayer
Then gone to the floor

No fire in the rafters
No shout from the frame
Just a small clean break
And my own true name

I’m leaving by the side door
I’m leaving by the side door
My hands are still shaking
But I know what they’re for
I’m leaving by the side door
I’m leaving by the side door
Soft as a prayer

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We shared one hatch, one view
Moon dust on your sleeve
Your laugh kept time with mine
Now it slips away from me
I count your boots on the floor
Like you might still come back

Our gloves touched once
Then twice
Now the silence has a pulse
I hear your name
In the glass
As the station turns us loose

Drift apart, drift apart
Still I feel your orbit
Drift apart, drift apart
But I can't unlearn your pull
Two small sparks
In a cold black water
Drift apart, drift apart
And I'm still circling you (still circling you)

You float near the hatch now
I stay by the porthole light
Same stars, different hunger
Same breath, different night
Your postcard home on the panel
Keeps bending in the spin
I say your name to the static
Like it might bring you in

Our gloves touched once
Then twice
Now the silence has a pulse
I hear your name
In the glass
As the station turns us loose

Drift apart, drift apart
Still I feel your orbit
Drift apart, drift apart
But I can't unlearn your pull
Two small sparks
In a cold black water
Drift apart, drift apart
And I'm still circling you (still circling you)

If we meet in the dark
Will you know my face?
If the tether comes undone
Can love find a place?

I hold the frame
You hold the sky
We let the whole thing slip
And still we try

Drift apart, drift apart
Still I feel your orbit
Drift apart, drift apart
But I can't unlearn your pull
Two small sparks
In a cold black water
Drift apart, drift apart
And I'm still circling you (still circling you)

But I can't unlearn your pull
Two small sparks
In a cold black water
Drift apart, drift apart
And I'm still circling you (still circling you)

If we meet in the dark
Will you know my face?
If the tether comes undone
Can love find a place?

I hold the frame
You hold the sky
We let the whole thing slip
And still we try

Drift apart, drift apart
Still I feel your orbit
Drift apart, drift apart
But I can't unlearn your pull
Two small sparks
In a cold black water
Drift apart, drift apart
And I'm still circling you (still circling you)

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Floating free in this tin can
Miles from home a cosmic plan
You're next to me but far away
What did the stars have to say

Our love a satellite
Lost in endless night

Zero G heartbreak
A lonely space ache
Gravity gone wild
Lost like a space child

The comms are crackling low
Your eyes a distant glow
We built a world out here
Now it's just cosmic fear

Remember Earth that blue marble
Our promises seemed so stable

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
The coastguard calls on channel nine
A storm's a-brewin'
A dark
Cold sign
But I’m up here
In the old lighthouse tower
Spinning records
Hour after hour
The salt spray mists the window pane
Just me and the static
And the driving rain

Oh
The airwave corsair
Sailin' free
Across the ether
For all to see
No flags to fly
No laws to mind
Just voices whisperin' on the wind
The airwave corsair
Bold and grand
Bringing music to the barren land

They say it's wrong
A trespassin' sound
Against the rules
On hallowed ground
But what's a voice
If it ain't heard?
A silent song
A forgotten word
So I crank the gain
And I mend the wire
Feeding the hungry
Burning fire

Oh
The airwave corsair
Sailin' free
Across the ether
For all to see
No flags to fly
No laws to mind
Just voices whisperin' on the wind
The airwave corsair
Bold and grand
Bringing music to the barren land

They sent their men
With their warrants and their frowns
Searched every cove in these forgotten towns
But a signal's like a ghost
You see
Here one moment
Then suddenly
It's free
Just a flicker on the dial
Then gone
Wait for the next moon
And the break of dawn

Oh
The airwave corsair
Sailin' free
Across the ether
For all to see

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Down by the pier at first light
I wound the wire through the rain
A tin shed leaned by the water
With a candle and a pocket of names
You came with a box of records
And a grin like a cutlass blade
We named the thing after thunder
Then we hid it in the bay

Turn the dial, love
Hear it wake
Every hungry heart
Finds a lane

Send it out, send it out
Over tide and town
Pirate radio
Keep the signal down
Send it out, send it out
Through the gulls and spray
Pirate radio
We’ll out-sing the day (out-sing the day)

Mackerel crates for the speakers
Salt on the soldered seam
Old map nailed to the rafters
And a lamp with a smoky beam
When the coastguard circled the harbor
We went still as the pilings below
Then I flipped that switch and laughed, dear
Let them chase a ghost

Turn it up, love
Soft and sly
One clean note
Cuts the sky

Send it out, send it out
Over tide and town
Pirate radio
Keep the signal down
Send it out, send it out
Through the gulls and spray
Pirate radio
We’ll out-sing the day (out-sing the day)

If they knock on the dockside door
If they follow the wire we laid
We’ll pack up the moon and roses
And sail where the old songs play
But tonight, in the black of the harbor
We’ve got one more fire to feed
One more voice for the wanderers
One more tune to leave

Send it out, send it out
Over tide and town
Pirate radio
Keep the signal down
Send it out, send it out
Through the gulls and spray
Pirate radio

songs.subject: a post-rock song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Dust on the trophy case
Years of losing seasons
Coach spitting fire
Empty seats echo

One last shot
Against the titans

Underdog heart
Beating loud
No surrender
Last chance saloon

Sweat stinging eyes
Bruises tell a story
Every single play
A prayer a scream a fight

They say we cant win
They say we dont belong
But something stirs tonight
A rumble in the gut

Underdog heart
Beating loud
No surrender
Last chance

Empty seats echo

One last shot

Underdog heart
Beating loud
No surrender
Last chance saloon

Bruises tell a story
Every single play
A prayer a scream a fight

They say we cant win
They say we dont belong
But something stirs tonight
A rumble in the gut

Underdog heart
Beating loud
No surrender
Last chance

songs.subject: a post-rock song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Fresh tape on the knees
Mud on the white lines
Fifth-string bench boys
Standing in the bright lime
The season wore thin
Like paint on the rail
But you kept showing up
With your name in the mail

One more snap
One more fight
One small town
Holding the night

Run for the last line
Run for the last line
We were never supposed to stay
Run for the last line
Run for the last line
Make them remember today
(remember today)

Coach lost his voice by noon
Hands in his jacket pockets
Dad in the metal bleachers
Grip white on the brackets
The scoreboard blinked red
Like a wound in the dark
But your hardest hit yet
Was the quiet in your hearts

One more snap
One more fight
One small town
Holding the night

Run for the last line
Run for the last line
We were never supposed to stay
Run for the last line
Run for the last line
Make them remember today
(remember today)

When the whistle went
It felt like a prayer
Every scar on the field
Still shining there
And if this was the end
Then end standing tall
With grass in your mouth
And your backs to the wall

Run for the last line
Run for the last line
We were never supposed to stay
Run for the last line
Run for the last line
Make them remember today
(remember today)
Run for the last line
Run for the last line
Til the night gives way to gray
Run for the last line
Run for the last line
You were giants all day

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The teacups sleep by the sink
Your boots by the fire
We cleared one corner for dancing
Though the night runs tired
Your hand found mine like a coat
Folded soft and old
And I saw the years in your knuckles
Like rings in gold

The gramophone coughs to life
One scratch, one sigh
You smile like you did on our wedding day
And I don't ask why

Come on then, love, one last dance
One last slow turn on the floor
Come on then, love, one last dance
We know this step by heart
(one last dance)
(one last dance)
Hold me close and let it go
Till the room can't hold no more

Your dress brushed the ash on the hearth
My sleeve caught the lace
The clock in the hall kept its old poor pace
We laughed in its face
You hummed that tune from the fair
When your skirt was blue
And I kissed the cheek where the salt dried fast
Like I always do

The floorboards creaked our names
Like they used to then
We took the long road round to the same song
And found it again

Come on then, love, one last dance
One last slow turn on the floor
Come on then, love, one last dance
We know this step by heart
(one last dance)
(one last dance)
Hold me close and let it go
Till the room can't hold no more

If morning comes with its plain white face
Let it come, let it stay
I have your hand, and you have mine
That is enough today
The candle leans in the glass
The fiddle holds one note
And every mile we nearly lost
Still lives in your coat

Come on then, love, one last dance
One last slow turn on the floor
Come on then, love, one last dance
We know this step by heart
(one last dance)
(one last dance)
Hold me close and let it go
Till the room can't hold no more
Come on then, love, one last dance
Last dance, and nothing more

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The dust motes dance in the window's gleam
Just like we did
A waking dream
The old wood groans beneath my feet
Remembering rhythms
Bittersweet

One last jig
My love
One last jig with me
Before the morning breaks
Before we cease to be
One last jig
My dear
Though the fire's low
One last jig
My love
Before it's time to go

Your hand
A map of all our years
Weathered and worn
Through laughter
Tears
The way it fit
Just so in mine
A story told in every line

One last jig
My love
One last jig with me
Before the morning breaks
Before we cease to be
One last jig
My dear
Though the fire's low
One last jig
My love
Before it's time to go

The fiddle sighs a lonesome tune
Underneath a sliver of the moon
The floorboards creak
A memory's sound

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
I set one plate by the sink
One cup by the stove
Her apron still on the hook
By the cracked back door
Burned my first batch of beans
Stood there grinnin' through the smoke
Didn't know a man could miss
A skillet this much

Now I read the box twice
And I taste as I go
Learnin' how to feed
A house that talks so low

Cookin' for one, cookin' for one
(One pan, one flame)
Cookin' for one, cookin' for one
(Still say her name)
If the gravy runs thin
I start again
Cookin' for one
Till it feels like home again

Found her recipe card
Stuck in the flour tin
Said a pinch of salt
And a little more thin
I chopped up an onion
Cried like it was rain
Then I laughed at myself
For lovin' the burn

Now the porch light's on
When I wash up the dish
It's a small kind of courage
But it's a start like this

Cookin' for one, cookin' for one
(One pan, one flame)
Cookin' for one, cookin' for one
(Still say her name)
If the gravy runs thin
I start again
Cookin' for one
Till it feels like home again

Tonight I made cornbread
Round as a full moon
Set the second spoon back
Like a thank-you too soon
I still leave the radio low
For the shape of her laugh
And I eat slow, real slow
On the good side of half

Cookin' for one, cookin' for one
(One pan, one flame)
Cookin' for one, cookin' for one
(Still say her name)
If the gravy runs thin
I start again
Cookin' for one
Till it feels like home again

One plate by the sink
One cup by the stove
And a little more healing
In the pot than I know

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Dust on the skillet
Cobwebs on the spice rack
Never touched a stove top
Always had her back
Now the kitchen's quiet
Too big for just me
Guess I'll learn to boil water
Eventually

Empty plate blues
Singin' low and slow
Empty plate blues
Nowhere left to go
Empty plate blues
Gonna learn to chew
Empty plate blues
Just me and you

Tried to make a casserole
Burned it to a crisp
Felt like a fool
Clenching my fist
Neighbors brought some supper
Bless their kindly hearts
Said "Son you gotta eat"
Tearing me apart

Empty plate blues
Singin' low and slow
Empty plate blues
Nowhere left to go
Empty plate blues
Gonna learn to chew
Empty plate blues
Just me and you

She loved her gravy thick
And biscuits light as air
Now I stare at recipes
With a vacant stare
One day I'll get it right

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
You fold the napkins slow
Like they still know our names
Your ring taps the glass
In a small, patient frame
The fish on your plate
Has gone cold by the time
You smile at the floor
And I trace the old line

We have danced these rooms
In our Sunday clothes
Through the crowded years
And the soft rain roads

One last dance, my dear
One last turn
Hold me close tonight
Let the cool floor burn
One last dance, my dear
We know the song
One last dance, my dear
Then we pass along

The photo by the lamp
Has your hair in a curl
From a summer in June
And a smaller world
I button your coat
Though the night feels mild
You laugh at that habit
The same way you smiled

We have worn these steps
Through the clocks and dust
You were my whole house
You were my first trust

One last dance, my dear
One last turn
Hold me close tonight
Let the cool floor burn
One last dance, my dear
We know the song
One last dance, my dear
Then we pass along

If the music ends
Before we do
Leave the porch light on
I’ll be good to you
We were never young
For very long
Still your hand in mine
Still our little song

One last dance, my dear
One last turn
Hold me close tonight
Let the cool floor burn
One last dance, my dear
We know the song
One last dance, my dear
Then we pass along

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The waiter stacks our plates
like tiny moonlit boats
Your ring still taps the glass
when you reach for my coat
The salsa on your sleeve
is from a better day
We laugh at the old slow tune
and let the years lean away

One more spin
one more glide
Keep my hand
close to your side

Last dance, last dance
hold me in the sway
Last dance, last dance
before we drift away
We were a house of fire
now we’re a field of rain
Last dance, last dance
say my name again (say my name again)

The clock above the bar
won’t notice what we know
Your shoes have lost their shine
but your steps still steal the show
We learned each other’s storms
the way the cedar bends
And even now your smile
feels like coming home again

One more spin
one more glide
Let the night
fold us inside

Last dance, last dance
hold me in the sway
Last dance, last dance
before we drift away
We were a house of fire
now we’re a field of rain
Last dance, last dance
say my name again (say my name again)

If this is where the song
lets go of the refrain
I’ll wear your palm in mine
like summer after rain
No need to turn the light
no need to say it plain
Just keep your forehead close
and spin me once again

Last dance, last dance
hold me in the sway
Last dance, last dance
before we drift away
We were a house of fire
now we’re a field of rain
Last dance, last dance

now we’re a field of rain
Last dance, last dance
say my name again (say my name again)

If this is where the song
lets go of the refrain
I’ll wear your palm in mine
like summer after rain
No need to turn the light
no need to say it plain
Just keep your forehead close
and spin me once again

Last dance, last dance
hold me in the sway
Last dance, last dance
before we drift away
We were a house of fire
now we’re a field of rain
Last dance, last dance

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They paved the roads on his ribs
Put windows in his skin
Coffee carts on the corner
Where his old bones begin
I hear him shift at midnight
Under trash bins and rain
Every manhole trembles
Like he knows my name

And the church bells lean
When he turns in sleep
We all hold our breath
When the shadows creep

We live on the giant
We live on the giant
One eye in the water
One hand in the dirt
We live on the giant
We live on the giant
If he wakes up slowly
This whole town gets heard

Kids kick dust by the seawall
Paint their names on stone
Skate past the old quarry
Like they own the bones
My mother says be careful
With your feet on this ground
It was built on a heartbeat
That still moves around

And the glass on Main Street
Rings before the storm
Like a secret warning
Under every porch

We live on the giant
We live on the giant
One eye in the water
One hand in the dirt
We live on the giant
We live on the giant
If he wakes up slowly
This whole town gets heard

Last night the harbor lights swayed
Though the air was still
I saw the old hill breathing
Past the paper mill
So I ran to the edge road
With my heart in my throat
And I heard him dreaming
Like a freight train low

We live on the giant
We live on the giant
One eye in the water
One hand in the dirt
We live on the giant
We live on the giant
If he wakes up slowly
This whole town gets heard
We live on the giant
We live on the giant
And if he opens both eyes
We’ll know what we were worth

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Concrete towers scrape the sky
But down below something sleeps
Deep earth rumbles
A low sigh
The city never weeps

Sleeping giant
Stone heart beating slow
Sleeping giant
Where did you go
Underneath us all this time
A silent colossal show

Cracked pavement
Shifting plates
He breathes beneath the stone
What fate
A restless dream
We build our homes alone

Sleeping giant
Stone heart beating slow
Sleeping giant
Where did you go
Underneath us all this time
A silent colossal show

We dance on his chest
Unknowing unaware
Every tremor
A whispered prayer
Will he wake
Does he even care

Sleeping giant
Stone heart beating slow
Sleeping giant
Where did you go
Underneath us all this time

songs.subject: a ska song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The kettle clicked on
too loud for the house
Your coat still hung
like you were stepping out
Black shoes by the door
Wet from last night
I stood there staring
at the church hat on the chair

There were cups in the sink
and plates from the wake
Half a loaf of bread
and a slice nobody ate
Your cousin laughed once
then bit her lip tight
We all moved slow
in the morning after the fight

Sun on the curtains
made it all plain
Every little thing
still calling your name
And I wanted to say
it would all be fine
But the room held its breath
like it knew the line

Morning after tears
morning after tears
We keep on moving
through the morning after tears
Pass that toast around
hold each other near
We live one more day
through the morning after tears
(morning after tears)

The flower cards bent
in the window heat
A wet black ribbon
slapped against my sleeve
Your brother fixed the gate
like that would help somehow
He said, “Eat something”
and we both looked down

Sun on the curtains
made it all plain
Every little thing
still calling your name
And I wanted to say
it would all be fine
But the room held its breath
like it knew the line

Morning after tears
morning after tears
We keep on moving
through the morning after tears
Pass that toast around
hold each other near
We live one more day
through the morning after tears
(morning after tears)

I took your program
folded it in half
Put it in my pocket
like a map back
No grand goodbye
just a scraped-up grace
A sink full of water
and your empty place

Morning after tears
morning after tears
We keep on moving
through the morning after tears
Pass that toast around
hold each other near
We live one more day
through the morning after tears
(morning after tears)

songs.subject: a ska song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
I woke up with my tie on the chair
Same black shoes by the door
Leftover rice in the tray downstairs
And your name on the floor
My phone was dead, the room too still
The kettle clicked once, then quit
I stood by the sink with my hands half full
Like I forgot how to live with it

Sun on the tiles
Too clean, too bright
Everybody gone
But the garden’s still wet
I can hear your laugh
In the yard out back
And I hate that I’m smiling
At that

Morning after
Morning after
We still breathe
Morning after
Morning after
Same old street
But you’re not here
And I’m right here
Trying to stand
On a shaky pair

There were chairs folded up in the hall
Paper cups by the sink
A cousin took home the empty vase
And didn’t say what he think
I found your coat on the chair by the hall
Still smelling like rain and wood
I touched the sleeve like a bad habit
Like that might do some good

Sun on the tiles
Too clean, too bright
Everybody gone
But the garden’s still wet
I can hear your laugh
In the yard out back
And I hate that I’m smiling
At that

Morning after
Morning after
We still breathe
Morning after
Morning after
Same old street
But you’re not here
And I’m right here
Trying to stand
On a shaky pair

No more speeches
No more stairs
Just your chair
And the air
I fold the card
Read the line
Lose my place
Then find my time

Morning after
Morning after
We still breathe
Morning after
Morning after
Same old street
But you’re not here
And I’m right here
Trying to stand
On a shaky pair
Morning after
Morning after
We still breathe
Morning after
Morning after
I will be
Here with the ache

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning
Lamp on the page
Cold tea gone bitter
Room feels like a cage
My pencil keeps tapping
Like a loose porch board
I read that same paragraph
And ask for one more

Just me and the ceiling
Just me and the rain
One more chapter
One more try at the name
If I stay with it
I might get it right
Keep my eyes on the line
Keep my heart in the light

Two in the morning
I’m still here
Two in the morning
I’m drawing near
(Page by page)
I won’t give in
(Two in the morning)
I’ll let it in

Stack of old notes
Bent at the spine
Little blue tab marks
Running straight down the line
My window’s foggy
My chair gives a groan
But I’m building something
Out here all alone

Just me and the ceiling
Just me and the rain
One more chapter
One more try at the name
If I stay with it
I might get it right
Keep my eyes on the line
Keep my heart in the light

Two in the morning
I’m still here
Two in the morning
I’m drawing near
(Page by page)
I won’t give in
(Two in the morning)
I’ll let it in

Sun ain’t risen yet
But I know this road
Every quiet hour
Adds another load
To the mule that’s pulling
This tired mind ahead
I’ll take one more breath
Then I’ll turn that last page

Two in the morning
I’m still here
Two in the morning
I’m drawing near
(Page by page)
I won’t give in
(Two in the morning)
I’ll let it in

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The sink holds a paper plate
My chair makes one soft creak
Books piled like chopped-up wood
On a table worn and weak
Two in the mornin', black as coal
My pencil's runnin' thin
I teach myself this hard old world
One page at a time again

The lamp burns low and gold
The house is holdin' still
I count the facts out slow
Like seeds on a windowsill

Two in the mornin'
I keep goin'
Two in the mornin'
Pages glowin'
If I get it now
I'll set it right
Two in the mornin'
That's my fight (that's my fight)

There's a red cup by the sink
And a dent in my old cap
A note from my brother says
"Don't let your bright mind slip"
I trace the lines with tired eyes
But I don't let go the pen
'Cause every little thing I learn
Comes back to me again

The clock keeps tapping nails
Through the beams above my head
But I can hear my name
In every word I've read

Two in the mornin'
I keep goin'
Two in the mornin'
Pages glowin'
If I get it now
I'll set it right
Two in the mornin'
That's my fight (that's my fight)

When the rooster calls from far off
I'll still have one more line
The dawn can find me bent over
But it won't say I quit this time
No, it won't say I quit this time

Two in the mornin'
I keep goin'
Two in the mornin'
Pages glowin'
If I get it now
I'll set it right
Two in the mornin'

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Oy vey this ship it rocks and rolls
My stomach it controls
The captain he looks green
The worst I’ve ever seen

A golem in the hold
A story to be told
This cargo it is cursed
My bladder it might burst

We left the port so grand
With blessings from the land
Now storms they rage and bite
I fear for morning light

A golem in the hold
A story to be told
This cargo it is cursed
My bladder it might burst

The crates they groan and creak
A silent eerie speak
I hear a whispered plea
Is it just me or do you see

A golem in the hold
A story to be told
This cargo it is cursed
My

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Ocean stretchin' wide
Nowhere left to hide
This old ship it groans
Full of creakin' bones
And one heavy chest
Givin' us no rest

Oy vey this cargo
Heavy like my sorrow
It just won't go
This cursed cargo
We sail and we sail
Against every gale
This cursed cargo
It makes grown men wail

Days turn into weeks
The captain he speaks
Of shadows that dance
A most evil glance
From the lock on the lid
What is truly hid

Oy vey this cargo
Heavy like my sorrow
It just won't go
This cursed cargo
We sail and we sail
Against every gale
This cursed cargo
It makes grown men wail

We tried to throw it deep
While the whole crew did sleep
But it floated right back
On the ship's weary track
It laughs at our fear
Whispers in our ear

Oy vey this cargo
Heavy like my sorrow
It just won't go
This cursed cargo

songs.subject: a synthwave song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Punch in at six
Punch out at noon
Badge on my belt
Then I change rooms
Grease on my cuffs
Receipt in my hand
I eat in the car
And start over again

One check goes fast
The next one vanishes
Rent at the door
And the fridge stays empty
I keep my head up
I keep my head down
Still every week
I end up behind

Clocked twice, still behind
Clocked twice, still behind
I run all day
And I can't outrun time
Clocked twice, still behind
Clocked twice, still behind
I give you my all
It still don't buy enough

Second shift starts
When the sun goes low
Blue shirt at one job
Black apron at the next
I count change twice
Then I count my steps
Same worn-out shoes
Same hole in my breath
My phone lights up
With another bill
I say "next payday"
But payday won't heal

One check goes fast
The next one vanishes
Rent at the door
And the fridge stays empty
I keep my head up
I keep my head down
Still every week
I end up behind

Clocked twice, still behind
Clocked twice, still behind
I run all day
And I can't outrun time
Clocked twice, still behind
Clocked twice, still behind
I give you my all
It still don't buy enough

If I fold clean shirts
If I smile at the line
If I skip my lunch
Will I finally be fine?
But the miles keep coming
And the numbers stay cold
I swear I'm moving
Just not moving up

Clocked twice, still behind
Clocked twice, still behind
I run all day
And I can't outrun time
Clocked twice, still behind
Clocked twice, still behind
I give you my all
It still don't buy enough

Clocked twice
Still behind

Still behind

songs.lyrics:
Wake up before the sun
Punch in at the store
Grease on my collar
Keys on the floor
Swing shift at the diner
Name tag bent
I count my tips
And they all got spent

I do the math at red lights
It never adds right
Bills come due in a line
And I’m still behind

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I run all day
Still losing time
Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
Payday comes

With my eyes half shut

Boss says, "Hang in"

But the rent man wants
What I never can

Two jobs, still behind

Still losing time

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
Payday comes
Then it walks on by

One more check
Won’t fix this drag
One more month
Won’t fill that bag
But I keep going
I keep my name
Even when the numbers
Know my shame

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I run all day
Still losing time
Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
Payday comes
Then it walks on by

songs.subject: a disco song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
I climb the stairs in the rain
With a milk crate full of names
A battery humming in my palm
And a needle shaking off the calm
I tape the dial in place
Hide the wire by the vent
Your landlord thinks you sleep
But I’m alive in the basement

Hear that crackle start to bloom
Turn the whole room into a boom
One more twist and we take flight
Keep it low till we hit the night

Spin it, spin it, black box radio
Spin it, spin it, let the whole block know
We run wild on the midnight wave
Out of sight but we behave
Spin it, spin it, black box radio
(Black box radio)

A taxi horn, a chipped-up cup
A girl in pearls with a dirty laugh
She says, “Play that record again”
I say, “Baby, I’m your secret map”
Cops roll slow at the curb
I mute the mic, hold my breath
Then your voice cuts through the static
Like a match in a room of theft

Hear that crackle start to bloom
Turn the whole room into a boom
One more twist and we take flight
Keep it low till we hit the night

Spin it, spin it, black box radio
Spin it, spin it, let the whole block know
We run wild on the midnight wave
Out of sight but we behave
Spin it, spin it, black box radio
(Black box radio)

If they find us, we’re a rumor
Just a pulse behind the wall
But tonight the whole city leans in
When the black box starts to call

Spin it, spin it, black box radio
Spin it, spin it, let the whole block know
We run wild on the midnight wave
Out of sight but we behave
Spin it, spin it, black box radio
(Black box radio)
(Black box radio)

Turn it loose
Every midnight

I can hear them
Lean right in
When the needle
Starts to spin

Black flag frequency
Black flag frequency
Come and steal the night with me
(Black flag frequency)
Black flag frequency
Black flag frequency
Spin that hush into the street
(Black flag frequency)

We are ghosts in a rented room
With a jammed-up timecard clock
Slipping songs through a chain-link cloud
And a pile of busted locks
My friend knows the ferry horn
My girl knows the bakery shift
I call out to the corner boys
Then I send another lift

Turn it up
Make it burn
Let the whole block
Catch the turn
If they shut us
We come back
On a cleaner
Silver track

Black flag frequency
Black flag frequency
Come and steal the night with me

Come and steal the night with me

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
I saw your name light up my screen
In the middle of my supper
The text said, “We’re through, don’t call me back”
Just like that, my heart went under
You sent a smile and then goodbye
Three little dots, then nothing
I stared at that blue little check mark
Till the whole room started humming

No knock on my door
No look in your eyes
Just cold little words
And a red sunset in disguise

Blue tick goodbye
Blue tick goodbye
You broke my heart
In a little white box
Blue tick goodbye
Blue tick goodbye
I still hear your name
When the whole phone stops
(Blue tick goodbye)
(Blue tick goodbye)

Your sweater’s still on my chair at home
Your record by the sink
I keep reaching for a voice that’s gone
More than you might think
We used to talk till the moon got pale
Now I talk to a screen
You left me standing in my own front hall
Like I’d never been seen

No slow goodbye
No last embrace
Just one cold line
And an empty little space

Blue tick goodbye
Blue tick goodbye
You broke my heart
In a little white box
Blue tick goodbye
Blue tick goodbye
I still hear your name
When the whole phone stops
(Blue tick goodbye)
(Blue tick goodbye)

I don’t need forever
I just needed truth
A little more mercy
From the one I chose for you
So keep your soft excuses
Keep your quiet lie
I learned the hardest lesson
In a single goodbye

Blue tick goodbye
Blue tick goodbye
You broke my heart
In a little white box
Blue tick goodbye
Blue tick goodbye
I let you go
But it still hurts a lot
(Blue tick goodbye)
(Blue tick goodbye)
(Blue tick goodbye)

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
You sent it clean
No ringing phone
Just three small words
And I was done
I stared at my screen
Like it could talk
But the little white bubble
Gave me my walk

Last night you wore
My borrowed coat
We split a milkshake
At the corner shop
This morning you typed
It’s over, babe
I dropped my keys
In the sink by the drain

Ooh, my hand got cold
Ooh, my heart went slow
One little buzz
And the whole world broke
Ooh, you let me go

You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
No goodbye kiss
No last slow dance
Just a tiny screen
And I lost my chance

I called your name
At the front door steps
Then laughed out loud
At what love becomes
A spell check curse
A tiny glow
You left me with
A one-line ghost

Ooh, my hand got cold
Ooh, my heart went slow
One little buzz
And the whole world broke
Ooh, you let me go

You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
No goodbye kiss
No last slow dance
Just a tiny screen
And I lost my chance

Maybe someday
You’ll miss my face
Not in your phone
But in some place
Where words have weight
And eyes stay kind
Where love don’t end
In a two-line sign

You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
No goodbye kiss
No last slow dance
Just a tiny screen
And I lost my chance
You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
You broke my heart by text
(Sha-la-la, sha-la-la)
Now I sing this tune
For a shattered night
One little buzz

songs.lyrics:
Needles in the tin
Tape on the chair
He cuts through denim
Like trouble in air
Lined up at dawn
For cuffs and hems
He knows your size
Before you speak

One pin
Two pins
Hold that breath
Hands move fast
Like a threat

Block Stitch!
He dresses the whole block
Block Stitch!
From the stoop to the block
Fresh seams!
Fresh heat!
Look sharp
Look mean
Block Stitch!
He dresses the whole block

Work shirt patched
School pants pressed
Auntie in emerald
Looking her best
Backyard wedding
Barbershop suit
Kids in crisp collars
Running in boots
Old heads nod
When he walks by
He makes that fabric
Tell the truth

One pin
Two pins
Hold that breath
Hands move fast
Like a threat

Block Stitch!
He dresses the whole block
Block Stitch!
From the stoop to the block
Fresh seams!
Fresh heat!
Look sharp
Look mean
Block Stitch!
He dresses the whole block

Scissors flash
Steam rise
He works that cloth
Like a fight
Call his name
Down the street
Everybody clean
Everybody neat

Block Stitch!
He dresses the whole block
Block Stitch!
From the stoop to the block
Fresh seams!
Fresh heat!
Look sharp
Look mean
Block Stitch!
He dresses the whole block

songs.subject: a hardcore song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
He got chalk on his sleeve
And pins in his mouth
Front stoop to the barbershop
He sews that block out loud
Canvas bag full of patches
Needle flashing like a blade
Your coat came in mangled
He made it look paid

Tap-tap, cut the line
He knows every fit
One hem, two snaps
Whole block in it

Block seam king
He dress the whole block
Block seam king
Make that old fit talk
Block seam king
Fresh from the floor
Block seam king
They come back for more

He takes worn denim
Turns it sharp as pride
Brings a hard crease back
To a kid from the side
Uncle need a sleeve patch
Shorty need a suit
He measure the hurt
Then he make it lookूट?
Every Friday night
The porch light stays on
Folks line up in silence
Till the job is gone

Tap-tap, cut the line
He knows every fit
One hem, two snaps
Whole block in it

Block seam king
He dress the whole block
Block seam king
Make that old fit talk
Block seam king
Fresh from the floor
Block seam king
They come back for more

Needle in the moonlight
Thread through the rain
He stitch up the broke ones
Name by name
From the school coat buttons
To the church-day gold
If you need a new shape
He got fold on fold

Block seam king
He dress the whole block
Block seam king
Make that old fit talk
Block seam king
Fresh from the floor
Block seam king
Whole block want more

Pins in his pocket
Pride in his hands
One man in the alley

songs.subject: a indie rock song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Corner store shut by nine
Chain-link fence and busted signs
We learned the block by cracks
Counted time in passing cars
Mama said, "Keep your head"
But the hallway knew my name
Same four blocks, same old talk
Same old porch, same old shame

Then I saw that bus light swing
Past the pawn shop, past the yard
Something in me said, "Run"
Not fast, just hard

I'm getting out
I'm getting out
My feet still know this ground
But I won't live here now
(I'm getting out)
I'm getting out
I'm getting out
You can call me back
I won't turn around
(I'm getting out)

We sold dreams on the stoop
For a buck and half a smoke
Watched the summers bend and bend
Till the pavement learned our jokes
My best friend in that alley
Said, "You never really leave"
I laughed and packed my one good shirt
Like a dare I had to keep

Then I saw that bus light swing
Past the pawn shop, past the yard
Something in me said, "Run"
Not fast, just hard

I'm getting out
I'm getting out
My feet still know this ground
But I won't live here now
(I'm getting out)
I'm getting out
I'm getting out
You can call me back
I won't turn around
(I'm getting out)

I took the long way home
Just to say it out loud
Every dead-end on this block
Ends where I stand now

I'm getting out
I'm getting out
Hear the screen door slam
Hear the fear back down
(I'm getting out)
I'm getting out
I'm getting out
Same block, new blood
And I won't look down
(I'm getting out)

songs.subject: a indie rock song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Pavement cracks my soul
Grey sky hangs low
Another morning same old hole
Yeah same old hole

Gotta break free
Gotta break out
From this dead-end street
No doubt

Concrete heartbeat
Pounding hard and fast
Gonna make it out
Gonna make it last

Walls closing in
Whispers of despair
A silent scream within
Just don't care

They say you can't
They say you won't
But I gotta plant

songs.subject: a klezmer song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I kissed the mezuzah,
then hid my face.
Dust on the siddur,
dust in my place.
The candles burned low,
my prayers went thin.
I heard my own doubt
move in my skin.

Still, at the window,
I watched the dawn.
Something was calling
when I was gone.

Oy, my heart came home
Oy, my heart came home
I lost my flame
Then I found my name
Oy, my heart came home
Oy, my heart came home
Hands in the dark
Then I reached for light

I walked by the river,
boots full of rain.
Every old blessing
felt like a chain.
I broke like a plate
on a stubborn floor.
I said, "If You’re there,
I don't know You no more."

But the blue on the water,
the bread in my hand,
the laugh of my mother
would not understand.

Oy, my heart came home
Oy, my heart came home
I lost my flame
Then I found my name
Oy, my heart came home
Oy, my heart came home
Hands in the dark
Then I reached for light

I didn't return
with thunder and signs.
Just one small word
opening blinds.
Not fixed, not whole,
not pure, not new—
just a trembling yes
pulling me through.

Oy, my heart came home
Oy, my heart came home
I lost my flame
Then I found my name
Oy, my heart came home
Oy, my heart came home
Hands in the dark
Then I reached for light

Baruch again,
barely and true.
I was not lost.
I was finding You.
