songs.subject: a klezmer song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I dropped my prayer on the floor
Beside the door
Under the winter boots
I laughed too hard at my own doubt
Let the candles out
And hid from the roots
The book sat closed on the shelf
Like a shell
Like it knew my name
And every night I heard the yidish wind
Tap-tap-tap again
Calling me the same

I said, "What good is a wound
If it never heals?"
What good is a song
When the whole room kneels?
But the old street did turn
In my blood, in my bones
And a tiny spark said
"You are not alone"

Oy, I was far away
Far away from home
Oy, I was far away
But I found my way back home
Not by thunder
Not by proof
Just a hand on my heart
And a crack in the roof
(Oy, I was far away)

I walked past the bakery smoke
Past the old stone
Past the gate with rust
The rabbi's window was warm
Like a borrowed song
Like it never left us
I did not come back as pure
Or sure
Or shining clean
I came back with my pockets full of questions
And a little bit of
"Maybe" in between

I said, "Can a darkened lamp
Learn flame again?"
Can a lost little soul
Come running in?
Then the fiddles rose up
From the dusty floor
And the answer danced
At the opened door

Oy, I was far away
Far away from home
Oy, I was far away
But I found my way back home
Not by thunder
Not by proof
Just a hand on my heart
And a crack in the roof
(Oy, I was far away)

I kissed the mezuzah frame
With shaking breath
Like a child at play
I heard my grandfather laugh
In the kugel steam
From a mile away
Now I know faith is a lantern
Passed by hand
Through the crowded night
You can lose it in the mud
And still return
By a stubborn light

Oy, I was far away
Far away from home
Oy, I was far away
But I found my way back home
Not by thunder
Not by proof
Just a hand on my heart
And a crack in the roof

Beside the door

I laughed too hard at my own doubt
Let the candles out
And hid from the roots
The book sat closed on the shelf
Like a shell
Like it knew my name
And every night I heard the yidish wind

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
You left the plate on the sink
I left the words on the floor
Now the room feels too big
And the dog won’t come near the door
Your jacket on the chair
My keys by the bowl
We both moved around
Like the house lost its soul
I keep hearing your step
But it’s only the fan
I hate how the silence
Still knows who we are

Don’t say goodbye tonight
Not with that look in your eyes
We can let the air breathe
We can let it slide
Come a little closer
Let the hard words cool
I’m still on your side
I’m still on your side

Quiet after the fight
Quiet after the fight
Stay right here with me
Let the night go soft
Quiet after the fight
Quiet after the fight
We still got us left
Even when we’re rocked
(quiet after the fight)
(quiet after the fight)

The kettle cooled down
The sink is full of light
You trace circles in steam
I trace circles in my mind
Your hand brushed my hand
Like you were checking if I’d stay
That tiny little touch
Said more than we could say
No one’s winning tonight
No one’s keeping score
We’re just two tired hearts
Trying to find the door

Don’t turn love into dust
Don’t let pride take the wheel
We can leave this mess here
We can start to heal
Come a little closer
Let the hard words cool
I’m still on your side
I’m still on your side

Quiet after the fight
Quiet after the fight
Stay right here with me
Let the night go soft
Quiet after the fight
Quiet after the fight
We still got us left
Even when we’re rocked
(quiet after the fight)
(quiet after the fight)

Say my name again
Like you mean it gentle
I’ll give you my hand
You give me your temper
We don’t need a speech
Just one slow breath
Then the moon on the tile
Then the weight off our chests

Quiet after the fight
Quiet after the fight
Stay right here with me
Let the night go soft
Quiet after the fight
Quiet after the fight
We still got us left
Even when we’re rocked
Quiet after the fight

Hold me like before
Before the words got hot
(quiet after the fight)
(quiet after the fight)

songs.lyrics:
The dishes still sit there
One glass by the sink
Your key in the bowl
My mouth won't even blink
The fan keeps turning slow
Like it knows what we lost
Your shoes by the door
And the whole room feels crossed

We said too much
Then said none
Now the air is thin
And the night won't run
I look at you
You look away
Baby, this silence
Is loud today

Quiet after us
Quiet after us (after us)
Don't let it break us
Don't let it break us
Hold me close now
Hold me close now (close now)
Quiet after us
Quiet after us

Your hoodie on the chair
My phone facedown
We move like strangers
In our own town
I reach for your hand
You let it stay
Then your thumb finds mine
And the anger drains away

We said too much
Then said none
Now the air is thin
And the night won't run
I look at you
You look away
Baby, this silence

Quiet after us

Don't let it break us
Don't let it break us

Let them hit the floor

I just want you more
One small touch
One deep breath
We can fix the shape
Of what almost left

Quiet after us

Don't let it break us
Hold me close now

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
You cut the deck on my knee
At the green felt table
Your ring hit the glass
Like a tiny warning
King of spades in your sleeve
And I let you smile

The ashtray filled up fast
The clock lost its face
You said, "one more hand"
And the room leaned in
(leaned in)
My pulse went thin

It went too far
It went too far
We kept on raising
Till the morning split
(too far)
It went too far
It went too far
Now the cards are wet
In your shaking fist

Pocket queens on the floor
Under broken chips
Your laugh turned sharp
Like a glass in water
I saw my name
On the back of the ace
And my throat went dry
Before I could fold

The dealer light burned white
On your red-taped thumb
You said, "trust me now"
But the air went cold
(so cold)
And the table bent

It went too far
It went too far
We kept on raising
Till the morning split
(too far)
It went too far
It went too far
Now the cards are wet
In your shaking fist

I can still see the flop
In the salt on your sleeve
Three hearts, one bruise
And the room gone blind
(oh, oh)
If I call your bluff
Will you let me leave?

It went too far
It went too far
We kept on raising
Till the morning split
(too far)
It went too far
It went too far
Now the cards are wet
In your shaking fist

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We started with a deck
On a sticky motel table
Your thumb on the queen of spades
My luck in the ashtray
First it was for chips
Then it was for names
You smiled like a loaded gun
And dealt me back my shame

One more hand
One more lie
Your ring on the felt
My pulse too high

House of hearts
House of hearts
We lost the whole night there
House of hearts
House of hearts
You played me to the bone

By midnight the blinds were bent
And the ice had turned to water
You said, “Don’t flinch now”
Like you knew my father
I raised with a shaking hand
You called with that same grin
When the last card hit the wood
Something in me split within

One more hand
One more bite
Your eyes went cold
Mine burned white

House of hearts
House of hearts
We lost the whole night there
House of hearts
House of hearts
You played me to the bone

The ceiling turned to smoke
The table turned to a grave
We were betting years
On a game we couldn’t save
And when you slid the final card
I heard the whole room fold
My heart in your left hand
Your laughter pure and cold

House of hearts
House of hearts
We lost the whole night there
House of hearts
House of hearts
You played me to the bone

Now every shuffled deck
Sounds like that motel floor
House of hearts

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Badge still warm in my palm
Paper cup on the desk
Pumpkin pie in the break room
Going cold by the clock
Two more names on the board
And my feet know the floor
I tie my hair with numb hands
Smile like I did before

The hall keeps breathing
Long and slow
I pass the room where
The fake tree glows

Holiday double
Hold me up
Holiday double
I can’t stop
Holiday double
Call my name
(holiday double)
I’ll stay the same

Room twelve wants ice chips
Room nine wants the lights low
Someone’s watching old movies
From a tinny TV glow
I smooth a blanket corner
Check the pulse, check my pulse
My phone face-down in my pocket
Like a small, sealed house

The midnight tray rolls by
Like a tide
I keep my heart
On the inside

Holiday double
Hold me up
Holiday double
I can’t stop
Holiday double
Call my name
(holiday double)
I’ll stay the same

At the window, frost is soft
On the glass, on the glass
A radio from the nurses’ desk
Sings through the half-light
I think of your empty chair
One more plate, one more place
Then the overhead pager cracks
And I go back

Holiday double
Hold me up
Holiday double
I can’t stop
Holiday double
Call my name
(holiday double)
I’ll stay the same

Boots on waxed tile
Head full of snow
Holiday double
Till I can go

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blur to streaks of gold
Another Christmas morn unfolds
But I'm not home with stories old
Just coffee cold

My feet ache a silent plea
This fluorescent jubilee

Holiday double
A lonely bubble
Yeah holiday double
Just endless trouble

The machines they hum a steady beat
No carols here no sugar sweet
Just beeps and boops a bitter treat
On tired feet

The clock it crawls a sluggish friend
Will this long shift ever end

Holiday double
A lonely bubble
Yeah holiday double

songs.subject: a ska song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama laid out the plans
On the good white table
Two cups, two rings, two names
Like I was born to label
But I saw my shoes by the door
And my hands were shaking
That little boxed-in life
Started cracking, breaking

You said, "Stay where you're placed"
But I won't wear that frame
I heard my own heart knock
And it called my name

I'm leaving the paper suit
I'm leaving the paper suit
I want a life I choose
I want a life I choose
(hey, hey)
I want the road, not rules
I want the road, not rules

He had a blue-gray tie
And a borrowed smile
Talked about the family house
And a hallway mile
But the future they built for me
Had no room to breathe
So I folded up their map
And I let it leave

You said, "Don't make a scene"
But my feet won't stay
There's a whole wide world out there
And I'm halfway away

I'm leaving the paper suit
I'm leaving the paper suit
I want a life I choose
I want a life I choose
(hey, hey)
I want the road, not rules
I want the road, not rules

Put the keys in my palm
Then I let them fall
No more vows on a schedule
No more answering the call
If love is real, it waits
If hope is real, it runs
I'm not a finished answer
I'm a fistful of suns

I'm leaving the paper suit
I'm leaving the paper suit
I want a life I choose
I want a life I choose
(hey, hey)
I want the road, not rules
I want the road, not rules

songs.subject: a ska song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
There’s a blue suit
Hanging by the door
My name stitched wrong
Like it was before
A folded guest list
A ring in a box
Your mother’s timeline
Locked with the clocks

I tried to wear it
Tight and clean
But my chest said
Run, not lean

Blue suit goodbye
I’m out that door
Blue suit goodbye
Can’t wear it anymore
Blue suit goodbye
You can keep the plan
I’m not your promise
I’m my own man

Your cousin’s piano
Played that march again
The room smelled like lilies
And somebody’s skin
You said, “Be grateful”
I bit my tongue
But my future tasted
Like a borrowed song

I saw my name there
On the card
All neat and waiting
All too hard

Blue suit goodbye
I’m out that door
Blue suit goodbye
Can’t wear it anymore
Blue suit goodbye
You can keep the plan
I’m not your promise
I’m my own man

Open the gate
Let the whole thing shake
I left the script
For my own sake
No more “soon”
No more “should”
I want my life
Raw and loud and good

Blue suit goodbye
I’m out that door
Blue suit goodbye
Can’t wear it anymore
Blue suit goodbye
Hear that hornline slam
I’m not your promise
I’m my own man

Blue suit goodbye
Bye-bye, blue suit
Blue suit goodbye

songs.subject: a reggae song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
You wore that blue dress home
I had the words in my throat
Moon on the mango tree
And your hand was close to mine
The corner shop clock blinked
Like it knew what I was owed
Taxi lights rolled past slow
And my heart kicked down the road

We stood there at your gate
Breath hanging in the air
I leaned in, you leaned back
Like love was almost there

Almost made it
Almost made it
That night we nearly crossed the line
Almost made it
Almost made it
You were mine for a little while

Your brother called your name
So you laughed and turned away
I watched the screen door swing
Like it stole the night away
Later by the bus stop bench
I kept your perfume on my shirt
One more step, one brave move
And I could've changed the earth

We stood there at your gate
Breath hanging in the air
I leaned in, you leaned back
Like love was almost there

Almost made it
Almost made it
That night we nearly crossed the line
Almost made it
Almost made it
You were mine for a little while

Now when the rain comes down
On that same old road
I taste that unfinished kiss
Like sugar on my soul
If I see you by the market
You still smile and let me through
And I wonder if you felt it too
The way I never made it to you

Almost made it
Almost made it
That night we nearly crossed the line
Almost made it
Almost made it
You were mine for a little while
Almost made it
Almost made it
Still burns when I think of you

Almost made it

You wore that blue dress home
I had the words in my throat
Moon on the mango tree
And your hand was close to mine
The corner shop clock blinked
Like it knew what I was owed
Taxi lights rolled past slow
And my heart kicked down the road

We stood there at your gate
Breath hanging in the air
I leaned in, you leaned back
Like love was almost there

Almost made it
Almost made it
That night we nearly crossed the line
Almost made it
Almost made it
You were mine for a little while

Your brother called your name

I watched the screen door swing

songs.subject: a reggae song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Last night had your name on it
Folded up in my shirt pocket
I took the long road home
With the moon on a lean like a locksmith
Your call rang once, then gone
I was one turn from the gold line
Had my hand on the gate
Then the gate moved in slow time

I could see it
Right there
So close I could taste it
In the air
One more breath
One more step
I swear I was ready
Then it slipped

Almost made it, almost made it
Almost made it, baby, almost made it
So close, so close
Then the night took it away
Almost made it, almost made it
Almost made it, baby, almost made it
Hold my hand
We were right there

Your laugh was still in the room
On the glass where the rain kept sliding
I said my goodbyes too soon
And the truth came back colliding
I had the right words lined up
But they fell like coins in water
You were half a step ahead
I was losing you by the border

I could see it
Right there
So close I could taste it
In the air
One more breath
One more step
I swear I was ready
Then it slipped

Almost made it, almost made it
Almost made it, baby, almost made it
So close, so close
Then the night took it away
Almost made it, almost made it
Almost made it, baby, almost made it
Hold my hand
We were right there

Now I laugh at the near-miss
But it still leaves a bruise
Every almost is a doorway
That I still can’t lose
If you ever feel that pull, love
When the dark gets kind
I’ll be waiting at the edge there
One more time

Almost made it, almost made it
Almost made it, baby, almost made it
So close, so close
Then the night took it away
Almost made it, almost made it
Almost made it, baby, almost made it
Hold my hand

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
One stop sign
One grocery store
Same two lanes
Been there before
Mom's porch light
Burns through the rain
I pack my bag
And don't look back again
The feed store cat
Knows my steps
Main Street blur
And the same regrets

I hear it now
That engine call
If I stay here
I stay too small
Ticket in my fist
Heart in my throat
One more night here
Then I'm gone for good

Gone for good
Gone for good
This town can't hold me
Gone for good
Gone for good
Gone for good
I was born to break free
Gone for good

Blue work shirt
Still smells like smoke
Half my friends
Already broke
They say, "Wait up"
I say, "Watch me"
I'm over that fence
And into the sea
Old red car
Shaking on the line
Full of dust
And borrowed time
If I turn around
I know I'll fold
So I keep my hands
On the wheel, stay bold

I hear it now
That engine call
If I stay here
I stay too small
Ticket in my fist
Heart in my throat
One more night here
Then I'm gone for good

Gone for good
Gone for good
This town can't hold me
Gone for good
Gone for good
Gone for good
I was born to break free
Gone for good

No more names on the diner wall
No more Sundays, no more stalls
If I miss you, I won't stay
I'll send a postcard far away

Gone for good
Gone for good
This town can't hold me
Gone for good
Gone for good
Gone for good
I was born to break free
Gone for good

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The diner closed at nine
Like it always did
My name still on the wall
In that chipped-up red
One-horse road, dead-end
Dust on the county sign
I watched my whole first life
From the same porch line

But my bag was packed by the door
Keys in my palm
You can keep the cracked front steps
I’m already gone
The train yard called my name
Through the sleeping bins
I felt the whole place shake
When I let go of it

I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
I won’t look back
I’m leaving for good
I’m leaving for good
You can keep that map
(Leaving for good)

My boots hit the platform hard
Past the feed store sign
Past the rusted swing set
And the church bell chime
Every truck I ever knew
Sat under the rain
Every face I owed a debt
Called me by my name

But my bag was packed by the door
Keys in my palm
You can keep the cracked front steps
I’m already gone
The train yard called my name
Through the sleeping bins
I felt the whole place shake
When I let go of it

I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
I won’t look back
I’m leaving for good
I’m leaving for good
You can keep that map
(Leaving for good)

Let the gravel hold my footprints
Let the porch light burn
I was built for open hours
For the miles I earned
If I ever taste that winter
On the edge of town
I’ll know I was not made
To be buried down

I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
I won’t look back
I’m leaving for good
I’m leaving for good
You can keep that map
I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
Watch the horizon crack

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
I dream the same blue room
The ceiling comes apart
My feet forget the floor
And leave a silver mark
I drift above the roofs
Over laundry lines and clay
You wave from far below
Then slip away

And every time I rise
My name grows light
The world gets small
At the edge of night

I keep on flying back
Flying back to you
I keep on flying back
Like my heart is new
(again, again)
Up through the dark
I know the sky by heart

The clouds look like white sheets
Hanging over sleep
I pass the moon like fruit
Too bright to keep
I circle old train yards
The river, the church bells too
Then your face turns in the wind
And pulls me through

And every time I rise
My fear falls through
I wake half-smiling
Still reaching for you

I keep on flying back
Flying back to you
I keep on flying back
Like my heart is new
(again, again)
Up through the dark
I know the sky by heart

Maybe dreams remember
What the day forgets
Maybe I was made for this
To hover, then reset
So if I wake alone
With morning in my hands
I’ll close my eyes and let
The blue begin again

I keep on flying back
Flying back to you
I keep on flying back
Like my heart is new
(again, again)
Up through the dark
I know the sky by heart

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
I fall asleep with my feet on the ground
And wake up high above the town
A window opens in my chest
And I am light, and I am blessed

I know this sky
I know that bend
It calls my name
Again, again

I dream I fly
I dream I fly
Round and round
I never say goodbye
I dream I fly
I dream I fly
(way up there)
I come back feeling fine

Last night I crossed over the market roof
Past the awnings and the sleeping fruit
Your face was clear in the open blue
You laughed like rain on a tin canoe

I know this turn
I know this glow
The city falls
And then I go

I dream I fly
I dream I fly
Round and round
I never say goodbye
I dream I fly
I dream I fly
(way up there)
I come back feeling fine

If I could keep one thing from sleep
I'd keep the wind and the way it frees
Your hand in mine through the silver air
The same soft pull, the same old prayer

I dream I fly
I dream I fly
Round and round
I never say goodbye
I dream I fly
I dream I fly
(way up there)

songs.subject: a metal song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Bus stop bones in the rain
Your sleeve brushed mine
One torn umbrella
Bent like a broken sign
You said, “You got room?”
I said, “I guess”
We stood shoulder to shoulder
Under all that mess

Water hit the curb
Like teeth on steel
Your laugh came sharp
Made the whole night real
We locked eyes once
Then looked away
Two lost sparks
In the same gray rain

One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
One small roof, one hard storm
(one hard storm)
You and me in the damage
(you and me)
Standing close to stay warm
One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
No names, just the thunder’s roar
(no names)
For one dark mile
We were almost more

Your hand kept slipping
On the cracked black frame
Mine caught the rib
When the wind got mean
A truck hissed past
And soaked our shoes
You swore at the sky
I did too
The corner store glow
Was three blocks down
Your paper bag shook
Like it might come apart
You said, “This is stupid”
I said, “Yeah”
But you smiled then
With rain in your hair

Water hit the curb
Like teeth on steel
Your laugh came sharp
Made the whole night real
We locked eyes once
Then looked away
Two lost sparks
In the same gray rain

One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
One small roof, one hard storm
(one hard storm)
You and me in the damage
(you and me)
Standing close to stay warm
One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
No names, just the thunder’s roar
(no names)
For one dark mile
We were almost more

Then the corner light
Turned yellow, then red
You lifted the frame
And the storm spun ahead
I should’ve said stay
I should’ve said more
But your steps hit fast
On the wet front porch

Keep it up
Keep it moving
Don’t let go
Don’t let go
One umbrella
One small shield
Two strangers
Too real

One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
One small roof, one hard storm
(one hard storm)
You and me in the damage
(you and me)
Standing close to stay warm
One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
No names, just the thunder’s roar
(no names)
For one dark mile
We were almost more
One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
And the rain took the rest of us home

songs.subject: a metal song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the bus stop roof
You and I, wrong side of the storm
One black umbrella, bent and torn
Your hand on the handle, mine on the edge
We don't speak
We just stand there

Your sleeve is soaked at the cuff
My boots sink in the gutter mud
Cars hiss past in the flood
Your breath makes smoke in the cold gray air
You look away
I watch the rain

Two strangers, one thin shield
A little space, a little steel
Shoulder to shoulder, we hold still
The whole street howls, but we don't wilt

One umbrella
Two lost names
One umbrella
Same hard rain
You lean in
I lean too
One umbrella
Pulls us through

The wind hits hard, turns it inside out
We both laugh like it hurts our mouths
Your fingers tighten, then they let go
Mine meet yours for a second, then slide
No big vow
Just shared sky

Two strangers, one thin shield
A little space, a little steel
Shoulder to shoulder, we hold still
The whole street howls, but we don't wilt

One umbrella
Two lost names
One umbrella
Same hard rain
You lean in
I lean too
One umbrella
Pulls us through

Thunder splits the overpass
You say, "Keep it low"
I say, "Hold it fast"
For one dark mile
We move like a vow
Not friends yet
Not strangers now

One umbrella
Two lost names
One umbrella
Same hard rain
You lean in
I lean too
One umbrella
Pulls us through
One umbrella
We break the storm
One umbrella

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
One more badge on my wrist
Last cups in the grass
You laughed with your shoes in your hand
Said the time went fast
Dust on the vendor tarp
Half a pie in a tray
We watched the cleanup lights move slow
Like they’d miss the day

Don’t ask me to leave yet
Don’t fold up the sound
I’m still finding your face
In the crowd, in the crowd

Last wristband, last wristband
Don’t let it slip away
Last wristband, last wristband
We can sleep in the day
Hold that song in your hand
Hold that spark in the haze
Last wristband, last wristband
Stay a little longer, babe

Muddy cuffs on your jeans
Ticket stub in your phone
Some kids chased a paper moon
By the cooling drone
The snack stand shut its door
But we still had our own
A shared bite, a shared grin
And a walk back home

Don’t ask me to leave yet
Don’t fold up the sound
I’m still finding your face
In the crowd, in the crowd

Last wristband, last wristband
Don’t let it slip away
Last wristband, last wristband
We can sleep in the day
Hold that song in your hand
Hold that spark in the haze
Last wristband, last wristband
Stay a little longer, babe

Rented chairs in a row
Moon on the torn-up lawn
If this is the ending
Then let it linger on

Last wristband, last wristband
Don’t let it slip away
Last wristband, last wristband
We can sleep in the day
Hold that song in your hand
Hold that spark in the haze
Last wristband, last wristband
Stay a little longer, babe

songs.subject: a house song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up at dusk
With a seal of bone and wax
Names scratched out on crates
And tar that bled black tracks
The captain wouldn’t blink
When the gulls began to scream
He said, “Keep your hands off that”
But I touched it in my dream

Eight nights on the swell
The boards began to hum
Every lantern in the hold
Flickered like a drum
And the water at the rail
Held a face I used to know
Saying, “Turn this ship around”
But the current wouldn’t go

Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
We can’t let go
Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
Pulling us below
Say it low, say it low
Don’t wake the sea
Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
It’s got the best of me

The ropes grew slick with salt
Even when the sky was dry
We saw ash fall from the mast
Like some old alibi
Every man on deck got cold
At the mention of its name
And the lock began to sweat
Like it knew the shape of shame

Nine bells, then the fog
Came crawling through the teeth
One by one the footsteps
Vanished off the beat
I could hear a second crew
Moving under every plank
Tapping at the underside
Like they wanted out the tank

Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
We can’t let go
Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
Pulling us below
Say it low, say it low
Don’t wake the sea
Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
It’s got the best of me

Open water, open mouth
The moon went pale and thin
We threw gold into the dark
But the dark just let it sink
Then the lock split clean in two
And the hold lit up like fire
Something breathed my own name back
From the bottom of the wire

Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
We can’t let go
Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
Pulling us below
Say it low, say it low
Don’t wake the sea
Cursed cargo, cursed cargo
It’s got the best of me

It’s got the best of me
It’s got the best of me

Another book another page
My brain it screams in silent pain
This lonely scholarly stage

Two AM oh two AM

Two AM oh two AM
When will this nightmare end my friend

Coffee cold a bitter sip
My eyelids heavy want to droop
On knowledge cliffs my fingers slip
Stuck in this academic loop

Two AM oh two AM
The clock just laughs at me again
Two AM oh two AM
When will this nightmare end my friend

The moon a

songs.subject: a flamenco song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
At two in the morning, I sit with my books
A lamp on the table, a ring of old notes
My pencil makes scratches like dry olive wood
And the room keeps its secrets the way silence should
I trace every line till it starts to glow
The page is a field and I’m learning to sow
My coffee is cold in a chipped blue cup
But I keep on reading, I don’t get up

One more page
One more name
My eyes are tired
But I stay in the flame

Two in the morning, I’m still here
Two in the morning, I hold my fear
Page by page, I make my way
Two in the morning, I do not stray
(ay, ay, ay)
(two in the morning)

The neighbor’s guitar cries through the wall
A spoon in a glass makes a soft little call
My shadow leans long on the tile by the door
Like a dancer who’s waiting for one song more
I fight with the facts, then I make them mine
I mark every doubt with a red pencil line
If the whole world sleeps, let it sleep away
I’ve got this one question I’m born to slay

One more page
One more name
My eyes are tired
But I stay in the flame

Two in the morning, I’m still here
Two in the morning, I hold my fear
Page by page, I make my way
Two in the morning, I do not stray
(ay, ay, ay)
(two in the morning)

And if dawn comes crawling through the pane
It will find my heartbeat still in the game
A little worn, but I made it through
With ink on my fingers and a truth in view

Two in the morning, I’m still here
Two in the morning, I hold my fear
Page by page, I make my way
Two in the morning, I do not stray
(ay, ay, ay)
(two in the morning)

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Platform sweat on my palms
Blue bench, bent in half
Door chime cuts the air
And my name feels far
I step in with the crowd
Fifty faces, no sound
My shirt sticks to my back
Like I ran all the way here

The tunnel leans in close
My ribs go paper-thin
I count the hand grips
Then forget where I begin

Hold my breath, hold my breath
Don't let it show
Hold my breath, hold my breath
I need a slow way home
(hold my breath)
Hold my breath, hold my breath
Till the doors open

Map above the window
Flashes by too fast
A kid with a red cap
Keeps staring at the glass
My pulse kicks the seat
Like it wants out first
I taste pennies in my mouth
And the floor starts to surge

The tunnel leans in close
My ribs go paper-thin
I count the hand grips
Then forget where I begin

Hold my breath, hold my breath
Don't let it show
Hold my breath, hold my breath
I need a slow way home
(hold my breath)
Hold my breath, hold my breath
Till the doors open

Halfway through the dark
I press my thumb to my wrist
One stop, then another
You don't have to win this
A stranger moves aside
Like they know what to do
The doors crack the whole thing
And I spill back into you

Hold my breath, hold my breath
Don't let it show
Hold my breath, hold my breath
I need a slow way home
(hold my breath)
Hold my breath, hold my breath
Till the doors open

Cold air on my face
I get one clean line
One foot on the platform
Then the next in time

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
I got on at Canal
With a paper cup and a shaking hand
Your shoulder brushed mine
And the whole car tilted like sand
My phone lit up blue
I stared at the map for a way out
But the tunnel went black
And my chest started pounding out loud

Hold the pole
Hold my breath
Count the doors
Count again

I can’t stay calm
I can’t stay calm
My heart won’t sit still
My legs go numb
I can’t stay calm
I can’t stay calm
Please let this train
Keep running

The ads on the wall
All blurred into one fast-moving face
A kid in a red coat
Looked through me like I wasn’t in place
My jacket felt tight
Like somebody tied it with wire
I tried to look normal
But my skin was a live little wire

Hold the pole
Hold my breath
Count the stops
Count again

I can’t stay calm
I can’t stay calm
My heart won’t sit still
My legs go numb
I can’t stay calm
I can’t stay calm
Please let this train
Keep running

Then the doors opened wide
Cold air cut right through my face
I stepped into daylight
And it came back, slow, in waves
A hand on my sleeve
A voice saying, “Hey, you’re okay”
I learned how to breathe
One ugly second at a time

I can’t stay calm
I can’t stay calm
My heart won’t sit still
My legs go numb
I can’t stay calm
I can’t stay calm
But I made it through

Study alone

Study alone
Study alone

Cold tea gone in the cup

I been chasing the numbers up

Everybody sleepin' now
I'm still here with the work
Headphones on, house so still

I trace the words with my thumb
Like a map in the dark
I don't need a crowd tonight
Just a spark

Two a.m. notes
(two a.m. notes)
Keep me awake
Keep me afloat
Two a.m. notes
(two a.m. notes)
One more chapter
Then I can go

Window frame, half-asleep rain
Ticking soft on the glass
I rewrite what I missed
Then I write it at last

Like a row of small flags
I got quiet in my room
But I never feel trapped

songs.subject: a americana song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
I came back on a Thursday
With red dirt on my boots
A duffel in the truck bed
And a name tag worn right through
Mama stood on the porch boards
Like she’d been there all year
I tried to grin like nothing
But my hands gave me away

The yard smelled like cut grass
And fried onions in the pan
I stood there at the screen door
Learning how to be a man
Again

I’m home now
(Home now)
I’m home now
(Home now)
The war left scars, but the road let go
I’m home now
(Home now)

Daddy didn’t ask a question
He just handed me a rake
We worked the fence till sundown
Not a single word to waste
My little girl came running
With a drawing in her fist
Said, “I kept your chair warm”
And I nearly lost my grip

There’s a notch in the table
Where my coffee cup still fits
I’m relearning the small things
That I thought I’d never miss
I do

I’m home now
(Home now)
I’m home now
(Home now)
The war left scars, but the road let go
I’m home now
(Home now)

Some nights I still wake up
To a door that slams in my head
But the house is full of breathing
And the light beside my bed
If I shake, you can hold me
If I fall, I’ll rise slow
I brought back more than thunder
I brought back what I know

I’m home now
(Home now)
I’m home now
(Home now)
The war left scars, but the road let go
I’m home now
(Home now)
I’m home now
(Home now)
And your porch light found me on the road

songs.subject: a americana song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
My boots hit the station floor
With a name I barely knew
Old flag folded in my bag
And a scar that traveled too
Mama stood by the screen door
Hand on her apron seam
She said, "Boy, you made it back"
Like she was pulling me from a dream

I forgot the way the screen door creaks
The smell of beans and pine
How the porch light found my face
And made it look like mine

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
Let that gravel know my name
I'm coming home
I'm coming home

The church bells rang on Sunday
When I walked in a little late
Folks kept their eyes on their hymnals
Like they didn't know what to say
The barber took his time with me
Said, "Son, you sure look thin"
I laughed a laugh that sounded strange
And he gave it back again

I still wake to doors that slam
And boots on a hard-packed road
But here your hand on my shoulder
Tells my body where to go

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
Let that gravel know my name
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
A wounded man, but changed

There's a field behind the cider mill
Where the corn grows shoulder-high
I stood there with my brother
And watched the blackbirds cut the sky
He said, "You don't have to tell it all"
So I let the silence stay
Some things live in a soldier's chest
Even after he lays away

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
Let that gravel know my name

I'm coming home

I'm coming home

Mama stood by the screen door

She said, "Boy, you made it back"
Like she was pulling me from a dream

I forgot the way the screen door creaks

How the porch light found my face

songs.subject: a disco song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
She kneaded dough at dawn
with flour on her wrists
Blue veins like map lines
rose and fell like this
She tied my scarf too tight
when winter cut our breath
Then brushed my cheek with care
that never needed rest

Oh, I knew that touch
before I knew my name
One palm said stay
one palm said you’re safe

Grandma’s hands
Grandma’s hands
Hold me up again
Grandma’s hands
Grandma’s hands
Teach the hurt to mend
(Grandma’s hands)

They folded Sunday towels
and counted every bill
They caught my tears in secret
when the house went still
They found my hidden shame
and never made it sharp
Just held the light a while
right over my heart

Oh, I knew that touch
before I knew my name
One palm said stay
one palm said you’re safe

Grandma’s hands
Grandma’s hands
Hold me up again
Grandma’s hands
Grandma’s hands
Teach the hurt to mend
(Grandma’s hands)

Now the rings are gone
but the kindness stays
I see her in my fingers
in a hundred little ways
When I lift a child
when I smooth a brow
I’m her Friday night
I’m her here and now

Grandma’s hands
Grandma’s hands
Hold me up again
Grandma’s hands
Grandma’s hands
Teach the hurt to mend
(Grandma’s hands)

Soft as bread
strong as oak
Blessed by work
and love they spoke
(Grandma’s hands)

songs.subject: a disco song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
In the flour on the board
She drew little moons
Bent fingers, steady
Counting out spoons
Blue-veined map
Of a life well spent
She buttoned my coat
Like she knew what I meant

And when the room felt cold
She reached for me first
Those careful palms
Could break any curse

Grandma's hands, grandmama's hands
(Grandma's hands)
Gold in the light, they taught me how to stand
Grandma's hands, grandmama's hands
(Grandma's hands)
Hold me together, then let me dance

Pencil marks on the wall
For every tall year
Jam on her apron
And a laugh in my ear
She snapped green beans clean
By the radio's glow
Every wrinkle said
I am not done, no, no

And when the room felt cold
She reached for me first
Those careful palms
Could break any curse

Grandma's hands, grandmama's hands
(Grandma's hands)
Gold in the light, they taught me how to stand
Grandma's hands, grandmama's hands
(Grandma's hands)
Hold me together, then let me dance

Now I wear her ring
On a borrowed chain
When I miss her touch
I call her name
Line by line
Beat by beat
I feel her love
Under my feet

Grandma's hands, grandmama's hands
(Grandma's hands)
Gold in the light, they taught me how to stand
Grandma's hands, grandmama's hands
(Grandma's hands)

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Train car shakin' like a bony knee
Sweat drips down me for the world to see
Too many faces too many eyes
A thousand stories and a million lies

Air gets thin you know
Gotta get out gotta go

Subway shvitz oh mama please
Can't catch my breath can't find no ease
The doors won't open they just stay shut tight
Subway shvitz gonna lose my mind tonight

Fiddles screamin' in my head real loud
Lost in the middle of this metal crowd
My heart's a drum solo goin' wild and fast
Wonderin' how long this can even last

Subway shvitz oh mama please
Can't catch my breath can't find no ease
The doors won't open they just stay shut tight
Subway shvitz gonna lose my mind tonight

Maybe I'm crazy maybe I'm fine
This train keeps movin' on its crooked line
Just need a window just need some air
A little escape from this heavy

My heart's a drum solo goin' wild and fast
Wonderin' how long this can even last

Subway shvitz oh mama please
Can't catch my breath can't find no ease
The doors won't open they just stay shut tight
Subway shvitz gonna lose my mind tonight

Maybe I'm crazy maybe I'm fine
This train keeps movin' on its crooked line
Just need a window just need some air
A little escape from this heavy

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
I’m on the train at noon
Packed in like a glove
A man’s elbow, wet coat
A bag, a cough, above
The wheels go ticka-tick
My chest goes wrong, too fast
I count the ads in front of me
I cannot make it last

Oy, oy, oy
The tunnel leans and bends
Oy, oy, oy
My body will not end
My fingers on the pole
My throat goes dry and thin
Tell me where the air went
Tell me where to begin

Ai, ai, ai, hold me up
(hold me up)
Ai, ai, ai, don’t let go
(don’t let go)
This little train of terror
Will not run me low
Ai, ai, ai, hold me up
(hold me up)
Ai, ai, ai, let me breathe
(let me breathe)
At the next stop, darling
I will find my feet

A stroller bumps my knee
A silver ad turns gold
I stare at my own hands
Like they’re somebody old
The tracks begin to drum
Like boots inside my chest
I want to laugh, I want to cry
I want to do what’s best

Oy, oy, oy
The doors slide open wide
Oy, oy, oy
But fear won’t step aside
My mouth tastes like a coin
My pulse is all I hear
Still I keep on standing
Still I stay right here

Ai, ai, ai, hold me up
(hold me up)
Ai, ai, ai, don’t let go
(don’t let go)
This little train of terror
Will not run me low
Ai, ai, ai, hold me up
(hold me up)
Ai, ai, ai, let me breathe
(let me breathe)
At the next stop, darling
I will find my feet

Take my coat, take my coat
Let the world spin past
One hand on the pole now
One slow breath at last
The tunnel turns to daylight
The metal starts to sing
I am not a broken thing
I am a human thing

Ai, ai, ai, hold me up
(hold me up)
Ai, ai, ai, don’t let go
(don’t let go)
This little train of terror
Will not run me low
Ai, ai, ai, hold me up
(hold me up)
Ai, ai, ai, let me breathe
(let me breathe)
At the next stop, darling

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Boots on the mud, map folded twice
Tailgate full of gas and a cheap flashlight
County line rain in a paper cup
I said I’d rest, but I can’t sit still enough
Barn doors shake when the sky gets mean
I read the clouds like a cut-up scene
Mama says, "Boy, where you gonna land?"
I just grin and leave with the wheel in my hands

I hear that rattle
I hear that roll
The dark pulls low
Right through my soul

I can’t settle down
I chase the sound
I chase the storm
I’m never home
I can’t settle down
(never home)
I can’t settle down
I chase the storm

Sleepless towns with the diner glow
Half a pie gone cold by the window
Names in my phone, but I let them ring
I’m always one cloud from everything
We parked by the silo, waiting on the bend
You said, "Come back soon," I said, "Maybe when"
Your hand on my sleeve, my engine on
By morning your porch and my shadow were gone

I hear that rattle
I hear that call
If I stay too long
I start to fall

I can’t settle down
I chase the sound
I chase the storm
I’m never home
I can’t settle down
(never home)
I can’t settle down
I chase the storm

Maybe one day I’ll buy that chair
Put my boots by the door, keep a life somewhere
But the west keeps lighting up my name
And my hands go back to the wheel again

I can’t settle down
I chase the sound
I chase the storm
I’m never home
I can’t settle down
(never home)
I can’t settle down
I chase the storm

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Mic check, one two, hear the rumble, feel the bass
Another low-pressure system, another high-stakes chase
Weather map my canvas, Doppler my brush,
Chasing cumulonimbus, no time to hush.

Storm chaser, heart racer, never in one place,
Sky mover, cloud groover, setting my own pace.
Wind whispers secrets, lightning paints the night,
Always gone by morning, ain't no fading light.

Dust devils dance, a whirlwind of doubt,
But the horizon's calling, no turning about.
Satellite eyes watchin', ground zero my goal,
A lone wolf howling, losing all control.

Storm chaser, heart racer, never in one place,
Sky mover, cloud groover, setting my own pace.
Wind whispers secrets, lightning paints the night,
Always gone by morning, ain't no fading light.

Maybe one day, a calm front will appear,
A quiet eye, banishing all fear.
But till then, the thunder, the rain, the wild,
A restless spirit, forever undefiled.

Storm chaser, heart racer, never in one place,
Sky mover, cloud groover, setting my own pace.
Wind whispers secrets, lightning paints the night,

songs.subject: a funk song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Rolled by slow on Elm again
Rain on the glass, same old bends
Third-floor glow in the window pane
But that door ain't mine no more
Pizza box ghost by the old mailbox
Dead plant droopin' in the crack of the walk
I seen my name where the paint wore thin
I kept on drivin', didn't go in

Uh, my chest got light
Then it got tight
One look back
And the whole night cracked

Old apartment, old apartment
(Old apartment)
You still know my name
Old apartment, old apartment
(Old apartment)
I can't drive by the same
Not tonight, not tonight
I keep my hands on the wheel
Old apartment, old apartment
You cut me deep, still real

Blue porch bulb buzzin' like a bad thought
Same bent screen and the same chain lock
Somebody's bike with the rusted rim
Looked like a life I used to live in
I passed that curb, felt the engine dip
Saw my own laugh in the chrome of the lip
Four little rooms, one broken fan
And a stack of dreams in a grocery bag

Uh, my chest got light
Then it got tight
One look back
And the whole night cracked

Old apartment, old apartment
(Old apartment)
You still know my name
Old apartment, old apartment
(Old apartment)
I can't drive by the same
Not tonight, not tonight
I keep my hands on the wheel
Old apartment, old apartment
You cut me deep, still real

Maybe the floorboards creak for somebody new
Maybe the hall light burns on for a different view
But I swear the air kept a trace of me
In that busted little memory

Ooh, mm
That corner keeps its score
Ooh, mm
I used to live there before

Old apartment, old apartment
(Old apartment)
You still know my name
Old apartment, old apartment
(Old apartment)
I can't drive by the same
Not tonight, not tonight
I keep my hands on the wheel
Old apartment, old apartment
You cut me deep, still real

songs.subject: a funk song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Rolled by your block in the rain
Same busted sign, new name
Third-floor frame still there
Window AC in the air
Laundry cart by the gate
Dead plant on the fire escape
I eased off, let it breathe
Sat real still in the seat

Your old porch light flicked once
Like it knew my truck
I laughed at myself
Then I missed you, what

Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I kept my hands on the wheel
But my heart stayed parked there
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
Nothing looks the same
But it feels like you still live there

Trash bins lined like drums
Saturday kids got gone
Doormat curled at the edge
Beer stain on the hedge
I saw my name on the mail
Half a joke, half a hail
Pulled too slow at the curb
Couldn’t shake that old hurt

Your old porch light flicked once
Like it knew my truck
I laughed at myself
Then I missed you, what

Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I kept my hands on the wheel
But my heart stayed parked there
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
Nothing looks the same
But it feels like you still live there

A bass note in my chest
A quiet I couldn’t bless
I saw my ghost in the glass
Then I let it pass
No need to knock tonight
No need to pick that fight
Just one long look, then gone
With the radio on

Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I kept my hands on the wheel
But my heart stayed parked there
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
Nothing looks the same

songs.subject: a post-rock song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I hid my name in dust
Under the pews of June
The glass had lost its color
And the floor forgot my shoes
I touched the cold brass rail
Like it might answer back
Only the empty rafters
Kept giving me my echo back

I said, are you still there?
In the cracked and quiet air
My hands were full of ash
My throat was split with prayer

Bring me back
Bring me back
To the place I left
Bring me back
Bring me back
I am not done yet
(not done yet)

I walked through rain and station steam
With a bruise inside my chest
Every locked-up door I tried
Looked a little like the rest
Then in a stranger's steady hands
In bread and paper cups
Something small and living woke
And shook the darkness up

I said, are you still there?
In the cracked and waking air
My hands were full of ash
But the light was in the flare

Bring me back
Bring me back
To the place I left
Bring me back
Bring me back
I am not done yet
(not done yet)

I lost you in the wildfire
I found you in the smoke
Not as a shout from heaven
But in the way a heart broke
So take this shaking body
Take this stubborn breath
I walked out of the silence
And I walked in after it

Bring me back
Bring me back
To the place I left
Bring me back
Bring me back
I am not done yet
(not done yet)
Bring me back
Bring me back
Through the wreck and ache
Bring me back
Bring me back
Faith returns in waves
(in waves)
songs.lyrics_en:
I hid my name in dust
Under the pews of June
The glass had lost its color
And the floor forgot my shoes
I touched the cold brass rail
Like it might answer back
Only the empty rafters
Kept giving me my echo back

I said, are you still there?
In the cracked and quiet air
My hands were full of ash
My throat was split with prayer

Bring me back
Bring me back
To the place I left
Bring me back
Bring me back
I am not done yet
(not done yet)

I walked through rain and station steam
With a bruise inside my chest
Every locked-up door I tried
Looked a little like the rest
Then in a stranger's steady hands
In bread and paper cups
Something small and living woke
And shook the darkness up

I said, are you still there?
In the cracked and waking air
My hands were full of ash
But the light was in the flare

Bring me back
Bring me back
To the place I left
Bring me back
Bring me back
I am not done yet
(not done yet)

I lost you in the wildfire
I found you in the smoke
Not as a shout from heaven

Bring me back
Bring me back

songs.subject: a post-rock song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I left it on the shelf
Next to the winter coat
A name I couldn't hold
A door I wouldn't close
The pews were empty teeth
The candles burned out low
I walked out with my hands
Still cold from letting go

But in the black river
I heard my own name
Soft as a match flare
Soft as rain

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then it found me
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then it found me

It came in small things first
A hand on my shoulder
Steam on the bathroom glass
A song from the neighbor's porch
I saw the cracks in me
Still letting light through
What I had buried deep
Was still reaching for You

And in the silence
When I was nearly done
There was a spark in the dust
There was a sun

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then it found me
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then it found me

I was not gone
I was only underground
Under the stones
You were still around
So I came back
With my broken hands open
Not clean
Not proud
But hoping

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then it found me
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then it found me
Now I stand here
Still shaking
Still singing

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We left from Galway pier
With cedar in the hold
And nine dark crates of lamp-black salt
Bound tight in tar and gold
The skipper would not say
What mark was burned in oak
Just crossed his thumb and muttered low
When the first crate shook and woke

The tide ran green that night
The ropes began to cry
And every gull above our mast
Turned once and flew from sky

Black salt cargo
Hold that line
Black salt cargo
It’s mine, it’s mine
We carry it north
Through the storm and foam
Black salt cargo
Don’t call it home (don’t call it home)

By midweek, men were thin
With fever on their skin
We heard a knocking in the dark
From somewhere deep within
Tom swore he saw a hand
Press pale against the plank
Then half the watch went overboard
And none of them even sank

The tide ran green that night
The mast began to bow
The sea grew teeth around the hull
And we still sailed somehow

Black salt cargo
Hold that line
Black salt cargo
It’s mine, it’s mine
We carry it north
Through the storm and foam
Black salt cargo
Don’t call it home (don’t call it home)

At dawn we split the lid
And found a ring of bone
A little chapel bell of brass
And hair like wetting stone
The captain kissed the deck
Then tied the lid once more
He said, “If land we ever see
Leave one crate on the shore”

Black salt cargo
Hold that line
Black salt cargo
It’s mine, it’s mine
We carry it north
Through the storm and foam
Black salt cargo
Don’t call it home (don’t call it home)
Black salt cargo
Hear it sing
Black salt cargo
That old dead thing
We’ll leave one crate
Where the cold waves foam
Black salt cargo
Then maybe go home (then maybe go home)

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
The gulls cried out
A ragged line
Against the grey
Where sea meets sky
We loaded her deep
With what we found
A stone from a cairn
On hallowed ground
The old man warned
With a shaking hand
"That rock remembers
A different land."

Oh
The salt-kissed stone
It rides below
A silent weight
Where the currents flow
The salt-kissed stone
It calls the deep
While honest men
Can only weep
The salt-kissed stone

Three weeks out
The winds they died
A mirror sea
With nowhere to hide
The water went foul
The bread turned green
A sickness spread
Unseen
Unseen
The captain paced
His face a fright
He saw things moving
In the endless night

What price a cargo
When souls are lost?
What good is silver
At such a cost?
The compass spins
A madman's eye
Beneath that stone
The old gods lie

Oh
The salt-kissed stone
It rides below
A silent weight
Where the currents flow
The salt-kissed stone
It calls the deep
While honest men
Can only weep
The salt-kissed stone

The salt-kissed stone

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
You stood on my side of the curb
I said, “Move in”
You laughed at the word
One umbrella, bent on the frame
Your sleeve touched mine
And I knew your name

The rain got mean
The train went by
We both looked up
At the same gray sky
One small circle
Two lost shoes
You said, “I guess”
And I said, “I do”

One umbrella, one umbrella
(keep me close, keep me close)
One umbrella, one umbrella
(we can cross this road)
Shoulder to shoulder
Through the blue
One umbrella
Me and you

Your phone lit up
Then died in your hand
You shook your head
At the flooded street plan
I moved my grip
You moved your hair
We dodged each puddle
Like we were a pair

The rain got loud
The world went thin
You smiled like you’d
Let somebody in
One small circle
Two warm hands
You said, “Not bad”
And I said, “Damn”

One umbrella, one umbrella
(keep me close, keep me close)
One umbrella, one umbrella
(we can cross this road)
Shoulder to shoulder
Through the blue
One umbrella
Me and you

At the corner store
You turned to stay
I almost asked you
For one more day
You took the handle
I let it go
Then you kissed my cheek
In the falling snow

One umbrella, one umbrella
(keep me close, keep me close)
One umbrella, one umbrella
(now you know, now you know)
Shoulder to shoulder
Through the blue
One umbrella
Me and you

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
You stepped in from the rain
I stepped back, you stayed
One black umbrella
Tilting over two strange days
Your sleeve touched my knuckles
Cold drops on my wrist
We both looked down at once
Like we’d practiced this

One step left
Then you laughed
The curb was gone
And the whole block flashed

Under one umbrella
We move together
Under one umbrella
We borrow weather
One small circle
Two lost hearts
(under one umbrella)
Till the storm falls apart

Your shoes were red canvas
Mine were soaked through blue
We crossed at the bakery
You said, “is this seat for two?”
The bus hissed past us
Your hair brushed my face
We held that little shelter
Like it knew our place

One step right
Then you said
“Stay close now”
And the rain got loud instead

Under one umbrella
We move together
Under one umbrella
We borrow weather
One small circle
Two lost hearts
(under one umbrella)
Till the storm falls apart

A tram bell cut the block in two
You smiled like you’d been here too
No names yet
Just the shine
Of two strangers
Out of time

Rain on the nylon
Drip, drip, gold
Your hand found mine
At the edge of cold

Under one umbrella
We move together
Under one umbrella
We borrow weather
One small circle
Two lost hearts
(under one umbrella)
Till the storm falls apart
Under one umbrella
You and me forever
Under one umbrella
Just for the weather
One small circle
Two lost hearts
(under one umbrella)

songs.subject: a folk song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
The oil lamp flickers
A moth against the pane
Another storm is brewin'
Feel it in the rain
I dip my pen in ink
Black as the deepest tide
To write to you
My ocean
Where my heart resides

Oh
My sea
My wild
My ever-shifting blue
These brine-stained pages
They belong to you
Every word a whisper
Carried on the spray
To your dark currents
At the close of day

They say a man grows lonely
Up here in the height
Just the gulls for company
And the endless night
But I see your face in every wave that breaks and sighs
And the wisdom of the ages
Mirrored in your eyes

The wind
It howls a warning
A cold and ancient song

Oh
My sea
My wild
My ever-shifting blue
These brine-stained pages
They belong to you
Every word a whisper
Carried on the spray
To your dark currents
At the close of day

I've seen the ships go sailing
On to foreign lands
But my only journey's here
With ink and trembling hands
A thousand years of secrets
Held within your deep
And all my silent longings
That I can't quite keep

songs.subject: a folk song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I keep the lamp trimmed clean
By the paint-flaked door
Two gull feathers in the sill
And your salt on the floor
I write your name in pencil
On a blue envelope seam
Then I fold my little courage
And I send it downstream

Past the rocks and the net floats
Past the bell buoy sway
I tuck my words in bottle glass
And watch them drift away

Sea, sea, take my line
Sea, sea, be my sign
If you hold what I can't keep
Bring her back to me
Sea, sea, take my line
Sea, sea, be my sign
I leave my love in the tide
(leave my love in the tide)

There’s a crab in the window box
And a lantern with a crack
Every storm that hits this tower
Knocks my memory back
Your last letter smells of tar rope
And a dockworker's rain
You said meet me where the current bends
I said I’d wait again

Past the hooks and the harbor
Past the black-sanded shore
I write by the glass of midnight
Like I’ve written before

Sea, sea, take my line
Sea, sea, be my sign
If you hold what I can't keep
Bring her back to me
Sea, sea, take my line
Sea, sea, be my sign
I leave my love in the tide
(leave my love in the tide)

Tonight the moon is a coin
On a bruise-colored wave
I read your torn-up postcards
To the wind that saves
If you meet her on the deep road
Tell her I still burn
Tell her every letter I send
Is a door she can return through

Sea, sea, take my line
Sea, sea, be my sign
If you hold what I can't keep
Bring her back to me
Sea, sea, take my line
Sea, sea, be my sign
I leave my love in the tide
(leave my love in the tide)
I leave my love in the tide

songs.subject: a folk song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
I took two jars
And a lantern low
Down by the cane field
Where the mud runs slow
Old truck coughing
Like it knew the night
Hound dog barking
At the county line

I should've turned back
At the cedar gate
But your name was trouble
And trouble was late

Mason jar moon
Burned me clean
Mason jar moon
Took my wheels in the creek
I said run, run, run
Now I'm stuck with the truth
Mason jar moon
Went and ruined my blue

Headlights split
On that washout bend
Bottles clinked out
Like a row of sins
Deputy's siren
Cut the dark in two
I hit the ditch
For the devil and you

Boots in the cattails
Heart in my throat
One jar shattered
One prayer broke

Mason jar moon
Burned me clean
Mason jar moon
Took my wheels in the creek
I said run, run, run
Now I'm stuck with the truth
Mason jar moon
Went and ruined my blue

Now I sit on the porch
With a busted grin
Mud on my cuffs
And a lesson in
You can chase that shine
Till it takes your hand
Some roads look holy
Till they make their stand

Mason jar moon
Burned me clean
Mason jar moon
Took my wheels in the creek
I said run, run, run
Now I'm stuck with the truth
Mason jar moon
Went and ruined my blue

songs.subject: a folk song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded twelve jars down
In the back of my Ford
Mama said, "Boy, don't blink
When you cross that bridgeboard"
I watched the river rise
Brown as a bottle's stain
I took the lane by Jones Creek
And called it by name

Headlights cut through cane
Like knives in the fog
Old red dog at the fence
Just barked and then gone
I heard that rattle sound
From the jars in the crate
One loose cap, one hard curve
And I knew my mistake

Then the road went slick
Like a fresh poured lie
My wheels slid sideways
And the moon fell high
I saw blue in the glass
At the bend by the mill
And my heart hit the dash
Before I did

Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Gone in the ditch
Gone in the ground
Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Lord, I learned fast
What a fall can sound

Deputy stepped from dark
With his hat pulled low
Said, "Son, you took that curve
Like you wanted to go"
I kept my mouth shut tight
As the jars met stone
Sweet smell on the gravel
Where the good stuff roamed

Then the road went slick
Like a fresh poured lie
My wheels slid sideways
And the moon fell high
I saw blue in the glass
At the bend by the mill
And my heart hit the dash
Before I did

Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Gone in the ditch
Gone in the ground
Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Lord, I learned fast
What a fall can sound

Now I don't run nights
When the frost is thick
I keep to the porch
And I mind my drink
But some folks still laugh
When that bridge gets near
They say, "Boy, you left
Your fortune in the creek"

Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Gone in the ditch
Gone in the ground
Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Lord, I learned fast
What a fall can sound

If the road looks kind
It can turn mean quick
And a full tank's pride

Deputy stepped from dark
With his hat pulled low
Said, "Son, you took that curve
Like you wanted to go"
I kept my mouth shut tight
As the jars met stone
Sweet smell on the gravel
Where the good stuff roamed

Then the road went slick
Like a fresh poured lie
My wheels slid sideways
And the moon fell high
I saw blue in the glass
At the bend by the mill
And my heart hit the dash
Before I did

Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Gone in the ditch
Gone in the ground
Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Lord, I learned fast
What a fall can sound

Now I don't run nights
When the frost is thick
I keep to the porch
And I mind my drink
But some folks still laugh
When that bridge gets near
They say, "Boy, you left
Your fortune in the creek"

Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Gone in the ditch
Gone in the ground
Twelve jars down
Twelve jars down
Lord, I learned fast
What a fall can sound

If the road looks kind
It can turn mean quick
And a full tank's pride

songs.subject: a americana song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Dusty deck old plastic cups
Whiskey neat no fancy stuff
Table scarred like an old man's face
Another hand another race

Ace of spades and broken hearts
Tore my world right apart
Played too long played too deep
Secrets that the shadows keep

Felt so good at the start you see
Just a friendly game for you and me
Then the bets got high and wild
Like a hungry wolf a desperate child

One last card one last prayer
A losing hand hanging in the air

Ace of spades and broken hearts
Tore my world right apart
Played too long played too deep
Secrets that the shadows keep

Sunrise paints the kitchen wall
Empty chairs hear no call
Just the echoes

Ace of spades and broken hearts

Sunrise paints the kitchen wall

Just the echoes

songs.subject: a americana song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Midnight oil burnin
Smoke rings in the air
Felt a strange turnin
Didnt really care

Aces high
Aces low
Where the money go
Watch the river flow

Whiskey on the table
Promises so grand
Dealt a hand unstable
Right out of my hand

Aces high
Aces low
Where the money go
Watch the river flow

Sunrise creepin
Through the dusty pane
A quiet weeping
Never be the same

songs.subject: a reggae song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Keg in the back, moon high and bare
Cut through the cane road, me and my scare
Headlights low, keep it hush, keep it lean
Bottle glass clink in a tin machine
Old red truck on a rutted bend
Too much pride, not enough to spend
One sharp laugh, then the blue lights flash
Heart leap out like a match in ash

Hold on, hold on
Too late now
Hold on, hold on
Man, we can't slow down

Back road rum, back road rum
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Back road rum, back road rum
We were blind all night long
(Back road rum)
Back road rum, back road rum
Now the whole run's done

Poured it out in the ditch by the rail
Sweet smell rising like a busted tale
One boot stuck in the muddy ground
Sheriff said nothing, just looked around
Driver swore it was a harmless trade
Now the moonshine ghosts in the puddles stayed
I saw my reflection in a broken jar
Felt like a fool from afar

Hold on, hold on
Too late now
Hold on, hold on
Man, we can't slow down

Back road rum, back road rum
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Back road rum, back road rum
We were blind all night long
(Back road rum)
Back road rum, back road rum
Now the whole run's done

Cane field breathing in the dark
Lost my nerve when I heard that bark
One more mile and we'd be free
But the road turned snake on me

Back road rum, back road rum
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Back road rum, back road rum
We were blind all night long
(Back road rum)
Back road rum, back road rum
Now the whole run's done

songs.subject: a reggae song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Took two jars past the cane
In a rust-red truck bed, slow
Billy said, “Keep your eyes low”
Rain made the dirt road glow
We passed old Moses’ gate
Dog barked once, then dead hush
Moon sat fat on the hill
My hands were slick on the clutch

Then the tires hit mud
And the tail light flashed
Heard a horn in the dark
Then the whole thing crashed

Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Moonshine run gone wrong
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Now the night feel strong
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Glass on the gravel, gone
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
And the cold rain sing along

One jar rolled to the ditch
Sweet scent hit the wet air
Billy cursed at the sky
I just froze right there
Sirens came from the bend
Blue broke through the trees
We ran through the sorghum rows
With our boots and bleeding knees

Then the tires hit mud
And the tail light flashed
Heard a horn in the dark
Then the whole thing crashed

Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Moonshine run gone wrong
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Now the night feel strong
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Glass on the gravel, gone
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
And the cold rain sing along

Mama said, “Boy, don’t play
With fire in a jar”
Now I taste that lesson
Like smoke and rust and tar
Billy took the back road
I took the long way home
One bad turn in the rain
And I’m still paying it alone

Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Moonshine run gone wrong
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Now the night feel strong
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
Glass on the gravel, gone
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong

songs.subject: a folk song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
I write on the back porch boards
With a blue pen and a bent tin cup
They came with a polished smile
Said, "Boy, just dress it up"
But my lines still smell like rain
And the creek mud on my boots
If I trade my name for gold
What would any of it prove?

I've seen the shiny deals
I've seen them fold and crack
They love a quiet mouth
Till the truth comes walking back

I won't sell out
I won't sell out
Keep my hand on the page
Keep my feet in the clay
I won't sell out
I won't sell out
You can hear it in the wind
I was born this way

My old man mended fence wire
My mama sang while she fried the fish
Neither one had a silver stage
But they taught me what I should wish
To leave a mark that lasts
Like a name carved in river stone
Not a pretty lie in a frame
To hang where the rich folks roam

So if they bring the crown
I let it lie there cold
I'd rather keep these worn-out words
Than wear a borrowed soul

I won't sell out
I won't sell out
Keep my hand on the page
Keep my feet in the clay
I won't sell out
I won't sell out
You can hear it in the wind
I was born this way

Let them build their bright rooms
Let them clap for sound and shine
I'll take the cracked old road
And the hard-earned line by line
If my voice stays rough
If my coat stays torn
At least I'll know I kept
The thing I was made for

I won't sell out
I won't sell out
Keep my hand on the page
Keep my feet in the clay
I won't sell out
I won't sell out
When the fiddle starts to cry
I'll sing it anyway

I sing it anyway
I sing it anyway
Under rain, under dust
I sing it anyway

Keep my feet in the clay
I won't sell out
I won't sell out

I was born this way

songs.subject: a folk song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
He writes in the back room light
With ink on a borrowed chair
His coat smells like river smoke
And cold from the county fair
They came with a suitcase grin
Said, "Boy, make your words behave"
He shook that gold from his palm
Like stones from a grave

He said, "I won't paint my years
In somebody else's blue"
"I learned these hands from dirt
And I ain't tradin' truth for you"

He won't sell out
He won't sell out
He keeps his name in the clay
He won't sell out
He won't sell out
And the page won't learn to cave

The hotel man wants clean lines
The agent wants a simple tune
A crowd-pleaser, a softer edge
A little lie they can use
But he still counts the cracked porch boards
Still knows each scar by heart
And every town that tried to buy him
Only handed back the dark

He said, "I won't dress my scars
For a room that can't look true"
"I'd rather be a broken song
Than a pretty lie to you"

He won't sell out
He won't sell out
He keeps his name in the clay
He won't sell out
He won't sell out
And the page won't learn to cave

Let them flash their silver talk
Let them roll their polished chains
His words come like creek water
After three hard days of rain
If the world wants a cheaper heart
It can go and find one else
He'll stay where the honest ache is
And keep his soul to himself

He won't sell out
He won't sell out
He keeps his name in the clay
He won't sell out
He won't sell out
And the page won't learn to cave
He won't sell out
He won't sell out
Till the last bird leaves the gate
He won't sell out
He won't sell out

songs.subject: a funk song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
He writes on the bus seat
With a coffee ring crown
Got a pocket full of poems
And a city full of clowns
They came with a contract
Said, “Sign your soft side away”
He laughed at their gold pen
Then he tore it in the tray

He don’t bend
He don’t fold
His name still burns
On the page he sold
(He don’t bend)
(He don’t fold)

He won’t sell out
Won’t sell out
Keeps his ink black and proud
He won’t sell out
Won’t sell out
Won’t bow to the crowd
(He won’t sell out)

He spits truth in the square
Where the fine suits stand
Got a notebook scarred up
Like a working man’s hands
They said, “Tone it down, brother
Make the sharp edges smooth”
He said, “My words got teeth
And they came here to move”

He don’t bend
He don’t fold
He keeps that fire
In the lines he holds
(He don’t bend)
(He don’t fold)

He won’t sell out
Won’t sell out
Keeps his ink black and proud
He won’t sell out
Won’t sell out
Won’t bow to the crowd
(He won’t sell out)

Let them flash their badges
Let them dangle their bait
He’d rather eat hard bread
Than wear a borrowed face
If the room goes cold
He’ll still say what’s true
One poem for the broke ones
One poem for you

He won’t sell out
Won’t sell out
Keeps his ink black and proud
He won’t sell out
Won’t sell out
Won’t bow to the crowd
(He won’t sell out)

Ain’t no price on his name
Ain’t no chain on his tongue
He’ll stay wild with the word
Till the last line’s sung
(He won’t sell out)
(He won’t sell out)

songs.lyrics:
He writes on diner napkins
Grease stain, blue ink, bad sleep
Fingers stained with coffee rings
Still he keeps that paper key
They want him in a suit
With a smile and a sales pitch grin
He folds the check up small
Then he laughs and sets it in

He said, "I got my name
And I got my line"
"I don't trade a true word
For a brighter shine"

Won't sell out
Won't sell out
He keeps his fire in his mouth
Won't sell out
Won't sell out
You can hear him beat that doubt
(hey)
(hey)
Won't sell out

They sent a car with chrome
Told him, "Sign right here, sweet man"
Put gold on every page
Tried to own his right hand
He looked at that black pen
Like a snake in a silk tie
Then he wrote one clean verse
And watched their whole grin die

He said, "I got my name
And I got my line"
"I don't trade a true word
For a brighter shine"

Won't sell out
Won't sell out
He keeps his fire in his mouth
Won't sell out
Won't sell out
You can hear him beat that doubt
(hey)
(hey)
Won't sell out

He ain't rich, but he's real
That's a heavy kind of gold
Every line he ever made
Was a door he wouldn't fold
So if you hear him on the block
With the fold-up chair and grin
He'll be poor in their eyes
But he's rich where it counts within

Won't sell out
Won't sell out
He keeps his fire in his mouth
Won't sell out
Won't sell out
You can hear him beat that doubt
Won't sell out
Won't sell out

songs.subject: a house song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I used to pray out loud
With a cracked blue cup
Now the room felt bare
And my hands stayed up
The floorboards knew my name
The old screen door too
I said, "If You're still there
I need a sign from You"

And I walked that hill
With my chest all stone
If faith was a fire
Mine burned down to bones
Then a small thing moved
In the dark that night
Like a match in my ribs
Like a spark of light

Hold my hands
I’m finding my faith again
Hold my hands
I’m coming back in
Hold my hands
When the dark says "stay"
I still turn
I still pray

I lost it in the rush
In the bills on the table
In the bad news phone calls
At the edge of my patience
I wore that empty look
Like a coat too long
But a name in my mouth
Turned the whole world on

And I walked that hill
With my chest all stone
If faith was a fire
Mine burned down to bones
Then your voice came through
Like a door blown wide
And the parts of me gone
Came back alive

Hold my hands
I’m finding my faith again
Hold my hands
I’m coming back in
Hold my hands
When the dark says "stay"
I still turn
I still pray

I was lost, I was lost
But You found me first
Through the dust and the hurt
Through the thirst, through the thirst
Now I know that hope
Can grow in the wreck
I was empty for a while
Now I’m coming back

Hold my hands
I’m finding my faith again
Hold my hands
I’m coming back in
Hold my hands
When the dark says "stay"
I still turn
I still pray

Hold my hands
(hold my hands)
I’m coming back in
(finding my faith again)
Hold my hands
I still pray

songs.subject: a house song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I left my prayers in a drawer
Under a receipt and a key
I wore my doubt like a coat
Till it got heavy on me
Sunday glass on the floor
I watched it shine, then break
I said, “if you are still there”
But my throat went dry and blank

Then the room got thin
And the night got loud
I heard my own name
Like a hand pulled out
From the dark, from the dark
Something stayed alive
Not a fire, not a choir
Just a small inside

I lost my faith

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The clock ain't ticking right
On the stamping line tonight
Grease on the concrete floor
And the roll call's gone for sure
Lunch pails in the lockers
Names carved in the metal doors
The foreman folded paper
Said the order ain't coming no more

So we stand in the dust
With our sleeves rolled down
Hear the old beams groan
Like they're leaving town

Oy, the factory's going down
(going down)
Oy, the factory's going down
(going down)
Keep your shoes on the ground
One more turn before the sound
Oy, the factory's going down

My mother packed my father there
In a shirt with the oil stains
My sister learned her numbers
By the click of those machines
Now the windows hold the evening
Like a face that can't look back
And every little hammer
Feels a little harder to lack

So we stand in the dust
With our sleeves rolled down
Hear the old beams groan
Like they're leaving town

Oy, the factory's going down
(going down)
Oy, the factory's going down
(going down)
Keep your shoes on the ground
One more turn before the sound
Oy, the factory's going down

Take the bolts and the blueprints
Take the smell of the heat
Take the songs from the break room
And the ache in our feet
Let the clarinet cry
Let the accordion fold
We were built in that echo
Now the doors are cold

Oy, the factory's going down
(going down)
Oy, the factory's going down
(going down)
Lift your chin in the crowd
We were loud, we were proud
Oy, the factory's going down

One by one, we go home
With the dusk in our hands
But the floor keeps our footsteps
Like a tune in the bands
