songs.subject: a klezmer song about a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The gate hangs open now
Paint flaking off the sign
Six o'clock on the clock face
But nobody's in line
My father wore this floor out
My mother packed the tins
Now dust sits on the conveyor
Like snow against the bins

One more roll, one more light
One more shift that never starts
The foreman folded paper
And took the keys apart

Shut it down, shut it down
The last bell says goodbye
Shut it down, shut it down
We still sing and we still try
One more dance in the aisle
One more tear, one more round
Shut it down, shut it down
But the boys won't leave the ground

Lena on the loading dock
Kept her lunch pail in her hand
Moshe smoked outside the fence
Said, "I knew this day had plans"
The rivets in the window
Held the winter to the pane
Even now the cold comes in here
And calls each name again

One more roll, one more light
One more shift that never starts
The foreman folded paper
And took the keys apart

Shut it down, shut it down
The last bell says goodbye
Shut it down, shut it down
We still sing and we still try
One more dance in the aisle
One more tear, one more round
Shut it down, shut it down
But the boys won't leave the ground

Take the spanners off the wall
Take the chalk marks from the beam
Let the ash on the windowsill
Keep the shape of what we dreamed
If the river wants the rust
Let it find us standing strong
We were more than what we made here
We were hands inside a song

Shut it down, shut it down
The last bell says goodbye
Shut it down, shut it down
We still sing and we still try
One more dance in the aisle
One more tear, one more round
Shut it down, shut it down

songs.subject: a post-rock song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked curb
Same bent fence
Same boarded door
And the smell of rain on cement
Mama said
Keep your head down low
I learned the names
Of every car that rolled too slow

But I kept a map
In my chest all along
Tiny little spark
Saying hold on, hold on

I made it out
I made it out
From the dead-end block
I made it out
I made it out
I made it out
Still got the dust on me
But I made it out

The corner kids
Talked in smoke and dare
We drew our futures
On the backs of gas station air
I took the long way
Past the broken chain
One foot in the dark
One foot in the train

And the whole block watched
As I turned to go
I heard my own heart
Say move, move slow

I made it out
I made it out
From the dead-end block
I made it out
I made it out
I made it out
Still got the dust on me
But I made it out

There was a night
I almost folded in
Eyes full of sirens
Hands full of thin
Then that small spark
Broke through the wall
Said you are not the street
You are bigger than it all

I made it out
I made it out
From the dead-end block
I made it out
I made it out
I made it out
Still got the dust on me
But I made it out

songs.lyrics:
Same cracked step
Same torn hoop
Same blue bin tipped
In the alley wind
Corner store glass
Got my face in it
Same old me
But I’m not staying

I watched the ash
Leave the curb
I watched my name
Get up and turn
One small door
One hard light
I keep walking
I keep walking

Out the block
Out the block
I’m gone, I’m gone
Out the block
Out the block
Your chain won’t hold me

Mama worked late

I saved my breath

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I sat by the fan
Where the bulletin creased
Same red carpet stain
By the third pew seat
Mama said amen
I said it too
But my hands stayed folded
Like they knew

The clock on the wall
Kept moving slow
Past the hymnbooks
Past the row
I felt that door
Call my name
Like a quiet fire
Under my shame

I slipped out slow
I slipped out slow
Past the back pew glow
Past the back pew glow
Don’t call my name
I already know
I slipped out slow
I slipped out slow

The usher nodded once
At the scratch of my shoe
I held my breath
Like the saints taught you
Past the coat rack
Past the cracked glass light
Past the folded chairs
And the after-service bite

No scene, no sound
Just my coat in hand
And the old front steps
Like a map of land
I heard one prayer
Hit the air
Then the parking lot
Took me there

I slipped out slow
I slipped out slow
Past the back pew glow
Past the back pew glow
Don’t call my name
I already know
I slipped out slow
I slipped out slow

If you saw me go
You saw my face
Trying not to break
In that holy place
I loved those walls
I loved that song
I just couldn’t stay
When it felt so wrong

I slipped out slow
I slipped out slow
Past the back pew glow
Past the back pew glow
Don’t call my name
I already know
I slipped out slow
I slipped out slow

songs.subject: a soul song about a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
I keep that dream
like a folded note
I rise above
my block and my coat
Bare feet in air
over porches and gates
I know the route
though I never say

Every night I lean
to the open dark
Every night it starts
with a beating heart
I shake off the ground
like it knows my name
And the whole world fades
when I make that climb

I fly, I fly
(again, again)
I fly, I fly
(over the edge)
If I wake too soon
I come back down slow
But in that dream
I already know

I pass the clock tower
the churchyard stone
Blue fields below
like a patched-up road
People look up
with their hands at their eyes
I wave at the rooftops
and the rain in the sky
My chest goes light
my fear lets go
Even the smoke
moves out of my way

Every night I lean
to the open dark
Every night it starts
with a beating heart
I shake off the ground
like it knows my name
And the whole world fades
when I make that climb

I fly, I fly
(again, again)
I fly, I fly
(over the edge)
If I wake too soon
I come back down slow
But in that dream
I already know

Maybe it means
there's more in me
Than this heavy skin
and this memory
Maybe I learned
before I was grown
How to leave the weight
and come back home

I fly, I fly
(again, again)
I fly, I fly
(over the edge)
If I wake too soon
I come back down slow
But in that dream
I already know

I fly, I fly
soft as a prayer
I fly, I fly
and you meet me there

(over the edge)

I already know

soft as a prayer

and you meet me there

songs.subject: a soul song about a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blur to stars above
Every night I lift off from my bed
No parachute no fear no shove
Just air rushing past my head

Higher higher I go
Leaving troubles down below

Oh I got wings of wonder
Flying through the midnight thunder
Wings of wonder
Never gonna come asunder

City sleeps a distant glow
Little cars like toys down there
I dip and weave a silent show
Dancing on the evening air

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Your towers sit quiet
On a giant’s chest
Every block a footprint
Every window a guess
I cross the market
Past the cracked old well
They say sleep stay sacred
So the warning bells

But the ground hums low
When the trucks roll by
Like something turning over
Deep in the sky

Sleep, giant, sleep
Hold the whole town close
Sleep, giant, sleep
We know what you know
Under my feet
The whole world leans
Sleep, giant, sleep
And let us dream

Kids on the corner
Draw horns in chalk
Old men on the stoops
Stop talking when I walk
There’s a bakery lady
With flour on her wrist
Says she hears him breathing
When the river twists

And the church doors shake
When the storm clouds climb
Like a back still aching
After all that time

Sleep, giant, sleep
Hold the whole town close
Sleep, giant, sleep
We know what you know
Under my feet
The whole world leans
Sleep, giant, sleep
And let us dream

If you wake too soon
Will the bridges bend?
Will the rows of brick
Know where to stand?
I don’t need answers
Just this steady beat
A giant in the ground
And you here with me

Sleep, giant, sleep
Hold the whole town close
Sleep, giant, sleep
We know what you know
Under my feet
The whole world leans
Sleep, giant, sleep
And let us dream

We know what you know

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
The buses shake my window
At the edge of dawn
You can hear that big old breathing
Even when it’s gone
Brick by brick, this town leaned
On a giant’s chest
We grew mango stands and playgrounds
On a sleeping best

And every corner knows it
Every alley holds the name
Everybody walks a little softer
Like we’re listening to the same

Under the giant
We learned to dance slow
Under the giant
We keep that old glow
If he turns in his sleep
The whole block feels it
Under the giant
Baby, we still live it

My auntie sells her roses
By the butcher shop
Kids play hopscotch on the fault line
And never think to stop
The river wears his shadow
Like a silver ring
We hang our clothes on hard wind
And still make them sing

And every prayer is folded
In the smoke above the food
Every laugh inside the market
Has a pulse that feels like wood

Under the giant
We learned to dance slow
Under the giant
We keep that old glow
If he turns in his sleep
The whole block feels it
Under the giant
Baby, we still live it

I saw him in the thunder
In the cracked blue sky
A thousand windows trembling
When he rolled one eye
But he didn’t rise to take us
He just held us down
Made a shelter out of warning
Made a home from sound

Under the giant
We learned to dance slow
Under the giant
We keep that old glow
If he turns in his sleep
The whole block feels it
Under the giant
Baby, we still live it

So let the night keep turning
Let the market stay lit
We were born from a giant
And we move like this
Under the giant

songs.subject: a americana song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise alarm
Coffee strong and black
Another day another dollar
Always on the attack
My truck needs gas
My boots got holes
This ain't no life
It takes its tolls

Two sides of the coin
Always flippin'
Always landin' wrong
I'm just slippin'
Working two jobs
Still can't get ahead
Wish I could just
Stay in my bed

Lunch break quick
Another time card punch
Boss man grumbles
Never enough
Sweat on my brow
Aching in my bones
This ain't no dream
Just different homes

Two sides of the coin
Always flippin'
Always landin' wrong
I'm just slippin'
Working two jobs
Still can't get ahead
Wish I could just
Stay in my bed

They say hard work
Pays off in the end
But my end is just
Another bill to send
Maybe tomorrow
Things will turn around
Or maybe I'll just keep
Digging in the ground

Two sides of the coin

songs.subject: a americana song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Punch the clock at dawn
Coffee in a paper cup
By noon I'm on job two
Still can't catch up
Receipt paper in my pocket
Gas light on red
Every little thing I need
Costs more than I said

I keep the wheels on
I keep the bills paid
But the numbers on the page
Still won't behave

Two jobs, still behind
(Two jobs, still behind)
Running hard just to keep up
Still I come up short tonight
Two jobs, still behind
(Two jobs, still behind)
Baby, I'm doing all I can
But I can't outrun this life

Lunch break in the truck bed
Knees sore, shirt wet
Boss says "good work, son"
Then hands me one more check
Kids need shoes by Friday
Rent's staring me down
I keep my smile on my face
When I circle back around

I keep the wheels on
I keep the lights on
But the week keeps taking more
Than I can hold on

Two jobs, still behind
(Two jobs, still behind)
Running hard just to keep up
Still I come up short tonight
Two jobs, still behind
(Two jobs, still behind)
Baby, I'm doing all I can
But I can't outrun this life

One day I'll breathe easy
One day I'll sleep through
Tonight I'm just a man
With a long list to do
If I bend, don't break
If I lean, don't fall
I'm one paycheck from okay
And that's a hard road to call

Two jobs, still behind
(Two jobs, still behind)
Running hard just to keep up
Still I come up short tonight
Two jobs, still behind
(Two jobs, still behind)
Baby, I'm doing all I can

songs.subject: a americana song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The sign buzzes blue by the road
Coffee rings on the counter
A cook in a faded ball cap
Wipes rain off the pie glass
Truckers pull in for a minute
Then stay till the sky turns pink

You can keep your bad news here
Leave it with the door
This place has room for a hurting heart
And a little more

All night diner, stay open
All night diner, keep the light on
All night diner, stay open
You got a seat for me

Two cops on a red Formica booth
A bride with a broken heel
A kid with a backpack and a dream
A man with a paper cup
The jukebox hums low and steady
Like it knows what we need

You can keep your bad news here
Leave it with the door
This place has room for a hurting heart
And a little more

All night diner, stay open
All night diner, keep the light on
All night diner, stay open
You got a seat for me

When the world goes hard and cold
And the miles get too long
There’s a plate of eggs and a name tag smile
To get you through till dawn

All night diner, stay open
All night diner, keep the light on
All night diner, stay open
You got a seat for me

songs.subject: a americana song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The coffee’s been on since dusk
Pie case shining in the glass
Marlboro glow in the parking lot
Old men talking in the past
The bell above the door says come on in
It’s been swinging all night long
Mabel wipes the counter clean
And hums a little after-song

When the county goes to sleep
That diner stays awake
You can bring your empty hands
Or the worry you can’t shake

Open all night, open all night
(hashbrown window)
Open all night, open all night
Where the broken hearts go right
Two eggs, black coffee
A booth by the light
Open all night, open all night
That diner stays alive

Truckers fold their maps in half
Kids come in with muddy shoes
A waitress calls you honey once
Like she knows the shape of you
The jukebox coughs out three slow songs
One coin at a time
Somebody’s laughing in the back
Somebody’s trying not to cry

When the county goes to sleep
That diner stays awake
You can bring your empty hands
Or the worry you can’t shake

Open all night, open all night
(hashbrown window)
Open all night, open all night
Where the broken hearts go right
Two eggs, black coffee
A booth by the light
Open all night, open all night
That diner stays alive

Past the grain silos, past the water tower
Past the last porch screen
There’s a red neon promise
In the rain between
Mabel says, “Sit a spell”
And the whole world eases down
With a warm slice of pie
And a paper cup crown

Open all night, open all night
(hashbrown window)
Open all night, open all night
Where the broken hearts go right
Two eggs, black coffee
A booth by the light
Open all night, open all night

songs.subject: a swing song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
I write my lines on the back of receipts
With a stubby blue pen and a hole in my sleeve
They came in a coupe with a grin and a check
Said, "Change that last verse, and we'll handle the rest"
I looked at their watch and I looked at my page
Said, "My words ain't a coat I can hand you to wear"
I got ink on my cuff and a bruise on my pride
But I won't trade my truth for a polished lie

I heard the silk-talk
I heard the spin
But my name won't bend
Just to let you in

No sale tonight
No sale tonight (no sale tonight)
I keep my spine
I keep my bite
No sale tonight (no sale tonight)
My pen don't fold
My soul don't sign

They wanted my sorrow in cleaner clothes
Wanted the crack in the glass to be turned to gold
Wanted my mother turned into a trend
Wanted the ending to smile for their friends
I said, "Take your pearls and your paper rain
I won't dress my hunger in somebody's chain"
I got a ragged rhyme and a stubborn shoe
And every true word I saved is a brick I threw

I heard the silk-talk
I heard the spin
But my name won't bend
Just to let you in

No sale tonight
No sale tonight (no sale tonight)
I keep my spine
I keep my bite
No sale tonight (no sale tonight)
My pen don't fold
My soul don't sign

Let 'em call me hard
Let 'em call me slow
A locked-up heart
Still learns to glow
I sing for the bench
For the cracked-up plate
For the ones who wait
And refuse the bait

No sale tonight
No sale tonight (no sale tonight)
I keep my spine
I keep my bite
No sale tonight (no sale tonight)
My pen don't fold
My soul don't sign

Says, "My words ain't for rent"
Not for nickel or dine

They flash him a briefcase
They flash him a grin

I won't sell out
No, I won't sell out
I keep my ink

I won't sell out
No, I won't sell out
You can keep your gold
I keep my shout (I keep my shout)

He knows the hotshot

But the poet still walks

Says, "A clean empty hand
Still beats a dirty win"

They say, "Take the easy
Take the fast parade"
He says, "I don't write
Just to get my name made"

I won't sell out
No, I won't sell out
I keep my ink
Where my truth comes out
I won't sell out
No, I won't sell out

He'll stand in the middle
Bare and proud

I won't sell out

I keep my ink
Where my truth comes out
I won't sell out
No, I won't sell out

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Your hand found mine
by the cake table
White sleeves, gold ring
and a borrowed grin
You said one song
I said maybe
Then the floor moved
under our shoes

The room went soft
around your face
Your laugh got caught
in the lace
I lost the edge
of where I was
You pulled me in
just because

Dance with a stranger
hold me awhile
Dance with a stranger
borrow my smile
No names, no reason
just this spin
Dance with a stranger
and let me in

Champagne on your cuff
smudged with rose petals
A child ran past
with a paper crown
The band played thin
like a memory
We turned slow circles
toward the sound

Your breath on my ear
like a secret
My shy little heart
couldn't keep it
The groom's old friends
blurred into rain
But your warm palm
stayed the same

Dance with a stranger
hold me awhile
Dance with a stranger
borrow my smile
No names, no reason
just this spin
Dance with a stranger
and let me in

When the lights fell low
and the tables slept
I could feel the night
in your wrist
Maybe tomorrow
we'll laugh it off
But tonight I lived
inside this

Dance with a stranger
hold me awhile
Dance with a stranger
borrow my smile
No names, no reason
just this spin
Dance with a stranger
and let me in

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Your hand found mine
by the cake table
white frosting on your sleeve
and a laugh I knew
for only one minute
You said, "Come on"
like we'd done this before
I followed your shoes
through the circling guests
and the slow glass shine

Spun under the mirrors
spun under the bloom
your tie a little crooked
my heart out of tune
and I forgot the room

Dance with me, stranger
dance with me, stranger
til the whole room fades
Dance with me, stranger
dance with me, stranger
keep my name away

Champagne on the floor
from a table near us
your palm at my back
steady as rain
on the church steps
I saw your smile
in the edge of the cake knife
silver and soft
like a secret held close
between two songs

Spun under the mirrors
spun under the bloom
your tie a little crooked
my heart out of tune
and I forgot the room

Dance with me, stranger
dance with me, stranger
til the whole room fades
Dance with me, stranger
dance with me, stranger
keep my name away

When the band took a breath
you leaned in nearer
your ring on the napkin
your eyes on the door
then the song came back

Dance with me, stranger
dance with me, stranger
til the whole room fades
Dance with me, stranger
dance with me, stranger
keep my name away
Dance with me, stranger
dance with me, stranger

Your hand found mine
by the cake table
white frosting on your sleeve
and a laugh I knew
for only one minute
You said, "Come on"
like we'd done this before
I followed your shoes
through the circling guests
and the slow glass shine

Spun under the mirrors
spun under the bloom
your tie a little crooked
my heart out of tune
and I forgot the room

songs.subject: a folk song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You showed up late
With sand on your feet
Two paper cups
And a cracked phone screen
I was saving a chair
By the lifeguard stand
You said, "Mind if I?"
And took my hand

We laughed too loud
At the ice cream line
You stole one sip
I stole your time
The tide kept moving
But we stayed in place
Blue bandana
On your sunburned face

Salt on your shirt
Salt on my skin
One little summer
I still live in
Salt on your shirt
Don't wash it off
We were a match
Then the season was gone

Your truck door slammed
By the bait shop wall
You played me a song
From a speaker too small
We shared one fry
In the parking lot
You drew my name
In a piece of salt

The sky went gold
Past the pier lights
You said "maybe soon"
I said "maybe tonight"
Your hand on my waist
When the whole beach blurred
I kept that feeling
Like a borrowed word

Salt on your shirt
Salt on my skin
One little summer
I still live in
Salt on your shirt
Don't wash it off
We were a match
Then the season was gone

Now August is dust
In a mason jar
A faded wristband
And your dented car
If I close my eyes
I can still go back
To your bare feet
On that boardwalk track

Salt on your shirt
Salt on my skin
One little summer
I still live in
Salt on your shirt
Don't wash it off
We were a match
Then the season was gone
Salt on your shirt
Salt on my skin
I still smile hard
When I think of him
Salt on your shirt
Gone with the dawn
We were a match
Then the season was gone

songs.subject: a folk song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You came down barefoot
With a towel on your shoulder
Frying pan sky
And the pier all splintered gold
You had salt on your collar
And a laugh I could follow
We stole one cold beer
From a red cooler in the sand

And the waves kept time
Like they knew our names
We sat too close
In the heat of the day

Sand in your hair
Hands on my skin
We were a small fire
Burning in the wind
Sand in your hair
Gone by September
I still taste that day
Like I could remember

We wrote our names
On the side of a sandcastle
Watched them wash away
Before the gulls came back
Your flip-flops by my feet
My shirt full of sea spray
You said, "Don't make this
Mean more than it is"

But you looked at me
Like you nearly meant stay
Then the tide rolled in
And stole the night away

Sand in your hair
Hands on my skin
We were a small fire
Burning in the wind
Sand in your hair
Gone by September
I still taste that day
Like I could remember

Now every hot July
When the boardwalk's packed
I hear your name
In a shell I pick up
I don't chase ghosts
I just let them pass through
But that beach still knows
How to pull me to you

Sand in your hair
Hands on my skin
We were a small fire
Burning in the wind
Sand in your hair
Gone by September
I still taste that day
Like I could remember
Sand in your hair
I let it remain
A little sunlit ache

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise on the window pane
Empty coffee cold again
My phone buzzed a little note
Swallowed hard what she wrote

A little screen
So mean

Adiós Corazón
Gone gone gone
Over text
What comes next

No grand fight no stormy words
Just emoji little birds
Flying off into the blue
My heart broke right in two

A tiny chime
Lost all time

Adiós Corazón
Gone gone gone
Over text
What comes next

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise painted the desert gold
Another day same old story told
My coffee cold my heart much colder
You sent a text my world just older

A little buzz a little chime
Said goodbye way before its time

Ghost in my phone
Left me alone
Just a few words
Like mockingbirds
Ghost in my phone
Yeah you're gone
No more my own
Just a ghost in my phone

I read it twice I read it thrice
My eyes burned hot like desert spice
No explanation no good reason why
Just digital dust beneath a digital sky

Remember nights beneath the stars
Playing my guitar erasing scars
Now just a screen a tiny glow
Where did our forever go

Just a few words

Ghost in my phone
Yeah you're gone
No more my own
Just a ghost in my phone

I read it twice I read it thrice
My eyes burned hot like desert spice
No explanation no good reason why
Just digital dust beneath a digital sky

Remember nights beneath the stars
Playing my guitar erasing scars
Now just a screen a tiny glow
Where did our forever go

songs.subject: a disco song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Keys in the visor
Cash in the door
One last run tonight
Then I’m out the floor
Chrome on my knuckles
Mud on the mats
You said, “Don’t look back”
I said, “Copy that”

Red on the signal
Blue in the rain
My foot stayed calm
My hands stayed trained
You held the bag
I held the line
One clean exit
One last shine

Last job, last job
Let the tires sing
Last job, last job
Then I’m gone by spring
One more turn
One more song
Last job, last job
Then I’m gone

Toll booth counting
Like a church bell chime
Glovebox shaking
With borrowed time
You laughed so hard
At the silver cuffs
Said this whole town
Never gets enough

Backseat humming
With the heater low
I took the corner
Like I’d done before
Your ring tapped out
On the paper sack
Said, “If we make it”
I don’t come back

Last job, last job
Let the tires sing
Last job, last job
Then I’m gone by spring
One more turn
One more song
Last job, last job
Then I’m gone

At the warehouse gate
You kissed my cheek
Said, “Run it clean”
Then let go of me
I saw my name
In the rearview glass
A little white lie
Going way too fast

Last job, last job
Let the tires sing
Last job, last job
Then I’m gone by spring
One more turn
One more song
Last job, last job
Then I’m gone
Last job, last job
Watch me vanish clean
Last job, last job
Off the screen

songs.subject: a disco song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine hummin' low and sweet
Leather seats beneath my feet
One last score a final plea
Then the open road for me

Lights are flashin' red and blue
Got to make it make it through

Midnight rider pedal down
Ghostin' through this sleepy town
Midnight rider can't look back
On this one way one way track

Diamond heist a whispered plan
In this souped up getaway van
Heartbeat thumpin' like the bass
Gonna leave this whole darn place

They say you never beat the house
Just a whisper just a mouse
But tonight the dice are hot
This is all the chance I got

Midnight rider pedal down
Ghostin' through this sleepy town

songs.subject: a tango song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Silks and satins on the floor
Scissors flash a silver gleam
She knows my size she knows my dreams
From the rich man to the poor

Threads of fate
She weaves them straight

Seamstress of my soul
You make me whole
Every stitch a story told
Seamstress of my soul

Old Man Tiber wants new cuffs
Young Maria needs a ball gown bright
She works all day she works all night
Never says enough's enough

Seamstress of my soul
You make me whole
Every stitch a story told
Seamstress of my soul

They say she sees right through your skin
Knows the secrets you keep deep within

songs.subject: a tango song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
On Cypress, dawn came thin
Your hem was dragging on the bin
I took your coat, I took your sleeve
Set your name in the lining neat
Six floors waiting by my door
Mrs. Vale, the boxer, more
One chalk line, one silver pin
I make this block fit right within

Knock on wood, hear the scissors sing
Every door wants one more thing
Hold your chin, let the thread run true
I dress the street like I dress you

I dress the whole block
(whole block)
Sharp as a clock
(sharp as a clock)
I take the loose ends
And I make them talk
I dress the whole block
(whole block)

Mr. Cruz in a Wednesday suit
Little Ana in her red plaid boots
The porter wants a midnight cuff
The baker says his pockets puff
I patch up pride with a single seam
Turn old work shirts into a dream
When the rain hits hard and the buses shake
My window shines with the shapes I make

Knock on wood, hear the scissors sing
Every door wants one more thing
Hold your chin, let the thread run true
I dress the street like I dress you

I dress the whole block
(whole block)
Sharp as a clock
(sharp as a clock)
I take the loose ends
And I make them talk
I dress the whole block
(whole block)

Last night the landlord came in late
Asked for a suit to fix his face
I said, "Stand still, let the mirror learn"
Even a hard heart has a seam to turn

I dress the whole block
(whole block)
Sharp as a clock
(sharp as a clock)
I take the loose ends
And I make them talk
I dress the whole block

songs.subject: a flamenco song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
I sat down with four cards
And a grin I could not hide
You dealt like you had thunder
I dealt like I had pride
The table was a church floor
Every chip was a vow
We laughed at the first hand
We don’t laugh there now

One more turn
Then the blood ran hot
One more turn
And I missed my spot
Your eyes said take it
My hands said go
The room got small
And the moon got low

Ay, too far, too far
We played too far
(Saqué mi suerte)
Ay, too far, too far
We played too far
(Se fue la suerte)
No more bets on the floor
No more lies at the door
Ay, too far, too far
We played too far

You slammed down a queen high
I threw down a burned king
The candle shook sideways
Like it knew everything
Then fists met the timber
And glass kissed the ground
A chair went splinter-singing
That sound still haunts this town

One more turn
Then the room went thin
One more turn
And the night stepped in
Your laugh turned bitter
Mine turned to stone
We raised the stakes
Till we broke the bone

Ay, too far, too far
We played too far
(Saqué mi suerte)
Ay, too far, too far
We played too far
(Se fue la suerte)
No more bets on the floor
No more lies at the door
Ay, too far, too far
We played too far

Now the cards are face-down
In a cracked red tin
The dust on the table
Keeps the shape we were in
If you hear that shuffle
Don’t answer, don’t come
Some games leave a scar
Some games leave a drum

Ay, too far, too far
We played too far
(Saqué mi suerte)
Ay, too far, too far
We played too far
(Se fue la suerte)
No more bets on the floor
No more lies at the door
Ay, too far, too far
We played too far

songs.subject: a flamenco song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We sat by the olive cask
Two coins and a crooked grin
You dealt with a velvet hand
And I said, "Let the night begin"
The first round cut like honey
The second turned my mouth to stone
Your ring tapped hard on the table
Like it had a mind of its own

Ay, the cards kept falling
Like ash from a burned-out roof
Ay, your eyes got quiet
And the truth got sharp as proof

Too far, too far
We went too far
(too far, too far)
Too far, too far
The whole room went dark
(too far, too far)
The deck went cold
The blood ran hot
You won my name
And I forgot

You laughed when I raised my hand
Like you knew where the trap was laid
My friend on the far side froze
When you slid that blade in shade
Not on the felt, not on the rules
But under the plate and cloth
A little lie in a linen sleeve
A kiss with its teeth pulled off

Ay, the cards kept falling
Like ash from a burned-out roof
Ay, your eyes got quiet
And the truth got sharp as proof

Too far, too far
We went too far
(too far, too far)
Too far, too far
The whole room went dark
(too far, too far)
The deck went cold
The blood ran hot
You won my name
And I forgot

So I stood, and the chair went back
With a crack like split wood on stone
Your mother’s chain on your throat
Caught the lantern light and shone
One prayer, one shout, one broken laugh
Then the table turned to war
Now the cards lie face down still
And nobody asks for more

Too far, too far
We went too far
(too far, too far)
Too far, too far
The whole room went dark
(too far, too far)
The deck went cold
The blood ran hot
You won my name
And I forgot

Ay-ay-ay
The night had teeth
Ay-ay-ay

songs.subject: a hip hop song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Engine hummin'
Horizon comin'
Sky's a canvas, dark and deep, secrets it's keepin'
Map spread wide, lines I'm readin', ain't no time for sleepin'
Pressure droppin', wind's a whisper, then it's howlin'

Eye of the storm, yeah, that's my home
Never settled, always roam
Twister chaser, soul untamed
This wild ride, it's my claim

Radar blips, red and green, a symphony of light
Velocity shear, yeah, that's the scene, day turnin' into night
Funnel cloud, touchin' down, nature's rawest power
Every town, every sound, livin' for this hour
Some chase paper, some chase fame, me, I chase the weather

Eye of the storm, yeah, that's my home
Never settled, always roam
Twister chaser, soul untamed
This wild ride, it's my claim

They say I'm crazy, lost my mind, a wanderer unbound
But in that chaos, peace I find, no solid ground
Just wind and thunder, rain and hail, a violent, sweet embrace
Another town, another trail, pick

Engine hummin'
Horizon comin'
Sky's a canvas, dark and deep, secrets it's keepin'
Map spread wide, lines I'm readin', ain't no time for sleepin'
Pressure droppin', wind's a whisper, then it's howlin'

Eye of the storm, yeah, that's my home
Never settled, always roam
Twister chaser, soul untamed
This wild ride, it's my claim

Radar blips, red and green, a symphony of light

Every town, every sound, livin' for this hour

Another town, another trail, pick

songs.subject: a hip hop song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I got mud on my boots
And a room I don't use
One week in a town
Then I'm gone with the news
My phone on the dash
Maps folded in half
If the sky starts to bruise
I don't ask where it's at
I can sleep in the truck
With the engine still warm
Then I wake up hungry
For the edge of the storm

You say, "stay right here"
But my feet get loud
I see the first dark line
And I chase it down

Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I hear that low growl
And I run it down
Can't settle down
Can't settle down
When the clouds roll in
I'm already out

Got a motel stain
On a work shirt sleeve
And a gas station cup
Full of chipped iced tea
I left a girl by the lake
With her hand on the rail
She said, "pick one place"
I said, "I never will"
Got a box in the back
Full of maps and spare keys
Every town I swear off
Still keeps calling me

You say, "build a home"
But the wind's my room
I hear thunder talk
And I move too soon

Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I hear that low growl
And I run it down
Can't settle down
Can't settle down
When the clouds roll in
I'm already out

Maybe one day
I'll park for good
Hang my coat by a door
Like a good man should
But the horizon leans
And it knows my name
One hard rattle of rain
And I'm back in the game

Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I hear that low growl
And I run it down
Can't settle down
Can't settle down
When the clouds roll in
I'm already out
Can't settle down
Can't settle down

songs.subject: a indie rock song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
You shook off the rain
At the corner store
I was half a step away
From the spinning door
Your sleeve brushed mine
When the wind came hard
You looked at the sky
Like it punched too far

I said, "You can stay"
Under this little roof
You laughed like you knew
That I was telling the truth

One umbrella
Two of us
One umbrella
A little too close
One umbrella
Through the flood
You and me
That’s how it goes

Your shoes were soaked
Mine too by then
We watched cars spray white
At the crosswalk bend
You said your name
I said mine back
Like we both found out
We were missing that

The rain kept time
On the fabric above
And your hand found the edge
Then my hand found the glove

One umbrella
Two of us
One umbrella
A little too close
One umbrella
Through the flood
You and me
That’s how it goes

At the bus stop bench
We split the space
A drop on your hair
A smile on your face
Then the clouds moved on
And the street turned gold
You gave back the umbrella
But not the hold

One umbrella
Two of us
One umbrella
A little too close
One umbrella
Through the flood
You and me
That’s how it goes

If I see you again
At the corner store
I’ll hope for rain
Just a little more

songs.subject: a indie rock song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain caught us at the crosswalk
You held it like a shield
I was half a block from home
You said, "Go on, make a deal"
Your sleeve was soaked through brown
Mine was just getting there
We laughed at how one umbrella
Could make us both look paired

No names, just shoes on tile
No plans, just this storm
You stepped a little closer
Like you knew what I was for

One umbrella
One small circle
Two strangers in the rain
One umbrella
We kept it simple
Till the thunder lost its name
(One umbrella)
(One umbrella)

At the corner store, you shook out
A pocket full of change
I bought two coffees, bad idea
You said it tasted strange
Your ring made a little click
On the paper cup
You asked if I was always
This quiet when it’s rough

No map, just waiting lights
No vow, just staying warm
You leaned in when the sidewalk
Turned into a river form

One umbrella
One small circle
Two strangers in the rain
One umbrella
We kept it simple
Till the thunder lost its name
(One umbrella)
(One umbrella)

The storm moved off to somewhere else
Your hand still touched the handle
A bus rolled by and missed us both
Like we were part of the scandal
Then you smiled, looked down, and said
"I guess this is goodbye"
But for one more block of dripping street
We shared the same sky

One umbrella
One small circle
Two strangers in the rain
One umbrella
We kept it simple
Till the thunder lost its name
One umbrella
I still remember
How close the whole world stayed
(One umbrella)

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The plate still sits in the sink
One fork bent on the rim
Your shoes by the mat
Like you might come back in
I fold my hands in my lap
Count every crack in the wall
The room keeps your shape
Even when you don't call

I hear the house breathe low
I hear my own pulse slow
I don't know what to say
So I let it go

After the door
After the slam
I still reach out
For your hand
After the fight
After the flame
I love you more
For saying my name

Your sweater on the chair
Still warm from your skin
The rain taps the pane
Like it wants back in
I trace your words in the air
Then let them fade to gray
We hurt each other loud
Now it's quiet in the way

I hear the house breathe low
I hear my own pulse slow
I don't know what to say
So I let it go

After the door
After the slam
I still reach out
For your hand
After the fight
After the flame
I love you more
For saying my name

If you come back soft
I'll meet you there
With the last of my pride
And a loosened stare
We can leave the mess
Where it broke apart
Just don't make me learn
Your room from the dark

After the door
After the slam
I still reach out
For your hand
After the fight
After the flame
I love you more
For saying my name
After the door
After the pain
Stay a little while
Let us begin again

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The spoon is still in the sink
One glass on the sill
Your coat on the chair
Like you might come back in
We said too much
Then nothing at all
The room kept its shape
After we let it fall

Now the air feels thin
Like a page held up to light
I hear your steps
In the pipes tonight

Stay here, stay here
In the quiet we made
Stay here, stay here
Let the sharp words fade
Stay here, stay here
On the edge of the day
Stay here, stay here
Till the hurt turns gray

The bruise on your wrist
Was my hand on the door
I watched your eyes go glassy
Then stare at the floor
Your name on my tongue
Tastes like rain on zinc
I want to say sorry
But I don't know how to think

Now the air feels thin
Like a page held up to light
I hear your breath
On the other side tonight

Stay here, stay here
In the quiet we made
Stay here, stay here
Let the sharp words fade
Stay here, stay here
On the edge of the day
Stay here, stay here
Till the hurt turns gray

I don't need a winner
I just need your hands
Not the door between us
Not the thing I planned
If you turn toward me
I will turn back slow
We can leave the wreckage
Where the cold taps grow

Stay here, stay here
In the quiet we made
Stay here, stay here
Let the sharp words fade
Stay here, stay here
On the edge of the day
Stay here, stay here

songs.subject: a reggae song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They paved the lane by the bakery
They paved the yard by the school
Now one green branch by the bus stop
Still bends like it knows the rules
I seen block after block go gray
I seen mud turn hard as stone
But one old tree by the fence line
Still stands there, skin and bone

And when the trucks roll by
It leans, then leans right back
Like it heard our names
From the dirt they took off track

Last tree in town
Hold on, hold on
Last tree in town
Stand strong, stand strong
(last tree in town)
You keep our shade
You keep our song

Kids chalk hearts on the sidewalk
Old folks rest where roots once ran
A mango drops on the curbside
Like a gift from a ghosted hand
The bird that nested in the springtime
Still comes back through the heat
Singing over cement dust
Singing over broken streets

And when the sun burns white
You spread one stubborn green
Like a promise left
In a place that got too clean

Last tree in town
Hold on, hold on
Last tree in town
Stand strong, stand strong
(last tree in town)
You keep our shade
You keep our song

If they come with more bright orders
If they come with more hard lines
Let the kids wrap rope around you
Let their laughter make you shine
We were never just these blocks
Never just this iron skin
Long as your roots keep breathing
We know where we began

Last tree in town
Hold on, hold on
Last tree in town
Stand strong, stand strong
(last tree in town)
You keep our shade
You keep our song

Stay green
Stay here
Stay green
Stay here
(last tree in town)

songs.subject: a reggae song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
The road is gray and hot
Where mango shade used to fall
They poured the town in concrete
And named it after a mall
But by the busted bus stop
One tree still leans and stays
Roots wrapped round old water pipes
Like memory under the clay

We gather there at noon
To catch one line of cool
Children touch the bark and grin
Like they found a hidden school

Last tree standing, stand
Last tree standing, stand
Hold up your green hands
Hold up your green hands
Last tree standing, stand
You still know who we are
Last tree standing, stand
Under that weathered star

Old men tell the same stories
Of birds in a thousand wings
Now pigeons circle drain pipes
And tug at broken strings
A lady lays down water
In a ring around the trunk
Says, “If this one keeps on breathing
Then the whole place ain’t sunk”

Hear that little rustle
In the noon-day heat?
That’s the town remembering
How to bend and breathe

Last tree standing, stand
Last tree standing, stand
Hold up your green hands
Hold up your green hands
Last tree standing, stand
You still know who we are
Last tree standing, stand
Under that weathered star

When the rain comes hard
It drums on tar and tin
But your leaves keep talking
To the children gathered in
If the bulldozers roll back
If they come for this last place
We’ll put our shoulders to the ground
And guard that living face

Last tree standing, stand
Last tree standing, stand
Hold up your green hands
Hold up your green hands
Last tree standing, stand
You still know who we are
Last tree standing, stand
Under that weathered star
Last tree standing, stand
Let the whole town hear
Last tree standing, stand

songs.subject: a swing song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
I had my coat on the chair
Ticket in my hand
Your laugh by the doorway
And the whole night in my plan
One more step, one more sign
And I’d be across the line

The band kept leaning in
The room got small
Your eyes said maybe
Then the moment stalled

Almost made it, almost made it
Right at the edge, I tasted it
Almost made it, almost made it
Right in my hands, then I lost it
(Almost made it)
(Almost made it)

The waiter cleared the candle
The rain hit the glass
You turned your head too quick
And the spell could not last
I held my breath too long
Watched the good thing pass

The horns went soft for me
The floor stayed bright
I reached for tomorrow
But you turned out of sight

Almost made it, almost made it
Right at the edge, I tasted it
Almost made it, almost made it
Right in my hands, then I lost it
(Almost made it)
(Almost made it)

Now every Sunday shoe tap
Every half-smile keeps score
I swear I know that doorway
I’ve stood there before
If you ever swing back through
I’ll be ready for more

Almost made it, almost made it
Right at the edge, I tasted it
Almost made it, almost made it
Right in my hands, then I lost it
Almost made it, almost made it
One more turn and I would have got it
(Almost made it)
(Almost made it)

songs.subject: a swing song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
We had the room, the tune, the shine
Your hand in mine, and mine on time
The marble floor was slick with hope
My shiny shoes and a tighter coat
The band looked up, then grinned at me
Like, "Son, tonight might set you free"
I took one breath, then took that step
And heard the crowd go half a bet

One more turn
One more smile
I could taste it
For a while
One more note
One more chance
We were standing
In the dance

That was the night
I almost made it
Almost got high
Right where I aimed it
Almost, almost
Right on the line (right on the line)
That was the night
I almost made it

The lights came down, the clock came loud
I saw my name in the swing of the crowd
You laughed so hard, you shook your curls
I thought, "Tonight, I might change worlds"
But the floor rolled out like a trick of fate
My heel slipped wrong at the open gate
The song kept smiling, fast and sweet
While my big old dream missed the beat

One more turn
One more spin
I was so close
To coming in
One more drink
One more glance
And the whole thing
Felt like a dance

That was the night
I almost made it
Almost got high
Right where I aimed it
Almost, almost
Right on the line (right on the line)
That was the night
I almost made it

Oh, I can still see the shine
On that thin little line
Where the maybe lived
And the almost did
I didn't fall
I just bounced
And the whole world
Kept its count

That was the night
I almost made it
Almost got high
Right where I aimed it
Almost, almost
Right on the line (right on the line)
That was the night
I almost made it

So pour one more
For the near and dear
The night I missed it
But came too near (came too near)
Yeah, that was the night

One more turn

For a while
One more note
One more chance

That was the night
I almost made it
Almost got high
Right where I aimed it
Almost, almost
Right on the line (right on the line)
That was the night
I almost made it

The lights came down, the clock came loud
I saw my name in the swing of the crowd
You laughed so hard, you shook your curls
I thought, "Tonight, I might change worlds"
But the floor rolled out like a trick of fate
My heel slipped wrong at the open gate
The song kept smiling, fast and sweet
While my big old dream missed the beat

One more turn
One more spin
I was so close
To coming in
One more drink
One more glance
And the whole thing
Felt like a dance

That was the night
I almost made it
Almost got high
Right where I aimed it
Almost, almost
Right on the line (right on the line)
That was the night
I almost made it

Oh, I can still see the shine
On that thin little line
Where the maybe lived
And the almost did
I didn't fall
I just bounced
And the whole world
Kept its count

That was the night
I almost made it
Almost got high
Right where I aimed it
Almost, almost
Right on the line (right on the line)
That was the night
I almost made it

So pour one more
For the near and dear
The night I missed it
But came too near (came too near)
Yeah, that was the night

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You left your cup at my place
Blue rim, chipped at the edge
Still by the sink
Still there
I saw your name on a receipt
Folded deep in my coat
Like a small old wave
That never left

And I told myself
It was just a season
Just a train
That passed too close
But every night
When the fan turns slowly
I hear your laugh
Under the floor

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
The sea kept you
I stayed
You were the one that got away
(away, away)
My hands still know
Your shape

Your sandal marks on my tile
Faded like chalk in rain
But I still step around
That little stain
The mango tree in the yard
Grew a new crooked limb
I thought of you
And the way you'd grin

I met a dozen bright days
None of them held you
None of them moved
The same way
So I let the clock go by
And I let the tide come
Still every moon
Calls your name

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
The sea kept you
I stayed
You were the one that got away
(away, away)
My hands still know
Your shape

Maybe you're dancing somewhere
Bare feet on a different floor
Maybe you forgot my face
But I keep your outline
In the hush between the waves
I still hear the almost of us
Soft as salt on skin
Gone, but not gone

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
The sea kept you
I stayed
You were the one that got away
(away, away)
My hands still know
Your shape

Blue rim, chipped at the edge
Still by the sink
Still there

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You left your scarf
On my chair
Blue cotton on Sunday
Still hanging there
I tried to move on
Tried to be kind
But your name keeps drifting
Back through my mind

I saw your train
Pull out slow
You waved like you knew
I’d never let go

You were the one
The one that got away
You were the one
That slipped through my hands
Oh, I still hum
Your old refrain
You were the one
The one that got away

Now the café’s crowded
On Rua do Mar
Same little table
Same silver jar
And when the rain comes
Soft on the glass
I taste that goodbye
That never passed

I fold the night
Into three
But your laughing mouth
Still comes back to me

You were the one
The one that got away
You were the one
That slipped through my hands
Oh, I still hum
Your old refrain
You were the one
The one that got away

Maybe in another town
We’d stay
Maybe in another hour
You’d have said okay
But the tide kept turning
And so did we
Now all I have
Is the shape of you in me

You were the one
The one that got away
You were the one
That slipped through my hands
Oh, I still hum
Your old refrain
You were the one

songs.subject: a swing song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
He keeps a map in the glove
Coffee gone cold in the cup
Radio crackles a warning
He just grins and sleeves up
Dust on his boots, red clay
Mud on the doors of his truck
He says, “I was born for the chase”
Then he burns right out of luck

He knows that rumble
He knows that sky
When the air gets mean
He comes alive

Tornado man, tornado man
He can’t sit still, he can’t hold hands
Tornado man, tornado man
He’s gone again with the thunderband
(Go on, go on)
(He can’t settle down)

Mama tried porch swings and Sundays
A town with a church and a train
He had one foot out on the gravel
One eye on the wall of rain
He loves the green hush before it
That weird, deep breath in the heat
Then the fence posts bend like matchsticks
And he’s smiling in his seat

He hears the sirens
He hears the call
If the clouds start rolling
He wants it all

Tornado man, tornado man
He can’t sit still, he can’t hold hands
Tornado man, tornado man
He’s gone again with the thunderband
(Go on, go on)
(He can’t settle down)

He says, “Don’t pin me to a front door
Don’t ask me where I stay”
The road keeps rolling under him
Like he was built that way

Tornado man, tornado man
He can’t sit still, he can’t hold hands
Tornado man, tornado man
He’s gone again with the thunderband
(Go on, go on)
(He can’t settle down)
Tornado man, tornado man
When the sky goes dark, he understands
(Go on, go on)
(He can’t settle down)

songs.subject: a swing song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I learn the sky by name
Every low front, every flame
Roll out when the crows go quiet
Boots on dirt, can't fight it
Map on the seat, coffee gone cold
Phone full of miles and weather I fold
When the air gets heavy, I grin
Baby, that's where I begin

If the horizon starts to curl
I’m already out the door
That dark down on the barley field
Keeps calling me for more

I can't settle down
I chase that thunder sound
I can't settle down
Till the whole town spins around
(Turn it loose)
(Turn it loose)
I can't settle down
I was born to run that cloud

Pulled off by a county line sign
Mud on my cuffs, rain in my wine
I try to sit still in some room
Then the sirens bloom
One hand on the wheel all night
One eye on the bruise up high
If peace looks soft, I let it go
Storms know me better, you know

When the wind starts stacking up
And the trees begin to lean
I feel that restless fever
Burning clean through me

I can't settle down
I chase that thunder sound
I can't settle down
Till the whole town spins around
(Turn it loose)
(Turn it loose)
I can't settle down
I was born to run that cloud

Maybe one day, maybe not
I'll park the truck and take my shot
But the road keeps whistling my name
And the sky plays rough and plays games
So I laugh, hit the gas, let it roll
A little rain won't hold my soul

I can't settle down
I chase that thunder sound
I can't settle down
Till the whole town spins around
(Hey now)
(Hey now)
I can't settle down

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise on the water bright
Leaving everything behind
Packed my hope and little fear
Ocean stretching far and wide

Waves crashin' loud and low
Where my new life gonna go

Oh ocean heartbeat
Yeah ocean heartbeat
My heart beats strong
Ocean heartbeat

Stars above a lonely boat
Every wave a whispered note
Mama's face a memory
Future's calling out to me

Salt spray on my weary face
Searching for my rightful place

Oh ocean heartbeat
Yeah ocean heartbeat
My heart beats strong
Ocean heartbeat

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Left my shoes by the door
Folded my old street in two
Mama kissed my forehead slow
Said, “Go before the dawn breaks through”
Bag so light, heart so full
One shirt, one prayer, one name
I watched the shore get small
Like it never knew my pain

Water kept calling me
Take what you can, come on
I held my breath for the jump
Then I let the fear move on

Across the sea, across the sea
My name still rides with me
Across the sea, across the sea
I’m gone, but I’m still me
Hold that line
Don’t let it break
Across the sea
I’m on my way (across the sea)

New town, cold room, cheap floor
Salt still stuck in my hair
I learned to count my coins
And hide my homesick stare
Every call back home
Cuts through the orange light
But I send my love in cash
And I send my love in fight

Water kept calling me
Take what you can, come on
I held my breath for the jump
Then I let the fear move on

Across the sea, across the sea
My name still rides with me
Across the sea, across the sea
I’m gone, but I’m still me
Hold that line
Don’t let it break
Across the sea
I’m on my way (across the sea)

I carry the dock in my bones
I carry your voice in my chest
If the world says “start again”
I start with what’s left
One day I’ll send for you
One day we’ll laugh out loud
Till then I keep stepping
With my feet in the cloud

Across the sea, across the sea
My name still rides with me
Across the sea, across the sea
I’m gone, but I’m still me
Hold that line
Don’t let it break
Across the sea

songs.subject: a dream pop song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
You said my last name first
Like you knew the spelling
From the room with the red brick wall
By the bowling alley
I still know that turn
Past the clinic and the laundromat
You were there in my old town
Now my old town wants you back

We laughed too loud
In the parking lot rain
Your hand on my sleeve
And I felt my name change
Slow, slow
Like a summer flood
I looked at you once
And it got me good

From my hometown
You got me, you got me
From my hometown
You caught me, you caught me
Same cracked sidewalks
Same old song
Now every road
Leads where you belong

Your mom still works at the market
Mine still talks too much
At the diner booth with the torn blue seat
We kept our eyes on cups
You knew my little scars
From the fence and the river rocks
Now I’m learning your soft new face
In the places we were not

We laughed too loud
In the parking lot rain
Your hand on my sleeve
And I felt my name change
Slow, slow
Like a summer flood
I looked at you once
And it got me good

From my hometown
You got me, you got me
From my hometown
You caught me, you caught me
Same cracked sidewalks
Same old song
Now every road
Leads where you belong

If I leave, take me with you
Past the fields and the chain-link fence
I can hear our kids in the distance
From a place we haven’t met
Maple Street, your front porch
I’m not sure what this is
But I know when you said my name
It sounded like a kiss

From my hometown
You got me, you got me
From my hometown
You caught me, you caught me
Same cracked sidewalks
Same old song
Now every road
Leads where you belong

songs.subject: a dream pop song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I saw you by the laundromat
With your blue plastic basket
Same cracked sidewalk I knew at ten
Now it feels different again
You said, “Hey, do I know you?”
And I forgot my own name
Your laugh hit that old corner store
Like the first warm rain

We grew up on the same dead end
Same rusted fence, same late bus wind
Now your hand on the cup I’m holding
Turns the whole block into gold

You feel like home to me
You feel like home to me
Right where my heart should be
You feel like home to me
(Feels like home)
(Feels like home)

We cut through the soccer field
Past the park with the bent-back swing
You told me your mom still waters
That stubborn row of marigolds in spring
I knew that porch, that chipped white step
That mailbox leaning in the heat
But I never knew one street could hide
Something this sweet

Now the corners that I used to leave
Keep pulling me back when you smile at me
Every old shop window reflection
Shows us as something new

You feel like home to me
You feel like home to me
Right where my heart should be
You feel like home to me
(Feels like home)
(Feels like home)

And if we take the long way back
Past the field and the fence and the track
I don’t need maps, I don’t need proof
I found my way by following you
By following you

You feel like home to me
You feel like home to me
Right where my heart should be
You feel like home to me
(Feels like home)

songs.subject: a afrobeats song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I left my shoes by the door
But I never stayed
Rain on the windshield
Another town today
Map in the glovebox
Coffee gone cold
I hear thunder calling
And I lose control

You say, "Babe, come home"
But the clouds look alive
My heart hits the road
When the sky starts to rise
I try to be still
Try to stay by your side
Then the wind pulls my name
And I’m gone in the night

I can’t settle down
I chase the storm
I can’t settle down
My feet want more
When the sky gets loud
I’m already gone
I can’t settle down
I chase the storm
(chase the storm)
(chase the storm)

Red dirt on my denim
Mud on my phone
Missed your last three calls
I was out there alone
Gas station coffee
By the county line
You said pick a place
I said give me time

You say, "Babe, come home"
But the clouds look alive
My heart hits the road
When the sky starts to rise
I try to be still
Try to stay by your side
Then the wind pulls my name
And I’m gone in the night

I can’t settle down
I chase the storm
I can’t settle down
My feet want more
When the sky gets loud
I’m already gone
I can’t settle down
I chase the storm
(chase the storm)
(chase the storm)

Maybe one day
I’ll slow my run
Hang my coat up
Watch the morning sun
But tonight, baby
Hear that rumble roll
I was born for the road
With a restless soul

I can’t settle down
I chase the storm
I can’t settle down
My feet want more
When the sky gets loud
I’m already gone
I can’t settle down
I chase the storm
(chase the storm)
(chase the storm)

songs.subject: a swing song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
The heavens opened,
A sudden, soaking spree.
I was caught without a notion,
Just me, myself, and the street.
Then a shadow fell,
A dome of midnight blue.
"Mind if I share?" you asked so well,
And my world turned brand new.

Oh, the umbrella built for two,
A little roof against the rain.
Suddenly the grey skies flew,
Chasing all my lonely pain.
Underneath that tiny span,
A perfect fit, it's true.
Just a girl and a man,
And an umbrella built for two.

Your shoulder brushed mine,
A polite, accidental sway.
My heart picked up the time,
And danced the blues away.
We talked of weather,
Of silly, small affairs.
But my thoughts were all together,
Dreaming of future shares.

Oh, the umbrella built for two,
A little roof against the rain.
Suddenly the grey skies flew,
Chasing all my lonely pain.
Underneath that tiny span,
A perfect fit, it's true.
Just a girl and a man,
And an umbrella built for two.

The rain has stopped its falling,
The sun peeks through the gray.
But this moment's still enthralling,
I wish we could just stay.
Underneath this magic dome,
No need to rush or flee.
Could this be my new home,
With you, and this, and me?

Oh, the umbrella built for two,
A little roof against the rain.
Suddenly the grey skies flew,
Chasing all my lonely pain.
Underneath that tiny span,
A

songs.subject: a swing song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like a grin
You and me got pinned
At the corner by the train
Same black coat, same rain
You said, “Mind if I?”
I said, “That’s mine”
We laughed at the way
The sky got mean tonight

Shoulder to shoulder
We leaned a little closer
One hand on the handle
One heart getting bolder

One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
One little roof, no danger
(one umbrella)
You on your side, me on mine
Still we stole the same dry line
One umbrella, two strangers
And the rain felt kind

Your shoes made a splash
My sleeve caught a lash
We walked like we knew
How this sort of thing would do
You asked my name
I asked the same
The puddles in the street
Kept time with our feet

Shoulder to shoulder
The crowd got a little older
But your laugh in the downpour
Made the whole world warmer

One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
One little roof, no danger
(one umbrella)
You on your side, me on mine
Still we stole the same dry line
One umbrella, two strangers
And the rain felt kind

At the last corner light
You looked up, said, “Stay?”
I said, “For a minute”
Then you took my breath away
The storm kept tapping
The curb, the glass, the rail
But your hand found mine
Like it already knew the trail

One umbrella, two strangers
(one umbrella)
One little roof, no danger
(one umbrella)
You on your side, me on mine
Still we stole the same dry line
One umbrella, two strangers
And the rain felt kind

At the crosswalk parting
You smiled, then you were gone
Still I kept that umbrella

songs.subject: a soul song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise paints the window pane
Another day the same old game
Buildings reachin' for the sky
But down below a big thing lie

Concrete colossus dreamin' deep
Secrets the city tries to keep
One day he's gonna stretch and yawn
Then where will all our towers be gone

Traffic hums a sleepy tune
Beneath the pale electric moon
Folks rushin' here folks rushin' there
No one knows what's sleepin' down there

A tremor in the asphalt street
Just a big guy shiftin' his feet

Concrete colossus dreamin' deep
Secrets the city tries to keep
One day he's gonna stretch and yawn
Then where will all our towers be gone

They paved him over built him tall
Thought they could never ever fall
But giants sleep a heavy sleep
And promises they always keep

songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blinkin'
Like tired eyes
Another Monday mornin'
Under smoggy skies
This whole town hummin'
A low soft drone
Built on something ancient
Yeah we make it our home

But down below
A rumble deep

Concrete dreamin'
On a sleeping giant
Concrete dreamin'
Oh so defiant
Concrete dreamin'
We build it high
Concrete dreamin'
Underneath the sky

Skyscrapers reachin'
Like bony fingers
For a sun that's hidin'
Where the city lingers
We pave the pathways
With our busy feet
Never mind the tremors
Beneath the street

They say he's just resting
A long long sleep
But sometimes I wonder
What secrets he'll keep
Will he stretch and yawn
And shake us all down
Or just turn over slowly
Without a sound

Concrete dreamin'
On a sleeping giant
Concrete dreamin'
Oh so defiant
Concrete dreamin'
We build it high
Concrete dreamin'

songs.subject: a R&B song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You left your ring mark
On the glass by the sink
I still move it aside
Like it's too hard to think
Your coat's on the chair
Still got rain in the sleeve
I tell myself "move on"
But you still live in me

I saw your name
On my screen tonight
My thumb froze there
Like it knew you twice
And I don't even know
If you meant goodbye
But I still hold that door
For the version we might

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
You were the one that got away
I still say your name in the gray
(gray, gray)
You were the one that got away

Now my friends say "she's old news"
I just smile and lie
'Cause they don't see your laugh
Hanging in my mind
That corner booth at nine
Half a slice, one fork
You said "maybe someday"
Then you walked out the door

I saw your name
On my screen tonight
My thumb froze there
Like it knew you twice
And I don't even know

If you come back round
I won't act surprised
I kept your place
In the back of my life
Not one to beg
Not one to break
But some goodbyes
Still don't fade

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
You were the one that got away
I still say your name in the gray
(gray, gray)
You were the one that got away

songs.subject: a R&B song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:

Still smells like your cherry soap
I put it in the top drawer
Like it might bring you home
Your name still sits on my phone

I swear I heard you laugh last week

songs.lyrics:
I held the phone
Like it might burn
Your name on the screen
A small blue wound
I traced the crack
By my thumbprint line
Twenty missed winters
And one more time
The kettle clicked

I stared at the sink
And heard your tune

Hello, Mama

Mama, pick up now

I made it through the years
Mama, say my name

Hello, Mama
Don’t hang this up
Hello, Mama

Mama, pick up now
Mama, I’m here
Mama, pick up now
I made it through the years
Mama, say my name

Mama, pick up now

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I held the phone like a stone
Thumb on your old number
The screen lit up my face
Like it knew my shame
Six winters, maybe more
Since I heard you say my name
I watched the rain on glass
And let it say it first

Then I heard your breath
Small and far away
My heart leaned in
Like a door in wind

Ma, are you there?
Ma, can you hear me?
I’ve been gone so long
I’m still your child
Ma, are you there?
Ma, can you hear me?
I broke the silence
Please, stay awhile

You said, “Who is this?”
And I almost hung up
My tongue forgot its shape
My hands forgot to move
I told you where I work
You laughed at my new voice
Said I still sounded small
When I said your name

Then your silence cracked
Like a cheap glass cup
And I heard us both
In the space between

Ma, are you there?
Ma, can you hear me?
I’ve been gone so long
I’m still your child
Ma, are you there?
Ma, can you hear me?
I broke the silence
Please, stay awhile

I kept every wrong
Like a match in my coat
Burned my pocket full
Then called anyway
If you got the scar
I got mine too
Can we start again
From this little room?

Ma, are you there?
Ma, can you hear me?
I’ve been gone so long
I’m still your child
Ma, are you there?
Ma, can you hear me?
I broke the silence

songs.subject: a dream pop song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The chairs still face the door
Your coat hangs by the hall
I pour the morning sun
Across the paper flowers
The cake sits in its box
Untouched on the counter
I touch the folded cards
And read your name again

The kettle starts to sing
Like nothing ever changed
But every little sound
Feels one room farther away

Hold me in the morning
Hold me in the morning
Everything is softer
After the night we lost
Hold me in the morning
Hold me in the morning
I can hear your silence
Under the window frost

Your sister laughs too soon
Then covers up her mouth
My hands keep finding yours
In places you were not
The driveway is full of leaves
The steps are wet and gold
I stand where you stood last
And let the cold roll through

The kettle starts to sing
Like nothing ever changed
But every little sound
Feels one room farther away

Hold me in the morning
Hold me in the morning
Everything is softer
After the night we lost
Hold me in the morning
Hold me in the morning
I can hear your silence
Under the window frost

If I close my eyes
You are only light
On the edge of sleep
On the edge of white
And I know the world
Will keep moving on
But I want this ache
To have your name in it

Hold me in the morning
Hold me in the morning
Everything is softer
After the night we lost
Hold me in the morning
Hold me in the morning
I can hear your silence
Under the window frost

songs.subject: a dream pop song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise creeping through the blinds
Another day no more of your kind
Empty coffee cup sits there
Just like the echo of a prayer

My head still fuzzy
My thoughts all hazy

Shadows on the sun
Yeah the day has just begun
Shadows on the sun
But the feeling's still undone

Your old jacket on the chair
Smells of nothing now just air
The world keeps spinning fast and slow
I wish I knew where souls all go

Maybe you're a bird flying high
Maybe you're a cloud in the sky
Maybe you're the breeze I feel
This quiet ache it feels so real

Shadows on the sun
Yeah the day has just begun

Maybe you're a bird flying high

Maybe you're the breeze I feel
This quiet ache it feels so real

Shadows on the sun
Yeah the day has just begun

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You wore blue, I wore red
We both got shoved to the floor
Ma said, "Smile and mind your steps"
But I still wanted the door
Your heel tapped mine on beat
My jaw stayed tight all night
We spun like sparks in lace
And still we did it right

Ooh, the room went still
Ooh, then the lights grew dim
You said, "Don't fight the spin"
I said, "Let's learn this whim"

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Dance with me, rival
We'll figure it out
Ooh, side by side
Ooh, toe to toe
Dance with me, rival
Don't let go

Your brother laughed from the wall
My crew hollered "watch that grin"
You stepped on my patent shoe
I stepped back and spun you in
The band hit that sleepy groove
The floorboards gave their sigh
We traded all our hard-eyed looks
For a flicker in the eye

Ooh, the room went still
Ooh, then the lights grew dim
You said, "Don't fight the spin"
I said, "Let's learn this whim"

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Dance with me, rival
We'll figure it out
Ooh, side by side
Ooh, toe to toe
Dance with me, rival
Don't let go

Now your hand's in mine
Like it always knew the way
The whole room fades
When we sway
You can keep your name
I can keep mine too
For one slow song
We become new

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Dance with me, rival
We'll figure it out
Ooh, side by side
Ooh, toe to toe
Dance with me, rival
Don't let go
Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Dance with me, rival
We won't back down

songs.subject: a post-rock song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I folded my coat in two
Put my city in a bag
Mother stood by the rail
Holding one small flag
The dock was wet with rain
The ropes were slick and white
I said my last goodbye
Before the losing light

The sea kept calling my name
Like it knew what I had left
I watched the shoreline thin
Till it turned to breath

Across the sea
I carried your face
Across the sea
I made a new place
(hold on) (hold on)
Across the sea

Three nights of salt and steel
Three nights of waking slow
I learned the shape of waves
From the window glow
A boy in a borrowed coat
Shared bread and half a prayer
We named the hidden land
Before we got there

The sea kept calling my name
And I answered with my hands
The old world sank behind
Like sand through sand

Across the sea
I carried your face
Across the sea
I made a new place
(hold on) (hold on)
Across the sea

When the dark split open
And the deck went bare
I heard my father laugh
Somewhere in the air
Not gone
Not gone
Just moved by water

Across the sea
I carried your face
Across the sea
I made a new place
(hold on) (hold on)
Across the sea
Across the sea
I learned how to stay
Across the sea
I found my name
(hold on) (hold on)
Across the sea

songs.subject: a post-rock song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
They stamped my paper thin
At the harbor gate
My name in a new hand
My old shoes on the grate
I folded up the map
Till the creases bled
Left the key in the bowl
Left the bread uncut

One shore behind me
One dark line ahead
The gulls kept circling
Like words I never said

Cross the sea with me
Cross the sea with me
Hold the line, hold on
We are still here
Cross the sea with me
Cross the sea with me
Salt on my tongue
Home in my hands

The deck rolled under us
Like a sleeping beast
Tin cups slid and rang
From the west to the east
I watched the wake break white
Then vanish by dawn
Every mile took a name
Every wave took one

One shore behind me
One dark line ahead
The gulls kept circling
Like words I never said

Cross the sea with me
Cross the sea with me
Hold the line, hold on
We are still here
Cross the sea with me
Cross the sea with me
Salt on my tongue
Home in my hands

If I lose my language
Let the water keep time
If I lose my doorway
Let this body be mine
The old world fades out
Like foam on the rail
I was born in a leaving
I was made for the sail

Cross the sea with me
Cross the sea with me
Hold the line, hold on
We are still here
Cross the sea with me
Cross the sea with me
Salt on my tongue
Home in my hands
Cross the sea with me
Cross the sea with me
Not gone, not gone

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
On Rampart, black cars glide
White gloves, slow hands, cold sky
Beads on a casket, red and gold
Your mama folds that memory close
Second line, brass in the air
Umbrella steps in the smoke and glare
I see your name on the prayer card
I keep my face still, but it hits hard

Low drums, slow tears
Everybody move like this year
Carry that weight, carry that pride
Even the pain got a second line

Siete clavos, cielo y suelo
Siete clavos, mami, qué duelo
Siete clavos, back to the block
We dance for the gone, we don’t ever stop
Siete clavos, hands in the air
Siete clavos, crying and care
Siete clavos, beat of the street
Love goes heavy on Bourbon and Claiborne

At the wake, that food still hot
Red beans, rice, and a whole lotta talk
Aunties laugh through the heavy room
Then they hush when the trumpet blooms
Shoes tap out on the church floor
Kids ask why the song hurts more
Old men nod with their heads bowed low
Like they know where the good ones go

Low drums, slow tears
Everybody move like this year
Carry that weight, carry that pride
Even the pain got a second line

Siete clavos, cielo y suelo
Siete clavos, mami, qué duelo
Siete clavos, back to the block
We dance for the gone, we don’t ever stop
Siete clavos, hands in the air
Siete clavos, crying and care
Siete clavos, beat of the street
Love goes heavy on Bourbon and Claiborne

When the last bell rings
We don’t lose their name
We pass that fire
We pass that flame

Siete clavos, cielo y suelo
Siete clavos, mami, qué duelo
Siete clavos, back to the block
We dance for the gone, we don’t ever stop
Siete clavos, hands in the air
Siete clavos, crying and care
Siete clavos, beat of the street
Love goes heavy on Bourbon and Claiborne

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Hear the black umbrellas sway
On Rampart, all the way
Slow feet on wet old stones
Gold trim on the satin cloth
A brass band on the block
And your mama holds her rosary
While the second line moves on

Hands up, then let it go
We grieve, but we won't fold
Tambourines in the air
Footsteps beating truth
One more name, one more note
Let the whole street know

Palo y brass, palo y brass
We cry, then we pass
Palo y brass, palo y brass
Love don't ever die (hey)
Palo y brass, palo y brass
Lift your hands, let it rise
Palo y brass, palo y brass
Dance him home tonight

White gloves, slow parade
Beads hanging from the gate
A candle in the rain
Your uncle blows the horn
Your cousin wipes her face
Then she spins that skirt around
Like pain can learn to sway

From the hurt to the bend

We still sing through it
We still sing through it

We cry, then we pass
Palo y brass, palo y brass
Love don't ever die (hey)
Palo y brass, palo y brass
Lift your hands, let it rise
Palo y brass, palo y brass

songs.subject: a klezmer song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We patched the seam with tape
By the dock of painted ice
Your glove touched mine for luck
Then the hatch breathed twice
We laughed through cracked-up glass
Like old men at a feast
Now your window's smaller
And mine won't stay east

Turn your visor left
Say my name again
The tether sings thin
Like a wire in the rain

Two helmets, one star
Pulling far, pulling far
Two helmets, one star
We can't stay where we are
Hold on, hold on
Till the dark lets go
Two helmets, one star
And the gap won't close

Your coffee froze in midair
A brown bead on the cord
My lucky red scarf
Went spinning through the void
You took the east corridor
I drifted to the west
Same black sea around us
Still we failed the test

Turn your visor left
Say my name again
The tether sings thin
Like a wire in the rain

Two helmets, one star
Pulling far, pulling far
Two helmets, one star
We can't stay where we are
Hold on, hold on
Till the dark lets go
Two helmets, one star
And the gap won't close

In the little blue hour
Before the station wakes
I hear your laugh in static
I hear the metal shake
If I can't reach your hand now
Let my last light be
A stripe across your shadow
So you'll remember me

Two helmets, one star
Pulling far, pulling far
Two helmets, one star
We can't stay where we are
Hold on, hold on
Till the dark lets go
Two helmets, one star
And the gap won't close
