songs.subject: a klezmer song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
You took the right-hand tether,
I drifted to the left.
Two helmets in the dark-blue
and one thin breath we’ve kept.
Your glove against the porthole,
my thumb on scratched-up glass.
We waved like fools at Mars dust,
then watched each small light pass.

The station fell behind us,
a silver seed adrift.
I called your name in circles,
and space said, “Let it slip.”

Two comets apart, apart
(two comets apart)
You shine your way, I shine mine
(two comets apart)
Hold on, hold on
till the dark moves through
Two comets apart, apart
but I still spin toward you

Your radio came whisper-soft,
then broke into a hiss.
I counted every heartbeat
like beads I’d once had kissed.
We shared one peeled orange,
one song from back home town.
Now all my crumbs go floating
and all your stars go down.

The tether line is fraying,
it sings a skinny wire.
I lean into the silence
and dress it up as choir.

Two comets apart, apart
(two comets apart)
You shine your way, I shine mine
(two comets apart)
Hold on, hold on
till the dark moves through
Two comets apart, apart
but I still spin toward you

If I catch your name in static,
I’ll tuck it in my sleeve.
If you find my little signal,
don’t let the cold deceive.
We were never made for landing,
just circling the blue.
Still, every drifting mile away
is one more mile to you.

Two comets apart, apart
(two comets apart)
You shine your way, I shine mine
(two comets apart)
Hold on, hold on
till the dark moves through
Two comets apart, apart
but I still spin toward you
Two comets apart, apart
(two comets apart)
One sky for you, one sky for me

songs.subject: a swing song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We docked in the dark
Two helmets, one grin
You waved from the port
I waved back through tin
Same red planet below
Same radio sigh
But your glove found the latch
And mine reached the sky

Now the tether feels thin
Like a thread in a storm
Your heartbeat went east
Mine stayed too warm
I call, you delay
Then the silence begins
Two moons in a spin
On separate winds

Loose orbit, loose orbit
You drift, I drift too
Loose orbit, loose orbit
What else can we do?
(Loose orbit)
(Loose orbit)
We circle the loss
Like we still circle you

Your coffee went cold
In the cup by the hatch
My tool bag is empty
Your name on the patch
We traded our maps
For a crack in the glass
Now I count every burn
As the comets pass

You said, “stay on my line”
Then the line went slack
Your starboard side bright
Mine never came back
I reach for your hand
Through the gray interlink
But the whole ship leans left
When we both start to think

Loose orbit, loose orbit
You drift, I drift too
Loose orbit, loose orbit
What else can we do?
(Loose orbit)
(Loose orbit)
We circle the loss
Like we still circle you

Maybe that’s how it goes
When two hearts run cold
One burns for the future
One clings to the old
So I salute your light
As it slips from my view
If I can’t bring you home
I’ll still carry you

Loose orbit, loose orbit
You drift, I drift too
Loose orbit, loose orbit
What else can we do?
(Loose orbit)
(Loose orbit)
We circle the loss
Like we still circle you

songs.subject: a swing song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We shared one window
One scratched-up glass
You said “count the comets”
I said “they pass”
Your glove on my panel
My name on your wrist
Two tin can hearts
In a slow-grind drift

Now the tether’s singing
Thin as a wire
Your shadow slides
A little higher
I reach for your shoulder
You’re already gone
The spinning gets louder
And then you’re off

Drift apart, drift apart
Out past the mark
Drift apart, drift apart
Two sparks in the dark
Hold my line
Hold my line
Then let it fade
Drift apart, drift apart
That’s how we drifted away

Your coffee floated
In a silver bead
Mine hit the console
And turned to steam
We laughed at zero
Like kids in a storm
Now even your silence
Feels cold and warm

The Earth went blue
Then out of sight
You turned like a comet
In dead-black night
I called your call sign
You called mine back
But every little echo
Slid off the track

Drift apart, drift apart
Out past the mark
Drift apart, drift apart
Two sparks in the dark
Hold my line
Hold my line
Then let it fade
Drift apart, drift apart
That’s how we drifted away

If I could dock
One minute more
I’d press my helmet
To your door
But the stars don’t bargain
The miles don’t bend
We were built for floating
Not for the end

Drift apart, drift apart
Out past the mark
Drift apart, drift apart
Two sparks in the dark
Hold my line
Hold my line
Then let it fade
Drift apart, drift apart
That’s how we drifted away
Drift apart, drift apart
Still in my name
Drift apart, drift apart

songs.subject: a gothic country song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Dust devils danced on the cracked tar road
The general store, boarded up, a heavy load
Another year gone, same four walls, same tired sky
Just a whisper of a future, watchin' the good times die

But somethin' stirred deep, a flicker in the dark
A ghost of a notion, leavin' its mark

This dead-end block ain't gonna hold me
These rusty chains, they won't enfold me
I'm takin' the last turn home, even if it's unknown
Gonna break free from this dust and bone

Mama's tears, a river on her worn-out face
Said, "Son, this town's got a hold, it'll keep you in your place"
But I saw the mountains, dark against the fading light
A promise whispered, breakin' through the endless night

The old man's words, they echoed in my head
"You make your bed, son, lie in it 'til you're dead"
But some beds are coffins, long before the breath is gone
And I ain't ready for that final, mournful dawn

This dead-end block ain't gonna hold me
These rusty chains, they won't enfold me
I

songs.subject: a gothic country song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
I grew up where the screen door stuck
And the gas pump leaned like a drunk
Paint peeled off the corner store
Same two trucks, same locked front door
We counted months by the county fair
And bad news sat in every chair
Mamma said, "Boy, keep your head down low"
But that dead-end block just wouldn't let go

I watched the river take the rust away
I watched my good name rot where I lay
Then I found one crack in the black-top seam
And I heard my own heart say

I got out, I got out
From that dead-end block
I got out, I got out
With a prayer and a rock
(Dead-end block)
I got out, I got out
Now the ghosts can't talk
(They can't hold me)
I got out, I got out
From that dead-end block

Old man Tucker with his broken jaw
Taught me fear like a back-porch law
And the boys by the liquor sign
Laughed real mean when I crossed that line
I buried hurt in a feed sack bag
Kissed my fear like a stolen flag
Then I saved my breath and I saved my shoes
For the first long mile I had to choose

When the moon hung low like a bent-out nail
I heard that town hiss, "You won't prevail"
But I kept one flame in my coat-palm seam
And I fed it on a busted dream

I got out, I got out
From that dead-end block
I got out, I got out
With a prayer and a rock
(Dead-end block)
I got out, I got out
Now the ghosts can't talk
(They can't hold me)
I got out, I got out
From that dead-end block

If you see me on a backroad now
I ain't that kid with his head hung down
I still know the graves and the barbed-wire lines
But I don't live there anymore in my mind

I got out, I got out
From that dead-end block
I got out, I got out
With a prayer and a rock
(Dead-end block)
I got out, I got out
Now the ghosts can't talk
(They can't hold me)
I got out, I got out

songs.subject: a blues song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Got my girl beside me
Hands on ten and two
Sweat on my brow drippin'
What's a poor dad to do

She's got that learner's permit
Looks like trouble in her eyes

Steering wheel blues
Oh these steering wheel blues
Every stop sign a prayer
Every turn a surprise

Told her ease off the gas now
Feelin' like a passenger in a bad dream
She says "Dad I got this"
Then she stalls on the main street it seems

Remember my own father
White knuckled the whole ride
Now I'm him and she's me
Nowhere left to hide

Steering wheel

songs.subject: a blues song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
I put her on the driveway
At the edge of dawn
Hands on that black wheel
Radio crackled on
She said, "Daddy, I'm shaking"
I said, "Baby, that's okay"
We learned the word brake
One mile at a time that day

Foot on the pedal
Eyes on the line
Don't chase the mirror
Take your sweet time
I watched her jaw set
Like she meant every move
And the old Ford rolled
Like it had something to prove

Easy now, girl
Easy now, girl
Keep it straight, keep it slow
Easy now, girl
Easy now, girl
I'm right here, let you go (let you go)

We passed the cotton fields
Past the bait shop sign
She missed that first turn
Then she laughed and tried
I said, "No rush, darlin'
Let the road come near"
Her knuckles went white
Then she drove through the fear

Stop at the mailbox
Signal before you bend
Check that blind spot
Then ease it back again
That little engine moaned
Like it knew her name
And the whole wide world
Didn't look quite the same

Easy now, girl
Easy now, girl
Keep it straight, keep it slow
Easy now, girl
Easy now, girl
I'm right here, let you go (let you go)

At the county line
She said, "I got this, Dad"
I heard my own heartbeat
Soft and proud and sad
She parked that old truck
Clean between the posts
Now my baby drove
And I felt it most

Easy now, girl
Easy now, girl
Keep it straight, keep it slow
Easy now, girl
Easy now, girl
I'm right here, let you go (let you go)
Easy now, girl
Easy now, girl
You can take the wheel, I know (I know)

songs.subject: a gospel song about dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I came in late with my shoes all clean
White petals on the floor
You smiled at me by the table line
Like we’d met in a prayer before
You said, “Come on, don’t you stand so still”
And I felt my feet say yes
When the band hit that old good tune
I was wrapped in your joy and dress

The room got holy
The room got loud
Hands went up
And the whole place bowed
I took your hand
You took the lead
And something old
Got loose in me

Dancing with a stranger
Praise in my feet
Dancing with a stranger
Couldn’t stay in my seat
Oh, what a mercy
Oh, what a sight
We were moving like rejoicing
On a wedding night

Your laugh cut bright through the sugar and cake
Your spin was quick and kind
You said your mama taught you this step
And I said mine taught mine
The uncles clapped on the two and four
The aunties sang out low
And every face around that hall
Felt the good Lord start to flow

The room got holy
The room got loud
Hands went up
And the whole place bowed
I took your hand
You took the lead
And something old
Got loose in me

Dancing with a stranger
Praise in my feet
Dancing with a stranger
Couldn’t stay in my seat
Oh, what a mercy
Oh, what a sight
We were moving like rejoicing
On a wedding night

Maybe that’s how grace arrives
In a name you never knew
A hand you trust for one sweet song
A smile that carries you
We came in alone
We left as kin
With a blessing in the spin
With a blessing in the spin

Dancing with a stranger
Praise in my feet
Dancing with a stranger
Couldn’t stay in my seat
Oh, what a mercy
Oh, what a sight
We were moving like rejoicing
On a wedding night
Dancing with a stranger
(hallelujah)
Dancing with a stranger
(amazing grace)

In a name you never knew
A hand you trust for one sweet song
A smile that carries you
We came in alone
We left as kin
With a blessing in the spin
With a blessing in the spin

Dancing with a stranger
Praise in my feet
Dancing with a stranger
Couldn’t stay in my seat
Oh, what a mercy
Oh, what a sight
We were moving like rejoicing
On a wedding night
Dancing with a stranger
(hallelujah)
Dancing with a stranger
(amazing grace)

songs.subject: a gospel song about dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I came in late with my cousin
Wearing these shoes that pinch
You were laughing by the punch bowl
Like you knew one holy inch
The band was tuning up softly
The bride said “dance if you dare”
Then you held out your hand to me
And I found grace in the air

You said, “Don’t mind the floor now”
“Just follow the room”
My heart went quick
My feet went wild
Like Sunday in June

Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)
God put joy in the room
Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)
He can move through me and you

Your tie was loosened a little
My hair had come down loose
We stepped around the table
Near the cake with the blue sugar fruit
Every spin felt like laughter
Every laugh felt like prayer
I did not know your name yet
But I knew kindness was there

You said, “Keep your eyes up”
“Let the night unfold”
My hands were trembling
Then they weren’t
When the music took hold

Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)
God put joy in the room
Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)
He can move through me and you

And if I never see you again
Still I’ll bless that hall
For the way the Lord met me
Right past my fear and all
One hand opened another
And a burden fell down
I came to watch a wedding
Left with heaven on the ground

Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)
God put joy in the room
Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)
He can move through me and you
Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)
Everybody join in now
Dance with a stranger
Dance with a stranger (dance with a stranger)

songs.subject: a swing song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
My boots got mud on the heel
And a dent in the tin cup lid
Three years of dust in my coat
And the old town still knows what I did
I step off slow at the station
With a duffel and a letter that bends
Mama's porch lamp is burning
Like it never learned to end

I hear that screen door slap
I hear my name in the yard
I got a whole life back
And it hits me hard

I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
Hear that train wheel moan
I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
To the face I know
I traded mud for the front step
I traded night for dawn
I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
And I won't be gone

The screen door creaks like a fiddle
When my sister runs out in the rain
She says, "Boy, you look older"
Then she laughs through the crying again
Dad stands there with his cap in his hands
Like he can't find the right thing to say
So we lean in hard for a shoulder
And the silence starts to pray

I taste pie on the wind
I see the porch swing sway
I left a young man back then
But I'm here today

I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
Hear that train wheel moan
I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
To the face I know
I traded mud for the front step
I traded night for dawn
I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
And I won't be gone

Let the thunder roll behind me
Let the hard road fade away
I got a table set for supper
And a little room where I can stay
Maybe I flinch at the hinges
Maybe I wake to the past
But your arms are a kind of shelter
And this one is built to last

I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
Hear that train wheel moan
I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
To the face I know
I traded mud for the front step
I traded night for dawn
I'm coming home, coming home (home, home)
And I won't be gone

Mud on my boots
Love in the hall
I made it back
That's all, that's all

songs.subject: a swing song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
I came in on a gray train
Boots still caked with mud
Ma was in the doorway
With flour on her hands
The stew was on the stove
And the dog knew my name
I set my bag by the chair
Like I never left

I heard the screen door swing
I heard the spoons in the sink
Your voice said, "Sit right down"
And my heart took a drink

I'm home, I'm home
Right where I belong
I'm home, I'm home
After the long, long road
(I'm home, I'm home)
Your arms are the map
(I'm home, I'm home)
And I'm not looking back

The table had one extra plate
Like faith kept it warm
Dad passed me the pepper
And said, "You made it, son"
The radio was rattling
A tune from years ago
I laughed at how the floorboards
Still knew my step

I heard the screen door swing
I heard the spoons in the sink
Your voice said, "Sit right down"
And my heart took a drink

I'm home, I'm home
Right where I belong
I'm home, I'm home
After the long, long road
(I'm home, I'm home)
Your arms are the map
(I'm home, I'm home)
And I'm not looking back

There were names I had to carry
There were miles that bit my skin
But the porch light kept burning
So I found my way in
Now the kettle sings soft
And the night can fall
I've got this place around me
Like a promise made whole

I'm home, I'm home
Right where I belong
I'm home, I'm home
After the long, long road
(I'm home, I'm home)
Your arms are the map
(I'm home, I'm home)
And I'm not looking back

Set the bag by the chair
Let the night come down
I'm home, I'm home

songs.subject: a pop song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Early morning sun
Shines on my sharpest blade
Another day begun
Another story made

Oh I'm the barber man
With the scissors and the comb
I know the whole darn town
Every secret every home

Mrs Henderson's new dye
A whispered little fling
Young Jimmy's alibi
The gossip that they bring

Oh I'm the barber man
With the scissors and the comb
I know the whole darn town
Every secret every home

They sit in my old chair
They talk and

songs.subject: a pop song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
He knows your mama’s laugh
Your brother’s new tattoo
Knows who came back from Dallas
And who just lost a tooth
One stripe on the cape
He reads you like a page
You sit down with a story
He sends you out with grace

He says, “You need a little edge”
He says, “You need a little break”
A mirror full of neighbors
A thousand little names
And every comb gets lighter
When the gossip starts to swing
He looks up, grins slow
And everybody leans in

Barber knows the whole town
Whole town, whole town
From the courthouse steps
To the cracked-out bus stop sound
Barber knows the whole town
Whole town, whole town
He can fade your fears
And hear who’s moving out

Friday noon is packed
With caps and Sunday shirts
Old men trade their weather
Kids trade their homework words
He trims a birthday smile
He fixes up the wrong
Even in the silence
You can hear the town sing along

He says, “You need a little edge”
He says, “You need a little break”
A mirror full of neighbors
A thousand little names
And every comb gets lighter
When the gossip starts to swing
He looks up, grins slow
And everybody leans in

Barber knows the whole town
Whole town, whole town
From the courthouse steps
To the cracked-out bus stop sound
Barber knows the whole town
Whole town, whole town
He can fade your fears
And hear who’s moving out

When your world’s gone crooked
He squares you up again
One clean line, one sharp line
And the bad days cave in
He don’t need your headline
He knows the way you bend
By the time the cape comes off
You feel like yourself again

Barber knows the whole town
Whole town, whole town
From the courthouse steps
To the cracked-out bus stop sound
Barber knows the whole town
Whole town, whole town
He can fade your fears

songs.subject: a house song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I pass your building slow
Third floor, half-lit frame
Your plant still on the sill
That cracked brass door the same
The taxi meter blinks
Rain on the windshield blue
I know that balcony
Still knows the shape of you

My hands stay on the wheel
My heart tries to rewind
That hallway in my head
Keeps turning out of time

Past the old apartment
Past the old apartment
I keep on driving by
Past the old apartment
Past the old apartment
But you don’t get in the light

Corner deli spilling steam
Late shift kids on the stoop
A laundry bag by 4B
And your name in the loop
I saw your curtains move
Or maybe that was me
The block looks new enough
To hide what used to be

My hands stay on the wheel
My heart tries to rewind
That hallway in my head
Keeps turning out of time

Past the old apartment
Past the old apartment
I keep on driving by
Past the old apartment
Past the old apartment
But you don’t get in the light

One more lap around the block
One more story I won’t stop
Your shadow in the glass
Then it fades in parked car light
I don’t turn, I don’t turn
I just let it burn

Past the old apartment
Past the old apartment
I keep on driving by
Past the old apartment
Past the old apartment
But you don’t get in the light

Past the old apartment
(Old apartment)
Past the old apartment
(I keep on driving by)

songs.subject: a house song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I turned the corner slow
Past your old apartment glow
Same dent in the rail
Same mail slot, same pale paint
Your curtain still hangs wrong
Like a note that can’t move on
I kept both hands tight
On the wheel, on the night

And I should’ve kept straight
Should’ve kept my pace
But the window pulled me in
To that frozen place

Past your old apartment
Past your old apartment
I keep rolling by
Like I’m not even mine
Past your old apartment
Past your old apartment
That door, that floor, that light
Still knows my name at night

The takeout place was closed
Trash bags by the stoop in rows
A broken bike chain
Swinging in the wind like blame
I saw us in the glass
For a second, then it passed
Your laugh in my head
Like a song that’s stuck instead

And I should’ve kept straight
Should’ve kept my pace
But the window pulled me in
To that frozen place

Past your old apartment
Past your old apartment
I keep rolling by
Like I’m not even mine
Past your old apartment
Past your old apartment
That door, that floor, that light
Still knows my name at night

I don’t park
I don’t knock
I just circle the block
One more time
One more time
Let it fade, let it shine

Past your old apartment
Past your old apartment
I keep rolling by
Like I’m not even mine
Past your old apartment
Past your old apartment
That door, that floor, that light

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Each night the same green hill
Waits under my window
I lay down in the dark
And the grass starts to glow
A path of broken moon
Runs straight through the heather
Then your old black crows
Lift me into the weather

Up from the stone wall
Up past the yew tree
My feet leave the earth
And I go easy

I fly in my sleep
I fly, I fly
Hold on to the wind
Till the dawn cuts the sky
I fly in my sleep
I fly, I fly
When I wake, you're still there
In the corner of my eye

Over the harbor cranes
Past the salt on the windows
I skim the church spire
And the row of lone ships
Your voice down below
Calls my name through the weather
I dip my left wing low
And I circle you better

Up from the stone wall
Up past the yew tree
My feet leave the earth
And I go easy

I fly in my sleep
I fly, I fly
Hold on to the wind
Till the dawn cuts the sky
I fly in my sleep
I fly, I fly
When I wake, you're still there
In the corner of my eye

One day I asked the dream
Why it won't leave me
It said, "You were made
For more than the doorway"
So I wear that sky
Like a salt-stained collar
And I wait for the night
Like a child for water

I fly in my sleep
I fly, I fly
Hold on to the wind
Till the dawn cuts the sky
I fly in my sleep
I fly, I fly
When I wake, you're still there
In the corner of my eye

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
The mist rolls in from the sea
A familiar gray
And I’m standing on the cliff
Same as yesterday
Wind whips my hair
A cold
Sweet breath
Whispering promises
Defying death

Oh
The wings of old
They call to me
From the ancient sky
Wild and free
A feather falls
A silent plea
The wings of old
They call to me

My feet lift
Light as dust
From the crumbling stone
Above the gulls
Above the foam
I am not alone
The world below
A patchwork green and blue
A dream I know
Always new

Oh
The wings of old
They call to me
From the ancient sky
Wild and free
A feather falls
A silent plea
The wings of old
They call to me

I stretch my arms
A hollow ache inside
For feathers never grown
For a place to hide
Among the clouds
Where silence reigns
Escaping earthly chains

Oh
The wings of old

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I packed my shirt in a paper sack
Left my name on the porch out back
Mama cried, “Boy, don’t be long”
But this little town held me too strong
Bus stop dust on my shoes
Same old faces, same old views

Now the screen door swings like a tired goodbye
And my heart keeps beatin’ time
One more look at that water tower
Then I’m gone by the hour

I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(Ba-ba-ba-ba-ba)
I’m gone for good
Don’t wait on me
I’m gone for good
That road is mine
That road is mine
(Ba-ba-ba-ba-ba)
I’m gone for good
And I ain’t lookin’ back tonight

Old Mr. Kent at the corner store
Said “You’ll come home,” like he said before
But the hills don’t sing my song no more
I heard my life on the far side shore
Train smoke cut through the morning air
Like it knew me standing there

Now the screen door swings like a tired goodbye
And my heart keeps beatin’ time
One more look at that water tower

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning
My desk is a small island
Three open tabs
And your name in the corner
The page goes white
Then turns to dust again
I read the same line
Like it might read me back

My eyes feel heavy
But I stay right here
A pen in my hand
Like a tiny flare
The fan keeps turning
The room keeps its shape
I’m still awake
I’m still awake

Stay with me, paper
Stay with me, light
I’m all by myself
At two in the night
Stay with me, paper
Stay with me, light
I’m all by myself
At two in the night

Cold tea gone flat
Beside a stack of pages
My phone face-down
Like it knows I’m fragile
I trace the underlines
With my thumb and breath
Try to make room
For one more fact

My eyes feel heavy
But I stay right here
A pen in my hand
Like a tiny flare
The fan keeps turning
The room keeps its shape
I’m still awake
I’m still awake

Stay with me, paper
Stay with me, light
I’m all by myself
At two in the night
Stay with me, paper
Stay with me, light
I’m all by myself
At two in the night

If I lean too far
I can hear the floor
If I close my eyes
The whole room turns to foam
But the next small word
Finally comes through
And I hold it close
Like it’s holding me too

Stay with me, paper
Stay with me, light
I’m all by myself
At two in the night
Stay with me, paper
Stay with me, light
I’m all by myself
At two in the night

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning
Your desk lamp burns small
Highlighter dust on my sleeves
And the page says it all
My coffee went cold
By the edge of the sink
I keep reading the same line
Like I’m losing the ink

Outside, the room leans
In that slow, blue way
My phone face down
Like it knows to stay
I trace every word
Till it starts to glow
Hold on, hold on
I’m not letting go

Stay with me now
Stay with me now
Two in the morning
And I’m still here somehow
Stay with me now
Stay with me now
I’ll learn it all
If I keep my eyes on the line

Papers on the floor
Like snow from a storm
My notes in the margins
Keep me almost warm
Your name on a tab
That I never close
A tiny red mark
At the end of my rows

Outside, the room leans
In that slow, blue way
My phone face down
Like it knows to stay
I hear my own breath
Over rain on the pane
Hold on, hold on
Say it again

Stay with me now
Stay with me now
Two in the morning
And I’m still here somehow
Stay with me now
Stay with me now
I’ll learn it all
If I keep my eyes on the line

The stars are gone
But the lamp still stays
And every hard page
Turns soft in the haze
I’m half asleep
Still fighting for dawn
Just one more chapter
Then I’m not gone

Stay with me now
Stay with me now
Two in the morning
And I’m still here somehow
Stay with me now
Stay with me now
I’ll learn it all
If I keep my eyes on the line

Two in the morning
My world stays small
But I’m still turning

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I found your shirt in the drawer
Still folded like before
Your name on my screen
Like a wave on the floor
You left with the rain
Left me holding the frame
But I’m not that same boy
Who kept feeding the flame

I was mad in the dark
I was clutching the hurt
Kept your ghost in my hands
Like it still had a worth
Now I see what it cost
All the nights I was tossed
I can let it go now
Let it go, let it wash

I forgive you now
Even gone, I say your name
I forgive you now
Let the hurt fall off my face
You’re already gone
But I’m not staying there
I forgive you now
I forgive you now

Your last voicemail plays low
In the crack of the sink
I hear how you tremble
Then the silence in-between
I wrote all my anger
Then I tore it apart
'Cause poison can’t keep me
When I want a new heart

I was mad in the dark
I was clutching the hurt
Kept your ghost in my hands
Like it still had a worth
Now I see what it cost
All the nights I was tossed
I can let it go now
Let it go, let it wash

I forgive you now
Even gone, I say your name
I forgive you now
Let the hurt fall off my face
You’re already gone
But I’m not staying there
I forgive you now
I forgive you now

If you can hear me somewhere
I’m putting down the blame
I don’t need your apology
I don’t need the chain
I kept the wound open
Just to prove it was real
But peace feels stronger
Than the cut that I heal

I forgive you now
Even gone, I say your name
I forgive you now
Let the hurt fall off my face
You’re already gone
But I’m not staying there
I forgive you now
I forgive you now

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I found your note
in a blue tin box
stained with rain
and old café sugar
Your name still shines
on my cracked phone screen
I let it ring
then I let it fade

You’re already gone
across that line
but my hands still know
your shape at night
So I fold the hurt
like a shirt I wore
I don’t need it now
at my door

Go in peace, go in peace
I forgive you now
Go in peace, go in peace
I let you out
If love went cold
I warm my own hands
Go in peace, go in peace
I understand (I understand)

Your jacket’s gone
from the back of the chair
Only dust and sun
in the wooden grain
I used to keep
every little cut
like proof of us
like a lock and key
Now I wash the cup
and I leave it bare
I don’t chase your ghost
through the empty air

You’re already gone
past the last goodbye
but I hear your laugh
when the buses sigh
So I lay it down
on the floor, unclasped
what I couldn’t say
is slipping past

Go in peace, go in peace
I forgive you now
Go in peace, go in peace
I let you out
If love went cold
I warm my own hands
Go in peace, go in peace
I understand (I understand)

No more holding
the weight of your name
No more burning
for what won’t change
If you can hear me
wherever you are
I release you slow
from my heart (from my heart)

Go in peace, go in peace
I forgive you now
Go in peace, go in peace
I let you out
If love went cold
I warm my own hands
Go in peace, go in peace

songs.subject: a punk song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Keys in my fist
Gas on the floor
Tommy don't wait
Tommy wants more
Ten in the backseat
No names, no talk
Radio dead
We didn't stop

Hands on the wheel
Eyes on the glass
One bad turn
And it goes fast
I heard the sirens
Past the lot
Tommy said
"Drive what you got"

Last run, Tommy
Last run, Tommy (last run)
Burned out, baby
We don't come back
Last run, Tommy
Last run, Tommy (last run)
One more street
Then the whole thing snaps

He wore a grin
Like a busted tooth
Mapped every turn
Like it was truth
I saw the blood
On the seat seam
He said, "Don't look"
So I looked straight ahead

Fence on the left
Truck in the lane
One red light
And the heat came
He laughed too hard
At the wrong time
I felt my jaw
Go tight with the line

Last run, Tommy
Last run, Tommy (last run)
Burned out, baby
We don't come back
Last run, Tommy
Last run, Tommy (last run)
One more street
Then the whole thing snaps

I pulled to the curb
He didn't blink
Reached for the bag
Then he tipped and sank
My hands kept shaking
On the dash
I left with the keys
And a mouth full of ash

Last run, Tommy
Last run, Tommy (last run)
Burned out, baby
We don't come back
Last run, Tommy
Last run, Tommy (last run)
One more street
Then the whole thing snaps
Last run, Tommy
(last run)
Last run, Tommy
(and I was gone)

songs.lyrics:
Keys in my fist
Rain on the glass
One more block
Then I’m gone fast
Phone on mute
Seat tore wide
Your bag of cash
On the passenger side

No questions now
No names tonight
If the red and blue
Cut across my light

Last run, last run
Burn it and go
Last run, last run
No looking back, no
Last run, last run
I know the road
(last run) (last run)
I’m out of this hole

Corner store froze
When I hit the lane
Headlights split
Like a knife in rain
You said “drive”
I said “done”
Now the whole damn town
Hears the engine run

Hands on the wheel
Jaw locked tight
I can taste that smoke
At the edge of the night

Last run, last run
Burn it and go
Last run, last run
No looking back, no
Last run, last run
I know the road
(last run) (last run)
I’m out of this hole

Red light flash
My pulse like a drum
Tires squeal hard
Yeah, here they come
I ditch the map
I ditch the dream
One last turn
Then I break clean

Last run, last run
Burn it and go
Last run, last run
No looking back, no
Last run, last run
I know the road
(last run) (last run)
I’m out of this hole

Engine dies
Dawn in my teeth
One last job

songs.subject: a punk song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
My shoes know this road
Like the back of my hand
Same gas pump glow
Same dead-end plan
Every porch I pass
Feels like a dare
I’m sick of being
The kid who’s still there

Packin’ my name
In a busted-up bag
Got one good chance
And I’m never lookin’ back

I’m gone for good
Gone for good
This town can keep
Its cracked-up roots
I’m gone for good
Gone for good
(Yeah, I’m gone)
I’m gone for good

The diner clock lies
At the top of the wall
Every lost Friday
Feels too small
You said I’d come back
When the winter hits
But I learned my fire
Don’t live like this

Packin’ my name
In a busted-up bag
Got one good chance
And I’m never lookin’ back

I’m gone for good
Gone for good
This town can keep
Its cracked-up roots
I’m gone for good
Gone for good
(Yeah, I’m gone)
I’m gone for good

Hear that engine
Bite the night
I’m done with the drain
Done with the fight
No more fence lines
No more staying small
If I don’t leave now
I’ll never leave at all

I’m gone for good
Gone for good
This town can keep
Its cracked-up roots
I’m gone for good
Gone for good
(Yeah, I’m gone)
I’m gone for good

songs.lyrics:

Same road, same dead sign
Every porch knows my name
And I hate that line
Mom says, "Stay close"
Like close means safe
But the whole damn map
Feels like a locked gate

I packed one shirt
And a busted grin
No more counting cracks
In the house I'm in

I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
Say my name, then let it fade
I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
This town won't keep me caged (won't keep me caged)

Gas station dogs
And the Rusty Nail bar
One stoplight dream
With a broken guitar
Every Friday night
Ends in the same old talk
Who's getting out
Who's stuck in the block

I heard my chance
Call from outside
So I jumped that fence
With my teeth set wide

I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
Say my name, then let it fade
I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
This town won't keep me caged (won't keep me caged)

Take your chain-link
Take your weathered rules
Take the gossip dogs
And the tired old schools
I learned enough
From the dust and rust
Now I want new ground
I want more than dust

I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
Say my name, then let it fade
I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
This town won't keep me caged (won't keep me caged)

If I look back
I might burn
So I keep on
At the border turn
I'm gone for good
songs.lyrics_en:
My shoes hit the mud
Same road, same dead sign
Every porch knows my name
And I hate that line
Mom says, "Stay close"
Like close means safe
But the whole damn map
Feels like a locked gate

I packed one shirt
And a busted grin
No more counting cracks
In the house I'm in

I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
Say my name, then let it fade
I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
This town won't keep me caged (won't keep me caged)

Gas station dogs
And the Rusty Nail bar
One stoplight dream
With a broken guitar
Every Friday night
Ends in the same old talk
Who's getting out
Who's stuck in the block

I heard my chance
Call from outside
So I jumped that fence
With my teeth set wide

I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
Say my name, then let it fade
I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
This town won't keep me caged (won't keep me caged)

Take your chain-link
Take your weathered rules
Take the gossip dogs
And the tired old schools
I learned enough
From the dust and rust
Now I want new ground
I want more than dust

I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
Say my name, then let it fade
I'm gone for good
I'm gone for good
This town won't keep me caged (won't keep me caged)

If I look back
I might burn
So I keep on
At the border turn
I'm gone for good

songs.subject: a hip hop song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Packed jars in the trunk, lid to lid
Back road bending where the corn rows slid
Kept my eyes on the curve, hands white-knuckled tight
Moon on the hood and the dash all night
Old Ford coughing, but she still could run
Two towns back and I was almost done
Then that blue light flicked like a blade in the dark
My stomach dropped hard when it lit up the park

I said, hold that breath
Keep your face still
But the gravel sang loud
And the tires lost will
One wrong move
One flash, one call
Then the whole road shook
And I knew I’d fall

Run went wrong, run went wrong
Moonshine run went wrong
Run went wrong, run went wrong
Now the whole night gone
I was one turn short
One step too slow
Run went wrong, run went wrong
And the sirens know

I bailed by the ditch, boots in the mud
Jars in the dark like a red-bellied flood
One cracked open, sweet smell hit the air
A whole year’s work just hanging there
Heard my dog bark from a yard up the lane
Heard a door slam, then the count-up began
Deputy called out, “Boy, don’t make me chase”
I left my pride on that country place
Ran through the pines with a lantern beam
Heart in my throat and my lungs on steam
Found my keys in the weeds, found my hat in the grass
But I couldn’t outrun what I’d done in the past

I said, hold that breath
Keep your face still
But the gravel sang loud
And the tires lost will
One wrong move
One flash, one call
Then the whole road shook
And I knew I’d fall

Run went wrong, run went wrong
Moonshine run went wrong
Run went wrong, run went wrong
Now the whole night gone
I was one turn short
One step too slow
Run went wrong, run went wrong
And the sirens know

By dawn I was wet and cold at the edge of town
Watching smoke rise soft where the old truck sat down
Mama said, “Baby, that road don’t love you back”
I laughed through the shame, but I felt that crack
One bad choice can hollow out a man
Leave him talking to the dirt with an empty hand
Now I keep my head low when the moon hangs high
'Cause I learned the hard way on that blacktop drive

Run went wrong, run went wrong
Moonshine run went wrong
Run went wrong, run went wrong
Now the whole night gone
I was one turn short
One step too slow
Run went wrong, run went wrong
And the sirens know

songs.subject: a hip hop song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Old Ford coughing on the gravel
Jar seat-belted in a towel
Daddy said, “Drive slow, son”
I said, “I know how”
Headlamps cut through the pines
Dogs barking off the fence
Metal lid under my elbow
Ten pounds of bad sense
County road, one bridge out
So I took the creek line
Mud on the whitewall tires
Heartbeat on a hard climb
Radio low, windows fogged
Moon hanging like a threat
Thought I had a clean little run
Thought I owed them nothin’ yet

Then the red flash hit
Like a verdict in the night
Hands went cold on the wheel
I knew I lost that fight
Sirens woke the holler
Every lie came loose
Bottle in the ditch now
Running with the juice

Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
(Bad road, bad luck)
Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
(Backseat full of truth)
One blue light
Then two
Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
And I’m still stuck on you

Took the curve too wide
Saw the mailbox lean
Deputy by the feed store
With a grin too clean
I ditched it by the washout
Tried to sprint through cane
Boots kept finding water
Mud kept saying my name
Then the jar blew open
Sweet smoke in the air
Little glass moon landing
Shattered everywhere
He said, “Son, you smell that?”
I said, “Ain’t no need”
He cuffed me by the tailgate
While my granddad watched the weeds

Then the red flash hit
Like a verdict in the night
Hands went cold on the wheel
I knew I lost that fight
Sirens woke the holler
Every lie came loose
Bottle in the ditch now
Running with the juice

Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
(Bad road, bad luck)
Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
(Backseat full of truth)
One blue light
Then two
Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
And I’m still stuck on you

Mama left a note on the counter
Said, “Boy, you bring it back”
But all I brought was gravel
And a date with the track
Now I sit with that fine print
And the rain on my name
One bad night in the county
Turned the whole thing lame

Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
(Bad road, bad luck)
Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong
(Backseat full of truth)
One blue light
Then two
Run gone wrong
Run gone wrong

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sun on the folded chairs
Coffee in a paper cup
Your black dress on the back of the door
Still hanging like a thought
The flowers lean in the heat
Their water turning sweet
I keep hearing your name in the hall
Like it won't let me sleep

And I don't know what to say
When the house gets this quiet
Your mama stares at the floor
Like she's trying to deny it
So I hold my breath
Count the steps to the gate
One more day in the same clothes
One more heartache

Morning after, morning after
Hold me through the morning after
We were strong, then we shattered
Now we dance through the morning after
(oh-oh)
Morning after, morning after
Your name still lives in my laughter
Even now, even after
We still rise in the morning after

The plates are stacked by the sink
Half the cake gone stale
Your brother smoking by the curb
With his eyes set pale
The priest said mercy and peace
But peace took the back road
So we drove home real slow
With the windows closed

And the sky looked washed out blue
Like it cried before sunrise
I wanted to call your phone
Just to hear you reply
But all I got was the wind
Pressing on the glass
And that same old memory
That refuses to pass

Morning after, morning after
Hold me through the morning after
We were strong, then we shattered
Now we dance through the morning after
(oh-oh)
Morning after, morning after
Your name still lives in my laughter
Even now, even after
We still rise in the morning after

Let the tears hit the tile
Let the sorrow speak
I carried your last hello
Like a secret in my sleeve
If love is a doorway
Then grief is the key
I lost you in one night
But you still live in me

Morning after, morning after
Hold me through the morning after
We were strong, then we shattered
Now we dance through the morning after
(oh-oh)
Morning after, morning after
Your name still lives in my laughter
Even now, even after
We still rise in the morning after

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise creeping through the blinds
Head is pounding from cheap wines
Last night’s tears still on my face
This empty house a lonely space

Another day another ache
For goodness sake

Ashes to ashes dust to dust
Party’s over now it's just us
Memories flashing bright and bold
A story that can’t be told
Ashes to ashes dust to dust

Coffee brewing bitter strong
Where did everything go wrong
Used to laugh and dance and sing
Now just silence everything
What a sting

They say time heals all the wounds
But these heavy lonely tunes
They just play on and on and on
Since you been gone

Ashes to ashes dust to dust

Used to laugh and dance and sing
Now just silence everything
What a sting

They say time heals all the wounds
But these heavy lonely tunes
They just play on and on and on
Since you been gone

songs.subject: a klezmer song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
I grew up where the curb cracks wide
By the corner store and the rusted sign
Same gray steps and the busted gate
Every kid on the block learned wait, wait, wait
Mama said, “Boy, keep your shoes clean”
Papa said, “Don’t let this street own me”
I watched that bus take the world away
Then I built my hope from a workday face

One foot on the stoop
One eye on the road
I heard my name in the brass
I heard my bones say, go

Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
I’m dancing my way through
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
No more same-old view
Out of the dead-end block

The laundry line swung like a flag in rain
Tin-roof thunder and a windowpane
Kids on the stairs with a bottle cap grin
We made a whole parade from the life we’re in
I took the hard jobs, took the late-night shifts
Carried my dreams in a lunch pail grip
Every slammed door tried to teach me fear
But I kept my best shoes pointed here

One foot on the stoop
One eye on the road
I heard my name in the brass
I heard my bones say, go

Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
I’m dancing my way through
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
No more same-old view
Out of the dead-end block

Now I come back with a shine on my coat
Same broken sidewalk, new kind of hope
I kiss the old brick, I bless that lane
It tried to hold me down, but it couldn’t keep my name

Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
I’m dancing my way through
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
Hear that band cut through
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
Out of the dead-end block
I found my road, I did
Out of the dead-end block

songs.subject: a klezmer song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hum a sad old tune
Another night beneath the moon
Cracked pavement my only view
Same old story nothing new
Got that feeling deep inside
Gotta run gotta hide

This ain't no place for dreams to grow
Time to pack my bags you know

I'm a concrete bloom yeah I'm breaking through
Leaving this dead-end block for something new
Concrete bloom watch me rise
Under different skies

Every corner every wall
Heard the whispers heard them all
Said I'd never make it far
Just another fading star
But my heart beats a wilder beat
Gonna dance right off this street

My spirit yearns my soul takes flight
Stepping into morning light

I'm a concrete bloom yeah I'm breaking through
Leaving this dead-end block for something new
Concrete bloom watch me rise

Concrete bloom watch me rise

Every corner every wall

My spirit yearns my soul takes flight

I'm a concrete bloom yeah I'm breaking through

songs.subject: a synthwave song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the crosswalk, cold and thin
Your sleeve touched mine in the bookstore wind
One black umbrella, bent at the rim
We laughed like we both just stepped right in

No name yet, just your hand
Warm on the metal stand
The corner light turned gold
And the whole block slowed

One umbrella, two strangers
One small space, two heartbeats
Side by side in the water
You and I, just passing
One umbrella, two strangers
Still we matched every step
(One umbrella)
Till the rain forgot us

You had a ticket folded in your coat
I had a receipt and a broken phone
Your shoes made dents in the silver street
Mine kept time in the puddles beneath

You said "keep it, take the lead"
I said "you can walk with me"
The night leaned close and low
Like it knew our names would grow

One umbrella, two strangers
One small space, two heartbeats
Side by side in the water
You and I, just passing
One umbrella, two strangers
Still we matched every step
(One umbrella)
Till the rain forgot us

At the corner, you almost left
Then you turned back and laughed
Your fingers brushed my wrist
Like a spark in the dark
No promise, no map, no plan
Just the pull of your hand
And the rain kept falling down
While the world spun around

One umbrella, two strangers
One small space, two heartbeats
Side by side in the water
You and I, just passing
One umbrella, two strangers
Still we matched every step
(One umbrella)
Till the rain forgot us
(One umbrella)
Till the rain forgot us

songs.subject: a synthwave song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the platform
You held the corner
I held the handle
Like we knew the drill
Your coat was soaking
Mine was barely dry
Two strangers leaning
Toward the same small sky

One step left
Then one step right
We matched the rhythm
Under the streetlamp light
You laughed like
We’d done this before
The rain kept tapping
At the station door

One umbrella
One small circle
We keep it close, keep it close
One umbrella
Two slow strangers
You move in, I don’t let go
(one umbrella)

Your bag hit my elbow
The wind turned sharp
A paper ticket
Stuck to your arm
We both said sorry
In the same breath
Then looked at each other
Like that meant yes

One step left
Then one step right
We matched the rhythm
Under the streetlamp light
You laughed like
We’d done this before
The rain kept tapping
At the station door

One umbrella
One small circle
We keep it close, keep it close
One umbrella
Two slow strangers
You move in, I don’t let go
(one umbrella)

At the corner crosswalk
You pointed west
Said your stop was farther
Than you expected
I said “take it”
You said “walk with me”
So the night got smaller
And the rain backed off

One umbrella
One small circle
We keep it close, keep it close
One umbrella
Two slow strangers
Now you’re beside me
And I won’t let go
(one umbrella)
One umbrella
One small circle
We keep it close, keep it close

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You save me a seat
By the window glass
You laugh like it’s easy
And I let it pass
Your hand on the cup
Mine shaking a bit
I know where I stand
Too near, not in it

I keep my smile
Folded neat
Like a note
I’ll never send to you

I’m here
You’re there
Same small room
Different air
I’m here
You’re there
And you don’t know
I care

You talk about someone
With a soft little glow
I nod at the table
While my heart says go
The spoon makes a circle
The night drifts slow
I almost confess it

I’m here

I care

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I saved you a seat
By the window glass
Your name on my cup
Let the hours pass
You laugh like rain
On a tin roof dome
I keep my heart
Where you don't come home

You turn your head
Like I’m not there
And I pretend
I do not care

Meu coração
Still calls your name
Meu coração
You feel the same? (you feel the same?)
I wait in vain
But I still stay
Meu coração
Won’t look away

Your scarf on the chair
Mine on the floor
You borrow my light
Then ask for more
I read your eyes
Like a little clue
Every soft maybe
Means nothing new

You smile, then slip
Right through my hands
I learn your silence
The best I can

Meu coração
Still calls your name
Meu coração
You feel the same? (you feel the same?)
I wait in vain
But I still stay
Meu coração
Won’t look away

If love’s a wave
I’m the shore
You touch me once
Then ask for more
So I fold my wish
Into the night
And keep your shadow
Holding me tight

Meu coração
Still calls your name
Meu coração
You feel the same? (you feel the same?)
I wait in vain
But I still stay
Meu coração
Won’t look away

I know the truth
It’s plain to see
You are not mine

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
I put my hand on the wheel
You said, "Daddy, is this real?"
Your sneakers on the brake
Your grin had me awake
I said, "Easy on the gas"
You said, "Tell me when to pass"
You backed out slow and proud
The whole block felt too loud

Ooh, little steady now
Ooh, you're learning how
Eyes up, hands soft
We got this, we got this

Roll, roll, baby, roll
(roll, roll, baby, roll)
Keep your cool and take control
(keep your cool and take control)
Turn that wheel, don't let go
(turn that wheel, don't let go)
Daddy's right here, moving slow
(daddy's right here, moving slow)

At the stop sign, you froze
Then you laughed and let it go
Your fingers found the lane
Like they always knew the way
I saw your mama's face
In the mirror by your brace
Same brave look, same spark
Shining through the park

Ooh, little steady now
Ooh, you're learning how
Eyes up, hands soft
We got this, we got this

Roll, roll, baby, roll
(roll, roll, baby, roll)
Keep your cool and take control
(keep your cool and take control)
Turn that wheel, don't let go
(turn that wheel, don't let go)
Daddy's right here, moving slow
(daddy's right here, moving slow)

When the world feels wide
I'll be by your side
One more block, one more try
Then you touch the sky

Roll, roll, baby, roll
(roll, roll, baby, roll)
Keep your cool and take control
(keep your cool and take control)
Turn that wheel, don't let go
(turn that wheel, don't let go)
Daddy's right here, moving slow
(daddy's right here, moving slow)

Ooh, little steady now
Ooh, you're learning how
Roll, roll, baby, roll
Roll, roll, baby, roll

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
In the blue seat, hands on ten
You said, “Can I try it again?”
I buckled up, tried not to grin
Your ponytail bouncing in the wind
You checked the mirror, bit your lip
Tiny hands on the wheel, one slip
I said, “Easy now, baby, slow”
You said, “Dad, I already know”

Ooh, the curb looked high
Ooh, but you held that line
Heart beatin’ in the dash light glow
One more breath and off we go

Drive, baby, drive
Keep it straight and alive
Drive, baby, drive
I’m right here by your side
Ooh, ooh, ooh
Little by little, you learn
Ooh, ooh, ooh
And my whole world turns

At the stop sign, you came to rest
Gave the horn a tiny test
I laughed so hard, you rolled your eyes
Then you nailed that left on time
Past the market, past the park
Sunset folding soft and dark
You said, “See? I’m getting there”
I said, “Yeah, kid, and then some more”

Ooh, the road felt wide
Ooh, with you beside
Hands were shaking, but you stayed slow
One more breath and off we go

Drive, baby, drive
Keep it straight and alive
Drive, baby, drive
I’m right here by your side
Ooh, ooh, ooh
Little by little, you learn
Ooh, ooh, ooh
And my whole world turns

One day you’ll roll away
On your own to chase your day
But tonight, in this borrowed space
You’re still my girl in the driver’s seat place

Drive, baby, drive
Keep it straight and alive
Drive, baby, drive
I’m right here by your side
Ooh, ooh, ooh
Little by little, you learn
Ooh, ooh, ooh

songs.subject: a punk song about a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
He sets up on the bench
By the cracked-up chessboard
Faded cap, quick grin
Two queens in his sleeve
He says, "Five minutes, kid"
Then he taps the clock
You lose one pawn
And the whole block laughs

He moves like a thief
Hands too clean
You think you're ahead
Then you hear the scream

Parkside gambit!
He takes your king
Parkside gambit!
You brought the ring
Parkside gambit!
Cold little grin
Parkside gambit!
Play again

Old men crowd around
Like it’s gospel time
A kid tosses cash
He’s already resigned
Knight to the corner
Snap, your rook is gone
He drinks from a can
And keeps talking on

He moves like a thief
Hands too clean
You think you're ahead
Then you hear the scream

Parkside gambit!
He takes your king
Parkside gambit!
You brought the ring
Parkside gambit!
Cold little grin
Parkside gambit!
Play again

He smiles when you sweat
He loves the choke
One bad move
And your pride is broke
"House rules, kid"
He says with a wink
Then the board goes wild
And you miss the link

Parkside gambit!
He takes your king
Parkside gambit!
You brought the ring
Parkside gambit!
Cold little grin
Parkside gambit!
Play again
Parkside gambit!
Give him your best
Parkside gambit!
He beats the rest

songs.subject: a punk song about a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Under the elm by the bench
He sets the board like a trap
Gold tooth grin, worn coat
Pocket full of cheap match books
He says, “Kid, take white”
Then he slides me the pawn
(“Make it quick”)
His eyes never leave my hands

Three coins down
One move too slow
The old men laugh
Then go quiet low
He taps the clock
Like he knows

Park king, park king
He takes your queen
Park king, park king
He makes you bleed
One bad move
And you’re gone
Park king, park king
Don’t play long
(park king)

Dog-walker circling slow
Kids on swings yelling “check”
He smokes my nerves for free
With a split lip, clean trick
His bishop cuts my line
Like a blade through cloth
(too late)
He leans in close and says
“Son, you were lost at the start”

Three coins down
One move too slow
The old men laugh
Then go quiet low
He taps the clock
Like he knows

Park king, park king
He takes your queen
Park king, park king
He makes you bleed
One bad move
And you’re gone
Park king, park king
Don’t play long
(park king)

He leaves the board
With my money and my pride
A crumb of bread in his palm
And the whole park on his side
He waves one finger
Like a saint or a thief
Then he’s gone past the swings
And I’m left with the grief
(yeah, yeah)

Park king, park king
He takes your queen
Park king, park king
He makes you bleed
One bad move
And you’re gone
Park king, park king
Don’t play long

songs.subject: a R&B song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I kept your coat by the door
Though you never came back for it
Dust on the sleeve, your old cologne
Still alive in the closet light
I learned your silence by heart
Every crack in the ceiling knew
How I held that hurt in my hands
Like a glass I refused to lose

But time kept moving
And my hands got tired
I was carrying flames
You were already gone

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Let the ash fall down
Let it leave me now
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You can't hear it here
But my heart hears loud

Your name on my phone stayed bright
Like a match in the dark for years
I would press that screen and pray
For a voice that would never come
Mama said, "Baby, let go"
But I needed my rage to breathe
Still the anger turned thin in me
Like rain on a window pane

And the days kept shifting
Like train cars passing through
I was stuck at the station
You were already gone

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Let the ash fall down
Let it leave me now
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You can't hear it here
But my heart hears loud

If grief is a room
I have walked every wall
If love is a wound
I have touched it all
I won't call it right
I won't call it fate
I just won't carry
What the dark once made

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Let the ash fall down
Let it leave me now
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
You can't hear it here
But my heart hears loud
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
And I set you free
As I let me down

songs.subject: a R&B song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I kept your coat by the door
Though you left that winter before
Your name on my screen still glows
Like a text I can't let go
The plates still sit for two
One cracked cup, one for you
I tell the room your jokes
Then laugh alone and choke

I was angry for so long
At the way you moved along
Now the edge is wearing thin
And I let the hurt come in

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Even gone, somehow
I lay it down
I forgive you now
Let the ache burn out
You can't hear me now
But I forgive you now

Your shoes still by the wall
Dust on the picture frame and all
I used to stare at your last goodbye
Like a door that never closed right
I blamed the sky, blamed time
Blamed every reason I could find
But the truth came soft and slow
Some wounds just learn to go

I was angry for so long
At the way you moved along
Now the edge is wearing thin
And I let the hurt come in

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Even gone, somehow
I lay it down
I forgive you now
Let the ache burn out
You can't hear me now
But I forgive you now

If heaven kept score, let it fade
Let the hard words be unmade
I won't carry all that weight
Not another empty day
So I light a little grace
Where your shadow used to stay
And I breathe where we broke through
Forgiving me, forgiving you

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Even gone, somehow
I lay it down
I forgive you now
Let the ache burn out
You can't hear me now

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Her apron hung by the door
Powdered with flour and light
Pots on the stove like old church bells
Steaming through the noon-day bright
I’d come in slow from the yard
And that first breath would stay

On the windows, on my sleeves
On the tip of my tongue

That kitchen smell
That kitchen smell
Beans and sage in the air
That kitchen smell
That kitchen smell
Grandma’s right there

Cans lined up by the sink
Labels half gone and worn
A biscuit tin with a dent in the lid
And a threadbare towel on the arm
She’d stir with a spoon of wood
Like she knew my whole day

And the skillet sang low
As the sun turned gold

That kitchen smell
That kitchen smell
Beans and sage in the air
That kitchen smell
That kitchen smell
Grandma’s right there

Now the house stands quiet
But I can still walk through
One breath and I’m back at the table
With a plate of hot cornbread too

That kitchen smell
That kitchen smell
Beans and sage in the air
That kitchen smell
That kitchen smell
Grandma’s right there

Grandma’s right there
Grandma’s right there

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
When I push that screen door slow
I catch flour in the air
On the stove, a pot starts humming
Like she knew we'd all be there
Pepper on the cutting board
And bacon in a cast-iron pan
That kitchen held the whole world
In a dish towel on her hand

Sweet tea on the counter
Supper on the heat
I was small enough to vanish
In the comfort of that heat

Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Smells like home to me
Brown bread, beans, and butter
Poured out wide and free
Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Call me back again
(Grandma's kitchen)
To that door, to that table
To her love and her hands

There was onion in the skillet
And a pie cooling by the sink
Fresh yeast riding through the rafters
Making my whole heart think
Of Sunday hats and barefoot feet
Of her laugh out in the yard
And the way one simple supper
Could make a hard week soft

Apron strings and old glass jars
Sun on the linoleum floor
Every little thing she touched
Made me hungry for more

Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Smells like home to me
Brown bread, beans, and butter
Poured out wide and free
Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Call me back again
(Grandma's kitchen)
To that door, to that table
To her love and her hands

Now the house is quiet
But I still know the way
To the place where joy was rising
In a skillet every day
If I close my eyes real steady
I can almost hear her say
"Child, come eat while it's still warm"
And the hurt falls away

Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Smells like home to me
Brown bread, beans, and butter
Poured out wide and free
Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Call me back again
(Grandma's kitchen)
To that door, to that table
songs.lyrics_en:
When I push that screen door slow
I catch flour in the air
On the stove, a pot starts humming
Like she knew we'd all be there
Pepper on the cutting board
And bacon in a cast-iron pan
That kitchen held the whole world
In a dish towel on her hand

Sweet tea on the counter
Supper on the heat
I was small enough to vanish
In the comfort of that heat

Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Smells like home to me
Brown bread, beans, and butter
Poured out wide and free
Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Call me back again
(Grandma's kitchen)
To that door, to that table
To her love and her hands

There was onion in the skillet
And a pie cooling by the sink
Fresh yeast riding through the rafters
Making my whole heart think
Of Sunday hats and barefoot feet
Of her laugh out in the yard
And the way one simple supper
Could make a hard week soft

Apron strings and old glass jars
Sun on the linoleum floor
Every little thing she touched
Made me hungry for more

Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Smells like home to me
Brown bread, beans, and butter
Poured out wide and free
Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Call me back again
(Grandma's kitchen)
To that door, to that table
To her love and her hands

Now the house is quiet
But I still know the way
To the place where joy was rising
In a skillet every day
If I close my eyes real steady
I can almost hear her say
"Child, come eat while it's still warm"
And the hurt falls away

Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Smells like home to me
Brown bread, beans, and butter
Poured out wide and free
Smells like Grandma's kitchen
Call me back again
(Grandma's kitchen)
To that door, to that table

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise paints the harbor gold
Got my little bag and stories old
Leaving home a heavy cost
Praying not to get lost

Wind whips salty spray
Hope it guides me on my way

Ocean blue goodbye
Tears in my eye
New world calling me
Wild and free

Wooden ship a creaking sound
No familiar face around
Just the waves and sky so vast
Hoping this new life will last

Remember Mama's gentle hand
Told me I'd find a promised land
Papa's words
songs.lyrics_en:
Sunrise paints the harbor gold

Leaving home a heavy cost
Praying not to get lost

Wind whips salty spray
Hope it guides me on my way

Ocean blue goodbye
Tears in my eye
New world calling me
Wild and free

Wooden ship a creaking sound
No familiar face around
Just the waves and sky so vast
Hoping this new life will last

Remember Mama's gentle hand
Told me I'd find a promised land
Papa's words

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I left my name in Cork
Folded in my coat
Mama tied a white cloth
Around the little boat
The dock was full of hands
Waving in the rain
I kissed that cold old harbor
And I never was the same

The gulls were screaming north
The rope burned through my palm
I watched the shore grow smaller
But I kept my eyes on calm

Across the sea, across the sea
I carried you in me
Across the sea, across the sea
With salt on my boots and a prayer to be free
(Oh, across the sea)
(Oh, across the sea)

Three nights in the dark
With the boards all groaned and shook
I held a tin of buttons
And your Bible in my book
A boy on the bunk below me
Fevered through the storm
We sang to keep from sinking
Till the ragged dawn was born

The waves kept punching hard
Like they knew my secret name
Still I held to that horizon
And I answered it the same

Across the sea, across the sea
I carried you in me
Across the sea, across the sea
With salt on my boots and a prayer to be free
(Oh, across the sea)
(Oh, across the sea)

Now I work these hard new fields
With blisters in my hand
I learned the shape of hunger
In a stranger's promised land
But when the wind turns north
I hear that harbor ring
And I see your white cloth waving
Like the first day of spring

Across the sea, across the sea
I carried you in me
Across the sea, across the sea
With salt on my boots and a prayer to be free
Across the sea, across the sea
I found what I had to be
(Oh, across the sea)

songs.subject: a house song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blur to a neon smear
Another Friday night I'm still right here
Empty room echoes loud you see
Just the ghost of you dancing with me

Memories spin like a broken record
My heart's a beat I can't quite master

You're a ghost in my stereo
A frequency I can't let go
Yeah a ghost in my stereo
I'm forgiving you letting it flow

Thought I'd buried it deep and far
Underneath a silent star
But your laugh still rings in this lonely space
A phantom smile on a faded face

The rhythm pounds a familiar ache
For goodness sake

You're a ghost in my stereo
A frequency I can't let go
Yeah a ghost in my stereo
I'm forgiving you letting it flow

Empty room echoes loud you see
Just the ghost of you dancing with me

Yeah a ghost in my stereo
I'm forgiving you letting it flow

Thought I'd buried it deep and far
Underneath a silent star
But your laugh still rings in this lonely space
A phantom smile on a faded face

The rhythm pounds a familiar ache
For goodness sake

You're a ghost in my stereo
A frequency I can't let go
Yeah a ghost in my stereo
I'm forgiving you letting it flow

songs.subject: a house song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
You left your cup by the sink
I still wash it by hand
Little blue ring on the rim
Like you might come back again
I said the hard thing first
Then the door took your name

Now the room keeps still
And I finally hear it
What you carried home
What I never held
If love is a lock
Then I’ve got the key

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Gone too far, too fast
Still, I let it pass
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Take my anger with you
I can set it down

Your shoes by the wall
Still pointed to the hall
Mug on the top shelf
Dust on the photo frame
I kept every receipt
Every small little ache

But the night can’t bite
What the morning softens
I was holding smoke
In a closed-up fist
If love is a door
Then I’m letting it swing

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Gone too far, too fast
Still, I let it pass
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Take my anger with you
I can set it down

If there’s a room beyond
I hope it’s kind
On the other side
I’m not keeping score
I’m not keeping stone
I’m loosening my grip
Letting you go home

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Gone too far, too fast
Still, I let it pass
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Take my anger with you
I can set it down

Set it down
Set it down
I forgive you now

songs.subject: a indie rock song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Cardboard dust on my jeans
One last lamp by the door
You wrote the new address
On the inside of the drawer
I fold the flaps down slow
Like I’m closing a bad year
My hand shakes on the tape roll
But I’m still standing here

Got the old place echoing
Every room says your name
Keys in the bowl
Same dull flame

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
I’m out of this house
With my heart in a lock
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One clean strip and I’m gone
Like I meant what I thought

The sink is bare and white
The fridge hum stopped at noon
There’s a dent in the wall
From that chair we moved too soon
I find a sock behind the couch
And laugh like it hurts
You took the mirror, I took the books
And the quiet in my shirt

Got the old place echoing
Every room says your name
Keys in the bowl
Same dull flame

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
I’m out of this house
With my heart in a lock
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One clean strip and I’m gone
Like I meant what I thought

At the curb, the rain starts in
Soft on the broken lawn
I lift that box to my chest
Like a letter left too long
The tape bites tight
Then lets me breathe
I watch that last shape vanish
And it takes a piece of me

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
I’m out of this house
With my heart in a lock
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One clean strip and I’m gone
Like I meant what I thought

One last box
One last box
Tape and let go
Tape and let go

songs.subject: a indie rock song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The fridge is gone
The wall looks pale
A thumbprint map
Where the frames hung stale
One last box
By the doorframe crack
You press the tape
And don’t look back

Your hands know the shape
Better than your heart
Fold the flaps down slow
Like a kid at art

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Take what you need now
Leave what you lost
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One hard pull
Then the room is yours

Loose change in a bowl
A mug with a chip
The plant on the sill
Still trying to live
You find that note
In a drawer with a key
Half of your name
Still belongs to me

The hallway waits
With a tired glow
You hear the truck
And the neighbors go

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Take what you need now
Leave what you lost
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One hard pull
Then the room is yours

The tape makes a sound
Like a zipper in the dark
Like a clean little wound
Right across the heart
So carry it out
Through the stairwell air
No grand goodbye
Just a box and a stare

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Take what you need now
Leave what you lost
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One hard pull
Then the room is yours
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
The porch light fades

songs.subject: a punk song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Years gone by
Dead air static
What to say
A hollow laugh
She’ll just sigh
Or worse react
My old path

Nerves shot
Hand shakes
What’s the plot
My mistakes

Ghost in the phone
Who’s there
Ghost in the phone
Do I care
Ghost in the phone
Just a dial tone
Ghost in the phone
All alone

Her voice crack
Familiar sound
No turning back
Lost and found
A memory attack
Spins me around
Just a black
Hole profound

Or worse react
My old path

Nerves shot

My mistakes

Ghost in the phone

Ghost in the phone

Ghost in the phone
Just a dial tone
Ghost in the phone
All alone

Her voice crack
Familiar sound
No turning back
Lost and found
A memory attack
Spins me around
Just a black
Hole profound

songs.subject: a punk song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Phone in my hand
Sweat on my brow
Ten years gone by
What do I say now
Static hums loud
A ghost in the line

My own damn fault
Yeah I know it's true

Mother's call
Ring ring ring
Mother's call
What's it gonna bring
Mother's call
Fear in my gut
Mother's call
Door slamming shut

Remember that fight
Words like a knife
Screamin' and yellin'
Destroyed my whole life
Packed up my bags
Never looked back

Stupid pride
Yeah it always wins

Mother's call
Ring ring ring
Mother's call
What's it gonna bring
Mother's call
Fear in my gut
Mother's call

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I pack my boots in a paper bag
Keep a map in the glove compartment
Your town looks sweet in the afternoon
But I’m already hearing the pressure
My name rolls on with the weather front
I follow clouds like a lost saint
You ask me why I never stay
Baby, I was born in the rain

I try to breathe on a steady street
Try to learn the shape of a home
Then the sky goes black over the bean field
And my old heart starts to roam

I can’t settle down
I hear the thunder call
I can’t settle down
I’m gone before it falls
(spiral on, spiral on)
I can’t settle down

Last night I kissed you by the motel ice
With your hand warm on my shoulder
You said, “Come back when the danger fades”
But the danger only gets older
I’ve got a scar from a roadside fence
And a grin when the wind gets louder
I know your bed has a softer kind
Of peace than a breaking cloud has

I try to breathe on a steady street
Try to learn the shape of a home
Then the radio crackles my name again
And I’m halfway out the door

I can’t settle down
I hear the thunder call
I can’t settle down
I’m gone before it falls
(spiral on, spiral on)
I can’t settle down

Maybe one day the sky will open
And leave me a clean blue line
Maybe I’ll trade my chasing shoes
For a chair and a sheet on the line
But tonight I’m tracing the lightning
With my hands on a borrowed wheel
If you see me on the edge of town
You know how the storm feels

I can’t settle down
I hear the thunder call
I can’t settle down
I’m gone before it falls
(spiral on, spiral on)
I can’t settle down

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I keep a map in the glove box
creased by rain and thumb
Every county line looks lonely
when the sky starts to hum
I watch the fields turn silver
I watch the fence line bend
Then I’m gone past the filling station
before the day can end

You say, stay where I can find you
Let the thunder pass
But my boots know every back road
and they never last

I can’t settle down
I hear the storm come calling
I can’t settle down
With the dark clouds falling
Hold my hand, then let me go
I’m a highway running loose
I can’t settle down
I was born to chase the roof

I’ve slept in a truck stop booth
with my boots on the floor
Coffee cold as a ditch water
by the screen door
Your postcard on the dashboard
keeps slipping out of view
But the wind hits the wheat like a warning
and I follow through

You say, stay where I can find you
Let the thunder pass
But my boots know every back road
and they never last

I can’t settle down
I hear the storm come calling
I can’t settle down
With the dark clouds falling
Hold my hand, then let me go
I’m a highway running loose
I can’t settle down
I was born to chase the roof

Maybe there’s a little porch light
burning just for me
Maybe there’s a chair by the window
and a calmer sea
But the minute air turns heavy
and the gulls go wild
I’m already down that blacktop
with my luck and my child

I can’t settle down
I hear the storm come calling
I can’t settle down
With the dark clouds falling
Hold my hand, then let me go
I’m a highway running loose
I can’t settle down
I was born to chase the roof

Let the weather write my footprints
let it wash them clean
I’ll be gone by morning
in a half-moon dream
I can’t settle down
