songs.subject: a bossa nova song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
I saw your hands today
The veins like pencil lines
You folded your work shirt slow
Like time was on your side
You laughed at the old fan
And shook your silver head
The same small crooked smile
That used to light my bed

Now every step is careful
Every joke is soft
I’m learning how to hold you
Before you drift too far

Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
I still need your stories
I still need your laugh
Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
While your name is warm
In the air I have

The porch light found your face
At dinner, plain and kind
You asked me for the salt again
Then forgot what was inside
I wanted to say forever
But the words got stuck
So I just poured the tea
And watched the steam rise up

Now every photo breathes
Now every room has changed
Your shoes by the door
Feel like a passing rain

Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
I still need your stories
I still need your laugh
Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
While your name is warm
In the air I have

If your memory goes quiet
I’ll be the one who knows
The road you took to reach here
The way your garden grows
And if your hands grow shakier
I’ll carry what you can
You carried me through childhood
Now let me be your man

Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
I still need your stories
I still need your laugh
Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
While your name is warm
In the air I have
songs.lyrics_en:
I saw your hands today
The veins like pencil lines
You folded your work shirt slow
Like time was on your side
You laughed at the old fan
And shook your silver head
The same small crooked smile
That used to light my bed

Now every step is careful
Every joke is soft
I’m learning how to hold you
Before you drift too far

Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
I still need your stories
I still need your laugh
Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
While your name is warm
In the air I have

The porch light found your face
At dinner, plain and kind
You asked me for the salt again
Then forgot what was inside
I wanted to say forever
But the words got stuck
So I just poured the tea
And watched the steam rise up

Now every photo breathes
Now every room has changed
Your shoes by the door
Feel like a passing rain

Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
I still need your stories
I still need your laugh
Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
While your name is warm
In the air I have

If your memory goes quiet
I’ll be the one who knows
The road you took to reach here
The way your garden grows
And if your hands grow shakier
I’ll carry what you can
You carried me through childhood
Now let me be your man

Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
I still need your stories
I still need your laugh
Stay a little longer
Stay a little longer
While your name is warm
In the air I have

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
I watched your hands get thin
Over Sunday soup
Your watch felt loose on your wrist
Like it had more room than you
You laughed at the same old joke
Then forgot the punch
I held the door too long
Like time might let you up

And the radio played low
By the window glass
You asked me twice my name
I said it slow so it could last

Slow down, father, slow down
(hold my hand)
Slow down, father, slow down
(I am here)
Your white shirt by the chair
Still tells me you were strong
Slow down, father, slow down
Stay with me a little long

The stairs sound heavier now
When you climb them late
You pause to catch your breath
At the top step by the gate
I see the boy in your smile
When the pain lets go
Same crooked little grin
Same fire down below

And the porch light makes your hair
Look silver in the rain
You wave me off like always
But your wave has grown restrained

Slow down, father, slow down
(hold my hand)
Slow down, father, slow down
(I am here)
Your white shirt by the chair
Still tells me you were strong
Slow down, father, slow down
Stay with me a little long

If I could fold the years
Back into your palms
I’d leave the ache outside
And keep your voice calm
But all I have is now
This little borrowed time
So I’ll learn your slower steps
And walk them close to mine

Slow down, father, slow down
(hold my hand)
Slow down, father, slow down
(I am here)
Your white shirt by the chair
Still tells me you were strong
Slow down, father, slow down
Stay with me a little long

Stay with me
A little long
Father, slow down

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Down by the square at dawn
Mick opens up the shop
His razor’s sharp as a church bell
His broom’s already in the shop
He knows who paid, who lied
Who left with boots unlaced
And every tale in Ballymore
Ends up in his chair, face to face

He trims the mustache fine
Then leans in like he’s praying
One eyebrow, one slow grin
And you know what he’s saying

Mick knows the whole town
Mick knows the whole town
He’s got your name in his hands
Mick knows the whole town

The postman drops his secrets
The widow drops her ring
The lads come in for a tidy cut
And a little gossiping
He hears it in the doorbell
He smells it on the rain
If the mill girl’s gone to Galway
He already knows her train

He parts your hair just right
Then studies your reflection
One smile, one small cough
And he’s gone to the confession

Mick knows the whole town
Mick knows the whole town
He’s got your name in his hands
Mick knows the whole town

At closing time he sweeps the floor
And counts the stories told
A broken heart, a borrowed bike
A promise made too bold
He tucks away the barber cape
Like secrets in a chest
If you need the truth by morning
Mick will shave the rest

Mick knows the whole town
Mick knows the whole town
He’s got your name in his hands
Mick knows the whole town
Mick knows the whole town
Mick knows the whole town
No one leaves there unchanged
Mick knows the whole town

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Old Finn clips my fringe
By the saltwood sign
Knows my father’s cough
And the saint I hide
He says, “Boy, your trouble
Sits above your ear”
Then he shaves my jaw
Like he’s known me years

He knows the dockman’s lies
He knows the baker’s heart
He knows who left at dawn
And who fell apart

The barber knows the town
The barber knows the town
He trims your name right down
The barber knows the town
(He knows the town)
(He knows the town)

Maggie brought her tears
And a wedding ring
Finn just shook his head
Didn’t say a thing
Cut the split ends off
Of a widow’s grief
Left her with a grin
And a prayer of relief

He knows the child who sings
He knows the drunk who sways
He knows which dreams come back
And which ones drift away

The barber knows the town
The barber knows the town
He trims your name right down
The barber knows the town
(He knows the town)
(He knows the town)

When the fog rolls in
And the gulls fly low
He ties the apron string
And lets the stories go
From the church bell rope
To the pier at night
Every head he touches
Leaves a little light

The barber knows the town
The barber knows the town
He trims your name right down
The barber knows the town
(He knows the town)

songs.subject: a hardcore song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Door swings hard
I’m hit first
Onion skins
Grease and thyme
Old wood table
Flour on tile
Big black pot
Growling low

You know that smell
It grabs my chest
Burns right through
Every bad day
Hands in dough
Knuckles white
You built a home
Out of heat

Grandma’s kitchen!
(Grandma’s kitchen!)
I taste the salt
I taste the fire
Grandma’s kitchen!
(Grandma’s kitchen!)
One breath in
And I’m all the way home

Peppers crack
In the pan
Metal spoon
Scrapes the truth
Window fogged
Rain on glass
Your radio
Bled low hymns
Apron stained
Laughed-through years
You fed a war
With a calm wrist

You know that smell
It doesn’t leave
Stays on my coat
Stays in me
Chili steam
Bread on rise
You turned a room
Into a life

Grandma’s kitchen!
(Grandma’s kitchen!)
I taste the salt
I taste the fire
Grandma’s kitchen!
(Grandma’s kitchen!)
One breath in
And I’m all the way home

Cold years hit
But that scent stays
In my bones
In my jaw
On my tongue
On my hands
I hear your pan
Bang once more

Garlic
Onion
Bay leaf
Heat
Grandma’s kitchen!
(Grandma’s kitchen!)

Grandma’s kitchen!
(Grandma’s kitchen!)
I taste the salt
I taste the fire
Grandma’s kitchen!
(Grandma’s kitchen!)
One breath in
And I’m all the way home

Onion skin, black pepper

Stone-cold table

Grandma's kitchen
Grandma's kitchen

I carry that kiss
Grandma's kitchen
Grandma's kitchen

Another night we barely yield
My throat is raw my head is thick
This dusty ground makes me feel sick

But the rhythm pounds my chest

Muddy boots and empty bottles
Screaming out our last throttles
One more song one final roar
Till we cant take anymore
Muddy boots and empty bottles
Yeah we push it to the max throttles

The bass drum hits a primal beat
My aching legs cant find my feet
Lost my friends somewhere back there
Dont even know and dont even care

Sweat and grime a holy sheen
Best damn mess Ive ever seen

Muddy boots and empty bottles
Screaming out our last throttles
One more song one final roar
Till we cant take anymore

Yeah we push it to the max throttles

songs.subject: a pop song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
You met me on a county line
Mud on my boots, rain in my eyes
Maps on the seat, tea gone cold
I was already halfway gone

Hear that thunder in my chest
It calls me out when I should rest
You hold the door, you say stay still
But I was born to chase the spill

I can't settle down
I chase the dark clouds
I need the sky to break
Before my heart can stay
I can't settle down
I hear the storm now
I love you like the rain
Then I run away

Hotel towels, a borrowed room
Another town, another bloom
You left your name on my jacket sleeve
I kept it there like a warning key

You can light a porch for me
Warm the whole world, make it easy
But when the wind starts calling loud
I turn my face into the crowd

I can't settle down
I chase the dark clouds
I need the sky to break
Before my heart can stay
I can't settle down
I hear the storm now
I love you like the rain
Then I run away

If I came back with empty hands
Would you still take this restless man?
I see your red door in the haze
But the road keeps changing shape

I can't settle down
I chase the dark clouds
I need the sky to break
Before my heart can stay
I can't settle down
I hear the storm now
I love you like the rain
Then I run away

songs.subject: a pop song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I keep my boots by the door
Map folded on the dash
Fuel light on, heart too loud
I’m gone before it asks
Chasing clouds on county roads
With your name in my pocket
Every time I hear that siren cry
I can’t help but lock it

You say stay, stay
But the sky calls me out
You say wait, wait
When the thunder rolls south
I try to breathe
I try to be still
Then the horizon bends
And I’m running again

I can’t settle down
I chase the break in the clouds
I can’t settle down
My wheels keep calling me out
Hold me close, then let me go
I’m built for the blow
I can’t settle down
I can’t settle down

Motel key on a red string
Mud on my sleeves
Coffee gone cold in a paper cup
While the morning breaks its teeth
You want a porch, a steady flame
A dog, a fence, a Sunday
I want the line where the sky turns green
And the whole town looks one way

You say stay, stay
But the sky calls me out
You say wait, wait
When the thunder rolls south
I try to breathe
I try to be still
Then the horizon bends
And I’m running again

I can’t settle down
I chase the break in the clouds
I can’t settle down
My wheels keep calling me out
Hold me close, then let me go
I’m built for the blow
I can’t settle down
I can’t settle down

If I came home tonight
Would I know how to be
A porch light, a promise
A body in a seat?
But the wind gets my name
And the dark starts to spin
I love you like home
I just don’t know how to live in it

I can’t settle down
I chase the break in the clouds
I can’t settle down
My wheels keep calling me out
Hold me close, then let me go
I’m built for the blow
I can’t settle down

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain fell down like a river wild
I was stuck like a lonely child
You appeared from the misty gray
Waving hello what a brighter day

Umbrella built for two
Suddenly skies were blue
Side by side we stood
Misery misunderstood

My hat was soaked my shoes were squish
Didn't even have a single wish
Then your smile a sunshine bright
Chased away the stormy night

Umbrella built for two
Suddenly skies were blue
Side by side we stood
Misery misunderstood

We walked along a country lane
Sharing stories and the pouring rain
Two strangers now with a common thread
Under that umbrella over our heads

Umbrella built for two
Suddenly skies were blue
Side by side we stood
Misery

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
The sky broke open at the depot
You had one sleeve up to the rain
I had a paper sack of peaches
And a train that never came
You said, "Move in here beside me"
Like we both belonged on time
So we stood there shoulder to shoulder
Under one old umbrella line

Wind kept tugging at the handle
Mud kept lashing at our shoes
You laughed like you'd been waiting
For a stranger to choose

One umbrella
One small cover
You and me
Two folks, one another
One umbrella
Rain don't matter
When you lean in close
We fit better and better

Your boots were brown with road dust
Mine were soaked right through the seams
You told me you were headed nowhere
Same as me, it seems
We watched the freight cars calling
Through the slats of the gray board fence
I said, "This kind of weather"
Makes a man believe in chance

You tipped it toward my shoulder
I tipped it back your way
Funny how a little storm
Can make a long day stay

One umbrella
One small cover
You and me
Two folks, one another
One umbrella
Rain don't matter
When you lean in close
We fit better and better

The whistle cried out from the tracks
You grinned and shook your head
Said, "If we're still strangers by supper
Then we've done something wrong," you said

One umbrella
One small cover
You and me
Two folks, one another
One umbrella
Rain don't matter
When you lean in close
We fit better and better

The rain let up by the county line
But you kept walking my side
One umbrella, two strangers

songs.subject: a gospel song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
He wrote on scrap and stone
With a heart made up of flame
They came with silver promises
And called his name by name
But he kept his table lean
And his conscience wide awake
Said, “I won’t trade my true song
For a colder, easier way”

He heard the crowd grow loud
He saw the money shine
But truth sat by his doorway
Like bread and water in time
So he lifted up his hands
And he chose the harder road
The page may cost him plenty
But it won’t cost him his soul

He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
Ink in his hands, and truth in his mouth
He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
He’d rather be poor than bow down now

They said, “Change your words a little
Make your fire safe to sell”
He said, “A song that flatters power
Is a chain I know too well”
So he wrote for the widow’s tears
For the child who needs a song
For the ones with tired faces
Who kept on holding on

He heard the crowd grow loud
He saw the money shine
But truth sat by his doorway
Like bread and water in time
So he lifted up his hands
And he chose the harder road
The page may cost him plenty
But it won’t cost him his soul

He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
Ink in his hands, and truth in his mouth
He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
He’d rather be poor than bow down now

Let the tables turn
Let the deals fall through
If the world won’t bless his witness
He’ll still sing what’s true
And when the night gets heavy
He won’t bend, he won’t break
There’s a crown for the faithful
And no sale can take his name

He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
Ink in his hands, and truth in his mouth
He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
He’d rather be poor than bow down now

No, he won’t sell out
No, he won’t sell out
He keeps the flame alive

songs.subject: a gospel song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
He wrote by the porch light
On a dented old chair
With a church fan waving
Hot summer air
They came with a briefcase
And a smile too smooth
Said, "Change those hard lines"
But he stayed true

'Cause the page knows his name
And the blood in his pen
He won't bow for applause
He won't fake it for men
Every scar in his rhythm
Every prayer in the rain
Says, "Tell it plain"
Says, "Tell it plain"

He won't sell out
He won't sell out
Ink in his hands, truth in his mouth
He won't sell out
He won't sell out
Lord, keep him singing it loud

They offered him gold chains
And a bigger stage
A faster name
And a cleaner page
But he seen the kids hungry
Seen the mothers cry
So he wrote for the broken
And he let it fly

'Cause the page knows his name
And the blood in his pen
He won't bow for applause
He won't fake it for men
Every tear in his measure
Every line born in pain
Says, "Tell it plain"
Says, "Tell it plain"

He won't sell out
He won't sell out
Ink in his hands, truth in his mouth
He won't sell out
He won't sell out
Lord, keep him singing it loud

If the room goes cold
And the deal falls through
He'll still have a song
That'll carry him through
Let the crowd get small
Let the pride burn down
He'll keep that old fire
He'll keep that crown

He won't sell out
He won't sell out
Ink in his hands, truth in his mouth
He won't sell out
He won't sell out
Lord, keep him singing it loud
He won't sell out
He won't sell out

songs.subject: a blues song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I keep your name in ink
In a tin by the lamp
Paper gone soft with salt
Edges curled and damp
Every dawn I write you
While the gulls cry low
Fold it up, seal it tight
And watch the tide door go

I know you don't answer
Like a woman would
But you take my words
Like you understand good

Sea, sea, my sea
Carry what I send
Sea, sea, my sea
Bring me back again
Sea, sea, my sea
Take my heart along
Sea, sea, my sea
Till the break of dawn

I wrote about the storm bins
The rope burn on my hands
About the child in the harbor
And the broken boats on land
I told you my lonely
I told you my sin
How the tower feels empty
When the dark rolls in

Still I light that window
Like you might come near
White on black, I keep it
Year by year by year

Sea, sea, my sea
Carry what I send
Sea, sea, my sea
Bring me back again
Sea, sea, my sea
Take my heart along
Sea, sea, my sea
Till the break of dawn

One night in the wet wind
I swear I heard you hum
Not a saint, not an angel
Just the deep, and a drum
I laughed at my own tears
On the salt-stung floor
Then I wrote you one more line
And I wanted more

Sea, sea, my sea
Carry what I send
Sea, sea, my sea
Bring me back again
Sea, sea, my sea
Take my heart along
Sea, sea, my sea
Till the break of dawn

songs.subject: a blues song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I climb that iron stair
With salt in my eyes
Every dawn I stamp my name
On a sheet of gray-blue sky
Got a lantern on the sill
And a heart that won't behave
I write my love to the water
And the water writes back waves

I fold it neat
In a bottle's throat
Set it free
Watch it learn to float

Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my heart
(Sea, take my letter)
Don't tear us apart
Sea, take my letter
Carry it deep
(Sea, take my letter)
Where your cold mouths sleep

Last night the gulls were screaming
Round the break of the dock
I wrote about your silver back
And the rhythm of your rock
Wrote how your foam comes running
Like a dog to my door
Wrote how I miss your mercy
When the winter shakes the shore

I seal it with
A wax-red flame
If you won't stay
At least say my name

Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my heart
(Sea, take my letter)
Don't tear us apart
Sea, take my letter
Carry it deep
(Sea, take my letter)
Where your cold mouths sleep

The storm came in like trouble
Bit the coast down clean
I stood there at the window
Watching everything between
Then I heard your answer
In the groan of timber beams
You kept on coming back to me
In the wreckage and the gleam

Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my heart
(Sea, take my letter)
Don't tear us apart
Sea, take my letter
Carry it deep
(Sea, take my letter)
Where your cold mouths sleep

If I drown in your blue hunger
Let my words keep me afloat
Salt on the paper

songs.subject: a metal song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Boardwalk boards under bare feet
Your tan line burned my name
Coconut oil on your shoulder
And a taste like summer rain
We stole shade from the lifeguard tower
Split a beer by the pier
You laughed like you meant forever
Then the tide pulled you near

Surf roared like a warning
Moon rose white and mean
You bit down on my shirt collar
Said, "Don't make this a dream"

Salt on skin, burn it in
( salt on skin )
We were loud, we were thin
( salt on skin )
One hot week, then the sea
Took your hands away from me
Salt on skin, salt on skin
( salt on skin )

We were backseat saints in a rental
Sand still in your hair
Neon from the arcade melted
On your throat, on your stare
You drew hearts in the condensation
Then wiped them with your thumb
Said "August never keeps its promise"
And I knew what you meant, love

Thunder hid in the distance
Storm clouds split the blue
You kissed me like a match strike
Then the wind cut through

Salt on skin, burn it in
( salt on skin )
We were loud, we were thin
( salt on skin )
One hot week, then the sea
Took your hands away from me
Salt on skin, salt on skin
( salt on skin )

Now I hear gulls like sirens
See your footprints fill with foam
My chest still tastes of that shoreline
Like a blade I dragged home
If you ever hear my shouting
Through the breakers, through the dark
Know I kept that little wildfire
Even after we fell apart

Salt on skin, burn it in
( salt on skin )
We were loud, we were thin
( salt on skin )
One hot week, then the sea
Took your hands away from me
Salt on skin, salt on skin
( salt on skin )

Ooh, the tide took you
Ooh, but it left the scar
Salt on skin
Salt on skin

songs.subject: a metal song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Your towel on my board
Bare feet in the foam
You laughed at my scar
Like you knew my bones
Sunburn on your shoulders
Salt in your hair
You said, "Meet me back here"
Like you lived there

And the tide kept dragging
Our names through the blue
One look in the water
And I came unglued

Saltbite summer
You tore me open
Saltbite summer
Then the waves kept rolling
Hold me under
Say my name one time
Saltbite summer
Burned into my mind

Cheap beer in the cooler
Cracked lid, cold can
You traced your ring finger
Across my hand
A gull stole our fries
We laughed through the spray
You kissed me like thunder
Then slipped away

And the tide kept dragging
Our names through the blue
One look in the water
And I came unglued

Saltbite summer
You tore me open
Saltbite summer
Then the waves kept rolling
Hold me under
Say my name one time
Saltbite summer
Burned into my mind

I still taste the brine
On the back of my tongue

That July felt endless
Till the daylight was gone

You were heat on my skin
You were foam in my fist
Now the shore keeps your shadow
In the black of my ribs

Saltbite summer
You tore me open
Saltbite summer
Then the waves kept rolling
Hold me under
Say my name one time
Saltbite summer
Burned into my mind
Saltbite summer

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Your hands got thinner
Still fix that gate
Blue shirt on the line
Same old tape
You move slower now
But you still insist
“Pass me that wrench, boy”
Like nothing’s hit
I watch your back bend
At the sink each night
The same strong shadow
Just less in the light

And I don’t say it
But I feel it burn
Every little pause
Every little turn
Time sits heavy
On your shoulders, too
And I’m learning fear
From the way you do

Father, don’t fade now
Stay in the room
Father, don’t fade now
I still need you soon
Hold my name tight
Hold my name tight
You were my giant
Now I hold you right
(hold my name tight)
(hold my name tight)

You count the pills
By the glass on the tray
Then laugh it off
Like it’s just the day
You tell old stories
At the table chair
Same fishing hole
Same cracked hands there
I catch your silence
Between every joke
Like a paper boat
On a dark, slow choke
So I dry my face
Before you can see
Cause you taught me pride
And it lives in me

And I don’t say it
But I feel it burn
Every little pause
Every little turn
Time sits heavy
On your shoulders, too
And I’m learning love
From the way you do

Father, don’t fade now
Stay in the room
Father, don’t fade now
I still need you soon
Hold my name tight
Hold my name tight
You were my giant
Now I hold you right
(hold my name tight)
(hold my name tight)

If your knees get weak
Lean into me
If your voice gets small
I’ll still believe
I’ll pack your lunch
I’ll walk that road
I’ll be your son
When your steps get slow

Father, don’t fade now
Stay in the room
Father, don’t fade now
I still need you soon
Hold my name tight
Hold my name tight
You were my giant
Now I hold you right
(hold my name tight)
(hold my name tight)

Father, don’t fade now
Stay in the room
Father, don’t fade now
I still need you soon
Hold my name tight
Hold my name tight

Now I hold you right
(hold my name tight)
(hold my name tight)

If your knees get weak
Lean into me
If your voice gets small
I’ll still believe
I’ll pack your lunch
I’ll walk that road
I’ll be your son
When your steps get slow

Hold my name tight

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
I watch you move slower now
Across the kitchen tile
Same blue cup in your hand
Same grin, tired but wild
Your hair went salt and smoke
Your back learned a new bend
You still try to lift the bags
Like you were twenty then

And I catch myself
Holding my breath
When you stand too long
I see it in your steps
Time took its cut
Piece by piece
But you still look at me
Like I’m your baby

Papi en la silla
Papi en la silla
You still run my heart
Even when you sit still
Papi en la silla
Papi en la silla
I’m scared of the years
But I love you more

Now the TV hums at dawn
While you rub your wrist
You point at old family lines
With that same crooked finger lift
You tell me where you worked
And how the rain came down
How your father held you once
On this same worn ground
I laugh, then I look away
So you won’t see me break
You taught me how to stand up
Now I learn how to stay

And I catch myself
Counting the gray
Like it’s stealing you
A little every day
But when you call my name
It lands so bright
You make the room feel young
One more time

Papi en la silla
Papi en la silla
You still run my heart
Even when you sit still
Papi en la silla
Papi en la silla
I’m scared of the years
But I love you more

Take my arm, take your time
We can walk this slow
I’ll carry the heavy things
You carried my whole world
If your hands shake now
Mine will hold on tight
You gave me my first brave
Let me give you mine tonight

Papi en la silla
Papi en la silla
You still run my heart
Even when you sit still
Papi en la silla
Papi en la silla
I’m scared of the years
But I love you more
Papi en la silla
Papi en la silla
One day you taught me life

songs.subject: a post-rock song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Dust in the air thick heat heavy
Brass wailin' low mournful sound
Another soul gone another body buried
Six feet under sacred ground

Ash and bone parade
Marchin' through the street
Shadows long and swayed
Bitter and so sweet

Black suits sweat soaked faces
Tears mixin' with the rain
No smiles just empty spaces
A lifetime of joy and pain

Ash and bone parade
Marchin' through the street
Shadows long and swayed
Bitter and so sweet

Why we here why we

songs.subject: a post-rock song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
We walked down Perdido slow
Black coats in the rain
Marta held the folded hat
Against her chest again
The second line was quiet
Just a bootstep and a bell

And the house kept breathing
Through the iron in the air
Your name on the doorway
Still hanging there

Carry him home
Carry him home
Past the oak and the brick
Carry him home
Carry him home
Let the whole block sing it

A trumpet leaned like prayer
By the corner store
Mints in a paper cup
Coins on the floor
The drummer hit the silence
Like a nail through wood

And the river took its time
With the ache we left behind
Every face in the window
Turned toward the line

Carry him home
Carry him home
Past the oak and the brick
Carry him home
Carry him home
Let the whole block sing it

Then the sky split open wide
Gold on the water
One by one we lifted up
What the dark had offered
No one said goodbye
We just kept moving on

Carry him home
Carry him home
Past the oak and the brick
Carry him home
Carry him home
Let the whole block sing it
Carry him home
Carry him home

songs.subject: a pop song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You leave your blue cup
On my sink again
Like you might come back
And stay till dawn
I learn your footsteps
In the hallway floor
You say "you're sweet"
Then you shut the door

And I keep hoping
For a different turn
My name in your mouth
Like it finally burns

I want you, I want you
You don't want me back
I want you, I want you
That's the part that cracks
One more look, one more lie
I keep spinning out
I want you, I want you
And you don't want me now

Your jacket's still here
On the chair by the wall
I fold it slow
Like it means something at all
You laugh with me
Like it's easy, light
Then you disappear
Into someone else's night

And I keep hoping
For a different turn
My name in your mouth
Like it finally burns

I want you, I want you
You don't want me back
I want you, I want you
That's the part that cracks
One more look, one more lie
I keep spinning out
I want you, I want you
And you don't want me now

Maybe I made
A home out of signs
A held-out hand
And borrowed time
If love is a room
Then mine's half-lit
You were the door
I never fit

I want you, I want you
You don't want me back
I want you, I want you
That's the part that cracks
One more look, one more lie
I keep spinning out
I want you, I want you
And you don't want me now

songs.subject: a pop song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You say my name like a friend
Like I’m just here again
I catch your laugh in the hall
And it wrecks me, that’s all
You hand me your coat
I hold it too long
You talk about someone else
And I smile along

My chest keeps getting loud
But you don’t hear that sound
I’m one step from your door
Still somehow not the one you want

I love you from the edge
I love you, I love you
You never make that step
I love you, I love you
I’m right here in the light
But you look past my heart
I love you from the edge
And that’s the hardest part

Your text stays blue on my screen
I read too much between
One word, one little joke
Feels like I’m about to choke
You tell me your weekend plans
I’m trying to stand
In the shape of your almost
That I can’t command

My chest keeps getting loud
But you don’t hear that sound
I’m one step from your door
Still somehow not the one you want

I love you from the edge
I love you, I love you
You never make that step
I love you, I love you
I’m right here in the light
But you look past my heart
I love you from the edge
And that’s the hardest part

Maybe I was just a room
You could pass through
Maybe I was always
Only good for loving you
So I’ll fold this ache
Into something small
Still when you call my name
I want to give you all

I love you from the edge
I love you, I love you
You never make that step
I love you, I love you
I’m right here in the light
But you look past my heart
I love you from the edge
And that’s the hardest part
I love you from the edge
I love you, I love you
I let you have the whole

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You came down to the pier
With your rolled-up jeans
Barefoot on the boards
And a face like a dream
I was chewing salt
From a paper cone
You said, “Save me one”
Then you stole my cone

And the sky went peach
When you looked my way
My heart did flips
Like kids in the spray
You laughed so soft
I lost my name
By the time you turned
I was half your flame

Saltwater kiss
Saltwater kiss
We were gone by dusk
But I still miss this
Saltwater kiss
Saltwater kiss
One little tide
And I can’t resist (can’t resist)

Your towel was striped
Blue, red, and gold
Mine was a thrift-store
Thing that always got cold
We shared one soda
By the snack shack wall
You drew my initials
Then let them fall

And the moon came up
Like a silver coin
You said, “Stay a while”
So I said, “I’m yours”
Your fingers brushed
My sunburned hand
And I knew right then
I was in the sand

Saltwater kiss
Saltwater kiss
We were gone by dusk
But I still miss this
Saltwater kiss
Saltwater kiss
One little tide
And I can’t resist (can’t resist)

September came fast
With its empty beach
Just shells in the foam
And your laugh out of reach
But when the heat comes back
And the boardwalk hums
I swear I can taste
That little bit of sun

Saltwater kiss
Saltwater kiss
We were gone by dusk
But I still miss this
Saltwater kiss
Saltwater kiss
One little tide
And I can’t resist (can’t resist)

Oooh, by the waves
Oooh, we were brave
Oooh, saltwater kiss
Still calling my name

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Boardwalk sand in your shoes
Soda pop on your lips
You came down from the pier
With a tan and a grin
I was leaning on the rail
You were laughing at my name
One look in that July heat
And the whole world changed

Ooh, the tide came in
Ooh, you pulled me close
Ooh, I knew right then
This was all she wrote

Saltwater kiss, saltwater kiss
You stole my heart like this
Saltwater kiss, saltwater kiss
One summer, one sweet wish
(oooh) Saltwater kiss
(oooh) Saltwater kiss

We danced by the snack shack lights
Bare feet in the foam
You said you were here till Friday
Then you’d be going home
We shared a cherry ice cream
On the hood of my friend’s old car
You wrote your number in the sand
Then the waves took parts

Ooh, the moon got bright
Ooh, the night got slow
Ooh, you held my hand
Like you didn’t know

Saltwater kiss, saltwater kiss
You stole my heart like this
Saltwater kiss, saltwater kiss
One summer, one sweet wish
(oooh) Saltwater kiss
(oooh) Saltwater kiss

Now September comes around
And I smell sunscreen in the air
I still see your laughing eyes
When the gulls cut through the glare
Just one week, one little spark
Still it burns like the blue surf flame
If I hear that soft goodbye
I fall back in love again

Saltwater kiss, saltwater kiss
You stole my heart like this
Saltwater kiss, saltwater kiss
One summer, one sweet wish
(oooh) Saltwater kiss
(oooh) Saltwater kiss
Saltwater kiss, saltwater kiss

songs.subject: a R&B song about dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Champagne bubbles pop and fizz
Aunt Carol doing her awkward dance
Uncle Frank asleep on a chair
This wedding is a total blur

Then my eyes they caught a flicker
Across the room a new beat

Stranger stranger on the floor
Spinning me around wanting more
Lost in the rhythm a sweet unknown
Stranger stranger take me home

Your smile a supernova bright
My heart a drum solo wild
We moved like we invented moves
Forgot all about the bride and groom

Maybe it's the open bar
Maybe it's the moonlight gleam
Maybe it's just pure dumb luck
This feeling is a vibrant dream

Stranger stranger on the floor
Spinning me around wanting more

songs.subject: a R&B song about dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
You in that navy dress
Me in a borrowed suit
Your hand on my sleeve
Like you already knew
Cake on the table
Names I can’t recall
But when you looked my way
I forgot them all

One slow spin
Then another one
Your smile hit me
Like the sun
Everyone fading out
Till it was just us there
I said, “Don’t let go”
You said, “I’m already there”

Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me close
(oh, dance with me)
Under the flowers
Nobody knows
Dance with me, stranger
Till the room turns gold
(till the room turns gold)
I was a guest
Now I’m caught in your hold

Your heels tapped time
On the church hall floor
Your laugh came quick
When I asked for more
The bride saw us smiling
From across the room
Like she knew my heart
Was about to bloom
Your perfume stayed
When the song moved on
You said, “Find me later”
Then you were gone

One slow spin
Then another one
Your eyes said stay
Before I’d run
I don’t know your last name
But I know that stare
I said, “Don’t let go”
You said, “I’m already there”

Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me close
(oh, dance with me)
Under the flowers
Nobody knows
Dance with me, stranger
Till the room turns gold
(till the room turns gold)
I was a guest
Now I’m caught in your hold

Out in the courtyard
Air on your skin
You laughed and said
“We could begin”
No grand promise
No ring, no speech
Just your hand in mine
And a beat I could reach

Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me close
(oh, dance with me)
Under the flowers
Nobody knows
Dance with me, stranger
Till the room turns gold
(till the room turns gold)
I came alone
Now I’m not going home
Dance with me, stranger
Dance with me close
(oh, dance with me)
One more slow turn

songs.subject: a country song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
From my daddy’s old shed, rusted tin roof
Bare bulb swingin', a moth in its loop
Down by the creek where the bullfrogs croak
I strung up an antenna, from an old oak

Dusty old mixer, a thrift store find
Wires tangled up, like thoughts in my mind
Got a stack of old records, scratched and worn
Played 'em all night, 'til the early morn

Static on the dial, a whisper in the night
Rebel radio waves, shinin' ever so bright
No FCC license, no corporate decree
Just pure honest music, for folks like me

Folks started callin', on a cracklin' line
Said "Son, your music, it's truly divine"
Lonely old farmers, and truck drivers too
Found a little comfort, in what I put through

Sheriff drove by once, slowed down his truck
Saw the glow in the shed, but he wished me good luck
Some things you just know

songs.lyrics:
Out behind the feed store
past the blackberry briars
I strung that little wire
up between two tired tires
Paint can as a mast
old truck battery for the spark
Your name comes in at midnight
like a song across the dark

County says keep quiet
Says that thing's a crime
But the whole wide holler
starts to wake up right on time
When I twist that knob and pray
through the chicken-wire rain
I can hear your porch swing
sing my station name

Fence post signal, fence post signal
Whisper through the pines
Fence post signal, fence post signal
Rollin' out them lines
If they shut the whole town down
I'll still get through to you
Fence post signal, fence post signal
Baby, tell me you're tuned in too
(fence post signal)

Got a jar full of requests
and a list of borrowed songs
Old heartbreak, backroad gospel
and the broke-down singalong
Sheriff drove by slow
he leaned and looked my way
I waved him off with static
and a grin that said I stayed

Mamma says be careful
Don’t go making waves
But every lonely mailbox
needs a little sound these days
So I feed that little fire
with a midnight lucky charm
and let the whole ridge hear me
from the barn behind the barn

Fence post signal, fence post signal
Whisper through the pines
Fence post signal, fence post signal
Rollin' out them lines
If they shut the whole town down
I'll still get through to you
Fence post signal, fence post signal
Baby, tell me you're tuned in too
(fence post signal)

Maybe I'm a outlaw
maybe I'm just kind
of a stubborn fool with moonlight
and a song too hard to hide
But when your world goes lonesome
and the blacktop's all you know
I'll be down in that hollow
lettin' that old heartbeat roll

Fence post signal, fence post signal
Whisper through the pines
Fence post signal, fence post signal
Rollin' out them lines
If they shut the whole town down
I'll still get through to you
Fence post signal, fence post signal
Baby, tell me you're tuned in too

songs.subject: a afrobeats song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You came in sharp
I came in harder
Eye for an eye
Like we both had armor
One step left
Then you step right
We circle the floor
Like we came to fight
Your jaw stay tight
My pride stay high
But the DJ grins
And the room goes wild

Now the bass call your name
And my knees lose blame
We can fake that flame
Till we move the same

Step in sync
Step in sync
You and me
Step in sync
Step in sync
Step in sync
No more war
Just this blink

You cut one look
I threw one back
Old score still fresh
Like a red-hot crack
But your heel found mine
And I missed that beat
Then you laughed out loud
That shocked my seat
Now the crowd get loud
When we lock that sway
Even bad blood fades
When the hips obey

Now the bass call your name
And my knees lose blame
We can fake that flame
Till we move the same

Step in sync
Step in sync
You and me
Step in sync
Step in sync
Step in sync
No more war
Just this blink

Face to face
Then side by side
That old pride
Can't survive tonight
One two three
Let it fall away
If we keep this pace
We might dance the hate away

Step in sync
Step in sync
You and me
Step in sync
Step in sync
Step in sync
No more war
Just this blink

songs.subject: a afrobeats song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You rolled your eyes at me
I felt the same, you know
Same room, same reason
Same cold cup of soda
Your friends on one side
Mine on the other side
DJ called our names out
Now the floor is wide

Step too close, then step away
Look at me, don't run
One beat left, one beat right
We both know what's up

Forced to dance, forced to dance
(Forced to dance)
Hip to hip, we take a chance
We can fight it, we can laugh
But tonight, we start like that
Forced to dance, forced to dance
(Forced to dance)

You missed the first turn
I did it on purpose
Then you caught the timing
And smiled like it burned us
Your shoulder hit my sleeve
My pride hit the ground
Everybody cheering
Like they figured us out

Step too close, then step away
Look at me, don't run
One beat left, one beat right
We both know what's up

Forced to dance, forced to dance
(Forced to dance)
Hip to hip, we take a chance
We can fight it, we can laugh
But tonight, we start like that
Forced to dance, forced to dance
(Forced to dance)

Now the room goes quiet
Now your guard falls low
We were born to argue
Now we move like smoke
Say my name like trouble
I’ll say yours like fire
One more turn, one more grin
We might change our minds

Forced to dance, forced to dance
(Forced to dance)
Hip to hip, we take a chance
We can fight it, we can laugh
But tonight, we start like that
Forced to dance, forced to dance
(Forced to dance)
Forced to dance, forced to dance

songs.subject: a R&B song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Your towel in the sand
Next to my old tee
Sun on your shoulders
And you looking at me
Cold drink in your hand
Mine got warm too fast
You laughed with your mouth full
Said, "Make this day last"

We kept drifting close
Like the tide knew where
Your fingers on my wrist
Held me right there

Salt on our skin
Don’t let it end
Stay a little longer
Then run again
Salt on our skin
You and my heart
Summer fling, summer fling
Tearing me apart (oh)

Late drive home in heat
Hair still full of sea
A cracked little smile
When you glanced at me
Your voice in my ear
Like a secret wave
Said you never stay
That was all you gave

But I kept drifting close
Like the tide knew where
Your kiss hit my mouth
Then disappeared in air

Salt on our skin
Don’t let it end
Stay a little longer
Then run again
Salt on our skin
You and my heart
Summer fling, summer fling
Tearing me apart (oh)

Bare feet, wet hair
You were gone by dawn
One last red stripe
And then you were on
Back to the road
Back to your name
I kept the taste of you
Like a small flame

Salt on our skin
Don’t let it end
Stay a little longer
Then run again
Salt on our skin
You and my heart
Summer fling, summer fling
Tearing me apart (oh)
Salt on our skin
Still on my tongue
Summer fling, summer fling
We were the sun

songs.subject: a R&B song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You came with sand on your heels
Blue towel, salt on your skin
I said you look like a fever
You laughed like you knew I'd win
Your hand found mine by the boardwalk
Cold drink, warm sun on our necks
We talked like the night was easy
Like we had no plans to make

And the tide kept pulling close
Closer than we meant to go
You bit your lip when I said
"Stay a little more"

Beach fling, don't leave me
Beach fling, come tease me
Salt on my skin
You and me, just this
Beach fling, don't leave me
Beach fling, come tease me
One more wave
Then you fade away

We fell asleep on a blanket
Half-dressed under pink sky haze
Your nails left stars on my shoulder
That I still feel on my days
You wrote your name in the sand there
Then the water took it fast
I should've known from the first kiss
Summer love was made to pass

But the tide kept pulling close
Closer than we meant to go
You smiled like you were already
One foot out the door

Beach fling, don't leave me
Beach fling, come tease me
Salt on my skin
You and me, just this
Beach fling, don't leave me
Beach fling, come tease me
One more wave
Then you fade away

Now every shell I pick up
Sounds a little like your name
Every road back to that shoreline
Brings me right back to the flame
If you call, I might still answer
If you don't, I still replay
How you kissed me by the water
And let the summer slip away

Beach fling, don't leave me
Beach fling, come tease me
Salt on my skin
You and me, just this
Beach fling, don't leave me
Beach fling, come tease me
One more wave
Then you fade away

Salt on my skin
Still tastes like you
Beach fling, beach fling

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
I hauled that tower on a rain-soaked cart
Up Whistle Ridge by the junkyard yard
Old tin shack with a rusted door
One wire hung loose, I fixed one more
My daddy said, "Boy, mind your pace"
I said, "I'm fixed on a different place"
Not on the dial where the safe folks sit
I'm the midnight crackle, the outlaw spit

Turn the knob slow
Hear that glow
Boots on the stairs
And nobody knows

Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio (radio)
Spin that tune, let it roll
Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio (radio)
If the law comes pounding low
We'll keep on singin' slow

I got a jar of nails by the static fan
And a coffee can for the power band
A fiddle tune and a Bible verse
From a truck stop preacher with a broken curse
Mabel calls from the valley road
Says, "Play that one by the river road"
So I cue it up with a prayer and grin
And the whole dark holler leans back in

Turn the knob slow
Hear that glow
Boots on the stairs
And nobody knows

Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio (radio)
Spin that tune, let it roll
Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio (radio)
If the law comes pounding low
We'll keep on singin' slow

Last night, the sheriff rode by
Saw that beam cuttin' through the sky
He tipped his hat, kept on his way
He knew a song needs air to stay
So I fed the flame and I cracked that tune
Let it sail past the cornfield moon
If you hear me, friend, tap twice
I'll be your ghost on the line tonight

Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio (radio)
Spin that tune, let it roll
Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio
Whistle Ridge Radio (radio)
If the law comes pounding low
We'll keep on singin' slow

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Got a shack on the hill
And a bent tin roof
A homemade sign
And a coaxial loop
Copper wire in a coffee can
A mic with a nick in the head
I turn that dial at midnight
And wake the county from the dead

The sheriff don't know
What the pine trees keep
One little spark
And a hundred hearts leap

Spin that signal, spin
Out past the line
Pirate radio
On my own time
Spin that signal, spin
Let the whole town know
We’re singing from the ridge
On a secret radio

Mama says, boy
You’ll get caught one day
But she taps her foot
When the old songs play
Gas pump moonlight
On a gravel road
I read the weather
To the trucks below

If the church bells sleep
And the fields turn black
I send my tune
And they send it back

Spin that signal, spin
Out past the line
Pirate radio
On my own time
Spin that signal, spin
Let the whole town know
We’re singing from the ridge
On a secret radio

I heard a trucker laugh
Way down in Tullahoma
Said, “That’s the one”
Through the rain and coma
A schoolgirl sang
With a cracked-back grin
Now every late-night window
Leans right in

Spin that signal, spin
Out past the line
Pirate radio
On my own time
Spin that signal, spin
Let the whole town know
We’re singing from the ridge

songs.subject: a country song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Book spread out on the table
Highlighter cap in my teeth
A cold cup of coffee gone bitter
And a notebook staring at me
Outside, the town is sleeping
I’m learning how to stay awake
Turning one more page for the future
While the house makes a soft little creak

Two in the morning, I’m still here
Chasing a grade and a prayer
If I can make it through tonight
Maybe I can get somewhere

Two in the morning
Me and this old lamp
Two in the morning
Trying to make my stand
I’m writing my way
Through the hush and the rain
Two in the morning
And I’m still in the game
(Two in the morning)

Pencil marks on the worksheet
Lined up like fence posts in a row
My phone face down by the Bible
So I won’t let the whole night go
Mom left me a note on the counter
Said, “Baby, keep going, don’t quit”
I read it when my eyes got heavy
And it pulled me back in a bit

Two in the morning, I’m still here
Trying to earn what I need
If I can hold this line tonight
Maybe I can plant that seed

Two in the morning
Me and this old lamp
Two in the morning
Trying to make my stand
I’m writing my way
Through the hush and the rain
Two in the morning
And I’m still in the game
(Two in the morning)

I know the sun will find me
With my head on these same pages
But tonight I’m building something
One small dream at a time
And when the rooster calls tomorrow
I’ll be tired, but I’ll be proud
’Cause I learned how to keep on going
When nobody else was around

Two in the morning
Me and this old lamp
Two in the morning
Trying to make my stand
I’m writing my way
Through the hush and the rain
Two in the morning
And I’m still in the game
(Two in the morning)

songs.subject: a country song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning
And the lamp's gone gold
Your notes on the table
And the room feels cold
Highlighter bleeding
Across page forty-nine
I'm chasing one answer
Before I lose my mind

The house stays quiet
My coffee's gone dry
I keep turning pages
With my red-rimmed eyes
If I get this chapter
I might get by

Just me and these books tonight
(Just me and these books tonight)
I’m learning how to hold on tight
(hold on tight)
One more page, one more fight
One more page, one more fight
Just me and these books tonight

Your picture in my pocket
Keeps falling on the floor
Between algebra and hist'ry
I miss you more and more
The sink keeps drip-drip-dripping
Like a clock out in the hall
I mark up every margin
Till the hard ones start to call

The house stays quiet
My coffee's gone dry
I keep turning pages
With my red-rimmed eyes
If I get this chapter
I might get by

Just me and these books tonight
(Just me and these books tonight)
I’m learning how to hold on tight
(hold on tight)
One more page, one more fight
One more page, one more fight
Just me and these books tonight

When dawn taps the window
I'll still be awake
With a little more courage
Than the night can take
If the world gets heavy
I'll know what I can do
I can sit here alone
And make it through

Just me and these books tonight
(Just me and these books tonight)
I’m learning how to hold on tight
(hold on tight)
One more page, one more fight
One more page, one more fight
Just me and these books tonight

And the lamp's gone gold
Your notes on the table
And the room feels cold
Highlighter bleeding
Across page forty-nine
I'm chasing one answer
Before I lose my mind

The house stays quiet
My coffee's gone dry
I keep turning pages
With my red-rimmed eyes
If I get this chapter
I might get by

Just me and these books tonight

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I found your number
On a folded card
In my old jacket
Stuffed in the drawer
The screen went steady
My thumb went cold
I heard your porch fan
Through years of hold

Ring, ring, ring
I almost hung up
Ring, ring, ring
Then you picked it up

Mama, I’m calling
Mama, I’m calling
Don’t say a word yet
Just stay on the line
Mama, I’m calling
Mama, I’m calling
I was a stranger
Now I’m your son tonight
(ooh) now I’m your son tonight

You said, “Who’s speaking?”
I said my name
Like a small loose button
From the old shame
You laughed a little
Then you got quiet
I heard the spoon hit
Some supper plate

Ring, ring, ring
The room got small
Ring, ring, ring
Then you said it all

Mama, I’m calling
Mama, I’m calling
Don’t say a word yet
Just stay on the line
Mama, I’m calling
Mama, I’m calling
I was a stranger
Now I’m your son tonight
(ooh) now I’m your son tonight

You said, “I kept your room
Just like you left it”
Dust on the dresser
But the frame still kept it
I said, “I’m sorry”
You said, “Come home slow”
Then your breathing shook me
Like a held-back rose

Mama, I’m calling
Mama, I’m calling
Don’t say a word yet
Just stay on the line
Mama, I’m calling
Mama, I’m calling
I was a stranger
Now I’m your son tonight
Mama, I’m calling
Mama, I’m calling
(ooo) you answered first
And I’m still here tonight
(ooh) I’m still here tonight

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I found your number
On a yellow card
Stuck in my wallet
Bent and scarred
Years went quiet
I let them go
Now my thumb is trembling
On the phone

Can you hear me?
It’s me at last
I’ve been miles away
In my own past
My voice is rusty
My heart’s on show
I’m dialing slow
I’m dialing slow

Mama, pick up
Mama, pick up
I’m here now
I’m here now
Mama, pick up
Mama, pick up
Don’t let this ring
Be our last sound

I still remember
Your chipped blue cup
And how you’d hum low
When times got rough
I built my silence
Like it was strong
But every birthday
Felt too long

Can you hear me?
I’m not a ghost
I miss your kitchen
I miss it most
If you say my name
I might come home
I’m dialing slow
I’m dialing slow

Mama, pick up
Mama, pick up
I’m here now
I’m here now
Mama, pick up
Mama, pick up
Don’t let this ring
Be our last sound

If you cry, I’ll stay
If you’re mad, I’ll wait
I know I was late
I know I was late
I brought my fear
I brought my shame
But I still know
Your gentle name

Mama, pick up
Mama, pick up
I’m here now
I’m here now
Mama, pick up
Mama, pick up
Let this old heart
Find its way home
Mama, pick up
Mama, pick up
I’m here now

songs.subject: a americana song about the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The mower's still warm in the shed
Sunflower heads droop by the fence
Your mama packs paper plates
And the bus-stop air feels dense
I got one clean shirt in the wash
One notebook, one borrowed pen
The screen door slaps like it knows
This summer won't come again

We drag our feet through the yard
Like we can slow the dark
Your brother tosses a football
Past the rust-red parked-up cars

One more day of summer
One more drive down Main
One more taste of home
Before I learn new names
One more day of summer
Keep it in my bones
When I miss this porch light
I'll know where to go

We drove past the county fair
Saw the Ferris wheel come down
Dust on the Baptist sign
And the ball field shutting down
You laughed with your shoes half-tied
Said, "Don't go turning serious"
I said, "I won't forget this road"
Even if I get too curious

So we stand by the mailbox
With the cicadas loud
And every little goodbye
Feels bigger than it sounds

One more day of summer
One more drive down Main
One more taste of home
Before I learn new names
One more day of summer
Keep it in my bones
When I miss this porch light
I'll know where to go

And if the dorm room feels too small
If the days run hard and fast
I'll hear your laugh in the gravel
And the wheels on that old tractor track
I'll carry this town with me
In the crease of a folded map
In a hoodie that smells like rain
And the last sweet corn off the stack

One more day of summer
One more drive down Main
One more taste of home
Before I learn new names
One more day of summer
Keep it in my bones
When I miss this porch light
I'll know where to go

The crickets tune up early
The sky goes denim-blue
I leave with dirt on my boots
And the whole warm world in view

songs.subject: a americana song about the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise orange on my old beat-up truck
Engine coughs a little still gives a pluck
Mama's coffee tastes like goodbye today
Summer's last breath just drifts away

Oh summer's last stand
Sand between my toes in this promised land
College calls me a different kind of wild
Just a big kid pretending to be a child

Packed up my books my dreams and my fears
Gonna trade these fields for concrete frontiers
Last look at the creek where we used to swim
This chapter closing a brand new whim

Oh summer's last stand
Sand between my toes in this promised land
College calls me a different kind of wild
Just a big kid pretending to be a child

Remember nights under a million stars
No worries no scars no locked up bars
Now a highway stretches long and wide
Nowhere to run nowhere to hide

Oh summer's last stand
Sand between my toes in this promised land
College calls me a different kind of wild
Just a big kid pretending to be a child

songs.subject: a americana song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
He keeps his poems in a tin coffee can
Under the sink by the red dust fan
Writes on receipts from the diner till dawn
With a busted-up pen and his boots still on

They came in a suit with a shiny gold grin
Said, “Sign your name and the whole world wins”
But he looked at the page, then he looked at the door
Said, “I’d rather be broke than be owned anymore”

He’s got roots in the clay
And a fire in his chest
If the price is his soul
Then he knows what’s best

He won’t sell out
No, he won’t sell out
He’ll keep his own song
He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
He’ll sleep poor and right
(He won’t sell out)

Now he reads to the kids at the county fair
To the old men leaning in folding chairs
And the verses land hard like a hammer on pine
Truth don’t need polish, it just needs time

He’s got roots in the clay
And a fire in his chest
If the price is his soul
Then he knows what’s best

He won’t sell out
No, he won’t sell out
He’ll keep his own song
He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
He’ll sleep poor and right
(He won’t sell out)

Let the gold pass by
Let the greedy folks stare
He’s got a name in the dust
And a world in his care
One clean page
Can outlast a lie
When the porch light burns
And the honest lines rise

He won’t sell out
No, he won’t sell out
He’ll keep his own song
He won’t sell out
He won’t sell out
He’ll sleep poor and right
(He won’t sell out)

songs.subject: a americana song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
He writes on brown paper bags
At the feed store counter
Ink on his thumb
And hay in his collar
They offer him money
For a sweeter kind of truth
He just taps his pen
And laughs at the booth

He says, "I seen the price
Of a borrowed soul"
"Keep your gold in your pocket
I'll keep my old road"

I won't sell out
I won't sell out
I keep my name
With the dirt and the drought
I won't sell out
I won't sell out
You can buy the bar
But you can't buy out loud

He reads at the fairgrounds
By the kettle corn tent
To a few folding chairs
And a tired old saint
Big man in a suit
Says, "Sign here, son"
He folds up the page
And he's already gone

He says, "I seen the price
Of a borrowed soul"
"Keep your gold in your pocket
I'll keep my old road"

I won't sell out
I won't sell out
I keep my name
With the dirt and the drought
I won't sell out
I won't sell out
You can buy the bar
But you can't buy out loud

His shoes got holes
His coat got rain
But the words in his mouth
Still mean the same
One hand on the page
One hand on the plow
He makes his own way
And he makes it now

I won't sell out
I won't sell out
I keep my name
With the dirt and the drought
I won't sell out
I won't sell out
You can buy the bar

songs.subject: a indie rock song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
We parked by the chain-link fence
Your hand on the dash
The moon split the hood in half
And I said, "This is it"
The ticket in your jacket
The crumbs on your sleeve
We laughed like it was already ours
Like the door would just give

Then the guard walked slow
And the clock went thin
My heart hit hard
In my ribs again

We almost made it
Almost made it
Right to the edge
And we watched it shake
We almost made it
Almost made it
Held it in our hands
Then it slipped away

Your voice went quiet
Mine went small
Headlights moved like a warning line
Across the lot
You said, "One more minute"
I said, "I know"
But the night was folding up
And the world said no

Then the gate swung shut
Like a final chord
We stood there frozen
Wanting more

We almost made it
Almost made it
Right to the edge
And we watched it shake
We almost made it
Almost made it
Held it in our hands
Then it slipped away

I still see your face
In the rearview glass
That half-smile
When the moment passed
If I close my eyes
I can taste that ache
The kind that proves
You were close enough to break

We almost made it
Almost made it
Right to the edge
And we watched it shake
We almost made it
Almost made it
Held it in our hands
Then it slipped away

Not a loss
Not a win
Just the spark
Before it dimmed
We almost made it
We almost made it

songs.subject: a indie rock song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
We parked by the loading dock
Your hand on my sleeve
The moon on the fence line
And dust in the leaves
I had the note in my pocket
Folded four times small
Your name like a matchhead
Burning through it all

And the door was half open
The crowd was a wave
I could see my own shadow
On the edge of the stage

We almost made it
Almost made it
Almost made it, baby
Right to the edge
We almost made it
Almost made it
Almost made it, baby
Then it slipped away

The driver kept yawning
Keys in his fist
You laughed like a dare
Then you bit your lip
The bass from inside
Hit the loading bay steel
Like a second heartbeat
I could almost feel

And the door was half open
The room held its breath
I was one step from falling
One step from yes

We almost made it
Almost made it
Almost made it, baby
Right to the edge
We almost made it
Almost made it
Almost made it, baby
Then it slipped away

I still wear the moment
Like a bruise on my skin
That half-lit doorway
And the world we were in
If I close my eyes now
I can taste that place
You turn your head back
And I lose my face

We almost made it
Almost made it
Almost made it, baby
Right to the edge
We almost made it
Almost made it
Almost made it, baby
Then it slipped away
We almost made it
Almost made it
Almost made it, baby

songs.subject: a disco song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
I push through the screen door
At quarter to one
The griddle's still singing
Like it just begun
Red vinyl booths
And a coffee pot shine
You know that old clock
Never tells me it's time
A girl in the window
Pours one more cup
She says, "Sit where you want"
And the house never shuts

When the cold comes crawling
We come right in
Tin of the sugar
And a fresh stack grin

Open all night
Open all night
Keep the hash browns hot
And the pie in sight
Open all night
Open all night
If the world goes quiet
We stay alive
(Open all night)
(Open all night)

Truckers and dancers
And boys with wet hair
A mother in slippers
Still braiding her stare
The jukebox is leaning
On a Marvin groove
Coins in the bowl
Like a dare to move
She slides me a booth
With a wink and a plate
Says, "Honey, the sunrise
Can go and wait"

When the cold comes crawling
We come right in
Tin of the sugar
And a fresh stack grin

Open all night
Open all night
Keep the hash browns hot
And the pie in sight
Open all night
Open all night
If the world goes quiet
We stay alive
(Open all night)
(Open all night)

Morning can knock
But it won't find a lock
There's a red-checkered promise
In every coffee talk
One more refill
One more laugh at the door
We don't need tomorrow
We got tonight and more

Open all night
Open all night
Keep the hash browns hot
And the pie in sight
Open all night
Open all night
If the world goes quiet
We stay alive
Open all night
Open all night
Till the first birds call
And the booths still shine
(Open all night)
(Open all night)
songs.lyrics_en:
I push through the screen door
At quarter to one
The griddle's still singing
Like it just begun
Red vinyl booths
And a coffee pot shine
You know that old clock
Never tells me it's time
A girl in the window
Pours one more cup
She says, "Sit where you want"
And the house never shuts

When the cold comes crawling
We come right in
Tin of the sugar
And a fresh stack grin

Open all night
Open all night
Keep the hash browns hot
And the pie in sight
Open all night
Open all night
If the world goes quiet
We stay alive
(Open all night)
(Open all night)

Truckers and dancers
And boys with wet hair
A mother in slippers
Still braiding her stare
The jukebox is leaning
On a Marvin groove
Coins in the bowl
Like a dare to move
She slides me a booth
With a wink and a plate
Says, "Honey, the sunrise
Can go and wait"

When the cold comes crawling
We come right in
Tin of the sugar
And a fresh stack grin

Open all night
Open all night
Keep the hash browns hot
And the pie in sight
Open all night
Open all night
If the world goes quiet
We stay alive
(Open all night)
(Open all night)

Morning can knock
But it won't find a lock
There's a red-checkered promise
In every coffee talk
One more refill
One more laugh at the door
We don't need tomorrow
We got tonight and more

Open all night
Open all night
Keep the hash browns hot
And the pie in sight
Open all night
Open all night
If the world goes quiet
We stay alive
Open all night
Open all night
Till the first birds call
And the booths still shine
(Open all night)
(Open all night)

songs.subject: a disco song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights bleedin' colors
Midnight city hums
Greasy spoon dreams
Where the night owl comes

Coffee strong and black
Sugar sweet and bright

Neon Glow
Never sleeps
Neon Glow
Secrets it keeps

Vinyl booths gleamin'
Chrome shinin' bold
Stories whispered softly
As the hours unfold

Jukebox plays a tune
Under the fluorescent light

Neon Glow
Never sleeps
Neon Glow

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The brass wakes up the block
By the church on Chartres Street
Black shoes on the damp stone
Slow steps in the summer heat
Magnolia on a lapel
White gloves, folded neat
Mrs. Bell walks past the porch
And the whole road feels her beat

Hear that second line turn
Round the corner slow
One hand on the memory
One hand letting go

Walk me down slow
Under the drum and rain
Walk me down slow
Say the name again
Second line rain
On a bright green street
Second line rain
We carry love with our feet

A trumpet leans and cries
Then it smiles right through
Baby, old man, child alike
Find the rhythm true
The widow holds her fan
Like a small white sail
And the cousins all keep time
With a soft-edged wail

Hear that second line turn
Like the river does
What we lose in the body
Stays alive in us

Walk me down slow
Under the drum and rain
Walk me down slow
Say the name again
Second line rain
On a bright green street
Second line rain
We carry love with our feet

At the graveyard gate
The band falls back
Just a wind in the trees
And a coat of black
But the hand on my shoulder
Says, “Keep going on”
What is gone from the table
Still lives in the song

Walk me down slow
Under the drum and rain
Walk me down slow
Say the name again
Second line rain
On a bright green street
Second line rain
We carry love with our feet

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Down on Chartres, black shoes shine
Slow march in the humid line
White handkerchiefs, slow and clean
Beads on the brass, all polished keen
A second line turns the corner slow
Tambourine trembles, heads all low
The old church door, the jasmine air
Your name still hangs with the morning prayer

We carry what we cannot keep
Step by step through the heavy heat
Pale roses in the folded hands
Love is gone, but the river stands

Second line, take me home
Second line, let the soul roll on
Drums so soft, hearts so strong
We cry, we dance, we carry on
Second line, take me home
Second line, where the ones still roam
Raise me up through the song
We cry, we dance, we carry on

On Rampart, the horns lean in
A silver tear, a crooked grin
The band gives breath to every loss
And every face bears every cost
Mothers hum through folded lace
Old men shine with a solemn grace
Even grief can sway a little
When the rain falls warm and gentle

We carry what we cannot keep
Step by step through the heavy heat
Pale roses in the folded hands
Love is gone, but the river stands

Second line, take me home
Second line, let the soul roll on
Drums so soft, hearts so strong
We cry, we dance, we carry on
Second line, take me home
Second line, where the ones still roam
Raise me up through the song
We cry, we dance, we carry on

Ooh, let the horn speak low
Ooh, let the sorrow go
From the church to the open street
Memory moves with steady feet

Second line, take me home
Second line, let the soul roll on
Drums so soft, hearts so strong
We cry, we dance, we carry on
Second line, take me home
Second line, where the ones still roam
Raise me up through the song

songs.subject: a folk song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
We came in on fumes
With rain on the hood
Your shoes full of mud
My hands full of good luck
The diner was closed
But the sign still glowed
Like it knew our names
And it wanted us home

One more hill
One more bend
You said, "Hold on"
I said, "Amen"

We almost made it
We almost made it
Right to the edge
Right to the gate
We almost made it
We almost made it
And that almost stayed
The whole night through

The map on your lap
Had a crease like a scar
A gas station dog
Kept barking at our car
Your laugh hit the dark
Like a bottle break
And I saw the whole world
Lean a little our way

One more mile
One more sign
You said, "Look close"
I said, "This time"

We almost made it
We almost made it
Right to the edge
Right to the gate
We almost made it
We almost made it
And that almost stayed
The whole night through

Morning came pale
Over cracked blacktop
You let out a breath
That I still can't stop
It wasn't a loss
It wasn't a win
Just two brave fools
At the start again

We almost made it
We almost made it
Right to the edge
Right to the gate
We almost made it
We almost made it
And that almost stayed
The whole night through

We almost made it

songs.subject: a folk song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
I wore my father’s coat
To the county line
Your name in my mouth
Like a copper dime
The map was folded wrong
But I knew the road
I could taste the rain
Before it even showed

The crowd was thinning out
By the grain silo
Your porch light burned
Like a low-star glow
I had the speech half-tied
In my raw throat
Then the screen door banged
And the moment slipped afloat

I could see it there
Right past my hand
One foot from the door
One breath from land

I almost made it
I almost made it
Right to your front step
Right to your heart
I almost made it
I almost made it
Then the dark pulled wide
And tore me apart

Morning found my boots
By the ditch in mud
A crushed-up flyer
And a stain of blood
Not enough to call
Not enough to stay
Just a bent-back night
And the boy I used to be

I could see it there
Right past my hand
One foot from the door
One breath from land

I almost made it
I almost made it
Right to your front step
Right to your heart
I almost made it
I almost made it
Then the dark pulled wide
And tore me apart

Now I laugh it off
When they ask me why
There’s a scar-shaped moon
In the edge of my eye
But I still hear that gate
In the field swing shut
And I still keep score
On what I almost was

I almost made it
I almost made it
Right to your front step
Right to your heart
I almost made it
I almost made it
Now I sing the ghost
Of that false start
I almost made it
I almost made it
And I learned a little

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
I came north for the cold
With my coat in my hand
Your green ribbon started
Moving over the land
It curled like a secret
Above the dark bay
And I forgot my suitcase
I forgot what to say

Now the sky is bending
Just for you and me
Painted in slow motion
On a winter sea

Aurora, stay with me
Aurora, stay with me
Green fire in the dark
You dance inside my heart
Aurora, stay with me
Aurora, stay with me
Turn the night to silk
And let me watch you drift

Boots on the wooden pier
Snow in my hair
A fisherman pointed up
Like you were right there
The windows went golden
Then blue, then gold
Your colors kept changing
Like a story told

And the whole town hushed down
When you started to sway
Like a slow sweet fever
I couldn’t give away

Aurora, stay with me
Aurora, stay with me
Green fire in the dark
You dance inside my heart
Aurora, stay with me
Aurora, stay with me
Turn the night to silk
And let me watch you drift

If this is a dream, don't wake me
Let the frozen harbor glow
I came here for the silence
Now I know what silence knows

Aurora, stay with me
Aurora, stay with me
Green fire in the dark
You dance inside my heart
Aurora, stay with me
Aurora, stay with me
Turn the night to silk
And let me watch you drift

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
I came north for the dark
For the long white road
Snow on the window
Your hand on my coat
The sky went green
Like a slow silk flame
You said, "Look up now"
I said your name

And it moved like a prayer
Across the cold wide air
Soft as a palm leaf
Turning there

Green sky, hold me
Green sky, fold me
(hold me close)
Green sky, show me
Where your wild heart goes
Green sky, hold me
Green sky, hold me
(turn and glow)
I never knew
The night could sing like this

Boots in the powder
Breath in a cloud
You laughed so quiet
I heard it loud
The whole lake shimmered
Like glass in a bowl
Your eyes were fuller
Than the moon could hold

Then the colors leaned down
Over every pine
And I forgot my name
For a little while

Green sky, hold me
Green sky, fold me
(hold me close)
Green sky, show me
Where your wild heart goes
Green sky, hold me
Green sky, hold me
(turn and glow)
I never knew
The night could sing like this

If the world turns hard
Let it turn outside
I got your warm breath
And the green tide
Let it pass through me
Let it pass through you
For one bright moment
We were the view

Green sky, hold me
Green sky, fold me
(hold me close)
Green sky, show me
Where your wild heart goes
Green sky, hold me
Green sky, hold me
(turn and glow)
I never knew

songs.subject: a disco song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise on the river
Misty morning gray
Another soul departed
Gone on home today

But we ain't shedding tears
Not on this fine day

Gonna dance him to heaven
Yeah dance him on up
Second line stepping
Fill my spirit cup

Black suits and white handkerchiefs
Shiny shoes tap tap
Trumpets wailing joyful noise
Ain't no turning back

Hear that rhythm calling
From the street today

Gonna dance him to heaven
Yeah dance him on up
Second line stepping
Fill my spirit cup

songs.subject: a disco song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Black veil on the banister
Cards on the piano
Mister June in polished shoes
Gone where the brass can blow
Mama folds a white handkerchief
Cool as a church fan
Everybody stepping slow
Out on Basin Street tonight

Hear that low drum roll
Feel that shoulder sway
Grief got gold on it
As we move this way

Second line, second line
Lift your feet, let it shine
Second line, second line
Cry today, dance tonight
Oh, we march it through
Oh, we spin it high
Second line, second line
Love don’t say goodbye

We pass the bakery window
We pass the corner store
Kids in bright red shoes
Running out the front door
Uncle with the silver cane
Laughing through the rain
Auntie says, "Keep that beat"
While the trumpets spell his name

Hear that low drum roll
Feel that shoulder sway
Heaven near the sidewalk
Calling us this way

Second line, second line
Lift your feet, let it shine
Second line, second line
Cry today, dance tonight
Oh, we march it through
Oh, we spin it high
Second line, second line
Love don’t say goodbye

When the parade turns the corner
We don’t fall apart
We carry the heavy shadow
With a lifted heart
One more round, sweet brother
One more turn in view
If the road keeps going
We keep going too

Second line, second line
Lift your feet, let it shine
Second line, second line
Cry today, dance tonight
Oh, we march it through
Oh, we spin it high
Second line, second line

songs.subject: a dream pop song about quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I folded my program small
Kept it under my coat
The fan turned slow above us
Like it knew what I’d let go
The pew beside me cracked
When I stood without a word
My mother bowed her head
I heard my shoes, I heard my nerves

Past the glass, the parking lot
Held its cold blue line
I held my breath till the door
Made a soft goodbye

Quietly I go
Quietly I go
I still know the road
But I let it go
Quietly I go
Quietly I go
Pews and folded coats
I let them go

The basement smell of coffee
The potluck paper plates
The hymn with the broken speaker
The way the summer saved my face
I learned to keep my questions
Small as coins in my palm
But they grew teeth in the waiting
And they wouldn’t stay calm

Past the glass, the parking lot
Held its cold blue line
I held my breath till the door
Made a soft goodbye

Quietly I go
Quietly I go
I still know the road
But I let it go
Quietly I go
Quietly I go
Pews and folded coats
I let them go

I am not a storm
I am not a scene
Just a girl in her own name
Stepping clean between
The back row, the front row
The old locked frame
I loved you like a home once
I can love you still from miles

Quietly I go
Quietly I go
I still know the road
But I let it go
Quietly I go
Quietly I go
Pews and folded coats
I let them go
