songs.lyrics:
My shoes knew the aisle
Before I did
The oak bench creaked
Like it had a grudge
I held my breath
At the hymn book edge
Past the pastor’s hands
Past the little red badge

I kept my face still
Kept my coat closed
Sun through stained glass
On my sleeve like gold
The door was already
Halfway open
And my name in the air
Felt tired, not broken

I’m leaving quiet
Leaving quiet
Soft as the dust
On the back row
I’m leaving quiet
Leaving quiet
I loved you there
But I have to go

The foyer smelled
Like wet umbrellas
And sweet old coffee
In paper cups
Moms by the bulletin
Kids by the water fountain
No one looked up
When I stepped out
I kept the keychain
From youth group summer
Kept the green felt
From the offering plate

I learned the words
I learned them well
But my throat went thin
When I tried to tell
So I let the hinge
Do all the talking
One small click
And I was walking

I’m leaving quiet
Leaving quiet
Soft as the dust
On the back row
I’m leaving quiet
Leaving quiet
I loved you there
But I have to go

No thunder came
No floor gave way
Just my heart
In the middle of the day
And the sky outside
Was plain and blue
Like it had been waiting
For me too

I’m leaving quiet
Leaving quiet
Soft as the dust
On the back row
I’m leaving quiet
Leaving quiet
I loved you there

songs.subject: a soul song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I saved your number
like it meant mine
Late bus home
and a low red sign
You laughed so easy
with your hand on mine
Then pulled it back
like it burned that time

I keep that look
like a hole in my chest
You give me just enough
to make me want the rest

But you don't want me
you don't want me
I know it now
Still I come back
still I come back
to where you are now
You say my name
like a goodbye
I hold the door
and watch you walk by

Your coat on my chair
for a week in spring
Your scent stayed there
like a broken thing
I made you soup
when your throat got sore
You kissed my cheek
then looked at the floor

I read your silence
like a letter with no end
Every little maybe
breaks me again

But you don't want me
you don't want me
I know it now
Still I come back
still I come back
to where you are now
You say my name
like a goodbye
I hold the door
and watch you walk by

If love is a house
then I'm out in the rain
Hands on the glass
calling your name
You turn the key
but you never step through
I keep the light on
just for you

But you don't want me
you don't want me
I know it now
Still I come back
still I come back
to where you are now
You say my name
like a goodbye
I hold the door
and watch you walk by
You don't want me
you don't want me
and I still don't know why
Still I come back
still I come back
one more time

songs.subject: a soul song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I seen your coat on my chair
Like you might come back for it
I kept your cup by the sink
Even when the ice all quit
You say my name real sweet
Like it don't mean a thing
I wear that sound like a chain
And you wear it like a ring

I read your eyes too slow
You let me hope too long
You lean in like rain
Then turn and move along
I keep the door unlocked
You never stay that late
I learn your kind of love
Comes in and hesitates

Pressed my name
Pressed my name on your mouth
(Pressed my name)
Then you walked right out
I keep giving you room
You keep making me wait
Pressed my name
But you never stayed

Your chair stays pulled out
Like a promise in disguise
I catch myself smiling
At the wrong part of your eyes
You call when you're lonely
I show up every time
You want my hands for comfort
Not the rest of my life

I hear your steps, then gone
Before the dawn can break
I build my heart in parts
You leave it in your wake
I know your touch by heart
Still I don't know your truth
You gave me almost enough
To keep me after you

Pressed my name
Pressed my name on your mouth
(Pressed my name)
Then you walked right out
I keep giving you room
You keep making me wait
Pressed my name
But you never stayed

If I could love you smaller
Maybe I'd let you go
But every time you linger
You pull me back below
So I'll fold up your sweater
And learn your empty space
Some loves don't leave a scar
They just keep your shape

Pressed my name
Pressed my name on your mouth
(Pressed my name)
Then you walked right out
I keep giving you room
You keep making me wait
Pressed my name
But you never stayed
Pressed my name
And I still say yours anyway
(Pressed my name)
Like a prayer in the rain
I was never your home
Just a place you could take
Pressed my name

songs.subject: a reggae song about being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I come in early, papers in my hand
Got my notes lined up, got my plan
Boss say, “Go ahead,” then never stay
Soon as I start, they drift away
Karen talk over me, then Dan jump in
Three voices loud, and I fold again
My point sit there like a plate gone cold
While the same room turns to a story told

Mi raise mi hand
Mi wait mi turn
But every word
Just get ignored

Pass the mic, pass the mic
I was here, I was here all night
Pass the mic, pass the mic
Let me finish one time right
(One time right)
Pass the mic, pass the mic
Stop stepping on my line
Pass the mic, pass the mic
I got a voice, it’s mine
(It’s mine)

Zoom screen freeze, but my face still there
I say “Can we,” and nobody cares
Mia says “Actually,” right on top
Now my whole idea just get chopped
I type in chat, “Please let me land”
Still get cut by a waving hand
Every Monday, same old show
I bring the light, then they steal the glow

Mi raise mi hand
Mi wait mi turn
But every word
Just get ignored

Pass the mic, pass the mic
I was here, I was here all night
Pass the mic, pass the mic
Let me finish one time right
(One time right)
Pass the mic, pass the mic
Stop stepping on my line
Pass the mic, pass the mic
I got a voice, it’s mine
(It’s mine)

One breath, one beat, one chance to speak
I’m not too quiet, just hard to reach
If you want the truth, then hear me through
When I talk, that’s the room too

Pass the mic, pass the mic
I was here, I was here all night
Pass the mic, pass the mic
Let me finish one time right
(One time right)
Pass the mic, pass the mic
Stop stepping on my line
Pass the mic, pass the mic
I got a voice, it’s mine
(It’s mine)

songs.subject: a reggae song about being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
We sit in the room
I raise my hand
I got a point
You do not understand
I say one sentence
Then it drifts away
Somebody jumps in
And takes my place

I wait for my turn
I keep it cool
But the clock keeps running
And I play the fool

Talked over again
Talked over again
I had a whole thought
Now it’s gone with the wind
Talked over again
Talked over again
Hear me one time
Let me finish, my friend

Same little table
Same long agenda
Same one loud voice
Stealing the center
My notes on paper
My words in line
But every opening
Gets cut in time

I breathe in slow
I hold my ground
But the whole damn room
Keeps talking out loud

Talked over again
Talked over again
I had a whole thought
Now it’s gone with the wind
Talked over again
Talked over again
Hear me one time
Let me finish, my friend

Maybe you heard me
Maybe you didn’t
Maybe my piece
Was never permitted
I’m not shouting
I’m just in the chair
Give me the floor
I’m already there

Talked over again
Talked over again
I had a whole thought
Now it’s gone with the wind
Talked over again
Talked over again
Hear me one time

songs.subject: a folk song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
I was halfway under
On the blue line south
A glove in my pocket
A taste of old gum in my mouth
The rail kept rocking
Like a bottle on its side
And my heart hit the window
Then hid from my eyes

My knees went hollow
My hands turned stone
I counted the stations
Then lost the count alone

Hold my name, hold my name
Don’t let me go under
Hold my name, hold my name
Till this train rolls through
I am here, I am here
Even when I shudder
Hold my name, hold my name
I’m coming back to you

A school kid’s backpack
A man with a paper cup
A coat on my shoulder
I could not button up
The ads in the tunnel
Started bending at the edge
Like they knew my secret
And leaned in to the ledge

My throat went tight
My vision split
I wanted the door
I wanted out of it

Hold my name, hold my name
Don’t let me go under
Hold my name, hold my name
Till this train rolls through
I am here, I am here
Even when I shudder
Hold my name, hold my name
I’m coming back to you

At the next stop, I stepped onto the platform
Cold air hit my face like a hand
I sat on a bench by the vending machine
And waited for my feet to land
One breath, then another
One brick, then the sky
My body got tired of running
And finally answered why

Hold my name, hold my name
Don’t let me go under
Hold my name, hold my name
Till this train rolls through
I am here, I am here
Even when I shudder
Hold my name, hold my name
I’m coming back to you

songs.subject: a folk song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
My thumb won't stop its shake
On the cold black rail
The train rocks hard
And my coat feels nailed
Your face goes far
Then the floor comes near
I count the ads
Till my own breath disappears

I can hear the tunnel
Biting at the dark
Heart on the glass
Like a fist on bark

Hold on, hold on
I am here
Hold on, hold on
Let me clear
One small breath
Then one more breath
Hold on, hold on
I am here

A kid in a red cap
Licks rain from his sleeve
An old man sleeps
With a grocery bag of leaves
I try to count shoes
I try to count stops
But my thoughts keep slipping
Like coins in a box

The doors keep opening
Like a mouth in heat
I press my palm
To the plastic seat

Hold on, hold on
I am here
Hold on, hold on
Let me clear
One small breath
Then one more breath
Hold on, hold on
I am here

When the light goes green
And the train jerks back
I find my name
In the beat of the track
Not gone
Not broken
Just caught in the tide
Hands on my knees
Till the wave walks by

Hold on, hold on
I am here
Hold on, hold on
Let me clear
One small breath
Then one more breath
Hold on, hold on
I am here

At the next stop
I step into the air
And the world is small

songs.subject: a punk song about being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I show up on time
Got notes in my hand
Open my mouth
And you start your stand
I say one small thing
You wave it away
Talk right through me
Like I’m part of the chair

I raise my voice
You raise the room
I’m trying to speak
You bring the doom

Talk me over
Talk me over
Talk me over
I’m still here
Talk me over
Talk me over
I’m done with it
This year

Your slide deck spins
Your ego glows
I had a thought
But nobody knows
I wait my turn
Get cut in half
You steal the air
Then laugh, then laugh

I clear my throat
You clear the way
For every word
You want to take

Talk me over
Talk me over
Talk me over
I’m still here
Talk me over
Talk me over
I’m done with it
This year

No more nodding
No more nice
I’ve got a name
I’ve got a spine
Let me finish
Let me land
Try that again
I won’t stand

Talk me over
Talk me over
Talk me over
I’m still here
Talk me over
Talk me over
I’m done with it
This year
Talk me over
Talk me over
Not one more time
Not in here

songs.subject: a punk song about being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I clear my throat
You keep on going
I raise my hand
You keep on talking
My pen hits paper
I vanish in the stack
My name stays parked
Way in the back

I got a thought
But you cut the line
I see your mouth
Win every time

Talked over again
Talked over again
I’m right in the room
But you won’t let me in
Talked over again
Talked over again
I’m not disappearing
I’m just sick of this game
(talked over again)

Slide deck glowing
Under that fake white grin
You steal my point
Then write it in
I start the sentence
You steal the air
Like my best ideas
Aren’t already there

I got a plan
You call it “later”
You take the floor
I get quieter

Talked over again
Talked over again
I’m right in the room
But you won’t let me in
Talked over again
Talked over again
I’m not disappearing
I’m just sick of this game
(talked over again)

One beat
One breath
Let me finish
Let me speak
One beat
One breath
You keep stealing
I’m on my feet

Talked over again
Talked over again
I’m done with the smile
And the nod and the grin
Talked over again
Talked over again
Hear me the first time
Or watch me leave the room

songs.subject: a folk song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
At six he rolls the iron door
Pins in his teeth, back sore
Blue chalk marks on calico
Mrs. Lane needs Sunday clothes
He hems up cuffs for Mr. Pike
Makes overalls fit just right
Little Leon gets his first tie
And nobody asks him why

'Cause he knows where the seams go
Knows how a life should show
One clean stitch, one neat line
Makes a hard week look fine

He dresses the whole block
(Whole block)
Needle in his hand won't stop
He dresses the whole block
(Whole block)

From the baker to the bus stop

He kept a box of broken thread
Buttons green and buttons red
And if your pocket split at dawn
He'd have you mended by the lawn

'Cause he knows where the seams go

He dresses the whole block

songs.subject: a folk song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Down on Alder Street
In the narrow shop
Mr. Hem and Thread
Never lets the needle stop
Blue jeans for the boys
Black skirts for the choir
He can turn a thrift-store coat
Into something you admire

He knows every waist
Every cuff, every seam
Keeps a spare pin in his sleeve
And a bright-eyed grin

He dresses the whole block
Dresses the whole block
From Monday to the wedding day
He dresses the whole block
Dresses the whole block
(make it fit, make it stay)

Marta needs a hem
For her son’s first dance
Old Man Reyes wants
One more shine on his pants
Little Dee wants stars
Sewn into her vest
And the twins in Unit 4
Say his shoulders fit them best

He don’t ask for much
Just a cup and a chair
Three chalk lines, one careful cut
And a little time to spare

He dresses the whole block
Dresses the whole block
From Monday to the wedding day
He dresses the whole block
Dresses the whole block
(make it fit, make it stay)

At dusk he shuts the door
Brushes threads from his palm
Says every tear can mend
If you keep your hands calm
Then the block walks by
In patches, plaid, and gold
Looking like a million names
Held together by thread and soul

He dresses the whole block
Dresses the whole block
From Monday to the wedding day
He dresses the whole block
Dresses the whole block
(make it fit, make it stay)
He dresses the whole block
Dresses the whole block

songs.subject: a country song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Papa always said, "Girl, your path is clear"
Pointed to the north forty, said, "You'll build your life right here"
A white picket fence, a man with calloused hands
A future all mapped out across these dusty lands
But my heart felt a tug, a whisper in the breeze
Somethin' more than cottonwood trees

Dust on my boots, a map in my hand
Leavin' behind what was always planned
No fancy farewells, no teary goodbyes
Just the wide open road and the blue in the skies
This ain't no easy choice, Lord knows it's true
But some dreams just gotta bloom anew

Mama packed a lunch, a Bible, and a prayer
Said, "Don't forget your roots, child, no matter where"
The old Ford's engine coughed, a cloud of exhaust it blew
Leavin' tire tracks on the future I once knew
Felt a pang in my chest, a bittersweet release
Tradin' comfort for a little peace

That old farmhouse shrinkin' in the rearview mirror
Every mile I drive, the vision gets clearer

Dust on my boots, a map in my hand
Leavin' behind what was always planned
No fancy farewells, no teary goodbyes
Just the wide open road and the blue in the skies
This ain't no easy choice, Lord knows it's true
But some dreams just gotta bloom anew

They'll talk in the diner, down at the general store
Say I threw away a good life, askin' for somethin' more
Maybe they're right, maybe I'm a fool
But a caged bird can't sing, can't break free from the rule
I gotta find my own song, my own rhythm and rhyme
Even if it takes me 'til the end of time

songs.subject: a country song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama tied pearls on my neck
Daddy shook hands in the yard
They laid out a table for two
Like my life was already carved
Small-town names on a white cake
Same road, same porch, same ring
But my hands stayed cold in the lace
And my heart kept saying one thing

I watched that pen hit the page
Saw my whole life in ink
You can call it duty
But I can't watch my own dreams sink

I won't wear that borrowed ring
I won't live that planned-out thing
Let the papers burn, let the pasture go
I'm choosing my own road
I won't keep a fixed-up fate
I was born to break that gate
If they built my life in the dust and stone
I'm leaving it all alone

I packed my boots by the door
Left a note on the kitchen shelf
Said I'm not a prize to be passed
I'm a woman all by myself
The dawn hit red on the gravel
As I turned the truck around
Felt the weight of a thousand yeses
Drop like chains to the ground

I heard that house go quiet
Like it knew what I'd do
Some promises sound like cages
And I am done being used

I won't wear that borrowed ring
I won't live that planned-out thing
Let the papers burn, let the pasture go
I'm choosing my own road
I won't keep a fixed-up fate
I was born to break that gate
If they built my life in the dust and stone
I'm leaving it all alone

Maybe they'll call it heartbreak
Maybe they'll call it shame
But I'd rather taste the open air
Than answer to their name
One day I'll find a small white house
With my own songs in the hall
And nobody picking my future
Like apples from the wall

I won't wear that borrowed ring
I won't live that planned-out thing
Let the papers burn, let the pasture go
I'm choosing my own road
I won't keep a fixed-up fate
I was born to break that gate
If they built my life in the dust and stone

songs.lyrics:
We stand here now
So close
So near
My heart beats loud
(ooh-yeah!)
Can't hide the fear

The beat
It drops
We gotta move

Rival dance
Rival dance
Spinning 'round
No chance
Rival dance
Rival dance
Lose control
Lose control

Your eyes on me
I feel the heat
A symphony
Of dancing feet

The rhythm takes
Us to the floor

Rival dance
Rival dance
Spinning 'round
No chance
Rival dance
Rival dance
Lose control
Lose control

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You hate my grin
I hate your stare
Now spin that hand
Like you meant to care
Your jacket's sharp
Mine's all pinned-up pride
We step on glass
And still we glide
The room goes hush
When we get close
Your eyes say fight
Your feet say close

One, two, three
Don't trip on me
One, two, three
Come take the lead
Your jaw stays hard
My pulse stays loud
We move like sparks
Inside the crowd

Back to back, we dance
Back to back, we clash
Back to back, we dance
And we never look back
Back to back, we burn
Back to back, we turn
Back to back, we dance
Till the whole floor learns

You miss the cue
I steal the beat
Your heel catches mine
But we don't retreat
The bassline hits
Like a dare in the chest
You shove, I twirl
Then we do our best
Your hand on my waist
Feels like a threat
My laugh comes out
As a beat you won't forget

One, two, three
Don't break on me
One, two, three
Just keep the heat
You glare, I smirk
We trade that fire
Then hit that turn
Like a spark on wire

Back to back, we dance
Back to back, we clash
Back to back, we dance
And we never look back
Back to back, we burn
Back to back, we turn
Back to back, we dance
Till the whole floor learns

You almost say
What you won't say out loud
We miss the step
And laugh too loud
For one hard beat
We stop the war
Then hit the drop
Like we wanted more

Back to back, we dance
Back to back, we clash
Back to back, we dance
And we never look back
Back to back, we burn
Back to back, we turn
Back to back, we dance
Till the whole floor learns
Back to back, we dance
Back to back, we dance
Back to back, we dance

songs.subject: a ska song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Another holiday clocking in
The fluorescent lights a wicked grin
Sunrise through the window pane
Just another shift just more of the same

My feet are tired my coffee's cold
This hospital story getting old

Double time double pay
Another holiday gone away
Double time double pay
Saving lives every single day

Beeping machines a constant hum
Wishing for a beach wishing for some fun
Sweat on my brow a sterile smell
This crazy life I know so well

Families wait with anxious eyes
I tell them everything will be alright
A quick smile a gentle touch
This job it means so very much

Double time double pay
Another holiday gone away
Double time double pay
Saving lives every

songs.subject: a ska song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Badge on her scrubs
Steam in the hall
Coffee gone cold
But she still stands tall
Sixteen hours in
Holiday glow
She ties up her hair
And answers the call

Wheel squeak, chart flip
Monitor beeps
She checks one more room
Before she gets to sleep
New mom smiling
Kid with a fever
She says, "I got you"
Like it weighs less, easier

Double shift, holiday
Hands stay steady all day
Double shift, holiday
She keeps the panic away
Hey, hey, nurse in the light
Hey, hey, she’ll make it right
Double shift, holiday
She keeps the panic away

Room twelve is joking
Room eight is scared
Room three wants answers
She’s already there
Tape on the wristband
Ice chips, warm tea
She learns every name
Like they’re family

Hallway buzz
Midnight bends
She counts her steps
Then starts again
One more curtain
One more prayer
She smooths the blanket
Says, "I’m right here"

Double shift, holiday
Hands stay steady all day
Double shift, holiday
She keeps the panic away
Hey, hey, nurse in the light
Hey, hey, she’ll make it right
Double shift, holiday
She keeps the panic away

When the whole house sleeps
She’s still on her feet
Gum in her pocket
And a bruise on her knee
She laughs real quick
At the bad old joke
Then she lifts that chin
And gets back to work

Double shift, holiday
Hands stay steady all day
Double shift, holiday
She keeps the panic away
Hey, hey, nurse in the light
Hey, hey, she’ll make it right
Double shift, holiday

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
The fire was low
The whiskey lower still
Old man Finnegan
He just wouldn't quit
A stained deck of cards
A table scarred and worn
The wind howlin' outside
Like a child newly born

Mickey the blacksmith
His face a thundercloud
Young Liam
Pale as milk
Spoke not a word aloud
The stakes kept risin'
With each turn of the card
A farmer's last shilling
A future left unmarred

One more hand
They cried
Just one more hand

Oh
The hand that took too much
It did
The hand that took too much
It took the peace from every soul
And left us in the clutch

Finnegan
He smiled then
A serpent in his eye
He laid down his aces
Watched the hope just die
Liam pushed his chair back
A whisper
Cold and deep
"That's not how it goes
Old man
Secrets you don't keep."

A flick of the wrist
A glint of something sharp
The cards scattered
Blood on the harp
The fire crackled
A final
Hungry roar
No winner at that table
Just silence evermore

Pale as milk

With each turn of the card

A future left unmarred

One more hand
They cried
Just one more hand

Oh
The hand that took too much
It did
The hand that took too much
It took the peace from every soul
And left us in the clutch

Finnegan
He smiled then

Liam pushed his chair back

Cold and deep
"That's not how it goes

A flick of the wrist

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We lit the lamp by the laneway gate
With a tin cup of ale and a crooked stack
Old Seamus laughed, said, “Deal me in”
And I should have turned and taken that back
The first few hands were a friendly lie
Five hearts, two kings, and a grin of gold
But when the moon climbed up the hill
That little game turned sharp and cold

The stakes went high
The room went still
A ring of hands
Around the will

Too far, too far
We went too far
(too far)
Too far, too far
We went too far
And the night kept score

Paddy bet his watch, then his mother’s ring
Mairi laid down the keys to the croft
I wagered the mare with the white left eye
And Seamus just kept his gaze too soft
The cards came down like church bells struck
A blackened queen and a devil’s grin
When he swept the table clean at last
I heard the whole room breathe in

The fire burned low
The dogs went mute
No one spoke
No one moved

Too far, too far
We went too far
(too far)
Too far, too far
We went too far
And the night kept score

Then Seamus stood with the final hand
And he tapped the ash from his pipe to the floor
Said, “A game can feed you, a game can flay
But only fools come back for more”
I had my pride
I had my name
Both gone thin
In the lantern flame

Too far, too far
We went too far
(too far)
Too far, too far
We went too far
Now the dawn knows who we are
Too far, too far
We went too far

songs.subject: a swing song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Sunday came in through the screen
With a crack and a golden gleam
There was flour on the table
And peas in the enamel bowl
Butter melted in the pan
Like it knew just where it ran
And that old linoleum floor
Sang under my shoes once more

Oh, the air changed shape
When the latch gave way
First sweet, then sharp
Then it wrapped my heart

That smell in Grandma’s kitchen
(Grandma’s kitchen)
Pepper, grease, and biscuit
(Grandma’s kitchen)
It held me like a hymn
And pulled me back again
That smell in Grandma’s kitchen
(Grandma’s kitchen)

Cinnamon in a coffee can
Tin foil by her hand
A pot lid would tremble
When the beans began to sing
She’d laugh with a wooden spoon
As the window let in June
And the whole house leaned in close
For the secrets on the stove

Oh, the air changed shape
When she said, “Come taste”
A little smoke
A little grace

That smell in Grandma’s kitchen
(Grandma’s kitchen)
Pepper, grease, and biscuit
(Grandma’s kitchen)
It held me like a hymn
And pulled me back again
That smell in Grandma’s kitchen
(Grandma’s kitchen)

Now I catch it in a raincoat sleeve
In a thrift shop on the east side street
In the crust of bread
In the end of a day
And I’m eight years old
On the backscreen porch again

That smell in Grandma’s kitchen
(Grandma’s kitchen)
Pepper, grease, and biscuit
(Grandma’s kitchen)
It held me like a hymn
And pulled me back again
That smell in Grandma’s kitchen

Shoo-be-doo, I’m home
Shoo-be-doo, I’m home

With a crack and a golden gleam
There was flour on the table

And that old linoleum floor

Oh, the air changed shape

First sweet, then sharp
Then it wrapped my heart

songs.subject: a swing song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
You open the door, and there it goes
Sweet onion, butter, cinnamon rolls
A big old pot on a low flame hum
And the whole wide house says, here I come

Pepper in the air
Sage on the pan
Your apron sway
And I’m a little kid again

That grandma smell, that grandma smell
(That grandma smell)
Rosemary, sugar, and Sunday spells
(Oh, that grandma smell)
I breathe it in, I’m home so fast
Like every worry slips right past
That grandma smell, that grandma smell
(That grandma smell)

Carrots in a bowl, peas on the plate
Glass jar beans and a dented yellow crate
Tea in the cup and a tin can click
You stir that spoon like it knows the trick

Steam on the glass
Flour on your hand
You smile at me
And I’m a little kid again

That grandma smell, that grandma smell
(That grandma smell)
Rosemary, sugar, and Sunday spells
(Oh, that grandma smell)
I breathe it in, I’m home so fast
Like every worry slips right past
That grandma smell, that grandma smell
(That grandma smell)

When the rain hit hard and the world felt cold
You had a way with a pot of gold
One deep inhale and the room stood still
Like love had a name, and it lived on the sill

That grandma smell, that grandma smell
(That grandma smell)
Rosemary, sugar, and Sunday spells
(Oh, that grandma smell)
I breathe it in, I’m home so fast
Like every worry slips right past
That grandma smell, that grandma smell

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I was raised by a road map
And a hat pulled low
Mama said, "Boy, where you running?"
I said, "I don't know"
Got a truck full of weather
And a tank that won't stay full
If the sky starts growling
I can feel it in my bones

Last spring in Elk County
I saw that funnel turn
Red dirt in the headlights
And the whole world burn
I should've gone home then
Put my boots by the door
But the wind kept calling
Like it had before

You can lock that gate
You can board that room
But the thunder in me
Still hears the boom

I can't settle down
I can't settle down
Got a storm in my blood
And a drift in my crown
I can't settle down
I can't settle down
When the dark clouds roll
I go chasing them down

There's a girl back in Tulsa
With a ring in a drawer
Said, "Choose me or the highway"
I was gone by four
Now the porch light's fading
And the mailbox leans
I keep leaving love
For the edge of the scene

You can call my name
From the back step porch
But my heart runs hard
When it smells that storm

I can't settle down
I can't settle down
Got a storm in my blood
And a drift in my crown
I can't settle down
I can't settle down
When the dark clouds roll
I go chasing them down

One of these mornings
I'll get still, I swear
Plant my heels in clay
And breathe that summer air
But the sky turns black
And my old truck hums
I was born for the running
Till the running's done

I can't settle down
I can't settle down
Got a storm in my blood
And a drift in my crown
I can't settle down
I can't settle down
When the dark clouds roll
I go chasing them down

Mud on my boots
Dust in my veins
One hand on the wheel
One eye on the rain

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I keep a map on the dash
Creased from county lines
Coffee in a paper cup
And hail in my spine
When the sky goes green and mean
I can’t stay still
I hear that siren in my bones
And I always will

Mama said, “Boy, sit down”
Let the floorboards hold you
But the west wind hit that porch
And it called me like it knew me

Can’t settle down
Can’t settle down
When the clouds roll low
I’m gone from town
Can’t settle down
Can’t settle down
Mud on my boots
And my name in the sound

I’ve slept in rest stop chairs
With the radar glow
Seen a barn turn silver
In a hard white snow
Met a girl in a feed store
With a laugh like rain
She said, “You ever stay?”
I said, “I can’t explain”

She traced my palm with her thumb
Said, “You’re always running”
But thunder cracked the tin roof
And I was already coming

Can’t settle down
Can’t settle down
When the clouds roll low
I’m gone from town
Can’t settle down
Can’t settle down
Mud on my boots
And my name in the sound

One day maybe I’ll wake up
With a fence to mend
Maybe pour my boots out
And let the road end
But when that low sky leans in
And the fields turn blue
I’ll be back out chasing
Like I was born to do

Can’t settle down
Can’t settle down
When the clouds roll low
I’m gone from town
Can’t settle down
Can’t settle down
Mud on my boots
And my name in the sound
Mud on my boots

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
I found you by the bus stop cracks
One green hand in a world of tracks
Roots in the dust where the tiles were laid
You held your breath in the heat that stayed
Brick by brick, they swallowed the yard
Painted over the apple tree scar
Now kids drag carts through the parking lot
And you still stand where the sparrows sat

Tell me how
You’re still here
In the soot
In the grit
Tell me how
You keep your crown
When the whole town
Keeps coming down

Last tree standing, stay
Last tree standing, pray
I keep your shade in my chest all day
Last tree standing, stay
Last tree standing, stay
You’re the green in the gray

I saw your bark with the names carved deep
Old love notes from the block’s old sleep
The barber’s boy with a pocket knife
The baker’s girl when the hill was live
Now the windows glare, and the fences bite
Cats on the hood and the dust at night
Still your leaves make a soft green sound
Like a promise nobody paved down

Tell me how
You’re still here
In the soot
In the grit
Tell me how
You keep your crown
When the whole town
Keeps coming down

Last tree standing, stay
Last tree standing, pray
I keep your shade in my chest all day
Last tree standing, stay
Last tree standing, stay
You’re the green in the gray

If they cut you, I’ll call it theft
Of every breath that this place has left
I’ll stand with you when the trucks roll by
One slim prayer under a blank white sky

Last tree standing, stay
Last tree standing, stay
I need your shade in this hard-baked day
Last tree standing, stay
Last tree standing, stay
You’re the green in the gray

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hum a buzzing tune
No green thing beneath the moon
Just asphalt rivers running black
No turning back

One green whisper
A lonely flicker

Last tree standing tall
Against the concrete wall
A rebel leaf a stubborn root
Sweet forbidden fruit
Last tree standing now
How how how

They paved paradise
Put up a parking lot twice
My old boots know every crack
No going back

Wind sighs through it
A silent poet

Last tree standing tall
Against the concrete wall
A rebel leaf a stubborn root
Sweet forbidden fruit
Last tree standing now

songs.subject: a synthwave song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I turned off Cedar Lane
Past the laundromat sign
Your window was dark
But I knew that line
Same cracked mailbox
Same cheap welcome mat
I slowed like a fool
Then I looked back

My hands on the wheel
Heart in a spin
The balcony railing
Still bent in
I almost called out
To nobody there
Like your name might answer
From the air

Drive by, drive by
Old apartment glow
Drive by, drive by
I don’t let go
(hold on, hold on)
Drive by, drive by
Through the afterglow
Drive by, drive by
I know where it goes

There’s a new dent
In the blue front gate
A trash bag flaps
Like it can’t stay
I see your old plant
In the second-floor pane
Or maybe I want
What used to remain

The stairwell light
Still flickers low
Like it remembers
How we’d go
Two keys, one lock
One worn-down dream
Now the night just cuts
Through the in-between

Drive by, drive by
Old apartment glow
Drive by, drive by
I don’t let go
(hold on, hold on)
Drive by, drive by
Through the afterglow
Drive by, drive by
I know where it goes

And I don’t need
To climb those stairs
I just need one look
To prove you were there
The road keeps rolling
I let it take me
Past what I lost
And what made me

Drive by, drive by
Old apartment glow
Drive by, drive by
I don’t let go
(hold on, hold on)
Drive by, drive by
Through the afterglow
Drive by, drive by
I know where it goes
(know where it goes)
Drive by, drive by
Let the blue world show

songs.subject: a synthwave song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hum a sleepy tune
Chrome reflects a fading moon
Ghost of you in every pane
Whispering my lonely name

Engine sighs a quiet plea
Don't look back just let it be

Past your window past your door
Don't live here anymore
Neon heartbreak burning bright
Driving through the lonely night

Shadows dance on vacant walls
Hear your laughter hear your calls
Every brick a memory
Haunting me relentlessly

Used to park right over there
Kissed you in the cool night air

songs.subject: a tango song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Your hands are twelve and two
Like you read it in a book
I say keep your eyes ahead
Don’t let the mirror hook
You laugh at my left hand
On the wheel like a guard
But I was shaking too
On my first day in the yard

Ease the clutch, breathe slow
Give it room, let it roll
If the world leans hard
You lean soft, hold the road

Go on, daughter, go
(Go on, daughter, go)
Easy on the brake
Easy on the brake
Go on, daughter, go
(Go on, daughter, go)
Steady in the lane
Steady in the lane

We creep past the bakery
The baker lifts his head
You wave like you own the sun
Then stall at the light in red
Your grin hits the windshield
Like a coin in my chest
I say no shame in slow
You’re doing your best

Check your turn, then turn
Let the signal speak first
Keep your palms loose now
Don’t let fear take the first

Go on, daughter, go
(Go on, daughter, go)
Easy on the brake
Easy on the brake
Go on, daughter, go
(Go on, daughter, go)
Steady in the lane
Steady in the lane

At the edge of the park
You stop and look at me
I see my own old nerves
In your knees and your seat
Then you tap the gas
And the car moves clean
Like a secret passed
From your heart to me

Go on, daughter, go
(Go on, daughter, go)
Easy on the brake
Easy on the brake
Go on, daughter, go
(Go on, daughter, go)
Steady in the lane
Steady in the lane
Go on, daughter, go
(Go on, daughter, go)
I’m right here, all right
I’m right here, all right

songs.subject: a tango song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
I fasten your seat belt slow
Your hands on the wheel, both small
You laugh at the blinking light
I say, "Easy now, that's all"
The curb looks mean tonight
The mirror keeps showing fear
But I was just like you
First time with the gear

Left foot soft
Right foot brave
Hear that idle?
Feel the wave
Don't chase the horn
Don't rush the flame
Just look ahead
And say your name

Easy, hija, easy
(hold it, hold it)
Easy, hija, easy
(roll it, roll it)
One more inch
Then breathe
Easy, hija, easy
You're teaching me

You stall at the corner stop
Then grin like you meant to test
I tap the dash with two knuckles
And laugh, "That was your best"
A bread van blocks our lane
A dog cuts through the rain
You learn the shape of space
By meeting it again

Clutch to heart
Slow, then go
Little by little
You learn the road
No shame in pause
No prize in speed
The world bends open
When you watch it breathe

Easy, hija, easy
(hold it, hold it)
Easy, hija, easy
(roll it, roll it)
One more inch
Then breathe
Easy, hija, easy
You're teaching me

At the red light, you ask me why
My voice still shakes when cars go by
I say, "Because I love you hard
Because I know what's out there"
You turn the wheel
You find the lane
And suddenly
I feel your name

Easy, hija, easy
(hold it, hold it)
Easy, hija, easy
(roll it, roll it)
One more inch
Then breathe
Easy, hija, easy
You're teaching me

Park it close
Cut the key
Night leans in
And lets us be
You smile like dawn
I blink and see
The child I drove

Slow, then go
Little by little
You learn the road
No shame in pause
No prize in speed
The world bends open
When you watch it breathe

Easy, hija, easy
(hold it, hold it)
Easy, hija, easy
(roll it, roll it)
One more inch
Then breathe
Easy, hija, easy
You're teaching me

At the red light, you ask me why
My voice still shakes when cars go by
I say, "Because I love you hard
Because I know what's out there"
You turn the wheel
You find the lane
And suddenly
I feel your name

Easy, hija, easy
(hold it, hold it)
Easy, hija, easy
(roll it, roll it)
One more inch
Then breathe
Easy, hija, easy
You're teaching me

Park it close
Cut the key
Night leans in
And lets us be
You smile like dawn
I blink and see
The child I drove

songs.subject: a R&B song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
You clock me at the door
Name tag hanging low
I’m pacing Marble Hall
Where the old frames glow
Midnight on the tile
And it’s just me and you
You in that gold dress
Looking like you knew

I dust off your frame
Like I’m handling truth
Every little crack
Feels like it’s moving through
The tourists all day
Never saw you this way
But when the room goes still
You start to sway

And I swear, when the hall gets quiet
Your eyes keep finding mine
I pretend it’s just the shadows
But baby, that’s a sign

Museum guard
You got me on watch
Museum guard
I can’t leave this spot
That painting talks
And it says your name
Museum guard
I’m never the same

I lean by the rope line
You lean out the gold
A hundred years in oil
Still you feel so bold
I know I’m supposed to stay
All cool and slow
But every time I pass
I let my guard go

And I swear, when the hall gets quiet
Your eyes keep finding mine
I pretend it’s just the shadows
But baby, that’s a sign

Museum guard
You got me on watch
Museum guard
I can’t leave this spot
That painting talks
And it says your name
Museum guard
I’m never the same

If they ever ask me
Why I stayed so late
I’ll say I found a heartbeat
Under all that paint
You held me in your silence
Soft and out of reach
Now every room in this old place
Feels like it’s next to me

Museum guard
You got me on watch
Museum guard
I can’t leave this spot
That painting talks
And it says your name
Museum guard
I’m never the same
Museum guard
Stay right in my sight
Museum guard
Till the end of night
That painting talks
And it says your name
Museum guard
I’m never the same

songs.subject: a R&B song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
I clock in with the keys on my hip
Polished floor, same quiet drip
You hang there bold in a gold frame
Red on your mouth like a live flame
I know your name by the little brass plaque
Every night I look, then I look back
You got that stare like you know my sins
Like you caught me breathing through my grin

And the room gets still when I pass your side
Moon through glass on the white stone floor
I should walk on by
But I stay a little more

Guard me like that
Paint me like that
Say my name slow
Hold me like that
(hold me like that)
Guard me like that
Paint me like that
I lose my cool
When you look like that

Last Tuesday, rain tapped the front door
You were glowing darker than before
The tourists left, the hall went bare
Just me and your eyes in the cooling air
I traced your dress with a flashlight beam
Caught a little gold where the varnish gleamed
If I stayed too long, would you mind my stare?
Girl, you got me trapped in a velvet chair

And the room gets still when I pass your side
Moon through glass on the white stone floor
I should walk on by
But I stay a little more

Guard me like that
Paint me like that
Say my name slow
Hold me like that
(hold me like that)
Guard me like that
Paint me like that
I lose my cool
When you look like that

I know you're oil and frame
Still you feel like a pulse
Every lonely night
You pull me back enough
If this is just a dream
Let the morning wait
Let the morning wait

Guard me like that
Paint me like that
Say my name slow
Hold me like that
(hold me like that)
Guard me like that
Paint me like that
I lose my cool
When you look like that
(Paint me like that)

songs.subject: a gospel song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The coffee’s black at midnight
The door still swings both ways
A tired cook in a white cap
Keeps the griddle hot for days
There’s a booth by the window
Where the weary come and sit
And every broken story
Gets a plate before it quits

When the rain taps hard on the glass
And the road feels long and cold
I hear a mercy in that place
Better than silver, better than gold

Open all night, open all night
Grace still shines in that diner light
Open all night, open all night
He feeds the lost and holds them tight (holds them tight)
Open all night, open all night
Come as you are, He makes it right

Truckers with their double shifts
Mothers with their red-eyed sons
A preacher with a paper cup
And a kid who missed the bus
The jukebox hums a soft amen
While the syrup pours so slow
And every cup of something warm
Says you’re not alone

When my hands shake from the day
And my prayers get weak and small
That little place on Highway Nine
Feels like a church with a papered wall

Open all night, open all night
Grace still shines in that diner light
Open all night, open all night
He feeds the lost and holds them tight (holds them tight)
Open all night, open all night
Come as you are, He makes it right

And if my heart is empty
If my strength has slipped away
I’ll pull into that promise
Where the broken folks still stay
There’s a blessing in the biscuits
There’s a shout in every cup
The night can’t keep me from Him
When mercy opens up

Open all night, open all night
Grace still shines in that diner light
Open all night, open all night
He feeds the lost and holds them tight (holds them tight)
Open all night, open all night
Come as you are, He makes it right
Open all night, open all night
That table’s set, and love won’t tire (love won’t tire)

songs.subject: a gospel song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Marlene flips that sign at ten
But mercy stays awake
Coffee pots and biscuit steam
And a prayer for every plate
Old men, truckers, tired moms
Find a seat and catch their breath
In that little vinyl booth
We taste a kindness we can’t forget

And when the night runs long
You keep the light on
When the road cuts deep
You keep the light on

Keep the light on
Keep the light on
Marlene’s all night
Marlene’s all night
Bread still rising
Hope still coming
Marlene’s all night
Marlene’s all night

Grease-sweet taters, cherry pie
Paper cup in trembling hands
Two more dollars in the jar
For the fatherless and damned
Janey hums with apron strings
While the preacher takes a bite
Says this table feels like grace
In the middle of the night

And when the storm rolls in
You keep the light on
For the weary hearts
You keep the light on

Keep the light on
Keep the light on
Marlene’s all night
Marlene’s all night
Bread still rising
Hope still coming
Marlene’s all night
Marlene’s all night

Some doors close hard
Some hearts get cold
But this place says, come on in
And don’t let go
Hands up, tears down
We sing it plain
There’s room for the broken
There’s room in Your name

Keep the light on
Keep the light on
Marlene’s all night
Marlene’s all night
Lost get found here
Fear gets quiet
Marlene’s all night
Marlene’s all night
Keep the light on
Keep the light on
Amen in the booth

songs.subject: a funk song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We left the dock with steel in the hold
Sixteen crates and a story untold
Seals on the wood, black wax and rope
Captain said, “Brother, just stay on hope”
First night calm, then the mast gave a groan
Dice in my bunk and a bone in my throat
Saw my name scratched into the salt
Like the sea itself knew what was locked

Ooh, the lantern shook
Ooh, the boards went cold
Every man on deck
Heard that cargo moan

Cursed cargo, roll on by
Cursed cargo, black tide high
If it calls your name, don’t look
If it opens up, you’re took
Cursed cargo, roll on by
Cursed cargo, don’t ask why

By day four, the fish went gray
By day five, the crew wouldn’t play
Cards on the rail, hands like lead
One bloke swore he heard footsteps in the spread
We pried one crate, just a sliver, a crack
Saw a gold tooth grin in the darkened black
Then the sea went hard, like a fist on glass
And the youngest deckhand vanished fast

Ooh, the lantern shook
Ooh, the boards went cold
Every man on deck
Heard that cargo moan

Cursed cargo, roll on by
Cursed cargo, black tide high
If it calls your name, don’t look
If it opens up, you’re took
Cursed cargo, roll on by
Cursed cargo, don’t ask why

Captain tied the keys to his wrist that night
Said, “I paid in blood, and I paid in spite”
Then the moon came down on a wave of ink
And the whole damn hold started talking back at him

Cursed cargo, roll on by
Cursed cargo, black tide high
If it calls your name, don’t look
If it opens up, you’re took
Cursed cargo, roll on by
Cursed cargo, don’t ask why
Cursed cargo, seal the lid
Cursed cargo, let it live

songs.subject: a funk song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up at dawn
With a crate marked black and cold
Salt on the planks
And a name we weren’t told
Old man said, "Don’t peek"
But the latch kept twitching loose
Every rat in the hold
Got a limp by the noon

It rattled like teeth
In a fevered jaw
Crewboys crossed themselves
When the lanterns crawled

Black salt cargo
Heavy on the soul
Black salt cargo
Won’t let the ship go
(Black salt cargo)
(Black salt cargo)

Seventh night at sea
The moon went dirty red
Tommy heard his mother
Calling from the dead
Maggots in the grain
Gold turning into rust
A velvet voice in the dark
Said, "Feed me your trust"

The compass spun slow
Like a drunk on the floor
And the sea kept knocking
On the iron door

Black salt cargo
Heavy on the soul
Black salt cargo
Won’t let the ship go
(Black salt cargo)
(Black salt cargo)

We cut the seals
The air came out like smoke
Every rope on deck
Started learning how to choke
Captain laughed one laugh
Then he aged in place
Now the waves take orders
From the crate’s cold face

Black salt cargo
Heavy on the soul
Black salt cargo
Won’t let the ship go
(Black salt cargo)
(Black salt cargo)

Turn the wheel, my brother
Pray the gulls stay near
Black salt cargo

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Dust on my boots
still from the road
Your porch light burns
like it knows my code
I kept your photo
in my jacket seam
Counted every mile
just to hold that dream

I was gone too long
too long, too long
Now I hear your name
and my chest goes strong
Step by step
I’m crossing back
Got my heart in hand
and my world on track

I’m coming home
(I’m coming home)
To your front door
(To your front door)
I’m coming home
(I’m coming home)
Baby, I’m yours
Baby, I’m yours

Metal in my ears
fading with the rain
I left with the fear
came back with the pain
But your voice on the line
kept me alive
Said “walk me back”
and I learned to survive

I was gone too long
too long, too long
Now I hear your name
and my chest goes strong
Step by step
I’m crossing back
Got my heart in hand
and my world on track

I’m coming home
(I’m coming home)
To your front door
(To your front door)
I’m coming home
(I’m coming home)
Baby, I’m yours
Baby, I’m yours

No more dawn alarms
No more long goodbyes
Just your hands on me
and my tear-wet eyes
If I shake a little
hold me real tight
I made it back to you
I made it back tonight

I’m coming home
(I’m coming home)
To your front door
(To your front door)
I’m coming home
(I’m coming home)
Baby, I’m yours
Baby, I’m yours

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Volví con la mochila rota
y el nombre de mi barrio en la boca
la puerta lloró al abrir
mamá me miró sin hablar
Traigo polvo en la camisa
y una flor seca en la mano
tu foto pegada al pecho
me salvó del frío

Y ahora piso mi calle
como quien vuelve a nacer
las sombras quedan lejos
ya te quiero ver

Estoy de vuelta, de vuelta
mi casa me llama fuerte
Estoy de vuelta, de vuelta
ya no me quita la suerte
(Estoy de vuelta)
(Estoy de vuelta)

La mesa sigue en su sitio
pero falta tanta voz
los vecinos hacen fiesta
cuando oyen mi motor
Yo dejé la rabia afuera
junto al casco y la sal
hoy me curan tus abrazos
y el pan sobre la mesa

Y ahora piso mi calle
como quien vuelve a nacer
las sombras quedan lejos
ya te quiero ver

Estoy de vuelta, de vuelta
mi casa me llama fuerte
Estoy de vuelta, de vuelta
ya no me quita la suerte
(Estoy de vuelta)
(Estoy de vuelta)

Si preguntan por mis noches
diles que aprendí a volver
que la guerra se quedó sola
yo elegí mi piel

Estoy de vuelta, de vuelta
mi casa me llama fuerte
Estoy de vuelta, de vuelta
ya no me quita la suerte
(Estoy de vuelta)

songs.subject: a blues song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man at the pier by dawn
Palm like driftwood, thumb worn down
He pointed where the water curved
Said, “Baby, that river still got nerve”
He drew me lines in the sand
Said, “Don’t fight it with your hands”
“If you want to know where to go
Watch how the blue roads pull and roll”

He said, “Every pull got a reason”
“Every fall got a name”
“Learn the grace in the leaving”
“And you’ll ride the same”

Teach me the tide, old man
Teach me the tide
(teach me the tide)
Teach me the bend and the slide
(teach me the tide)
Don’t stand stiff on the shore
You gotta move with the roar
Teach me the tide, old man
Teach me the tide

He showed me crab tracks in the mud
How they vanish, how they flood
Said, “That’s a lesson in plain sight
Hold on too hard and you lose the light”
He laughed when the gulls cut low
Said, “There’s a time to stay, a time to go”
“Even your sorrow got a pace
Let it wash, then lift your face”

He said, “Hunger don’t last forever”
“Nor does a hard black sky”
“Listen close to the turning”
“And let it answer why”

Teach me the tide, old man
Teach me the tide
(teach me the tide)
Teach me the bend and the slide
(teach me the tide)
Don’t stand stiff on the shore
You gotta move with the roar
Teach me the tide, old man
Teach me the tide

Now the dock groans under my boots
I hear his voice in the roots
When the moon drags silver through
I know what the water’s trying to do
It takes, then it gives back
Leaves a shell and a colored track
So when the world pulls out too far
I keep my feet where the deep things are

Teach me the tide, old man
Teach me the tide
(teach me the tide)
Teach me the bend and the slide
(teach me the tide)
If I fall, I’ll rise again
With the moon and the rain
Teach me the tide, old man
Teach me the tide

songs.subject: a blues song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Down by the pilings
where the blue crab bed,
old man Jonah sat me
on a splintered red plank.
He said, "Watch that water
like you watch a liar.
It takes what it's owed,
then it gives you back."
He drew in the mud
with a broken cane,
two little curves
and a line through rain.
"Son, this world keeps turning
when your feet feel stuck.
Learn the tide's old habit,
you'll learn how to walk."

When it pulls away,
don't chase what went.
When it leans back in,
hold your ground, then

The tide comes back
The tide comes back
(comes back)
It takes, it gives
then it comes back
(old man said)
The tide comes back
The tide comes back
So let it move
and don't fight back

He showed me the oyster
with its bruised-up shell,
said, "Open slow,
you'll do better well."
He showed me the net
with the torn-up thread,
"Patch what you can
and leave what bled."
He pointed at gulls
circling low and wild,
"Even hard rain
still rocks a child."
Then he laughed that laugh
like a rusted bell,
"Sea don't keep promises,
just stories it tells."

When it pulls away,
don't curse the shore.
When it leans back in,
you'll be rich in more

The tide comes back
The tide comes back
(comes back)
It takes, it gives
then it comes back
(old man said)
The tide comes back
The tide comes back
So let it move
and don't fight back

Now Jonah's gone
and the bench is bare,
but I hear his voice
in the briney air.
When I lose my way
and the night runs deep,
I watch that water
while it teaches me

The tide comes back
The tide comes back
(comes back)
It takes, it gives
then it comes back
(old man said)
The tide comes back
The tide comes back
So let it move
and don't fight back

Down by the pilings
I stand and learn,
every low water

songs.subject: a synthwave song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The break room clock says six
But I’m still on my feet
Paper cup of burnt-up coffee
And a turkey tray I can’t eat
Hallway smells like bleach
Pine soap on the floor
You got a fever in room twelve
And I’m already at fourteen more

If I lean, I might fall
So I straighten up tall
One more chart, one more call
I can handle it all

Double shift holiday
I’m still here, I stay
Double shift holiday
Hold on, I’ll find a way
Take my name, take the night
I keep the room in light
Double shift holiday
I’m still here, I stay

Got a crumpled gift tag
Stuck in my scrub top seam
My sister sent a message
And I haven’t seen the screen
Kids on the ward want cocoa
Old man wants one more song
I smile through the long hours
Like I’ve been strong all along

If I lean, I might fall
So I straighten up tall
One more chart, one more call
I can handle it all

Double shift holiday
I’m still here, I stay
Double shift holiday
Hold on, I’ll find a way
Take my name, take the night
I keep the room in light
Double shift holiday
I’m still here, I stay

Somewhere there’s a table
With a chair saved for me
But this building needs my hands
And that’s where I need to be
I hear the distant laughter
Through the automatic door
I pack it in my pocket
And come back for more

Double shift holiday
I’m still here, I stay
Double shift holiday
I’ll get home someday
Take my name, take the night
I keep the room in light
Double shift holiday
I’m still here, I stay

songs.subject: a synthwave song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Badge on her chest, red pen in her hair
Turkey in the break room, but she’s not there
Hallway carts rolling, slippers on tile
She checks every room with a tired half-smile
Phone in her pocket, it keeps going off
Family sends pictures, she’s staring them off
Hands full of charts, eyes dry but awake
She says, “I’ll be home when the candles all break”

One more call, one more bed
One more name she hasn’t met
She breathes in, counts to ten
Then she walks back out again

Holiday double
Holiday double
She keeps the room alive
Holiday double
Holiday double
She works while they arrive

Coffee gone cold in a paper cup
She fixes a pillow, tucks the blanket up
Snow on the windows, watch on her wrist
She missed the toast, but she made the list
A little kid asks, “Will you stay a while?”
She says, “I’m right here,” with that exhausted smile
The ward feels huge when the night runs long
But she hums the line from a half-remembered song

One more call, one more bed
One more name she hasn’t met
She breathes in, counts to ten
Then she walks back out again

Holiday double
Holiday double
She keeps the room alive
Holiday double
Holiday double
She works while they arrive

When the family calls, she steps into the hall
Leaning on the vending-machine wall
A minute for her name, a minute for the ache
Then she squares her shoulders and goes back to work again

Holiday double
Holiday double
She keeps the room alive
Holiday double
Holiday double

songs.subject: a americana song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hummin a low tune
Coffee cold as the pale moon
Books spread wide like a broken fan
Two AM and I'm still the man

Brain cells fizzin
A quiet prison

Oh midnight oil burner
Another lonely learner
Yeah midnight oil burner
Will I ever be a earner

The dog snores soft in the hallway
Wishing I was dreaming away
Equations dance on the page
A different kind of stage

Maybe tomorrow the sun will shine
Maybe tomorrow these answers are mine
Just

Brain cells fizzin
A quiet prison

Oh midnight oil burner
Another lonely learner
Yeah midnight oil burner
Will I ever be a earner

The dog snores soft in the hallway
Wishing I was dreaming away
Equations dance on the page
A different kind of stage

Maybe tomorrow the sun will shine
Maybe tomorrow these answers are mine
Just

songs.subject: a americana song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp hums low on the page
Stack of notes and a blue-ring mug
My pen keeps time on the margins
While the whole house sleeps it off
I read the same line twice
Then I read it one more time
Tryin' to keep my eyes awake
At two in the mornin' line

The clock ticks loud in the kitchen
My name feels far away
But I can taste that tomorrow
If I stay

Two in the mornin'
I won't fold now
Two in the mornin'
I'm still around
(page by page)
I lean in close
Two in the mornin'
And I'm learnin' it slow

Rain taps soft on the window
Keys and coins in a saucer dish
My highlighter slips through the chapter
Like a match on a stubborn wick
I write down what I can't hold
In the folds of a yellow sheet
Then I stand up and stretch out
Like the floor might give me heat

The clock ticks loud in the kitchen
My name feels far away
But I can taste that tomorrow
If I stay

Two in the mornin'
I won't fold now
Two in the mornin'
I'm still around
(page by page)
I lean in close
Two in the mornin'
And I'm learnin' it slow

When the house goes still like timber
And my eyelids start to fall
I hear my mama say, keep goin'
Even if it's only one more line
So I mark the place and breathe deep
Take the page back in my hands
This is work and this is wanting
This is how I make my stand

Two in the mornin'
I won't fold now
Two in the mornin'
I'm still around
(page by page)
I lean in close
Two in the mornin'

songs.subject: a funk song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Used to walk with a swagger
Head held high
Baby (oo-yeah!)
Thought I knew it all
Every single why
Maybe
Sun shinin' bright
No cloud in the sky
No doubt
Then the rain started fallin'
Started to wipe me out (uh-huh)
Lost my groove
Lost my step
Lost my way
What can I say?
Felt like the music just faded
Faded to gray
Day after day

Oh
The funk was gone
Gone
Gone
Couldn't find my beat
Searching for that rhythm
Dancin' in the street
But the funk is back
Yeah
The funk is back in town
Pickin' up the pieces
Wearin' my funky crown! (Gotta get down!)

Tried to fake it
Tried to front
Tried to pretend (no way!)
But a soul without a rhythm
Baby
Can't truly mend
Felt like a record scratch
A broken-down machine
Lost that good vibration
If you know what I mean (you dig?)
Thought my spirit was busted
Flat on the floor
Didn't think I could boogie
Not anymore
No more

Oh
The funk was gone
Gone
Gone
Couldn't find my beat
Searching for that rhythm
Dancin' in the street
But the funk is back
Yeah
The funk is back in town
Pickin' up the pieces
Wearin' my funky crown! (Feel that sound!)

Then a little whisper
A bassline in my ear
Said "Don't you give up
Baby
Don't you shed a tear" (naw, naw)
Felt a rumble in my soul
A little kick drum beat
Suddenly my feet started movin'
Couldn't be beat! (Can't be beat!)
The light came shinin'
A groove so strong and true
Woke me up
Shook me up
Made me brand new!

Oh
The funk was gone
Gone
Gone
Couldn't find my beat
Searching for that rhythm
Dancin' in the street
But the funk is back
Yeah
The funk is back in town
Pickin' up the pieces
Wearin' my funky crown! (Get on down!)

songs.subject: a funk song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I lost my little flame
In a box of parking tickets
Pocket full of “maybe”
And a mouth full of guilt
I wore my good intentions
Like a shirt with missing buttons
Smiled at Sunday faces
Then I split by the back door

I said, “Lord, if You’re near
Why’s my chest feel so empty?”
But the night kept answering
With my own hard heartbeat

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Right by the sink, in the dark
I found my faith
I found my faith
In the crack of a stubborn heart
(oh-oh)
I found my faith
I found my faith

Met it at the gas pump
On a rain-stained Tuesday
Lady with a paper cup
Said, “You still got breath”
I saw it in a kid’s laugh
In a busted church fan turning
In the old bruise of my name
When I said, “I’m still here”

I said, “Maybe I’m too late
Maybe I used it up”
Then a small voice in my bones said
“Get up, get up”

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Right by the sink, in the dark
I found my faith
I found my faith
In the crack of a stubborn heart
(hey-hey)
I found my faith
I found my faith

I was out on the edge
With my hands full of ashes
But the ground kept my secret
And the mercy kept passing
No sermon, no spotlight
Just a door left open
Just a name in the silence
Calling me home again

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Right by the sink, in the dark
I found my faith
I found my faith
In the crack of a stubborn heart
(oh-oh)
I found my faith
I found my faith

Yeah, I found it again
Yeah, I found it again

songs.subject: a hardcore song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You wore red, I wore black
Two hard heads on the same track
Last one standing, that was the vow
Now they made us circle up now
Heel to heel, jaw to jaw
Crowd wants blood, we play it raw
Your hand in mine, my grip like vice
We hate this song, but we own this night

Say my name like a threat
I step in close, you don't forget
One wrong move, one tight spin
We were born to break, not bend

Dance with me
Dance with me
Bite your pride and dance with me
Dance with me
Dance with me
If we fall, we fall angry

You keep time like you keep score
I slam the floor, you slam once more
Knee to knee, we lock and turn
Every stare a spark that burns
I see your grin, I know that game
You want the crown, I want the same
So drag me hard through the beat
Two rivals caged in moving feet

Say my name like a threat
I step in close, you don't forget
One wrong move, one tight spin
We were born to break, not bend

Dance with me
Dance with me
Bite your pride and dance with me
Dance with me
Dance with me
If we fall, we fall angry

Push
Pull
We don't kneel
We don't fold
Lock these hands
Watch us burn
Your stare hits hard
I hit back first

Dance with me
Dance with me
Bite your pride and dance with me
Dance with me
Dance with me
If we fall, we fall angry
songs.lyrics_en:
You wore red, I wore black
Two hard heads on the same track
Last one standing, that was the vow

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They built the town by the foothill seam
On a back so broad it could hold a dream
Orange roofs on the old stone spine
Every dawn walks where the shadows climb

Grandma says you can hear him breathe
Under the market and the pepper trees
When the river runs high, he turns in sleep
And the bells all shiver in the street

So we tip our hats to the buried king
Keep our voices soft when the church bells ring
'Cause the ground still hums with an ancient name
And nobody here wants to wake that flame

La ciudad del gigante
La ciudad del gigante
We live on his shoulder
We live on his shoulder
If he opens one eye
If he opens one eye
Every heart in the valley
Will learn to be smaller

Tailors, bakers, and the boys at noon
Lay their bets on rain and a silver moon
Kids draw crowns in the dust by the wall
Like they know one day he may rise and call

So we tip our hats to the buried king
Keep our voices soft when the church bells ring
'Cause the ground still hums with an ancient name
And nobody here wants to wake that flame

La ciudad del gigante
La ciudad del gigante
We live on his shoulder
We live on his shoulder
If he opens one eye
If he opens one eye
Every heart in the valley
Will learn to be smaller

Last year the hills went wild with rain
Mud in the plaza, sparks in the train
We saw the ridge make a slow dark move
And the whole town held its breath, then swore it knew

La ciudad del gigante
La ciudad del gigante
We live on his shoulder
We live on his shoulder
If he opens one eye
If he opens one eye
We will sing to the mountain
Till the mountain sings back

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Dusty boots walk
On a sleepy giant
Sun beats down hard
He don't make a sound
Sky is big blue
Town just keeps growing
Beneath our feet now
A big rumbling mound

We build it up high
We don't ask why

Sleeping giant city
Oh sleeping giant city
Underneath the concrete dreams
Sleeping giant city
What does it all mean

Tequila flows free
Fiestas they rumble
Mariachi loud
He just takes a tumble
In his big dreams
We dance and we sing
He don't seem to mind
The joy that we bring

One day he might wake up
Stretch out his big toe

songs.subject: a gospel song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
They counted us out at dawn
They wrote the loss in ink
Shoes all worn from the road
But we came to stand and think
Coach bowed his head in the tunnel
We held that old flag high
If this is our last game, Lord
Let us play till we die

Hands to the heavens
Hearts in the fire
One more breath
One more mile
We may be small
But You are higher
Lift us up now
Lift us up higher

Raise us up, Lord
Raise us up
Under the lights, we trust
Raise us up, Lord
Raise us up
When the ground says enough
(raise us up)
Raise us up, Lord

First quarter, their crowd got loud
Second, they took the lead
Our legs were heavy as sacks of grain
Still, we planted the seed
Every missed shot, every bruise
Every scar on the floor
Was another way You taught us
How to praise You more

Hands to the heavens
Hearts in the fire
One more breath
One more mile
We may be small
But You are higher
Lift us up now
Lift us up higher

Raise us up, Lord
Raise us up
Under the lights, we trust
Raise us up, Lord
Raise us up
When the ground says enough
(raise us up)
Raise us up, Lord

If we win, You get the glory
If we lose, You stay the same
We came in with a promise
We will call Your name
When the clock is nearly empty
And the final horn is near
Put a song inside our shaking
Turn our fear to cheer

Raise us up, Lord
Raise us up
Under the lights, we trust
Raise us up, Lord
Raise us up
When the ground says enough
(raise us up)
Raise us up, Lord
(amen)
Raise us up, Lord

songs.subject: a gospel song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
They counted us out on Monday
Said the season was done
Shoes worn thin on the sideline
Still we came to run
We got mud on our jerseys
And fire in our eyes
Little team, big prayer
Standing under the sky

If this is our last hill
Then we climb it high
If this is our last breath
Let it testify
Hands up, hearts open
We won't bow down now
One more play, one more prayer
Hear the whole house shout

One last whistle
One last praise
We were made for this
In the fire and rain
One last whistle
Keep the faith
When we fall, we rise
In Your name, amen

Visors cracked from the impact
Benches holding their breath
Coach said, "Stay together"
Like a promise at the edge
No gold on our fingers
No crown on our heads
But we know who brought us
Through the green and red

If this is our last hill
Then we climb it high
If this is our last breath
Let it testify
Hands up, hearts open
We won't bow down now
One more play, one more prayer
Hear the whole house shout

One last whistle
One last praise
We were made for this
In the fire and rain
One last whistle
Keep the faith
When we fall, we rise
In Your name, amen

And if the scoreboard stings
You still carried us
Every bruised old step
Was mercy in the dust
So sing it loud, sing it true
Let the rafters ring
Underdog, but held
By the King of kings

One last whistle
One last praise
We were made for this
In the fire and rain
One last whistle
Keep the faith
When we fall, we rise
In Your name, amen
One last whistle
Hearts awake
We were never alone

songs.subject: a hip hop song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
I had the key in my palm
Had the door half-open
Suitcase on the floor
Heart beatin' like it knew me
Mama said, "boy, don't blink"
So I stared at the ceiling
Counted every bad break
Like it owed me a feeling
Blue badge in my hand
Backseat full of sweat
One call from the room
And I still ain't there yet
I wore my clean white tee
Like a flag on a stick
And I swear I could taste it
Right there on my lips

I was one step out
One name away
One more breath
And it might've changed
I seen the whole thing shine
Then it slipped through slow
You know that kind of hurt
That don't let go

Almost made it
Almost made it
Right there, right there
I could've had it
Almost made it
Almost made it
One more move
And I would've grabbed it

Now I laugh with my boys
Like it didn't cut deep
But I still hear that hallway
When the house go asleep
They was clappin' upstairs
I was stuck at the gate
Watchin' one little moment
Turn into "too late"
Had my name on a list
Had my shoes by the door
Then the phone went dead quiet
And I wanted it more
Could've been my whole life
Could've been my break
Instead I held that maybe
Like a plate full of shakes

I was one step out
One name away
One more breath
And it might've changed
I seen the whole thing shine
Then it slipped through slow
You know that kind of hurt
That don't let go

Almost made it
Almost made it
Right there, right there
I could've had it
Almost made it
Almost made it
One more move
And I would've grabbed it

But I ain't bitter, nah
I learned the shape of loss
Learned to keep on walkin'
Even when the line got crossed
Now I move with that fire
Quiet in my chest
'Cause close don't mean finished
And I know what comes next

Almost made it
Almost made it
Right there, right there
I could've had it
Almost made it
Almost made it
One more move
And I would've grabbed it

Yeah, I was that close
Yeah, I felt that rush
Almost made it hurt
But it made me tough

songs.lyrics:
Yo, mic check, one two, still kickin' dust, huh?
Almost felt that crown, almost felt that rush
Big city lights, a million dreams in the air
Felt the spotlight's glare, almost took me there
Hand on the door, VIP line, no wait
My name on the list, sealed by fate

Felt that buzz, felt the heat
On the verge, couldn't be beat

Almost famous, almost heard my name
Almost caught that fire, almost played that game
Almost, almost, yeah, the story goes
Almost had it all, from my head to my toes

The demo tape spun, speakers thumpin' hard
Manager's eyes wide, pullin' out his card
Said, "Kid, you got it, got that raw, got that real"
Felt the future comin', how that feel?
Penciled in the contract, almost signed the deal
Just a breath away, felt the moment steal

What if, what if, echoes in my head
Those unplayed tracks, those words unsaid
The path diverged, a fork in the road
A different story, a heavier load

Almost famous, almost heard my name
Almost caught that fire, almost played that game
Almost, almost, yeah, the story goes

The train it rattles
My heart it shakes
A hundred faces
For goodness sakes

Too many people
Too much noise
Losing my cool
No other choice

Panic on the platform
Panic in the car
Oh my stomach's churning
Wishing I was far far far
Panic on the platform
Nowhere left to hide
Just a lonely cowboy
On this crazy ride

The doors they open
The doors they close
My breath is shallow
Everyone knows
They all are staring
Right through my soul
Losing my marbles
Losing control

The mariachi plays a sad song
Just for me I swear
In this metal monster
Full of fear and despair
I tap my foot a little
Try to keep the beat
But my hands are sweating
And I'm feeling beat

Panic on the platform
Panic in the car
Oh my stomach's churning
Wishing I was far far far
Panic on the platform
Nowhere

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
I took the train downtown
With my coat in my lap
A folding map in my hand
And my name going slack
My chest got small as a fist
When the doors bit shut
A stranger brushed my sleeve
And I lost my footing, cut

Hold on, hold on
I know this bend
Hold on, hold on
I can breathe again

Turn the tunnel, turn it back
Turn the tunnel, turn it back
My heart is racing down that track
Turn the tunnel, turn it back

The ads on the wall went blurred
Like they were talking fast
My tongue went dry as dust
And my eyes would not ask
I counted boots on the floor
I counted nails in my palm
I looked for one clean corner
To keep my body calm

Hold on, hold on
Let the air come through
Hold on, hold on
I’m still here with you

Turn the tunnel, turn it back
Turn the tunnel, turn it back
My heart is racing down that track
Turn the tunnel, turn it back

If I stand still, I won’t fall
If I name it, it gets small
One breath in, one breath out
I can make it through this crowd

Turn the tunnel, turn it back
Turn the tunnel, turn it back
My heart is racing down that track
Turn the tunnel, turn it back
Turn the tunnel, turn it back
Turn the tunnel, turn it back
The door opens, I come back

songs.lyrics:
Yo, remember shawty, smile bright, summer sun?
Yeah, the one that got away, never had no fun
With nobody else, whole world felt numb
Stupid me, playing games, now the damage done

She the one, the one, the one that got away
Miss that vibe, every night, every single day
The one, the one, the one that got away
Shoulda held tight, now I just pray

Met her downtown, coffee shop, rain outside
Eyes deep pools, nowhere left to hide
Conversation flowed, like a river, wide
My whole damn world, just took a joyride

Then I messed it up, typical me
Thought I was slick, couldn't see

She the one, the one, the one that got away
Miss that vibe, every night, every single day
The one, the one, the one that got away
Shoulda held tight, now I just pray

Every street corner, see her face, a blur
Every new joint, just a faded purr
No other woman, can compare, for sure
This regret, a heavy, permanent scar

No other woman, can compare, for sure
This regret, a heavy, permanent scar

songs.lyrics:
You was in my T-shirt, hair still wet
Laughin' at my jokes like you ain't seen regret
I had a full tank, you had one foot out
Should've read your eyes when you looked around
We were kitchen-floor dreams and cheap red wine
Two phones lit up, still couldn't find time
Now your name hit different when it scrolls on past
Like a train through smoke, like a shot too fast
I kept your hoodie in the back of the chair
Like if I leave it there, you might still be there
But the room don't lie, and the air don't save
What we had that night from the grind that came

I reached out late, you were already gone
Seen that last blue tick, and it stayed too long
Now every new girl got a shadow of you
And I hate that it's true

The one that got away
The one that got away
I still hear your name
When the house goes gray
The one that got away

You were right there, babe

I told my boys I'm good, I'm all the way moved on
Then I caught your laugh in a song on the phone
That old wound woke up, fresh and mean
Like a scar got split from a memory
I don't blame the rain, I don't blame time

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
At dawn the floor was cold
My locker held the dust
Green paint on broken rails
A wrench still in my hand
The whole town stood outside
Counting windows gone dark

We heard the foreman say
One last shift, then gone
Machines held their breath
Like saints in the wall

Ay, la última sirena
La última sirena
We kissed the iron gate
(la última sirena)
Ay, la última sirena
It shook the whole block
Then silence took the yard
(la última sirena)

Rosa took down her lunch pail
Blue tin, dent in the side
Miguel kissed the time clock
Like it was goodbye
The forklift sat alone
In a sunlit empty aisle

We heard the foreman say
One last shift, then gone
Machines held their breath
Like saints in the wall

Ay, la última sirena
La última sirena
We kissed the iron gate
(la última sirena)
Ay, la última sirena
It shook the whole block
Then silence took the yard
(la última sirena)

Mama said keep your back straight
Even when the roof caves in
But the river of our wages
Ran thin, then thin again
So sing for the hands we lost
Sing for the hands we bend

Ay, la última sirena
La última sirena
We carried home the smoke
(la última sirena)
Ay, la última sirena
Hold me by the gate
The bell went off at noon
(la última sirena)
