songs.subject: a trap song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Pulled up where the black pines lean
Snow on the road, no sleep, no screen
Sky tore open, green on green
Like a ghost in a silk machine
My chest go hard when the whole world dims
Boots in the slush, I don’t fold, I grin
You said “stay low,” I said “watch me then”
I been chasing that glow since I was ten

Hands to the glass
Don’t fade now
Make that arc last
Spin the clouds out

Northern lights, northern lights
Cut the sky, cut the night
Northern lights, northern lights
I keep my heart in that sight
Northern lights, northern lights
Tell me what you came to show
Northern lights, northern lights
I still follow where you go

Cabin got steam on the window pane
Radio low with a far-off train
You in my coat, I’m in my lane
We both look up when the colors change
I seen fake love fold in the cold
I seen big talk turn brittle and old
But that green flame, it don’t need gold
It just move like it knows my soul

Hands to the glass
Don’t fade now
Make that arc last
Spin the clouds out

Northern lights, northern lights
Cut the sky, cut the night
Northern lights, northern lights
I keep my heart in that sight
Northern lights, northern lights
Tell me what you came to show
Northern lights, northern lights
I still follow where you go

One step, then the whole sky sways
Cold breath hangs in a silver haze
If I’m lost, let me be this way
Under green fire, I don’t miss my place

Northern lights, northern lights
Cut the sky, cut the night
Northern lights, northern lights
I keep my heart in that sight
Northern lights, northern lights
Tell me what you came to show
Northern lights, northern lights
I still follow where you go

songs.subject: a trap song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Pulled up where the sky got scars
Green thread dancing past the stars
Snow on my boots, frost on the gate
I seen the whole night bend its shape
Truck idling low in the dark
Window down, breath in the spark
Phone in my hand, but I don't call
I just watch heaven switch and fall

Spin that glow
Let it breathe
Move so slow
Over me
It don't ask
It don't stay
Cuts the black
Then fades away

Aurora on ice
Aurora on ice
Cut through the night
Aurora on ice
Green in the sky
Cold in my eyes
I keep it close
Aurora on ice

Back road black with a sheet of snow
Cousin said, "Boy, now you know"
City stayed far, but the world felt near
Like the whole north side came down here
I was broke, still I felt rich
Hands in my coat, heart in a switch
Every flash had a new name
Every second looked the same

Spin that glow
Let it breathe
Move so slow
Over me
It don't ask
It don't stay
Cuts the black
Then fades away

Aurora on ice
Aurora on ice
Cut through the night
Aurora on ice
Green in the sky
Cold in my eyes
I keep it close
Aurora on ice

Half the world asleep
I'm still outside
That sky keep talking
I don't need a guide
One more wave
Then it's gone
Still feel that cold
All night long

Aurora on ice
Aurora on ice
Cut through the night
Aurora on ice
Green in the sky
Cold in my eyes
I keep it close

songs.subject: a tango song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The black suit's on the chair
by the church receipt
Your mother left roses
in a plastic sheet
I made the coffee weak
and let it go cold
The house still smells like lilies
and wet overcoat

The window shows the yard
all bare and gray
Your shoes by the door
won't move today
I hear the spoon
tap the cup
Like a small, hard clock
that won't shut up

Morning after the funeral
morning after you
I still set two plates down
like I don't know what to do
Morning after the funeral
I can hear your name
On the stair, on the floor
on the edge of the frame

The neighbors left by noon
with their careful words
A blue silk scarf
on the back porch chair
Your sister took the photos
I kept one too long
A man can hold a memory
and still be wrong

The priest said, Let him rest
I said, I know
But every room in this house
is moving slow
The ash in the tray
the folded prayer
Your jacket on the hook
is still right there

Morning after the funeral
morning after you
I still set two plates down
like I don't know what to do
Morning after the funeral
I can hear your name
On the stair, on the floor
on the edge of the frame

I walked to the gate
just after dawn
The lawn was damp
and the world went on
A sparrow hit the fence
then flew by
I stood there learning
how to say goodbye

Morning after the funeral
morning after you
I still set two plates down
like I don't know what to do
Morning after the funeral
I can hear your name
On the stair, on the floor
on the edge of the frame
Morning after the funeral
hold me in the blue
I wake up, I reach out
and there is only you

songs.subject: a tango song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The table kept its shape.
Four chairs, one empty plate.
Your coat still by the door,
like you might ask for more.
The flowers started bending.
The priest's last words were ending.
I drank the black coffee cold
and watched the dawn turn gold.

In the mirror, I looked older.
In the sink, the water folded.
I tied my tie again,
then undid it with my hand.

Morning after, stay with me.
Morning after, let me breathe.
Your name still on my tongue.
Your name still on my tongue. (stay with me)
Morning after, don't turn gray.
Morning after, hold the day.
I learned the shape of loss.
I learned the shape of loss. (hold the day)

The street was swept and clean.
A boy ran after me.
His ball rolled to my shoe,
then stopped, and I did too.
The bakery opened early.
The clerk looked past me, surly.
I bought two rolls, one for you,
then laughed at what I knew.

Every step was slow and narrow.
Every sound was thin as marrow.
I kept your ring in my coat,
cold as a snapped-off note.

Morning after, stay with me.
Morning after, let me breathe.
Your name still on my tongue.
Your name still on my tongue. (stay with me)
Morning after, don't turn gray.
Morning after, hold the day.
I learned the shape of loss.
I learned the shape of loss. (hold the day)

If you were here, you'd scold me.
Say, "Eat that bread before it goes."
I'd swear I heard you laughing
through the gate where the cypress grows.
So I raise this little sorrow,
not to freeze it, not tomorrow.
Just to dance it, heel to heel,
till the hurt knows how to kneel.

Morning after, stay with me.
Morning after, let me breathe.
Your name still on my tongue.
Your name still on my tongue. (stay with me)
Morning after, don't turn gray.
Morning after, hold the day.
I learned the shape of loss.
I learned the shape of loss. (hold the day)

The sun came in like mercy.
The cup was still and earthy.
I wore the ache like lace,

songs.subject: a americana song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The parking lot is full of frost
And the gift shop’s dark till dawn
Your badge still swings at your chest
When the world has moved on
The coffee’s gone cold twice
In a paper cup by ten
You tie your hair and wash your hands
And walk that hallway again

Everybody else is home
With sweet potatoes on the plate
But you’re rolling down that corridor
Keeping one more promise straight

On a holiday, you stay
On a holiday, you stay
While the rest of town sits down
You keep the dark at bay
On a holiday, you stay
On a holiday, you stay
Paper cup in your hand
And the whole ward calls your name

Room twelve wants a blanket
Room seven wants a prayer
A kid in four with a fever
Grips your sleeve like it’s rare
You flash that tired smile
Past the ache behind your eyes
There’s pie at home in the fridge
And a missed call lighting up sky

But the pumps keep ticking steady
And the monitors hum low
You move like someone built for this
Even when your feet say slow

On a holiday, you stay
On a holiday, you stay
While the rest of town sits down
You keep the dark at bay
On a holiday, you stay
On a holiday, you stay
Paper cup in your hand
And the whole ward calls your name

At midnight, the chapel’s empty
Save the vending machine glow
You lean your head to the lockers
For one breath, soft and slow
Then you square your shoulders back
And answer down the hall
Love don’t always wear a ribbon
Sometimes it wears a hall pass and a stall

On a holiday, you stay
On a holiday, you stay
While the rest of town sits down
You keep the dark at bay
On a holiday, you stay
On a holiday, you stay
Paper cup in your hand
And the whole ward calls your name

songs.subject: a americana song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Sun-up through the ER glass
She's tying back her hair
Paper cup of burnt black coffee
Cold in the breakroom air
Holiday cards on the desk by triage
She reads one line, then goes
A double shift in white shoes
And the whole town moves like it knows

Old clock blinks seven-oh-eight
Another bed just filled
She checks that pulse, she checks that face
Then swallows what she feels

She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
While the rest go home in waves
She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
And still she stays, she stays
Paper cup holiday
Paper cup holiday

Maggie on seven wants her blanket
Ray needs a phone call home
A kid with a busted red stocking
Keeps asking when he'll go
She tapes up charts and shades the hallway
Bends down with a tired smile
Keeps the night from breaking open
One small kindness at a time

Machines hum low like distant rain
The halls stay half asleep
She rubs her wrists, she squares her shoulders
And answers from the deep

She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
While the rest go home in waves
She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
And still she stays, she stays
Paper cup holiday
Paper cup holiday

At dawn the sky turns pale blue sugar
On the frozen parking lot
She sits beside the curb a minute
Holding all she's got
Then she goes back in for one more round
For one more name to see
A nurse can carry half a city
When the season won't let be

She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
While the rest go home in waves
She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
And still she stays, she stays
Paper cup holiday
Paper cup holiday
songs.lyrics_en:
Sun-up through the ER glass
She's tying back her hair
Paper cup of burnt black coffee
Cold in the breakroom air
Holiday cards on the desk by triage
She reads one line, then goes
A double shift in white shoes
And the whole town moves like it knows

Old clock blinks seven-oh-eight
Another bed just filled
She checks that pulse, she checks that face
Then swallows what she feels

She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
While the rest go home in waves
She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
And still she stays, she stays
Paper cup holiday
Paper cup holiday

Maggie on seven wants her blanket
Ray needs a phone call home
A kid with a busted red stocking
Keeps asking when he'll go
She tapes up charts and shades the hallway
Bends down with a tired smile
Keeps the night from breaking open
One small kindness at a time

Machines hum low like distant rain
The halls stay half asleep
She rubs her wrists, she squares her shoulders
And answers from the deep

She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
While the rest go home in waves
She's working Christmas
She's working Christmas
And still she stays, she stays

songs.subject: a ska song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The closet's bare
The couch is gone
A broom leans lonely
By the screen door
You found one sock
In the hall again
And one last lamp
In a box of plates

Roll that tape
Pull it tight
One more corner
And we're out tonight

Tape that last box up
Tape that last box up
One more room, one more load
Tape that last box up

Your name's still traced
In dust on the shelf
A thumbprint ghost
On the fridge handle
Old keys jingle
In the kitchen bowl
We laugh at the mess
Like it knows the joke

Roll that tape
Hear it snap
Old place empty
Ain't getting us back

Tape that last box up
Tape that last box up
One more room, one more load
Tape that last box up

We carried the bedframe
We carried the frame
We carried the table
We carried the rain
On our jackets, on our shoes
On the cardboard seams
Now the hallway echoes
With a hundred new dreams

Tape that last box up
Tape that last box up
One more room, one more load
Tape that last box up
Tape that last box up
Tape that last box up
Door swings shut, keys go cold
Tape that last box up

songs.lyrics:

Underneath the sofa
A forgotten sock too
This whole place a goner

Another chapter closing
A new one starting soon

Tape it up tape it up last box done
My moving day blues they are gone
Tape it up tape it up the final seal
What a crazy long ordeal

That wobbly old chair
The one with three legs
Now packed up tight
No more clumsy pegs

Empty rooms echo
My last footsteps
A brand new beginning

songs.lyrics:
Back in the day
When the 'fro was high (so high!)
Butterflies in my belly
Lookin' up at the sky
Sweaty palms
Mic in hand
A little nervous sigh (uh-huh!)
First open mic
Baby
Time to let it fly

Oh
Open mic night
Yeah (open mic!)
Felt so right
Yeah (so right!)
Open mic night
Baby
Shinin' bright (like a star!)
Open mic night
What a beautiful sight!

Walked on up
Knees a-knockin' (shaky, shaky!)
Saw those faces
Hearts a-rockin'
Dropped the beat
Felt the rhythm lockin' (tight now!)
Then the crowd started flockin'
No stoppin'

Oh
Open mic night
Yeah (open mic!)
Felt so right
Yeah (so right!)
Open mic night
Baby
Shinin' bright (like a star!)
Open mic night
What a beautiful sight!

Felt the energy soar (oo-yeah!)
They wanted more
Knockin' on my door (come on in!)
Never felt that feelin' before
Gotta explore
This groove
This vibe
Forevermore!

songs.subject: a funk song about looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
New shoes, too much shine
Hands shook on the mic that night
Cheap beer on the floorboards
My name on a paper light
I cleared my throat like a prayer
Told myself, "Don't run"
One brave step to the edge
Then the room said, "Come on"

My knees kept time
With the bass drum kick
Could've turned back then
But I leaned in quick

First open mic, first open mic
I wore my fear like a suit
First open mic, first open mic
You can hear it in the truth
I was small, I was wild
I was trying to grow
First open mic, first open mic
Man, I needed that show (that show)

There was a girl in the back
Laughing into her cup
A dude in a red cap
Nodding when I messed up
I forgot half the line
Found the rest in the crowd
Every crack in my voice
Made me a little more loud

That old stage light
Hit like gold
I was nervous, yes
But I let it hold

First open mic, first open mic
I wore my fear like a suit
First open mic, first open mic
You can hear it in the truth
I was small, I was wild
I was trying to grow
First open mic, first open mic
Man, I needed that show (that show)

Now I walk in smiling
Less smoke in my chest
Still hear that old clap
When I thought I was a mess
And every room since then
Gets a piece of that night
I found my voice in the crack
Found my step in the light

First open mic, first open mic
I wore my fear like a suit
First open mic, first open mic
You can hear it in the truth
I was small, I was wild
I was trying to grow
First open mic, first open mic

songs.subject: a blues song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The river got my shoes
Got my Sunday suit
I walked down Rampart
With the mourners in blue
Her name on the card
White lilies in a row
The band turned the corner
And the whole block said slow

Drums in my chest
Slow step, don't fall
I held that umbrella
Like it meant it all
The preacher said, baby
Let the hard rain go
But the tears kept coming
To the brass band glow

Play it low, play it low
For the one I love
Play it low, play it low
Let the trumpets cry above
(Play it low)
We walk her home today
(Play it low)
Let the poor heart sway

White gloves on the widow
Black veil on her face
A boy tossed petals
At the cemetery gate
The snare kept tapping
Like a lock on wood
And every old auntie
Sang her neighborhood good
I tasted salt
When the tuba leaned in
That long slow ache
Came back again

Drums in my chest
Slow step, don't fall
I held that umbrella
Like it meant it all
The preacher said, baby
Let the hard rain go
But the tears kept coming
To the brass band glow

Play it low, play it low
For the one I love
Play it low, play it low
Let the trumpets cry above
(Play it low)
We walk her home today
(Play it low)
Let the poor heart sway

When the procession bent
Past the corner store
I seen her in the window
Like a year before
Laughing at rain
With a hand on mine
Now the sky went gray
And the bells outlined

Play it low, play it low
For the one I love
Play it low, play it low
Let the trumpets cry above
(Play it low)
We walk her home today
(Play it low)
New Orleans don't forget her name

songs.lyrics:
Black suit shined shoes
Slow drag down the street
Tuba moans low
A bittersweet beat

Ashes to ashes
Dust to dust
Second line shuffles
A joyful trust

Trumpet wails high
A tear in my eye
But a smile on my face
We don't say goodbye

Ashes to ashes
Dust to dust
Second line shuffles
A joyful trust

He loved his liquor
He loved his women
He lived his life loud

songs.lyrics:

"Son
You gotta get that paper
Yeah!" (Get that paper!)
Papa said
"Boy
Don't you be no slacker!" (No slacker!)
They had it all mapped out
A straight and narrow lane
But my soul was screamin' for a different kind of game (Woo!)

Felt that rhythm deep inside
Couldn't keep it all locked up
Nowhere to hide!

Oh
I'm breakin' free
Baby
From the master plan (Break it down!)
Yeah
I'm steppin' out
Movin' to my own command (So funky!)
This ain't no pre-fab destiny
No
No
No (Uh-uh!)
I'm choosin' my own path
Watch me go
Go
Go! (Go, go, go!)

Suit and tie
Nine to five
That ain't my vibe (Naw, man!)
Got this funky beat
Keeps my spirit alive (So alive!)
They wanted me to settle
Wanted me to conform
But my heart's a wild drum
Weatherin' any storm (Oo-yeah!)

Used to walk that line
Head bowed low
Now I'm dancin' to the rhythm
Watch my spirit grow! (Grow, baby, grow!)
Left the past behind
No regrets
No doubt
This new groove's got me spinnin' all about!

songs.subject: a funk song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama laid the table.
Daddy picked the bride.
I wore that blue suit
like a trap I couldn’t hide.
Silver on the napkin,
names already said.
Future in a folder,
burning in my head.

But my hands got restless,
my feet knew the door.
I could hear my own name
calling for something more.

I’m leaving that blue suit behind
(blue suit behind)
I’m taking my own damn time
(my own damn time)
You can keep that planned-out life
(keep it)
I’m gone before it can define
(define me)

She had a ring box,
he had the keys.
Everybody smiling
like they knew what I should be.
Sunday after Sunday,
same old hand-me-down.
I was looking at the window
like a train was coming now.

Then your voice hit low
like a match on stone.
You said, “If it’s not your life,
why are you still home?”

I’m leaving that blue suit behind
(blue suit behind)
I’m taking my own damn time
(my own damn time)
You can keep that planned-out life
(keep it)
I’m gone before it can define
(define me)

I walked past the altar
with my name still warm.
One hand on the suitcase,
one hand on the storm.
No more borrowed tomorrow,
no more nod and bow.
I’m making my own future
right here, right now.

I’m leaving that blue suit behind
(blue suit behind)
I’m taking my own damn time
(my own damn time)
You can keep that planned-out life
(keep it)
I’m gone before it can define
(define me)
I’m leaving that blue suit behind
(blue suit behind)
Watch me step into the light
(step into the light)
I was made for a different life
(different life)
I’m gone, and I’m feeling right

(define me)

I’m leaving that blue suit behind

(different life)

songs.subject: a ska song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We packed our names in tin
Two helmets, one old grin
Shared a bunk in the dark
Shared one map, one spark
The Earth went small below
Like a coin we couldn't hold
You counted stars for luck
I counted all the things we lost

Now your line gets thin
My glove taps glass again
You spin, I spin, we miss
Same sky, different fist

Drift apart, drift apart
Two hearts in the dark
Drift apart, drift apart
Still close, still far
(Drift apart)
(Drift apart)

Your side of the window's frost
Mine is a scratch and a cross
You laugh through static snow
I laugh, but you don't know
We pass like wires cut
Like old songs under glass
Your shadow on my wall
Then nothing, then it passed

Now your line gets thin
My glove taps glass again
You spin, I spin, we miss
Same sky, different fist

Drift apart, drift apart
Two hearts in the dark
Drift apart, drift apart
Still close, still far
(Drift apart)
(Drift apart)

If I reach, you fade
If you call, I slip away
We were one bright fire
Now two small trails in space

Drift apart, drift apart
Two hearts in the dark
Drift apart, drift apart
Still close, still far
Drift apart, drift apart
I still know your spark
Drift apart, drift apart
Out here, we don't stop

songs.subject: a ska song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We launched from the same tin dawn
Shared one tiny bunk and a joke
Your glove left gray on my visor
Mine left a smudge on your scope
We counted Earth by the dock lights
Little blue coin in the black
Now your name rides the radio
And mine comes back half cracked

We laughed when the tether went tight
Said, "Hey, that's how we fly"
Now the line keeps getting longer
And I don't ask you why

Split at periapsis
Split at periapsis
Hold on, hold on, hold on
We're gone in a slow-burn drift
Split at periapsis
Split at periapsis
Wave from your side of the glass
I wave from the side I get

You took the east window, baby
I took the cracked port seam
You chase the dark around Jupiter
I chase the edge of a dream
Your food pack's floating in silence
Mine clinks against the wall
We used to trade stale mangoes
Now we don't trade at all

The station hums like a jury
The bolts say what we won't say
Two suits, two paths, one old orbit
Breaking softer every day

Split at periapsis
Split at periapsis
Hold on, hold on, hold on
We're gone in a slow-burn drift
Split at periapsis
Split at periapsis
Wave from your side of the glass
I wave from the side I get

Maybe the map was the problem
Maybe the void got a name
Maybe we grew in two directions
And still kept calling it the same
If you see my little blink code
Send one back, don't let it fade
I learned that love can be distance
And still be the road we made

Split at periapsis
Split at periapsis
Hold on, hold on, hold on
We're gone in a slow-burn drift
Split at periapsis
Split at periapsis
Wave from your side of the glass

songs.subject: a ska song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
You still wear my ring
I still tie your shoe
Seventy winters in
And it’s still me and you
The floorboards know our steps
The frame knows our grin
One last song, my love
Before the room thins in

Spin me slow
Spin me right
Hold me close
For the last dance night

Last dance, my love
Last dance, my dear
One more spin
Then we’ll stay right here
Last dance, my love
Don’t let go
We were young once
Now we move like we know

The cake is on the table
The guests all drift to sleep
Your laugh still hits me
Like a stone in the creek
Your hands are paper-soft
Mine are work-worn wood
We learned the hard hard parts
And we loved the good

Spin me slow
Spin me right
Hold me close
For the last dance night

Last dance, my love
Last dance, my dear
One more spin
Then we’ll stay right here
Last dance, my love
Don’t let go
We were young once
Now we move like we know

Let the band play on
Let the heels scrape round
All our loud old years
In one small sound
If tomorrow waits
It can wait outside
Tonight your forehead
Finds my shoulder right

Last dance, my love
Last dance, my dear
One more spin
Then we’ll stay right here
Last dance, my love
Don’t let go
We were young once
Now we move like we know
Last dance, my love
Slow and true
All I ever was
Still comes back to you

songs.subject: a house song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise creeping
Barely sleeping
My body aching
My spirit waking

Another day another beat
Can't feel my feet

Festival of lights
Burning so bright
One more night
Everything's alright

Dust in my hair
Don't even care
Sweat and glitter

My body aching
My spirit waking

Another day another beat
Can't feel my feet

Festival of lights
Burning so bright
One more night
Everything's alright

Dust in my hair
Don't even care
Sweat and glitter

songs.lyrics:
The wristband's frayed
Your shoes got dust
One more drink
Then we leave the mud
The food trucks close
The banners bend
You said "last night"
Like it's a prayer

Don't make me go yet
Don't kill the flame
Your hand in mine
Says the same thing
The sky goes gray
But we stay here
One more minute
Then disappear

Last night, last night
Stay with me
Last night, last night
Don't let it leave
One more song
One more pull
Last night, last night
Feels so full

Your voice is hoarse
From shouting names
Somebody's laughing
Through the rain
We lost the map
Found the sweet spot
By the fence
Where the drums don't stop

Don't make me go yet
Don't kill the flame
Your hand in mine

Last night, last night
Stay with me
Last night, last night
Don't let it leave
One more song
One more pull

Feels so full

And the cups are crushed
I'll still taste your laugh
Like a rush
So spin me once
Before it ends
I want that glow
Again and again

Last night, last night
Stay with me
Last night, last night
Don't let it leave
One more song
One more pull
Last night, last night
Feels so full

Last night, last night
We held on tight
Last night, last night

songs.subject: a reggae song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
I see your knees move slow
At the screen door step
Your shirt got soft from years
And your laugh still cuts the wet
You used to lift the world
Now you rest your palm
On the table by the sink
Like you’re trying to keep it calm

And I watch your eyes
Find the same old yard
You don’t say much now
But you still hold hard

Old man, stay with me
Stay with me
Your hands taught mine to stand
Stay with me
Old man, stay with me
Stay with me
I’m not ready for the sky
Stay with me

The radio on low
By the fan and flour tin
You tell me where you broke
And where you tried again
Your boots by the back step
Cracked like winter soil
You still taste every day
Like you earned it in the toil

And I hear your breath
In the hallway night
One more story, dad
Before you dim the light

Old man, stay with me
Stay with me
Your hands taught mine to stand
Stay with me
Old man, stay with me
Stay with me
I’m not ready for the sky
Stay with me

If the road gets thin
Let me walk ahead
If your memory slips
I’ll sing it back instead
You gave me your name
And a stubborn kind of grace
So I’ll hold your chair
Till the last warm place

Old man, stay with me
Stay with me
Your hands taught mine to stand
Stay with me
Old man, stay with me
Stay with me
I’m not ready for the sky
Stay with me
Old man, stay with me
Stay with me
Till your slow breath turns to song
Stay with me

songs.lyrics:
Sunrise creepin'
On a face I know
Lines like rivers
Where the good times flow
Used to lift me way up high
Now he just stares at the sky

Oh Father Time
He's a-movin' on
Father Time
Soon he'll be gone
Oh Father Time
His steps are slow
Father Time
Watchin' him go

Hands that built our little shack
Now they tremble can't hold back
Stories told a thousand times
Still I listen to his rhymes
He forgets the little things
But his spirit still sings

Oh Father Time
He's a-movin' on
Father Time
Soon he'll be gone
Oh Father Time
His steps are slow
Father Time
Watchin' him go

Used to chase me through the yard
Now he's playin' a different card
Life's a cycle round and round
New beginnings can be found
But the old roots hold so tight
Fadin' slowly into night

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
I know your blocks by the curve
Where the river cuts clean
Your market wakes up early
Your shadows sleep in the seam
They built you over a giant
With a stone in his chest
Now every footstep you take
Feels like a drum in his rest

Hold your breath
When the ground starts to sway
Hold my waist
Let the old thing pray
You know it
You feel it in your bones
One more turn
And the giant rolls

Wake the giant
Wake the giant
When you move, he moves
Wake the giant
Wake the giant
We make the whole place groooooove
Wake the giant
Wake the giant
Hear the old walls sing
Wake the giant
Wake the giant
City on a sleeping king

Your buses are burning the dawn
With their doors open wide
Fruit carts lean into the corner
Like they’ve got nowhere to hide
Kids kick dust off the steps
While the church bells fight
And the giant under your avenues
Turns in his bed at night

Hold your breath
When the ground starts to sway
Hold my waist
Let the old thing pray
You know it
You feel it in your bones
One more turn
And the giant rolls

Wake the giant
Wake the giant
City on a sleeping king

If he opens one eye
We all shine back
If he lifts that arm
No road stays flat
So dance on the fault line
Dance like you know
The whole town was dreaming
Long before we were born

Wake the giant
When you move, he moves
Wake the giant
Wake the giant
We make the whole place groooooove
Wake the giant

Hear the old walls sing

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Tu barrio duerme abajo
de un pecho de piedra
las calles aprendieron
a caminar en tierra
La marea le da nombre
a cada esquina
y el sol pega en los techos
como en una herida
Yo te vi respirar lento
bajo el mercado
con frutas, sal y promesas
en el mismo lado

Y si despierta
tiembla el suelo
pero tú bailas
sobre ese fuego

City on a giant
city on a giant
baila sobre el gigante
que nunca se levanta
City on a giant
city on a giant
si abre los ojos
nadie nos aguanta

Hay un puente de madera
sobre su hombro
los buses van cantando
por su contorno
Las abuelas lo conocen
por el pulso
dicen "no lo molestes"
que es viejo y justo
Pero en la noche tú y yo
cruzamos cerca
con la sal en la boca
y la luna inquieta

Y si despierta
tiembla el suelo
pero tú bailas
sobre ese fuego

City on a giant
city on a giant
baila sobre el gigante
que nunca se levanta
City on a giant
city on a giant
si abre los ojos
nadie nos aguanta

Déjalo soñar
déjalo en paz
que la ciudad sabe andar
sobre su espalda
Yo quiero tu sudor
tu ritmo, tu mar
si el mundo se rompe
lo vamos a bailar

City on a giant
city on a giant
baila sobre el gigante
que nunca se levanta
City on a giant
city on a giant
si abre los ojos
nadie nos aguanta
City on a giant
city on a giant
y cuando amanece

songs.subject: a hip hop song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
I came back with a duffel and a limp in my step
Mama stood in the doorway, tried to hold her breath
Screen door squeak, same porch light, same cracked paint
Dog in the yard went wild like he knew my name
I kept my hands in my pockets, kept my eyes on the floor
'Cause the house looked the same, but I wasn't before
Got medals in a box and a whole lot of dust
And a heart that still flinches when the trucks go by the block

I don't sleep like I used to
I don't laugh like I should
Every sound got a shadow
Every night got wood on wood
But I'm here, I'm here
Still standing in the frame
Trying to make a home
Out the pieces of my name

Back from the war
Back through the door
I made it home
But I carry more
Back from the war
Back through the door
(Back from the war)
I'm still yours

Little brother got taller, had to look up at me
He asked about the desert and I changed the whole scene
Said the road was long and the sun was mean
Left out the nights and the names and the smoke in between
Mama set a plate down, said, "Eat while it's hot"
I nodded like a robot, forgot what I forgot
There’s a ring in my ears when the house goes still
And I grip that table edge till my knuckles turn pale

I don't sleep like I used to
I don't laugh like I should
Every sound got a shadow
Every night got wood on wood
But I'm here, I'm here
Still standing in the frame
Trying to make a home
Out the pieces of my name

Back from the war
Back through the door
I made it home
But I carry more
Back from the war
Back through the door
(Back from the war)
I'm still yours

If I go quiet, don't mistake it for gone
I'm just learning how to live when the battles stop
Got a hand on the Bible and a hand on the rail
Trying to leave that last mile where my old life fell
One day at a time
One breath at a time
I came back broken
Now I want to be fine

Back from the war
Back through the door
I made it home
But I carry more
Back from the war
Back through the door
(Back from the war)
I'm still yours

songs.subject: a hip hop song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots by the door, I'm home at last
Momma on the porch, she don't ask fast
Just eyes on mine, and a tight hug
Like she held that line with a prayer and love
My hands still shake when the plates hit the table
I say "I'm here" like I ain't always able
The dog runs wild, tail on the wall
I flinch, then laugh, then I almost fall

I got sand in my clothes
Got names in my chest
Took a long road back
Now I'm trying my best
One more breath, baby
One more step, baby
I made it through the dark
Now I need the rest

Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
Leave the war at the gate
Let me sleep, let me breathe
Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
I still carry the scars
But they don't carry me

Old room same paint, new crack in the wall
Little brother taller, he don't look small
He says "You made it," then he looks away
Like he saw a ghost in the frame today
Dad sets two chairs on the busted deck
No big speech, just a nod to respect
I count the cars like I counted days
Trying to find my place in the little things

I got noise in my head
But the yard feels calm
I got fire in my hands
But I want your palm
One more breath, baby
One more step, baby
I made it through the dark
Now I need the rest

Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
Leave the war at the gate
Let me sleep, let me breathe
Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
I still carry the scars
But they don't carry me

If I wake up hard, hold the lamp
If I go too quiet, call my name
I'm not built the same as I was before
But I'm standing here at my own front door
I learned the price, I learned the pain
I learned the face of the pouring rain
Now I want slow days, honest food
A second chance in a borrowed mood

Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
Leave the war at the gate
Let me sleep, let me breathe
Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
I still carry the scars

Just eyes on mine, and a tight hug
Like she held that line with a prayer and love
My hands still shake when the plates hit the table
I say "I'm here" like I ain't always able
The dog runs wild, tail on the wall
I flinch, then laugh, then I almost fall

I got sand in my clothes
Got names in my chest
Took a long road back
Now I'm trying my best
One more breath, baby
One more step, baby
I made it through the dark
Now I need the rest

Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
Leave the war at the gate
Let me sleep, let me breathe
Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
I still carry the scars
But they don't carry me

Old room same paint, new crack in the wall
Little brother taller, he don't look small
He says "You made it," then he looks away
Like he saw a ghost in the frame today
Dad sets two chairs on the busted deck
No big speech, just a nod to respect
I count the cars like I counted days
Trying to find my place in the little things

I got noise in my head
But the yard feels calm
I got fire in my hands
But I want your palm
One more breath, baby
One more step, baby
I made it through the dark
Now I need the rest

Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
Leave the war at the gate
Let me sleep, let me breathe
Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
I still carry the scars
But they don't carry me

If I wake up hard, hold the lamp
If I go too quiet, call my name
I'm not built the same as I was before
But I'm standing here at my own front door
I learned the price, I learned the pain
I learned the face of the pouring rain
Now I want slow days, honest food
A second chance in a borrowed mood

Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
Leave the war at the gate
Let me sleep, let me breathe
Back home, back home
I'm back home, I'm back home
I still carry the scars

songs.subject: a soul song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I kept your jacket in the hall
Three winters and a spring
Still find your name in the screen door
Where the old latch used to sing
Your chair still leans by the window
Like you might come in for tea
But the house has learned your silence
And it’s teaching it to me

I was bitter for a long time
Carried that iron in my chest
Then the rain hit the porch one evening
And I knew I’d done my best

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Gone from my hand
But not from my heart
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
(yeah, I forgive you)
Even in the dark

I don’t know where you’re resting
If the grass is high and kind
I just know the years got heavy
And the hurt kept changing shape in my mind
There were words I held like thunder
There were nights I couldn’t sleep
Now I lay them on the table
Let them fold into the deep

If love is just a wound that travels
Then I’ve carried mine enough
I won’t drag your ghost through winter
I will let it turn to dust

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Gone from my hand
But not from my heart
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
(yeah, I forgive you)
Even in the dark

If you hear me, let it reach you
Through whatever takes us on
I am tired of being brittle
I am tired of holding on
So I lay your name down gently
By the basin, by the thorn
What was broken can be answered
What was buried can be mourned

I forgive you now
I forgive you now
Gone from my hand
But not from my heart
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
(turn it loose)
Even in the dark

Gone from my hand
Not from my heart
I forgive you now
I forgive you now

songs.subject: a soul song about forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I kept your number
in a cracked old phone
Like I could call up
what I should've known
The last thing you said
still sits in the hall
By the empty chair
that heard it all

I held that hurt
like a loaded stone
It weighed my hands down
when I was alone

I let you go
I let you go
You can't come back
But I let you go
I bless your name
and I unclench my fist
I let you go
I let you go (let you go)

Your coat still hangs there
by the back door frame
Rain on the window
still spells your name
I used to replay
every word you said
Now I lay it down
beside the bed

Time took the edge
but I took the blame
Now I can say it
without the shame

I let you go
I let you go
You can't come back
But I let you go
I bless your name
and I unclench my fist
I let you go
I let you go (let you go)

If there's a road
where the goodbyes end
I hope it found you
my old friend
I don't need answers
I don't need proof
I just need peace
where I stood with you

I let you go
I let you go
You can't come back
But I let you go
I bless your name
and I unclench my fist
I let you go
I let you go (let you go)
I forgive you now
I forgive you now
And in this quiet

songs.subject: a R&B song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Your name under my shoes
Like a road I know by heart
The whole town leans one way
When the old ground starts to part
Brick by brick, we learned
How to keep a fire low
But I can hear it breathing
Way down where the river goes

And the streets hold their breath
When you call my name
Something deep under us
Starts to wake again
I know that pull
I know that spark
We’ve been walking on a giant
Sleeping in the dark

Sleeping giant city
Hold me close, hold me close
Sleeping giant city
You wake in my bones
Sleeping giant city
Don’t let me go
Sleeping giant city
You wake in my bones

I saw your shadow move
In the glass of the deli door
I felt the whole block tremble
Like it wanted something more
Kids on the corner laugh
But they don’t know the sound
Of that old pulse under pavement
Turning over underground

And the streets hold their breath
When you call my name
Something deep under us
Starts to wake again
I know that pull
I know that spark
We’ve been walking on a giant
Sleeping in the dark

Sleeping giant city
Hold me close, hold me close
Sleeping giant city
You wake in my bones
Sleeping giant city
Don’t let me go
Sleeping giant city
You wake in my bones

If the morning comes loud
Let it shake the glass
If the whole sky bends
Let the moment last
I don’t fear the rise
I was made to stay
When the giant opens both eyes
I won’t run away

Sleeping giant city
Hold me close, hold me close
Sleeping giant city
You wake in my bones
Sleeping giant city
Don’t let me go
Sleeping giant city
You wake in my bones (wake in my bones)

songs.subject: a R&B song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
We wake on your shoulder
Crossing your old bone bridge
Trains run like a rumor
Past the scar in the ridge
My window sees your eyelid
Tremble under concrete skin
And every block I grew up on
Knew you were breathing in

Still I hear that low hum
Under market stalls
Under taxi tires
Under church hall walls
If you ever turn over
We all know the call
So I hold my love gentle
When the night leans tall

Sleeping giant, hold me close
Sleeping giant, don’t let go
This whole town knows your name
This whole town knows your pulse
Sleeping giant, stay asleep
Keep your dream beneath the street
We were built from your silence
We were built from your sleep

Laundry lines like prayer flags
Between the roofs and rust
Kids kick cans by the canal
Old men taste the dust
My mother says you fed us
With the warmth in your chest
Now she taps the window frame
Like she’s checking your rest

Still I hear that low hum
Under market stalls
Under taxi tires
Under church hall walls
If you ever turn over
We all know the call
So I hold my love gentle
When the night leans tall

Sleeping giant, hold me close
Sleeping giant, don’t let go
This whole town knows your name
This whole town knows your pulse
Sleeping giant, stay asleep
Keep your dream beneath the street
We were built from your silence
We were built from your sleep

If your eyes ever open
Will the river run dry?
Will the bells lose their meaning
Will the ash take the sky?
I press my ear to the pavement
Hear your heart somewhere deep
And I choose you in the dark
I choose your sleep

Sleeping giant, hold me close
Sleeping giant, don’t let go
This whole town knows your name
This whole town knows your pulse
Sleeping giant, stay asleep
Keep your dream beneath the street
We were built from your silence

songs.lyrics:
Sweat drips down my face
Heartbeat like a drum solo
Last game last chance
Gonna take our place

They laugh they jeer
But we got no fear

Underdog anthem gonna play it loud
Underdog anthem gonna make them proud
Underdog anthem yeah we here to stay
Underdog anthem win it today

Dusty shoes and worn out dreams
Nobody gave us a chance it seems
But fire in our eyes
We're gonna surprise

They talk they doubt
We gonna scream and shout

Underdog anthem gonna play it loud
Underdog anthem gonna make them proud
Underdog anthem yeah we here to stay
Underdog anthem win it today

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Mud on the socks
Tape on the wrist
Nobody picked us
Nobody missed
Old bus rattled
Down by the field
Coach said one more
Now make it real
We got the bench
And a scratched-up grin
Last game, last chance
Let the whole thing in

Hear that count?
Feel that ground?
One more step
Then we go loud
Hands up high
Chin up, stay
They came to watch us
We came to take

Last game, last game
We won't fold
Last game, last game
Hearts of gold
One more run
One more fight
Last game, last game
We own tonight

Scoreboard cold
Wind in the net
Their names on the banner
Not ours yet
But we kept close
Every line, every mark
Won in the corners
Won in the dark
Keeper's glove
Grass-stained sleeve
Whole wide crowd
Starts to believe

Hear that count?
Feel that ground?
One more step
Then we go loud
Hands up high
Chin up, stay
They came to watch us
We came to take

Last game, last game
We won't fold
Last game, last game
Hearts of gold
One more run
One more fight
Last game, last game
We own tonight

Five minutes left
And the air went thin
Captain yelled, "Now"
So we dug back in
One clean break
One sharp scream
Then the whole team flew
Like a live wire dream

Last game, last game
We won't fold
Last game, last game
Hearts of gold
One more run
One more fight
Last game, last game
We own tonight
Last game, last game
Hear us call
Underdogs standing
Gonna rise or fall
Last game, last game
We won't fade
Final whistle

songs.subject: a gothic country song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
By the locker room bulb
Your jerseys hang low
Mud on the hem
Like a field after snow
Coach drew up mercy
On a napkin that night
But you kept your helmets
Like bruises in light

Now the crowd’s half gone
And the grass looks mean
Still you tie up tight
For the last old scene

One last run, one last run
(One last run)
We were born to break the gun
We were the lost ones
We were the few
But tonight, baby
We make them look at you

Fresh paint on the bleachers
Peels off in the rain
Old men in the stands
Say your name like a prayer for pain
The scoreboard’s a graveyard
Of zeros and scars
But you’ve got a fire
In your busted-up stars

Hear that chain-link shake
Hear that whistle cry
If we go down now
We go down baptized

One last run, one last run
(One last run)
We were born to break the gun
We were the lost ones
We were the few
But tonight, baby
We make them look at you

When the floodlights tremble
And the fourth quarter falls
You don’t need a miracle
You just need nerve and calloused soles
So curse the clock
And lace it tight
If this is our ending
Make it holy tonight

One last run, one last run
(One last run)
We were born to break the gun
We were the lost ones
We were the few
And when the whistle takes us
We’ll still be standing true
One last run, one last run
(One last run)
We were born to break the gun
We were the lost ones
We were the few
Tonight they’ll remember
What the underdogs do

songs.subject: a gothic country song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
We got mud on the cleats
And frost on the rail
Town shut down early
Like it knew we’d fail
Faded numbers
On a busted white shirt
But we ran out smiling
Through the dust and hurt

The bandstand leaned
In the bitter wind
Mama at the fence
With her hands clenched in
Coach kept saying
“Hold that line”
Like a prayer in the dark
At the end of time

And the scoreboard glowed
Like a graveyard lamp
One more snap
One more stand
We heard the crowd go
Hold on, hold on
Like they were trying
To raise the dead by song

Last game, last stand
Hold that line
(Last game, last stand)
Till the cold bone chimes
We may go down
But we go proud
(Go proud, go proud)
With the whole town loud

Their helmets shone
Like polished stone
They had size and speed
And a heart of chrome
But our quarterback
Had a tore-up sleeve
And a look in his eye
That made you believe

Third and long
On the final drive
Every porch in town
Felt alive
We took that hit
And we took it mean
Like a rusted blade
In a baptist dream

Then the night got still
And the crowd leaned in
We could taste that loss
On the sharpened wind
Coach said “Now”
And the whole field shook
Like a black dirt page
In an old book

Last game, last stand
Hold that line
(Last game, last stand)
Till the cold bone chimes
We may go down
But we go proud
(Go proud, go proud)
With the whole town loud

If this is the end
Let it ring out clean
Let the dirt remember
What we meant to be
No gold on the wall
No parade, no throne
Just ten hard hearts
And a fire grown

Last game, last stand
Hold that line
(Last game, last stand)
Till the cold bone chimes
We may go down
But we go proud
(Go proud, go proud)
With the whole town loud

When the whistle cried
And the dark rolled in
They carried our name
Like a hymn in the wind
Last game, last stand
And the night took note
(Last game, last stand)

songs.subject: a jazz song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You came in with the tide
Barefoot, tan-line bright
Catching shells in your hand
Like you owned the whole beach
My name on your lips
Salt on your collar
We laughed in the waves
Till the sky turned copper

Now the moon’s up high
And your skin still shines
You said “don’t ask me where”
I said “baby, fine”

Salt on your collar
Sand in my shirt
One summer, one spark
That’s how it works
Salt on your collar
Gone with the dawn
We had that fling
And the tide moved on
(salt on your collar)
(one summer, one spark)

Late-night fries by the pier
Sticky hands, paper cups
You stole my last sweet bite
Then you smiled and shrugged
Your phone face down
Mine never rang
Just seagulls crying
And a band playing

If I close my eyes
I can taste that day
You were all heat and breeze
Then you slipped away

Salt on your collar
Sand in my shirt

songs.subject: a jazz song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You came in barefoot
With a paper cup grin
Sun on your shoulders
Salt on your skin
I was at the bar
You were at the rail
We made a little history
Before the ice could fail

The tide kept time
With your wrist on mine
You said, "stay awhile"
I said, "all right"

Salt on your collar
Sand in my hair
You were a summer flame
I still wear
Salt on your collar
Dancing like rain
One hot July
And I’d do it again

We stole a boat dock kiss
By the weathered pier
Your laugh hit the gulls
And I held it near
Cheap sunglasses
Bent on the dash
Your name on my mouth
Like a cigarette ash

The tide kept time
With your wrist on mine
You said, "stay awhile"
I said, "all right"

Salt on your collar
Sand in my hair
You were a summer flame
I still wear
Salt on your collar
Dancing like rain
One hot July
And I’d do it again

Now the beach is quiet
And the boards don't sway
But your red scarf memory
Won’t wash away
If I hear that offbeat laugh
I’m back in the heat
One more sweet mistake
Walking by the sea

Salt on your collar
Sand in my hair
You were a summer flame
I still wear
Salt on your collar
Dancing like rain
One hot July

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning
Your voicemail glows blue
I got snow on the mirrors
And a long road through
The coffee went bitter
An hour ago
Still I keep your number
Like a loaded coat

Rest stop windows
Sleep in rows
My hands on the wheel
And my heart comes home

Call me home
Call me home
I need your voice
To keep me warm
Call me home
Call me home
Till the morning breaks
On mile marker four

Gas station gum
And a paper cup moon
A kid in a hoodie
Stares through the gloom
I hear your laugh
Through the static thread
Like you’re here beside me
At the end of the bed

Tail lights fading
Far ahead
I hold on tight
To the words you said

Call me home
Call me home
I need your voice
To keep me warm
Call me home
Call me home
Till the morning breaks
On mile marker four

If the world gets heavy
If I drift too far
Leave the porch on for me
Leave the screen door ajar
I’ll cross these highways
With the dark in tow
Till your breath says stay
And I know, I know

Call me home
Call me home
I need your voice
To keep me warm
Call me home
Call me home
Till the morning breaks
On mile marker four
Call me home
Call me home
I’m almost there
Don’t hang up now

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blur to streaks of gold
Another mile another toll
This lonely road it takes its hold
My story always left untold

Static hums a sleepy tune
Underneath the desert moon

3 AM blues on the telephone line
Whisper baby are you still mine
3 AM blues everything's fine
Just checking in one more time

Coffee black and radio low
Another sunrise soft and slow
The engine hums a steady flow
Where does all this time go

I see your face in every cloud
Lost inside this truck stop crowd
Wishing I could scream out loud
But silence is my only shroud

3 AM blues on the telephone line
Whisper baby are you still mine

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Starlight, dark night
No air, just fright
Used to be us, a bright cosmic dust
Now just rust, a fading trust

Zero G, no we
Just space, no embrace
Drifting, shifting, no liftin'
Two souls, unkempt roles

Your voice, a static hiss
My thoughts, a silent abyss
Once a team, a shared dream
Now a glitch, a broken seam

Remember Earth? That green blur?
Our laughter, a distant stir
Now just silence, a cold violence
No guidance, just defiance

Zero G, no we
Just space, no embrace
Drifting, shifting, no liftin'
Two souls,

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We shared one window
same blue on the glass
your glove brushed mine
then it slipped past
Two cups on the dash
one bent silver spoon
you said stay close
I said see you soon

Now your light’s a dot
on the edge of my view
my hand finds empty space
where I used to find you

Drift, drift away
drift, drift away
I still call your name
but the dark won’t say
Drift, drift away
drift, drift away
same old orbit
different day

My map’s got your route
crossed out in red
your sleep in the back
my thoughts in my head
A scratch on the pane
from your ring and mine
two small marks
on a line of time

You’re a pulse I lost
in the hush of the night
I keep turning toward you
but you’re out of sight

Drift, drift away
drift, drift away
I still call your name
but the dark won’t say
Drift, drift away
drift, drift away
same old orbit
different day

If I reach too far
I might lose my own course
so I let you go
with a quiet force
One more message
floating from my hand
just a paper comet
in a borderless land

Drift, drift away
drift, drift away
I still call your name
but the dark won’t say
Drift, drift away
drift, drift away
same old orbit

songs.subject: a soul song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I saw you by the courthouse steps
Same cracked paint, same summer sweat
You laughed like a memory I knew
Like my last good day came back for you
You wore that old Main Street grin
With your hair caught up in the wind
And I swear the whole block changed
When you said my name that way

Now I can't act cool
Can't play it tough
You got that small-town pull
And baby, that's enough

I fell for you, hometown love
Right where the river bends
I fell for you, hometown love
And I would do it all again
(you know I would)
Hometown love
Hometown love
Right where we began

We cut through the feed store lot
Past the screen door and the porch light spot
Your mama called, "Boy, come in"
I got weak when you smiled at me
Saturday game, bleachers high
You leaned in close, I lost my pride
Now every road that I used to know
Leads me back where your hands feel like home

Now I can't look away
Can't slow this rush
Every little thing you say
Keeps building up

I fell for you, hometown love
Right where the river bends
I fell for you, hometown love
And I would do it all again
(you know I would)
Hometown love
Hometown love
Right where we began

If I ever left these county lines
I'd still see you in every sign
In the paint-chipped doors, in the gravel drive
In the way my heart comes alive
Come closer now
Don't let this fade
I knew that place
Then you said my name

I fell for you, hometown love
Right where the river bends
I fell for you, hometown love
And I would do it all again
(again and again)
Hometown love
Hometown love
Right where we began

songs.subject: a soul song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I knew your mama from the market line
You had your laugh in your brother's eyes
Same old bus stop, same red dust road
I never thought you'd make my whole world fold

Seen you in church with your Sunday shoes
Hair tied back like you never had a clue
You said, "Boy, you still talk the same"
And my heart did a little hometown flame

Now the diner clock feels soft and slow
Every back road starts to glow
If you say my name like that
I don't know where my feet are at

Hometown honey, you got me now
Hometown honey, I don't know how
That small-town smile
Went a long, long mile
Hometown honey, you got me now
(Hometown honey)

Your porch light cuts through the Friday rain
We laugh so hard we forget the pain
Your hand on mine like it always fit
Like this whole wide life was made for it

Now the diner clock feels soft and slow
Every back road starts to glow
If you say my name like that
I don't know where my feet are at

Hometown honey, you got me now
Hometown honey, I don't know how
That small-town smile
Went a long, long mile
Hometown honey, you got me now
(Hometown honey)

Maybe I was waiting on this all along
For a place I knew to feel this strong
Same old town, but you're the change
You make my worn-out heart feel new again

Hometown honey, you got me now
Hometown honey, I don't know how
That small-town smile
Went a long, long mile
Hometown honey, you got me now

songs.subject: a post-rock song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Your hand is cold in mine
A room full of pinned-up eyes
You wore your old sharp grin
I wore my better disguise

One step, then another
The floor starts to tilt
You pull, I pull harder
And neither of us yields

Dance with me
Dance with me
Turn the blade into gold
Dance with me
Dance with me
Till we both lose control
(keep on)
(keep on)

We learned the same hard rules
Different scars, same bruise
Your heel cuts the same line
My name tastes like a fuse

One step, then another
The room starts to glow
Your shoulder against mine
Like a storm in slow motion

Dance with me
Dance with me
Turn the blade into gold
Dance with me
Dance with me
Till we both lose control
(keep on)
(keep on)

The band of light bends low
Your breathing in my ear
All that we brought to break
Is trembling here

Dance with me
Dance with me
Turn the blade into gold
Dance with me
Dance with me
Till we both lose control
Dance with me
Dance with me
We were never the same
Dance with me
Dance with me
And leave with my name
(keep on)
(keep on)

songs.subject: a post-rock song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You wore your silver stare
I wore my broken grin
They put us on the floor
And turned the tension in
Your hand on my sleeve
Like a dare I couldn't dodge
The band counts down one, two
My pride goes soft in the fog

Heel to heel
We circle slow
You hate me
I know, I know
The room leans in
Like it wants to see
If we can move
Without mercy

Dance with me
Bitter one
Dance with me
Till the edges run
Dance with me
Don't look away
We both know
This is how we stay

Your laugh hits like glass
Mine lands like a brick
We learned every bad word
And every sharper trick
But your fingers found time
In the seam of my guard
Now the old fight dissolves
In the flare of the dark

Step back
Pull me close
You hate me
But you know
The room goes still
When we give in
Two rivals
Learning skin

Dance with me
Bitter one
Dance with me
Till the edges run
Dance with me
Don't look away
We both know
This is how we stay

When the drums fall wide
And the floor turns gold
I see your anger
Starting to fold
One last turn
One shaking breath
We don't surrender
We just don't wreck

Dance with me
Bitter one
Dance with me
Till the edges run
Dance with me
Don't look away
We both know
This is how we stay
Dance with me
War and all
Dance with me
Before we fall
Dance with me
Right through the flame
Two rivals spinning

songs.subject: a synthwave song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I left my hands on the pew
Dust on the old red book
I walked out into the rain
Telling myself, "I'm done"
The radio ate my prayer
Static where the promise was
Your name got stuck in my throat
Then I let it go

But every wrong road
Had your shadow on it
Every empty room
Still knew my song

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then you found me first
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Still you kept your word

Saw my face in a shop glass
Eyes like a broken door
I chased that easy noise
Till it ran me cold
I sat on the bathroom floor
Counted the cracks in time
Felt that old fire start
Underneath the shame

And every wrong road
Had your shadow on it
Every empty room
Still knew my song

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then you found me first
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Still you kept your word

When I was half a man
You still called my name
When I was all locked up
You came through the frame
No grand light, just a spark
In a borrowed heart
I said, "If you're still there
Don't let me fall apart"

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then you found me first
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Still you kept your word
I found my faith
I found my faith
In the wreck and rain
I found my faith
I found my faith

I chased that easy noise
Till it ran me cold
I sat on the bathroom floor
Counted the cracks in time
Felt that old fire start
Underneath the shame

And every wrong road
Had your shadow on it
Every empty room
Still knew my song

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then you found me first
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Still you kept your word

When I was half a man
You still called my name
When I was all locked up
You came through the frame
No grand light, just a spark
In a borrowed heart
I said, "If you're still there
Don't let me fall apart"

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then you found me first
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Still you kept your word
I found my faith
I found my faith
In the wreck and rain
I found my faith
I found my faith

songs.subject: a swing song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Your name lit up my screen
Like a dime-store dream
Then the words came fast
Short as a taxicab scream
You said, “It’s over now”
One thumb, one flat goodbye
I stared at that little bubble
Till the moon fell out the sky

No ring, no knock
Just a cold little chime
You took our whole love
And made it fit in a line

Over a text, babe
Over a text
You broke my heart
With a thumbs-up next
Over a text, babe
That’s how it went
We used to dance slow
Now I’m left with the text

I had your coffee mug
Still warm in the sink
Your red scarf on my chair
And a phone I couldn’t think
You left my “are you sure?”
On read all night
Like a bandstand in the rain
With the last note cut too tight

No fight, no floor
Just a screen gone white
You folded up forever
In a pocket of light

Over a text, babe
Over a text
You broke my heart
With a thumbs-up next
Over a text, babe
That’s how it went
We used to dance slow
Now I’m left with the text

I laughed so hard I shook
Then I sat right down
What kind of love leaves town
Without a sound?
If you ever miss
What you threw away
Baby, call me back
In a better way

Over a text, babe
Over a text
You broke my heart
With a thumbs-up next
Over a text, babe
That’s how it went
We used to dance slow
Now I’m left with the text

That little screen
Still burns my chest
You said goodbye
And the phone did the rest

songs.subject: a swing song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The way you left me, honey
Was a modern art
A little digital dagger
Straight through my analog heart

No handwritten letter, no tear-stained goodbye
Just a glowing screen
And a single, cold line
Oh my, oh my

Disconnected, baby, that’s what we are
A love story ended, beneath a blinking star
One little message, a buzz and a chime
Wiped out our future, and wasted our time

I re-read that text
A dozen times or more
"It's over," it said,
Then nothing, just the door

I typed out a reply
A passionate plea
But my thumbs just fumbled
And I let it be

Disconnected, baby, that’s what we are
A love story ended, beneath a blinking star
One little message, a buzz and a chime

songs.subject: a blues song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I packed my shirt in a paper sack
Sun came up on the grain silo black
Mama stood there by the screen door frame
Didn’t say much, but she knew my name

That road been callin’ me all year
Same old hard luck, same old fear
I can’t stay where the dust won’t sleep
I’ve got a promise I gotta keep

I’m leavin’ for good (leavin’ for good)
I’m gone, for good (gone, for good)
This town knows my name
But it don’t know my pain
I’m leavin’ for good (leavin’ for good)

The feed store clock kept a broken time
Kids on the curb with their nickels and limes
Old man Ray waved from the hardware side
Said, “Boy, that river runs deep, but it runs both sides”

I spent too long on borrowed ground
Waitin’ on a life that never came round
The fields got narrow, the sky got wide
I need one new dawn on the other side

I’m leavin’ for good (leavin’ for good)
I’m gone, for good (gone, for good)
This town knows my name
But it don’t know my pain
I’m leavin’ for good (leavin’ for good)

If I ever look back, let it be
For the porch light glowin’ after me
Not for the years I let slip through
Not for the boy I used to be

I’m leavin’ for good (leavin’ for good)
I’m gone, for good (gone, for good)
This town knows my name
But it don’t know my pain
I’m leavin’ for good (leavin’ for good)

songs.subject: a blues song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Mama packed my shirt
In the red wash tub
Said, boy, don’t look back
When the engine wakes up
I kissed that screen door
Like a rusted saint
Left my name on the porch
And the house paint faint

The feed store clock
Still missed by ten
The same old men
And the same old grin
They talked my life small
Like a broken chain
But the highway called
By my first name

I heard that whistle cry
I felt my boots move
One foot in the dust
One foot in the truth
The sky got wide
And my chest did too
I said out loud
What I had to do

I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(can’t chain me down)
I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(not coming round)
This town kept score
But I broke that rule
I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(can’t chain me down)

There’s a ditch out back
With my old boy dreams
A dented bike
And a pocket of beads
I gave ’em all
To the gravel road
Let the wind take names
Let the river know

I heard that whistle cry
I felt my boots move
One foot in the dust
One foot in the truth
The sky got wide
And my chest did too
I said out loud
What I had to do

I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(can’t chain me down)
I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(not coming round)
This town kept score
But I broke that rule
I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(can’t chain me down)

If they ask for me
Tell them I stayed kind
Tell them I took my hurt
And left the rest behind
No hard luck sermon
No parade, no fuss
Just a little less dust
And a whole lot more of us

I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(can’t chain me down)
I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good
(not coming round)
This town kept score
But I broke that rule
I’m gone for good
I’m gone for good

songs.subject: a trap song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Your name lit my screen
Then it split me in two
One blue little bubble
Took all I knew from you
I was still in the cab
With your scarf on my seat
Then the message came through
And the whole world skipped a beat

Seen it fade, then it stays
Like a bruise under skin
I kept typing "baby"
Then I deleted it again
You said "don't make a scene"
Like that made it clean
But you left our whole year
In a tiny rectangle screen

You ended it over a text
Cold little text
You left my heart on read
Left my heart on read
I was all in your hands
Then you hit me and fled
You ended it over a text
Cold little text

Now my phone on dark mode
Still I check it at night
Got the thread in my head
Like a blade in the light
You can mute the whole room
But the hurt still rings loud
Saw your dots, then the silence
Had me lost in the crowd

Seen it fade, then it stays
Like a bruise under skin
I kept typing "baby"
Then I deleted it again
You said "don't make a scene"
Like that made it clean
But you left our whole year
In a tiny rectangle screen

You ended it over a text
Cold little text
You left my heart on read
Left my heart on read
I was all in your hands
Then you hit me and fled
You ended it over a text
Cold little text

No face, no fight
Just a quiet goodbye
Four little words
And I don't know why
I held that glow
Like it meant you might stay
Now I got your last line
Playing back all day

You ended it over a text
Cold little text
You left my heart on read
Left my heart on read
I was all in your hands
Then you hit me and fled
You ended it over a text
Cold little text

songs.subject: a trap song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Your name lit up my screen
At one-fifteen in the rain
Just a blue box, two lines
And you took my whole day
I was mid-sip, half asleep
Then my hands started shaking
You said, "Don't make this hard"
But that line kept me breaking
Seen on the last one
No call, no fight
Just a tiny little screen
Cut the room in two that night

I stared at the dots
Then they disappeared
You left me on read
Then you left me here
Now my chest feel tight
And the whole place froze
One little text
Was all she wrote

One text, one cut
You said it and gone
One text, one cut
Now I'm holding on
Blue check, cold kiss
That was your goodbye
One text, one cut
And I don't know why

Your hoodie on the chair
Your cup still half full
I kept it by the sink
Like a bad rehearsal
I typed "are you sure?"
Then deleted my pride
I watched that cursor blink
Like a pulse on my life
No face in my phone
Just words on a glass
You turned years to a sentence
And made it move too fast
I could've heard the truth
If you'd just stayed near
Instead I got your ending
In a box full of fear

I stared at the dots
Then they disappeared
You left me on read
Then you left me here
Now my chest feel tight
And the whole place froze
One little text
Was all she wrote

One text, one cut
You said it and gone
One text, one cut
Now I'm holding on
Blue check, cold kiss
That was your goodbye
One text, one cut
And I don't know why

Maybe I wanted a fight
Maybe I wanted a frame
Something I could point to
Something I could blame
But you chose the screen
And it stayed so clean
No tears on your shirt
No cracks in between

One text, one cut
You said it and gone
One text, one cut
Now I'm holding on
Blue check, cold kiss
That was your goodbye
One text, one cut
And I don't know why
One text, one cut
Still stuck in my chest
One text, one cut
You could've said less
Blue check, cold kiss
That was your goodbye
One text, one cut

songs.subject: a pop song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
You stood knee-deep at dawn
With salt on your sleeves
Said, watch how the water
Comes back to leave
You traced a line in the sand
Like a map for my feet
And the shore held still
While you taught me to breathe

You said, let it go
Then let it return
Every wave knows
What the heart has learned

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
How to come back
How to arrive
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Hold me like moonlight
Then let me survive

You counted the shells
Like old family names
Said every rough edge
Still answers the same
When the water pulls back
Don’t run after the foam
Just stay with the edge
And you’ll never be alone

You said, let it go
Then let it return
Every wave knows
What the heart has learned

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
How to come back
How to arrive
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Hold me like moonlight
Then let me survive

Now your hands are slower
But the lesson stays
You left me the shoreline
You left me the waves
When the night gets heavy
And I lose my guide
I hear your soft voice
Rolling with the tide

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
How to come back
How to arrive
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Hold me like moonlight
Then let me survive
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
I am your ocean
I am your child

songs.subject: a pop song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
He met me at low tide
Boots in the black sand
Palm out to the water
Like he knew its plans
He said, “Watch the line, child”
“Don’t fight what it turns”
“Every wave comes back around”
“Every soul returns”

He traced a curve in the foam
Said, “This is how you know”
“Hold on, then let it go”
“Hold on, then let it go”

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Carry me back
Carry me back
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
What leaves can return
What stays can survive

His hands were all weather
His laugh was all salt
He pointed to the moon
Said, “That’s the switch, that’s all”
I asked why the water
Never stays one shape
He smiled at my silence
And let the answer wait

He traced a curve in the foam
Said, “This is how you know”
“Hold on, then let it go”
“Hold on, then let it go”

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Carry me back
Carry me back
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
What leaves can return
What stays can survive

When the wind got louder
And my fear got deep
He stood where the shore broke
And counted for me
One for the pulling
Two for the hush
Three for the leaving
Then he told me, “Trust”

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Carry me back
Carry me back
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
I know how to bend
I know how to last
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
What leaves can return

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The kettle clicked once
In your kitchen
I stood in the dark
With my shoes in my hand
Your black coat still hung
On the back of the chair
And the flowers by the door
Bent under their own air

Morning came thin
Through the blinds
I could still taste
The chapel in my mind

Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
White chrysanthemum
Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
I keep your name in my mouth

The cards on the table
Went soft at the edge
Your sister kept folding
That broken white edge
I washed the glass
But the ring stayed there
A pale little halo
On the sink’s cold stare

Morning came thin
Through the blinds
I could still hear
That heavy quiet line

Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
White chrysanthemum
Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
I keep your name in my mouth

The sky looked scraped clean
Like a bruise going pale
I walked to the curb
Past the wet paper trail
If I call your old phone
It rings in my chest
If I say your name now
It cracks through the rest

Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
White chrysanthemum
Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
I keep your name in my mouth

By noon I was just
A slow-moving stain
Holding your morning
Like a windowpane
songs.lyrics_en:
The kettle clicked once
In your kitchen
I stood in the dark
With my shoes in my hand
Your black coat still hung
On the back of the chair
And the flowers by the door
Bent under their own air

Morning came thin
Through the blinds
I could still taste
The chapel in my mind

Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
White chrysanthemum
Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
I keep your name in my mouth

The cards on the table
Went soft at the edge
Your sister kept folding
That broken white edge
I washed the glass
But the ring stayed there
A pale little halo
On the sink’s cold stare

Morning came thin
Through the blinds
I could still hear
That heavy quiet line

Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
White chrysanthemum
Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
I keep your name in my mouth

The sky looked scraped clean
Like a bruise going pale
I walked to the curb
Past the wet paper trail
If I call your old phone
It rings in my chest
If I say your name now
It cracks through the rest

Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
White chrysanthemum
Don't leave me here
Don't leave me here
I keep your name in my mouth

By noon I was just
A slow-moving stain
Holding your morning
Like a windowpane

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The kettle clicked at six
Your coat still on the chair
I found your black tie
Folded on the stair
A glass with water left
By the folded program
The house held its breath
Like it knew what happened

I opened every room
For a little air
But the silence stayed
And sat down there

Morning after
Morning after
Everything is soft and wrong
Morning after
Morning after
The day goes on and on

The flowers on the table
Bent in the window light
A spoon in the sink
Catching the cold white
My phone kept lighting up
Names I could not read
I stood by the door
And forgot to breathe

Your laughter in my head
Like a radio ghost
I turn it down
It comes back close

Morning after
Morning after
Everything is soft and wrong
Morning after
Morning after
The day goes on and on

I wore your old gray sweater
To the corner shop
Milk and bread in my hands
Like that could make it stop
The sky was pale and open
The bins, the wet brick wall
I thought I saw you
In the steam and the mall

Morning after
Morning after
Everything is soft and wrong
Morning after
Morning after
The day goes on and on
Morning after
Morning after

songs.subject: a synthwave song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We cut the deck on a diner booth
Grease on the glass, we were chasing truth
Your lucky ring and my borrowed grin
One more hand, then the night walked in
The dealer smiled with a paper face
He slid me fear in a velvet case
Ace of hearts in a blood-red sleeve
You said "I'm in," and I couldn't leave

Chip stack high
Pulse on fire
One more round
Climbing higher
Your eyes said stop
My hands said stay
The room got cold
Then faded away

Dead man's hand, hold tight
Dead man's hand tonight
We bet it all
We bet it all
Dead man's hand, hold tight
Dead man's hand tonight
No more luck
No way back

The king went down with a cracked gold crown
And the table turned like a funeral sound
Your laugh got thin, then it split in two
When the joker came with a tab of blue
I saw my name in the mirrored tray
I saw our breath in a purple haze
You pushed your stack, I pushed too far
Now every card cuts like a scar

Chip stack high
Pulse on fire
One more round
Climbing higher
Your hand touched mine
Then slipped away
The house came alive
To take its pay

Dead man's hand, hold tight
Dead man's hand tonight
We bet it all
We bet it all
Dead man's hand, hold tight
Dead man's hand tonight
No more luck
No way back

The lights went low
The floorboards cracked
You whispered my name
Like a deal come back
I laid my soul
Where the dealer smiled
Now the whole damn room
Is a loaded dial

Dead man's hand, hold tight
Dead man's hand tonight
We bet it all
We bet it all
Dead man's hand, hold tight
Dead man's hand tonight
No more luck
No way back
Dead man's hand, hold tight
Dead man's hand tonight
You and I
Went too far

songs.subject: a synthwave song about a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights bleed on wet asphalt
Chrome gleam on my leather
A bad deal a hard fault
We played games together

Felt the tension build up slow
One more card one more throw

Neon Aces on the table
Hearts beat like a drum
This game ain't no fable
Where did we go wrong
Neon Aces flashing bright
Lost in the digital night

The dealer's smile a wicked twist
His eyes like burning coals
One last hand a final risk
Gambling with our souls

The stakes got higher so fast
This crazy night it won't last

Neon Aces on the table
Hearts beat like a drum
This game ain't no fable
Where did we go wrong
Neon Aces flashing bright
