songs.subject: a funk song about the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Back porch, sunburned knees
Paper bag, peaches and keys
Pool towel on the fence
One more day, that's all we get
Mom's on the screen door swing
You got boxes full of big new things

Say it slow, don't rush the clock
Bare feet sliding on the dock
We been young all afternoon
Now the moon is coming soon

One last slip, one last slip
Hold this day, don't let it slip
One last slip, one last slip
Summer on my fingertips
(One last slip)
(One last slip)

Your blue car smells like gas and mint
Cracked phone case, that faded print
Dorm room map on the dash
You grin like this goes way too fast
Ice melts in a red cup ring
We keep laughing at everything

Say it slow, don't rush the clock
Bare feet sliding on the dock
We been young all afternoon
Now the moon is coming soon

One last slip, one last slip
Hold this day, don't let it slip
One last slip, one last slip
Summer on my fingertips
(One last slip)
(One last slip)

Tonight, the cicadas cut loose
Tomorrow got a harder groove
But right now, the whole back yard
Fits inside your open car
Take my hand, take one more spin
Let the whole slow season in

One last slip, one last slip
Hold this day, don't let it slip
One last slip, one last slip
Summer on my fingertips
One last slip, one last slip
We were here, and it was bliss
(One last slip)
(One last slip)

songs.subject: a funk song about the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Mango stain on my T-shirt
Screen door slamming in the heat
Pool water on my backpack
Flip-flops slapping down the street
Dad said, "You ready?"
I lied and said, "For sure"
Got one foot in the driveway
And one foot out the door

Might be gone by morning
Might be brave by then
But right now the whole block knows
This summer's wearing thin

Last day, last day
Lemonade on my tongue
Last day, last day
We were young till we were done
Spin me round, don't slow me down
I can feel the season turn
Last day, last day
Let it burn, let it burn
(last day, last day)

Mama packed some towels
Like I'm moving in a ship
My buddies on the corner
Passing one last plastic cup
Your laugh on the sidewalk
Came in hot like afternoon
I wanted to say forever
But forever left too soon

Might be gone by morning
Might be brave by then
But right now the whole block knows
This summer's wearing thin

Last day, last day
Lemonade on my tongue
Last day, last day
We were young till we were done
Spin me round, don't slow me down
I can feel the season turn
Last day, last day
Let it burn, let it burn
(last day, last day)

Suitcase by the door
Heart still in the yard
One more dirty tennis shoe
One more scarlet sky so hard
Call me when the boxes
Start to feel like me
I'll be learning how to miss you
In some new city

Last day, last day
Lemonade on my tongue
Last day, last day
We were young till we were done
Spin me round, don't slow me down
I can feel the season turn
Last day, last day
Let it burn, let it burn
(last day, last day)

songs.subject: a blues song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Big boat calling
Water wide and deep
Left my mama crying
Secrets I will keep
New world waiting
Got no time to sleep

Ocean blues
Got those ocean blues
This long journey
What's a man to do

Packed my old guitar
Felt the engine groan
Left my dusty troubles
Left my childhood home
Just a whisper wind
Singing all alone

Ocean blues
Got those ocean blues
This long journey
What's a man to do

Waves are rocking me
Rocking me to sleep
Dreams of golden streets
Promises to

songs.subject: a blues song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
My trunk got a label
My coat got the rain
Mama pressed my forehead
Said, "Don't forget your name"
I watched that old dock shake
When the rope let go
And the shoreline faded
Like a page in smoke

Lord, the sea was tall
And the sky was mean
I held my ticket tight
Like a prayer in my jeans

Cross that water, Lord
Cross that water
(come on over)
Leave this hurt on shore
Cross that water, Lord
Cross that water
(take me home)
I been poor too long

First night I got salt burns
On my hands and face
A man near the railing
Lost his wedding lace
A child kept asking
When the land would show
I said, "Keep your eyes up
We'll know when we know"

Lord, the moon went thin
And the waves rolled black
Every mile from home
Was a nail in my back

Cross that water, Lord
Cross that water
(come on over)
Leave this hurt on shore
Cross that water, Lord
Cross that water
(take me home)
I been poor too long

I ain't chasing gold
I ain't running free
I'm just running toward
A place that might take me
If my name sounds strange
If my shoes look worn
I still got this heartbeat
And I still got dawn

Cross that water, Lord
Cross that water
(come on over)
Leave this hurt on shore
Cross that water, Lord
Cross that water
(take me home)

songs.lyrics:
Sun bleedin' low
Orange and purple
One last scorcher day
Sweat stickin' to my skin
Grime on the window pane
This town's a cage

August blues
Yeah August blues
Summer's a ghost
Gone too soon
August blues
Yeah August blues
What's next for me
No clue

Packed up boxes
Tape ripped cardboard
Dust motes dancin'
Empty rooms echo
My old man's sigh
Mom's fake smile

Freedom callin'
Or just a new trap
Same old fear
Different address

August blues
Yeah August blues
Summer's a ghost
Gone too soon
August blues
Yeah August blues
What's next for me
No clue

Highway hummin'
Engine coughin'
Future's a blur
Can't see past the next mile
Just gotta drive
Just gotta go

songs.subject: a indie rock song about the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Sun bleeding orange across the highway
Another cheap motel another empty promise
Dust devils dance in the rearview mirror
Leaving it all behind yeah just like that

Engine hummin' low and mean
Got a knot in my gut yeah what's it mean

Summer's last stand ain't no turning back
No turning back no turning back
The road unwinds no looking back
No looking back no looking back

Fake ID tucked in my wallet
Stolen kisses in the back of a beat-up pickup
Promises whispered on humid nights
Now just echoes in a vacant lot

What's out there
Beyond this familiar dirt road
A new world or just the same old lies

songs.subject: a punk song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man sea dog
Eyes like storm clouds
Salt-crusted hands
Pointin' at the waves
Tellin' secrets
To the crashin' foam
Like it listens
Like it knows

Tide's comin' in
Tide's goin' out
Who the hell needs
To learn about that
Just let it be
Let the ocean breathe
Don't need no guru
For the endless deep

He mutters rhymes
'Bout the moon's pull
Ancient whispers
From some forgotten book
Says the water
Got its own damn mind
But it answers
To a cosmic grind

Tide's comin' in
Tide's goin' out
Who the hell needs
To learn about that
Just let it be
Let the ocean breathe
Don't need no guru
For the endless deep

What's the lesson
What's the big reveal
Just a rhythm
The world's harsh feel
No fancy answers
No magic words
Just the ocean

songs.subject: a punk song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
She stood by the pier
With boots full of rain
Said, “Watch that water”
“Learn it by name”
Her fingers were rope
Her laugh was a scar
She pointed at foam
Like it hid who we are

Wait for the pull
Then step with it
Don’t fight that mouth
Just talk to it
Low tide, high bite
It comes and goes
Hold your ground
Then let it roll

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Salt in her hands
And fire in her eyes
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
You bend, you stand
You learn, you survive

She marked every rock
With a chipped pocket blade
Said, “Even hard things
Get worn and shaped”
The gulls cut loose
The wind got loud
She smiled at the flood
Like it knew her now

Wait for the pull
Then step with it
Don’t fight that mouth
Just talk to it
Low tide, high bite
It comes and goes
Hold your ground
Then let it roll

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Salt in her hands
And fire in her eyes
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
You bend, you stand
You learn, you survive

I used to swing
At every wave
Now I hear her
In the spray
“Move with the moon”
She said, “Move like this”
Then she laughed
And gave the dock a kiss

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Salt in her hands
And fire in her eyes
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
You bend, you stand
You learn, you survive
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
I’m still here

songs.subject: a americana song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The fridge is a bare white cave
Just one magnet on the floor
You fold up the paper plates
Set the broom against the door
Your fingers stick with old brown tape
You tuck the flaps just right
One last box by the threshold
In that late Sunday light

You press it down with your thumb
Hear that rip and let it go
Whole place shrinking by the minute
Like a song you already know

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more load and then we’re gone
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Baby, we were here too long

The hallway smells like dust and rain
And someone else’s stew
A nail hole in the yellow wall
Where your old picture used to
You find a spoon in the sink
A dime under the mat
Little things that say we lived here
And now we ain’t coming back

You press it down with your thumb
Hear that rip and let it go
Whole place shrinking by the minute
Like a song you already know

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more load and then we’re gone
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Baby, we were here too long

On the porch, the keys go cold
In your pocket, in my hand
We laugh at how a whole damn life
Fits in cardboard, rope, and tape
Then the truck bed coughs and waits
Like it’s got nowhere else to be

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more load and then we’re gone
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Baby, we were here too long
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Put the past in, shut it down
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Let the back door fade away now
songs.lyrics_en:
The fridge is a bare white cave
Just one magnet on the floor
You fold up the paper plates
Set the broom against the door
Your fingers stick with old brown tape
You tuck the flaps just right
One last box by the threshold
In that late Sunday light

You press it down with your thumb
Hear that rip and let it go
Whole place shrinking by the minute
Like a song you already know

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more load and then we’re gone

Baby, we were here too long

The hallway smells like dust and rain
And someone else’s stew
A nail hole in the yellow wall
Where your old picture used to
You find a spoon in the sink
A dime under the mat
Little things that say we lived here
And now we ain’t coming back

You press it down with your thumb

Whole place shrinking by the minute

songs.subject: a americana song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The kitchen looks hollow now
Boxes stacked by the door
Your old mug by the sink
And one chair on the floor
I found that blue tape roll
In the junk drawer back left
Now I'm sealing up the scraps
Of the life we both kept

One more corner, one more seam
One more room gone quiet
Got dust on my jeans
And a knot in my chest tonight

Tape it up, tape it up
Last box by the door
Tape it up, tape it up
Ain't a home here anymore
(Last box taped)
(Last box taped)
Hands shaking, heart split wide
Tape it up, tape it up
Then I step outside

Lamp in the hallway gone
Scuffs where the couch once sat
A nail in the wall
Where your calendar hung flat
I wrapped your books in newsprint
Your winter coat in brown
I kept finding little things
That slowed my leaving down

One more corner, one more seam
One more room gone quiet
Got dust on my jeans
And a knot in my chest tonight

Tape it up, tape it up
Last box by the door
Tape it up, tape it up
Ain't a home here anymore
(Last box taped)
(Last box taped)
Hands shaking, heart split wide
Tape it up, tape it up
Then I step outside

Out in the truck bed
That box will ride all night
Past the gas station glow
And the county line
I'll miss that cracked front step
I'll miss the screen door slam
But I can't carry this place
Any further than I am

Tape it up, tape it up
Last box by the door
Tape it up, tape it up
Ain't a home here anymore
(Last box taped)
(Last box taped)
Some things you leave behind
Tape it up, tape it up

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Blue badge on my chest
Keys on my ring
Midnight in the hall
I hear everything
Shoe polish floor
And a cracked red frame
You sit on the wall
Like you know my name

I pace by the ropes
Count steps, not saints
Your gold leaf glows
Through the quiet paint
One eye on the door
One eye on you
You never move
But you always do

Then the room leans in
And the hour turns
I swear that gaze
Makes the old air burn
I say, stay calm
Keep it in line
But you look too alive
For a dead old time

Don’t blink on me
Don’t blink on me
You know my shift
You know my key
Don’t blink on me
Don’t blink on me
You’re still the one
That watches me

School kids tomorrow
Will circle and stare
Point at the brushwork
Like I’m not there
I’ll stand in the gap
Till the sun comes through
Keeping your secret
And mine too

Then the room leans in
And the hour turns
I swear that gaze
Makes the old air burn
I say, stay calm
Keep it in line
But you look too alive
For a dead old time

Don’t blink on me
Don’t blink on me
You know my shift
You know my key
Don’t blink on me
Don’t blink on me
You’re still the one
That watches me

A cracked gold smile
In the dim square light
I catch my face
Inside your night
If you ever leave
I’ll know the sound
Of the wall going thin
As the whole place drowns

Don’t blink on me
Don’t blink on me
You know my shift
You know my key
Don’t blink on me
Don’t blink on me
You’re still the one
That watches me
Don’t blink on me
Don’t blink on me
I lock the door
You lock on me

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum
Another night
My feet they ache
Under the dim light
Just me and the art
A silent pact
No one to see
This lonely act

She's a masterpiece
A painted dream
Every brush stroke
A silent scream
I'm just the guard
With a flashlight beam
Watching her shine
A beautiful scene

The Mona Lisa
She just smirks
But your eyes darling
They do all the work
A splash of red
A streak of blue
I'm falling for you
What can I do

She's a masterpiece
A painted dream
Every brush stroke
A silent scream
I'm just the guard
With a flashlight beam
Watching her shine
A beautiful scene

They say art is dead
But they don't know
The secrets we keep
The feelings that grow
In this quiet hall
Underneath the stars
We're breaking all rules
No matter the scars

She's a masterpiece
A painted dream
Every brush stroke

songs.subject: a mariachi song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I saw you by the bakery
With sugar on your sleeve
You laughed like my old block knew
A secret it should keep
I knew your mama’s porch light
I knew your last name too
Then you looked across the square
And I forgot what to do

Now every little corner
Has your shadow in the dust
Every road I used to know
Turns around and points to us

From my hometown
You stole my heart
From my hometown
Right from the start
Same old streets
New kind of spark
(from my hometown)
You lit my dark

We danced by the taquería
When the band struck up too fast
Your boots kept time like Sunday
Your hand fit like the past
I used to call this place “small”
Now it feels like fate
Every bell and every window
Led me to your gate

Now every little corner
Has your shadow in the dust
Every road I used to know
Turns around and points to us

From my hometown
You stole my heart
From my hometown
Right from the start
Same old streets
New kind of spark
(from my hometown)
You lit my dark

And if I leave these hills behind
Your voice will pull me near
I don’t need faraway dreams
When you are standing here
Let the whole town sing along
Let the whole world see
The best thing I ever found
Was already part of me

From my hometown
You stole my heart
From my hometown
Right from the start
Same old streets
New kind of spark
(from my hometown)
You lit my dark
From my hometown
You’re my best part
From my hometown
You own my heart

songs.subject: a mariachi song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Te vi en la plaza, junto al puesto de maíz
con tu rebozo azul y tu risa de anís
yo iba pasando, fingiendo no mirar
pero tu nombre ya quería regresar
La panadería soltó su olor a pan
y el perro del barrio se puso a ladrar
tú me dijiste: “¿Todavía te acuerdas?”
y el corazón se me fue con tus vueltas

De la misma calle
del mismo portal
tú y yo cargamos
la misma sal
Y aunque lo negué
se me fue la voz
cuando vi tus ojos
diciendo que no

Del mismo pueblo
del mismo sol
me fui enamorando
sin pedir perdón
Del mismo pueblo
tu voz me ganó
(y me quedó temblando)
el corazón
Del mismo pueblo
qué fácil cayó
tu risa en mi pecho
como una canción

En la fiesta del santo te vi bailar
con los zapatitos llenos de polvo y mar
tu mamá en la mesa, sirviendo atole
y yo con mi miedo jugando al valiente
Me hablaste de los cerros, del río al bajar
de la vecina nueva, del campo al sembrar
y entre tanta historia tan de aquí, tan tuya
sentí que mi casa también eras tú

De la misma calle
del mismo portal
tú y yo cargamos
la misma sal
Y aunque lo negué
se me fue la voz
cuando vi tus ojos
diciendo que no

Del mismo pueblo
del mismo sol
me fui enamorando
sin pedir perdón
Del mismo pueblo
tu voz me ganó
(y me quedó temblando)
el corazón
Del mismo pueblo
qué fácil cayó
tu risa en mi pecho
como una canción

Si me preguntas
qué fue lo mejor
no fue la feria
ni aquel acordeón
Fue verte llegar
con el mismo andar
de quien se conoce
desde siempre ya

Del mismo pueblo
del mismo sol
me fui enamorando
sin pedir perdón
Del mismo pueblo
tu voz me ganó
(y me quedó temblando)
el corazón
Del mismo pueblo
qué fácil cayó
tu risa en mi pecho
como una canción

Y si me voy lejos
yo sé dónde estoy
porque llevo tu nombre

songs.subject: a gothic country song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Holiday wreath on the ward door
Half-lit hall and a hand-me-down cart
She signs in with her wet hair pinned up
Coffee gone cold in a paper cup
Eight more hours to the first daybreak
Then the night shift calls her back

Kids in Room Six got applesauce smiles
Old Mr. Kane wants his TV low
She checks their lines by the blinking green glow
Writes little stars on the chart in pen
Somebody’s mama brought pie to the desk
She saved one slice for the break room floor

And the clock keeps dragging
Like a chain through bone
She rubs her eyes and says
“I’ll get y’all home”
One more round
Then one more prayer
The whole town’s sleeping
She’s still right there

Holiday double, hold her name
(holiday double)
Holiday double, keep her flame
She nurses the living
While the dead year waits
Holiday double, call her brave
(holiday double)
Holiday double, through the grave
She walks that corridor
Like a church on wheels

At the chapel window, the snow looks gray
In the vending machine light, she counts her coins
A screen door memory in her hands
Daddy’s boots and a pie on the sill
She’d trade this badge for one slow dance
But the ward keeps ringing her back again

And the clock keeps dragging
Like a chain through bone
She rubs her eyes and says
“I’ll get y’all home”
One more round
Then one more prayer
The whole town’s sleeping
She’s still right there

Holiday double, hold her name
(holiday double)
Holiday double, keep her flame
She nurses the living
While the dead year waits
Holiday double, call her brave
(holiday double)
Holiday double, through the grave
She walks that corridor
Like a church on wheels

When the sun comes up
On the coated glass
She’ll peel off gloves
And the hard night passes
But she leaves a light
In every room
A warm little ghost
That gets them through

Holiday double, hold her name
(holiday double)
Holiday double, keep her flame
She nurses the living
While the dead year waits
Holiday double, call her brave
(holiday double)
Holiday double, through the grave
She walks that corridor
Like a church on wheels

songs.subject: a gothic country song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The hospital halls, they hummed a low tune
Empty and cold beneath the pale moon
Another Christmas Eve, another double shift
Just me and the ghosts, a lonesome gift
The snow fell outside, silent and deep
While the city was lost in its holiday sleep.

Mrs. O'Malley, she coughed through the night
Her breath coming ragged, a flickering light
Young Billy from down by the creek, he just cried
For his mama and daddy, nowhere to hide
I checked all the monitors, watched every screen
The saddest Christmas pageant you've ever seen.

No carols or tinsel, no sweet sugar plum
Just the smell of antiseptic and the beating of a drum
A slow, steady rhythm, counting the hours
Withered hopes blooming like graveyard flowers.

Oh, it's a double shift Christmas, a lonely old song
Where the only bells ringing are the ones going wrong
And the angels are weary, with wings made of lead
Watching over the living, and those almost dead.
Double shift Christmas, no place to be
But here in this ward, for eternity.

A flicker of tinsel on old Doctor Gray's door
He left it up since last year, and maybe before
Said, "Nurse, you're a saint, for sticking around"
But saints don't get paid for the comfort they've found
Just a hot cup of coffee, black as the night
And the promise of dawn, with its cold, silver light.

I remember one year, by the old fireplace glow
My mama would hum, soft and low
Now the fire's gone out, the embers are cold
Just stories and memories, getting so old.
Is there anybody out there, hearing my plea?
Or is this double shift Christmas, just for me?

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise came like a rusty nail
Pried open the night
Left a ragged trail
Of fog in the hollers
Cold and gray
Another dollar
Another day

The ’shine was cookin’
Sweet and strong
Smelled of corn mash
Where it all went wrong
Three hundred gallons
Packed in jars
Reflecting back the distant stars

Old Man Tiber
He just winked
Said
"Boy
This batch is finely linked."

Copper kettle
Copper kettle
Don’t you weep
Secrets that the mountain keeps so deep
Copper kettle
Copper kettle
Morning light
Stole my fortune
Stole my night

Down from the ridge
A whisper flew
"Revenue men are comin' through."
A cold dread settled
Thick and slow
Nowhere left for a poor man to go

The truck tires spun
A frantic sound
Mud splattered high
All around
They shot out the lights
A blinding flash
My life savings
Turned to ash
songs.lyrics_en:
Sunrise came like a rusty nail
Pried open the night
Left a ragged trail
Of fog in the hollers
Cold and gray
Another dollar
Another day

The ’shine was cookin’
Sweet and strong
Smelled of corn mash
Where it all went wrong
Three hundred gallons
Packed in jars
Reflecting back the distant stars

Old Man Tiber
He just winked
Said
"Boy
This batch is finely linked."

Copper kettle
Copper kettle
Don’t you weep
Secrets that the mountain keeps so deep
Copper kettle
Copper kettle
Morning light
Stole my fortune
Stole my night

Down from the ridge
A whisper flew
"Revenue men are comin' through."
A cold dread settled
Thick and slow
Nowhere left for a poor man to go

The truck tires spun
A frantic sound
Mud splattered high
All around
They shot out the lights
A blinding flash
My life savings
Turned to ash

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
I loaded two cans in the dark
Under a tarp by the sheep yard gate
Belted down to the back road bend
With a full jar moon and a restless fate
The old cart squeaked like a guilty man
Every stone made the axles cry
I told my brother, keep your head low
Keep your eyes on the hollow sky

But the hill turned steep
And the mare lost nerve
We heard the dogs
Then we saw the curve

Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Down by the blackthorn lane
Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Gone wrong, gone wrong
We ran from the shame
But the shame knew our names

A lantern flashed from the barley field
And my brother swore like a sinking bell
One can cracked on the gatepost stone
And the sweet smell rose like a spell
We heard boots on the gravel road
We heard a shout through the morning mist
I threw my hat in the ditch and prayed
While the mare kicked free of the harness twist

But the fence held fast
And the wheel gave way
By the time we turned
It was too late to pray

Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Down by the blackthorn lane
Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Gone wrong, gone wrong
We ran from the shame
But the shame knew our names

Now the alehouse laughs when I walk in
They slap the bar and shake their heads
I buy my round with an honest coin
And dream of jars in cedar beds
Still I hear that wheel in the winter mud
Still I see that lantern’s sting
And I bless that night for the lesson learned
A poor man’s gold is a dangerous thing

Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Down by the blackthorn lane
Oh, the moonshine cart went wrong
Gone wrong, gone wrong
We ran from the shame

songs.subject: a blues song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up at dusk
By the black-water pier
Crates sealed with red wax
And a name nobody’d hear
Old man said, "Boy, don’t ask"
But I asked anyway
He crossed himself slow
Then he went on his way

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
That cursed cargo
Won’t leave me alone
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
Every wave I ride
Feels like a stone

First night out, the ropes
Started tightening by hand
Masts gave a hard groan
Like a dying brass band
One crate leaked black brine
On the deck plank seam
And my first mate swore
He heard somebody scream

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
That cursed cargo
Won’t leave me alone
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
Every wave I ride
Feels like a stone

I threw a prayer to the sky
But the sky stayed blind
Moon broke on the water
Like a glass in time

Third dawn, the cook was gone
Only his apron stayed
Fourth tide, I saw my own face
In the soup we made
The compass spun wild
Then it pointed below
Like something in the dark
Wanted out of that hole

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
That cursed cargo
Won’t leave me alone
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
Every wave I ride
Feels like a stone

If I make it to shore
I’ll burn every crate
But the sea keeps my name
Like a debt it won’tేట్

songs.subject: a blues song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Loaded at the dawn pier
With a crate marked black and red
Harbor master crossed himself
And turned his face instead
Three nails in the pine lid
Salt crust on the seam
I heard a moan inside it
Like a thing caught in a dream

We rolled out on a blue-black sea
Wind had teeth that night
Every wave kept calling me
Like it knew my name was tied

That cursed cargo, it won't sleep
That cursed cargo, it sits deep
Rock me, water, take me low
I can't lose what I don't know
(That cursed cargo)
(That cursed cargo)

First mate took the watch at nine
Said he heard it tapping twice
By the second bell he swore he saw
A hand crawl through the ice
The cook quit boiling the beans
Said the steam had human eyes
And the deck grew cold as church stone
Even under summer skies

We tried to throw it overboard
But the rope went tight as wire
Three old men on the forecastle
Swore they saw the sea catch fire

That cursed cargo, it won't sleep
That cursed cargo, it sits deep
Rock me, water, take me low
I can't lose what I don't know
(That cursed cargo)
(That cursed cargo)

At the seventh storm, I opened it
Just enough to see
Not gold, not silk, not contraband
Just my own face staring back at me
Then the hull began to groan
Like it knew the debt I'd stole
Now every port I pass by
Feels like a hole inside my soul

That cursed cargo, it won't sleep
That cursed cargo, it sits deep
Rock me, water, take me low
I can't lose what I don't know
That cursed cargo, hold me fast
Same black shadow from my past
Rock me, water, take me low
I can't lose what I don't know
(That cursed cargo)

(That cursed cargo)
(That cursed cargo)

(That cursed cargo)
(That cursed cargo)

That cursed cargo, hold me fast
Same black shadow from my past
Rock me, water, take me low
I can't lose what I don't know
(That cursed cargo)

songs.subject: a blues song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Our bus rolled in at dusk
Paint peeling off the side
Jackets smelled like rain and road
And a hundred hurts we hide
The field lights buzzed above us
On a Friday cut in half
We taped up what was broken
And laughed our way through that

Coach said, “boys, one more fight”
“Leave it on this grass”
We looked at those old bleachers
And nobody looked back

One last game
One last stand
(grit in our teeth, dirt in our hands)
One last game
We were boys with a plan
(broke, but we still came)

Their helmets gleamed like money
Their bench was loud and clean
Our center wore a dented shell
From a title back in nineteen
By the second quarter
We were down by a pile
But Earl caught one on the sideline
And turned it into miles

Coach kept pacing the chalk line
Like he could move the sky
We heard the crowd get thinner
But not the fire inside

One last game
One last stand
(grit in our teeth, dirt in our hands)
One last game
We were boys with a plan
(broke, but we still came)

Fourth quarter cold and lean
Scoreboard mean as sin
Then that little halfback slipped through
Like a prayer that found a pin
The whole town held its breath
When the clock was chewing time
We lost by seven at the horn
But Lord, we felt alive

One last game
One last stand
(grit in our teeth, dirt in our hands)
One last game
We were boys with a plan
(broke, but we still came)
One last game
Ain’t shame in that
(we gave it all we had)
One last game
And we walked off proud
(boys from the back row crowd)

songs.subject: a blues song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
They taped my number to a jersey hem
Faded red, with the grass still on it
Locker room smelled like bleach and rain
Coach said, boys, one last swing at it
The scoreboard glared like a mean old face
Crowd wore hope like a hand-me-down
We were the last ones left in the race
But we still came to that burnt-out town

Tie my shoes, one more time
Cracked old cleats on a painted line
I seen bigger men fall down
I seen this heart keep up somehow

One last game
One last chance
We got mud on our names
And a storm in our hands
One last game
We can still stand tall
If we lose this one
We gave it all

Their pitcher threw like he held the moon
Their back row moved like a locked-up gate
But our shortstop smiled at the sixth-inning gloom
Said, boys, I didn’t come here to wait
Mama in the bleachers wrung that cap
Like she could squeeze out one more prayer
Little kid yelled, go get ’em back
And for a second, I swore we were there

Tie my shoes, one more time
Cracked old cleats on a painted line
I seen bigger men fall down
I seen this heart keep up somehow

One last game
One last chance
We got mud on our names
And a storm in our hands
One last game
We can still stand tall
If we lose this one
We gave it all

Bottom of nine, and the sky fell low
Our bench was quiet as an empty church
Then that first hit rolled soft and slow
And everybody rose off the dirt
I heard my name like a bell gone wild
I dug in deep, let the fear run past
Ain’t no trophy, ain’t no crown for the child
Just one hard swing at the very last

One last game
One last chance
We got mud on our names
And a storm in our hands
One last game
We can still stand tall
If we lose this one
We gave it all

Give it all
Give it all
On a field gone cold

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I packed my coat in June
And kissed the old front door
Mama held my face so tight
I heard her breathing on the floor
They said the road was water
So I learned to trust the tide

Oh, the waves kept calling
Name by name
My heart was small
But it came

Across the blue, across the blue
(I’m coming through)
Across the blue, across the blue
(To find something true)
Hold my hand, hold my hand
Till the new land stands
Across the blue, across the blue
(I’m coming through)

The deck was all salt and rain
And shoes that never dried
A boy from Naples hummed with me
While the moon kept pace beside
We shared a loaf, a picture
And a prayer we barely knew

Oh, the waves kept calling
Name by name
My heart was small
But it came

Across the blue, across the blue
(I’m coming through)
Across the blue, across the blue
(To find something true)
Hold my hand, hold my hand
Till the new land stands
Across the blue, across the blue
(I’m coming through)

When morning found that harbor
My name felt brand-new
I stepped onto the hard old dock
With a tear and a shoe
But I brought my song with me
And I still hear you too

Across the blue, across the blue
(I’m coming through)
Across the blue, across the blue
(To find something true)
Hold my hand, hold my hand
Till the new land stands
Across the blue, across the blue
(I’m coming through)

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
My coat was blue and worn
My name was on one page
Mama packed dried figs
And a shirt for rainy days
At the harbor I stood still
With your hand in mine
The ferry looked so large
And the shore fell behind

I kept my eyes on you
You kept your eyes on me
One deck light, one old hymn
And miles of open sea

Across the sea
Across the sea
Hold my name, keep it near
Across the sea
Across the sea
I’m coming, can you hear?

Salt burned on my lips
The cabin rocked all night
I traced your letter twice
By the yellow cabin light
A man played cards below
A baby cried in tune
I learned the roll of waves
Like a song I’d have to use

I kept my eyes on you
You kept your eyes on me
One deck light, one old hymn
And miles of open sea

Across the sea
Across the sea
Hold my name, keep it near
Across the sea
Across the sea
I’m coming, can you hear?

New boots on a wooden dock
New air in my chest
I missed the sound of home
But I missed you the best
If the door is hard to find
I’ll knock till it breaks
I crossed that wild black water
For the life we’d make

Across the sea
Across the sea
Hold my name, keep it near
Across the sea
Across the sea
I’m coming, can you hear?
Across the sea
Across the sea

songs.subject: a tango song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
White shoes scuff the tile
At the gift shop clock
You pin your hair back up
And lock the locker lock
The floor keeps calling you
Like a stubborn fire
Coffee gone cold again
But you stay required

Holiday parade outside
You hear it through the glass
You smooth the wrinkled sleeves
And let the moment pass
One more bed, one more chart
One more name to save
You lift that tired chin
Like a flag in the rain

Dance, nurse, dance
Through the double shift
Dance, nurse, dance
With a feverish wrist
(ay, ay)
Hold that line
On the holiday night
Dance, nurse, dance
Till the morning light

In room twelve, old Mr. Lee
Asks if supper’s late
You joke about the pie
And the paper crown you taped
A kid with glitter hands
Waves from the hallway door
You save a candy cane
For the kid with the sore
Your name tag bends a little
When the gurney turns
But your kindness still arrives
Before the call light burns

Holiday parade outside
You hear it through the glass
You smooth the wrinkled sleeves
And let the moment pass
One more bed, one more chart
One more name to save
You lift that tired chin
Like a flag in the rain

Dance, nurse, dance
Through the double shift
Dance, nurse, dance
With a feverish wrist
(ay, ay)
Hold that line
On the holiday night
Dance, nurse, dance
Till the morning light

When the unit goes quiet
And the jokes run thin
You lean on the counter
And breathe it in
The world gets warm and blind
With its family feast
You keep the pulse alive
On the edge of peace

Dance, nurse, dance
Through the double shift
Dance, nurse, dance
With a feverish wrist
(ay, ay)
Hold that line
On the holiday night
Dance, nurse, dance
Till the morning light

songs.subject: a tango song about a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Badge on my coat
Steam on my tea
The hall clock laughs
At Christmas Eve
I tie my hair
Pull on my gloves
Room twelve is crying
Room nine wants love

Two shifts, one name
I keep on moving
Heel to toe
My heart still grooving
The floor keeps time
Under my shoes
I do not stop
I cannot lose

I dance, I dance
Through the long night
I dance, I dance
Holdin' each life

songs.subject: a synthwave song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in your small hand
Both feet on the floor
You grin at the driveway
Like you’ve done this before
I buckle you in slow
Say, "Easy on the brake"
Your fingers find the wheel
And the whole world starts to shake

Look left, then look right
Breathe out, hold still
Blue glow on the dash
And your eyes on the hill

Keep it straight, baby
Keep it straight
Little white line
We’ll meet it and wait
Keep it straight, baby
Don’t you fear
I’m right here
(I'm right here)

The radio’s low and dim
Your laugh cuts through the rain
You tap the turn signal
Like you’ve memorized the lane
A shopping cart rolls by
And you freeze for a beat
Then you ease the car forward
Like a secret in your teeth

Look left, then look right
Hands soft, not tight
Green glow on the hood
And the curb sliding by

Keep it straight, baby
Keep it straight
Little white line
We’ll meet it and wait
Keep it straight, baby
Don’t you fear
I’m right here
(I'm right here)

One day you’ll go alone
Past the corner store
Past the cracked paint mailbox
Past the houses keeping score
But tonight in this old car
We’re learning how to trust
You take the bend so gently
And the fear turns into dust

Keep it straight, baby
Keep it straight
Little white line
We’ll meet it and wait
Keep it straight, baby
You can steer
I’m right here
(I'm right here)
Keep it straight, baby
Keep it straight
One small mile
And you’re almost there

Park it slow
Let go
That’s my girl
You know

songs.lyrics:
Streetlights glow neon bright
My girl grips the wheel so tight
Foot on the gas foot on the brake
A nervous smile for goodness sake

Mirrors adjusted
Seatbelt clicked in

Midnight driver yeah that's you
Learning the ropes everything new
Midnight driver feel the hum
Future calling here it comes

Parking lot spins we go slow
Parallel dreams where do they go
Engine hums a steady beat
No more backseat can't be beat

Remember my words stay alert stay keen
This open road a brand new scene

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama laid the table
For a name I never wore
Papa shook hands quiet
With a man from three farms more
They had my dress picked out
By the feed store and the creek
But I kept hearing my own feet

Down by the cottonwood gate
I watched that sundown shake
And the little gold ring
Felt heavy in my hand

I’m taking the back road now
I’m taking the back road now
You can keep that future set in stone
I was born to roam alone
(taking the back road now)

He said, “Girl, that house is safe”
Like a lock on a screen door
But safe is just a grave
If it’s all you’re living for
So I packed my calico
And a fiddle undercloth
Left a note on the porch post
For the life I nearly lost

Down by the cottonwood gate
I watched that sundown shake
And the little gold ring
Fell hard in the dust

I’m taking the back road now
I’m taking the back road now
You can keep that future set in stone
I was born to roam alone
(taking the back road now)

Let the preacher call it sin
Let the neighbors shake their heads
I heard my own heart speaking
Louder than the words they said
And if I come home someday
It’ll be by my own vows
Not a life somebody else drew out

I’m taking the back road now
I’m taking the back road now
You can keep that future set in stone
I was born to roam alone
(taking the back road now)

Maple road behind me
River road ahead
I chose the open morning
Over what they said

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama set the plates
Papa named the day
White shoes by the door
Like I had no say
The preacher had a date
The cousins had a plan
But my heart was loading up
In the back of a van

I watched the river run
Past the mill and cane
I felt my own name
Calling me again

I’m not your kept bird
I’m not your kept bird
Let the cage door swing
I’m gone before dawn
I’m not your kept bird
I’m not your kept bird
I was born for the road
And I’m moving on

Your ring sat cold as glass
On a folded cloth
Your promises were neat
But my fire was lost
I packed my Sunday dress
Left it on the chair
Took the old train map
And a cut of wind for air

You can keep the pew
Keep the chosen name
I’m chasing my own tracks
Through the sugar cane

I’m not your kept bird
I’m not your kept bird
Let the cage door swing
I’m gone before dawn
I’m not your kept bird
I’m not your kept bird
I was born for the road
And I’m moving on

By the county line
I finally learned
A life picked for me
Is a house that never burns
So I shook the dust
From my heels and pride
If love is a field
Then let it grow wild

I’m not your kept bird
I’m not your kept bird
Let the cage door swing
I’m gone before dawn
I’m not your kept bird
I’m not your kept bird
I was born for the road
And I’m moving on

Mama, don’t you cry
Papa, bless my name
I’m leaving this future

songs.subject: a country song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise finds me pourin' coffee,
Down at the diner, steam in the air.
Greasy griddle, short-order dreams,
Wipin' down counters, brushin' off despair.
Clock hands movin' slow as molasses,
Another dollar earned, another bill to bear.

Two steps forward, three steps back, that's my pace
Runnin' this race, can't find my place.
Sweat on my brow, ache in my bones,
Just tryin' to keep a roof over these old stones.
Two steps forward, three steps back, ain't it the truth?
Workin' my life away, since my wild youth.

When the diner closes, the factory whistle calls,
Punchin' the clock, through concrete halls.
Assembly line hummin', a hypnotic drone,
Another shift starts, feelin' all alone.
Tightenin' bolts, watchin' the minutes crawl,
Wishin' for sleep, before I lose it all.

Two steps forward, three steps back, that's my pace
Runnin' this race, can't find my place.
Sweat on my brow, ache in my bones,
Just tryin' to keep a roof over these old stones.
Two steps forward, three steps back, ain't it the truth?
Workin' my life away, since my wild youth.

Used to dream of a little patch of land,
A porch swing and a helping hand.
Now it's just bills pilin' higher than my hope,
At the end of my rope, tryin' hard to cope.
Sometimes I wonder, what's it all for?
Knockin' on a closed door, beggin' for more.

Two steps forward, three steps back, that's my pace
Runnin' this race, can't find my place.
Sweat on my brow, ache in my bones

songs.subject: a country song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Punch in before the sun
Punch out when it’s gone
Coffee in a paper cup
Keep my head down, keep on
First job takes the daylight
Second one takes the night
By the time I count my tips
I’m just trying to make it right

Gas light on E
Fridge is mostly air
Bills piled high
Like they don’t care
I’m doing all I can
But I’m still one step late

Two jobs, still behind
(two jobs, still behind)
One paycheck at a time
Still behind
I keep running
But the math don’t lie
Two jobs, still behind
Still behind

Boots by the back door
Worn through at the heel
Lunch was a bologna slice
Folded in a paper seal
Mama says hold steady
Hard work pays someday
But the rent man wants his money
And he wants it yesterday

Gas light on E
Fridge is mostly air
Bills piled high
Like they don’t care
I’m doing all I can
But I’m still one step late

Two jobs, still behind
(two jobs, still behind)
One paycheck at a time
Still behind
I keep running
But the math don’t lie
Two jobs, still behind
Still behind

I ain’t asking for a mansion
Just a little room to breathe
A Sunday without worry
And a tank with some relief
If I get one break in this storm
I’ll learn to hold on tight
Till then I’ll keep on showing up
And trying every night

Two jobs, still behind
(two jobs, still behind)
One paycheck at a time
Still behind
I keep running
But the math don’t lie
Two jobs, still behind

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We trained for the dark
Two names on the glass
You drew the first line
I followed too fast
Our gloves touched once
Then the tether slid
Now I spin slow
Like a fault-line lid

Red line blinking
Farther away
Your face gets small
By the second wave
I call your name
Through static and dust
You turn your shoulder
Like you forgot us

We are drifting apart
Drifting apart
(take my hand)
We are drifting apart
Drifting apart
(stay with me)
One ship, two hearts
Breaking the chart
We are drifting apart
Drifting apart

Your window blooms blue
Mine flickers gold
Same dead silence
Different cold
I see your light pack
A thin bright thread
Mine’s wrapped tight
Around my head
You say “hold on”
But the words fall thin
Past the hull seam
And the miles in between

Red line blinking
Farther away
Your face gets small
By the second wave
I call your name
Through static and dust
You turn your shoulder
Like you forgot us

We are drifting apart
Drifting apart
(take my hand)
We are drifting apart
Drifting apart
(stay with me)
One ship, two hearts
Breaking the chart
We are drifting apart
Drifting apart

If I reach now
I might miss you more
If you pull back
We’ll both hit the floor
Earth is a coin
Far off and dim
We were one fire
Now we’re just skin

We are drifting apart
Drifting apart
(take my hand)
We are drifting apart
Drifting apart
(stay with me)
One ship, two hearts
Breaking the chart
We are drifting apart
Drifting apart

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Zero G got us floatin
No ground beneath our feet
Just starlight glowin
Our love a fading heat

Echoes in the comms
Static in the dark

Cosmic driftin
Worlds apart we fly
No more liftin
Just a lonely sky
Cosmic driftin
Goodbye goodbye

Your eyes were constellations
Now just distant gleam
Lost in space stations
A forgotten dream

We built a universe
Just you and me
Now it's a hearse
For what used to be

songs.subject: a tango song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
I got on at Delancey
with a paper cup of cold tea
your sleeve brushed mine
and my chest went thin
The car had that metal smell
wet coats and old coins
the map blinked red
like it knew my name

Then the doors locked shut
and the floor began to tilt
my hands went numb
my throat went still
I tried to count the stops
but the numbers broke apart
one more breath
one more breath

Hold the rail
hold the rail
my heart won't stay
Hold the rail
hold the rail
let this train pass by
One stop, one stop
keep me here
one stop, one stop
through the fear

A kid in a yellow cap
laughed at a falling straw
somebody's shopping bag
kept tapping my knee
I stared at a ad for soap
and the letters swam
my ears filled up
with a private storm

Then the tunnel took my face
and squeezed it into dark
my lungs were glass
my ribs were sparks
I pressed my palm to steel
like it could remember me
one more breath
one more breath

Hold the rail
hold the rail
my heart won't stay
Hold the rail
hold the rail
let this train pass by
One stop, one stop
keep me here
one stop, one stop
through the fear

At Hoyt Street
I saw the light
not holy, just white
but it split the night
I bent my head
to the station floor
and asked my body
for five seconds more

Hold the rail
hold the rail
my heart won't stay
Hold the rail
hold the rail
let this train pass by
One stop, one stop
keep me here
one stop, one stop
through the fear

The doors opened slow
the cold air came
I stepped onto the platform
with my name again

songs.subject: a tango song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
I slid down the steps
with a paper cup
Your name in my pocket
my throat locking up
The turnstile snapped
like a judge’s jaw
My hands went cold
when the tunnel roared

The floor leaned left
then the walls moved in
I counted my ribs
I lost my skin

Hold me now
hold me now
I can’t make the train stop
Hold me now
hold me now
My heart hit the platform

A kid in a school coat
kept chewing gum
A man read the paper
like nothing was wrong
The air got thin
in a flashing frame
I watched my reflection
forget my name

The lights grew long
then they split in two
I needed your voice
to pull me through

Hold me now
hold me now
I can’t make the train stop
Hold me now
hold me now
My heart hit the platform

Open the door
or let me breathe
Count with me slow
one-two-three
The train lurched past
with its iron cry
I found the edge
and stayed alive

Hold me now
hold me now
I can’t make the train stop
Hold me now
hold me now
My heart hit the platform
Hold me now
hold me now
songs.lyrics_en:
I slid down the steps
with a paper cup
Your name in my pocket
my throat locking up
The turnstile snapped
like a judge’s jaw
My hands went cold
when the tunnel roared

The floor leaned left
then the walls moved in
I counted my ribs
I lost my skin

Hold me now
hold me now
I can’t make the train stop
Hold me now
hold me now
My heart hit the platform

A kid in a school coat
kept chewing gum
A man read the paper
like nothing was wrong
The air got thin
in a flashing frame
I watched my reflection
forget my name

The lights grew long
then they split in two
I needed your voice
to pull me through

Hold me now
hold me now
I can’t make the train stop
Hold me now
hold me now
My heart hit the platform

Open the door
or let me breathe
Count with me slow
one-two-three
The train lurched past
with its iron cry
I found the edge
and stayed alive

Hold me now
hold me now
I can’t make the train stop
Hold me now
hold me now
My heart hit the platform
Hold me now
hold me now

songs.subject: a metal song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I light the lamp at dusk
And seal your name in wax
My hands smell like lamp oil
And wet rope on the racks
I write on coarse gray paper
By the gull-scratched glass
Each line a black-winged offering
That I never can take back

The tide keeps chewing the pilings
The moon keeps dragging chains
I fold my heart in shipwreck shapes
And send it out again

Sea, take my letters
Sea, take my letters
I write your name in salt
I write your name in salt
Sea, take my letters
Sea, take my letters
If you cannot love me
Then keep me in your mouth

I watched your foam-backed shoulders
Break on the blackened reef
You came in white and furious
Then left me with your teeth
Now every envelope I burn
Turns to a flock of sparks
They fly like drowned kites upward
And vanish in the dark

The storm glass shakes like a warning
The window groans and bends
Still I lean my whole life forward
For one more crash and send

Sea, take my letters
Sea, take my letters
I write your name in salt
I write your name in salt
Sea, take my letters
Sea, take my letters
If you cannot love me
Then keep me in your mouth

I know you are not gentle
I know you do not stay
You wear a crown of wreckage
And pull the brave away
Still I hear you in the tower
When the foghorn cuts the air
Like some vast and broken heartbeat
Calling me to bare

Sea, take my letters
Sea, take my letters
I write your name in salt
I write your name in salt
Sea, take my letters
Sea, take my letters
If you cannot love me
Then keep me in your mouth
Sea, take my letters
Sea, take my letters
My last flame in the tower
My last love on the foam

songs.subject: a metal song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I ink my hands at dusk
On paper cut from maps
Address it to the tide
By the black stair’s iron clasp
I seal it with whale wax
And slide it through the spray
For you who wear the moon
And take my breath away

Bring me your cold mouth
Bring me your endless skin
I write your name in brine
Then watch it sink again

Salt glass letters
Take my name
Salt glass letters
Call the waves
If you keep me
I’ll keep the flame
Salt glass letters
Lead me home

Your shoulders crack the reef
Your voice is teeth and foam
You hammer on the rocks
Like you are making home
I watch the rigs go dark
I count the gulls in rows
I send you every night
What only fever knows

Bring me your cold mouth
Bring me your endless skin
I write your name in brine
Then watch it sink again

Salt glass letters
Take my name
Salt glass letters
Call the waves
If you keep me
I’ll keep the flame
Salt glass letters
Lead me home

I know you take and take
I know your mercy burns
Yet I still fold my heart
And wait for your return
One day the tower falls
One day the glass will split
Still I will send you more
Until the sea says yes

Salt glass letters
Take my name
Salt glass letters
Call the waves
If you keep me
I’ll keep the flame
Salt glass letters

songs.subject: a blues song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Your name stayed folded
In my denim coat
Turned up by the dryer
With a busted note
I saw your porch light
From the county road
You were gone by morning
I was late, I know

You were the one that got away
Yeah, the one that got away
I held the door too long
Now you live in the rain
You were the one that got away
(The one that got away)

Your grandma’s dishes
Still in my sink
Blue rim, little roses
One chipped at the brink
I poured black coffee
In your green glass cup
Now every damn sunrise
Tastes like giving up

You were the one that got away
Yeah, the one that got away
I held the door too long
Now you live in the rain
You were the one that got away
(The one that got away)

I can hear your boots on the oak wood floor
I can hear that screen door slam
But the truck left dust on the gravel line
And it never came back, damn

You were the one that got away
Yeah, the one that got away
I held the door too long
Now you live in the rain
You were the one that got away
(The one that got away)

songs.subject: a blues song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You left your scarf on the chair
and my keys in the bowl
There’s still two blue cups
by the sink in a row
Your name in my shirt
like a bruise in the hem
I reach for your side
and come back again

Saw your red taillights
turn the corner slow
Rain on the diner glass
and the same old glow
I said “stay one more”
you said “I can’t”
Now I’m holding that door
like a fool in my hand

And I know
I know
Some doors don’t stay
Some hearts don’t wait
You were already halfway gone
when I learned your face

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
You were the one that got away
(sing it now)
I kept the bills, I kept the ring
but I lost the one sweet thing
You were the one that got away
(away, away)

I still hear your laugh
in the screen door slap
See your boots by the mat
when the porch wind taps
Took your Polaroid
from the frame by the bed
Still the same hole in me
where you used to sit

And I know
I know
Some doors don’t stay
Some hearts don’t wait
You were already halfway gone
when I learned your face

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
You were the one that got away
(sing it now)
I kept the bills, I kept the ring
but I lost the one sweet thing
You were the one that got away
(away, away)

If I see you down that road
I won’t beg you back
I’ll just tip my hat
and let the evening crack
But Lord, if mercy’s real
send me one last sign
A worn-out miracle
with your hand in mine

You were the one that got away
(away, away)
You were the one that got away
(sing it now)
I kept the bills, I kept the ring
but I lost the one sweet thing
You were the one that got away

songs.subject: a gospel song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in my hand heart in my throat
Lord give me strength before I choke
She's sixteen now a wild young thing
This driving lesson gonna make me sing

Foot on the gas foot on the brake
For goodness sake

Jesus take the wheelchair
Out on the highway I swear
This child needs a prayer
Jesus take the wheelchair

Mirror check signal check she says
Lord help us all through this mess
Parallel parking a holy fright
I'm seeing stars in broad daylight

Jesus take the wheelchair

This child needs a prayer

Mirror check signal check she says

songs.subject: a gospel song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
I buckled you in slow
Sun on the windshield
Your hands on the wheel
Mine on the prayer
You said, Daddy, I'm scared
I said, Baby, breathe deep
Look at that open road
And keep your eyes there

One hand steady
One heart trusting
Every lesson
Turns to blessing

Jesus, guide her home
Jesus, guide her home
Keep her calm and strong
Jesus, guide her home
Take the wheel
Take the wheel
Through the bends and the hills
Jesus, guide her home

You missed the first turn
Then you laughed out loud
The neighbor waved back
From the end of the block
You checked your mirrors twice
Like you were checking faith
I saw a brave new you
In that little white car

One hand steady
One heart trusting
Every lesson
Turns to blessing

Jesus, guide her home
Jesus, guide her home
Keep her calm and strong
Jesus, guide her home
Take the wheel
Take the wheel
Through the bends and the hills
Jesus, guide her home

And if the road gets long
And if the rain comes down
Let her remember this
She's never alone
You were born for this
You were made to go
I spoke it over you
Now let it grow

Jesus, guide her home
Jesus, guide her home
Keep her calm and strong
Jesus, guide her home
Take the wheel
Take the wheel
Through the bends and the hills
Jesus, guide her home

One day she'll drive away
With her own sweet song
But I'll still hear that prayer

songs.subject: a hip hop song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the pier with a palm full of bread
Said, “Watch how it leaves, then come back instead”
He drew in the sand with a broken cane line
Showed me where the water gets wise over time
Low tide bare rocks, high tide takes blame
Every mark that it makes still remembers its name
He laughed at my shoes, said, “Boy, stay awake”
“Even calm water moves when the moon learns to shake”

He pointed at foam on the posts by the dock
Said, “Nothing stays still when it’s built to unlock”
“Don’t fight what returns, don’t chase what you miss”
“The shore keeps a secret in every eclipse”

Teach me the tide, teach me the tide
How it comes back hard and still lets go wide
Teach me the tide, teach me the tide
I want that old calm rolling deep inside
(Teach me the tide)
(Teach me the tide)

His hands were a map of the salt and the sun
Creases like gull tracks where the years had run
He said, “When it pulls out, don’t call it a loss
That empty wet ground is a lesson in pause”
“If your heart runs dry, let it wait for the moon
What leaves in the morning can circle back soon”
I held that old truth like a crab in my palm
Small, sharp, alive, but it turned me calm

He smiled at the line where the sea met the stone
Said, “You don’t own the wave, but you’re never alone”
“Learn from the pull, and learn from the shove”
“Give what you can, and come back with love”

Teach me the tide, teach me the tide
How it comes back hard and still lets go wide
Teach me the tide, teach me the tide
I want that old calm rolling deep inside
(Teach me the tide)
(Teach me the tide)

That night it was cold and the water was black
I heard his words moving the whole shore back
No straight line living, no pride in the same
Just give, then return with a changed-up name
He left one last look where the moonlight spread
Said, “Son, keep your heart where the current’s fed”

Teach me the tide, teach me the tide
How it comes back hard and still lets go wide
Teach me the tide, teach me the tide
I want that old calm rolling deep inside
(Teach me the tide)
(Teach me the tide)

songs.subject: a hip hop song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the pier
Palm like a map
Said, "Watch that water
It don't move by chance"
He drew in the sand
Two lines and a curve
"This one comes from hunger
This one comes from the earth"
My shoes got wet
He just laughed it off
"Baby, every pull-back
Is a lesson in the loss"

Wait for the breath
Right before it breaks
Don't chase what leaves you
Let it make its wake

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Pull, then return
Hold me tonight
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
What leaves my hand
Comes back with the sky

He said, "See that gull
How it rides the foam?
Never fights the wind
Still it makes it home"
"Same with your hurt
Same with your pride
If you grip too hard
It slips right by"
He tapped his cane
On a busted post
"Even stubborn wood
Learns to float the most"
I nodded slow
As the water climbed
He said, "That's the world
Writ in low and high"

Wait for the breath
Right before it breaks
Don't chase what leaves you
Let it make your shape

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Pull, then return
Hold me tonight
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
What leaves my hand
Comes back with the sky

He got quiet then
Like the moon got near
Said, "I was young once
Full of fight and fear"
"I learned the shore
By the scar it gave
What bends with the water
Can outlast the wave"

Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
Pull, then return
Hold me tonight
Teach me the tide
Teach me the tide
What leaves my hand

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The red sign hums by the road
Two cars sleep in the lot
A spoon clinks in the sink
The counter's worn and hot
Pie sits under glass
Like a moon in a box
The fryers crackle low
Till the whole place rocks

And the clock hands lean
Like they know my name
You pour one more cup
Nothing here feels tame

Stay open all night
Stay open all night
Pour me the dark
Keep the door in sight
Stay open all night
Stay open all night
I can drift here slow
Till the morning light

Your apron has old grease stains
And a pen chain by the till
The jukebox only sighs
But it bends me still
A booth with torn blue vinyl
Holds my coat and my guilt
You slide me eggs and toast
Like a life I almost built

And the glass keeps fogging up
From the coffee steam
Your tired smile cuts through
Like a half-remembered dream

Stay open all night
Stay open all night
Pour me the dark
Keep the door in sight
Stay open all night
Stay open all night
I can drift here slow
Till the morning light

When the highway empties out
And the rain pins down the lot
We are only breath and chrome
And the plates that won't go cold

Stay open all night
Stay open all night
Pour me the dark
Keep the door in sight
Stay open all night
Stay open all night
I can drift here slow
Till the morning light

songs.lyrics:
The pie case sleeps in glass
But the door stays open
Two tired stools by the register
And your name in the sugar jar
A cook turns eggs on a blackened pan
While the ice machine coughs

And the clock keeps leaning
Into the same slow hour
Your coat on the hook
Like you might come back

Stay here with me
All night, all night
Stay here with me
Till the plates turn white
(All night)
Stay here with me
When the sky goes thin
And the diner keeps
Its little fire in

Rain climbs down the window
In crooked silver threads
Your spoon makes a tiny circle
In the bottom of my cup
The jukebox hums through a missing song
Under the booth vinyl seam

And the napkin holder
Shakes with every truck
Past the bleach-bright floor
Past the empty mugs

Stay here with me
All night, all night
Stay here with me
Till the plates turn white
(All night)
Stay here with me
When the sky goes thin
And the diner keeps
Its little fire in

Near four, the waitress laughs once
At the world outside
Fried onions, burnt toast, worn boots
Piled by the door
If morning comes too fast
We can hide in the booth
Let the last warm slice
Hold our names

Stay here with me
All night, all night
Stay here with me
Till the plates turn white
(All night)
Stay here with me
When the sky goes thin
And the diner keeps

songs.subject: a country song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
He clocks in before dawn
Keys on his belt
Walks past the marble lions
Pretend they’ve never felt
He tips his cap to a woman
In a red painted frame
Says, “Morning, ma’am”
Like she might know his name

Every day at nine
He traces that gold line
Painted dress, quiet room
Same two steps, same shine

She hangs in the blue room
Blue room halo
He stands by the velvet rope
Slow as a shadow
He guards that frame
Like it’s made of glass and flame
Blue room halo
He don’t know why
But he stays

Rain taps on the windows
Tourists come and go
He watches little children
Press their noses to the glow
One asks, “Who is she?”
He smiles and says, “Well
Some folks call it art
I call her hard to sell”

And late when the halls
Go thin and hollow
He swears her painted eyes
Follow where he go

She hangs in the blue room
Blue room halo
He stands by the velvet rope
Slow as a shadow
He guards that frame
Like it’s made of glass and flame
Blue room halo
He don’t know why
But he stays

One night the power flickers
The whole place turns to hush
Moon on her collarbone
Moon on his mustache dust
He says, “If you can hear me
You sure have got the grace”
Then he laughs at himself
In that dark and empty space

She hangs in the blue room
Blue room halo
He stands by the velvet rope
Slow as a shadow
He guards that frame
Like it’s made of glass and flame
Blue room halo
He don’t know why
But he stays

Next morning at first light
He’s back in his chair
Tip of his hat to the painting
Like she’s waiting there
Blue room halo
Blue room halo

songs.subject: a country song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
He clocks in at six
With a thermos and a key
Walks past kings in frames
And dust on the century
His boots know every tile
Every crack in the hall
He tips his hat to portraits
Like they might tip one back at all

She hangs in Room Four
In a ribboned velvet dress
A little chipped at the corners
But she still looks her best
Moonlight on her varnish
Like honey on glass
He says, "You sure got quiet
For a woman built to last"

He stands one step away
From a velvet rope and thread
And every night she seems to lean
A little out of bed

He guards that painting
She guards him right back
He watches the hallway
She keeps him on track
One man, one frame
In a sleepless little dance
He guards that painting
And she gives him a chance

Snow comes through the gutters
And the radiator moans
He eats a ham sandwich
In the shadow of old bones
Kids in field trip sneakers
Ask if she can hear
He says, "If you keep it gentle
Maybe she'll draw near"

The clock above the staircase
Ticks like an honest lie
He swears her painted blue eyes
Meet him when no one's by

He guards that painting
She guards him right back
He watches the hallway
She keeps him on track
One man, one frame
In a sleepless little dance
He guards that painting
And she gives him a chance

One night the power flickers
The whole place goes gray
He lights his little flashlight
And the shadows start to sway
Then on the glass she catches
A blink of yellow gold
And for just a breath she looks
Less painted, more told

He guards that painting
She guards him right back
He watches the hallway
She keeps him on track
One man, one frame
In a sleepless little dance
He guards that painting
And she gives him a chance

Now he walks a little slower
Past her frame at closing time
Says, "Save me one more evening

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Four AM, same booth
Your fries still hot
Coffee in a metal pot
You slide in close to me
Window fogged up with steam
Cash drawer sings
To the girl in the apron tie
She knows our name by heart
She knows what we want

And the clock keeps spinning
But this place stays with us
Oil and cinnamon
On your shirt, on my lips
The door says open
Like it means forever

Open all night
Open all night
We can stay right here
Till the sun comes in
Open all night
Open all night
Your seat, my side
Baby, this is home

Blue booth vinyl shine
Syrup on the plate
You laugh with your mouth full
I reach for your hand
Pride in the corner
A cook with a grin
He flips one more order
Like he knows our song
Like he knows our song

And the clock keeps spinning
But this place stays with us
Oil and cinnamon
On your shirt, on my lips
The door says open
Like it means forever

Open all night
Open all night
We can stay right here
Till the sun comes in
Open all night
Open all night
Your seat, my side
Baby, this is home

When the whole town sleeps
We’re still alive
One more refill
One more bite
Hands on the table
World outside
We got time
We got time

Open all night
Open all night
We can stay right here
Till the sun comes in
Open all night
Open all night
Your seat, my side
Baby, this is home
Open all night
Open all night
Don't move, don't go
Just let the morning wait

Like you always had the time

Door keeps swinging in
Strangers and lost friends
The cook nods once
Then the whole room bends
Silverware talks back
When the jukebox breathes
One more round, baby
Stay here with me

Open all night
Open all night
Give me that bite
Open all night
Open all night
Open all night
Till the sunrise
Open all night

Feeding ghosts and the living dead

Door keeps swinging in

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
He clocks in at dawn
Keys at his hip
Coffee gone cold
By the marble lip
Room forty-two
Dust on the frame
He knows every crack
He knows it by name
The tourists come close
Then drift away
But he stands by the wall
Like he’s part of the day

Then the curtain of sun
Hits gold on the oil
And the old painted face
Starts to twist and coil
He straightens his cap
Like he’s seen a sign
One quiet guard
And one stolen divine

Guard the painted girl
Guard the painted girl
He guards her heart
From the crowd’s wild whirl
Painted girl
Painted girl
He bows his head
When the brass horns twirl

At noon, a child asks
“Who is she now?”
He shrugs and smiles
Like he knows somehow
“Maybe a saint
Maybe a queen”
He taps the rope
Keeps the distance clean
But when no one’s there
And the halls are still
He hears her inhale
Against the hill
A brushstroke sigh
A velvet stare
Like she’s been waiting
For him right there

Then the curtain of sun
Hits gold on the oil
And the old painted face
Starts to twist and coil
He straightens his cap
Like he’s seen a sign
One quiet guard
And one stolen divine

Guard the painted girl
Guard the painted girl
He guards her heart
From the crowd’s wild whirl
Painted girl
Painted girl
He bows his head
When the brass horns twirl

Late in the afternoon
The rooms turn slow
A trumpet note hangs
In the afterglow
He takes one step
Past the velvet line
She looks back first
Then she looks divine
He says, “Not today”
But he means, “Not yet”
And the whole old wall
Starts to shimmer and sweat

Guard the painted girl
Guard the painted girl
He guards her heart
From the crowd’s wild whirl
Painted girl
Painted girl
Even stone goes soft
When the trumpets curl
Guard the painted girl
Guard the painted girl
He keeps her secret
In his work-worn world
Painted girl
Painted girl
One old guard
And one wild swirl

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Midnight rolls in slow
Empty halls they gleam
Just me and the old masters
Living out a dream
My flashlight cuts the dark
A silent watchful beam

Oh Mona Lisa you
You sly old smiling queen
I see you every night
What secrets do you mean
Just me and you my love
A silent painted scene

The Rembrandt's heavy gaze
The Van Gogh swirls so bright
I walk my lonely rounds
Under the gallery light
These colors they just sing
Through the long and quiet night

Oh Mona Lisa you
You sly old smiling queen
I see you every night
What secrets do you mean
Just me and you my love
A silent painted scene

They say you're just a canvas

songs.subject: a flamenco song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I passed your window slow
Same broken gate
Same blue paint peeling
Same dead plant at eight
My hands on the wheel
Your side of town
The bakery was closed
But the oven smell hung round

I kept my eyes ahead
I tried, I tried
But that fourth-floor shadow
Pulled me back inside
And the night said your name
Soft, then loud
My chest went tight
When I saw that crowd

Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
My heart won't turn away
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I drove by anyway
(way, way, way)
(oh, I drove by anyway)

The balcony rail
Still leaned left
Like it missed our weight
Like it had not slept
I saw your curtain move
Or maybe not
Just a trick of glass
And the rain on the block
There was laundry out
On the line alone
A white shirt turning
In the porch light's glow

I wanted to stop
I wanted to call
But every red light
Was a little wall
And the engine hummed
Like a low lament
I knew that street
And what it meant

Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
My heart won't turn away
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I drove by anyway
(way, way, way)
(oh, I drove by anyway)

If I turn around
Do I lose the ache?
Do I lose your laugh
In the window shade?
So I let it pass
Let the tires sing
Let the old moon hang
On a chain of things

Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
My heart won't turn away
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I drove by anyway
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I know what I can't say
Past my old apartment
Past my old apartment
I drove by anyway
(way, way, way)
(oh, I drove by anyway)

songs.subject: a flamenco song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blur to streaks of gold
My old apartment stands so cold
A ghost of laughter in the pane
Driving past this memory lane

Engine hums a lonely tune
Underneath a silver moon

Midnight memory lane
Midnight memory lane
Yeah the ghosts they call my name
Midnight memory lane

That fire escape where we would talk
Beneath the stars a silent clock
Each brick each window has a story
Lost in faded midnight glory

Should I stop or just keep going
This old feeling overflowing

songs.subject: a blues song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
That block raised me hard
Broken glass by the stoop
Store got bars on the window
And the bodega sold soup
Mama prayed over my jacket
Daddy left with the rent
I watched the boys on the corner
Like that was all the world meant

Same cracked steps, same sirens
Same dice on the hood
Same old men on the fold-up chairs
Talking 'bout who never made it good
I had dreams in my pocket
But they felt too small to hold
When the nights kept calling my name
And the day felt cold

But I kept my feet moving
Even when the road got thin
Took the long way home
Then I learned where home begins

I made it out the block
I made it out the block
(Come on, come on)
Still got the dust on my shoes
Still got the same old talk
I made it out the block
I made it out the block
(Ah, ah)
Ain't no chain on my future
Ain't no key in their lock

Now I sign my name clean
On a page that was mine
Still I hear that screen door slam
In the back of my mind
I remember June heat
On the hood of that car
How a kid can get lost
Before he knows who he are

But I kept my feet moving
Even when the road got thin
Took the long way home
Then I learned where home begins

I made it out the block
I made it out the block
(Come on, come on)
Still got the dust on my shoes
Still got the same old talk
I made it out the block
I made it out the block
(Ah, ah)
Ain't no chain on my future
Ain't no key in their lock

If you see me smiling now
It took a whole lot of rain
Took a hard-headed heart
And a body full of pain
But I ain't looking back mad
I just know what I survived
That dead-end corner tried me
Still, I walked out alive

songs.lyrics:
Same cracked curb, same bent sign
Same rent note folded over time
Chain-link fence and a barking dog
I knew every hole in that fog
Mama punched in, Papa stayed gone
I learned real quick how to move on
Six apartments in that one brick line
And I swore I'd break that spine

But I kept one eye on the train
Kept one hand steady in the rain
If this block wants my name

I got out that block
I got out that block

I kept my feet, kept my talk
I got out that block
I got out that block

songs.subject: a house song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Toll tag blinking red
Coffee gone cold
Eighteen wheels hummin'
Crossed three state roads
Baby, you awake?
It's me on the line
I saw your porch light
In my mind

Rest stop rain on the glass
Map fold in my lap
I keep one hand on the wheel
One hand on the past

Call me back, call me back
When the house gets quiet
Call me back, call me back
Keep my name alive
Mile marker home
Mile marker home
I’m still rollin', baby
Till I hear your hello

Mud on my boots
Receipt in the dash
Your laugh in my pocket
Like a photograph
Billboard fades out
County line signs
I count your breaths

Rest stop rain on the glass
Map fold in my lap
I keep one hand on the wheel

Call me back, call me back
When the house gets quiet
Call me back, call me back
Keep my name alive
Mile marker home

I’m still rollin', baby
Till I hear your hello

songs.subject: a house song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Cab light on my knuckles
Rest stop coffee gone cold
Forty miles of blacktop
And your voice on hold
Says, "You still awake?"
I say, "I'm close enough"
Hammer on the windows
But I need your love

Three AM, I dial slow
Hear your breath come through
Big rig idling low
But I come back to you
Three AM, don't say goodbye
Keep me on the line
Got the miles in my bones
And your name in my spine

Call me back at three
Call me back at three
Your voice keeps me alive
Call me back at three
Call me back at three
Till I pull into the drive
(three AM)
(three AM)

Mud on my work boots
Map folded by the seat
Bridge lights in the rain
And the ache in my knees
You ask if I'm lonely
I laugh and say, "Yeah"
But your little hello

Three AM, I dial slow
Hear your breath come through
Big rig idling low
But I come back to you
Three AM, don't say goodbye

Call me back at three

One more county line
Then I'm yours
Say my name once
Let it cut the dark
I'll bring my whole road home
In the quiet of your heart

Call me back at three
Call me back at three
Your voice keeps me alive
Call me back at three
Call me back at three
Till I pull into the drive
(three AM)

songs.subject: a folk song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Punch the clock at dawn
Wash my hands in the sink
Take the bus uptown
While the black coffee stinks
Stocking shelves till dark
Then I change my shirt
By the time I make it home
I'm already hurt

Rent's due on Friday
Grocery list is long
I keep on counting dollars
But the numbers don't belong

I'm working two jobs
Still falling behind
Two jobs
Still can't buy time
I'm working two jobs
And I can't get ahead
By the end of the week
I'm already in the red

Second shift at the diner
Grease on my sleeves
Tips in a mason jar
Barely enough to breathe
Boss says hold on
Better days are coming soon
I nod and wipe my face
Under the buzzing room

Shoes are wearing thin
And the truck won't start
I keep my prayers folded
Like a note inside my heart

I'm working two jobs
Still falling behind
Two jobs
Still can't buy time
I'm working two jobs
And I can't get ahead
By the end of the week
I'm already in the red

Mama used to tell me
Don't let the road eat you alive
But I'm too tired to wonder
If we'll ever get by
The leak in the ceiling
The light bill on the wall
Every little thing I earn
Gets swallowed whole

I'm working two jobs
Still falling behind
Two jobs
Still can't buy time
I'm working two jobs
And I can't get ahead
By the end of the week
I'm already in the red
