songs.subject: a folk song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
I punch in at dawn
At the grocery on Pine
Tape on my right shoe
Coffee gone cold by nine
At noon I change shirts
Cross town in the rain
By the time I get home
It's starting again

My rent wants the first half
The lights want the rest
I keep a little back
But the math does what it does best

Two jobs and still behind
Two jobs and still behind
I stack these hours up
But they keep stealing time
Two jobs and still behind
Two jobs and still behind
I bring my best all day
Still can't outrun the line

The boss at the diner
Says, "You look tired, man"
I smile and I nod
As I wipe down the pans
My kid leaves a drawing
On the fridge with a pin
Says, "Dad, come home early"
I fold that one in

There's a leak in the ceiling
And a bill on the chair
I do my small repairs
Like anybody who cares

Two jobs and still behind
Two jobs and still behind
I stack these hours up
But they keep stealing time
Two jobs and still behind
Two jobs and still behind
I bring my best all day
Still can't outrun the line

I know the moon by the curb
I know the beep of the door
I know the shape of a week
When it keeps asking for more
If I don't have much left
I still got this to say
I'm here, I'm trying
I'm making my way

Two jobs and still behind
Two jobs and still behind
I stack these hours up
But they keep stealing time
Two jobs and still behind
Two jobs and still behind
My hands are full of work

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I knew your face by the bakery wall
By the red-brick door and the football call
You were ten years gone and still the same
Same grin, same rain, same hometown name

Then you laughed like Sunday
And I lost my ground
All those years between us
Fell right down

From our own lane
I fell for you
From our own lane
Like I always knew
Same muddy road
Same old sky
Now your name
Won't let me by

We stood by the chip shop, paper in hand
You said, "I still know every line in this land"
Your scarf blew wild in the cold night air
And the whole old town felt young right there

Then you said my name
Like a match to flame
And every half-forgotten place
Came back again

From our own lane
I fell for you
From our own lane
Like I always knew
Same muddy road
Same old sky
Now your name
Won't let me by

Take me past the allotment gate
Past the field where we learned to stay out late
If home is a door
Then you’re the key
And every road back
Leads you to me

From our own lane
I fell for you
From our own lane
Like I always knew (I always knew)
Same muddy road
Same old sky
Now your name
Won't let me by
From our own lane
I fell for you
From our own lane
And I’m home with you

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I saw you by the baker’s door
With flour on your sleeve
Your laugh came out like Sunday bells
Through the rain and leaves
I knew your name from years ago
From schoolyard dust and stone
Now all at once my heart went home
By falling for my own

You know the lane, the bridge, the bend
The cricket on the wall
You wore the town like it was skin
And wore it best of all

You’re from my own lane
You’re from my own lane
And now my heart won’t behave
You’re from my own lane
You’re from my own lane
The whole wide world feels small

Your mam still keeps the curtains blue
Your da still mends the gate
You hum the songs my granny sang
At every harvest wake
We’d pass like shadows in the lane
Then stop and look again
Now every step from your front gate
Feels like the start of rain

You know the names on every stone
The foxgloves on the hill
I never thought the place I loved
Could make me love you still

You’re from my own lane
You’re from my own lane
And now my heart won’t behave
You’re from my own lane
You’re from my own lane
The whole wide world feels small

If I leave, I’ll hear your laugh
In every market square
If I stay, I’ll meet your eyes
And find you waiting there
So let the old road see us through
Let the river take our blame
I came back home to find my heart
Had learned to call your name

You’re from my own lane
You’re from my own lane
And now my heart won’t behave
You’re from my own lane
You’re from my own lane
The whole wide world feels small
You’re from my own lane
You’re from my own lane

songs.subject: a folk song about texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight oil burnin' low
A blue light on my face
Another shadow on the wall
In this lonely
Hollow place
Fingers hover
Cold and slow
Over numbers I shouldn't trace
A name I know
A history
A forgotten time and space

One soft glow
A quiet hum
What have I become?

Ghost in the machine
Whisperin' through the wire
A message unseen
Setting my soul afire
Ghost in the machine
A silent
Dark desire
Am I still me
Or just a ghost of what we were?

The old familiar ache
A knot beneath my ribs
A memory I can't quite shake
Like a tune on broken glibs
Did you ever truly break
Or just learn to live with fibs?
This screen
It holds a fragile stake
In a game of 'what ifs' and 'maybes'

The clock keeps ticking
A steady beat
Outside the window pane
Rain starts falling
Bittersweet
Washing away the stain
Of all the words I can't repeat
All the joy
All the pain
A digital retreat
From a truth I can't sustain

Ghost in the machine
Whisperin' through the wire
A message unseen
Setting my soul afire
Ghost in the machine
A silent
Dark desire
Am I still me

songs.subject: a folk song about texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
I saw your name
Light up my phone
Like a moth in a jar
I should've left it alone
Three little dots
Then your first reply
I told myself "don't"
Still I let you slide

Now the screen keeps glowing
In my hand like a dare
I know where this goes
I know, I don't care

Text me back, text me back
(come on, text me back)
I know I shouldn't
But I like that crack
Text me back, text me back
(keep me in the dark)
One more bad decision
With your name in my heart

Your words come slow
Like rain on tin
I read them twice
Then I read again
I keep my thumb
On the edge of send
Like a fence I know
I ought to bend

Outside my window
The night stays still
But your little blue glow
Says "I probably will"

Text me back, text me back

Text me back, text me back
(keep me in the dark)
One more bad decision
With your name in my heart

If I wake up clean
I'll delete the thread
But tonight I'm yours
In the words I said

Text me back, text me back
(come on, text me back)
I know I shouldn't
But I like that crack
Text me back, text me back
(keep me in the dark)
One more bad decision

Light up my phone
Like a moth in a jar
I should've left it alone
Three little dots
Then your first reply
I told myself "don't"
Still I let you slide

Now the screen keeps glowing
In my hand like a dare
I know where this goes
I know, I don't care

Text me back, text me back
(come on, text me back)
I know I shouldn't
But I like that crack
Text me back, text me back
(keep me in the dark)
One more bad decision
With your name in my heart

Your words come slow
Like rain on tin
I read them twice
Then I read again
I keep my thumb
On the edge of send
Like a fence I know
I ought to bend

Outside my window
The night stays still
But your little blue glow
Says "I probably will"

songs.subject: a metal song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I stared at the phone like a blade
Your name in the glass, half-buried, half-brave
Ten winters learned me how to stay away
But tonight my thumb won’t obey
I hear your porch screen slam in my skull
Old arguments sharp, old silence full
My mouth tastes like pennies and rain
I breathe once, then I call your name

The ring cuts hard
Through the static scar
I almost hang up
Then I hear your heart

Mother, pick up
It’s me
Mother, pick up
Please breathe
I’m still here
I’m still yours
After all these years
I’m at your door

You say hello like you’ve been awake
Like you’ve been holding the shape of my ache
I can’t find words that don’t break wrong
So I let the silence drag along
There’s a dog barking far down the block
A screen-door sigh, a ticking clock
You ask how I’ve lived, I say “Badly, but”
Then I swallow the rest and shut it up

The line goes thin
Then you laugh at me
A small cracked sound
Like relief with teeth

Mother, pick up
It’s me
Mother, pick up
Please breathe
I’m still here
I’m still yours
After all these years
I’m at your door

I was a boy in a room full of hail
Learning to armor up, learning to fail
You were the fire I ran from first
Then the only hand I ever cursed
Now every mile comes due in pain
Every wrong turn spells out your name
If I’m honest, I was never free
Not while your shadow was carrying me

Say my name
Say it slow
I’m not a ghost
I’m not a hole
Open the hurt
Open the past
I made it this far
I’m coming back

Mother, pick up
It’s me
Mother, pick up
Please breathe
I’m still here
I’m still yours
After all these years
I’m at your door
Mother, pick up
Don’t let go
Mother, pick up
Let me know
I’m still here
I’m still yours
After all these years
I’m at your door

songs.subject: a metal song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I found your number
folded in a bill
ink gone pale
but the digits still
I stood there staring
at the chipped blue tile
thumb on the screen
for a dangerous while
Years like rust
on the same old chain
I heard my name
like a guilty stain
The room went small
the fan went thin
I hit call
and it started ringing in

One ring
two ring
my throat turned stone
Three ring
four ring
I’m coming home
I said hello
like a man on fire
You breathed my name
and the line got higher

Mama, I’m here
(mama, I’m here)
Mama, I’m here
(after all these years)
If I break now
let me break clean
Mama, I’m here
(mama, I’m here)

You asked if I ate
you asked if I slept
like every bad year
was something kept
Behind your laugh
was a trembling blade
I heard the years
we never made
I said I’m sorry
for the war I wore
For every slammed door
for the months I ignored
You went quiet
then you said my name
and the guilt in my chest
learned a new shape

One ring
two ring
my hands won’t shake
Three ring
four ring
this heart won’t break
I said I’m scared
you said come near
That old ache
turned into clear

Mama, I’m here
(mama, I’m here)
Mama, I’m here
(after all these years)
If I break now
let me break clean
Mama, I’m here
(mama, I’m here)

I was a ghost in my own bloodline
All sharp teeth
and borrowed time
But your voice
cut through the iron
like a warm hand
through the wire and grime
Say my name
one more time
Say my name
and I’ll step inside

Mama, I’m here
(mama, I’m here)
Mama, I’m here
(after all these years)
Let the hurt fall
let the shame bleed out
Mama, I’m here
(mama, I’m here)
Mama, I’m here
(mama, I’m here)
I still know you

songs.subject: a tango song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the asphalt deep
Right over the baker’s lane
The barber shop went first
Then the fountain and the train
Now your roots hold fast
In a crack by the post office wall
One green fist in the dust
While the bulldozers call

I saw your branches shake
When the trucks rolled through
Like you knew the names
Of every roof we knew
The windows stared in black
The dogs went still at noon
And you stood there, tall
Under a paper moon

Last tree in town
Hold your ground
(Last tree in town)
Last tree in town
Don’t bow down
(Last tree in town)
Give us one more shade
One more sign
In this hard baked place
Be the final line

The mayor marked the blocks
With a red felt pen
Said “fresh and modern” twice
Like a prayer for men
But your bark still keeps
Old initials in its skin
A kid carved “Mara loves Javi”
And the year they moved in

Now the pigeons land
Like they’re late for grace
And the butcher’s old awning
Blows from its place
I hear your leaves at dusk
Like coins in a jar
Like the town we lost
Still circling hard

Last tree in town
Hold your ground
(Last tree in town)
Last tree in town
Don’t bow down
(Last tree in town)
Give us one more shade
One more sign
In this hard baked place
Be the final line

Let them pave the square
Let them name the blocks
I’ll bring you water
In a rusted box
If the sirens come
If the cameras flash
I’ll stand at your trunk
Till the last trucks pass

Last tree in town
Hold your ground
(Last tree in town)
Last tree in town
Don’t bow down
(Last tree in town)
Give us one more shade
One more sign
In this hard baked place
Be the final line
Last tree in town
Stay alive
(Last tree in town)
Last tree in town
Teach us how to rise

songs.subject: a tango song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights glowin' on a tar sea
No green thing left just you and me
One lonely tree standin' tall
Hear the city's concrete call

Wind whispers secrets through your leaves
What this asphalt town believes

Concrete Tango a silent sway
Last tree dancin' come what may
Concrete Tango a final sigh
Underneath a grey sky

Roots crackin' pavement a gentle fight
Against the endless urban blight
Birds don't sing here they just fly
To some place green way up high

They paved paradise to build their mall
Didn't think about the fall
Of every flower every vine
Just a dollar a bottom line

Concrete Tango a silent sway
Last tree dancin' come what may

songs.subject: a reggae song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
At the bus stop bent in rain
Your shoes were soaked, same as mine
You stared at the darkening street
I held my bag, you held your pride
One umbrella, one small dome
Side by side, we took it slow
Your sleeve brushed mine by accident
And neither of us let it show

The rain came down in sheets
We laughed at how it found us
Two strangers in the middle of it all
Standing close enough to count us

One umbrella, one umbrella
(don't let go) one umbrella
We don't need a name tonight
Just this little shared light
One umbrella, one umbrella
(oh-oh) one umbrella
You and me against the rain
Till the next train comes again

You said you missed your stop
I said mine was three blocks more
We both watched water run
Off the cracked old corner store
Your hand found the handle first
Then mine wrapped around the same
For a minute, all the world
Was just the sound of dripping rain

The sky kept talking loud
But we talked even softer
Two strangers under borrowed luck
Walking farther, walking farther

One umbrella, one umbrella
(don't let go) one umbrella
We don't need a name tonight
Just this little shared light
One umbrella, one umbrella
(oh-oh) one umbrella
You and me against the rain
Till the next train comes again

At the crosswalk, we both stopped
For a second, face to face
You smiled like you knew my name
Then you vanished into place
But I kept that folded scene
Like a note inside my coat
How a storm can open doors
With one small roof of hope

One umbrella, one umbrella
(don't let go) one umbrella
We don't need a name tonight
Just this little shared light
One umbrella, one umbrella
(oh-oh) one umbrella
You and me against the rain
Till the next train comes again

songs.subject: a reggae song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came hard on Market Lane
You laughed like you knew my name
I had one hood, one tired coat
You had wet hair and a train to catch
I said, "Come close, take half of this"
You said, "Fine, but don't make this a thing"
So we walked slow, heel to heel
Two strangers under one small shield

Your shoulder brushed my sleeve
The whole sky cracked above
You looked at me and smiled
Like that was enough

One umbrella, one road
You and I in the downpour
One umbrella, one road
Barely knew you, wanting more
Stay a little closer now
Let the rain fall all around
One umbrella, one road
Two strangers, one road

At the red bus stop, your bag dripped brown
You shook off drops and looked around
I split my bread, you split your peach
We ate in silence by the curb
Then you told me your town was south
I told you mine was four stops out
No big plans, no clever line
Just two hearts in borrowed time

The rain beat soft and low
On the shop awning tin
You said, "If I miss this ride"
I said, "Then walk with me again"

One umbrella, one road
You and I in the downpour
One umbrella, one road
Barely knew you, wanting more
Stay a little closer now
Let the rain fall all around
One umbrella, one road
Two strangers, one road

When the bus door hissed and sighed
You paused there by my side
Your hand touched mine for half a second
Like a match in the rain
You said, "If we meet by chance
Don't act like you forgot my face"
Then you stepped into the window glow
And I stood still in the pouring day

One umbrella, one road
You and I in the downpour
One umbrella, one road
Barely knew you, wanting more
Stay a little closer now
Let the rain fall all around
One umbrella, one road
Two strangers, one road

If I pass that stop again
I’ll bring two cups and one old grin
'Cause some small things change your day
Like one umbrella, and a name you almost say

songs.subject: a metal song about looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
I wore my thrift-store boots
And a borrowed black tee
Hands shook over the strings
Like they knew the crowd knew me
Mic stand leaned like a jail bar
Floorboards split under my weight
I said my name too fast
Then I met my own mistake

Mouth went dry
Room went still
One bad breath
And I lost my will
But I held that chord
Like a blade to the night
And the first note cracked
Like a warning bite

First open mic
I still see it
First open mic
I still bleed it
(First open mic)
I was coming undone
(First open mic)
But I sang to the sun
First open mic
I still keep it

Someone dropped a glass
Somebody laughed too loud
My voice hit the rafters
Then fell back down
I missed one line
Then I made it rhyme
Turned a shame-soaked stumble
Into stolen time
Every weak little tremble
Every face in the dim
Built a fire in my ribs
Now I feed it again

Mouth went dry
Room went still
One bad breath
And I lost my will
But I held that chord
Like a blade to the night
And the first note cracked
Like a warning bite

First open mic
I still see it
First open mic
I still bleed it
(First open mic)
I was coming undone
(First open mic)
But I sang to the sun
First open mic
I still keep it

Now the years hit harder
But I learned the sound
Of a shaking hand
Refusing to bow
That room was a furnace
That night was a test
And the kid on that stage
Was the start of the rest
So cut me open
I remember the spark
One cheap little mic
And a hole in the dark

First open mic
I still see it
First open mic
I still bleed it
(First open mic)
I was coming undone
(First open mic)
But I sang to the sun
First open mic
I still keep it
First open mic
And I beat it

songs.subject: a metal song about looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
I wore my thrift-store suit
And shook in the side-room mirror
One cheap mic, one hot bulb
And a page full of borrowed fire
My hands looked bruised by fear
But I stepped up anyway

The room smelled like spilled beer
And old wood, and bad decisions
You leaned on the back wall
With that grin like you knew my name
I opened with a joke
Then my voice cracked in the light

I heard the crowd go still
My pulse went wild like a siren
Every word hit bare skin
Every breath cost me blood

First open mic
I still hear it
First open mic
I still feel it
Paper cup throne
And a mouthful of thunder
First open mic
I was born in the rupture

I missed a line, kept going
Saw one kid nod in the corner
That was enough for me
To stop apologizing
I left with ringing ears
And the taste of iron pride

I heard the crowd go still
My pulse went wild like a siren
Every word hit bare skin
Every breath cost me blood

First open mic
I still hear it
First open mic
I still feel it
Paper cup throne
And a mouthful of thunder
First open mic
I was born in the rupture

Now I walk on bigger stages
But I keep that first scar close
One bad room changed my blood
One shaky night made a road
If I fall, I remember
How the fear turned to flame

First open mic
I still hear it
First open mic
I still feel it
Paper cup throne
And a mouthful of thunder
First open mic

I wore my thrift-store suit
And shook in the side-room mirror
One cheap mic, one hot bulb
And a page full of borrowed fire
My hands looked bruised by fear
But I stepped up anyway

The room smelled like spilled beer
And old wood, and bad decisions
You leaned on the back wall
With that grin like you knew my name
I opened with a joke
Then my voice cracked in the light

I heard the crowd go still
My pulse went wild like a siren
Every word hit bare skin
Every breath cost me blood

First open mic
I still hear it
First open mic
I still feel it
Paper cup throne
And a mouthful of thunder
First open mic
I was born in the rupture

I missed a line, kept going
Saw one kid nod in the corner
That was enough for me
To stop apologizing
I left with ringing ears
And the taste of iron pride

Every breath cost me blood

First open mic
I still hear it
First open mic

First open mic

But I keep that first scar close

One shaky night made a road
If I fall, I remember
How the fear turned to flame

First open mic
I still hear it
First open mic
I still feel it

songs.subject: a synthwave song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blur electric haze
Another year another phase
Your laugh lines deeper now I see
A quiet storm inside of me

Time it flies so fast
A shadow cast

Fading neon dream
You're fading neon dream
My hero strong and true
What am I gonna do

Your stories spin a silver thread
Of battles fought and words unsaid
Your hair it gleams a winter white
Reflecting back the fading light

I see your eyes a gentle gleam
A quiet knowing a silent scream
The

songs.subject: a synthwave song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hum a neon glow
Another Friday night moving slow
Dad watches the news so low
His hair now white like winter snow

The future calls
The past it stalls

Father Time keeps marching on
Sunrise to sunset gone
Father Time a silent song
Where did the young man run

He tells old stories of his youth
A fading picture a painful truth
His eyes hold a gentle proof
Life's fleeting unkempt roof

I see his hands they tremble light
Once strong they held me through the night

He tells old stories of his youth

His eyes hold a gentle proof
Life's fleeting unkempt roof

I see his hands they tremble light
Once strong they held me through the night

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in with the dawn
Clock out in the dark
One name on my badge
One more on my shirt
Bus pass in my hand
Coffee gone cold
I count what I owe
Then watch it unfold
The rent on my phone
The gas on E
I’m doing the math
But the math won’t keep

Running on fumes
Still I show face
Two hands, two jobs
One slipping place
I’m chasing the day
It keeps moving back
I’m close to the line
Then I’m off the track

Two jobs, still behind
Still behind, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I can’t catch my own life
Two jobs, still behind
Still behind, still behind
I keep moving fast
But I fall further down

Late by a week
Short by a bill
Got no time to breathe
But the ledger won’t fill
Boss wants a smile
Landlord wants more
My shoes hold the rain
On the break-room floor
I text I’m okay
Then I work through pain
Name on the list
But it gets erased again

Running on fumes
Still I show face
Two hands, two jobs
One slipping place
I’m chasing the day
It keeps moving back
I’m close to the line
Then I’m off the track

Two jobs, still behind
Still behind, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I can’t catch my own life
Two jobs, still behind
Still behind, still behind
I keep moving fast
But I fall further down

If I stop, it stacks
If I sleep, it grows
I’ve been splitting myself
Till nobody knows
One more hour
One more ride
I’m trying, I’m trying
To stay on my side

Two jobs, still behind
Still behind, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I can’t catch my own life
Two jobs, still behind
Still behind, still behind
I keep moving fast
But I fall further down
Two jobs, still behind
Still behind, still behind
I’m still in the race
Even losing ground

songs.lyrics:
Clock in early
Clock out late
Two badges on me
Still can't clear the slate
Coffee gone cold
Bills on the floor
I make the hours
They make more
My left hand pays
My right hand waits
Rent comes first
Then the fridge gets scraped

I keep running
But the lane stays long
I keep giving
And the week stays gone

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I chase the day
It cuts the line
Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I work, I work
Still out of time

Bus home standing
Back aches in knots
Tired in my bones
But I can't stop
Boss says "good hustle"
Like it's a prize
I smile, I nod
With sleep in my eyes
One hand on a tray
One hand on the wheel
I'm patching up a life
That won't stay still

I keep running
But the lane stays long
I keep giving

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I chase the day
It cuts the line
Two jobs, still behind

My chest goes tight
One more bill

I don't need gold
I need a break
I need one week
That I can actually take

Two jobs, still behind
Two jobs, still behind
I chase the day
It cuts the line

Two jobs, still behind
I work, I work

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
I climbed the hill by the sheepfold gate
Boots full of mud, the air so keen
Snow on the peat and the blackthorn bare
And the night held still between
A lantern dim in the farmer's shed
A curlew crying over stone
Then the sky began to tremble soft
Like a prayer that found its tone

Up they rose
Green and gold
Over heather, over frost
And my heart
Lost its hold
At the edge where the dark was crossed

Dance for me, green fire
Dance across the moon
Dance for me, green fire
Over the bay at noon
Oh, northern lights
Hold me in your ring
Oh, northern lights
Make the old hills sing

My mother said, "When the winter bites
Look north and keep your eyes alive"
So I stood with her words in my coat
Like a coal that would not die
The river wore a ribbon of glass
The cattle slept in their little steam
And the heavens turned like a spinning wheel
In a cloak of silver dream

Up they rose
Green and gold
Over heather, over frost
And my heart
Lost its hold
At the edge where the dark was crossed

Dance for me, green fire
Dance across the moon
Dance for me, green fire
Over the bay at noon
Oh, northern lights
Hold me in your ring
Oh, northern lights
Make the old hills sing

If I am far from the island foam
If I am lost on a stranger road
Send me that wild and woven glow
To lighten the load I hold

Dance for me, green fire
Dance across the moon
Dance for me, green fire
Over the bay at noon
Oh, northern lights
Hold me in your ring
Oh, northern lights
Make the old hills sing

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
I walked the peat road home
With frost on my boots
The black lake held the stars
Like coins in a bruise
My old dog stopped and stared
At the hilltop stone
Then the sky began to move
Like the sea had come

Green fire on the ridge
Silver in the dark
It called my name in rings
It lit the hawthorn bark

Dance, northern lights, dance on
(Over the brae)
Dance, northern lights, dance on
(Lead me away)
I will follow your trail
Till the cold turns gold
Dance, northern lights, dance on
Over my soul

Mother said, "Don't name it
It listens when you do"
Father kept the kettle warm
And the turf fire too
But I saw the colors turn
In the deer-track snow
A ribbon from the great north wind
Letting the whole world know

Green fire on the ridge
Silver in the dark
It called my name in rings
It lit the hawthorn bark

Dance, northern lights, dance on
(Over the brae)
Dance, northern lights, dance on
(Lead me away)
I will follow your trail
Till the cold turns gold
Dance, northern lights, dance on
Over my soul

If I am gone by dawn
Leave a lamp in the sill
Tell the sea to keep its pace
Tell the hill to keep its chill
I'll be walking with the geese
Past the edge of the thorn
Where the sky bends low to kiss
The ones who kept me warm

Dance, northern lights, dance on
(Over the brae)
Dance, northern lights, dance on
(Lead me away)
I will follow your trail
Till the cold turns gold
Dance, northern lights, dance on

songs.subject: a funk song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I sleep in the truck bed
Mud on my boots
Radar on the dash
And a split in the roof
One hand on the wheel
One eye on the sky
If the clouds start talking
I don’t ask why
I got a red dirt map
Folded in my glove
Every small-town stop
Still ain’t enough
You say slow it down
Settle in with me
But my heart hears thunder
And it starts to leave

Take my hand, baby
Hold on tight
I’m already leaning
Toward the night
That rumble calling
Way off in the west
You know I’m happiest
When I’m hard to catch

I can’t settle down
I chase the thunder now
I can’t settle down
I’m gone when the sky turns loud
(Thunder now)
I can’t settle down
I chase the thunder now
(Oh no)
I can’t settle down
I’m gone when the sky turns loud

Gas station coffee
Paper cup smoke
County fair parking lot
Six minutes broke
You kissed me quick
By the tarp and the chain
Said “stay this time”
I just said “maybe” again
I know your porch light
Knows my silhouette
Knows I’m half in town
And half a regret
But when the wind leans in
And the windows shake
I’m already out there
At the edge of the wake

Take my hand, baby
Hold on tight
I’m already leaning
Toward the night
That rumble calling
Way off in the west
You know I’m happiest
When I’m hard to catch

I can’t settle down
I chase the thunder now
I can’t settle down
I’m gone when the sky turns loud
(Thunder now)
I can’t settle down
I chase the thunder now
(Oh no)
I can’t settle down
I’m gone when the sky turns loud

Maybe I was built
For the long road spin
For the big dark front
And the rush I’m in
If I ever came home
And stayed too long
Would the quiet keep me
Or prove me wrong?

Woo!
Let it roll
Let it roll

I can’t settle down
I chase the thunder now
I can’t settle down
I’m gone when the sky turns loud
(Thunder now)
I can’t settle down
I chase the thunder now
(Oh no)
I can’t settle down
I’m gone when the sky turns loud
I can’t settle down
I chase the thunder now
(Thunder now)
I can’t settle down
I’m gone when the sky turns loud

songs.subject: a funk song about a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I wake up facing west
With mud on my boots
Map full of red lines
And a tank low on juice
I kiss the porch goodbye
Before the kettle sings
You know I love the road
But the road don't love me back

Clouds roll in
My name in the sky
I pack my coat
When the sirens cry
Hold on tight
I don't stay long
When the wind gets mean
I am gone

Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I'm gone with the storm now
Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I hear that thunder call me home
(Thunder call me home)

Your mama says slow down
Your dog knows my steps
I leave a fresh set of tracks
And a promise I can't keep yet
I got a ring in my pocket
And a face I can't forget
But a low black wall
Keeps rolling over the land

Clouds roll in
My name in the sky
I pack my coat
When the sirens cry
Hold on tight
I don't stay long
When the wind gets mean
I am gone

Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I'm gone with the storm now
Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I hear that thunder call me home
(Thunder call me home)

Maybe one day
I'll stop at your gate
Plant my keys in the dirt
And let the rain wait
But the horizon keeps
That hungry grin
And I chase what breaks
Instead of letting it in

Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I'm gone with the storm now
Can't settle down
Can't settle down
I hear that thunder call me home
(Thunder call me home)

Mud on my boots
Wind in my bones
I love you hard
But I can't stay home
Can't settle down

songs.subject: a gospel song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The chairs are folded now
Coffee in the hall
Black dresses by the door
And your picture by the wall
We walked through tears last night
We called your name aloud
But morning came slow
And the house is quiet now

Still, the sun came up
On a broken heart
Still, Your mercy found us
Right where we are

Morning after mercy
Hold us in Your hand
Morning after mercy
Help us understand
You are still our refuge
Still the only way
Morning after mercy
Carry us today

The lilies in the vase
Lean toward the light
Grandma’s old blue Bible
Open to Psalm twenty-three tonight
There’s an empty chair
But not an empty room
We feel the tears around us
And we feel You moving too

Still, the sun came up
On a broken heart
Still, Your mercy found us
Right where we are

Morning after mercy
Hold us in Your hand
Morning after mercy
Help us understand
You are still our refuge
Still the only way
Morning after mercy
Carry us today

We will not forget
We will not let go
Grief may shake the table
But it won’t shake our soul
You were with us then
You are with us now
And the stone of sorrow
Will be rolled away somehow

Morning after mercy
Hold us in Your hand
Morning after mercy
Help us understand
You are still our refuge
Still the only way
Morning after mercy
Carry us today

Carry us today
Carry us today
Amen

songs.subject: a gospel song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning
Sun in my eyes
Another day dawning
Still a surprise
Thought the world ended
With your last breath
Guess it just bended
Defying all death

My heart feels heavy
My soul feels torn
But something inside me
Says a new day is born

The sun still shines
Oh it still shines
Breaking through the blinds
Oh it still shines
A new day is here
No more tears
Wipe away the fear
The sun still shines

Coffee is brewing
Same old routine
Life keeps on moving
A familiar scene
Footsteps on the pavement
Kids off to school
A strange rearrangement
Breaking every rule

They say time heals all wounds
A bitter sweet lie
But maybe these tunes
Will help me get by
A little bit stronger
A little bit more
Standing here longer
Knocking on hope's door

The sun still shines
Oh it still shines
Breaking through the blinds
Oh it still shines

songs.subject: a dream pop song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
You open the door
And it finds me first
Lemon on the air
And a sugar burst
Blue apron hanging
By the pantry frame
Your hands moving slow
Calling out my name

Steam on the window
Tin on the stove
I lean in closer
And I already know
My chest goes quiet
My head goes white
Like falling asleep
In the middle of light

Butter cake window
Stay with me
Butter cake window
Sweet as can be
Your kitchen stays
On my sleeve
Butter cake window
Mend me

Rice in the colander
Beans in the pot
Cardamom crushed
In a chipped teacup spot
Your laugh in the hallway
Your keys in the bowl
A lace curtain breathing
Like it has a soul

Steam on the window
Tin on the stove
I lean in closer
And I already know
My chest goes quiet
My head goes white
Like falling asleep
In the middle of light

Butter cake window
Stay with me
Butter cake window
Sweet as can be
Your kitchen stays
On my sleeve
Butter cake window
Mend me

When the house got empty
I kept that scent
On my coat, in the draw
Where the winter went
Now one small sugar note
Can bring you home
Can bring you home

Butter cake window
Stay with me
Butter cake window
Sweet as can be
Your kitchen stays
On my sleeve
Butter cake window
Mend me

songs.subject: a dream pop song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The house remembers
Before I do
Steam on the window
Salt in the stew
Your yellow apron
Hanging by the sink
Bay leaves drifting
When the kettle links

I breathe it in
And I’m small again
Flour on your wrist
Warm light on the pan

Pine soap, aprons
Pine soap, aprons
I’m back in your kitchen
Hands in the flour
Pine soap, aprons
Pine soap, aprons
Your love on the air
Stays for an hour

Peaches in a bowl
Brown sugar rain
Radio humming
Soft as the rain
The spoon against the pot
The door half-closed
Cardamom and onions
On my clothes

I breathe it in
And I drift away
Your laugh in the steam
Still here today

Pine soap, aprons
Pine soap, aprons
I’m back in your kitchen
Hands in the flour
Pine soap, aprons
Pine soap, aprons
Your love on the air
Stays for an hour

When the world gets loud
I come back here
To the worn old table
And the soft old years
One deep breath
And you are near
In the whole warm room
You’re everywhere

Pine soap, aprons
Pine soap, aprons
I’m back in your kitchen
Hands in the flour
Pine soap, aprons
Pine soap, aprons
Your love on the air

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded the jars in the dark
Wrapped them in my old coat
Your dog barked once, then stopped
At the county line I felt it slow
Glass clinked soft in the back
Like a warning in a jar
You said, "Keep your eyes on me"
But I could hear the sirens far

White road, black sky
Hands on the wheel, too tight
Your name on my tongue
Like a match in the night

Mason jar moon
Breaks on my roof
Mason jar moon
We were too soon
(too soon)
Mason jar moon
Still shines blue
When the road went wrong
I was holding you

Flares washed the ditch in red
Mud on my boots, on your dress
You laughed like it was a joke
Then your laugh turned into smoke
One jar rolled to the grass
One burst open on the curb
Sweet spill on the cold night air
And the whole dark world got heard

White road, black sky
Hands on the wheel, too tight
Your name on my tongue
Like a match in the night

Mason jar moon
Breaks on my roof
Mason jar moon
We were too soon
(too soon)
Mason jar moon
Still shines blue
When the road went wrong
I was holding you

Now the fields go pale
When the wind comes through
I still smell that sugar burn
When I think of you
If I close my eyes
I can see it all
Silver on the broken glass
And the way we watched it fall
(fall, fall)

Mason jar moon
Breaks on my roof
Mason jar moon
We were too soon
(too soon)
Mason jar moon
Still shines blue
When the road went wrong
I was holding you
(holding you)
Mason jar moon
Mason jar moon

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
We left at dusk
Tin cans in the bed
Sugar smell on my jacket
Road dust in my head
Your taillight swayed
Like a warning sign
I kept my eyes on the lane
And the bottle line

Then the river road
Split like a scar
Blue on the hill
And a doorless car
You said, keep moving
Keep your chin low
But my hands shook hard
At the fork in the road

Gone wrong, gone wrong
We were gone too long
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Now the moon won't stay gone
(gone wrong)
Gone wrong, gone wrong
We were gone too long

Glass in the ditch
And your coat half-burned
I saw your name
In the smoke, unlearned
The deputy's light
Cut the field in two
You laughed like you knew
What the dark would do

Then the river road
Went white as bone
I heard that siren
Way down low
You grabbed my sleeve
Said, don't look back
But the night got wide
And the sky went black

Gone wrong, gone wrong
We were gone too long
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Now the moon won't stay gone
(gone wrong)
Gone wrong, gone wrong
We were gone too long

Your hand in mine
Like a match in rain
I still taste the fear
And the sweet, sweet stain
If we make it out
We won't speak of this
Just a bent-up truck
And a cracked eclipse

Gone wrong, gone wrong
We were gone too long
Gone wrong, gone wrong
Now the moon won't stay gone
(gone wrong)
Gone wrong, gone wrong
We were gone too long
Gone wrong, gone wrong

songs.subject: a house song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning, page by page
My lamp makes a tiny stage
Pen taps out a steady code
I’m still here, I’m in the mode
Stacks on the desk, one clean line
Highlighter marks in a yellow shine
My phone face down, let it sleep
I’ve got a promise I can keep

Turn the page
Count to ten
One more proof
Then again
Heart stays in
Slow, controlled
I’m not lost
I’m getting whole

I’m studying alone
I’m studying alone
Two in the morning
And I’m in the zone
I’m studying alone
I’m studying alone
(paper by paper)
I’m coming home

Cold cup beside the corner note
Headphones resting on my coat
Wrong answers crossed, then crossed again
Little victories in the pen
Outside, the block is half asleep
But my little room still learns and breathes
I underline what I don’t know
Then watch the hard parts start to glow

Turn the page
Count to ten
One more proof
Then again
Heart stays in
Slow, controlled
I’m not lost
I’m getting whole

I’m studying alone
I’m studying alone
Two in the morning
And I’m in the zone
I’m studying alone
I’m studying alone
(paper by paper)
I’m coming home

If I shake, I stay
If I doubt, I stay
Little by little
I make my way
One more line
One more sign
Morning can wait
I’m doing fine

I’m studying alone
I’m studying alone
Two in the morning
And I’m in the zone
I’m studying alone
I’m studying alone
(paper by paper)
I’m coming home

songs.subject: a house song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hum a lonely tune
My laptop glows a pale moon
Coffee cold my brain's a mess
This textbook gives me endless stress

Tick tock goes the clock
My eyelids droop a heavy lock

Two AM and I'm still here
Whispers of doubt in my ear
Two AM the night is deep
Secrets the quiet hours keep

Highlighting words I barely see
The deadline mocks its coming glee
Another chapter another page
Trapped inside this study cage

The world sleeps soundly in its bed
While concepts dance inside my head
I yearn for slumber soft and kind
But knowledge I must truly find

Two AM and I'm still here
Whispers of doubt in my ear

songs.subject: a metal song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Engine roars
Black asphalt bleeds
Past the old place
A blur
Memories
Festering seeds

Haunting
Night's cold grip
Past the old pain
Rip
Rip
Rip!

Shadows dance
Twisted forms
Streetlights
Broken eyes
The window
Empty now
Echoes of screams
Lies

Haunting
Night's cold grip
Past the old pain
Rip
Rip
Rip!

No warmth
No light
Just the dark
The endless fight

songs.subject: a metal song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Headlights cut the laundromat sign
That old brick face still owns my spine
Balcony rust, bent screen door frame
Your plant pot dead, but it knows my name
I keep the window rolled up tight
Like that could keep me out tonight

Same cracked walk
Same broken step
I taste that dust
I never left
My hands go cold
On the wheel again
I pass the curb
And it starts to bend

Drive by the dead front door
Drive by the dead front door
I can’t live there anymore
But it lives in my bones
Drive by the dead front door
Drive by the dead front door
I leave the shell, I keep the war
I drive alone

Mailbox dents from some hard-lit fight
Old peeling paint in the porch light
Your name’s gone from the little square
Still I swear I can hear you there
One more block and the world goes black
Then that whole damn year hits back

Same cracked walk
Same broken step
I taste that dust
I never left
My jaw locks hard
My throat burns thin
I pass the curb
And it starts again

Drive by the dead front door
Drive by the dead front door
I can’t live there anymore
But it lives in my bones
Drive by the dead front door
Drive by the dead front door
I leave the shell, I keep the war
I drive alone

Half-speed now
Windows shake
No prayer left
No more brake
I let it burn
In the rearview glass
What killed us there
Can’t reach me back

Drive by the dead front door
Drive by the dead front door
I can’t live there anymore
But it lives in my bones
Drive by the dead front door
Drive by the dead front door
I leave the shell, I keep the war
I drive alone
Drive by the dead front door
Drive by the dead front door
I’m not that kid outside no more

songs.subject: a indie rock song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I see your name on my phone
And I hold my breath
You laugh like it costs you nothing
I pay with the rest
Your hoodie on my chair
Still smells like the rain
I fold it like a secret
Then put it back again

You look right through me
Like a window pane
I’m standing in the hall
But you don’t say my name

I keep my glass jar heart
On a high, high shelf
I keep my glass jar heart
Breaking by itself
You reach, then you leave
Like it’s nothing at all
I keep my glass jar heart
And I let it fall

At the corner store
You ask me for a light
Then you tell some story
And the room gets bright
I nod like I’m fine
Like I’ve got my lines
But I lose them all
When you smile at mine

You look right through me
Like a window pane
I’m standing in the hall
But you don’t say my name

I keep my glass jar heart
On a high, high shelf
I keep my glass jar heart
Breaking by itself
You reach, then you leave
Like it’s nothing at all
I keep my glass jar heart
And I let it fall

Maybe I’m the spare seat
In the back of your mind
Maybe I’m the almost
You leave behind
So I’ll keep my hands still
And my mouth shut tight
While you light up the room
Like a late train ride

I keep my glass jar heart
On a high, high shelf
I keep my glass jar heart
Breaking by itself
You reach, then you leave
Like it’s nothing at all
I keep my glass jar heart
And I let it fall

songs.subject: a indie rock song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I saw you by the copier
Gray hood, red marker stain
You laughed at something broken
And I forgot my name
You asked me for a pen
Like I was made of air
I handed you the blue one
You never knew I was there

Now I keep your picture
Folded in my wallet
Little paper heartbeat
Hitting when I’m alone

I’m still here, still here
At the edge of your door
I’m still here, still here
Wanting less and less more
You say my name
Like it’s easy to say
I fall apart
You walk away

Friday in the parking lot
Rain on your backpack seam
You talked about some boy
And I bit down on my dream
I bought your favorite candy
Left it by your seat
You picked it up and smiled
At the wrong one next to me

Now I keep your picture
Folded in my wallet
Little paper heartbeat
Hitting when I’m alone

I’m still here, still here
At the edge of your door
I’m still here, still here
Wanting less and less more
You say my name
Like it’s easy to say
I fall apart
You walk away

If I confess tonight
Would it change your face
Or just turn all this waiting
Into one more empty space
I know the shape of losing
I wear it in my sleeve
I built a little altar
From the things you didn’t need

I’m still here, still here
At the edge of your door
I’m still here, still here
Wanting less and less more
You say my name
Like it’s easy to say
I fall apart
You walk away

Paper cup heart
Half full, half cracked
I keep looking forward

I fall apart
You walk away

Friday in the parking lot
Rain on your backpack seam
You talked about some boy
And I bit down on my dream
I bought your favorite candy
Left it by your seat
You picked it up and smiled
At the wrong one next to me

Now I keep your picture
Folded in my wallet
Little paper heartbeat
Hitting when I’m alone

I’m still here, still here
At the edge of your door
I’m still here, still here
Wanting less and less more
You say my name
Like it’s easy to say
I fall apart
You walk away

If I confess tonight
Would it change your face
Or just turn all this waiting
Into one more empty space
I know the shape of losing
I wear it in my sleeve
I built a little altar
From the things you didn’t need

I’m still here, still here
At the edge of your door
I’m still here, still here
Wanting less and less more
You say my name
Like it’s easy to say
I fall apart
You walk away

Paper cup heart
Half full, half cracked
I keep looking forward

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise on the river
Mourning doves all sigh
Another soul takes flight
On a streetcar to the sky

Oh the streetcar to the sky
Rumble and chime
A sweet goodbye
Streetcar to the sky

Black suits and white handkerchiefs
Umbrellas in the sun
We dance them to their rest
When their long race is run

Oh the streetcar to the sky
Rumble and chime
A sweet goodbye
Streetcar to the sky

Trombones wail a mournful tune
Then brass bands start to swing
We cry a tear we laugh a laugh
What joy this sadness brings

Oh the streetcar to the sky
Rumble and chime

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The house is full of lilies
And plate glass in the rain
Your Sunday shoes by the doorway
Won't know your feet again
The fan turns slow above us
Like it knows your name
And all the old men nodding
Carry the weight the same

We follow the brass
Down Claiborne stone by stone
I keep your folded handkerchief
Like a flag I almost know

Lift me slow
Through the pouring gold
Lift me slow
Let the river hold
Mardi Gras veil
Over every face
Mardi Gras veil
We keep your place

At the corner, somebody's crying
Into a paper cup
A little girl in patent leather
Keeps looking up and up
The trumpet leans like memory
On a hot wet note
And the road turns soft with petals
On the back of every coat

We follow the brass
Past the shotgun porches and stone
I hear your laugh in the screen door
Then it's gone

Lift me slow
Through the pouring gold
Lift me slow
Let the river hold
Mardi Gras veil
Over every face
Mardi Gras veil
We keep your place

When the last drum hushes
And the saints go thin
I'll set your name in the window
Let the morning in
All that sorrow, shining
All that smoke, unspun
You were here in every footstep
And gone in every one

Lift me slow
Through the pouring gold
Lift me slow
Let the river hold
Mardi Gras veil
Over every face
Mardi Gras veil

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in before the sun
Clock out when it’s gone
Name tag on my chest
And a second one at dawn
Coffee on my shirt
Receipt roll in my hand
I keep doing the math
Still don’t understand

Rent took the red line
Gas took the blue
I’m running on borrowed time
Trying to make it through

Still falling behind
Still falling behind
I work both sides
And I’m still behind
(Still behind)
Still falling behind

Bus ride, back stairs
Groceries in a sack
Break room prayer, two bites
Then I’m right back
Phone keeps lighting up
Bills with my name
I smile for the customers
Then I swallow the shame

Monday to Sunday
I never slow down
But every little paycheck
Finds a hole in the ground

Still falling behind
Still falling behind
I work both sides
And I’m still behind
(Still behind)
Still falling behind

Mama said, "Baby, hold on"
So I’m holding on tight
One hand on the morning
One hand on the night
Maybe I’m not broken
Maybe I’m just tired
Maybe I’m a candle
Burning both sides

Still falling behind
Still falling behind
I work both sides
And I’m still behind
(Still behind)
Still falling behind

songs.lyrics:

Coffee black and extra strong
Another day another dollar gone
Yeah it's gone

My feet are tired
My soul is wired

Two jobs one heart
Tearing me apart
Working double time
Still losing ground I'm losing my mind

Evening shift the neon glow
Tips are slow so very slow
Dreams I had they come and go
Where do they go

My spirit's frayed
My bills unpaid

Two jobs one heart
Tearing me apart
Working double time

songs.subject: a pop song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights hum a broken tune
Another night beneath the moon
Same old faces same old plight
Wishing for a different light

Felt that pull a heavy chain
Whispering my name again

Concrete kisses on my feet
Leaving this dead-end street
Gonna fly gonna soar
Never looking back no more

Cracked pavement told my story
No grand tales no golden glory
Just the hustle just the grind
Leaving all that dust behind

Heard a whisper heard a call
Gonna stand up gonna stand tall

Concrete kisses on my feet
Leaving this dead-end street
Gonna fly gonna soar
Never looking back no more

songs.subject: a pop song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Cracked pavement dreams
Sunrise on broken screens
Another day another fight
Gonna make it shine so bright

Hear the city hummin
Something new is comin

Concrete rose yeah thats me
Broke the mold finally free
Left that dead-end street behind
A brand new world for me to find

Felt stuck in the gray
No sunlight no way
But a whisper in the breeze
Said darling just believe please

They said I couldnt fly
They watched me wave goodbye

songs.subject: a disco song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Mister Vale at dawn
Rolls his sleeves and grins
Blue chalk on the cloth
Pins between his pins
Mrs. Diaz wants a sway
Rico needs a cut
Little kids in Sunday white
All of them get dressed up

He says, "Stand still, baby"
Then he snaps that thread
Line by line, he builds you
From your shoes to your head

He dresses the block
(He dresses the block)
From the corner to the dock
(He dresses the block)
Fresh fit, sharp fit, all night long
He dresses the block
(He dresses the block)

Patch the hole in Leon's coat
Cuff the wedding suit
Hem it up for Carla's dance
Make it fit her roots
Old men smiling in the shade
Teenage queens in lace
Every back door on the block
Looks like a runway space

He says, "Stand still, baby"
Then he shakes that seam
One good stitch from Mister Vale
And you can change your scene

He dresses the block
(He dresses the block)
From the corner to the dock
(He dresses the block)
Fresh fit, sharp fit, all night long
He dresses the block
(He dresses the block)

Silver tape on a denim knee
Gold thread in the sun
Everybody on this avenue
Walks like they've won

He dresses the block
(He dresses the block)
From the corner to the dock
(He dresses the block)
Fresh fit, sharp fit, all night long
He dresses the block
(He dresses the block)

Tape on his neck, chalk dust on the floor

songs.subject: a hardcore song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Saw you by the market sign
Same cracked curb, same old line
You knew my name like a loaded gun
From the town where the mill burned out
You laughed like trouble in the summer heat
And I went weak on that broken street

Now my chest hits the floor
Every time you walk through the store
I try to act like I don't care
But you got me right there

Hometown, you got me
Hometown, you got me
From the first hard look
I was gone already
Hometown, you got me
Hometown, you got me
Same old streets, same old blood
Now it's you I can't shake off

We grew up under the factory smoke
Same skinny river, same cheap jokes
Your mother waved from the faded porch
My friends all said, "Man, don't start that torch"
But I saw your face in every crowd
Like a match struck hard and loud

Now my hands shake raw
When you say my childhood name
I been tough all my life
You still cut through the grain

Hometown, you got me
Hometown, you got me
From the first hard look
I was gone already
Hometown, you got me
Hometown, you got me
Same old streets, same old blood
Now it's you I can't shake off

Back fence, dust on my boots
Your front step, my old roots
If I run, I run to you
If I break, you break me too
Say my name
Say it loud
Little town, big trouble
Right here now

Hometown, you got me
Hometown, you got me
From the first hard look
I was gone already
Hometown, you got me
Hometown, you got me
Same old streets, same old blood
Now it's you I can't shake off

With a bruise on your knee
Same cracked sidewalks
Same old block as me
You laughed like trouble
Kicked dust off your shoe
I knew that look
I was done with you

Born on the same dead end
Same train that broke our sleep
You said, "Don't make this a thing"
Too late, baby
Too deep

Same old block
Same old blood
You hit me hard
Like home should
Same old block
Same old flame
I fell for you
And I say your name
(Same old block)
(Same old block)

Your mom still knows mine
Your uncle owes my dad
We learned to lie here

You got that grin
Like you stole it from the street
Now every siren in my chest
Puts your face on repeat

Born on the same dead end

Too deep

Same old block

All that rusted pain
Still looks good on me

Hometown heat!
Hometown scars!
I want you close
I want you far!
Hometown heat!
Hometown grace!
You were my first hard truth
My safest place!

Same old block

And I say your name

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You danced in my doorway
With rain on your shoes
I said, "Stay a little"
You said, "I gotta move"
Your collar was blue
From the diner at five
I watched that bus leave
And I let you ride

Now the moon on my window
Looks like your ring
I reach for the pillow
Still hearing you sing

You were the one that got away
The one that got away
I call your name, and it fades away
The one that got away
(doo-wop, doo-wop)

I kept your postcard
By the sugar jar
It said "Don't wait up"
Now it reads like a scar
I still see your laugh
In the glass at dawn
Every little thing here
Keeps pulling you on

If I could rewind
That summer night
I'd hold on tighter
I'd hold you right

You were the one that got away
The one that got away
I call your name, and it fades away
The one that got away
(doo-wop, doo-wop)

Maybe you found
A kinder shore
Maybe I'm just
The fool you wore
But if you come back
When the church bells sway
I'll be on the corner
Begging you to stay

You were the one that got away
The one that got away
I call your name, and it fades away
The one that got away
You were the one that got away
The one that got away
Still in my heart where the old dreams stay
The one that got away

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I saw you by the record stand
Blue dress, lemon in your hand
You laughed like you knew my name
Then the evening split in two
We danced on the painted floor
Your shoe tapped out a line of code
When I reached, you slipped right through
Like a note I couldn't hold

Ooh, I said stay
Ooh, you said wait
The door swung open
And it was too late

You were the one that got away
(One that got away)
I still hear your sweet hideaway
(Sweet hideaway)
My heart went ooh, my hands stayed still
You were the one that got away

Now I pass that corner store
Same red trim, same screen door
But the night don't wear your face
And the jukebox misses you
I keep your smile in a little box
Folded up with ticket stubs
Every time the summertime rains
I taste that first love bruise

Ooh, I call your name
Ooh, down the lane
The moon says nothing
But it sounds the same

You were the one that got away
(One that got away)
I still hear your sweet hideaway
(Sweet hideaway)
My heart went ooh, my hands stayed still
You were the one that got away

If you ever hear this tune
Under some far-off roof
Know I saved a seat for you
Right next to the moon

You were the one that got away
(One that got away)
I still hear your sweet hideaway
(Sweet hideaway)
My heart went ooh, my hands stayed still
You were the one that got away
(You were the one)

Now I pass that corner store
Same red trim, same screen door
But the night don't wear your face
And the jukebox misses you
I keep your smile in a little box
Folded up with ticket stubs
Every time the summertime rains
I taste that first love bruise

Ooh, I call your name
Ooh, down the lane
The moon says nothing
But it sounds the same

You were the one that got away
(One that got away)
I still hear your sweet hideaway
(Sweet hideaway)
My heart went ooh, my hands stayed still
You were the one that got away

If you ever hear this tune
Under some far-off roof
Know I saved a seat for you
Right next to the moon

You were the one that got away
(One that got away)
I still hear your sweet hideaway
(Sweet hideaway)
My heart went ooh, my hands stayed still
You were the one that got away
(You were the one)

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Corner store screen glow
Old paint on the bricks
Same cracked sidewalk
Same tired tricks
Mama said, "Keep walking"
Don't plant roots in the ache
I watched the bus route pass
And I learned to wait

Hands full of groceries
Dreams in a paper sack
I saw the lock on the gate
And I knew I had to crack
One foot in the mud
One foot out the door
I was born on that block
But I ain't there no more

I made it out that block
I made it out that block
Still got dust on my shoes
Still got love in my heart
I made it out that block
I made it out that block
Now I'm singing my name
Where the streetlights start
(out that block)

Little ones hooping
On a bent-up rim
Old heads on the stoop
Saying, "Boy, don't dim"
I carried my years
Like a chain-link sound
Then I found my voice
When I turned it around

Hands full of scars
Eyes on a wider road
Every goodbye I kissed
Turned into a fold
I used to count the cracks
Now I count the miles
If the block was a grave
I still learned to smile

I made it out that block
I made it out that block
Still got dust on my shoes
Still got love in my heart
I made it out that block
I made it out that block
Now I'm singing my name
Where the streetlights start
(out that block)

But I don't hate that place
It made me hard, made me wise
It put a fire in my chest
And a map in my eyes
To the ones still there
I send a steady light
You can leave that block
And still hold it right

I made it out that block
I made it out that block
Still got dust on my shoes
Still got love in my heart
I made it out that block
I made it out that block
Now I'm singing my name
Where the streetlights start
(out that block)

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked steps by the bodega door
Same old block with the busted boards
Mama said, "Boy, keep your shoes on time"
'Cause the world outside don't wait for mine
I learned hope in a laundromat chair
Cold coin pockets, thin gray air
But I kept one eye on the bus line glow
Like a seed in the sidewalk, ready to grow

Every night I heard that gate slam shut
Every day I said, "This can't be us"
If the road gets thin, I still walk it straight
I got a name, I got a date

We made it out the block
Made it out the block
(We made it out)
We made it out the block
Hearts on the line, but we never stopped
Made it out the block

Now my shoes hit floors I ain't seen before
Still smell that rain on the corner store
I call my cousins when the money lands
Tell 'em, "Hold that dream in your two hands"
I still fold my fear like a shirt at night
Still kiss my scars like they did me right
'Cause every little win got a stone-cold proof
That the dead-end road had a hidden loop

Every night I heard that gate slam shut
Every day I said, "This can't be us"
If the road gets thin, I still walk it straight
I got a name, I got a date

We made it out the block
Made it out the block
(We made it out)
We made it out the block
Hearts on the line, but we never stopped
Made it out the block

For the ones still posted by the laundries
For the ones still dreaming past the sirens
I lift my glass to the fence-line codes
And the ones who carried me down that road
Oh, the block tried to hold me close
But my future kept saying, "Go"
(Go, go)
Now I sing it for the ones who knew
A way out can start in you

We made it out the block
Made it out the block
(We made it out)
We made it out the block
Hearts on the line, but we never stopped
Made it out the block
We made it out the block
Made it out the block
(We made it out)
We made it out the block
Still got love for the place we got

songs.subject: a americana song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
I clock in by the east wing door
Keys on my belt, shoes on the tile
The room goes quiet when I pass
Even the frames stand still a while
There’s one blue coat in oil and gold
A woman near a ferry rail
I sweep the crumbs from her corner bench
And nod at the brass nameplate

She’s been here longer than my wife
Longer than this tired town
I catch her eyes on the closing round
Like she knows what I won’t say now

Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
You know my name before I do
Blue coat painting, what do you see?
Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
While I lock this hall for the night
(keep on watching me)

At noon, the school kids stampede in
Sticky hands and whispered jokes
One boy asks why she never smiles
I say some things just hold their pose
But when the rain hits hard on glass
And the tourists thin out fast
I swear that coat turns toward the door
Like she’s waiting for something back

Maybe the painter lost his way
Maybe she stayed for love
Maybe I keep this quiet house
So her stare has someone to trust

Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
You know my name before I do
Blue coat painting, what do you see?
Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
While I lock this hall for the night
(keep on watching me)

Last winter, my mother fell
And I missed her final call
Tonight I stand beneath that frame
With the dust and the alarm
I say, “If there’s a door for grief
If there’s a road back through
Save me a place by that ferry dock
And I’ll come back clean and new”

Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
You know my name before I do
Blue coat painting, what do you see?
Blue coat painting, keep on watching me
While I lock this hall for the night
(keep on watching me)

I turn the key, the room stays warm
Her blue coat fades in the dark
And I swear I hear, from the canvas side
A soft “come home” from the heart

songs.subject: a americana song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
He clocks in at nine
Keys on a ring
Checks the front door glass
And the tourist folks in
Red rope lined up
Shoes on the floor
He knows every crack
In that old oak door

But he always slows down
By the west wall frame
Where the red dress girl
Has no name

Red Rope Mary, stay with me
Red Rope Mary, set me free
You in that frame
And me on this floor
Red Rope Mary
I keep coming back for more

Her paint is thin
In the blue at her throat
There’s a hand on a hat
And a boat in the moat
Kids press close
When the room goes still
He hears her heels
On the polished sill

And he swears some nights
When the place gets low
She turns her face
Like she knows

Red Rope Mary, stay with me
Red Rope Mary, set me free
You in that frame
And me on this floor
Red Rope Mary
I keep coming back for more

Storm on the glass
Rain in the trees
He stands by the rope
With the keys in his sleeve
The whole town sleeps
But the varnish gleams
Like a river holding
An old sweet dream

Red Rope Mary, stay with me
Red Rope Mary, set me free
You in that frame
And me on this floor
Red Rope Mary
I keep coming back for more

He locks the door
Tips his cap
One last look

songs.subject: a country song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Mama left the porch light on
Supper still in the pan
Daddy stood out by the gate
With a hat in his hand
Little gray dog barked once
Then he knew my scent
I carried dust on my boots
And a name I meant to forget

The road had a way
Of taking my years
But your front door
Pulled me through my tears

I'm comin' home
I'm comin' home
Lay that burden down
I'm comin' home (home)
I'm comin' home
Back to your arms
Back where I belong

Grandma asked about the scars
Like she was askin' rain
I smiled and said, "I'm still here"
But I felt every day
You set a plate by my side
No questions, just grace
And I broke right there
In the plainest place

The road had a way
Of hardenin' stone
But one look at you
And I knew I was home

I'm comin' home
I'm comin' home
Lay that burden down
I'm comin' home (home)
I'm comin' home
Back to your arms
Back where I belong

I don't need a parade
Don't need my name in the sky
Just your hand in mine
And a porch light burnin' bright
Let the red dirt take my boots
Let the silence heal my chest
I brought back a broken man
But you love me through the rest

I'm comin' home
I'm comin' home
Lay that burden down
I'm comin' home (home)
I'm comin' home
Back to your arms
Back where I belong

Dust on my boots
Peace in my heart
I'm comin' home
At last

songs.subject: a country song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Got a duffel on my shoulder
And a name stitched in my sleeve
Train rolled past the soybean fields
Now I can almost breathe
Mama's porch light's burning
Like it knew I'd be late
I left a boy in camo
I'm a man that's walked away

Been a long road, hard road
Longer than I planned
I kept your picture in my wallet
And your ring in my hand

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
To that screen door slam
And your two strong arms
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
A little broke, a little worn
But I'm yours and that means more
(I'm coming home)

The mailbox leans a little
Fence line's got fresh paint
Dad fixed the old Ford tailgate
Said, "Son, it still ain't strange"
Little brother got taller
Mama's got tears she won't name
And the dog went wild at the driveway
Like he never forgot my face

Been a long road, hard road
I prayed through the dust and rain
Now the porch steps know my boots
And they know me by my name

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
To that screen door slam
And your two strong arms
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
A little broke, a little worn
But I'm yours and that means more
(I'm coming home)

I don't need a parade
I don't need a speech
Just your hand in mine
And the supper on the stove heat
If the nights get loud
And the shadows call
Hold me like a promise
And I'll let down my wall

I'm coming home
I'm coming home
To that screen door slam
And your two strong arms
I'm coming home
I'm coming home
A little broke, a little worn
But I'm yours and that means more

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
She sat me down at dawn
On a bench of salt and sand
Her hands were maps of years
And shells fit in her hand
She said, "Watch how it comes"
Not to hurry, not to hold
"Even the wide blue water
Learns its way back home"

"Listen to the pull"
She tapped my ankle bone
"Nothing here is lost
It only changes tone"

Teach me, tide
Teach me, tide
Come and go
But stay inside
Teach me, tide
Teach me, tide
I will move
I will arrive

Her laugh was low and soft
Like rain on a tin roof
She showed me traced-out lines
Where the moon had pushed through
"Don't fight the leaving
Don't shame the return
A heart gets strong enough
When it lets the shore burn"

"Listen to the pull"
She said it like a vow
"Every breaking wave
Knows what to do somehow"

Teach me, tide
Teach me, tide
Come and go
But stay inside
Teach me, tide
Teach me, tide
I will move
I will arrive

When my hands got full
And my breath got thin
She drew a circle slow
And let the water in
"Soft is not слаб?" she smiled
"Soft is how we last"
Then she left a trail of foam
Across the morning glass

Teach me, tide
Teach me, tide
Come and go
But stay inside
Teach me, tide
Teach me, tide
I will move
I will arrive

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
He sits on the pier in white
Palm to the morning blue
Says, "Watch how the water leaves
Then comes back looking for you"
His hands know the patient pull
Old rope, salt, and sand
He points at the shining line
Like a map drawn by his hand

"Don't chase it when it's near
Don't mourn it when it's gone"
"Every shore learns this dance
By the time the moon rolls on"

Teach me the tide, old man
(teach me the tide)
Teach me the tide, old man
(teach me the tide)
How to let go slow
How to return on time

He laughs at my hurried feet
At my pockets full of plans
Says, "Even the stubborn moon
Has to follow what it can't command"
We sit where the foam breaks thin
And the gulls wheel low and bright
He taps the dock with one small cane
Like he's counting out the night

"Listen when the water sighs
It tells you what to keep"
"Some things only learn to stay
After they learn to leave"

Teach me the tide, old man
(teach me the tide)
Teach me the tide, old man
(teach me the tide)
How to let go slow
How to return on time

When my name gets heavy in my mouth
When my hands grip too tight
He says, "Leave a little room
And the sea will make it right"

Teach me the tide, old man
(teach me the tide)
Teach me the tide, old man
(teach me the tide)
How to let go slow
How to return on time
Teach me the tide, old man

songs.subject: a post-rock song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I drive by slow
Past the brick face
Your old window
Dark as a bruise
The porch light’s gone
But the mailbox leans
Like it knows my name
And won’t say it

I don’t stop
I don’t look long
Just one more turn
Then I’m gone

That block still knows me
That block still knows me

That block still knows me

I can almost hear

I don’t look long

songs.subject: a post-rock song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I rolled past your block again
Same dent in the gate
Same brown stairwell light
Buzzing over number eight
The mailbox still leans left
Like it knows too much
My hands stayed on the wheel
But my chest went numb

And the porch still held your plant
Dead leaves in a cup
I kept on moving slow
Like I could wake it up

I drove by, I drove by
Window Three B
I drove by, I drove by
And you were still there to me
(Still there to me)
I drove by, I drove by
Didn’t stop the car
Just let the dark go on
With your name in the glass

The same blue trash bin
By the cracked brick wall
The same mat at the door
No welcome at all
A towel on the rail
A shoe on the stoop
Little ghost of a life
I never got to keep

And the screen door swung once
In the wind, then froze
I watched the second hand
In the building glow

I drove by, I drove by
Window Three B
I drove by, I drove by
And you were still there to me
(Still there to me)
I drove by, I drove by
Didn’t stop the car
Just let the dark go on
With your name in the glass

No one in the upstairs room
No kettle, no laugh
Just a fan in the square
Turning air into math
So I held that corner
Like a bruise I know
Then the whole block rose up
And I let it go

I drove by, I drove by
Window Three B
I drove by, I drove by
And you were still there to me
(Still there to me)
I drove by, I drove by
Didn’t stop the car
Just let the dark go on

songs.subject: a pop song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
I write with a chipped blue pen
On the back of a diner bill
They said, “Sign your name right here”
But I left that line there still
My words got mud on their boots
From the road and the rain outside
They want gold for the whole damn book
I want truth that can’t be priced

I’ve seen the offers come
All clean and dressed in white
But my pages know my pulse
And my pulse won’t lie

I won’t sell my soul
I won’t sell my soul
Keep the ink on the table
Let the whole room know
I won’t sell my soul
I won’t sell my soul
You can take the spotlight
I’ll keep my old road

They want a sweeter face
Want the hurt cut down to size
Want my wolves turned into dogs
Want my storms to talk in rhymes
But I got a name in my mouth
And it weighs like a loaded stone
If I trade it for applause
I’d come home to a stranger’s bones

I’ve seen the offers come
With a pretty little bow
But the lines I swore to keep
Are the only things I know

I won’t sell my soul
I won’t sell my soul
Keep the ink on the table
Let the whole room know
I won’t sell my soul
I won’t sell my soul
You can take the spotlight
I’ll keep my old road

Let them build their bright glass towers
I’ll be down where the real words grow
In a notebook full of weather
In a voice that still says “no”
If the crowd wants a safer song
They can have that polished glow
I’ll be here with the rough draft
And the heart they can’t control

I won’t sell my soul
I won’t sell my soul
Keep the ink on the table
Let the whole room know
I won’t sell my soul
I won’t sell my soul
You can take the spotlight
I’ll keep my old road

songs.subject: a pop song about a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise paints the window pane
Another day another drain
They want my words in a shiny box
For pretty people on pretty blocks
But my pen it writes what my heart bleeds
Planting wild and thorny seeds

They call me up they call me down
The richest sharks in this whole town

I ain't no sellout poet
No I ain't no sellout poet
My words are fire you know it
I ain't no sellout poet

They wave their cash a paper tide
Saying "write us something soft inside"
"Something safe something sweet and mild"
But my muse she's a screaming child
She dances wild she breaks the mold
Stories raw and brave and bold

They offer fame they offer gold
A life so neat a story told
But what's a voice if it ain't true
A painted sky a plastic blue

I ain't no sellout poet
No I ain't no sellout poet
My words are fire you know it

songs.subject: a swing song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old Mister Fitzwilliam, with his thimble and thread
Sits perched on his stool, with a song in his head
He’s got a keen eye, and a needle so quick
Can turn an old sack to a sartorial trick

Oh, the tailor of Tenth Street, he knows what you need
From a dapper new suit to a patch on a tweed
He’ll measure your dreams, with a smile and a wink
The best dressed on the block, thanks to him, don't you think?

Miss Agnes, the baker, with her flour-dusted hands
Needed seams taken in, for her culinary plans
And young Jimmy, the newsboy, with holes in his knee
Got a patch that was snappy, for all folks to see

Oh, the tailor of Tenth Street, he knows what you need
From a dapper new suit to a patch on a tweed
He’ll measure your dreams, with a smile and a wink
The best dressed on the block, thanks to him, don't you think?

He doesn't ask much, just a nod and a grin
For the confidence tailored, right there from within
A snip and a stitch, and a press and a fold
Stories in fabric, worth more than pure gold

Oh, the tailor of Tenth Street, he knows what you

songs.subject: a swing song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
On the corner by the laundromat door
Mister Vale keeps bolts by the score
Blue pinstripe for the barber next street
And a gold-thread vest for the butcher to eat
He pins up the hem with a grin and a dart
Knows every old waist and every hard heart
Cloth on the rack, chalk on the board
One cut from Vale and you look like a lord

He says, step in, son
Let me set you right
Your shoulders got trouble
I can fix them tonight

Tailor dress the whole block
(Whole block)
Needle in the beat, tick-tock
(Tick-tock)
From the porch to the bodega line
Everybody fresh, everybody fine
Tailor dress the whole block
(Whole block)

Miss June wants green with a lapel like fire
For Sunday pie and the church choir
Old Ray gets cuffs with a silver stitch
Says, “Mister Vale, you made me rich”
Kids on the stoop in a hand-me-down chain
Pose for the mirror like a Saturday train
Even the dog got a little red tie
Walks down Main like he owns the sky

He says, step in, kid
Let me work that seam
A little more swing
A little more sheen

Tailor dress the whole block
(Whole block)
Needle in the beat, tick-tock
(Tick-tock)
From the porch to the bodega line
Everybody fresh, everybody fine
Tailor dress the whole block
(Whole block)

Late in the dusk when the shutters come down
He still keeps stitching for half the town
One last cuff, one last smile
Makes a hard week look worthwhile

Tailor dress the whole block
(Whole block)
Needle in the beat, tick-tock
(Tick-tock)
From the porch to the bodega line
Everybody fresh, everybody fine
Tailor dress the whole block
(Whole block)
Tailor dress the whole block
