songs.subject: a swing song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Down by the harbor wall
The gulls know where to land
Cobblestones over old bone
And a promise under sand
Every bakery opens
On a crack that never healed
Morning steam and diesel
Over something huge concealed

You can hear it in the grates
When the trolley shakes the street
One slow breath beneath us
And the whole town finds the beat

Boots on granite, baby
Boots on granite
We dance on the giant
Boots on granite
He sleeps, but he knows us
He knows our names
We keep this city swayin'
On his old broad frame

The laundromat is buzzing
By the old foundry gate
Kids skip chalk around the seams
Where the river naps and waits
The mayor paints a postcard
Says the hills made us strong
But the dock boys grin and tell you
What this town was built upon

When the church bell swings low
And the windows start to hum
Everybody leans in
Like the earth is kicking drums

Boots on granite, baby
Boots on granite
We dance on the giant
Boots on granite
He sleeps, but he knows us
He knows our names
We keep this city swayin'
On his old broad frame

Last winter, after midnight
The ice split at the pier
A rumble rolled through basement bars
And every glass stood still
Then the blackout faded
And the baker lit the flame
We laughed and stamped our shoes
Like we were calling him by name

Boots on granite, baby
Boots on granite
We dance on the giant
Boots on granite
He sleeps, but he knows us
He knows our names
We keep this city swayin'
On his old broad frame
Boots on granite, baby
Boots on granite
Shine that horn and step light
Boots on granite

songs.subject: a swing song about a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
My block sits high on a ribbone ridge
Where the old big fella still holds the bridge
His elbow’s a hill by the bakery line
His knuckle makes shade at quarter to nine
The trolley rolls soft by his stone-gray cheek
The market sits close where his eyelids sleep
We learned how to tiptoe, learned how to grin
’Cause the whole town’s living on the skin he’s in

And when the pavement hums
We keep it light, keep it nice
One heavy step and the whole place jumps
So we dance on the edge of his dreams at night

Sleepy giant town
Don’t wake him now
Sleepy giant town
We’re built so proud
(Oh, sleepy giant town)
(Run, little feet, run)

The barber says his heartbeat shakes the jars
The school kids count it out from the yard
Even the bells swing slow in the square
Like they know he’s breathing somewhere down there
We paint our doors with a silver line
Marking the blocks where the sun can climb
Every brick knows the story by name
We came from his shadow, we stay the same

And when the pavement hums
We keep it light, keep it nice
One heavy step and the whole place jumps
So we dance on the edge of his dreams at night

Sleepy giant town
Don’t wake him now
Sleepy giant town
We’re built so proud
(Oh, sleepy giant town)
(Run, little feet, run)

Last night the thunder rolled in low
The windows rattled, then let go
For one long breath the whole town swayed
Then the big old giant dreamed our names

Sleepy giant town
Don’t wake him now
Sleepy giant town
We’re built so proud
(Sleepy giant town)
(Keep it kind, keep it low)
(Sleepy giant town)

songs.subject: a flamenco song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Dusty boots still dancing
Midnight moon is fading fast
One last churro dreaming
How long can this feeling last

My heart it beats a wild drum
A rhythm I can't outrun

Festival heartbreak
Oh festival heartbreak
The music stops my soul aches
Festival heartbreak

Sweat and glitter on my skin
Memories I'll hold so tight
Whispers carried on the wind
Vanishing into the night

Festival heartbreak
Oh festival heartbreak
The music stops

songs.subject: a flamenco song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The paper lanterns lean
Over spilled sangria
Your gold skirt spins slow
On the plaza stones
The ferris wheel is still
But my hands are not
I keep your red fan
Like a stolen prayer

One more song
One more round
Don't let the night
Put this fire down

Last night, last night
Hold me close tonight
Last night, last night
Let the embers bite
Dance it out
Dance it out
Till the dawn comes through
Last night, last night
I belong to you

The drummer's knuckles shine
With salt and wine
A child drops petals
From a candy bag
You laugh through the smoke
From the grill by the gate
And the whole square turns
Like it knows your name

One more song
One more round
Don't let the night
Put this fire down

Last night, last night
Hold me close tonight
Last night, last night
Let the embers bite
Dance it out
Dance it out
Till the dawn comes through
Last night, last night
I belong to you

When the banners fall
And the tables shake
I'll still hear your heels
On the stones we make
No more masks
No more leave-takings
Just this burning sky
And the heart it brings

Last night, last night
Hold me close tonight
Last night, last night
Let the embers bite
Dance it out
Dance it out
Till the dawn comes through
Last night, last night
I belong to you
Last night, last night
One more spin with you
Last night, last night

songs.subject: a country song about the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Cooler in the truck bed
Sunscreen on the dash
Your name on my wristband
And a shirt from the fair
Dad says, "Don't be late now"
Mama folds one more tee
I keep staring at my driveway
Like it ain't mine next week

We got one more sunset
One more dirt road ride
One more taste of home
Before we cross that line

Last day of summer
Hold on, hold on
Last day of summer
Before it all goes on
We were barefoot, wild
Now the campus calls
Last day of summer
Hold on, hold on

songs.subject: a country song about the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Bean dust on my boots
Screen door wide
Mama packed a cooler
By the truck outside
My cap on the dash
Books in a tote
One last barefoot walk
Down that gravel road

Corn in the field
Tall as prayers
Your hand found mine
Like we’d both been there
School bell dreams
Hung in the air
And I kept thinking
This is all we get

One more sunset
One more ride
One last summer
Before the college lights
Hold me close
Let it burn slow
We were kids all August
Now we have to go
One more sunset
One more ride

Your mom waved hard
From the front gate
My little brother laughed
And called it a parade
We sat on the tailgate
Counting how it ends
Saying same old town
Will still be here then

Corn in the field
Bent in gold
Every road I know
Starts letting go
Your hand in mine
A shaky little vow
We didn’t say forever
But we said right now

One more sunset
One more ride
One last summer
Before the college lights
Hold me close
Let it burn slow
We were kids all August
Now we have to go
One more sunset
One more ride

If I lose my way
Out past that gate
Send me back
To this heat wave
To your laugh in the cab
To the fence-line view
To the first hard ache
Of leaving you

One more sunset
One more ride
One last summer
Before the college lights
Hold me close
Let it burn slow
We were kids all August
Now we have to go
One more sunset
One more ride
One more sunset

songs.subject: a jazz song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I folded up my work shirt
Put my name in a sack
Ma left pie on the counter
But I ain't coming back
The bus rolled by the feed store
Past the grain and the field
I watched the water tower
Fade like it never healed

You can keep that courthouse clock
Keep the cracked front porch
Keep the same old story
I don't live there anymore

I'm gone for good
Gone for good
Past the county line
I'm gone for good
Tell my old street
Tell my old room
I wore that town too long
Now I’m gone for good

The diner knew my shadow
The barber knew my lie
Every summer felt like August
Stuck behind my eyes
I learned to wave at trouble
Like it was family
But my shoes were pointed outward
And my heart agreed

You can keep that courthouse clock
Keep the cracked front porch
Keep the same old story
I don't live there anymore

I'm gone for good
Gone for good
Past the county line
I'm gone for good
Tell my old street
Tell my old room
I wore that town too long
Now I’m gone for good

If they ask, I smiled polite
If they ask, I stayed too long
If they ask where I’m headed
Say the road called me strong

I'm gone for good
Gone for good
Past the county line
I'm gone for good
Tell my old street
Tell my old room
I wore that town too long
Now I’m gone for good

songs.subject: a jazz song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Main Street still smells like rain
And fryer grease on paper trays
I know every crack in the curb
Every face that looks away
My name hangs stale at the diner door
Like a coat I never wore
I’ve been small-town in my bones
Way too long, way too long

But the road keeps calling me
Past the grain silo, past the tracks
I packed my last hard truth
And I’m not turning back

I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
You can keep my shadow
I’m leaving for good
I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
With your dust on my shoes
I’m leaving for good

Mama said, “Boy, don’t run from home”
I said, “Home don’t run from me”
There’s a whole wide world outside
And I’ve got to let it breathe
At the gas station by the bridge
Old men watched me pump and stare
I nodded like I owed the town
More than the years I spent there

But the road keeps calling me
Past the grain silo, past the tracks
I packed my last hard truth
And I’m not turning back

I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
You can keep my shadow
I’m leaving for good
I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
With your dust on my shoes
I’m leaving for good

If I miss the creek in July
If I miss your front porch light
I’ll remember all the times
I was half alive
So pour one out for the kid
Who never fit that frame
I’ll wear the hurt like a ring
Then I’ll learn a new name

I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
You can keep my shadow
I’m leaving for good
I’m leaving for good
Leaving for good
With your dust on my shoes
I’m leaving for good

Last bus out
I’m on it now
Last bus out

songs.subject: a trap song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Rolled slow by the gate
Same busted mail slot
Same bent screen door
Still got that green lock
Your curtain's gone dark
Mine's hand on the wheel
I don't stop there, baby
But I know how it feels
That hall kept our names
In the paint by the stairs
Now the whole place looks small
Like it never held air
I told myself "keep moving"
But my chest don't agree
Every block got your echo
And it won't let me leave

Drive past 14B
Drive past 14B
I swear it still sees me
(14B)
Drive past 14B
Drive past 14B
That old door keeps breathing
(keeps breathing)

Street got quiet tonight
Just my tires and rain
Gold numbers on the brick
Still spell out your name
I seen new folks in your frame
New laugh through the blinds
But the place kept your ghost
In the gap between lines
I don't call, I don't knock
I just circle and fade
Like a note I won't send
On the edge of the page
You moved out of my reach
But not out of my head
I can leave that address
Still it sits in my chest

Past the curb, past the tree
Past the kid on the stoop
One more lap, one more breath
One more turn in the loop

Drive past 14B
Drive past 14B
I swear it still sees me
(14B)
Drive past 14B
Drive past 14B
That old door keeps breathing
(keeps breathing)

songs.subject: a trap song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I rolled by your block again
Same cracked curb, same bent rail
Your old door got fresh paint now
Mine still carries that jail-mail smell
I saw the window with the fan
Turnin' slow like it used to do
Felt that pull in my chest, damn
Like the past still got a grip on you

I don't stop, I just glide
Headlights on, eyes on mine
Used to dream in that hallways
Now I'm ghost when I cross that line

Right past my old apartment
Right past my old apartment
I don't park, I keep it movin'
That place still knows what I lost in it
Right past my old apartment
Right past my old apartment
Same stairs, same scars, same silence
I keep drivin', I keep driftin'

Trash bag by the side gate
Two kids laugh where I used to smoke
Landlord kept the mailbox rusted
Like the whole building never let go
I remember that cheap couch saggin'
Takeout boxes, cold ramen nights
You said "soon" and I heard forever
Now "soon" don't live in my life

I don't stop, I just glide
Rearview full, face ahead
Every light on that corner
Still got your name in my head

Right past my old apartment
Right past my old apartment
I don't park, I keep it movin'
That place still knows what I lost in it
Right past my old apartment
Right past my old apartment
Same stairs, same scars, same silence
I keep drivin', I keep driftin'

One lap, then I'm gone again
Ash on my sleeve, hands on ten
If I look too long, I might fold
So I let that building go

Right past my old apartment
Right past my old apartment
I don't park, I keep it movin'
That place still knows what I lost in it
Right past my old apartment
Right past my old apartment
Same stairs, same scars, same silence

songs.subject: a reggae song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Cardboard tower by the door
Tape gun stuck in my hand
One more fold on that old box
One last sweep of this land
Your keys are gone, the floor is bare
Dust on the window ledge
I press it down with tired thumbs
And kiss the corner’s edge

Easier now, I tell myself
With every strip I lay
Pack up the hurt, pack up the songs
And carry them away

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more seal and I’m moving on
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
I leave the room, but the love stays strong

Old mug chips in a paper nest
Receipt from the corner store
That red scarf lost under the bed
We used to fight for before
I hear my footsteps in the hall
Like somebody else came through
This place was ours in every crack
Now I hand it back to you

Easier now, I tell myself
With every strip I lay
Pack up the hurt, pack up the songs
And carry them away

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more seal and I’m moving on
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
I leave the room, but the love stays strong

Sun on the driveway, van door wide
Friends call out, “Let’s roll”
I stand there smiling with my keys
And feeling it in my soul
Not every leaving is a loss
Not every door stays closed
Some things you fold, some things you keep
Some things you never outgrow

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more seal and I’m moving on
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
I leave the room, but the love stays strong

songs.subject: a reggae song about taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
One lamp left on the floor
Dust on the closet door
I folded up the old blue map
And found your note in a coffee trap
Shoes by the front step line
One more run, one more time
I packed the last bent plate
Now the hallway’s got to wait

Tape rolls under my thumb
Corners fold, then they come
When the box is finally tight
I can leave this room tonight

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more seal and I’m gone
One more seal and I’m strong
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Door shuts soft, keys drop slow
Bless this place, then let it go

Bubble wrap around the glass
The echo moves when I pass
A broom leans by the wall
Sweeping up the things I call
Old rent stain on the tile
Childhood shoes in a pile
I laugh a little, wipe my face
This little room held my whole race

Tape rolls under my thumb
Corners fold, then they come
When the box is finally tight
I can leave this room tonight

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more seal and I’m gone
One more seal and I’m strong
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Door shuts soft, keys drop slow
Bless this place, then let it go

If the walls could talk, they’d say
You did good, now walk away
No more dust, no more trace
Just a small white mark on the base

Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
One more seal and I’m gone
One more seal and I’m strong
Tape up the last box
Tape up the last box
Door shuts soft, keys drop slow

songs.subject: a swing song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
The clock on the wall
Says it's quarter past three
Another long night
Just the old masters and me
I walk through the halls
A familiar routine
Past the marble and busts
And that one painted scene

Oh, the night watchman's dream
Is a curious thing
When the shadows all dance
And the portraits all sing
Just a flick of the wrist
Or a sly, knowing glance
In the night watchman's dream
It's a midnight romance

There's a lady in oils
With a smile so serene
She's been hanging right there
Since nineteen-seventeen
Her eyes seem to follow
Wherever I roam
Makes a fellow feel
Not so terribly alone

Is it just the late hour?
Or the quiet, still air?

Oh, the night watchman's dream
Is a curious thing
When the shadows all dance
And the portraits all sing
Just a flick of the wrist
Or a sly, knowing glance
In the night watchman's dream
It's a midnight romance

Some say it's the dust
Playing tricks on my eyes
But I swear that she winks
As the moonlight applies
A soft glow to her frame
And I whisper, "Goodnight"
'Til the first ray of dawn
Brings the morning

songs.subject: a swing song about a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Night falls, the doors all lock up tight
Just me and the masters, bathed in pale moonlight
Another evening, quiet and slow
Watching the centuries ebb and flow

I pace the polished marble, soft shoe on the floor
Past gilded frames and stories, I've seen them all before
But then there’s *her*, that canvas on the wall
A certain something answers to my call

Oh, the guard and the gaze, a curious affair
She’s stuck in her pigments, I’m just standing there
She’s a lady in waiting, a permanent sigh
And I’m just the fellow who’s watching her eye

They say she’s a Duchess, from some forgotten age
With a smile that’s a secret, penned on history’s page
I wonder what she’s thinking, behind that knowing stare
Does she ever notice me, standing over here?

Oh, the guard and the gaze, a curious affair
She’s stuck in her pigments, I’m just standing there
She’s a lady in waiting, a permanent sigh
And I’m just the fellow who’s watching her eye

Some nights I swear her lips just start to curve
A silent invitation, she gets on my nerve
In the sweetest way, of course, a painter's clever trick
songs.lyrics_en:
Night falls, the doors all lock up tight
Just me and the masters, bathed in pale moonlight
Another evening, quiet and slow
Watching the centuries ebb and flow

I pace the polished marble, soft shoe on the floor
Past gilded frames and stories, I've seen them all before
But then there’s *her*, that canvas on the wall
A certain something answers to my call

Oh, the guard and the gaze, a curious affair
She’s stuck in her pigments, I’m just standing there
She’s a lady in waiting, a permanent sigh
And I’m just the fellow who’s watching her eye

They say she’s a Duchess, from some forgotten age
With a smile that’s a secret, penned on history’s page
I wonder what she’s thinking, behind that knowing stare
Does she ever notice me, standing over here?

Oh, the guard and the gaze, a curious affair
She’s stuck in her pigments, I’m just standing there
She’s a lady in waiting, a permanent sigh
And I’m just the fellow who’s watching her eye

Some nights I swear her lips just start to curve
A silent invitation, she gets on my nerve
In the sweetest way, of course, a painter's clever trick

songs.subject: a reggae song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We were sitting by the shore
Salt on your bare feet
Then the whole sky turned green
Like a secret in the heat
You laughed and grabbed my hand
Said “look up, don’t blink”
All that purple over water
Made my worried heart think

If the night can paint that way
Then maybe we can too
One bright arc over our heads
And I’m coming back to you

Northern lights
Northern lights
Move over us tonight
Northern lights
Northern lights
Make my dark heart bright

Fishermen had gone home
Only waves and humming trees
Then those ribbons started dancing
Slow and wild above the leaves
I traced your name in air
You traced mine in the sand
Even heaven felt like motion
When you held my trembling hand

If the night can paint that way
Then maybe we can too
One bright arc over our heads
And I’m coming back to you

Northern lights
Northern lights
Move over us tonight
Northern lights
Northern lights
Make my dark heart bright

Roll that shadow back
Let the color stay
If the world goes quiet, baby
We can glow our own way

Northern lights
Northern lights
Move over us tonight
Northern lights
Northern lights
Make my dark heart bright
Northern lights
Northern lights
Stay until the morning comes (oh-oh)

No need for cities bright and loud

Like a happy little child

songs.subject: a indie rock song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning
My book lies open wide
A ring of yellow
The only thing alive
Pages keep turning
Like they got someplace to be
My pen makes small dents
In the edge of my sleeve

I keep a chair warm
For nobody but me
I know the answers
Hide in the seams

I’m still here
I’m still here
Two in the morning, I’m still here
(turn the page, turn the page)
I’m still here
I’m still here
Won’t let the dark make it disappear

Empty coffee cup
Cold on the windowsill
Highlighter marks
On the same hard hill
My phone face down
Like it knows not to speak
I chase one sentence
Then it slips all week

I keep a chair warm
For nobody but me
I know the answers
Hide in the seams

I’m still here
I’m still here
Two in the morning, I’m still here
(turn the page, turn the page)
I’m still here
I’m still here
Won’t let the dark make it disappear

If I fall asleep
Let the notes stay stacked
If I lose my place
I’ll come right back
Morning can wait
Outside that cracked blind
I’m building a little
Light from inside

I’m still here
I’m still here
Two in the morning, I’m still here
(turn the page, turn the page)
I’m still here
I’m still here
Won’t let the dark make it disappear
I’m still here

songs.subject: a indie rock song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning, desk lamp glow
Highlighter crumbs on the math I know
My phone face down like a guilty stone
I’m here with the page and the fan’s low drone

Last bus gone, and the hallway’s dead
Cold tea ring on the book I read
I write your name in the margin line
Then cross it out just to save my time

And the clock keeps chewing
The numbers slow
I’m half a ghost
With a pen that won’t let go

Two in the morning
I’m still here
Two in the morning
I’m making it clear
One more page
One more fight
I’ll earn my sleep
At two in the morning

Window cracked, and the page lifts loose
I’m chasing a fact like a wire-thin fuse
My room looks small but it holds me up
Every little answer in a paper cup

And the clock keeps chewing
The numbers slow
I’m half a ghost
With a pen that won’t let go

Two in the morning
I’m still here
Two in the morning
I’m making it clear
One more page
One more fight
I’ll earn my sleep
At two in the morning

If I fall apart
Let it be on these lines
If I lose my grip
I’ll find it in time
Just one more page
Then I’m gone
Just one more page
Then I’m strong

Two in the morning
I’m still here
Two in the morning
I’m making it clear
One more page
One more fight
I’ll earn my sleep

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise over Ipanema
Coffee gets cold
My phone glows dim
A little buzz a little sting

My heart it skips a beat
Then it just stops

A text message
Just three words
We are through
That's how you say goodbye

Samba drums play on
My world goes quiet
The city hums a tune
I don't recognize it

Was it my laugh too loud
My dreams too big
Or your heart too small
To even call me

A

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise through the blinds
Another day begins
My coffee gets cold
The phone just sits there
I remember your smile
Then the little screen

My heart it was humming
Like a bossa nova tune

Text message goodbye
Just three little words
No explanation why
Ghosted samba blues

I read it again
Did I miss something big
A secret code maybe
Just a cold white screen
My world turned to grey
Like a silent movie scene

We danced under moonlight
Felt like forever

songs.subject: a hip hop song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Rolled by slow on the avenue
Windows up, same little view
Third-floor light, then it disappeared
That same old hall still lives in here
Paint on the rail, that busted gate
I used to race in a little late
Now I just pass and keep it cool
Like I never learned those rooms

But the curb still knows my name
And the dark plays back that frame
One glance, I’m there again
At the corner where I changed

Drive past, don’t stop
That place still talks
Drive past, don’t stop
My heart still walks
(Drive past) I’m not the same
(Drive past) but it knows my name

Trash bags stacked by the back door
Same smell leak from the corner store
Old mail box with the chipped-out blue
I swear that crack still cut me through
I left a chair, I left a key
Left some version of me in three
Boxes taped, and the floorboards groan
Like the walls got a voice of their own

But the curb still knows my name
And the dark plays back that frame
One glance, I’m there again
At the corner where I changed

Drive past, don’t stop
That place still talks
Drive past, don’t stop
My heart still walks
(Drive past) I’m not the same
(Drive past) but it knows my name

I don’t hate it
I don’t miss it
I just feel the ghost
Of who I was in it
Head down, lights low
Let it fade behind
Some nights the road
Takes me back in time

Drive past, don’t stop
That place still talks
Drive past, don’t stop
My heart still walks
(Drive past) I’m not the same
(Drive past) but it knows my name

songs.subject: a hip hop song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I roll by slow
past the second-floor glow
same busted screen door
same stain by the hall
Your name gone quiet
but the mailbox still bent
I seen my shadow there
before I left that rent
Two chairs on the curb
trash bags tied tight
somebody else laughing
in the warm kitchen light
I kept both hands steady
on the wheel, on the wheel
like if I don't blink
I won't have to feel

And it hit me hard
right there in the dark
how a whole little life
can fade out the park
I pass that window
my throat gets tight
still got your ghost
in the passenger side

Drive past twelve
drive past twelve
I do it too much
I know myself
Drive past twelve
drive past twelve
That place ain't mine
but it still knows me well

New spot got more room
got less of your laugh
Same old crack in me
I can't photograph
The porch light buzz
like a tired-up horn
I remember the night
we got mad and stormed
You packed that blue box
I stacked that frame
I swore I moved on
but the street kept your name
Now my rearview's full
of the life we built
and the dashboard cold
like a cup of guilt

And it hit me hard
right there in the dark
how a whole little life
can fade out the park
I pass that window
my throat gets tight
still got your ghost
in the passenger side

Drive past twelve
drive past twelve
I do it too much
I know myself
Drive past twelve
drive past twelve
That place ain't mine
but it still knows me well

Same stop sign
same dead-end bend
I swear I left
but I came back again
One more lap
one more breath
same old hurt
same old steps

Drive past twelve
drive past twelve
I do it too much
I know myself
Drive past twelve
drive past twelve
That place ain't mine

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning
I’m rollin’ through Ohio
Moon on the chrome
And a red sign glow
Coffee gone cold
In a paper cup
One hand on the wheel
One phone lit up

I hear your breath
Through the crackle line
Little boy sayin’
“Daddy, you got time?”

Call home, call home
Hear my name
Call home, call home
Till the road feels tame
(call home)
One more mile
One more prayer
Call home, call home
You’re still there

Your mama’s got the porch light
Burnin’ by the stairs
Dog at the window
Too tired to care
Kid’s front teeth
Lost in a grin
He says “You comin’ back?”
I say “Soon as I can”

Map’s laid out
Like a folded dream
Eastbound promise
Runnin’ in between

Call home, call home
Hear my name
Call home, call home
Till the road feels tame
(call home)
One more mile
One more prayer
Call home, call home
You’re still there

I got pay in my pocket
Dust on my jeans
But your voice on that line
Means everything to me
If the engine keeps hummin’
Till the daylight comes through
I’ll keep on drivin’
’Cause I’m drivin’ back to you

Call home, call home
Hear my name
Call home, call home
Till the road feels tame
(call home)
One more mile
One more prayer
Call home, call home
You’re still there

songs.lyrics:

Headlights blur

Just another thousand miles

songs.subject: a jazz song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Sleeper cab shaking
On mile marker rain
I got your picture
Taped by the chain
Coffee gone bitter
Road dust on my hands
I’m at a payphone
Near a shuttered gas stand

It’s three in the morning
The moon on my hood
I hear your hello
And I’m back where I stood

Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man
Call me home
Three in the morning
Say my name slow
(Call me home)
Call me home

Six states behind me
One more bridge to cross
The radio’s tired
The sky is all frost
Your laugh through the speaker
Cuts through the sleep
You say don’t worry
Baby, come back to me

I count the white lines
Like beads in my palm
Your voice keeps me steady
Keeps the engine calm

Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man
Call me home
Three in the morning
Say my name slow
(Call me home)
Call me home

I’ve got forty more exits
And a stubborn old tank
But your soft little answer
Is all that I bank
If the road keeps on taking
I’ll still find my way
To your porch light waiting
At the break of day

Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man
Call me home
Three in the morning
Say my name slow
(Call me home)
Call me home

Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man
Call me home
Three in the morning
Say my name slow
(Call me home)
Call me home

I’ve got forty more exits
And a stubborn old tank
But your soft little answer
Is all that I bank
If the road keeps on taking
I’ll still find my way
To your porch light waiting
At the break of day

Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man
Call me home
Three in the morning
Say my name slow
(Call me home)
Call me home

songs.subject: a jazz song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning
I’m southbound and tired
My hands on the wheel
My thoughts on the wires
A diner sign fades
Like a coin in the rain
I pull for a phone booth
On the edge of the lane

I hear your hello
Through the crackle and drift
Like a light in the dark
That remembers this shift

Call me home
Call me home
Keep that line warm
Call me home
Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man
On this long road

There’s coffee gone cold
In a cup by the seat
A map on my knees
And red dust on my boots
The load in the trailer
A hundred miles more
But your voice on the line
Is the thing I keep for

Say the kids are asleep
Say the porch bulb’s on
I can almost stand there
When you say come on

Call me home
Call me home
Keep that line warm
Call me home
Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man
On this long road

I pass a white fence
And a grocery store sign
I think of your laugh
Cutting clean through the night
If I sound far away
It’s this iron and rain
But I’m driving to you
By the name and the flame

Call me home
Call me home
Keep that line warm
Call me home
Call me home
Call me home
I’m still your man

songs.subject: a tango song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I saw your coat on the chair
Near the red-dyed wall
You laughed for someone else
And I felt myself fall
My glass left a ring
On the white linen cloth
I stayed in the doorway
Like a name you forgot

You said my first name soft
Like it might turn to fire
But your hand found his sleeve
And I stood in the wire
One step closer, then gone
That’s the game you played
Now the whole room leans in
When I’m being betrayed

Dance with me, not him
Dance with me tonight
I’m the one you pass
In the edge of your light
Dance with me, not him
Let the old hurt spin
I keep one chair warm
For a love that won’t begin

Your perfume stayed late
On the back of my throat
While he kissed your cheek
Like he owned the note
The band changed its tune
To a slower refrain
I smiled with my mouth
But my heart had a stain

You looked right at me
Then you looked away
Like a blade with a ribbon
That cuts just the same
I could leave this floor
But I know I would stay
If you ever turned back
I’d be lost in the sway

Dance with me, not him
Dance with me tonight
I’m the one you pass
In the edge of your light
Dance with me, not him
Let the old hurt spin
I keep one chair warm
For a love that won’t begin

Maybe I loved you
For the space you left me
For the almost, the nearly
For the way you didn’t see
Now the room goes quiet
As the final notes bend
And I bow to the wound
That will not end

Dance with me, not him
Dance with me tonight
I’m the one you pass
In the edge of your light
Dance with me, not him
Let the old hurt spin
I keep one chair warm
For a love that won’t begin

One chair warm
One heart still
Waiting at the edge
Of your heel

songs.subject: a tango song about an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You wore that red handkerchief
Like a promise I could keep
I learned your steps by memory
While you slept inside my leap
I saved a chair beside the door
I saved your name in every glass
You smiled as if I were the floor
That held you up and let you pass

And I know, I know
You never came for me
But every night
I dress the wound and wait

Ay, ay, ay
You never loved me
Ay, ay, ay
Still I called you mine
One more turn
One more ache
I keep dancing
To your shadow line
Ay, ay, ay
You never loved me
Ay, ay, ay
But I still hold on
If you won't stay
Let me break
In the shape of your
Unfinished song

You took my laugh, my best white shirt
And left your perfume on the stair
The room still knows the way you moved
Even when you are not there
I tried to kiss the air you breathed
I tried to burn my pride to gold
But every rose I laid for you
Came back to me, already cold

And I know, I know
Your heart was never mine
But when you turned away
You made a saint of time

Ay, ay, ay
You never loved me
Ay, ay, ay
Still I called you mine
One more turn
One more ache
I keep dancing
To your shadow line
Ay, ay, ay
You never loved me
Ay, ay, ay
But I still hold on
If you won't stay
Let me break
In the shape of your
Unfinished song

Take the ribbon from my throat
Let the whole room see me bleed
I was foolish, yes, I was
To mistake need for need
But when the last chord falls away
And the floor goes dark and bare
I will bow to what you are
And not the love that wasn't there

Ay, ay, ay
You never loved me
Ay, ay, ay
Still I called you mine
One more turn
One more ache
I keep dancing
To your shadow line
Ay, ay, ay
You never loved me
Ay, ay, ay
Now I let you go
If you won't stay
Let me break
And turn this hunger

songs.subject: a funk song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
You left your cup on the table
Still got your ring on the sink
I’m walking soft through the hallway
Like the whole house heard us think
Your jacket on the chair back
My keys in a little red bowl
We said the hard things loudly
Now the quiet took control

And I hate this hush
More than the words we threw
The room feels too big
When it’s just me and you

Talk to me now
Talk to me now
Don’t let this hush
Wear your face down
Talk to me now
Talk to me now
We can be mad
But don’t disappear now

The sink still holds your coffee
Half a cup gone cold
I’m staring at the ceiling
Trying to unbreak my soul
You moved the picture crooked
I fixed it back by hand
Little signs of us still living
In this quiet little land

And I hate this hush
More than the words we threw
The room feels too big
When it’s just me and you

Talk to me now
Talk to me now
Don’t let this hush
Wear your face down
Talk to me now
Talk to me now
We can be mad
But don’t disappear now

Come back to the table
Even if it’s just to stay
We don’t need a victory
We just need a way
Say my name once
Say it like you mean
We can patch this dark
With the smallest seam

Talk to me now
Talk to me now
Don’t let this hush
Wear your face down
Talk to me now
Talk to me now
We can be mad
But don’t disappear now

songs.subject: a funk song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall
Tick-tockin' slow (tick-tock!)
Ain't nobody movin'
Ain't nobody gonna go (uh-huh!)
Yeah
The air's thick and heavy
Like a bad stew (so thick!)
After all that hollerin'
Me and you (just me and you)
We done said some things
Baby
Ain't that the truth? (Ooo-wee!)
Now we just starin'
Like some kinda goof

My heart's doin' the cha-cha
But my lips are sealed (sealed up tight!)
This silent treatment
Baby
How does it feel?

Oh
The quiet
The quiet
After the storm (the quiet storm!)
This ain't no cozy
Keepin' each other warm (no warmth at all!)
Just the sound of nothin'
A heavy
Heavy sound (heavy sound!)
Gotta break this silence
Spin it all around (spin it 'round!)
Yeah
The quiet
The quiet
It's a funky kind of dread (uh-huh!)
All these unspoken words
Messin' with my head (my poor head!)

I'm lookin' at your profile
Sharp as a knife (so sharp!)
Wish I could rewind
Back to a happier life (back in time!)
This tension is a monster
Growin' in the room (a big beast!)
Suckin' all the joy
Sendin' it to its doom (doom, doom, doom!)
My foot starts to tap
A rhythm of regret (tap-tap!)
Still no words comin'
Not one single peep yet

Oh
The quiet
The quiet
After the storm (the quiet storm!)
This ain't no cozy
Keepin' each other warm (no warmth at all!)
Just the sound of nothin'
A heavy
Heavy sound (heavy sound!)
Gotta break this silence
Spin it all around (spin it 'round!)
Yeah
The quiet
The quiet
It's a funky kind of dread (uh-huh!)
All these unspoken words
Messin' with my head (my poor head!)

Gotta find that groove again
Gotta find that beat (find that beat!)
Before this cold silence
Sweeps us off our feet (cold, cold feet!)
One little whisper
One little sigh (just one!)
Gonna reach out
Baby
Give it a try (gonna try, gonna try!)
Yeah
A little touch
A little glance (a little glance!)
Let's get back to dancin'

songs.subject: a synthwave song about a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors fold shut
Steel grip on my wrist
The car lurches hard
And my skin starts to hiss
A man in a suit
Stares through his lap
I count the poles
Then I lose the map

My throat goes thin
My hands go white
The tunnel bends
Like it knows my name tonight

Too close, too fast
My breath won't stay
Hold on, hold on
Until it breaks away
Too close, too fast
I fade in the glass
Hold on, hold on
Let the moment pass (let it pass)

A child kicks feet
On the orange seat
Somebody laughs
And it sounds like heat
I press to the door
But the door won't move
Every station sign
Won't give me proof

My heart runs wild
My ribs on fire
That silver line
Turns into a wire

Too close, too fast
My breath won't stay
Hold on, hold on
Until it breaks away
Too close, too fast
I fade in the glass
Hold on, hold on
Let the moment pass (let it pass)

Then the train slows down
Like a held-back cry
I find one crack
In the poisoned sky
One, two, three
Touch the cold rail
One, two, three
I'm still here, I prevail

Too close, too fast
My breath won't stay
Hold on, hold on
Until it breaks away
Too close, too fast
I fade in the glass
Hold on, hold on
Let the moment pass (let it pass)
Too close, too fast
I make it through
Hold on, hold on
Till the station view (comes through)

songs.lyrics:
Platform edge
My hand on the pole
Fluorescent rain
On a scratched-up window
Doors shut hard
And the train shakes loose
My chest goes small
In the second car

A stranger's shoe
An elbow, a bag
My thoughts run fast
Then they all lag
The tunnel bends
Like a ribcage bruise
I count the stops
But I lose the news

Hold on, hold on
Don't let go
Air gets thin
And it starts to show
Hold on, hold on
Count me in
One more breath
One more spin

Stay with me
Stay with me
My heart won't sit still
Stay with me
Stay with me
Till the doors unlock
Stay with me
Stay with me
I can make it through
Stay with me
Stay with me
On this shaking route

Map lights blink
By the metal seat
A kid nearby
With a candy treat
I press my palm
To my cold blue jeans
Try to borrow time
From the in-between

Hold on, hold on
Don't let go
Air gets thin
And it starts to show
Hold on, hold on

One more breath
One more spin

Stay with me
Stay with me

Stay with me

Stay with me
Stay with me
On this shaking route

When the brakes bite
I feel the floor
I want the world
To be less sore
I fix my eyes
On the silver line
One stop left
I will be fine

Stay with me
Stay with me
My heart won't sit still
Stay with me
Stay with me

Stay with me
Stay with me
I can make it through
Stay with me
Stay with me

songs.subject: a tango song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You took my corner
I took your name
We learned the hard way
This room likes pain
Black shoes on marble
Cold as a vow
The bandoneón calls us
So move now
Your hand is steady
Mine is a blade
We hate the same rhythm
That keeps us swayed

One step closer
Don't look away
Toe to toe now
We both obey

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Heel to heel
And we both bow
Dance with me, rival
Don't let go
If we must burn
Let the whole room know (dance with me)

You read my silence
Like a street in rain
I read your shoulders
For the crack in your game
We circle tighter
A knot in the floor
Every slow turn says
We're not at war
Your breath hits my collar
My pulse hits your skin
Funny how victory
Feels like stepping in

One step closer
Don't look away
Toe to toe now
We both obey

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Heel to heel
And we both bow
Dance with me, rival
Don't let go
If we must burn
Let the whole room know (dance with me)

The crowd holds still
Like a held-back knife
You find my weakness
I find your life
We were made for this
For the push and pull
For the beautiful ruin
Of refusing to fall

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Heel to heel
And we both bow
Dance with me, rival
Don't let go
If we must burn
Let the whole room know (dance with me)
Dance with me, rival
One more round
You and me
Till the lights come down (dance with me)

Of refusing to fall

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me now
Heel to heel
And we both bow
Dance with me, rival
Don't let go
If we must burn
Let the whole room know (dance with me)
Dance with me, rival
One more round
You and me
Till the lights come down (dance with me)

songs.subject: a tango song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You wear your black gloves
I wear my white blade
Two names on the program
One dance to be made
We bow like enemies
Smile through the room
The floor is a witness
To what we can’t consume

One step close
One step away
Your eyes say fight
My hands say stay

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me, now
Close to the cut
And don’t look down
Dance with me, rival
Dance with me, hard
We can’t leave proud
So we’ll leave marked

Your heel taps sharp
Mine answers back
We trade every glance
Like a thief on a track
The crowd holds still
For the spark in the air
You hate how I lead you
I hate how you’re there

One turn fast
One turn slow
If I pull too hard
You never let go

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me, now
Close to the cut
And don’t look down
Dance with me, rival
Dance with me, hard
We can’t leave proud
So we’ll leave marked

Say my name with your chin up high
I’ll meet that flame in your stubborn eyes
If this is war
Then make it art
One last circle
One last heart

Dance with me, rival
Dance with me, now
Close to the cut
And don’t look down
Dance with me, rival
Dance with me, hard
We can’t leave proud
So we’ll leave marked
Dance with me, rival
Dance with me, close
Two hands on fire

songs.subject: a indie rock song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
I got home with a duffel bag
And dust in my cuffs
Ma said, "leave your boots by the door"
Like I was never that rough
The porch light buzzed all night
Over chipped-up paint
I stood there with my good hand
Feeling like a saint

The dog hit the screen door hard
Like he knew my name
I laughed too loud, then froze up
At the sound of rain

I came home, I came home
With my old heart slow
I came home, I came home
To the red barn glow
(come on home)
(come on home)

Dad fixed the sink with a wrench
Said, "eat before it cools"
My little sister grew two inches
And still called me a fool
There’s a scar by my left wrist
From a wire I missed
She traced it with her fingertip
Like she knew I’d exist

The house kept every little thing
The screen, the smell, the crack
But I kept reaching for a pocket
That held me on my back

I came home, I came home
With my old heart slow
I came home, I came home
To the red barn glow
(come on home)
(come on home)

I sleep with the window open
Hear the creek in the dark
Some nights I wake up fighting
Some nights I just watch the park
But you set a plate for me
And nobody asked me why
So I learned the shape of morning
By the light in your eyes

I came home, I came home
With my old heart slow
I came home, I came home
To the red barn glow
I came home, I came home
And I’m learning to stay
(come on home)
(come on home)

songs.subject: a indie rock song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots by the door
Mud on the floor
Ma folds my name
Like a clean white shirt
The dog knows first
Runs to my knees
I stand real still
Let the room get me

I had sand in my hair
And smoke in my throat
Now the porch light shakes
When the front door opens slow

I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Put my ghost on the table
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Hand me back my first name
I’m coming home

Dad on the steps
Hands in his lap
We do that nod
Men do when they’re cracked
Sister laughs once
Then she starts to cry
I hold that sound
Like a hard-saved dime

There’s a scar on the wall
From a picture that fell
I look at it hard
Like it can tell
How to be plain
How to be new
How to sit at this table
And stay in the room

I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Put my ghost on the table
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Hand me back my first name
I’m coming home

I left with a bag
And a mouth full of prayers
Came back with the rain
Still stuck in my hair
If I shake at night
If I wake from the blast
Hold my hand hard
Tell me this part will pass

I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Put my ghost on the table
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Hand me back my first name
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
The door stays open

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I keep your name in salt
Folded in my coat
By the lamp I write
Every wave a note
Your blue mouth at my window
Keeps calling me home
So I seal these lines
In a bottle of foam

When the tide leans in
I feel your pull
The shore gets quiet
My hands go full
I write you soft
I write you slow
Till the dark line breaks
And I let it go

Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my letter
Carry my heart
To the edge of forever
Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my letter
If you return her touch
I'll be waiting better

I mend the lamp at dawn
With my sleeves rolled high
Sand in the porch boards
Storm in the sky
Your waves leave silver
On the rocks outside
I read your mouth in the spray
And it feels like reply

When the tide leans in
I feel your pull
The shore gets quiet
My hands go full
I write you soft
I write you slow
Till the dark line breaks
And I let it go

Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my letter
Carry my heart
To the edge of forever
Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my letter
If you return her touch
I'll be waiting better

I know you never stay
You move like a prayer
But every wrecked old promise
Still blooms in your care
So if my voice gets broken
On the rocks of goodbye
Keep one small word of me
Beneath your tide

Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my letter
Carry my heart
To the edge of forever
Sea, take my letter
Sea, take my letter
If you return her touch
I'll be waiting better

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I keep your note in the lantern glass
Folded small in a sailor clasp
Ink got thin from the salt and spray
Still I write to you every day
Your name on the back of a weathered chart
Pinned by a nail in the keeper’s heart
When the tide climbs up my windowpane
I swear you answer back in rain

Tell me you’re near
Tell me you stay
Tell me the dark
Still knows my name

Sea, take my letters
Take my hands
Sea, take my letters
If you can
I love you deep
I love you long
Sea, keep my words
Where they belong

At dawn I sweep the glass in gold
Count the ships like stories told
Tea goes cold beside the beam
While your curve lives inside my dream
I wrote you once on a torn page edge
About the gulls and the cliffside ledge
About the ache in my working chest
How your wide blue mouth knows me best

Tell me you’re near
Tell me you stay
Tell me the dark
Still knows my name

Sea, take my letters
Take my hands
Sea, take my letters
If you can
I love you deep
I love you long
Sea, keep my words
Where they belong

If I am stone, you are the tide
If I am lamp, you are the bride
Coming home in a silver roll
Kissing the rocks and stealing my soul
So if my ink runs out tonight
I’ll write in foam by lantern light
One last line for the waves to read
Come back to me
Come back to me

Sea, take my letters
Take my hands
Sea, take my letters
If you can
I love you deep
I love you long
Sea, keep my words

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Your hands had the map of miles,
Palm lines like old river bends.
They folded bread and broke bad news,
Still found a way to bless my head.
Scar on the thumb, gold ring worn thin,
You held that pot like it owed you.
You never said what pain you kept,
But baby, I saw it in your moves.

When the house felt too big,
You made it feel small.
One touch at the doorframe,
And I could stand tall.

Grandma’s hands, hold me now
(Grandma’s hands)
Grandma’s hands, still know how
(Still know how)
You made a whole life
Out of what they gave
Grandma’s hands
Keep me brave

You pinched the seam, you mended me,
Said, “Baby, don’t rush that tear.”
Your nails were short, your wrists were strong,
You crossed yourself before each year.
Sunday soap in the yellow sink,
Pepper on the greens, slow and sure.
You’d hum low while you shaped the dough,
Like every ache could be cured.

When the world got loud,
You lowered your voice.
That quiet love
Was a holy choice.

Grandma’s hands, hold me now
(Grandma’s hands)
Grandma’s hands, still know how
(Still know how)
You made a whole life
Out of what they gave
Grandma’s hands
Keep me brave

Now I catch my own reflection
In the way I smooth my son’s hair.
I see you in the kitchen light,
In the steady way I care.
If I ever lose my footing,
I just call your name.

Grandma’s hands, hold me now
(Grandma’s hands)
Grandma’s hands, still know how
(Still know how)
You made a whole life
Out of what they gave
Grandma’s hands
Keep me brave
Grandma’s hands
Keep me brave

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Your hands were a map of work
Cream on the knuckles, quiet and sure
Rose thorn scars on your thumb
Still you folded shirts like they were gold

You split a peach with one clean turn
Held my face when I got burned
Those brown hands knew every ache
Knew how much a small prayer takes

And when the house got loud
You made the room go still
One touch could calm the storm
One palm could bend my will

Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They carried me soft
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They never let go
(hold me now)
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
Still warm in my mind
(oh, grandma)
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They taught me to shine

You braided time in Sunday light
Poured sweet tea in the jar by the sink
You laughed with your whole front porch
Like joy was something you could keep

And when I lose my way
I see them in the rain
Two steady lanterns low
Bringing me back again

Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They carried me soft
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They never let go
(hold me now)
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
Still warm in my mind
(oh, grandma)
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They taught me to shine

Now I wear your touch
In the way I move
In the way I love
In the way I choose
If heaven has a door
Bet it opens like you
With a palm on my head
Saying, baby, come through

Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They carried me soft
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
They never let go
(hold me now)
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
Still warm in my mind
(oh, grandma)
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Corner store shut by nine
Fence with the busted sign
Same cracked block, same long line
Same red truck in my mind
Mama said, "Keep your head"
I kept that in when I went
Four steps out, ten steps back
Still I learned the shape of it

I saw the curb like a line
Saw the block like a trap
But I kept my shoes tied tight
Kept my face in the map

Out that block
Out that block
I kept moving
Out that block
Out that block
I ain't losing

Summer heat on the brick wall
Kids still hoop with a bent ball
Sirens braid through the night air
Dreams get small, then get tall
I stacked hope in my backpack
Milk crate seats, no plan B
Took the long road, took the hard road
Til the hard road looked like me

I saw the curb like a line
Saw the block like a trap
But I kept my shoes tied tight
Kept my face in the map

Out that block
Out that block
I kept moving
Out that block
Out that block
I ain't losing

Now I send bread back home
Now the little ones know
A dead-end don't mean dead dreams
If you learn where to go

Out that block
Out that block
I kept moving
Out that block
Out that block
I ain't losing

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked curb, same red gate
Same boys rolling dice by the laundromat
Mama said, "Keep your head up straight"
I kept my dreams in a grocery bag

Potholes know my whole first name
Chain-link fence with a bent-out frame
Every porch light felt the same
Till I saw my path past the smoke and rain

I been counting days
On the bus route home
I want more than this
I want more than stone

Out the dead-end block
Out the dead-end block
I'm not trapped here, nah
I'm moving past that spot
Out the dead-end block
Out the dead-end block
Take this hurt and turn it
Into something that won't stop

Now I write my name on clean white pages
Still got scars, but they don't own stages
Old friends ask if I miss those cages
I say love to the block, but I'm out these rails

If the road gets long
I don't fold, I don't fold
Every bruise I wore
Turned me into gold

Out the dead-end block
Out the dead-end block
I'm not trapped here, nah
I'm moving past that spot
Out the dead-end block
Out the dead-end block
Take this hurt and turn it
Into something that won't stop

Same cracked curb, different feet

songs.subject: a dream pop song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots by the mat again
Mud on the hem of you
Ma left the porch light on
Like she knew what I'd been through
The screen door sighed in the wind
Your house still knew my name
I stood there with my hands
Like they belonged to rain

And the fields were far away
But they never left my skin
I heard them in the pipes
I heard them in the tin
Then you opened up the door
And the whole world slowed down

I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Salt on the door
And your arms around me
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Lay my name down
Let the night unbind me

The table had an extra plate
A crack I never saw before
You asked me how the road was
I stared at the floor
My old dog hit my knee
Like time could be mended
I laughed too hard, too late
And cried when the room ended

But your voice was in the hall
Soft as a second chance
Pulling at the dark
Letting me breathe at last
Then you said my name
And the ache came undone

I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Salt on the door
And your arms around me
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Lay my name down
Let the night unbind me

If I wake in the cold
If the old sounds climb again
Hold my shaking hands
Till they learn your shape again
I was gone for a while
But I’m here, I’m here

I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Salt on the door
And your arms around me
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
Lay my name down
Let the night unbind me
I’m coming home
I’m coming home
And the house says my name
Like it never left me

Mud on the hem of you

songs.lyrics:
Boots by the mat
Dust in the seam
Ma leaves the porch light
Like she kept my dream
The screen door sticks
The yard smells like rain
I stand in the hall
With my old name

I count the frames
On the kitchen wall
Hands on the sink
I hear it all

I made it home
I made it home
Hold me close now
I made it home
I made it home
I made it home
Let my lungs learn
How to be home

Your little dog
Still circles my legs
I laugh too hard
Then I sit and shake
The radio hums
From the window ledge
Your voice in the room
Feels like the edge

I count the frames
On the kitchen wall
Hands on the sink
I hear it all

I made it home
I made it home
Hold me close now
I made it home
I made it home
I made it home
Let my lungs learn

Pull me back in
Tell me my shadow
Can start again
Wash off the road
From my shaking hands
Teach me the shape
Of your plans

I made it home
I made it home
Hold me close now
I made it home
I made it home
I made it home
Let my lungs learn
How to be home
I made it home
I made it home
Salt on the door

songs.subject: a ambient song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Sky bleeds purple
Green streaks across
Dancing fire
No cost

Up above us
A silent show

Northern lights ignite the night
Northern lights a cosmic flight
Northern lights a wild delight
Northern lights so pure and bright

Stars just pinpricks
Moon a pale ghost
Whispers of wonder
I love it the most

Colors shifting
A painter's dream
Washing over
A beautiful gleam

Northern lights ignite the night
Northern lights a cosmic flight
Northern lights
Aurora Drift

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Midnight oil burnin',
Stardust on the window pane,
Thinkin' 'bout that one, man,
A diamond in the rain.
She moved like a rhythm,
A beat I couldn't tame.

Ghost of a groove,
Yeah, the one that got away,
Echo in the static,
Every single day.
Ghost of a groove,
What more can I say?

Picture her laughin',
A melody so sweet,
Now just a silent movie,
On a lonely street.
Tried to catch that lightning,
Two left feet.

Ghost of a groove,
Yeah, the one that got away,
Echo in the static,
Every single day.
Ghost of a groove,
What more can I say?

Maybe it's better,
Some stories incomplete,
A perfect fade-out,
Bitter, yet so neat.
But damn, that track,
Still on repeat.

Ghost of a groove,
Yeah, the one

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Midnight thoughts, hazy glow
Streetlights blur, movin' slow
Another beat drops, then I know
You're the echo, where'd you go?

Ghost of you, a whisper in the breeze
Haunts my headphones, puts my mind at ease
Then rattles chains, disturbs the peace
Yeah, the ghost of you, never truly released

Sunrise creepin', through the blinds it leaks
Another memory, that your absence speaks
My brain's a record, skip and then it seeks
That one lost track, for weeks and weeks

Ghost of you, a whisper in the breeze
Haunts my headphones, puts my mind at ease
Then rattles chains, disturbs the peace
Yeah, the ghost of you, never truly released

Shoulda held tighter, yeah, shoulda known
Now just shadows, where your light was shown
A phantom limb, still feelin' grown

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Years fly by
Like a swift river (ooh-yeah!)
We danced so slow
Together
Hand in hand
A silent prayer
Time moves on
But love is there

Last dance
First kiss
My dear
Always you
Always near
One more spin
A gentle sway
Love's old song
Every day

Graying hair
And weathered lines
Stories told
In quiet times
Familiar touch
A knowing glance
Still feel that spark
Our first romance

Last dance
First kiss
My dear
Always you
Always near
One more spin
A gentle sway
Love's old song
Every day

Memories
Soft and true
Me and you
Me and you
Every

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The hall is empty now
Except our scuffed-up shoes
Your hand in mine is shaking
Like it still don’t know the news
The old ring on your finger
Catches light and lets it go
We came here young and laughing
Now we take it slow

You say, “One more time”
And I say, “Yes, ma’am”
We learned a lot of endings
By the work of these two hands

One last dance, darlin’
One last spin for two
One last dance, darlin’
The whole world fit in you
We’ve worn these boards down
With every hurt and every grace
One last dance, darlin’
Then we go home and rest our place

I remember county dances
Your cotton dress, my boots
The tobacco on my collar
And your laugh like breaking roots
We raised our sons on Friday
We buried friends in June
But you still save me room
When the fiddle plays a tune

You lean in, I catch
That little stubborn grin
The years may have bent us
But they never broke us in

One last dance, darlin’
One last spin for two
One last dance, darlin’
The whole world fit in you
We’ve worn these boards down
With every hurt and every grace
One last dance, darlin’
Then we go home and rest our place

If my steps go crooked
If your knees don’t turn like they used to do
Just hold me closer
I’ll keep time for you
And when the song is over
And the room goes still and bare
I’ll thank the Lord for this hard life
And the woman standing there

One last dance, darlin’
One last spin for two
One last dance, darlin’
The whole world fit in you
We’ve worn these boards down
With every hurt and every grace
One last dance, darlin’
Then we go home and rest our place

Then we go home
Hand in hand

songs.subject: a punk song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum
On a cracked red seat
Pie case shining
Like a busted-up dream
Coffee keeps pouring
From a dented steel pot
The fry cook's laughing
At the cops on the lot

Two in the morning
Still taking my cash
Door keeps swinging
Like it's mad at the lash

All night diner
All night diner
Keep the tray hot
Keep the floor wired
All night diner
All night diner
You got my name
In the grease and fire

Truckers with sugar
And kids with bruised knuckles
Moms in work boots
And a booth full of trouble
Salt on the table
Mugs stained brown
Every lost soul
Finds a place in this town

Window fogging
From the steam and the heat
I know your heartbeat
When you slide that seat

All night diner
All night diner
Keep the tray hot
Keep the floor wired
All night diner
All night diner
You got my name
In the grease and fire

Don't shut that door
Don't kill that light
I need this booth
Till the edge of night
Half-time clatter
Then the whole room shouts
One more refill
When the world burns out

All night diner
All night diner
Keep the tray hot
Keep the floor wired
All night diner
All night diner
You got my name
In the grease and fire
All night diner
All night diner
You stay open
You stay alive

songs.subject: a punk song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights bleedin' orange haze
Another night another maze
Coffee cold but still I drink
On this stool I start to think

Greasy spoon never sleeps
Secrets that this joint keeps
Flickerin' neon sign
This all-night diner is mine

Faces blurred through smoky air
No one seems to really care
Just the clatter plates and cups
Lost souls filling up their guts

Greasy spoon never sleeps
Secrets that this joint keeps
Flickerin' neon sign
This all-night diner is mine

What's the point of closing doors
When the world just wants some more
Of this cheap forgotten grace
A haven in this goddamn place

Greasy spoon never sleeps

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights bleed on wet asphalt
I see your ghost in every fault
The corner store the broken swing
Every memory makes my heart sing

My head's a fog
A sweet sweet bog

Hometown haze
Lost in your gaze
Yeah your gaze
My hometown daze

Old brick walls know every secret
Every whispered word every regret
Your laugh echoes in the empty park
Leaving its permanent mark

Maybe I'm just dreaming again
Caught

My head's a fog

Hometown haze

Yeah your gaze
My hometown daze

Old brick walls know every secret

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
You said my last name
like you knew the porch
like you knew the dent
in the screen door frame
I saw your bike chain
rust on your sleeve
Saturday dust on
your tennis shoes

We grew up on the same cracked road
same creek bed, same old store
Now your eyes keep pulling me
back to the field behind your house

Falling for you
from my hometown
Falling for you
where the weeds grow down
Moss on Main
on your front steps
Moss on Main
and I can't forget

You still point out names
on the water tower
and laugh like July
at the fairground gate
My mother says
she remembers your dad
from the yellow truck
and the years we had

Now your thumb finds mine
in the backseat dark
A quiet spark
in a place so known
We never left
but we never stayed
and now you feel
like the one I'd wait for

Falling for you
from my hometown
Falling for you
where the weeds grow down
Moss on Main
on your front steps
Moss on Main
and I can't forget

At the diner booth
you traced my hand
said "I know this town"
like a secret plan
And every road sign
every worn-out fence
turned into something
I could live in again

Falling for you
from my hometown
Falling for you
let it come down
Moss on Main
in the back of my mind
Moss on Main

songs.subject: a afrobeats song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Mama cut that cloth right
One glance, perfect fit
She knows my shoulder line
Makes old shirt look rich
Need a hem? Need a cuff?
She got that magic touch
From the market to my lane
Everybody trusts her much

Pins in her mouth
Tape on the floor
She says, "Stand still"
Then you want more
One quick turn
Then she smiles at you
When she lifts that sleeve
Whole block brand new

Dress the whole block
Dress the whole block
From the front yard to the bus stop
Dress the whole block
Dress the whole block
Mama make the whole street pop
(Whole block) dress the whole block
(Whole block) dress the whole block

Brother came with a rip
Left knee, ugly tear
Left with a clean cream suit
Like he was born in there
Auntie needs her aso
Kids need school-day shine
Even the young boys clap
When she marks the line

Pins in her mouth
Tape on the floor
She says, "Stand still"
Then you want more
One quick turn
Then she smiles at you
When she lifts that sleeve
Whole block brand new

Dress the whole block
Dress the whole block
From the front yard to the bus stop
Dress the whole block
Dress the whole block
Mama make the whole street pop
(Whole block) dress the whole block
(Whole block) dress the whole block

One stitch, two stitch
She fixes the pain
Old cloth, fresh life
Back again
Gold thread, sharp hands
Eyes on the seam
She don't just sew clothes
She sews a dream

Dress the whole block
Dress the whole block
From the front yard to the bus stop
Dress the whole block
Dress the whole block
Mama make the whole street pop
(Whole block) dress the whole block
(Whole block) dress the whole block

songs.subject: a afrobeats song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
On the corner by the market
Needle flashing like a comet
Jide cut my brother sharp
Now the whole lane wants the same fit
He got chalk on his fingers
Tape measure and a grin
Every zipper got a purpose
Every seam knows where it’s been

He say, stand still, lean back
One stitch, then you snap
From the gate to the kiosk
Everybody wants that

Dress the whole block, dress the whole block
Jide from the block, dress the whole block
Fresh for the Friday, fresh for the talk
Dress the whole block, dress the whole block
Every hem sit clean
Every waistline locked
When he step outside
Man, the whole place stop

Mama want a wrapper
Boys want a two-piece set
Uncle ask for church clothes
Even the twins not done yet
He know who likes fitted
Who wants loose and sweet
By the time the sun go down
Everybody look complete

He say, stand still, lean back
One stitch, then you snap
From the gate to the kiosk
Everybody wants that

When he step outside
Man, the whole place stop

Late nights by the machine

songs.subject: a indie rock song about the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
We pushed the chairs back slow
From the chipped oak table
Your ring left a pale line
On the sauce-stained label
The house held its breath
Like it knew the date
We laughed at the ice
Then we cried by the gate

You fixed my collar
I fixed your sleeve
Thirty-nine years
Then the room went still for me

One last dance, one last dance
Spin me through the years
One last dance, one last dance
Hold me while we're here
We were fire, we were bone
Now we're letting go
One last dance, one last dance
Before we both go home

The radio's bent song
Kept falling off key
Your shoes by the doorway
Still knew how to lead me
The kids sent their voices
From a crackling screen
Said "don't let the silence
Take the best of your dreams"

You touched my wrist
Like you used to do
We didn't need words
We just needed the truth

One last dance, one last dance
Spin me through the years
One last dance, one last dance
Hold me while we're here
We were fire, we were bone
Now we're letting go
One last dance, one last dance
Before we both go home

I won't call it failure
I won't call it fate
We grew like two vines
Around the same old gate
If love is a harbor
Then storms were our name
But your hand found mine
And I knew you the same

One last dance, one last dance
Spin me through the years
One last dance, one last dance
Hold me while we're here
We were fire, we were bone
Now we're letting go
One last dance, one last dance
Before we both go home

The floorboards remember
The bend in our knees
One last dance, one last dance
Then you can leave with me

songs.lyrics:
We put the lamps low
Left the dishes in the sink
Your dress on the chair
My shoes by the bedframe
The house held its breath
Like it knew our names
One slow song left
And all those years came

You took my hand
Like you still meant yes
We laughed at the floor
At our Sunday best

Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
Hold me one more time
Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
We are still alive
Last dance tonight

You traced that old scar
On my left hand
I said, "We were kids"
You said, "We were brave"
The record spun thin
Like a silver thread
Every worn-out vow
Back in my chest

You pulled me close
No words to spend
Just two old hearts
Learning again

Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
Hold me one more time
Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
We are still alive
Last dance tonight

Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
Hold me one more time
Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
Last dance tonight
We are still alive
Last dance tonight

The song ran out
But you didn't leave
You stayed in my arms

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Two bags on my floor
One name on the dock
Salt on my tongue
And a clock I can’t stop
I left my mother’s gate
With the key in my fist
Turned my back on the lane
That knew all my steps
The ferry breathed smoke
Under a pink-grey dawn
Men held their caps low
Till the shore was gone

And the water kept saying
Go on, go on
My fear in my pocket
But my feet moved on

Across the sea, across the sea
Take me across the sea
I left my home in my hands
Now make a new road for me
Across the sea, across the sea
Take me across the sea
If I lose my old name
Let the waves remember me

I learned to sleep upright
With my coat like a shield
Count the drum of the deck
And the gulls overhead
A child cried once
Then the whole room did too
Every face on that boat
Was a map split in two
I tasted iron rain
On a tin cup rim
And I thought of the stove
Back where I grew thin

And the water kept saying
Go on, go on
My fear in my pocket
But my feet moved on

Across the sea, across the sea
Take me across the sea
I left my home in my hands
Now make a new road for me
Across the sea, across the sea
Take me across the sea
If I lose my old name
Let the waves remember me

When the dark got long
I wrote your name in steam
Then the glass went blank
Like a half-closed dream
But I held that line
Like a thread in my teeth
Till the first pale shore
Split open beneath me

Across the sea, across the sea
Take me across the sea
I left my home in my hands
Now make a new road for me
Across the sea, across the sea
Take me across the sea
If I lose my old name
Let the waves remember me
Across the sea, across the sea
Take me across the sea
I’m still made of that crossing

songs.subject: a soul song about quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I know this floorboard by my name
I know that crack beside the stain
I learned my manners in these pews
Learned how to bow, learned how to choose
Mama sang with her eyes shut tight
Daddy said hold on, keep it right
But something in me stayed unseen
Like a bird trapped in the evergreen

Now I’m folding up my little chair
Puttin’ my heart in the winter air
I don’t want to make a scene
I just can’t live where I can’t breathe

I’m leaving quiet
(quiet)
I’m leaving quiet
I love you, but I can’t stay
I’m leaving quiet
(quiet)
I’m leaving quiet
Before my soul turns hard one day

I still can hear that Saturday choir
Milk-bread voices, holy fire
I kissed the doorframe on my way out
Like I was breaking from my own house
Nobody saw my hands shake
Nobody heard the promise break
I kept my coat around my shame
Walked past the cross and called my name

Now I’m folding up my little chair
Puttin’ my heart in the winter air
I don’t want to make a scene
I just can’t live where I can’t breathe

I’m leaving quiet
(quiet)
I’m leaving quiet
I love you, but I can’t stay
I’m leaving quiet
(quiet)
I’m leaving quiet
Before my soul turns hard one day

If love is real, it’ll know my face
Even at the edge of this place
I’m still the child you raised in prayer
I’m just not found in the same way there

I’m leaving quiet
(quiet)
I’m leaving quiet
I love you, but I can’t stay
I’m leaving quiet
(quiet)
I’m leaving quiet
Before my soul turns hard one day

songs.subject: a soul song about quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I still know that front door squeak
Every Friday on the week
The same green hymnal in my hand
Mama’s ring on the pew’s red stain
Pastor’s voice through the wooden beams
Calling names like holy steam
I learned to fold myself in half
And call that faith

But something in me stayed awake
While the ceiling swam with gold and gray
I kept my tears where they could hide
And let the choir carry on inside

I’m slipping out the back pew
(Back pew)
I’m loving you by letting go
(letting go)
I won’t throw stones at your door
I just can’t wear this hurt no more
Back pew, goodbye
(back pew, goodbye)

I left my shoes by the old coat rack
Past the bulletin and the cracked hymnback
Didn’t make a scene, didn’t slam a word
Just walked like someone finally heard
There’s a kind of grief that sits real still
Like a glass of water on the sill
You can bless the room and bless the leave
And still believe

I kept my hands in my lap
Kept my questions under wraps
But love got bigger than the walls
And softer than the altar calls

I’m slipping out the back pew
(Back pew)
I’m loving you by letting go
(letting go)
I won’t throw stones at your door
I just can’t wear this hurt no more
Back pew, goodbye
(back pew, goodbye)

If you see me, don’t look down
I’m still from this sacred town
I still know every name you knew
I’m just walking toward the truth

I’m slipping out the back pew
(Back pew)
I’m loving you by letting go
(letting go)
I won’t throw stones at your door
I just can’t wear this hurt no more
Back pew, goodbye

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights blur to yellow lines
Another lonely evening finds
Me on this familiar road
A ghost of times I used to know

My old place just ahead
Words unsaid

Midnight apartment
Still glowing soft and low
Midnight apartment
Where did all our feelings go

I slow the car a little bit
Just a glance a quick admit
To memories I try to fight
Flickering in the fading light

Same old window same old pane
Washing over me again
A quiet ache a gentle sting
What new songs does that

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I turn the corner slow
Past the grocery with the dented sign
Your old window's still there
Same screen, same spill of pale porch light
The number on the wall
Looks smaller than it did to me
I keep both hands on the wheel
Like the road might speak for me

I see your curtain move
Or maybe it's the wind
The whole block holds its breath
And lets the memory in

Old apartment, stay a while
Old apartment, let me smile
I drove by, I lost my nerve
Same old steps, same little curve
Old apartment, stay a while
Old apartment, let me smile

There’s a chair in the hall
Where your plant used to lean in light
A bike chain by the curb
And the bakery closing tight
I hear your laugh on glass
In the rain that taps the roof
I should keep on driving past
But I’m parked inside the truth

I see your curtain move
Or maybe it's the wind
The whole block holds its breath
And lets the memory in

Old apartment, stay a while
Old apartment, let me smile
I drove by, I lost my nerve
Same old steps, same little curve
Old apartment, stay a while
Old apartment, let me smile

If I called, would it ring
Through the rooms we split apart?
Would you know it’s just me
With a road map in my heart?
So I let the red tail glow
Fade down the avenue
And I leave that little door
To the version of us we knew

Old apartment, stay a while
Old apartment, let me smile
I drove by, I lost my nerve
Same old steps, same little curve
Old apartment, stay a while
Old apartment, let me smile

Drive on, drive on
I can wave and let you go
Drive on, drive on
