songs.subject: a soul song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning
and I’m still at this desk
Book open like a door
I can’t quite rest
Highlighter on the page
blue ink on my hand
I read the same sentence
and still don’t understand

My eyes get heavy
but I don’t quit
One more line
one more bit
If I stay with it
maybe I’ll see
What all these quiet hours
are doing to me

I’m learning on my own
(I’m learning on my own)
Two in the morning
and I’m holding on
I’m learning on my own
(I’m learning on my own)
One page at a time
until the dark is gone

Cup gone cold beside me
phone face-down and mute
The room is full of stillness
and a stack of truth
Scratch marks in the margin
a wrinkle in my brow
I’m chasing every answer
that I need right now

My eyes get heavy
but I don’t quit
One more line
one more bit
If I stay with it
maybe I’ll see
What all these quiet hours
are doing to me

I’m learning on my own
(I’m learning on my own)
Two in the morning
and I’m holding on
I’m learning on my own
(I’m learning on my own)
One page at a time
until the dark is gone

I don’t need a crowd
to prove I’m here
Just this chair
and a little fear
If I fall asleep
wake me up slow
I’ve got a future
I’m trying to know

I’m learning on my own
(I’m learning on my own)
Two in the morning
and I’m holding on
I’m learning on my own
(I’m learning on my own)
One page at a time
until the dark is gone

songs.subject: a soul song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning
And the book still open
Lamp light on your sleeve
Ink stains on your fingers
You read the same line twice
Try to make it stay

My mind keeps drifting
But I pull it back
One more page
One more line
Till the quiet turns to gold

I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still turning
(Page by page)
I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still earning
A little light tonight

Half-finished coffee
Cold on the table
A yellow legal pad
Full of crooked arrows
You don't need a crowd
To become the answer

My heart keeps asking
If I'll ever be enough
One more page
One more line
Till the fear goes soft and slow

I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still turning
(Page by page)
I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still earning
A little light tonight

When the room goes quiet
I can hear my name
Not the one they gave me
The one I'm making
So I breathe in deep
And I write it down

I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still turning
(Page by page)
I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still earning
A little light tonight

Two in the morning
And I keep on going
One more page
One more line
songs.lyrics_en:
Two in the morning
And the book still open
Lamp light on your sleeve
Ink stains on your fingers
You read the same line twice
Try to make it stay

My mind keeps drifting
But I pull it back
One more page
One more line
Till the quiet turns to gold

I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still turning
(Page by page)
I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still earning
A little light tonight

Half-finished coffee
Cold on the table
A yellow legal pad
Full of crooked arrows
You don't need a crowd
To become the answer

My heart keeps asking
If I'll ever be enough
One more page
One more line
Till the fear goes soft and slow

I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still turning
(Page by page)
I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still earning
A little light tonight

When the room goes quiet
I can hear my name
Not the one they gave me
The one I'm making
So I breathe in deep
And I write it down

I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still turning
(Page by page)
I'm still here, still learning
Still here, still earning
A little light tonight

Two in the morning
And I keep on going
One more page
One more line

songs.subject: a disco song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up at dawn
With crates of velvet coal
A signed black seal on one
And a hole in every hold
The captain kissed the chain
Then crossed himself and smiled
I saw the label move
Like a thing alive

Set sail, set sail
Past the foam
The moon kept pace
Like it knew our name

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
It follows us down
It won't let go
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
Dance for the deck
Then bury the cold

Night one, the nets were empty
Night two, the ropes went slack
By night three, every mirror
Showed a second face in black
The cook said, "Don't look in there"
Then dropped his tray and ran
But the crates kept tapping time
Like a brass-band hand

Set sail, set sail
Past the foam
The moon kept pace
Like it knew our name

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
It follows us down
It won't let go
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
Dance for the deck
Then bury the cold

We cut the locks at midnight
And the boards began to sing
Out poured a cloud of wedding lace
And a ring of rusted kings
One by one, the men fell laughing
To the planks in spinning lines
I grabbed the wheel and shouted
"Keep moving, keep the line"

Hey
Hey
The sea wants more
Hey
Hey
Keep your feet on the floor

Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
It follows us down
It won't let go
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
Dance for the deck
Then bury the cold
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
We’re still afloat
But nobody knows
Salt in the hold
Salt in the hold
Turn that fear
Into a glow

songs.subject: a disco song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Space dust thick on my jumpsuit
Another week another moon
The stars they blink a silent truth
This cargo humming soon

Feel it deep inside my bones
A weird vibration on the ship

Cosmic cargo taking hold
Yeah it's a funky kind of cold
Cosmic cargo lost control
Gonna rock your very soul

The captain says it's just a hum
A little engine tremor
But I hear whispers in the drum
A silent space remember

What's in the crate no one knows
Just a tag that says unknown
But every night the feeling grows
We ain't ever going home

Cosmic cargo taking hold
Yeah it's a funky kind of cold
Cosmic cargo lost control

songs.subject: a R&B song about two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You stepped in late
I held my place
Same hard stare
Same sharp face
The floor went still
When they called our name
Now we got one chance
In one small frame

Your hand on my waist
Like a dare, like a test
I can feel your pulse
Tryin' to outrun the rest
We move too close
For all that pride
One step in sync
You and I collide

Dance with me
Dance with me
If you win, I win
When we move, it bleeds
Dance with me
Dance with me
Two rivals in time
But we look too clean

Your heel cut in
My shoulder turned
Every room we crossed
Every eye got burned
You tried to lead
I pulled you back
That little smirk
Made my focus crack

Your hand on my waist
Like a dare, like a test
I can feel your pulse
Tryin' to outrun the rest
We move too close
For all that pride
One step in sync
You and I collide

Dance with me
Dance with me
If you win, I win
When we move, it bleeds
Dance with me
Dance with me
Two rivals in time
But we look too clean

Tell me why
It feels like truth
When every move
Still sounds like you
You spin me once
I don't pull away
Maybe on this floor
We both have to stay

Dance with me
Dance with me
If you win, I win
When we move, it bleeds
Dance with me
Dance with me
Two rivals in time
But we look too clean

songs.subject: a trap song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Dirt road dust on my boots
Same four blocks, same bad news
Mama said, "Boy, stay kind"
But the sign said, "Not this time"
I know every rusted gate
Every lie they like to say
Every face that wants me small
I got a plan to break that wall

I'm done with the same old talk
Done with the same old looks
I got a bag and a map
And a chip where the ceiling stood
One more night, then I'm gone
One more mile, then I'm free
If they ask where I went
Tell 'em don't look for me

I'm leaving for good
I'm leaving for good
No back road, no hood
I'm leaving for good
Burn that frame of wood
Burn that frame of wood
I said I would
I'm leaving for good

My name's a joke in the diner booth
My dreams get smaller in a small-town room
They keep score like I owe them time
But I don't fit in that "one day" line
I packed my chains and a change of clothes
Left the trophy and the summer glow
Left my old truck in the driveway dust
If I look back now, I turn to rust

I'm done with the same old talk
Done with the same old looks
I got a bag and a map
And a chip where the ceiling stood
One more night, then I'm gone
One more mile, then I'm free
If they ask where I went
Tell 'em don't look for me

I'm leaving for good
I'm leaving for good
No back road, no hood
I'm leaving for good
Burn that frame of wood
Burn that frame of wood
I said I would
I'm leaving for good

If the porch light waits
It can wait alone
I'm not that kid
I'm a road of my own
Tell the whole damn town
I don't belong in place
I was made for miles
Not for this cage

I'm leaving for good
I'm leaving for good
No back road, no hood
I'm leaving for good
Burn that frame of wood
Burn that frame of wood
I said I would
I'm leaving for good

songs.subject: a trap song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
One bus out, one bag packed
Mama on the porch, eyes glass-black
Same red dirt, same dead end
Same old boys talkin’ where I been
I know this road like a scar line
County fair gone, just a hard sign
Gas station clerk know my face too well
I need more than this little same-same hell

I been countin’ cracks in the screen door
I been hearin’ that town say “stay more”
But the map got a line I can cross now
And I’m done lettin’ fear make my route

I’m leavin’ for good
I’m leavin’ for good
Watch me go, watch me go
I’m leavin’ for good
No more backroads
No more “maybe”
I’m gone by dawn
You won’t chain me
I’m leavin’ for good
I’m leavin’ for good

Old friends call, say “don’t get big now”
Like a dream’s a crime if it lives out loud
I kept my boots by the front step
While my real life waited in a train wreck
I want glass towers and a cheap motel
New names, new noise, new stories to tell
If I fall, let me fall somewhere new
Not on the same curb I outgrew

I been countin’ cracks in the screen door
I been hearin’ that town say “stay more”
But the map got a line I can cross now
And I’m done lettin’ fear make my route

I’m leavin’ for good
I’m leavin’ for good
Watch me go, watch me go
I’m leavin’ for good
No more backroads
No more “maybe”
I’m gone by dawn
You won’t chain me
I’m leavin’ for good
I’m leavin’ for good

If they ask, tell ’em I was ready
Tell ’em I was tired of the same old levy
Same old weight, same old room
Same old sky that never moved
I ain’t angry
I just outgrew it
Keep your stories
I’m gon’ do this

I’m leavin’ for good
I’m leavin’ for good
Watch me go, watch me go
I’m leavin’ for good
No more backroads
No more “maybe”
I’m gone by dawn
You won’t chain me

songs.subject: a folk song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The dust motes dance in the window light
Just like they did back then
Funny how some things stay tight
While others just fray at the end

The phone felt heavy
A stone in my hand
A stranger’s number
Almost
A ghost of a memory
Across the land
Or maybe a forgotten post

Oh
The long silence
The long silence
Years folded
Like old letters
The long silence
The long silence
What does a lost child tell her betters?

I pictured the kitchen
The chipped blue cup
The way she’d hum a tune
My own voice caught
Trying to fill it up
Beneath a pale
Uncertain moon

A breath
A click
Then a sound so thin
Like autumn leaves that fall
“Hello?” she said
Where do I begin?
After no call at all

Oh
The long silence
The long silence
Years folded
Like old letters
The long silence
The long silence
What does a lost child tell her betters?

songs.subject: a folk song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I saved your number
on a scrap of blue
in the glovebox drawer
under church shoes
Rain on the windshield
gas gauge low
I stared at the phone
like it knew where to go

Years like dust
on the line
My thumb shook once
then I hit nine

Mama, are you there?
(are you there?)
Mama, can you hear?
(can you hear?)
I kept the door shut
for so many years
Mama, are you there?
(are you there?)

Your voice came small
like a kettle song
You said, "Baby, I knew
you'd call along"
I laughed, then cried
at the same old joke
You asked if I was fed
if I still smoked

I said, "I'm here
I'm all right"
And the old hurt loosened
in the night

Mama, are you there?
(are you there?)
Mama, can you hear?
(can you hear?)
I kept the door shut
for so many years
Mama, are you there?
(are you there?)

You said my name
like it still fit
Like broken things
can learn to sit
I told the truth
for the first time home
About the years I ran
and the bones I wore alone

Mama, are you there?
(are you there?)
Mama, can you hear?
(can you hear?)
I kept the door shut
for so many years
Mama, are you there?
(are you there?)

The moon stayed low
on the kitchen tile
And I held the phone
a little while
Mama, I'm here

songs.subject: a trap song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
You held the handle first
Like it was yours to keep
I was half a step behind
Getting soaked in my sleep
Black shoes, paper bag
Maples on your sleeve
You said, “move in a little”
I said, “yeah, let me breathe”
Your laugh hit soft
Through the rain on the roof
We were two locked eyes
In a one-off truth
I watched your fingers slide
Down the chipped blue grip
Then you let me share
That one little slip

Now we’re shoulder to shoulder
On a broken gray block
Too close to pretend
Too cool to talk
Your coat on my arm
My heart on parole
Every drop that missed us
Hit somewhere I know

One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, too close
One umbrella, stay there
Don’t let this moment go
One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, one road
You and me in the rain
Hold it low, hold it close

Bus hissed by slow
We both laughed at the splash
You said, “this your stop?”
I said, “maybe not that fast”
Your name tasted new
On the tip of my tongue
Like a song I knew
Before it was sung
You had a cracked phone screen
And a grocery store bag
I had wet cuff sleeves
And a paper-thin plan
We passed the corner store
Where the old men stare
Like they knew we’d be
Something almost there

Now the storm talks loud
But you talk low
And every little step
Makes the next one slow
Your elbow on mine
Like a dare, like a sign
If the sky keeps falling
I don’t mind the line

One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, too close
One umbrella, stay there
Don’t let this moment go
One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, one road
You and me in the rain
Hold it low, hold it close

Maybe we split at the curb
Maybe we don’t even try
Maybe this is the kind of thing
That lives and passes by
But your hand found mine
When the wind cut through
And for one more block
I was walking with you

One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, too close
One umbrella, stay there
Don’t let this moment go
One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, one road
You and me in the rain
Hold it low, hold it close

One umbrella
One little glow
Two strangers
Not strangers, though
One umbrella
And the rain came slow

songs.subject: a trap song about two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
You on my side of the curb
I was just trying to wait
You said, “Move a little over”
I said, “It’s a crazy day”
Your sleeve got my elbow
Your bag hit my knee
We laughed at the sky
Like it owed us some peace

Drop by drop
We both looked up
One small roof
Not enough
Your hand on the handle
Mine on the frame
Somebody said, “Go”
But we stayed

One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, no names
One umbrella, still strangers
But we stand through the rain
One umbrella, two strangers
Close enough to feel the same
One umbrella, no promises
Just the storm and your shade

Your shoes got muddy
Mine got worse
We took the long block
Just to beat the curse
You said your stop was near
I said mine too
Neither one of us
Believed it was true
Your laugh got quiet
When the thunder came
I kept my grip steady
You did the same
A bus pulled past us
Like it knew our luck
Then you looked my way
And the silence struck

Drop by drop
We both looked up
One small roof
Not enough
Your hand on the handle
Mine on the frame
Somebody said, “Go”
But we stayed

One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, no names
One umbrella, still strangers
But we stand through the rain
One umbrella, two strangers
Close enough to feel the same
One umbrella, no promises
Just the storm and your shade

At the corner light
You stepped back first
I almost said your name
But the moment hurt
You gave that half-smile
Then turned away
I kept one hand raised
Till you faded away

One umbrella, two strangers
One umbrella, no names
One umbrella, still strangers
But we stand through the rain
One umbrella, two strangers
Close enough to feel the same
One umbrella, no promises

songs.subject: a reggae song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama laid out lace
On the blue chair by the door
Papa shook his head
Like he’d seen this scene before
They picked a polished path
For a life I never chose
But my name was on my tongue
And it started as I rose

I looked down that road
Saw the same old fence
Same old grin
Same old sense
My heart said, run
My feet said, go
I was not born
For their perfect show

I’m leaving that road
I’m leaving that road
(Leaving that road)
I’m choosing my own
I’m choosing my own
(Choosing my own)
No hand on my life
No chain on my soul
I’m leaving that road
I’m leaving that road

She said, “Wear the ring
Keep the house by the hill”
I said, “I need a sky
That can hold my will”
There was sugar in the talk
But the taste went dry
So I packed my songs
And I watched the morning rise

I heard the neighbors pray
For the plan to stay
But I felt the tide
Pulling me away
My heart said, run
My feet said, go
I was not born
For their perfect show

I’m leaving that road
I’m leaving that road
(Leaving that road)
I’m choosing my own
I’m choosing my own
(Choosing my own)
No hand on my life
No chain on my soul
I’m leaving that road
I’m leaving that road

If love is a cage
Then I’ll miss the key
If home is a promise
Let it come to me
I won’t trade my breath
For a borrowed dream
I’m walking out
So I can finally be me

I’m leaving that road
I’m leaving that road
(Leaving that road)
I’m choosing my own
I’m choosing my own
(Choosing my own)
No hand on my life
No chain on my soul
I’m leaving that road
I’m leaving that road

Bye to the papers
Bye to the plan
I’m still here
With my own two hands
(With my own two hands)
I’m leaving that road

songs.subject: a reggae song about leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama picked the fabric
Father chose the date
Silver cups on the table
I smiled, but I was late
Your name sat in my pocket
Like a stone I couldn't hold
I wore that future nicely
But it never fit my soul

I heard the sea call my name
Past the gate and the palm trees
Heart like a runaway train
I was born to leave clean

I’m walking out
I’m walking out tonight
I’m leaving that road
I’m choosing my own life
I’m walking out
I’m walking out tonight
No paper ring can hold me
No matter how it shines

They said, "be grateful, darling"
"Good homes don't come twice"
But every smile was borrowed
And every vow was ice
I packed one dress and courage
Left the porch in fading blue
If love is made for saving
Then I’m saving me from you

I heard the sea call my name
Past the gate and the palm trees
Heart like a runaway train
I was born to leave clean

I’m walking out
I’m walking out tonight
I’m leaving that road
I’m choosing my own life
I’m walking out
I’m walking out tonight
No paper ring can hold me
No matter how it shines

Let the elders keep their stories
Let the flowers dry and fall
I won't trade my whole tomorrow
For a borrowed wedding shawl

I’m walking out
I’m walking out tonight
I’m leaving that road
I’m choosing my own life
I’m walking out
I’m walking out tonight
No paper ring can hold me

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
You slid me the keys
At the edge of the lot
Your hands all shook
Mine tried not to
Motel glass in the dash
Fried egg on the hood
You said, “One clean run”
I said, “Understood”

I saw the tremble
In your jawline
A last little favor
With a hard deadline
And the moon in the mirror
Kept fading away
Like it knew my name
Was gonna change today

Last job, last job
Run till it burns out
Last job, last job
Don’t look back now
Last job, last job
I keep the roads smooth
Last job, last job
But I can’t save you

I can’t save you

We took the canal road
Past the shuttered bins
Your phone went dead
Like a match in the wind
No sirens yet
Just your breath and the glass
And the orange glow
Of the place we passed
You stared at the water
Like it owed you money
Said, “After tonight
There won’t be any of me”

I wanted to ask
If you meant me too
But the words stayed folded
Like a loan in the blue
So I kept my hands
At a steady place
And the whole front seat
Felt like a borrowed space

Last job, last job
Run till it burns out
Last job, last job
Don’t look back now
Last job, last job
I keep the roads smooth
Last job, last job
But I can’t save you

At the quarry turn
You touched my sleeve
Said, “If this goes wrong
Just let me leave”
Then you laughed once
Like it hurt to do
And I drove on through
The dark and the dew

Last job, last job
Run till it burns out
Last job, last job
Don’t look back now
Last job, last job
I keep the roads smooth
Last job, last job
But I can’t save you

By dawn, you were gone
And the seat was cold
I still see your face
In the cracked-up gold
Last job, last job
Soft as rain on chrome
Last job, last job
Then I drove alone

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Keys in my palm
Cold as a coin
Your shoes hit the curb
And I don't ask why
Paper cup coffee
Half gone, gone bitter
You said, "Just one more run"
Like it's nothing at all

I watched your hand shake
Over the latch
One last promise
Then no look back

One last run
One last run
Salt on the wheel
And your name in my lungs
One last run
One last run
I took you home
But I never came back

The radio hissed
Through the county line
Your laugh in the dark
Like a dime dropped down
Rearview full of rain
And a red porch glow
You said, "If I go under
Don't let it show"

I kept it steady
At every bend
You held the bag like
It was a dead friend's hand

One last run
One last run
Salt on the wheel
And your name in my lungs
One last run
One last run
I took you home
But I never came back

At the washout bridge
You opened the door
Said, "Drop me here
Don't drive no more"
I saw the dawn
On your split lip grin
Then the road went blank
And the night leaked in

One last run
One last run
Salt on the wheel
And your name in my lungs
One last run
One last run
I took you home
But I never came back
One last run
One last run
If they ask my route

songs.subject: a jazz song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Grandma’s hands were maps
Palm lines like old roads
Flour on her knuckles
Blue veins in the cold
She folded Sunday sleeves
Pinned my tie straight too
Those fingers knew my name
Before I learned to

They held a bowl of peaches
Held a cough all night
Held the whole back porch
In that morning light

Those hands, those hands
Healed me slow
Those hands, those hands
Never let go
Grandma’s hands
Still guide me home
(Those hands)
Still guide me home

They shelled green peas
By the screen door hum
Taught me how to sting
And still stay kind to some
A dented ring of gold
A scar from years ago
She’d laugh and say, “Baby
That’s just where the work has grown”

They ironed out the worry
Lifted up the weak
Put a kiss on every bruise
And a coin in my cheek

Those hands, those hands
Healed me slow
Those hands, those hands
Never let go
Grandma’s hands
Still guide me home
(Those hands)
Still guide me home

Now the table’s quiet
But I can still see
Her thumbs in the biscuit dough
Swaying next to me
If I lose my way
I close my eyes and know
A prayer can live in fingers
And never let you go

Those hands, those hands
Healed me slow
Those hands, those hands
Never let go
Grandma’s hands
Still guide me home
(Those hands)
Still guide me home

I close my eyes and know

songs.subject: a jazz song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
My grandma's hands
Got linen lines
From Sunday dough
And garden vines
She'd fold my shirt
With that careful grace
A map of years
Across her face

They knew the weight
Of every plate
They knew the shake
Before the ache

Thimble fingers, hold me still
Thimble fingers, heal me still
In your hands, I learn to stay
Thimble fingers, lead the way

Rose jam stains
On the left thumb nail
A burn scar white
From the old stove rail
She'd shell green peas
At the chipped blue sink
And hum low notes
I still can think

They knew my name
Before I spoke
They patched the rent
They split the yoke

Thimble fingers, hold me still
Thimble fingers, heal me still
In your hands, I learn to stay
Thimble fingers, lead the way

Now when I kneel
At the worn front step
I kiss the hands
That the years have kept
Not gold, not lace
Just steady and true
A whole small world
Wrapped up in you

Thimble fingers, hold me still
Thimble fingers, heal me still
In your hands, I learn to stay
Thimble fingers, lead the way

If I lose my way
In a hard new day
I'll follow the curve

songs.subject: a trap song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
First chair in the morning
He know your mama too
Three towns in the mirror
And the stories running through
Line up like a courtroom
Every head got a case
He don’t ask for much
Just a name and a face

Door swings, people talk
He just nod and fold that cape
Every secret in the clippers
Every rumor got a place

He know the whole town
He know the whole town
Fade so clean, ears on sound
He know the whole town
(whole town)
He know the whole town
Got your business in his ground

Old truck, fresh boots
Baby seat in the back
Sunday suits and Friday fades
He heard it all like that
Who’s late on rent
Who left, who came back home
Who’s fronting in the parking lot
Who’s broke and never known

Door swings, people talk
He just laughs and sharpens steel
Every little lie you wear
He can tell what’s real

He know the whole town
He know the whole town
Fade so clean, ears on sound
He know the whole town
(whole town)
He know the whole town
Got your business in his ground

Scissors flash, chair spins
Names move soft through the shop
Kids grown, daddies gone
But the questions never stop
He keep it in the rafters
He keep it in the comb
If this town had a memory
It would sound like his home

He know the whole town
He know the whole town
Fade so clean, ears on sound
He know the whole town
(whole town)
He know the whole town
Got your business in his ground

songs.subject: a trap song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
He open up at nine
Fade cape on my lap
Old heads in the chair
Young boys by the back
He know who got work
He know who got church
He know who got locked
He know who got worse
Four chairs full, he move
Clipper hand like art
Every line he draw
Cut straight through the dark
If you come in quiet
He can read your face
By the time you leave
Whole town know your pace

He ain't just lining up
He lining up the facts
Tighten that edge on you
Then he hand it back
Baby on the braids
Brothers on the news
If the barber talk
There ain't much you can do

He know the whole town (whole town)
He know the whole town (whole town)
One cut, one look
And he know the whole town
He know the whole town (whole town)
He know the whole town (whole town)
Step in his chair
And your name come around

Auntie in the hood
Get the latest first
Who moved on who
And who done did worse
Friday after work
Line out the door
Every laugh in there
Got a little bit more
He don't need a phone
He got it in his head
Who gon' act cold
Who gon' end up red
Little kids peep
Tryna hear the lore
But he keep it smooth
Like the vinyl on the floor

He ain't just lining up
He lining up the facts
Tighten that edge on you
Then he hand it back
Baby on the braids
Brothers on the news
If the barber talk
There ain't much you can do

He know the whole town (whole town)
He know the whole town (whole town)
One cut, one look
And he know the whole town
He know the whole town (whole town)
He know the whole town (whole town)
Step in his chair
And your name come around

Call him the map man
Call him the source
Every faded head
Got a history course
Door swings open
And the room lean in
'Cause the man with the clippers
Know where trouble been

He know the whole town (whole town)
He know the whole town (whole town)
One cut, one look
And he know the whole town
He know the whole town (whole town)
He know the whole town (whole town)
Step in his chair

songs.subject: a gothic country song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded black salt at dawn
In a dockside churchyard rain
Crates marked with a red wax seal
And a name we won't say plain
The captain kissed his thumb and crossed
Then spat into the foam
He said, "If that box starts breathing, son
Don't bring it back home"

Seven days out
The gulls went mute
The moon hung low
Like a cut tin flute
And every night
That hold board groaned
Like something lost
Was clawing bone by bone

Black salt cargo
Pull me down
Black salt cargo
Sink this town
Hold on tight
When the tide turns mean
Black salt cargo
Don't you dream

First mate saw a woman in cloth
At the end of the chainlight beam
Hair like kelp around her face
Hands full of river reeds
She tapped one crate, then touched her throat
And laughed with a broken sound
By morning three men had gone pale
And not one stayed around

Seven bells struck
Then one struck twice
The horses on deck
Wouldn't eat at night
And every storm
That found our wake
Seemed hired hard
To keep that box awake

Black salt cargo
Pull me down
Black salt cargo
Sink this town
Hold on tight
When the tide turns mean
Black salt cargo
Don't you dream

I pried the lid with a rusted nail
And saw old mud and nails
A wedding ring, a baby shoe
And a pocket full of scales
Then the sea rose up in the lantern glass
And spoke my name like debt
Said, "You carried it from the dead man's dock
You ain't paid it yet"

Black salt cargo
Pull me down
Black salt cargo
Sink this town
Hold on tight
When the tide turns mean
Black salt cargo
Don't you dream

Now the harbor bells sound far away
But I smell brine in my hair
If you hear three knocks in the hull tonight
Leave that cargo there

songs.subject: a gothic country song about a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
From the docks of ol' Charleston,
To the grey North Sea.
We set sail with a cargo,
Just the captain and me.
Wooden crates, stacked high,
Smelled of salt and of dread.

The manifest read 'iron ore,'
But the compass spun wild.
Every man felt a chill,
Like a mother losing her child.
No moon on the water,
Just a fog, thick and cold.

Iron coffins in the hold,
Dragging us down, down, down.
A whisper on the wind,
No port in this cursed town.
Iron coffins, a heavy toll,
For every soul we've sold.

First mate, he saw shadows,
Dancing on the mast.
He swore he heard singing,
From a forgotten past.
He jumped overboard screamin',
And the waves swallowed him whole.

The rats left the vessel,
A week out from shore.
Smartest creatures on board,
They knew what was in store.
The planks began groaning,
A deep, mournful sound.

Iron coffins in the hold,
Dragging us down, down, down.
A whisper on the wind,
No port in this cursed town.
Iron coffins, a heavy toll,

songs.subject: a hardcore song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Steel in my teeth
Dust in my hair
I came back broke
But I’m still here
A box full of medals
A hole in my chest
Your front door squeaks
Like it knows the rest

Hands on the frame
I’m shaking hard
One step home
Feels twice as far

I’m home, I’m home
Breathe me in now
I’m home, I’m home
Don’t let go now
(Coming home)
From the war

Mailbox bent low
Dog barked first
Mama on the steps
Like she’d held her breath
I dropped that pack
By the kitchen sink
Learned real quick
Not to speak, just think

Fist to my mouth
Eyes on the floor
Out there I was gone
In here I’m more

I’m home, I’m home
Breathe me in now
I’m home, I’m home
Don’t let go now
(Coming home)
From the war

No more sirens
No more sand
Just your warm hand
And my ruined hands
Let me be weak
Let me be small
I lost some years
I kept my soul

Home!
Home!
I made it back
I made it back
Home!
Home!
I’m not the same
But I’m alive

I’m home, I’m home
Breathe me in now
I’m home, I’m home
Don’t let go now
(Coming home)
From the war
I’m home, I’m home
Here on your floor
(Coming home)
From the war

songs.lyrics:
Dirt road dust
Engine coughs a final bust
Empty house cold and still
Another day another pill

Memories flash
A sudden crash

Homecoming queen
Where have you been
Nowhere to go
Nowhere to grow

Shadows dance on the wall
Hear a whisper hear a call
Just the wind through broken pane
Driving me insane

Homecoming queen
Where have you been
Nowhere to go
Nowhere to grow

They said be strong

songs.subject: a neo-soul song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Your coat's still on the chair
Mine's on the floor by the lamp
The apartment holds its breath
Like it knows we came in heavy
I hear your keys by the bowl
I see my ring in the sink
Your eyes said more than your mouth
And I said the worst thing

Now the sink is running
But nobody's fixing
What got split in two
We stand in the kitchen
Like two full storms
Afraid to move

This quiet after us
It cuts so deep
This quiet after us
Still has your shape on me
Say my name soft
Don't let me go
This quiet after us
Is where we both still know

You lean against the fridge
I press my back to the wall
That little crack in the glass
Looks like a map of it all
Your phone screen lights up blue
Then dies in your palm
We both want to laugh
But we're too proud, too calm

Now the sink is running
But nobody's fixing
What got split in two
We stand in the kitchen
Like two full storms
Afraid to move

This quiet after us
It cuts so deep
This quiet after us
Still has your shape on me
Say my name soft
Don't let me go
This quiet after us
Is where we both still know

Maybe love is this
The pause before the hands come back
Maybe healing starts
In the space we never planned
You touch my sleeve
I let it stay
No grand speech, just a breath
And the night gives way

This quiet after us
It cuts so deep
This quiet after us
Still has your shape on me
Say my name soft
Don't let me go
This quiet after us
Is where we both still know

The door stays closed
The room stays warm
We don't have to win
To not be torn

songs.lyrics:
The door it just slammed
A sound so loud
My heart it just jumped
Into a cloud

Silence now
Heavy and deep

This quiet it screams
Louder than words
This quiet it bleeds
All of my nerves
This quiet it breaks
My fragile heart
This quiet it aches
Tearing apart

Your jacket still there
On the old chair
A ghost in the room
A breath in the air

I trace the lines

songs.subject: a dream pop song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I let the chapel go
Left my coat on the pew
Your candle burned low
While I walked out on you
My hands were empty
My tongue was dry
I called your name once
And heard no reply

Still, in the dark
I kept a little spark
Under my ribs
Like a small white mark

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Salt in the glass
And a tear on my face
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then I heard you
Call me back by name

I slept in the field
With the frost on my shoes
Counted my doubts
Like broken-down pews
The sky stayed closed
The road stayed long
I thought you were gone
I thought I was wrong

Still, in the dark
I kept a little spark
Under my ribs
Like a small white mark

I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Salt in the glass
And a tear on my face
I lost my faith
I lost my faith
Then I heard you
Call me back by name

And it came like rain
On a dry white stone
Not a blazing sign
Just a sound at home
In my shaking hands
In my beating chest
You were never far
You were just 숨?

songs.subject: a dream pop song about losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I lost it in the hallway
Where the coat rack leaned too hard
A prayer fell off my tongue
Like a coin into the yard
I wore my doubt like denim
Frayed at every seam
Your name went dim
In the salt of a dream

But the dark kept moving
And I kept moving too
Through the rain on the window
Through the bruise of the blue

I was gone, I was gone
Then you called me home
I was lost in the fog
Now I’m not alone
I was gone, I was gone
Then you called me home
(You called me home)
I can breathe again

I found you in a quiet room
In a face I almost missed
In the cracked edge of morning
In a hand around my wrist
Not a flame, not thunder
Just a steady little light
Like a porch lamp waiting
At the end of my night

And the walls came gentle
And the air went warm
Like a small green branch
After the longest storm

I was gone, I was gone
Then you called me home
I was lost in the fog
Now I’m not alone
I was gone, I was gone
Then you called me home
(You called me home)
I can breathe again

If faith is a river
I crossed it bone dry
If hope is a window
You were the sky
I came back empty
You met me there
With a quiet yes
In the broken air

I was gone, I was gone
Then you called me home
I was lost in the fog
Now I’m not alone
I was gone, I was gone
Then you called me home
(You called me home)

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
One gas station sign
Two cracked curb lines
I learned your silence
By the shape of time
My name on the mailbox
Paint peeling thin
Same old porch swing
Same old “where you been”

But I kept my shoes by the door
Kept my plans in the floor
Every map I ever drew
Pointed past this view

I’m gone for good
Gone for good
Say my name once
Then let it go
I’m gone for good
Gone for good
Small-town dust
Won’t follow me home

Friday at the diner
Fry oil in my hair
Half the room knew me
Like I’d always be there
But my backpack’s heavy
With a future in it
I’m not mad, I’m moving
I’m just done with this

Every road out of here
Looked the same to me
Till I hit that line
And it opened clean
Now the fields stay behind
Like a story told
I was born in that circle
Now I’m breaking out the mold

I’m gone for good
Gone for good
Say my name once
Then let it go
I’m gone for good
Gone for good
Small-town dust
Won’t follow me home

If you hear my radio
Echo through the rain
That just means I made it
Past the county line
No hard goodbye
Just a softer flame
I loved this little town
I outgrew its frame

I’m gone for good
Gone for good
Say my name once
Then let it go
I’m gone for good
Gone for good
Small-town dust
Won’t follow me home

songs.lyrics:
Sunrise creepin', paintin' the sky
Same old brick, every day gone by
Dust on the window, same old view
Time to dip, somethin' shiny and new

Engine hummin' low, ready to roll
Gotta chase that feelin', deep in my soul

Small town dust, gonna shake it off me
Big city lights, where I'm meant to be
No lookin' back, nah, that ain't the play
New horizon, startin' today

Main Street memories, kinda hazy now
Empty pockets, but I'll make it somehow
Folks talkin' slow, world spinnin' fast
This small town vibe, ain't built to last

What's out there, beyond the county line?
A fresh beat droppin', somethin' truly mine?
Got a ticket to nowhere, a map in my head
Leave the quiet, wake up the dead

Small town dust, gonna shake it off me

songs.subject: a punk song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
I push that screen door
And it hits me first
Onions in the pan
And a kettle curse
Flour on the counter
Grease in the air
Your apron on the chair
Like you were still there

Black pepper, butter
Soap and old wood
One deep breath
And I feel good

Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
It bites my eyes
It pulls me in
Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
Burnt toast smoke
And cardamom

Steam on the window
Mason jars lined up
Red beans in a pot
And a chipped blue cup
Your hands moving fast
Like you had time to spare
Every hard day vanished
In that thick warm air

Garlic on the edge
Cast iron sings
I still taste home
In broken things

Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
It bites my eyes
It pulls me in
Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
Burnt toast smoke
And cardamom

Now the house is quiet
But the air remembers
Honey, onion, coffee
And hot December
I was a bad kid
All knees and pride
But one breath of that room
And I come inside

Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
It bites my eyes
It pulls me in
Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
Burnt toast smoke
And cardamom
Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
I hear your laugh
In every tin

Your hands moving fast
Like you had time to spare
Every hard day vanished
In that thick warm air

Garlic on the edge
Cast iron sings
I still taste home
In broken things

Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
It bites my eyes
It pulls me in
Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
Burnt toast smoke
And cardamom

Now the house is quiet
But the air remembers
Honey, onion, coffee
And hot December
I was a bad kid
All knees and pride
But one breath of that room
And I come inside

Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
It bites my eyes
It pulls me in
Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
Burnt toast smoke
And cardamom
Grandma's kitchen, grandma's kitchen
(grandma's kitchen)
I hear your laugh
In every tin

songs.subject: a punk song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Old linoleum floor
Grease stains on the wall
Frying pan still smoking
From breakfast oh so small

Grandma's kitchen stink
Yeah grandma's kitchen stink
Burns your nose hairs out
Makes your stomach shrink

That weird spice rack
Dusty ancient jars
Smells like old socks and
Mothballs under the stars

Grandma's kitchen stink
Yeah grandma's kitchen stink
Burns your nose hairs out
Makes your stomach shrink

What's that bubbling pot
Something unidentifiable

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
He lives above the laundromat
With chalk on every sleeve
At dawn he pins the hems right up
And pockets what you need
Mrs. Bell in emerald plaid
The twins in borrowed blue
He knows your cuffs, your dates, your moods
He knows what fits you true

Snap goes the scissors
Slide goes the thread
He says, "Stand still, kid
Let me read you instead"

Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
He puts us all together
Tailor on the block
(hey, tailor)
Tailor on the block
He makes the streets look better

Big Ray wants a second chance
In a coat cut sharp and clean
The baker gets a midnight black
With silver in the seam
Kids come in with ripped-up knees
And leave like movie stars
He folds the week in neat little lines
Then hands it back with scars

Snap goes the scissors
Slide goes the thread
He says, "Wear it like a promise
Wear it on your head"

Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
He puts us all together
Tailor on the block
(hey, tailor)
Tailor on the block
He makes the streets look better

When the rent runs hot
And the windows shake
He mends what people drop
He mends what people break
One sleeve for hope
One cuff for pride
Whole block shining up
On the same front line

Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
He puts us all together
Tailor on the block
(hey, tailor)
Tailor on the block
He makes the streets look better
Tailor on the block
Tailor on the block
He stitches us together
Tailor on the block
(hey, tailor)
Tailor on the block
We wear each other better

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Mr. Vale at the corner shop
Pins in his lips, never stops
Blue cloth draped on a rusted frame
He knows your cuffs, he knows your name
He takes old coats from the thrift-bin stack
Turns them front ways, turns them back
A loose hem fixed with a steady hand
Makes a Sunday out of work pants

He says, "Come in, come in"
"Tiny tears don't win"
He measures twice, then smiles
"Let's make you fit this mile"

He dresses the whole block
The whole block, the whole block
From the stoop to the bus stop
He dresses the whole block
(whole block)
(whole block)

Mrs. Bell gets a green suit made
For the bingo hall and the parade
Kids get patches on their knees
So they can run through the chain-link trees
The barber wears a silver seam
The baker gets a checkered dream
Even me with my frayed-up sleeves
Walk out looking like I believe

He says, "Come in, come in"
"Let the good days begin"
He chalks the line, cuts clean
Turns worn-out into seen

He dresses the whole block
The whole block, the whole block
From the stoop to the bus stop
He dresses the whole block
(whole block)
(whole block)

At dusk the windows glow
Needles flash, then slow
A line of neighbors waiting
For something worth creating
He lifts the collar, nods
Like a blessing from the gods
And every soul on Maple
Leaves a little more able

He dresses the whole block
The whole block, the whole block
From the stoop to the bus stop
He dresses the whole block
(whole block)
(whole block)

songs.subject: a blues song about being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise coffee my brain is hazy
Another Monday feels kinda lazy
Walk in the office they all look busy
My good ideas make them dizzy

Meeting blues oh meeting blues
My voice is gone I just lose lose lose
Meeting blues yeah a real bad scene
Never get a word in always keen

I raise my hand I got something smart
They cut me off tear my thoughts apart
Someone else says it five minutes later
They get the praise I'm the spectator

My lips are movin' no sound comes out
Just a whisper a silent shout

Meeting blues oh meeting blues
My voice is gone I just lose lose lose
Meeting blues yeah a real bad scene
Never get a word in always keen

Maybe I'll just hum a little tune
Or maybe I'll just leave this room soon
They talk so fast a verbal freeway
I'm stuck on the shoulder no other way

Another Monday feels kinda lazy

My good ideas make them dizzy

My voice is gone I just lose lose lose
Meeting blues yeah a real bad scene
Never get a word in always keen

I raise my hand I got something smart
They cut me off tear my thoughts apart
Someone else says it five minutes later
They get the praise I'm the spectator

My lips are movin' no sound comes out
Just a whisper a silent shout

Meeting blues oh meeting blues
My voice is gone I just lose lose lose
Meeting blues yeah a real bad scene
Never get a word in always keen

Maybe I'll just hum a little tune
Or maybe I'll just leave this room soon
They talk so fast a verbal freeway
I'm stuck on the shoulder no other way

songs.subject: a blues song about being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I said, "Good mornin'," you rolled right through
I had my notes, and my red pen too
I clear my throat, I lean my way
Somebody talks like I'm not here to stay

Ten o'clock, same painted scene
I raise my hand, they cut the green
I got a thought tryin' to break the chain
But my words get lost in the windowpane

I wait my turn, I bite my tongue
Count to ten, then count to one
By the time I speak, it's old news
Baby, that's the way they do the blues

Talked over again
Talked over again
I got somethin' to say
But they talk over me
Talked over again
Talked over again
I raise my hand high
Still they talk over me

Karen says, "Let's circle back"
Then the room goes off the track
My name's on the slide, my voice don't land
Like a folded bill stuck in my hand

So I take my pause, I take my space
I plant my feet, I keep my place
Next time I speak, they better hear
I'm not that quiet kind of near

Talked over again
Talked over again
I got somethin' to say
But they talk over me
Talked over again
Talked over again
I raise my hand high
Still they talk over me

One day soon, mark my word
My little truth is gonna be heard
Talked over again

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
We rolled out past the feed store sign
Two mason jars and a quarter tank line
Mama said, "Boy, don’t race that hill"
But the moon was white and the night stood still
A dog barked once by the cedar fence
My hands were slick, my nerves not dense
Then that old truck coughed at the creek
And the whole dark world got mean real quick

Oh, babe, oh no
The rear wheels spun in the mud below
Oh, babe, oh my
That sweet shine tipped and said goodbye

Moonshine run gone wrong
Moonshine run gone wrong
We had it all, then it slipped away
Moonshine run gone wrong
(oh-oh-oh)
Moonshine run gone wrong
(oo-oo-oo)
Moonshine run gone wrong
One crooked turn, one busted wheel
Moonshine run gone wrong

We hit that ditch by the sycamore
Glass went skittering across the floor
One jar burst with a sugary flash
One jar vanished in the blackberry brash
Sheriff lights blinked from the county road
I hit that pedal, the engine groaned
Jesse yelled, "Leave it, let it go!"
But I chased that shine through the clay and snow

Oh, babe, oh no
The headlights caught us slow, so slow
Oh, babe, oh my
That sweet shine lit the whole sky

Moonshine run gone wrong
Moonshine run gone wrong
We had it all, then it slipped away
Moonshine run gone wrong
(oh-oh-oh)
Moonshine run gone wrong
(oo-oo-oo)
Moonshine run gone wrong
One crooked turn, one busted wheel
Moonshine run gone wrong

Now we sit on the porch in the morning haze
Laughing at the mess that the county made
Got mud on the cuffs and glass in the seams
But I still taste trouble in my dreams

Moonshine run gone wrong
Moonshine run gone wrong
We had it all, then it slipped away
Moonshine run gone wrong
(oh-oh-oh)
Moonshine run gone wrong
(oo-oo-oo)
Moonshine run gone wrong
One crooked turn, one busted wheel
Moonshine run gone wrong

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise creeping
Mama still sleeping
I got the truck all gassed up ready to roll
Empty jars clinking
What was I thinking
This old back road gonna take its toll

Oh honey honey
For a little bit of money

Moonshine Mama don't you cry
Your sweet tea ain't runnin' dry
But the revenuers they caught my eye
Moonshine Mama they made me sigh

Dust cloud chasing
My heart was racing
Felt like a movie a black and white scene
Tires a-screeching
My old truck leaching
That sweet potent liquid all over the green

Oh sugar sugar
This ain't no boogie woogie bugler

Moonshine Mama don't you cry
Your sweet tea ain't runnin' dry
But the revenuers they caught my eye

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The kettle’s on
A thin whistle sighs
Another day
Just like the last
Beneath these weary skies
But yesterday
The chapel door
It groaned
And a hollow space where laughter used to roam

The teacups clatter
A nervous sound
Words unsaid
Still echo all around
A chair sits empty
By the hearth’s cold grate
Too early for this quiet
This unbidden fate

Oh
The long good-bye
It lingers still
A shadow on the window sill
The long good-bye
A whispered name
And nothing feels the same
No
Nothing feels the same

The dog looks up
With hopeful eyes
He doesn't know the reason for these hushed replies
He sniffs the air
For a hand that’s gone
And lays back down
Before the rising dawn

The peat fire smokes
A comfort old and deep
Promises made
That we could not keep
Just quiet breathing
In the morning chill

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The chair still holds your coat
By the cold old door
Tea gone cold on the sill
And the fire sleeps no more
I sweep the floor in silence
From your wake last night
The dawn comes in slow silver
And the house won’t feel right

But the yard is full of rain
And the grass is bent and bowed
I hear your name in the gate latch
In the gulls above the plough

Morning after the funeral
Morning after the rain
Hold your heart, hold your heart
Till we learn your name again
Morning after the funeral
We will sing you home
(we will sing you home)

Your boots by the settle
Still muddy at the toe
Your brother fixed the hinge last week
Your sister baked the scone
The neighbors left black ribbons
And a wreath of heathered red
Now the milk burns in the saucepan
And the dog lies by your bed

But the road goes out to barley
And the bell has done its cry
I can’t keep the dark from visiting
I can only let it lie

Morning after the funeral
Morning after the rain
Hold your heart, hold your heart
Till we learn your name again
Morning after the funeral
We will sing you home
(we will sing you home)

If the winter takes your doorway
If the mirror keeps your face
We’ll leave a lamp for walking
In the corner of this place
And when the first light finds us
On the stone and on the stream
We’ll carry all your stories
Like a fire carries steam

Morning after the funeral
Morning after the rain
Hold your heart, hold your heart
Till we learn your name again
Morning after the funeral
We will sing you home
(we will sing you home)
Morning after the funeral
You are not alone

songs.subject: a punk song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
I caught you by the sink
Staring at your hands
Blue veins like road maps
Fading through the tan
Your key ring shook a little
At the Laundromat
You laughed it off too quick
I saw what sat beneath that

You used to hammer nails
Like nothing could move you
Now you lean on the doorframe
Like the house has to prove you

Don’t go soft on me
Don’t go soft on me
I still see the man
In the busted up knees
Don’t go soft on me
Don’t go soft on me
You taught me how to stand
Now I’m standing for you

Your work shirt’s in the chair
Folded wrong again
I fixed the cuff for you
You said, “I’m fine, son”
But the pillbox on the table
Says what you won’t say
Seven tiny doors
Opened every day

You used to take the stairs
Two at a time, no fear
Now you catch your breath
When the TV’s too near

Don’t go soft on me
Don’t go soft on me
I still see the man
In the busted up knees
Don’t go soft on me
Don’t go soft on me
You taught me how to stand
Now I’m standing for you

At the baseball game
You forgot the score
Then you told that story
I’d heard a hundred times before
And I laughed too loud
To keep from crying
You said, “Quit your face”
Then you kept on smiling

Don’t go soft on me
Don’t go soft on me
I still see the man
In the busted up knees
Don’t go soft on me
Don’t go soft on me
If your hands are shaking
Mine can hold them steady

songs.subject: a punk song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Your hands got thin
Like paper bags
Same old knuckles
Same old scars
You missed a rung
At the garage steps
I caught your elbow
You laughed it off

You said, "I'm good"
Then sat right down
Took twice as long
To stand back up

Old man hands
Still reach for me
Old man hands
Don't let them shake
I watched you bend
I watched you change
Old man hands
Still made me brave

Your boots by the door
Used to slam the floor
Now they sit quiet
By the tile
You forget names
Then find my face
And grin like hell
For a while

You lose the thread
Then catch it back
In that half-smile
You always had

Old man hands
Still reach for me
Old man hands
Don't let them shake
I watched you bend
I watched you change
Old man hands
Still made me brave

I used to think
You'd stay steel
You'd stay loud
You'd stay unkillable
Now the TV hums
And the pills line up
And your breath goes small
But your grip says stay

Old man hands
Still reach for me
Old man hands
Don't let them shake
I watched you bend
I watched you change
Old man hands
Still made me brave
Old man hands
Hold on to me
Old man hands

songs.lyrics_en:
Your hands got thin
Like paper bags
Same old knuckles
Same old scars
You missed a rung
At the garage steps
I caught your elbow
You laughed it off

You said, "I'm good"
Then sat right down
Took twice as long
To stand back up

Old man hands
Still reach for me
Old man hands
Don't let them shake
I watched you bend
I watched you change
Old man hands
Still made me brave

Your boots by the door
Used to slam the floor
Now they sit quiet
By the tile
You forget names
Then find my face
And grin like hell
For a while

You lose the thread
Then catch it back
In that half-smile
You always had

Old man hands
Still reach for me
Old man hands
Don't let them shake
I watched you bend
I watched you change
Old man hands
Still made me brave

I used to think
You'd stay steel
You'd stay loud
You'd stay unkillable
Now the TV hums
And the pills line up
And your breath goes small
But your grip says stay

Old man hands
Still reach for me
Old man hands
Don't let them shake
I watched you bend
I watched you change
Old man hands
Still made me brave
Old man hands
Hold on to me
Old man hands

But you stayed you
Through every stage
Old man hands

songs.lyrics:
Baby, grip the wheel
Ten and two, let it breathe
Empty lot, chalk lines
Your shoes tap the asphalt seams
I watch your jaw go tight

Headlights wash the cones

Easy now, easy
Take your time

Roll it slow, baby
Roll it slow
You’ve got the road
You’ve got the glow
Roll it slow, baby
Let it go
I’m right here

Mirror, signal, pause

And your favorite song somehow
The curb looks mean tonight
But you smile and thread it through
One smooth little lane
Like the world was built for you

Easy now, easy
Hold that wheel

Make it real
One more try

Roll it slow, baby
Roll it slow

Like the whole world sighed

You drove right through the fear
Like a steady midnight blue

Roll it slow, baby
Roll it slow
You’ve got the road
You’ve got the glow
Roll it slow, baby
Let it go
I’m right here
You’ll learn to know

Keys in your palm
Heart on fire
My little driver
Taking the sky higher

songs.subject: a reggae song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights glowin' kinda dim
My textbook open on a whim
Brain cells jumpin' a little slow
Where did all my energy go

Tick-tock goes the clock
Another chapter another block

Oh two AM study time
My lonely grind my lonely climb
Oh two AM ain't no crime
Just me and the books just me and time

Coffee cold beside my hand
Wish I was on some sandy land
Equations swimmin' in my head
Should be tucked up in my bed

The moon she watchin' from up high
Wonder if she ever sighs
At all the dreamers burnin' bright
Through the long and quiet night

Oh two AM study time
My lonely grind my lonely climb
Oh two AM ain'

songs.subject: a reggae song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Book light on the wall
Pen marks in the page
Cup gone cold beside me
Mind still backstage
Paper stack leaning left
Notes in a crooked line
I got this quiet room
And I got this time

Two in the morning
No crowd, no call
Just me and the chapter
That’s all

I go page by page
(Page by page)
I go line by line
(Line by line)
Two in the morning
And I still rise
(page by page)
I keep on learning
I keep on learning
I keep on learning
Till the sun comes by

Outside, the dog barks once
Then the whole yard sleeps
Fan on the ceiling
Cutting little white beats
My phone face down
No buzz, no pull
Just the next hard question
And a mind half full

Two in the morning
Still here, still round
I build my future
From the ground

I go page by page
(Page by page)
I go line by line
(Line by line)
Two in the morning
And I still rise
(page by page)
I keep on learning
I keep on learning
I keep on learning
Till the sun comes by

When my eyes get heavy
I breathe and start again
One more term, one more chart
One more small win

I go page by page
(Page by page)
I go line by line
(Line by line)
Two in the morning
And I still rise
(page by page)
I keep on learning
I keep on learning
I keep on learning

songs.lyrics:

By the blue sink light
Two forks in the drawer
I keep one out of sight

I read the card

One plate left
One plate left

One plate left
One plate left

One plate left

I’m learning to stay
One plate left

I can feed myself today

I leave one chair empty

One plate left
I can feed myself today

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Sunrise painting the ocean gold
Another day unfolds
I write these words for you my dear
Whispers only you can hear

My love letters to the sea
Sent on the tide for you to see
Each wave a word a secret plea
Just for you and me

Ink bleeds blue like the deep deep sea
My heart beats wild and free
The gulls cry out your name above
A lighthouse keeper's endless love

My love letters to the sea
Sent on the tide for you to see
Each wave a word a secret plea
Just for you and me

They say I'm mad to talk to waves
But only you my spirit saves
From lonely nights and stormy weather
We belong together

My love letters to the sea
Sent

songs.subject: a dream pop song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I stamp each page with brine
Folded by lantern light
Your name in the margin
Ink from a shaking hand
I watch the black water
Climb the old stone steps
And every window
Keeps your hush for me

If you come close
I’ll let it show
The whole coast knows
What I cannot hold

I send you salt postcards
Salt postcards, come home
I send you salt postcards
To the mouth of the foam
Hold me in the undertow
Hold me where the cold winds go
I send you salt postcards
Salt postcards, come home

The gulls tear open dawn
White notes over the bay
I write on damp paper
What I can’t say your way
Your tide line on the glass
Your shadow on the pier
I keep the flame alive
Like you were still near

If you come close
I’ll let it show
The whole coast knows
What I cannot hold

I send you salt postcards
Salt postcards, come home
I send you salt postcards
To the mouth of the foam
Hold me in the undertow
Hold me where the cold winds go
I send you salt postcards
Salt postcards, come home

I tied my heart to the bell rope
Left it swaying in the fog
If your hands find the shoreline
Take the ache, take the log
Then the moon turns silver
And the stairs begin to glow
I’m still here at the window
Still writing where you go

I send you salt postcards
Salt postcards, come home
I send you salt postcards
To the mouth of the foam
Hold me in the undertow
Hold me where the cold winds go
I send you salt postcards
Salt postcards, come home

Salt on my fingers
Blue in my throat
I keep your address

songs.subject: a gothic country song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
"I still love you," he wrote, and he folded the note
Then he tucked it in a bottle, sealed it with a cork
With his last cigarette, watched the sun set
And he felt the cold mist on his skin

"Oh, dear Mother Sea, why'd you leave me?"
The words left his lips in a prayer to the breeze
He could still hear her voice as the waves lapped the shore
He tossed the bottle to the sea

In a '66 Mercury parked on the grass
A lamp in the dark, on his chest and his heart
In his arms, a rose petal from the past

"Oh, dear Mother Sea, why'd you leave me?"
The words left his lips in a prayer to the breeze

He tossed the bottle to the sea

songs.subject: a jazz song about the last day of summer before college

The night before they leave for school
They stay out late and drink by the pool
And they talk about who they wish they could be
A professor, a thief, and a king
Oh, they're chasin' down their every last wish
Summer's last kiss
Oh, with her September eyes
And the night when their childhood died

As they say goodbye
He says, "See you next year"
She says, "You'll see me in your dreams, my dear"
As she leaves
Oh, they fall on their knees
As they scream at the trees

"God, we love you
For all the things that you've done
Oh, you brought us the rain
Oh, you gave us the sun
And we will love you
Oh, when the summer's all gone
So take us away
When the night is all done"

So the next mornin', they drive into town
And the snowflakes fall and cover the ground
And they sing, "Bye-bye, Miss American Pie"
As the town fades away from their eyes
As they enter the city and leave all they know
As they grow old
As they look back on their lives
They think of her September eyes

As they say goodbye
He says, "See you next year"
She says, "You'll see me in your dreams, my dear"

"God, we love you

And we will love you
Oh, when the summer's all gone
So take us away

Thick dark hair

Not a city boy
He's a surfer

Blue jean beach
Oh dear

But you know

Got your life
